sections.title_en: Opening

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين توى / ‏كه گذشت از مه به نورت مثنوى
entries.english: O Ziya ul-Haqq, Husamu’din, you are he through whose light the Masnavi has surpassed the moon.

entries.farsi: همت عالى تو اى مرتجا / مى‏كشد اين را خدا داند كجا
entries.english: O you in whom hopes are placed, your lofty aspiration is drawing this God knows where.

entries.farsi: گردن اين مثنوى را بسته‏اى / ‏مى‏كشى آن سوى كه دانسته‏اى
entries.english: You have bound the neck of this Masnavi: you are drawing it in the direction known to you.

entries.farsi: مثنوى پويان كشنده ناپديد / ناپديد از جاهلى كش نيست ديد
entries.english: The Masnavi is running on, the drawer is unseen— unseen by the ignorant one who has no insight.

entries.farsi: مثنوى را چون تو مبدا بوده‏اى / ‏گر فزون گردد تواش افزوده‏اى
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have been the origin of the Masnavi, if it increases, you have caused it to increase.

entries.farsi: چون چنين خواهى خدا خواهد چنين / ‏مى‏دهد حق آرزوى متقين
entries.english: Since you wish it so, God wishes it so: God grants the desire of the devout.

entries.farsi: كان هلل بوده‏اى در ما مضى / تا كه كان اللهَّ پيش آمد جزا
entries.english: In the past you have been “he belongs to God,” so that “God belongs” has come in recompense.

entries.farsi: مثنوى از تو هزاران شكر داشت / ‏در دعا و شكر كفها بر فراشت
entries.english: On your account the Masnavi had thousands of thanksgivings: it lifted up its hands in prayer and thanksgiving.

entries.farsi: در لب و كفش خدا شكر تو ديد / فضل كرد و لطف فرمود و مزيد
entries.english: God saw thanksgiving to you on its lips and in its hands: He showed grace and bestowed favour and increase;

entries.farsi: ز انكه شاكر را زيادت وعده است / ‏آن چنان كه قرب مزد سجده است
entries.english: For to him that gives thanks increase is promised, just as nearness is the reward for prostration.

entries.farsi: گر زيادت مى‏شود زين رو بود / نه از براى بوش و هاى و هو بود
entries.english: Our God has said, “And prostrate yourself and come near”: the prostration of our bodies is become the nearness of the spirit.

entries.farsi: گفت وَ اسْ جُدْ وَ اقْتَرِبْ يزدان ما / قرب جان شد سجده‏ى ابدان ما
entries.english: If increase is accruing, it is for this reason; it is not for the sake of vainglory and noise.

entries.farsi: با تو ما چون رز به تابستان خوشيم / ‏حكم دارى هين بكش تا مى‏كشيم
entries.english: We are glad with you as the vineyard in the summer heat: you have the authority: come, draw that we may always be drawing.

entries.farsi: خوش بكش اين كاروان را تا به حج / ‏اى امير صبر مفتاح الفرج
entries.english: Draw happily this caravan to the Pilgrimage, O Commander of “Patience is the key to joy.”

entries.farsi: حج زيارت كردن خانه بود / حج رب البيت مردانه بود
entries.english: The Pilgrimage consists in visiting the House, the Pilgrimage to the Lord of the House is worthy of a man.

entries.farsi: ز آن ضيا گفتم حسام الدين ترا / كه تو خورشيدى و اين دو وصفها
entries.english: I called you Ziya Husamu’din because you are the Sun, and these two are epithets descriptive;

entries.farsi: كاين حسام و اين ضيا يكى است هين / ‏تيغ خورشيد از ضيا باشد يقين
entries.english: For, mark you, this sword and this radiance are one: the sun’s sword is certainly of the radiance.

entries.farsi: نور از آن ماه باشد وين ضيا / آن خورشيد اين فرو خوان از نبا
entries.english: Nur belongs to the moon, and this radiance (Ziya) belongs to the sun: read this in the Qur’an.

entries.farsi: شمس را قرآن ضيا خواند اى پدر / و آن قمر را نور خواند اين را نگر
entries.english: The Qur’an has called the sun Ziya, O father, and it has called the moon Nur. Consider this!

entries.farsi: شمس چون عالى‏تر آمد خود ز ماه / ‏پس ضيا از نور افزون دان به جاه
entries.english: Since the sun is more exalted even than the moon, know, then, that Ziya is superior to Nur in dignity.

entries.farsi: بس كس اندر نور مه منهج نديد / چون بر آمد آفتاب آن شد پديد
entries.english: Many a one did not see the way in the moonlight; it became visible as soon as the sun rose.

entries.farsi: آفتاب اعواض را كامل نمود / الجرم بازارها در روز بود
entries.english: The sun displayed objects of exchange perfectly: of necessity, markets were in the daytime,

entries.farsi: تا كه قلب و نقد نيك آيد پديد / تا بود از غبن و از حيله بعيد
entries.english: In order that the false coin and the good money might come into view, and that he might be far from swindling and trickery.

entries.farsi: تا كه نورش كامل آمد در زمين / ‏تاجران را رَحْ مَةً للعالمين
entries.english: Until its light came to perfection on the earth, a universal mercy to the traders;

entries.farsi: ليك بر قالب مبغوض است و سخت / ‏ز انك ازو شد كاسد او را نقد و رخت
entries.english: But to the counterfeiter it is hateful and grievous, because by it his money and wares are made unsaleable.

entries.farsi: پس عدوى جان صراف است قلب / ‏دشمن درويش كه بود غير كلب
entries.english: Hence the false coin is the mortal foe of the money-changer: who is the enemy of the dervish but the dog?

entries.farsi: انبيا با دشمنان بر مى‏تنند / پس ماليك رب سلم مى‏زنند
entries.english: The prophets contend with their enemies; then the angels utter cries of “Save, O Lord,”

entries.farsi: كاين چراغى را كه هست او نور كار / از پف و دمهاى دزدان دور دار
entries.english: Saying, “Keep this Lamp, which is light-disseminating, far from the puffs and breaths of thieves”

entries.farsi: دزد و قالب است خصم نور بس / ‏زين دو اى فريادرس فرياد رس
entries.english: Only the thief and the counterfeiter are adversaries of the light: succour from these two, O Succourer!

entries.farsi: روشنى بر دفتر چارم بريز / كافتاب از چرخ چارم كرد خيز
entries.english: Shed light upon the Fourth Book, for the sun rose from the Fourth Heaven.

entries.farsi: هين ز چارم نور ده خورشيدوار / تا بتابد بر بالد و بر ديار
entries.english: Come, give light, like the sun, from the Fourth Book, so that it may shine upon countries and inhabited lands.

entries.farsi: هر كش افسانه بخواند افسانه است / ‏و انكه ديدش نقد خود مردانه است
entries.english: Whoever reads it an idle tale, he is an idle tale; and he who regards it as money in his own hands is like a man.

entries.farsi: آب نيل است و به قبطى خون نمود / قوم موسى را نه خون بد آب بود
entries.english: It is the water of the Nile, which seemed blood to the Egyptian, to the people of Moses was not blood, but water.

entries.farsi: دشمن اين حرف اين دم در نظر / شد ممثل سر نگون اندر سقر
entries.english: At this moment the enemy of these words is pictured in sight headlong into Hell-fire.

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق تو ديدى حال او / حق نمودت پاسخ افعال او
entries.english: O Ziya ul-Haqq, you have seen his state: God has shown unto you the answer to his actions.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى غيبت چو غيب است اوستاد / كم مبادا زين جهان اين ديد و داد
entries.english: Your eye which beholds the invisible is a master like the Invisible: may this vision and gift not vanish from this world!

entries.farsi: اين حكايت را كه نقد وقت ماست / ‏گر تمامش مى‏كنى اينجا رواست
entries.english: If you will complete this story, which is the current coin of our present state, it is fitting.

entries.farsi: ناكسان را ترك كن بهر كسان / ‏قصه را پايان بر و مخلص رسان
entries.english: Leave the unworthy folk for the sake of the worthy: bring the tale to the end and conduct it to the issue.

entries.farsi: اين حكايت گر نشد آن جا تمام / ‏چارمين جلد است آرش در نظام
entries.english: If this story was not finished there, it is the Fourth Volume: set it out in order.

sections.title_fa: تمامى حكايت آن عاشق كه از عسس گريخت در باغى مجهول خود معشوق را در باغ يافت و عسس ْرا از شادى دعاى خير مى‏كرد و مى‏گفت كه عَ سى‏ أَنْ تَكْرَهُوا شَ يْئاً وَ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُم
sections.title_en: Conclusion of the story of the lover who fled from the night-patrol into an orchard unknown to him
and for joy at finding his beloved in the orchard called down blessings on the night-patrol and said, “It may be that you loathe a thing although it is better for you.”

entries.farsi: اندر آن بوديم كان شخص از عسس / ‏راند اندر باغ از خوفى فرس
entries.english: We were at the point where that person in terror from the night-patrol galloped into the orchard.

entries.farsi: بود اندر باغ آن صاحب جمال / ‏كز غمش اين در عنا بد هشت سال
entries.english: In the orchard was the beauteous one for love of whom this had been in tribulation eight years.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى او را نبود امكان ديد / همچو عنقا وصف او را مى‏شنيد
entries.english: He had no possibility of seeing her shadow: he was hearing the description of her, as the Anqa,

entries.farsi: جز يكى لقيه كه اول از قضا / بر وى افتاد و شد او را دل ربا
entries.english: Except one meeting which happened to him by destiny at the first and ravished his heart

entries.farsi: بعد از آن چندان كه مى‏كوشيد او / خود مجالش مى‏نداد آن تند خو
entries.english: After that, however much effort he made, in sooth that cruel one would give him no opportunity.

entries.farsi: نه به البه چاره بودش نه به مال / ‏چشم پر و بى‏طمع بود آن نهال
entries.english: Neither entreaty nor wealth availed him: that sapling was fully satisfied and without desire.

entries.farsi: عاشق هر پيشه‏اى و مطلبى / ‏حق بيالود اول كارش لبى
entries.english: The lover of any craft or object of pursuit, God has touched his lip at the beginning of the affair;

entries.farsi: چون بد آن آسيب در جست آمدند / پيش پاشان مى‏نهد هر روز بند
entries.english: When at that contact they have entered upon the quest, He lays a snare before their feet every day.

entries.farsi: چون در افگندش به جست و جوى كار / بعد از آن در بست كه كابين بيار
entries.english: When He has plunged him into search for the matter, after that He shuts the door, saying, “Bring the dowry”

entries.farsi: هم بر آن بو مى‏تنند و مى‏روند / هر دمى راجى و آيس مى‏شوند
entries.english: Still they cling to that scent and go: at every moment they become hopeful and despairing.

entries.farsi: هر كسى را هست اوميد برى / ‏كه گشادندش در آن روزى درى
entries.english: Everyone has hope of the fruit to which a door was opened to him on a certain day;

entries.farsi: باز در بستندش و آن در پرست / ‏بر همان اوميد آتش پا شده‏ست
entries.english: Then it was shut again; that devotee to the door, in the same hope, has become fire-footed.

entries.farsi: چون در آمد خوش در آن باغ آن جوان / ‏خود فرو شد پا به گنجش ناگهان
entries.english: When the youth joyously entered that orchard, truly on a sudden his foot sank in the treasure.

entries.farsi: مر عسس را ساخته يزدان سبب / ‏تا ز بيم او دود در باغ شب
entries.english: God had made the night-patrol the means, so that in fear of him he should run into the orchard by night

entries.farsi: بيند آن معشوقه را او با چراغ / ‏طالب انگشترى در جوى باغ
entries.english: And should see the beloved one searching with a lantern for a ring in the rivulet of the orchard

entries.farsi: كه زيان كردم عسس را از گريز / بيست چندان سيم و زر بر وى بريز
entries.english: Therefore at that moment, from the delight, he joined praise of God with prayers for the night-patrol,

entries.farsi: پس قرين مى‏كرد از ذوق آن نفس / ‏با ثناى حق دعاى آن عسس
entries.english: Saying, “I caused loss to the night-patrol by fleeing: scatter over him twenty times as much silver and gold.

entries.farsi: از عوانى مر و را آزاد كن / ‏آن چنان كه شادم او را شاد كن
entries.english: Set him free from policing: make him glad even as I am glad.

entries.farsi: سعد دارش اين جهان و آن جهان / ‏از عوانى و سگى‏اش وارهان
entries.english: Keep him blest in this world and in that world, deliver him from policing and currishness—

entries.farsi: گر چه خوى آن عوان هست اى خدا / كه هماره خلق را خواهد بال
entries.english: Though it is the nature of that policeman, O God that he always desires the people to be afflicted”

entries.farsi: گر خبر آيد كه شه جرمى نهاد / بر مسلمانان شود او زفت و شاد
entries.english: If news come that the king has imposed a fine upon the Moslems, he waxes big and exultant;

entries.farsi: ور خبر آيد كه شه رحمت نمود / از مسلمانان فگند آن را به جود
entries.english: And if news come that the king has shown mercy and has generously taken off that from the Moslems,

entries.farsi: او عوان را در دعا در مى‏كشيد / كز عوان او را چنان راحت رسيد
entries.english: Mournfulness falls upon his soul thereat: the policeman has a hundred such depravities.

entries.farsi: ماتمى در جان او افتد از آن / ‏صد چنين ادبارها دارد عوان
entries.english: He was bringing the policeman into the prayer, because such solace had come to him from the policeman.

entries.farsi: بر همه زهر و بر او ترياق بود / آن عوان پيوند آن مشتاق بود
entries.english: He was poison to all, but to him the antidote: the policeman was the means of uniting that longing lover.

entries.farsi: پس بد مطلق نباشد در جهان / ‏بد به نسبت باشد اين را هم بدان
entries.english: Hence there is no absolute evil in the world: evil is relative. Know this also.

entries.farsi: در زمانه هيچ زهر و قند نيست / ‏كه يكى را پا دگر را بند نيست
entries.english: In Time there is no poison or sugar that is not a foot to one and a fetter to another—

entries.farsi: مر يكى را پا دگر را پاى‏بند / مر يكى را زهر و بر ديگر چو قند
entries.english: To one a foot, to another a fetter; to one a poison and to another like sugar

entries.farsi: زهر مار آن مار را باشد حيات / ‏نسبتش با آدمى باشد ممات
entries.english: Snake-poison is life to the snake, it is death in relation to man.

entries.farsi: خلق آبى را بود دريا چو باغ / ‏خلق خاكى را بود آن مرگ و داغ
entries.english: The sea is as a garden to the water-creatures; to the creatures of earth it is death and a brand.

entries.farsi: همچنين بر مى‏شمر اى مرد كار / نسبت اين از يكى كس تا هزار
entries.english: Reckon up likewise, O man of experience, this relativity from a single individual to a thousand.

entries.farsi: زيد اندر حق آن شيطان بود / در حق شخصى دگر سلطان بود
entries.english: Zayd, in regard to that one, may be a devil, in regard to another person he may be a sultan.

entries.farsi: آن بگويد زيد صديق سنى است / ‏وين بگويد زيد گبر كشتنى است
entries.english: That one will say that Zayd is an exalted siddiq, and this one will say that Zayd is an infidel who ought to be killed.

entries.farsi: زيد يك ذات است بر آن يك جنان / ‏او بر اين ديگر همه رنج و زيان
entries.english: Zayd is one person—to that one a shield, to this other one wholly pain and loss.

entries.farsi: گر تو خواهى كاو ترا باشد شكر / پس و را از چشم عشاقش نگر
entries.english: If you wish that he should be sugar to you, then look on him with the eye of lovers.

entries.farsi: منگر از چشم خودت آن خوب را / بين به چشم طالبان مطلوب را
entries.english: Do not look on that Beauteous One with your own eye: behold the Sought with the eye of seekers.

entries.farsi: چشم خود بر بند ز آن خوش چشم تو / عاريت كن چشم از عشاق او
entries.english: Shut your own eye to that Sweet-eyed One: borrow an eye from His lovers.

entries.farsi: بلك از او كن عاريت چشم و نظر / پس ز چشم او به روى او نگر
entries.english: Nay, borrow eye and sight from Him, and then look on His face with His eye,

entries.farsi: تا شوى ايمن ز سيرى و مالل / ‏گفت كان اللهَّ له زين ذو الجلال
entries.english: So that you may be secure from satiety and weariness: on this account the Almighty said, “God shall belong to him:

entries.farsi: چشم او من باشم و دست و دلش / ‏تا رهد از مدبريها مقبلش
entries.english: I shall be his eye and hand and heart,” to the end that His fortunate one should escape from adversities

entries.farsi: هر چه مكروه است چون شد او دليل / ‏سوى محبوبت حبيب است و خليل
entries.english: Whatever is loathed is a lover and friend when it becomes your guide towards your beloved.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت آن واعظ كه هر آغاز تذكير دعاى ظالمان و سخت دالن و بى‏اعتقادان كردى
sections.title_en: Story of the preacher who at the beginning of every exhortation used to pray for the unjust and hard-hearted and irreligious

entries.farsi: آن يكى واعظ چو بر تخت آمدى / ‏قاطعان راه را داعى شدى
entries.english: A certain preacher, whenever he mounted the pulpit, would begin to pray for the highway robbers.

entries.farsi: دست بر مى‏داشت يا رب رحم ران / ‏بر بدان و مفسدان و طاغيان
entries.english: He would lift up his hand, “O Lord, let mercy fall upon evil men and corrupters and insolent transgressors,

entries.farsi: بر همه‏ى تسخر كنان اهل خير / بر همه‏ى كافر دالن و اهل دير
entries.english: Upon all who make a mock of the good people, upon all whose hearts are unbelieving and those who dwell in the Christian monastery.”

entries.farsi: مى‏نكردى او دعا بر اصفيا / مى‏نكردى جز خبيثان را دعا
entries.english: He would not pray for the pure; he would pray for none but the wicked.

entries.farsi: مر و را گفتند كاين معهود نيست / ‏دعوت اهل ضاللت جود نيست
entries.english: They said to him, “This is unknown: it is no generosity to pray for the people of unrighteousness.”

entries.farsi: گفت نيكويى از اينها ديده‏ام / ‏من دعاشان زين سبب بگزيده‏ام
entries.english: He replied, “I have seen goodness from these folk: for this reason I have chosen to pray for them.

entries.farsi: خبث و ظلم و جور چندان ساختند / كه مرا از شر به خير انداختند
entries.english: They wrought so much wickedness and injustice and oppression that they cast me forth from evil into good.

entries.farsi: هر گهى كه رو به دنيا كردمى / ‏من از ايشان زخم و ضربت خوردمى
entries.english: Whenever I turned my face towards this world, I suffered blows and beating from them,

entries.farsi: كردمى از زخم آن جانب پناه / ‏باز آوردندمى گرگان به راه
entries.english: And took refuge from the blows yonder: the wolves were always bringing me back into the Way.

entries.farsi: چون سبب ساز صالح من شدند / پس دعاشان بر من است اى هوشمند
entries.english: Inasmuch as they contrived the means of my welfare, it behooves me to pray for them, O intelligent one.”

entries.farsi: بنده مى‏نالد به حق از درد و نيش / ‏صد شكايت مى‏كند از رنج خويش
entries.english: The servant complains to God of pain and smart: he makes a hundred complaints of his pain.

entries.farsi: حق همى‏گويد كه آخر رنج و درد / مر ترا البه‏كنان و راست كرد
entries.english: God says, “After all, grief and pain have made you humbly entreating and righteous.

entries.farsi: اين گله ز آن نعمتى كن كت زند / از در ما دور و مطرودت كند
entries.english: Make this complaint of the bounty that befalls you and removes you far from My door and makes you an outcast.”

entries.farsi: در حقيقت هر عدو داروى تست / ‏كيميا و نافع و دل جوى تست
entries.english: In reality every foe is your medicine: he is an elixir and beneficial and one that seeks to win your heart;

entries.farsi: كه از او اندر گريزى در خال / استعانت جويى از لطف خدا
entries.english: For you flee from him into solitude and would gladly implore help of God’s grace.

entries.farsi: در حقيقت دوستانت دشمنند / كه ز حضرت دور و مشغولت كنند
entries.english: Your friends are really enemies, for they make you far from the Presence and occupied.

entries.farsi: هست حيوانى كه نامش اشغر است / ‏او به زخم چوب زفت و لمتر است
entries.english: There is an animal whose name is ushghur (porcupine): it is stout and big by blows of the stick.

entries.farsi: تا كه چوبش مى‏زنى به مى‏شود / او ز زخم چوب فربه مى‏شود
entries.english: The more you cudgel it, the more it thrives: it grows fat on blows of the stick.

entries.farsi: نفس مومن اشغرى آمد يقين / ‏كاو به زخم رنج زفت است و سمين
entries.english: Assuredly the true believer’s soul is a porcupine, for it is stout and fat by the blows of tribulation.

entries.farsi: زين سبب بر انبيا رنج و شكست / از همه خلق جهان افزون‏تر است
entries.english: For this reason the tribulation and abasement upon the prophets is greater than all the creatures in the world,

entries.farsi: تا ز جانها جانشان شد زفت‏تر / كه نديدند آن بال قوم دگر
entries.english: So that their souls became stouter than souls; for no other class of people suffered that affliction.

entries.farsi: پوست از دارو بالكش مى‏شود / چون اديم طايفى خوش مى‏شود
entries.english: The hide is afflicted by the medicine, it becomes sweet like Tá’if leather;

entries.farsi: ور نه تلخ و تيز ماليدى در او / گنده گشتى ناخوش و ناپاك بو
entries.english: And if he did not rub the bitter and acrid liquor into it, it would become fetid, unpleasant, and foul-smelling.

entries.farsi: آدمى را پوست نامد بوغ دان / ‏از رطوبتها شده زشت و گران
entries.english: Know that Man is an untanned hide, made noisome and gross by humours.

entries.farsi: تلخ و تيز و مالش بسيار ده / ‏تا شود پاك و لطيف و بافره
entries.english: Give bitter and acrid and much rubbing, that he may become pure and lovely and exceedingly strong;

entries.farsi: ور نمى‏تانى رضا ده اى عيار / گر خدا رنجت دهد بى‏اختيار
entries.english: But if you cannot, be content, O cunning one, if God give you tribulation without choice,

entries.farsi: كه بالى دوست تطهير شماست / ‏علم او بالاى تدبير شماست
entries.english: For affliction by the Friend is your being purified: His knowledge is above your contrivance.

entries.farsi: چون صفا بيند بال شيرين شود / خوش شود دارو چو صحت بين شود
entries.english: The affliction becomes sweet when he sees happiness: the medicine becomes sweet when he regards health.

entries.farsi: برد بيند خويش را در عين مات / ‏پس بگويد اقتلوني يا ثقات
entries.english: He sees victory for himself in the very essence of checkmate; therefore he says, “Kill me, O trusty ones!”

entries.farsi: اين عوان در حق غيرى سود شد / ليك اندر حق خود مردود شد
entries.english: This policeman became a profit in respect of another, but he became reprobate in respect of himself.

entries.farsi: رحم ايمانى از او ببريده شد / كين شيطانى بر او پيچيده شد
entries.english: The mercy appertaining to the Faith was cut off from him; the hate inherent in the Devil enfolded him.

entries.farsi: كارگاه خشم گشت و كين‏ورى / ‏كينه دان اصل ضالل و كافرى
entries.english: He became a factory of anger and hatred: know that hate is the root of error and infidelity.

sections.title_fa: ‏سؤال كردن از عيسى عليه السلام كه در وجود از همه‏ى صعبها صعب‏تر چيست
sections.title_en: How they asked Jesus, on whom be peace, saying, “O Spirit of God, what is the hardest thing to bear of all the hard things in existence?”

entries.farsi: گفت عيسى را يكى هشيار سر / چيست در هستى ز جمله صعب‏تر
entries.english: A sober-minded man said to Jesus, “What is the hardest to bear of all things in existence?”

entries.farsi: گفتش اى جان صعب‏تر خشم خدا / كه از آن دوزخ همى‏لرزد چو ما
entries.english: He replied, “O soul, the hardest is God’s anger, on account of which Hell is trembling as we.”

entries.farsi: گفت از اين خشم خدا چه بود امان / ‏گفت ترك خشم خويش اندر زمان
entries.english: He said, “What is the security against this anger of God?” Jesus said, “To abandon your own anger at once.”

entries.farsi: پس عوان كه معدن اين خشم گشت / ‏خشم زشتش از سبع هم در گذشت
entries.english: Therefore, as the policeman became this anger’s mine, his ugly anger surpassed even a wild beast.

entries.farsi: چه اميدستش به رحمت جز مگر / باز گردد ز آن صفت آن بى‏هنر
entries.english: What hope is there for him of mercy, unless perchance that graceless man should turn back from that quality?

entries.farsi: گر چه عالم را از ايشان چاره نيست / ‏اين سخن اندر ضالل افكندنى است
entries.english: Although the world cannot do without them, this statement is a casting into error.

entries.farsi: چاره نبود هر جهان را از چمين / ‏ليك نبود آن چمين ماء معين
entries.english: The world cannot do without urine either, but that urine is not clear running water.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصد خيانت كردن عاشق و بانگ بر زدن معشوق بر وى
sections.title_en: The lover’s attempted treachery, and how the beloved scolded him.

entries.farsi: چون كه تنهايش بديد آن ساده مرد / زود او قصد كنار و بوسه كرد
entries.english: When that simpleton found her alone, at once he attempted to embrace and kiss her.

entries.farsi: بانگ بر وى زد به هيبت آن نگار / كه مرو گستاخ ادب را هوش دار
entries.english: The beauty with an awesome demeanor raised her voice against him, saying, “Do not behave impudently, be mindful of good manners!”

entries.farsi: كس نمى‏جنبد در اين جا جز كه باد / كيست حاضر كيست مانع زين گشاد
entries.english: He said, “Why, there is privacy, and no people: the water at hand, and a thirsty man like me!

entries.farsi: گفت آخر خلوت است و خلق نى / ‏آب حاضر تشنه‏اى همچون منى
entries.english: None is moving here but the wind. Who is present? Who will hinder from this conquest?”

entries.farsi: باد را ديدى كه مى‏جنبد بدان / ‏باد جنبانى است اينجا باد ران
entries.english: “O madman,” said she, “you have been a fool: a fool you are and have not hearkened to the wise.

entries.farsi: گفت اى شيدا تو ابله بوده‏اى / ‏ابلهى و ز عاقالن نشنوده‏اى
entries.english: You saw the wind moving: know that a Mover of the wind is here, who drives the wind along.”

entries.farsi: مروحه‏ى تصريف صنع ايزدش / ‏زد بر اين باد و همى‏جنباندش
entries.english: The fan, namely, the direction of its course by God’s action, smote upon this wind and is always keeping it in movement.

entries.farsi: جزو بادى كه به حكم مادر است / ‏باد بيزن تا نجنبانى نجست
entries.english: The portion of wind that is in our control does not stir till you move the fan.

entries.farsi: جنبش اين جزو باد اى ساده مرد / بى‏تو و بى‏بادبيزن سر نكرد
entries.english: Without you and without the fan the movement of this portion of wind does not arise, O simpleton.

entries.farsi: جنبش باد نفس كاندر لب است / ‏تابع تصريف جان و قالب است
entries.english: The movement of the wind of the breath, which is on the lips, follows the course directed by the spirit and the body.

entries.farsi: گاه دم را مدح و پيغامى كنى / ‏گاه دم را هجو و دشنامى كنى
entries.english: At one time you make the breath to be a eulogy and a message; at another time you make the breath to be a satire and a foul speech.

entries.farsi: پس بدان احوال ديگر بادها / ‏كه ز جزوى كل همى‏بيند نهى
entries.english: Understand, then, the cases of other winds; for from a part the intellect perceives the whole.

entries.farsi: باد را حق گه بهارى مى‏كند / در دى‏اش زين لطف عارى مى‏كند
entries.english: God sometimes makes the wind vernal: in December He divests it of this kindliness.

entries.farsi: باد دم را بر تو بنهاد او اساس / ‏تا كنى هر باد را بر وى قياس
entries.english: He makes it a sarsar (intensely cold and violent) for the people of ‘Ad; again, He makes it perfumed for Hud

entries.farsi: بر گروه عاد صرصر مى‏كند / باز بر هودش معطر مى‏كند
entries.english: One wind He makes the poison of the simoom; He makes the advent of the east-wind to be delightful.

entries.farsi: دم نمى‏گردد سخن بى‏لطف و قهر / بر گروهى شهد و بر قومى است زهر
entries.english: He has founded the wind of the breath in you, in order that thereby you may judge analogically of every wind.

entries.farsi: مى‏كند يك باد را زهر سموم / ‏مر صبا را مى‏كند خرم قدوم
entries.english: The breath does not become speech without gentleness or harshness: it is honey for one set of people and poison for another class.

entries.farsi: مروحه جنبان پى انعام كس / ‏و ز براى قهر هر پشه و مگس
entries.english: The fan is moving for the benefit of the person, and for the subjugation of every fly and gnat.

entries.farsi: مروحه‏ى تقدير ربانى چرا / پر نباشد ز امتحان و ابتال
entries.english: Why should not the fan of Divine fore-ordainment be fraught with trial and probation?

entries.farsi: چون كه جزو باد دم يا مروحه / ‏نيست الا مفسده يا مصلحه
entries.english: Inasmuch as the part, namely, the wind of the breath or the fan, is nothing but a cause of injury or advantage,

entries.farsi: اين شمال و اين صبا و اين دبور / كى بود از لطف و از انعام دور
entries.english: How should this north-wind and this east-wind and this west- wind be remote from favour and conferring bounty?

entries.farsi: يك كف گندم ز انبارى ببين / ‏فهم كن كان جمله باشد همچنين
entries.english: Look at a handful of wheat froth a granary, and apprehend that the whole of it will be just like this.

entries.farsi: كل باد از برج باد آسمان / ‏كى جهد بى‏مروحه‏ى آن باد ران
entries.english: How should the whole of the wind rush forth from the mansion of the wind in Heaven without the fan of that Driver of the wind?

entries.farsi: بر سر خرمن به وقت انتقاد / نه كه فالحان ز حق جويند باد
entries.english: Is it not the fact that at winnowing-time the labourers on the threshing-floor beseech God for wind?

entries.farsi: تا جدا گردد ز گندم كاهها / تا به انبارى رود يا چاهها
entries.english: In order that the straws may be separated from the wheat, so that it may go into a granary or pits?

entries.farsi: چون بماند دير آن باد وزان / ‏جمله را بينى به حق البه‏كنان
entries.english: When the blowing wind is long delayed, you may see them all making humble entreaty to God.

entries.farsi: همچنين در طلق آن باد والد / گر نيايد بانگ درد آيد كه داد
entries.english: Likewise, in child birth, if the wind of childbirth doesn’t come, there comes a woeful cry for help.

entries.farsi: گر نمى‏دانند كش راننده اوست / ‏باد را پس كردن زارى چه خوست
entries.english: If they are not aware that He is its Driver, then what disposes to pray piteously for the wind?

entries.farsi: اهل كشتى همچنين جوياى باد / جمله خواهانش از آن رب العباد
entries.english: Likewise, those in ship are desirous of the wind: they all are begging for it from the Lord of Mankind.

entries.farsi: همچنين در درد دندانها ز باد / دفع مى‏خواهى به سوز و اعتقاد
entries.english: Likewise, in toothache you beg ardently and earnestly to be defended from the wind.

entries.farsi: از خدا البه‏كنان آن جنديان / ‏كه بده باد ظفر اى كامران
entries.english: The soldiers beseech God humbly, saying, “Give the wind of victory, O You whose every wish is fulfilled!”

entries.farsi: رقعه‏ى تعويذ مى‏خواهند نيز / در شكنجه‏ى طلق زن از هر عزيز
entries.english: Also, in the throes of childbirth, folk beg from every venerated a piece of paper inscribed with a charm.

entries.farsi: پس همه دانسته‏اند آن را يقين / ‏كه فرستد باد رب العالمين
entries.english: Therefore all have known for certain that the wind is sent by the Lord of created beings.

entries.farsi: پس يقين در عقل هر داننده هست / ‏اينكه با جنبنده جنباننده هست
entries.english: Therefore in the mind of every one possessing knowledge this is certain, that with everything that moves there is a mover.

entries.farsi: گر تو او را مى‏نبينى در نظر / فهم كن آن را به اظهار اثر
entries.english: If you do not see him visibly, apprehend him by means of the manifestation of the effect.

entries.farsi: تن به جان جنبد نمى‏بينى تو جان / ‏ليك از جنبيدن تن جان بدان
entries.english: The body is moved by the spirit: you do not see the spirit; but from the movement of the body know the spirit.

entries.farsi: گفت او گر ابلهم من در ادب / ‏زيركم اندر وفا و در طلب
entries.english: He said, “If I am foolish in manners, I am wise in respect of faithfulness and pursuit.”

entries.farsi: گفت ادب اين بود خود كه ديده شد / آن دگر را خود همى‏دانى تو لد
entries.english: She replied, “Truly the manners were these which have been seen; as for the other, you yourself know, perverse fellow!

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى آن صوفى كه زن خود را با بيگانه بگرفت
sections.title_en: Story of the Sufi who caught his wife with a strange man

entries.farsi: صوفيى آمد به سوى خانه روز / خانه يك در بود و زن با كفش دوز
entries.english: A Sufi came to his house in the daytime: the house had one door, and his wife was with a cobbler.

entries.farsi: جفت گشته با رهى خويش زن / ‏اندر آن يك حجره از وسواس تن
entries.english: Pandering to physical temptation the woman slept with her lover in that room.

entries.farsi: چون بزد صوفى به جد در چاشت‏گاه / ‏هر دو درماندند نه حيلت نه راه
entries.english: When in the forenoon the Sufi knocked on the door with all his might, both were at a loss: neither device nor way.

entries.farsi: هيچ معهودش نبد كاو آن زمان / ‏سوى خانه باز گردد از دكان
entries.english: It was never known for him to return home from the shop at that time,

entries.farsi: قاصدا آن روز بى‏وقت آن مروع / ‏از خيالى كرد تا خانه رجوع
entries.english: But on that day the alarmed man purposely returned to his house at an unseasonable hour, because he was suspicious.

entries.farsi: اعتماد زن بر آن كاو هيچ بار / اين زمان با خانه نامد او ز كار
entries.english: The wife’s confidence was on the fact that he had never come home from his work at this time.

entries.farsi: آن قياسش راست نامد از قضا / گر چه ستار است هم بدهد سزا
entries.english: By destiny, her reasoning did not come right: though He is the Coverer, still He will impose the penalty.

entries.farsi: چون كه بد كردى بترس ايمن مباش / ‏ز انكه تخم است و بروياند خداش
entries.english: When you have done evil, be afraid, do not be secure, since it is seed, and God will cause it to grow.

entries.farsi: چند گاهى او بپوشاند كه تا / آيدت ز آن بد پشيمان و حيا
entries.english: For awhile He covers it up, to the end that sorrow and shame for that evil may come to you.

entries.farsi: عهد عمر آن امير مومنان / ‏داد دزدى را به جالد و عوان
entries.english: In the time of Umar, that Prince of the Faithful gave a thief over to the executioner and officer of police.

entries.farsi: بانگ زد آن دزد كاى مير ديار / اولين بار است جرمم زينهار
entries.english: The thief cried out, saying, ‘O Prince of the land, this is my first offence. Mercy!’

entries.farsi: گفت عمر حاش هلل كه خدا / بار اول قهر بارد در جزا
entries.english: ‘God protect,’ said ‘Umar, ‘that God should inflict severe punishment the first time.

entries.farsi: بارها پوشد پى اظهار فضل / ‏باز گيرد از پى اظهار عدل
entries.english: He covers up many times in order to manifest His grace; then again, He chastises in order to manifest His justice,

entries.farsi: بارها زن نيز اين بد كرده بود / سهل بگذشت آن و سهلش مى‏نمود
entries.english: To the end that both these attributes may be displayed, and the former be hope-inspiring and the latter deterrent’

entries.farsi: تا كه اين هر دو صفت ظاهر شود / آن مبشر گردد اين منذر شود
entries.english: The woman, too, had committed this wickedness many times: it passed lightly and seemed light to her.

entries.farsi: آن نمى‏دانست عقل پاى سست / ‏كه سبو دايم ز جو نايد درست
entries.english: The feeble intelligence was unaware that the pitcher does not for ever come whole from the brook.

entries.farsi: آن چنانش تنگ آورد آن قضا / كه منافق را كند مرگ فجا
entries.english: That destiny brought her to such straits as sudden death does the hypocrite,

entries.farsi: نه طريق و نه رفيق و نه امان / ‏دست كرده آن فرشته سوى جان
entries.english: Neither way nor comrade nor quarter, the angel has put out his hand to the soul.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كاين زن در آن حجره‏ى جفا / خشك شد او و حريفش ز ابتال
entries.english: Even as this woman in that chamber of iniquity was paralysed, she and her companion, by the tribulation.

entries.farsi: گفت صوفى با دل خود كاى دو گبر / از شما كينه كشم ليكن به صبر
entries.english: The Sufi said to himself, ‘O you two miscreants, I will take vengeance on you, but with patience.

entries.farsi: ليك نادانسته آرم اين نفس / ‏تا كه هر گوشى ننوشد اين جرس
entries.english: But at this moment I will feign ignorance, that every ear may not hear this bell.’

entries.farsi: از شما پنهان كشد كينه محق / ‏اندك اندك همچو بيمارى دق
entries.english: He who manifests the right takes vengeance on you secretly, little by little, like the malady of phthisis.

entries.farsi: مرد دق باشد چو يخ هر لحظه كم / ‏ليك پندارد به هر دم بهترم
entries.english: The man suffering from consumption dwindles incessantly like ice, but at every moment he thinks he is better.

entries.farsi: همچو كفتارى كه مى‏گيرند و او / غره‏ى آن گفت كاين كفتار كو
entries.english: Like the hyena which they are catching, and which is duped by their saying, ‘Where is this hyena?’

entries.farsi: هيچ پنهان خانه آن زن را نبود / سمج و دهليز و ره بالا نبود
entries.english: That woman had no secret room; she had no subterranean cellar or passage, no way to the top,

entries.farsi: نه تنورى كه در آن پنهان شود / نه جوالى كه حجاب آن شود
entries.english: No oven where he might hide, nor any sack that might be a screen for him.

entries.farsi: همچو عرصه‏ى پهن روز رستخيز / نه گو و نه پشته نه جاى گريز
entries.english: It was like the broad plain of Resurrection Day— no hollow or hillock or place of refuge.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان وصف اين جاى حرج / ‏بهر محشر ال ترى فيها عوج
entries.english: God has described this distressful place, for the scene of the Congregation; you will not see therein any unevenness.

sections.title_fa: ٌمعشوق را زير چادر پنهان كردن جهت تلبيس و بهانه گفتن زن كه إِنَّ كَيْدَكُنَّ عَ ظِيم
sections.title_en: How the wife, for the sake of imposition, hid the beloved one under her chador and offered a false excuse, “for truly, great is your cunning.”

entries.farsi: چادر خود را بر او افكند زود / مرد را زن ساخت و در را بر گشود
entries.english: She quickly threw her chadar upon him: she made the man a woman and opened the door.

entries.farsi: زير چادر مرد رسوا و عيان / ‏سخت پيدا چون شتر بر نردبان
entries.english: Beneath the chadar the man was exposed to view and clearly seen— very conspicuous, like a camel on a staircase.

entries.farsi: گفت خاتونى است از اعيان شهر / مر و را از مال و اقبال است بهر
entries.english: She said, ‘it is a lady, one of the notables of the town: she has her share of wealth and fortune.

entries.farsi: در ببستم تا كسى بيگانه‏اى / ‏در نيايد زود نادانانه‏اى
entries.english: I bolted the door, lest any stranger should come in suddenly unawares.’

entries.farsi: گفت صوفى چيستش هين خدمتى / ‏تا بر آرم بى‏سپاس و منتى
entries.english: The Sufi said, ‘Oh, what service is there for her, that I may perform it without any thanks or favour?”

entries.farsi: گفت ميلش خويشى و پيوستگى است / ‏نيك خاتونى است حق داند كه كى است
entries.english: She said, ‘her desire is kinship and alliance: she is an excellent lady, God knows who she is.

entries.farsi: باز گفت ار آرد باشد يا سبوس / ‏مى‏كنم او را به جان و دل عروس
entries.english: She wished to see our daughter privately; as it happens, the girl is at school;

entries.farsi: خواست دختر را ببيند زير دست / ‏اتفاقا دختر اندر مكتب است
entries.english: Then she said, whether she be flour or bran, with soul and heart I will make her bride.

entries.farsi: يك پسر دارد كه اندر شهر نيست / ‏خوب و زيرك چابك و مكسب كنى است
entries.english: She has a son, who is not in the town: he is handsome and clever, an active lad and one that earns a living.’

entries.farsi: گفت صوفى ما فقير و زار و كم / ‏قوم خاتون مال‏دار و محتشم
entries.english: The Sufi said, ‘We are poor and wretched and inferior; this lady’s family are rich and respected.

entries.farsi: كى بود اين كفو ايشان در زواج / ‏يك در از چوب و درى ديگر ز عاج
entries.english: How should this be an equal match for them in marriage? —one folding door of wood and another of ivory!

entries.farsi: كفو بايد هر دو جفت اندر نكاح / ‏ور نه تنگ آيد نماند ارتياح
entries.english: In wedlock both the partners must be equal, otherwise it will pinch, and happiness will not endure.’

sections.title_fa: ‏گفتن زن كه او در بند جهاز نيست مراد او ستر و صالح است و جواب گفتن صوفى اين را سر پوشيده
sections.title_en: How the wife said that she was not bent upon household goods and that what she wanted was modesty and virtue; and how the Sufi answered her cryptically.

entries.farsi: ما ز مال و زر ملول و تخمه‏ايم / ‏ما به حرص و جمع نه چون عامه‏ايم
entries.english: She said, ‘I gave such an excuse, but she said, No, I am not one who seeks means.

entries.farsi: گفت گفتم من چنين عذرى و او / گفت نه من نيستم اسباب جو
entries.english: We are sick and surfeited with possessions and gold; we are not like the common folk in regard to coveting and amassing.

entries.farsi: قصد ما ستر است و پاكى و صالح / ‏در دو عالم خود بدان باشد فالح
entries.english: Our quest is modesty and purity and virtue: truly, welfare in both worlds depends on that.’

entries.farsi: باز صوفى عذر درويشى بگفت / ‏و آن مكرر كرد تا نبود نهفت
entries.english: The Sufi once more made the excuse of poverty and repeated it, so that it should not be hidden.

entries.farsi: اعتقاد اوست راسختر ز كوه / ‏كه ز صد فقرش نمى‏آيد شكوه
entries.english: The wife replied, ‘I too have repeated it and have explained our lack of household goods;

entries.farsi: او همى‏گويد مرادم عفت است / ‏از شما مقصود صدق و همت است
entries.english: Her resolution is firmer than a mountain, for she is not dismayed by a hundred poverties.

entries.farsi: گفت زن من هم مكرر كرده‏ام / ‏بى‏جهازى را مقرر كرده‏ام
entries.english: She keeps saying, what I want is chastity: the thing sought from you is sincerity and high-mindedness.’

entries.farsi: گفت صوفى خود جهاز و مال ما / ديد و مى‏بيند هويدا و خفا
entries.english: The Sufi said, ‘In sooth she has seen and is seeing our household goods and possessions, the overt and the covert—

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى تنگى مقام يك تنى / ‏كه در او پنهان نماند سوزنى
entries.english: A narrow house, a dwelling-place for a single person, where a needle would not remain hid.

entries.farsi: باز ستر و پاكى و زهد و صالح / ‏او ز ما به داند اندر انتصاح
entries.english: Moreover, she in innocence knows better than we modesty and purity and renunciation and virtue.

entries.farsi: به ز ما مى‏داند او احوال ستر / وز پس و پيش و سر و دنبال ستر
entries.english: She knows better than we the aspects of modesty, and the rear and front and head and tail of modesty.

entries.farsi: ظاهرا او بى‏جهاز و خادم است / ‏وز صالح و ستر او خود عالم است
entries.english: Evidently she is without household goods and servant, and she herself is well-acquainted with virtue and modesty.

entries.farsi: شرح مستورى ز بابا شرط نيست / ‏چون بر او پيدا چو روز روشنى است
entries.english: It is not required of a father to dilate on modesty, when in her it is manifest as a bright day.’

entries.farsi: اين حكايت را بدان گفتم كه تا / الف كم بافى چو رسوا شد خطا
entries.english: I have told this story with the intent that you may not weave idle talk when the offence is glaring.

entries.farsi: مر ترا اى هم به دعوى مستزاد / اين بده‏ستت اجتهاد و اعتقاد
entries.english: O you who are likewise excessive in your pretension, to you there has been this exertion and belief.

entries.farsi: كه ز هر ناشسته رويى كپ زنى / ‏شرم دارى و ز خداى خويش نى
entries.english: You have been unfaithful, like the Sufi’s wife: you have opened in fraud the snare of cunning,

entries.farsi: چون زن صوفى تو خاين بوده‏اى / ‏دام مكر اندر دغا بگشوده‏اى
entries.english: For you are ashamed before every dirty braggart, and not before your God.

sections.title_fa: غرض از سميع و بصير گفتن خدا را
sections.title_en: The purpose for which God is called Samí (Hearing) and Basír (Seeing)

entries.farsi: از پى آن گفت حق خود را بصير / كه بود ديد وى‏ات هر دم نذير
entries.english: God has called Himself Basír, in order that His seeing you may at every moment be a deterrent.

entries.farsi: از پى آن گفت حق خود را سميع / ‏تا ببندى لب ز گفتار شنيع
entries.english: God has called Himself Sami‘, in order that you may close your lips from foul speech.

entries.farsi: از پى آن گفت حق خود را عليم / ‏تا نينديشى فسادى تو ز بيم
entries.english: God has called Himself Alím, in order that you may fear to meditate a wicked deed.

entries.farsi: نيست اينها بر خدا اسم علم / ‏كه سيه كافور دارد نام هم
entries.english: These are not proper names applicable to God: for even a Negro may have the name Kafur (Camphor).

entries.farsi: اسم مشتق است و اوصاف قديم / ‏نه مثال علت اولى سقيم
entries.english: The Names are derivative and denote Eternal Attributes: they are not unsound like the First Cause.

entries.farsi: ور نه تسخر باشد و طنز و دها / كر را سامع ضريران را ضيا
entries.english: Otherwise, it would be ridicule and mockery and deception, a deaf person Samí and blind men Ziya;

entries.farsi: طفلك نوزاده را حاجى لقب / ‏يا لقب غازى نهى بهر نسب
entries.english: Or Hayí should be the proper name of an impudent fellow, or Sabíh the name of a hideous moor.

entries.farsi: يا علم باشد حيى نام وقيح / ‏يا سياه زشت را نام صبيح
entries.english: You may confer the title of Haji (Pilgrim) or Ghazi (Holy Warrior) on a newborn child for the purpose of lineage;

entries.farsi: گر بگويند اين لقبها در مديح / ‏تا ندارد آن صفت نبود صحيح
entries.english: If these titles are used in praise, they are not correct unless he possess that quality.

entries.farsi: تسخر و طنزى بود آن يا جنون / ‏پاك حق عما يقول الظالمون
entries.english: It would be a ridicule and mockery, or madness: God is clear of what the unrighteous say.

entries.farsi: من همى‏دانستمت پيش از وصال / ‏كه نكو رويى و ليكن بد خصال
entries.english: I knew, before meeting, that you are good-looking but evil-natured;

entries.farsi: من همى‏دانستمت پيش از لقا / كز ستيزه راسخى اندر شقا
entries.english: I knew, before coming face to face, that by reason of rebelliousness you are set fast in damnation.

entries.farsi: چون كه چشمم سرخ باشد در عمش / ‏دانمش ز آن درد گر كم بينمش
entries.english: When my eye is red with disease, I know it is from the disease, if I do not see it.

entries.farsi: تو مرا چون بره ديدى بى‏شبان / ‏تو گمان بردى ندارم پاسبان
entries.english: You deem me as a lamb without the shepherd; you thought that I have none keeping watch.

entries.farsi: عاشقان از درد ز آن ناليده‏اند / كه نظر ناجايگه ماليده‏اند
entries.english: The cause why lovers have moaned in grief is that they have rubbed their eyes inappropriately.

entries.farsi: بى‏شبان دانسته‏اند آن ظبى را / رايگان دانسته‏اند آن سبى را
entries.english: They have regarded that Gazelle as being without a shepherd; they have regarded that Captive as cost-free,

entries.farsi: تا ز غمزه تير آمد بر جگر / كه منم حارس گزافه كم نگر
entries.english: Till an arrow from the glance comes upon the heart, to say, ‘I am the Keeper: do not look wantonly.

entries.farsi: كى كم از بره كم از بزغاله‏ام / ‏كه نباشد حارس از دنباله‏ام
entries.english: How am I meaner than a lamb, meaner than a kid, that there should not be a keeper behind me?

entries.farsi: حارسى دارم كه ملكش مى‏سزد / داند او بادى كه آن بر من وزد
entries.english: I have a Keeper whom it beseems to hold dominion: He knows the wind that blows upon me.

entries.farsi: سرد بود آن باد يا گرم آن عليم / ‏نيست غافل نيست غايب اى سقيم
entries.english: Whether that wind was cold or hot, that Knowing One is not unaware, is not absent, O infirm man.

entries.farsi: نفس شهوانى ز حق كر است و كور / من به دل كوريت مى‏ديدم ز دور
entries.english: The appetitive soul is deaf and blind to God: I with my heart was seeing your blindness from afar.

entries.farsi: هشت سالت ز آن نپرسيدم به هيچ / ‏كه پرت ديدم ز جهل پيچ پيچ
entries.english: For eight years I did not inquire after you at all, because I saw you full of ignorance, fold on fold.

entries.farsi: خود چه پرسم آن كه او باشد به تون / ‏كه تو چونى چون بود او سر نگون
entries.english: Why, indeed, should I inquire after one who is in the bath-stove, and say ‘How are you?’ when he is headlong?

sections.title_fa: ‏مثال دنيا چون گلخن و تقوى چون حمام
sections.title_en: Comparison of this world to a bath-stove and of piety to the bath

entries.farsi: شهوت دنيا مثال گلخن است / ‏كه از او حمام تقوى روشن است
entries.english: The lust of this world is like the bath-stove by which the bath, piety, is resplendent;

entries.farsi: ليك قسم متقى زين تون صفاست / ‏ز انكه در گرمابه است و در نقاست
entries.english: But the pious man’s portion from this stove is purity, because he is in the hot-bath and in cleanliness.

entries.farsi: اغنيا ماننده‏ى سرگين كشان / ‏بهر آتش كردن گرمابه بان
entries.english: The rich resemble those who carry dung for the bath-keeper’s fire-making.

entries.farsi: اندر ايشان حرص بنهاده خدا / تا بود گرمابه گرم و بانوا
entries.english: God has implanted cupidity in them, in order that the bath may be hot and well-provided.

entries.farsi: ترك اين تون گوى و در گرمابه ران / ‏ترك تون را عين آن گرمابه دان
entries.english: Abandon this stove and advance into the hot-bath: know that abandonment of the stove is the very essence of that bath.

entries.farsi: هر كه در تون است او چون خادم است / ‏مر و را كه صابر است و حازم است
entries.english: Anyone who is in the stove is as a servant to him that is self-denying and on his guard.

entries.farsi: هر كه در حمام شد سيماى او / هست پيدا بر رخ زيباى او
entries.english: Whoever has entered the bath, his sign is visible upon his comely face.

entries.farsi: تونيان را نيز سيما آشكار / از لباس و از دخان و از غبار
entries.english: The signs of the stokers are conspicuous too— in their dress and in the smoke and dust.

entries.farsi: ور نبينى روش بويش را بگير / بو عصا آمد براى هر ضرير
entries.english: And if you see not his face, smell him; smell is a staff for every one that is blind;

entries.farsi: ور ندارى بو در آرش در سخن / ‏از حديث نو بدان راز كهن
entries.english: And if you have not smell, induce him to speak, and from the new talk learn the old secret.

entries.farsi: پس بگويد تو نيى صاحب ذهب / ‏بيست سله چرك بردم تا به شب
entries.english: Then a gold-possessing stoker will say, “I have brought in twenty baskets of filth, till nightfall.”

entries.farsi: حرص تو چون آتش است اندر جهان / ‏باز كرده هر زبانه صد دهان
entries.english: Your cupidity is like fire in the world: every tongue has opened a hundred mouths.

entries.farsi: پيش عقل اين زر چو سرگين ناخوش است / گر چه چون سرگين فروغ آتش است
entries.english: In the sight of Reason, this gold is foul as dung, although, like dung, it is the blazing of the fire.

entries.farsi: آفتاب آن سنگ را هم كرد زر / تا به تون حرص افتد صد شرر
entries.english: The sun, which emulates the fire, makes the moist filth fit for the fire.

entries.farsi: آفتابى كه دم از آتش زند / چرك تر را اليق آتش كند
entries.english: The sun also made the stone gold, in order that a hundred sparks might fall into the stove of cupidity.

entries.farsi: آن كه گويد مال گرد آورده‏ام / ‏چيست يعنى چرك چندين برده‏ام
entries.english: He who says, “I have collected riches”—what is it? It means, “I have brought in all this filth.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن گر چه كه رسوايى فزاست / ‏در ميان تونيان زين فخرهاست
entries.english: Although this saying is exceedingly disgraceful, there are boasts on this account amongst the stokers.

entries.farsi: كه تو شش سله كشيدى تا به شب / ‏من كشيدم بيست سله بى‏كرب
entries.english: “You have carried six baskets before nightfall; I have carried twenty baskets without trouble.”

entries.farsi: آن كه در تون زاد و پاكى را نديد / بوى مشك آرد بر او رنجى پديد
entries.english: He that was born in the stove and never saw purity, the smell of musk produces a painful effect upon him.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن دباغ كه در بازار عطاران از بوى عطر و مشك بى‏هوش و رنجور شد
sections.title_en: Story of the tanner who fainted and sickened on smelling attar and musk in the bazaar of the perfumers.

entries.farsi: آن يكى افتاد بى‏هوش و خميد / چون كه در بازار عطاران رسيد
entries.english: A certain man fell senseless and curled up as soon as he came into the bazaar of the perfumers.

entries.farsi: بوى عطرش زد ز عطاران راد / تا بگرديدش سر و بر جا فتاد
entries.english: The scent of the perfume from the goodly perfumers smote him, so that his head reeled and he fell on the spot.

entries.farsi: جمع آمد خلق بر وى آن زمان / ‏جملگان ال حول گو درمان كنان
entries.english: He fell unconscious, like a carcase, at noontide in the middle of the thoroughfare.

entries.farsi: همچو مردار اوفتاد او بى‏خبر / نيم روز اندر ميان رهگذر
entries.english: Thereupon the people gathered over him, all crying La hawl and applying remedies.

entries.farsi: آن يكى كف بر دل او مى‏براند / و ز گالب آن ديگرى بر وى فشاند
entries.english: One was putting his hand on his heart, while another sprinkled rose-water upon him;

entries.farsi: او نمى‏دانست كاندر مرتعه / ‏از گالب آمد و را آن واقعه
entries.english: He did not know that from rose-water in the meadow that calamity had overtaken him.

entries.farsi: آن يكى دستش همى‏ماليد و سر / و آن دگر كه گل همى‏آورد تر
entries.english: One was massaging his hands and head, and another was bringing moist clay mixed with straw;

entries.farsi: آن بخور عود و شكر زد بهم / ‏و آن دگر از پوشش‏اش مى‏كرد كم
entries.english: One compounded incense of aloes-wood and sugar, while another was divesting him of part of his clothes;

entries.farsi: و آن دگر نبضش كه تا چون مى‏جهد / و آن دگر بوى از دهانش مى‏ستد
entries.english: And another felt his pulse, to see how it was beating; and another was smelling his mouth,

entries.farsi: تا كه مى‏خورده‏ست، يا بنگ و حشيش / ‏خلق در ماندند اندر بى‏هشيش
entries.english: To see whether he had drunk wine, eaten hashish: the people remained in despair at his insensibility

entries.farsi: پس خبر بردند خويشان را شتاب / ‏كه فالن افتاده است آن جا خراب
entries.english: So they speedily brought the news to his kinsfolk— “Such and such a person is lying there in a state of collapse;

entries.farsi: كس نمى‏داند كه چون مصروع گشت / ‏يا چه شد كاو را فتاد از بام طشت
entries.english: No one knows how he was stricken with catalepsy, or what it was that led to this public exposure.”

entries.farsi: اندكى سرگين سگ در آستين / ‏خلق را بشكافت و آمد با حنين
entries.english: That stout tanner had a brother, cunning and sagacious: he came at once in hot haste.

entries.farsi: يك برادر داشت آن دباغ زفت / ‏گربز و دانا بيامد زود تفت
entries.english: A small quantity of dog shit in his sleeve, he cut the crowd and approached with cries of grief.

entries.farsi: گفت من رنجش همى‏دانم ز چيست / ‏چون سبب دانى دوا كردن جلى است
entries.english: “I know,” said he, “whence his illness arises: when you know the cause, the curing is manifest.

entries.farsi: چون سبب معلوم نبود مشكل است / ‏داروى رنج و در آن صد محمل است
entries.english: When the cause is unknown, the remedy for the illness is difficult, and in that there are a hundred grounds to which it may be referred;

entries.farsi: چون بدانستى سبب را سهل شد / دانش اسباب دفع جهل شد
entries.english: When you have ascertained the cause, it becomes easy: knowledge of causes is the means of expelling ignorance.”

entries.farsi: گفت با خود هستش اندر مغز و رگ / ‏توى بر تو بوى آن سرگين سگ
entries.english: He said to himself, “The smell of that dog’s shit is multiplied in his brain and veins.

entries.farsi: تا ميان اندر حدث او تا به شب / ‏غرق دباغى است او روزى طلب
entries.english: Up to the waist in filth, he is absorbed in the tanner’s craft till nightfall, seeking his livelihood.

entries.farsi: پس چنين گفته است جالينوس مه / ‏آن چه عادت داشت بيمار آنش ده
entries.english: Thus then has the great Galen said: ‘Give the patient that to which he was habituated before his illness;

entries.farsi: كز خالف عادت است آن رنج او / پس دواى رنجش از معتاد جو
entries.english: For his illness arises from doing the contrary to his usual habit: therefore seek the remedy for his illness in that which is habitual to him.’

entries.farsi: چون جعل گشته است از سرگين كشى / ‏از گالب آيد جعل را بى‏هشى
entries.english: He, from carrying dung, has become like the dung-beetle: the dung-beetle is made insensible by rose-water.

entries.farsi: هم از آن سرگين سگ داروى اوست / ‏كه بد آن او را همى معتاد و خوست
entries.english: The remedy for him consists in that same dog’s dung to which he is habituated and accustomed.”

entries.farsi: الخبيثات الخبيثين را بخوان / ‏رو و پشت اين سخن را باز دان
entries.english: Recite, the wicked women for the wicked men: recognise the front and the back of this saying.

entries.farsi: مر خبيثان را نسازد طيبات / ‏در خور و اليق نباشد اى ثقات
entries.english: The sincere mentors prepare medicine for him with ambergris or rose-water to open the door;

entries.farsi: ناصحان او را به عنبر يا گالب / ‏مى‏دوا سازند بهر فتح باب
entries.english: Sweet words will not do for the wicked: it is not fitting and suitable, O you trusty ones!

entries.farsi: چون ز عطر وحى كژ گشتند و گم / ‏بد فغانشان كه تَطَ يَّرْنا بكم
entries.english: When from the perfume of the Revelation they became crooked and lost, their lament was, and “We augur evil from you.

entries.farsi: رنج و بيمارى است ما را اين مقال / ‏نيست نيكو وعظتان ما را به فال
entries.english: This discourse is illness and sickness to us: your exhortation is not of good omen to us.

entries.farsi: گر بياغازيد نصحى آشكار / ما كنيم آن دم شما را سنگسار
entries.english: If you once begin to admonish overtly, at that instant we will stone you.

entries.farsi: ما به لغو و لهو فربه گشته‏ايم / ‏در نصيحت خويش را نسرشته‏ايم
entries.english: We have waxed fat on frivolity and diversion: we have not steeped ourselves in admonition.

entries.farsi: هست قوت ما دروغ و الف و الغ / ‏شورش معده است ما را زين بالغ
entries.english: Our food is falsehood and idle boasts and jests: our stomachs are turned by your delivering this message.

entries.farsi: رنج را صد تو و افزون مى‏كنيد / عقل را دارو به افيون مى‏كنيد
entries.english: You are making the illness hundredfold and more: you are drugging the intelligence with opium.”

sections.title_fa: ‏معالجه كردن برادر دباغ دباغ را به خفيه به بوى سرگين
sections.title_en: How the tanner’s brother sought to cure him secretly with the smell of shit.

entries.farsi: خلق را مى‏راند از وى آن جوان / ‏تا عالجش را نبينند آن كسان
entries.english: The youth kept driving the people away from him, in order that those persons might not see his treatment.

entries.farsi: سر به گوشش برد همچون رازگو / پس نهاد آن چيز بر بينى او
entries.english: He brought his head to his ear, like one telling a secret; then he put the thing to his nose;

entries.farsi: ساعتى شد مرد جنبيدن گرفت / خلق گفتند اين فسونى بد شگفت
entries.english: For he had rubbed the dog’s shit on his palm: he had deemed it the remedy for the polluted brain.

entries.farsi: كاو به كف سرگين سگ ساييده بود / داروى مغز پليد آن ديده بود
entries.english: A short while passed: the man began to move: the people said, “This was a wonderful charm;

entries.farsi: كاين بخواند افسون به گوش او دميد / مرده بود افسون به فريادش رسيد
entries.english: For this recited charms and breathed into his ear: he was dead: the charms came to succour him.”

entries.farsi: جنبش اهل فساد آن سو بود / كه ز ناز و غمزه و ابرو بود
entries.english: The movement of iniquitous folk is to the quarter in which there is fornication and ogling glances and eyebrows.

entries.farsi: هر كه را مشك نصيحت سود نيست / ‏الجرم با بوى بد خو كردنى است
entries.english: Anyone to whom the musk, admonition, is of no use must necessarily make himself familiar with the bad smell.

entries.farsi: مشركان را ز آن نجس خوانده‏ست حق / ‏كاندرون پشك زادند از سبق
entries.english: God has called the polytheists najas (uncleanness) for the reason that they were born in dung from of old.

entries.farsi: كرم كاو زاده‏ست در سرگين ابد / مى‏نگرداند به عنبر خوى خود
entries.english: The worm that has been born in dung will nevermore change its evil nature by means of ambergris.

entries.farsi: چون نزد بر وى نثار رش نور / او همه جسم است بى‏دل چون قشور
entries.english: Since the largesse of sprinkled light did not strike upon him, he is wholly body, without heart, like husks.

entries.farsi: ور ز رش نور حق قسميش داد / همچو رسم مصر سرگين مرغ زاد
entries.english: And if God gave him a portion of the sprinkled light, the dung hatched a bird, as is the custom in Egypt—

entries.farsi: ليك نه مرغ خسيس خانگى / ‏بلكه مرغ دانش و فرزانگى
entries.english: But not the cheap domestic fowl; nay, but the bird of knowledge and wisdom.

entries.farsi: تو بدان مانى كز آن نورى تهى / ‏ز انكه بينى بر پليدى مى‏نهى
entries.english: “You resemble that for you are devoid of that light, inasmuch as you are putting your nose to filth.

entries.farsi: از فراقت زرد شد رخسار و رو / برگ زردى ميوه‏ى ناپخته تو
entries.english: Because of being parted your cheeks and face have become yellow you are yellow leaves and raw fruit,

entries.farsi: ديگ ز آتش شد سياه و دودفام / ‏گوشت از سختى چنين مانده است خام
entries.english: The pot was blackened by the fire and became like smoke in colour, the meat, on account of hardness, has remained so raw as this!

entries.farsi: غوره‏ى تو سنگ بسته كز سقام / ‏غوره‏ها اكنون مويزند و تو خام
entries.english: Eight years have I boiled, you in separation: your rawness and hypocrisy have not become less by a single mote.

entries.farsi: هشت سالت جوش دادم در فراق / ‏كم نشد يك ذره خاميت و نفاق
entries.english: Your young grape is hardened; for through sickness the young grapes are now raisins, while you are immature.”

sections.title_fa: عذر خواستن آن عاشق از گناه خويش به تلبيس و روى پوش و فهم كردن معشوق آن را نيز
sections.title_en: How the lover begged to be excused for his sin, with duplicity and dissimulation;
and how the beloved perceived that also.

entries.farsi: من همى‏دانستمت بى‏امتحان / ‏ليك كى باشد خبر همچون عيان
entries.english: The lover said, “I made the trial—do not take offence— that I might see whether you are a courtesan or a modest woman.

entries.farsi: گفت عاشق امتحان كردم مگير / تا ببينم تو حريفى يا ستير
entries.english: I knew without the trial, but how should hearing be the same as seeing?

entries.farsi: آفتابى نام تو مشهور و فاش / چه زيان است ار بكردم ابتالش
entries.english: You are the sun: your name is renowned and known to all: what harm is there if I have tested it?

entries.farsi: تو منى من خويشتن را امتحان / ‏مى‏كنم هر روز در سود و زيان
entries.english: You are I: every day I am making trial of myself in profit and loss.

entries.farsi: انبيا را امتحان كرده عدات / ‏تا شده ظاهر از ايشان معجزات
entries.english: The prophets were put to the trial by their enemies, with the result that miracles were displayed by them.

entries.farsi: امتحان چشم خود كردم به نور / اى كه چشم بد ز چشمان تو دور
entries.english: I made trial of my own eye with light, O you from whose eyes may the evil eye be far!

entries.farsi: اين جهان همچون خراب است و تو گنج / ‏گر تفحص كردم از گنجت مرنج
entries.english: This world is as the ruin and you the treasure: if I have made investigation concerning your treasure, be not aggrieved.

entries.farsi: ز آن چنين بى‏خردگى كردم گزاف / ‏تا زنم با دشمنان هر بار الف
entries.english: I recklessly committed such an indiscretion, that I may always boast to enemies;

entries.farsi: تا زبانم چون ترا نامى نهد / چشم از اين ديده گواهيها دهد
entries.english: So that, when my tongue bestows a name on you, my eye may give testimonies of this which I have seen.

entries.farsi: جز به دست خود مبرم پا و سر / كه از اين دستم نه از دست دگر
entries.english: If I have sought to rob you of your honour I come, O Moon, with sword and winding-sheet.

entries.farsi: گر شدم در راه حرمت راه زن / ‏آمدم اى مه به شمشير و كفن
entries.english: Do not cut off my feet arid head save with your own hand, for I belong to this hand, not to another hand.

entries.farsi: از جدايى باز مى‏رانى سخن / ‏هر چه خواهى كن و ليكن اين مكن
entries.english: You are talking again of separation: do whatsoever you will, but do not this!”

entries.farsi: در سخن آباد اين دم راه شد / گفت امكان نيست چون بى‏گاه شد
entries.english: The way is now made into the realm of Discourse; it is impossible to speak, since there is no time.

entries.farsi: پوستها گفتيم و مغز آمد دفين / ‏گر بمانيم اين نماند همچنين
entries.english: We have told the husks, but the kernel is buried; if we remain, this will not remain as it is now.

sections.title_fa: ‏رد كردن معشوقه عذر عاشق را و تلبيس او را در روى او ماليدن
sections.title_en: How the beloved rejected the excuses of the lover and rubbed his duplicity into him

entries.farsi: در جوابش بر گشاد آن يار لب / ‏كز سوى ما روز و سوى تست شب
entries.english: The loved one opened her lips to answer him, saying, “On my side it is day, and on your side it is night.

entries.farsi: حيله‏هاى تيره اندر داورى / ‏پيش بينايان چرا مى‏آورى
entries.english: Why in contention do you bring forward dark evasions before those who see?

entries.farsi: هر چه در دل دارى از مكر و رموز / پيش ما رسواست و پيدا همچو روز
entries.english: To us, all the deceit and dissimulations that you have in your heart are manifest and clear as day.

entries.farsi: گر بپوشيمش ز بنده پرورى / ‏تو چرا بى‏رويى از حد مى‏برى
entries.english: If we, in kindness to our servant, cover it up, why do you carry shamelessness beyond the limit?

entries.farsi: از پدر آموز كآدم در گناه / ‏خوش فرود آمد به سوى پايگاه
entries.english: Learn from your Father; for in sin Adam came down willingly to the vestibule

entries.farsi: بر سر خاكستر انده نشست / ‏از بهانه شاخ تا شاخى نجست
entries.english: When he beheld that Knower of secrets, he stood up on his feet to ask forgiveness.

entries.farsi: چون بديد آن عالم الاسرار را / بر دو پا استاد استغفار را
entries.english: He seated himself on the ashes of contrition: he did not jump from one branch of idle pleading to another

entries.farsi: ربنا انا ظلمنا گفت و بس / ‏چون كه جانداران بديد از پيش و پس
entries.english: He said only, ‘O Lord, truly we have done wrong,’ when he saw the life-guards in front and behind.

entries.farsi: ديد جانداران پنهان همچو جان / ‏دور باش هر يكى تا آسمان
entries.english: He saw the life-guards who are invisible, as the spirit is— each one’s mace to the sky—

entries.farsi: جز مقام راستى يك دم مه ايست / ‏هيچ الال مرد را چون چشم نيست
entries.english: Saying ‘Hola! Be the ant before Solomon, lest this mace cut you in two.

entries.farsi: كه هال پيش سليمان مور باش / ‏تا بنشكافد ترا اين دور باش
entries.english: Do not for one moment stand but in the place of truth: a man has no guardian like the eye.

entries.farsi: آدما تو نيستى كور از نظر / ليك إذا جاء القضاء عمى البصر
entries.english: If the blind man be purified by admonition, he continually becomes polluted again.

entries.farsi: كور اگر از پند پالوده شود / هر دمى او باز آلوده شود
entries.english: O Adam, you are not blind of vision, but when the Divine destiny comes, the sight becomes blind.’”

entries.farsi: عمرها بايد به نادر گاه گاه / ‏تا كه بينا از قضا افتد به چاه
entries.english: Lifetimes are needed—rarely and occasionally— for the seeing man to fall by destiny into the pit.

entries.farsi: كور را خود اين قضا همراه اوست / ‏كه مر او را اوفتادن طبع و خوست
entries.english: As regards the blind man, this destiny in sooth is his companion on the way; for it is his nature and disposition to fall.

entries.farsi: در حدث افتد نداند بوى چيست / ‏از من است اين بوى يا ز آلودگى است
entries.english: He falls into the filth and does not know what the smell is; “Is this smell from me or from being polluted?”

entries.farsi: ور كسى بر وى كند مشكى نثار / هم ز خود داند نه از احسان يار
entries.english: And likewise, if any one sprinkles some musk over him, he thinks it from himself and not from the kindness of his friend.

entries.farsi: خاصه چشم دل كه آن هفتاد توست / ‏وين دو چشم حس خوشه چين اوست
entries.english: Therefore to you, O man of vision, two clear eyes are a hundred mothers and a hundred fathers;

entries.farsi: پس دو چشم روشن اى صاحب نظر / مر ترا صد مادر است و صد پدر
entries.english: Especially the eye of the heart, which is seventy-fold and of which these two sensible eyes are the gleaners

entries.farsi: اى دريغا ره زنان بنشسته‏اند / صد گره زير زبانم بسته‏اند
entries.english: Oh, alas, the highwaymen are seated: they have tied a hundred knots beneath my tongue.

entries.farsi: پاى بسته چون رود خوش راهوار / بس گران بندى است اين معذور دار
entries.english: How should the smooth-paced horse move well, when his leg is tied? This is a very heavy chain: hold me excused!

entries.farsi: اين سخن اشكسته مى‏آيد دال / كاين سخن در است غيرت آسيا
entries.english: These words are coming brokenly, O heart; for these words are pearls, and jealousy is the mill;

entries.farsi: در اگر چه خرد و اشكسته شود / توتياى ديده‏ى خسته شود
entries.english: Though the pearls are broken into small fragments, they become polishing powder for the sore eye.

entries.farsi: اى در از اشكست خود بر سر مزن / ‏كز شكستن روشنى خواهى شدن
entries.english: O pearl, do not beat your head at your being broken, for through being broken you will become light.

entries.farsi: همچنين اشكسته بسته گفتنى است / ‏حق كند آخر درستش كاو غنى است
entries.english: It has to be uttered thus brokenly and in bandages: God, who is Self-sufficient, will make it whole at last.

entries.farsi: گندم ار بشكست و از هم در سكست / ‏بر دكان آمد كه نك نان درست
entries.english: If wheat is broken and torn asunder, it appears in the shop, saying, “Look! A perfect loaf!”

entries.farsi: تو هم اى عاشق چو جرمت گشت فاش / ‏آب و روغن ترك كن اشكسته باش
entries.english: “You too, O lover, since your crime has become manifest, abandon water and oil and be broken.

entries.farsi: آن كه فرزندان خاص آدمند / نفحه‏ى انا ظلمنا مى‏دمند
entries.english: Those who are the elect children of Adam sigh forth, ‘truly we have done wrong.’

entries.farsi: حاجت خود عرضه كن حجت مگو / همچو ابليس لعين سخت رو
entries.english: Submit your petition; do not argue like the accursed hard- faced Iblis.

entries.farsi: سخت رويى گر و را شد عيب پوش / ‏در ستيز و سخت رويى رو بكوش
entries.english: If impudence concealed his fault, go; exert yourself in obstinacy and impudence!

entries.farsi: آن ابو جهل از پيمبر معجزى / ‏خواست همچون كينه‏ور تركى غزى
entries.english: Abu Jahl, like a vindictive Ghuzz Turcoman, demanded a miracle from the Prophet;

entries.farsi: ليك آن صديق حق معجز نخواست / ‏گفت اين رو خود نگويد جز كه راست
entries.english: But that Siddiq of God did not crave a miracle: he said, ‘Truly, this face speaks nothing but truth.’

entries.farsi: كى رسد همچون تويى را كز منى / امتحان همچو من يارى كنى
entries.english: How should it be like you, from egoism, to make trial of a beloved like me?”

sections.title_fa: گفتن آن جهود على را عليه السلام كه اگر اعتماد دارى بر حافظى حق از سر اين كوشك خود را در انداز و جواب گفتن امير المؤمنين او را
sections.title_en: How the Jew said to Alí, may God honour his person, “If you have confidence in God’s protection, cast yourself down from the top of this kiosk”; and how the Prince of the Faithful answered him.

entries.farsi: مرتضى را گفت روزى يك عنود / كاو ز تعظيم خدا آگه نبود
entries.english: One day a contumacious man, who was ignorant of the reverence due to God, said to Murtaza,

entries.farsi: بر سر بامى و قصرى بس بلند / حفظ حق را واقفى اى هوشمند
entries.english: On the top of an exceedingly high terrace or pavilion, “Are you conscious of God’s protection, O intelligent man?”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى او حفيظ است و غنى / ‏هستى ما را ز طفلى و منى
entries.english: “Yes,” he replied; “He is the Protector and the Self-sufficient for my existence from infancy and conception.”

entries.farsi: گفت خود را اندر افكن هين ز بام / ‏اعتمادى كن به حفظ حق تمام
entries.english: He said, “Come, cast yourself down from the roof, put an entire confidence in the protection of God,

entries.farsi: تا يقين گردد مرا ايقان تو / و اعتقاد خوب با برهان تو
entries.english: So that your sure faith and your goodly proven conviction may become evident to me.”

entries.farsi: پس اميرش گفت خامش كن برو / تا نگردد جانت زين جرات گرو
entries.english: Then the Prince said to him, “Be silent, go, lest for this boldness your soul be pawned.”

entries.farsi: كى رسد مر بنده را كه با خدا / آزمايش پيش آرد ز ابتال
entries.english: How is it right for a servant to venture on an experiment with God by making trial?

entries.farsi: بنده را كى زهره باشد كز فضول / ‏امتحان حق كند اى گيج گول
entries.english: How should a servant have the stomach vaingloriously to put Him to the test, O mad fool?

entries.farsi: آن خدا را مى‏رسد كاو امتحان / ‏پيش آرد هر دمى با بندگان
entries.english: To God belongs that, which brings forward a test for His servants at every moment,

entries.farsi: تا بما ما را نمايد آشكار / كه چه داريم از عقيده در سرار
entries.english: In order that He may show us plainly to ourselves what beliefs we hold in secret.

entries.farsi: هيچ آدم گفت حق را كه ترا / امتحان كردم در آن جرم و خطا
entries.english: Did Adam ever say to God, “I made trial of You in this sin and trespass,

entries.farsi: تا ببينم غايت حلمت شها / اه كه را باشد مجال اين كه را
entries.english: That I might see the utmost limit of Your clemency, O King?” Ah, who would be capable of this, who?

entries.farsi: آن كه او افراشت سقف آسمان / ‏تو چه دانى كردن او را امتحان
entries.english: Forasmuch as your understanding is confused, your excuse is worse than your crime.

entries.farsi: عقل تو از بس كه آمد خيره‏سر / هست عذرت از گناه تو بتر
entries.english: How can you make trial of Him who raised aloft the vault of heaven?

entries.farsi: اى ندانسته تو شر و خير را / امتحان خود را كن آن گه غير را
entries.english: O you that have not known good and evil, make trial of yourself, and then of others.

entries.farsi: امتحان خود چو كردى اى فالن / ‏فارغ آيى ز امتحان ديگران
entries.english: When you have made trial of yourself, O such-and-such, you will be unconcerned with making trial of others.

entries.farsi: چون بدانستى كه شكر دانه‏اى / ‏پس بدانى كاهل شكر خانه‏اى
entries.english: When you have come to know that you are a grain of sugar, then you will know that you belong to the sugar-house.

entries.farsi: اين بدان بى‏امتحان از علم شاه / ‏چون سرى نفرستدت در پايگاه
entries.english: Know, then, without making any trial, that you are sugar, God will not send you to the wrong place.

entries.farsi: پس بدان بى‏امتحانى كه اله / ‏شكرى نفرستدت ناجايگاه
entries.english: Without making trial, know this of the King’s knowledge: when you are a chief, He will not send you to the vestibule.

entries.farsi: هيچ عاقل افكند در ثمين / ‏در ميان مستراحى پر چمين
entries.english: Does any intelligent man let a precious pearl fall into the midst of a privy full of ordure?

entries.farsi: ز انكه گندم را حكيم آگهى / ‏هيچ نفرستد به انبار كهى
entries.english: Inasmuch as a sagacious and attentive man will nowise send wheat to a straw-barn,

entries.farsi: شيخ را كه پيشوا و رهبر است / ‏گر مريدى امتحان كرد او خر است
entries.english: If a novice has made trial of the Shaykh who is the leader and guide, he is an ass.

entries.farsi: امتحانش گر كنى در راه دين / ‏هم تو گردى ممتحن اى بى‏يقين
entries.english: If you make trial of him in the way of religion, you will be tried, O man without faith.

entries.farsi: جرات و جهلت شود عريان و فاش / ‏او برهنه كى شود ز آن افتتاش
entries.english: Your audacity and ignorance will become naked and exposed to view: how should he be made naked by that scrutiny?

entries.farsi: گر بيايد ذره سنجد كوه را / ‏بر درد ز آن كه ترازوش اى فتى
entries.english: If the mote come and weigh the mountain, its scales will be shattered by the mountain, O youth;

entries.farsi: كز قياس خود ترازو مى‏تند / مرد حق را در ترازو مى‏كند
entries.english: For he applies the scales of his own judgement and puts the man of God in the scales;

entries.farsi: چون نگنجد او به ميزان خرد / پس ترازوى خرد را بر درد
entries.english: Since he is not contained by the scales of intellect, consequently he shatters the scales of intellect.

entries.farsi: امتحان همچون تصرف دان در او / تو تصرف بر چنان شاهى مجو
entries.english: Know that to make trial is like exercising authority over him: do not seek to exercise authority over such a king.

entries.farsi: چه تصرف كرد خواهد نقشها / بر چنان نقاش بهر ابتال
entries.english: What authority should the pictures desire to exercise over such an Artist for the purpose of testing Him?

entries.farsi: امتحانى گر بدانست و بديد / نى كه هم نقاش آن بر وى كشيد
entries.english: If it has known and experienced any trial, is it not the case that the Artist brought that upon it?

entries.farsi: چه قدر باشد خود اين صورت كه بست / ‏پيش صورتها كه در علم وى است
entries.english: Indeed, this form that He fashioned—what is it worth in comparison with the forms which are in His knowledge?

entries.farsi: وسوسه‏ى اين امتحان چون آمدت / ‏بخت بد دان كامد و گردن زدت
entries.english: When the temptation to make this trial has come to you, know that ill fortune has come and smitten your neck.

entries.farsi: چون چنين وسواس ديدى زود زود / با خدا گرد و در آ اندر سجود
entries.english: When you feel such a temptation, at once, at once turn unto God and begin the prostration.

entries.farsi: سجده‏گه را تر كن از اشك روان / ‏كاى خدا تو وارهانم زين گمان
entries.english: Make the place of prostration wet with flowing tears and say, “O God, do You deliver me from this doubt!”

entries.farsi: آن زمان كت امتحان مطلوب شد / مسجد دين تو پر خروب شد
entries.english: At the time when it is your object to make trial, the mosque, namely, your religion, becomes filled with kharrúb.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى مسجد اقصى و خروب و عزم كردن داود عليه السلام پيش از سليمان عليه السلام بر بناى آن مسجد
sections.title_en: Story of the Farther Mosque and the carob and how, before Solomon, on whom be peace, David, on whom be peace, resolved on building that Mosque.

entries.farsi: چون در آمد عزم داودى به تنگ / ‏كه بسازد مسجد اقصى به سنگ
entries.english: When David’s resolve that he would build the Farther Mosque with stone came to sore straits,

entries.farsi: وحى كردش حق كه ترك اين بخوان / ‏كه ز دستت بر نيايد اين مكان
entries.english: God made a Revelation to him, saying, “Proclaim the abandonment of this, for this place will not be achieved by your hand.

entries.farsi: نيست در تقدير ما آن كه تو اين / ‏مسجد اقصى بر آرى اين گزين
entries.english: It is not in Our fore-ordainment that you should raise this Farther Mosque (al Aqsa in Jerusalem), O chosen one.”

entries.farsi: گفت جرمم چيست اى داناى راز / كه مرا گويى كه مسجد را مساز
entries.english: He said, “O Knower of the secret, what is my crime, that You forbid me to construct the Mosque?”

entries.farsi: گفت بى‏جرمى تو خونها كرده‏اى / ‏خون مظلومان به گردن برده‏اى
entries.english: He said, “Without a crime, you have wrought much bloodshed: you have taken upon your neck the blood of persons who have suffered injustice;

entries.farsi: كه ز آواز تو خلقى بى‏شمار / جان بدادند و شدند آن را شكار
entries.english: For from your voice a countless multitude gave up the ghost and fell a prey to it.

entries.farsi: خون بسى رفته‏ست بر آواز تو / بر صداى خوب جان پرداز تو
entries.english: Much blood has gone to the score of your voice, to your beautiful soul-ravishing song.”

entries.farsi: گفت مغلوب تو بودم مست تو / دست من بر بسته بود از دست تو
entries.english: He said, “I was overpowered by You, drunk with You: my hand was tied up by Your hand.

entries.farsi: نه كه هر مغلوب شه مرحوم بود / نه كه المغلوب كالمعدوم بود
entries.english: Was not every one that was overpowered by the King the object of mercy? Was not he ‘The overpowered is like the non-existent’?”

entries.farsi: گفت اين مغلوب معدومى است كاو / جز به نسبت نيست معدوم ايقنوا
entries.english: He said, “This overpowered man is that non-existent one who is only relatively non-existent. Have sure faith!

entries.farsi: اين چنين معدوم كاو از خويش رفت / ‏بهترين هستها افتاد و زفت
entries.english: Such a non-existent one who has gone from himself is the best of beings, and the great.

entries.farsi: او به نسبت با صفات حق فناست / در حقيقت در فنا او را بقاست
entries.english: He has passed away (fana)in relation to the Divine attributes; in passing away he really has the life everlasting (baqa).

entries.farsi: جمله‏ى ارواح در تدبير اوست / ‏جمله‏ى اشباح هم در تير اوست
entries.english: All spirits are under his governance; all bodies too are in his control.

entries.farsi: آن كه او مغلوب اندر لطف ماست / ‏نيست مضطر بلكه مختار والست
entries.english: He that is overpowered in Our grace is not compelled; nay, he is one who freely chooses devotion.”

entries.farsi: منتهاى اختيار آن است خود / كه اختيارش گردد اينجا مفتقد
entries.english: In truth the end of free-will is that his free-will should be lost here.

entries.farsi: اختيارى را نبودى چاشنى / ‏گر نگشتى آخر او محو از منى
entries.english: The free agent would feel no savour if at last he did not become entirely purged of egoism.

entries.farsi: در جهان گر لقمه و گر شربت است / ‏لذت او فرع محو لذت است
entries.english: If there is delicious food and drink in the world, his pleasure is a branch of the extinction of pleasure.

entries.farsi: گر چه از لذات بى‏تاثير شد / لذتى بود او و لذت‏گير شد
entries.english: Although he was unaffected by pleasures, he was a man of pleasure and became the recipient of pleasure.

sections.title_fa: شرح إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْ وَةٌو العلماء كنفس واحده، خاصه اتحاد داود و سليمان و ساير انبياء عليهم السلام كه اگر يكى از ايشان را منكر شوى ايمان به هيچ نبى درست نباشد و اين عالمت اتحاد است َكه يك خانه از آن هزاران خانه ويران كنى آن همه ويران شود و يك ديوار قايم نماند كه ال نُفَرِّقُ بَيْن أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ و العاقل يكفيه الاشاره، اين خود از اشارت گذشت
