sections.title_en: Explanation of “Truly, the Faithful are brothers, and the ulama are as one soul”; in particular, the oneness of David, Solomon, and all the other prophets, on whom be peace: if you disbelieve in one of them, faith in any prophet will not be perfect; and this is the sign of oneness, that if you destroy a single one of those thousands of houses,

all the rest will be destroyed, and not a single wall will be left standing; for “We make no distinction between any of them.” Indication is sufficient for him that has intelligence: this goes even beyond indication.

entries.farsi: گر چه برنايد به جهد و زور تو / ليك مسجد را بر آرد پور تو
entries.english: “Although it will not be accomplished by your labour and strength, yet the Mosque will be erected by your son.

entries.farsi: كرده‏ى از كرده‏ى تست اى حكيم / ‏مومنان را اتصالى دان قديم
entries.english: His deed is your deed, O man of wisdom: know that between the Faithful is an ancient union.”

entries.farsi: مومنان معدود ليك ايمان يكى / ‏جسمشان معدود ليكن جان يكى
entries.english: The Faithful are numerous, but the Faith is one: their bodies are numerous, but their soul is one.

entries.farsi: غير فهم و جان كه در گاو و خر است / ‏آدمى را عقل و جانى ديگر است
entries.english: Besides the understanding and soul which is in the ox and the ass, Man has another intelligence and soul;

entries.farsi: باز غير جان و عقل آدمى / ‏هست جانى در ولى آن دمى
entries.english: Again, in the owner of that breath there is a soul other than the human soul and intelligence.

entries.farsi: جان حيوانى ندارد اتحاد / تو مجو اين اتحاد از روح باد
entries.english: The animal soul does not possess oneness: seek not you this oneness from the airy spirit.

entries.farsi: گر خورد اين نان نگردد سير آن / ‏ور كشد بار اين نگردد او گران
entries.english: If this one eats bread, that one is not filled; and if this one bears a load, that one does not become laden;

entries.farsi: بلكه اين شادى كند از مرگ او / از حسد ميرد چو بيند برگ او
entries.english: Nay, but this one rejoices at the death of that one and dies of envy when he sees that one’s prosperity.

entries.farsi: جان گرگان و سگان هر يك جداست / ‏متحد جانهاى شيران خداست
entries.english: The souls of wolves and dogs are separate, every one; the souls of the Lions of God are united.

entries.farsi: جمع گفتم جانهاشان من به اسم / ‏كان يكى جان صد بود نسبت به جسم
entries.english: I have spoken of their souls nominally in the plural, for that single soul is a hundred in relation to the body,

entries.farsi: همچو آن يك نور خورشيد سما / صد بود نسبت به صحن خانه‏ها
entries.english: Just as the single light of the sun in heaven is a hundred in relation to the house-courts,

entries.farsi: ليك يك باشد همه انوارشان / ‏چون كه برگيرى تو ديوار از ميان
entries.english: But when you remove the wall, all the lights on them are one.

entries.farsi: چون نماند خانه‏ها را قاعده / ‏مومنان مانند نفس واحده
entries.english: When the houses have no foundation remaining, the Faithful remain one soul.

entries.farsi: فرق و اشكالات آيد زين مقال / ‏ز انكه نبود مثل اين باشد مثال
entries.english: Differences and difficulties arise from this saying, because this is not a similitude: it is a comparison.

entries.farsi: فرق‏ها بى‏حد بود از شخص شير / تا به شخص آدمى زاد دلير
entries.english: Endless are the differences between the corporeal figure of a lion and the figure of a courageous son of man;

entries.farsi: ليك در وقت مثال اى خوش نظر / اتحاد از روى جان‏بازى نگر
entries.english: But at the moment of the comparison consider, O you who have good insight, their oneness in respect of hazarding their lives;

entries.farsi: كان دلير آخر مثال شير بود / نيست مثل شير در جمله‏ى حدود
entries.english: For, after all, the courageous man did resemble the lion; he is not like the lion in all points of the definition.

entries.farsi: متحد نقشى ندارد اين سرا / تا كه مثلى وا نمايم من ترا
entries.english: This abode does not contain any form one, so that I might show forth to you a similitude.

entries.farsi: هم مثال ناقصى دست آورم / تا ز حيرانى خرد را وا خرم
entries.english: Still, I will bring to hand an imperfect comparison that I may redeem your mind from confusion.

entries.farsi: شب به هر خانه چراغى مى‏نهند / تا به نور آن ز ظلمت مى‏رهند
entries.english: At night a lamp is placed in every house, in order that by its light they may be delivered from darkness.

entries.farsi: آن چراغ اين تن بود نورش چو جان / ‏هست محتاج فتيل و اين و آن
entries.english: That lamp is this body, its light like the soul; it requires a wick and this and that.

entries.farsi: آن چراغ شش فتيله‏ى اين حواس / ‏جملگى بر خواب و خور دارد اساس
entries.english: That lamp with six wicks, namely, these senses, is based entirely upon sleep and food.

entries.farsi: بى‏خور و بى‏خواب نزيد نيم دم / ‏با خور و با خواب نزيد نيز هم
entries.english: Without food and sleep it would not live half a moment; nor even with food and sleep does it live either.

entries.farsi: بى‏فتيل و روغنش نبود بقا / با فتيل و روغن او هم بى‏وفا
entries.english: Without wick and oil it has no duration, and with wick and oil it is also faithless,

entries.farsi: ز انكه نور علتى‏اش مرگ جوست / ‏چون زيد كه روز روشن مرگ اوست
entries.english: Inasmuch as its light, related to causes, is seeking death: how should it live when bright day is the death of it?

entries.farsi: جمله حسهاى بشر هم بى‏بقاست / ‏ز انكه پيش نور روز حشر الست
entries.english: Likewise all the human senses are impermanent, because they are nothing in the presence of the Day of Resurrection.

entries.farsi: نور حس و جان بابايان ما / نيست كلى فانى و ال چون گيا
entries.english: The light of the senses and spirits of our fathers is not wholly perishable and nothing, like the grass;

entries.farsi: ليك مانند ستاره و ماهتاب / ‏جمله محوند از شعاع آفتاب
entries.english: But, like the stars and moonbeams, they all vanish in the radiance of the Sun.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه سوز و درد زخم كيك / ‏محو گردد چون در آيد مار اليك
entries.english: It is just as the smart and pain of the flea’s bite disappears when the snake comes in to you.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه عور اندر آب جست / ‏تا در آب از زخم زنبوران برست
entries.english: It is just as the naked man jumped into the water, that in the water he might escape from the sting of the hornets:

entries.farsi: مى‏كند زنبور بر بالا طواف / ‏چون بر آرد سر ندارندش معاف
entries.english: The hornets circle above, and when he puts out his head they do not spare him.

entries.farsi: آب ذكر حق و زنبور اين زمان / ‏هست ياد آن فالنه و آن فالن
entries.english: The water is remembrance of God, and the hornet is the remembrance, during this time, of such-and-such a woman or such-and-such a man.

entries.farsi: دم بخور در آب ذكر و صبر كن / ‏تا رهى از فكر و وسواس كهن
entries.english: Swallow your breath in the water of recollection and show fortitude, that you may be freed from the old thought and temptation.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن تو طبع آن آب صفا / خود بگيرى جملگى سر تا به پا
entries.english: After that, you yourself will assume the nature of that pure water entirely from head to foot.

entries.farsi: آن چنانك از آب آن زنبور شر / مى‏گريزد از تو هم گيرد حذر
entries.english: As the noxious hornet flees from the water, so will it be afraid of you.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن خواهى تو دور از آب باش / ‏كه به سر هم طبع آبى خواجه‏تاش
entries.english: After that, be far from the water, if you wish; for in your inmost soul you are of the same nature as the water, O fellow-servant.

entries.farsi: در صفات حق صفات جمله‏شان / ‏همچو اختر پيش آن خور بى‏نشان
entries.english: Those persons, then, who have passed from the world are not anything, but they are steeped in the Attributes.

entries.farsi: پس كسانى كز جهان بگذشته‏اند / ال نيند و در صفات آغشته‏اند
entries.english: All their attributes are in the Attributes of God,

entries.farsi: گر ز قرآن نقل خواهى اى حرون / ‏خوان جميع هم لدينا محضرون
entries.english: even as the star is without trace in the presence of the sun. If you demand a citation from the Qur’an, O recalcitrant, recite all of them shall be brought into Our presence.

entries.farsi: محضرون معدوم نبود نيك بين / تا بقاى روحها دانى يقين
entries.english: Muhdarun is not non-existent ma‘dúm. Consider well, that you may gain certain knowledge of the everlasting life (baqa) of the spirits.

entries.farsi: روح محجوب از بقا بس در عذاب / ‏روح واصل در بقا پاك از حجاب
entries.english: The spirit debarred from everlasting life is exceedingly tormented; the spirit united in everlasting life is free from barrier.

entries.farsi: زين چراغ حس حيوان المراد / گفتمت هان تا نجويى اتحاد
entries.english: I have told you the purpose of this lamp of animal sense-perception. Beware of seeking to become one.

entries.farsi: روح خود را متصل كن اى فالن / ‏زود با ارواح قدس سالكان
entries.english: Make your spirit, O such-and-such, to be united speedily with the holy spirits of the Travellers.

entries.farsi: صد چراغت گر مرند ار بيستند / بس جدايند و يگانه نيستند
entries.english: Your hundred lamps, then, whether they die or whether they stand, are separate and are not single.

entries.farsi: ز آن همه جنگند اين اصحاب ما / جنگ كس نشنيد اندر انبيا
entries.english: On that account these companions of ours are all at war, no one heard of war amongst the prophets,

entries.farsi: ز انكه نور انبيا خورشيد بود / نور حس ما چراغ و شمع و دود
entries.english: Because the light of the prophets was the Sun, the light of our senses is lamp and candle and smoke.

entries.farsi: يك بميرد يك بماند تا به روز / يك بود پژمرده ديگر با فروز
entries.english: One dies, one lasts till daybreak; one is dim, another bright.

entries.farsi: جان حيوانى بود حى از غذا / هم بميرد او به هر نيك و بذى
entries.english: The animal soul is alive by nourishment; however good or bad its state may be, it dies all the same.

entries.farsi: گر بميرد اين چراغ و طى شود / خانه‏ى همسايه مظلم كى شود
entries.english: If this lamp dies and is extinguished, how should the neighbour’s house become dark?

entries.farsi: نور آن خانه چو بى‏اين هم به پاست / پس چراغ حس هر خانه جداست
entries.english: Inasmuch as without this the light in that house is still maintained, hence the lamp of sense-perception is different in every house.

entries.farsi: اين مثال جان حيوانى بود / نه مثال جان ربانى بود
entries.english: This is a parable of the animal soul, not a parable of the divine soul.

entries.farsi: باز از هندوى شب چون ماه زاد / در سر هر روزنى نورى فتاد
entries.english: Again, when the moon is born from the Hindu, Night, a light falls upon every window.

entries.farsi: تا بود خورشيد تابان بر افق / ‏هست در هر خانه نور او قنق
entries.english: Count the light of those hundred houses as one, for the light of this does not remain without the other.

entries.farsi: نور آن صد خانه را تو يك شمر / كه نماند نور اين بى‏آن دگر
entries.english: So long as the sun is shining on the horizon, its light is a guest in every house;

entries.farsi: باز چون خورشيد جان آفل شود / نور جمله خانه‏ها زايل شود
entries.english: Again, when the spiritual Sun sets, the light in all the houses disappears.

entries.farsi: اين مثال نور آمد مثل نى / ‏مر ترا هادى عدو را ره زنى
entries.english: This is a parable of the Light, not a similitude; for you a true guide, for the enemy a highwayman.

entries.farsi: بر مثال عنكبوت آن زشت خو / پرده‏هاى گنده را بر بافد او
entries.english: That evil-natured person resembles the spider: he weaves stinking veils.

entries.farsi: از لعاب خويش پرده‏ى نور كرد / ديده‏ى ادراك خود را كور كرد
entries.english: Of his own gossamer he made a veil over the Light: he made the eye of his apprehension blind.

entries.farsi: اندر اين آهنگ منگر سست و پست / ‏كاندر اين ره صبر و شق انفس است
entries.english: If one takes hold of a horse’s neck, he gains advantage; and if he takes hold of its leg, he receives a kick.

entries.farsi: كم نشين بر اسب توسن بى‏لگام / ‏عقل و دين را پيشوا كن و السلام
entries.english: Do not mount the restive horse without a bridle: make Reason and Religion your leader, and farewell.

entries.farsi: گردن اسب ار بگيرد بر خورد / ور بگيرد پاش بستاند لگد
entries.english: Do not look scornfully and contemptuously on this quest, for in this Way there is self-denial and grievous anguish to souls.

sections.title_fa: ‏بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى بناى مسجد اقصى
sections.title_en: The rest of the Story of the building of the Farther Mosque

entries.farsi: چون سليمان كرد آغاز بنا / ‏پاك چون كعبه همايون چون منى
entries.english: When Solomon began the building— holy like the Ka’ba, august like Mina—

entries.farsi: در بنا هر سنگ كز كه مى‏سكست / ‏فاش سيروا بى‏همى‏گفت از نخست
entries.english: In his building were seen splendour and magnificence: it was not frigid like other buildings.

entries.farsi: در بنايش ديده مى‏شد كر و فر / نى فسرده چون بناهاى دگر
entries.english: From the first, every stone in the building—that was broken off from the mountain—was saying clearly, “Take me along!”

entries.farsi: همچو از آب و گل آدم‏كده / ‏نور ز آهك پاره‏ها تابان شده
entries.english: As from the water and earth of the house of Adam, did light shine forth from the pieces of mortar.

entries.farsi: حق همى‏گويد كه ديوار بهشت / ‏نيست چون ديوارها بى‏جان و زشت
entries.english: The stones were coming without carrier, and those doors and walls had become living.

entries.farsi: سنگ بى‏حمال آينده شده / ‏و آن در و ديوارها زنده شده
entries.english: God says that the ‘wall of Paradise is not lifeless and ugly like walls;

entries.farsi: چون در و ديوار تن با آگهى است / ‏زنده باشد خانه چون شاهنشهى است
entries.english: Like the door and wall of the body, it is with intelligence: the house is living since it belongs to the King of kings.

entries.farsi: هم درخت و ميوه هم آب زالل / با بهشتى در حديث و در مقال
entries.english: Both tree and fruit and limpid water with the inhabitant of Paradise in conversation and discourse,

entries.farsi: اين بنا ز آب و گل مرده بده‏ست / ‏و آن بنا از طاعت زنده شده‏ست
entries.english: Because Paradise has not been fashioned out of materials; nay, but it has been fashioned out of deeds and intentions.

entries.farsi: اين به اصل خويش ماند پر خلل / و آن به اصل خود كه علم است و عمل
entries.english: This edifice has been of dead water and earth, while that edifice has arisen from living piety.

entries.farsi: ز انكه جنت را نه ز آلت بسته‏اند / بلكه از اعمال و نيت بسته‏اند
entries.english: This resembles its foundation full of defect, and that its foundation, which is knowledge and action.

entries.farsi: هم سرير و قصر و هم تاج و ثياب / ‏با بهشتى در سؤال و در جواب
entries.english: Both throne and palace and crown and robes are in question and reply with the inhabitant of Paradise.

entries.farsi: فرش بى‏فراش پيچيده شود / خانه بى‏مكناس روبيده شود
entries.english: The carpet is folded without the farrash (carpet- spreader); the house is swept without the broom.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى دل بين ز غم ژوليده شد / بى‏كناس از توبه‏اى روبيده شد
entries.english: Behold the house of the heart: it was disordered by cares: without sweeper it was swept by repentance.

entries.farsi: تخت او سيار بى‏حمال شد / حلقه و در مطرب و قوال شد
entries.english: Its throne moved along without carrier; its door-ring and door became musician and singer.

entries.farsi: هست در دل زندگى دار الخلود / در زبانم چون نمى‏آيد چه سود
entries.english: The life of the everlasting Abode exists in the heart: since it comes not on to my tongue, what is the use?

entries.farsi: چون سليمان در شدى هر بامداد / مسجد اندر بهر ارشاد عباد
entries.english: When Solomon went into the Mosque every morning to guide the servants in the right way,

entries.farsi: پند دادى گه به گفت و لحن و ساز / گه به فعل اعنى ركوعى يا نماز
entries.english: He would give exhortation, sometimes by speech and melody and harmony, sometimes by act—I mean a bowing or prayer.

entries.farsi: پند فعلى خلق را جذاب‏تر / كه رسد در جان هر با گوش و كر
entries.english: The exhortation of act draws people more powerfully, for it reaches the soul of every one that has hearing and the deaf.

entries.farsi: اندر آن وهم اميرى كم بود / در حشم تاثير آن محكم بود
entries.english: In that the conceit of princedom is less: the impression made by it upon the followers is strong.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى آغاز خالفت عثمان و خطبه‏ى وى در بيان آن كه ناصح فعال به فعل به از ناصح قوال به قول
sections.title_en: Story of the beginning of the Caliphate of Uthman, may God be well-pleased with him, and his sermon expounding that the doer who exhorts by deeds is better than the speaker who exhorts by words.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى عثمان كه بر منبر برفت / ‏چون خالفت يافت بشتابيد تفت
entries.english: The story of Uthman is that he mounted the pulpit: when he obtained the Caliphate, he made hot haste.

entries.farsi: منبر مهتر كه سه پايه بده‏ست / ‏رفت بو بكر و دوم پايه نشست
entries.english: The pulpit of the Chief, which had three steps: Abu Bakr went and seated himself on the second step.

entries.farsi: بر سوم پايه عمر در دور خويش / ‏از براى حرمت اسالم و كيش
entries.english: Umar, in his reign, on the third step in order to show reverence for Islam and the Religion

entries.farsi: دور عثمان آمد او بالاى تخت / ‏بر شد و بنشست آن محمود بخت
entries.english: The reign of Uthman arrived, he, that man of praised fortune, went up on to the top of the throne and seated himself.

entries.farsi: پس سؤالش كرد شخصى بو الفضول / ‏كان دو ننشستند بر جاى رسول
entries.english: Then a person given to idle meddling questioned him, saying, “Those two did not sit in the Prophet’s place:

entries.farsi: پس تو چون جستى از ايشان برترى / ‏چون به رتبت تو از ايشان كمترى
entries.english: How, then, have you sought to be higher than they, when you are inferior to them in rank?”

entries.farsi: بر دوم پايه شوم من جاى جو / گويى بو بكر است و اين هم مثل او
entries.english: He replied, “If I tread on the third step, it will be imagined that I resemble Umar;

entries.farsi: گفت اگر پايه‏ى سوم را بسپرم / ‏وهم آيد كه مثال عمرم
entries.english: I seek a seat on the second step, you will say, ‘it is Abu Bakr, and this one too is like him.’

entries.farsi: بعد از آن بر جاى خطبه آن ودود / تا به قرب عصر لب خاموش بود
entries.english: This top is the place of Mustafa: no one will imagine that I am like that King.”

entries.farsi: هست اين بالا مقام مصطفى / وهم مثلى نيست با آن شه مرا
entries.english: Afterwards, in the preaching-place, that loving man kept silence till near the afternoon-prayer.

entries.farsi: زهره نه كس را كه گويد هين بخوان / ‏يا برون آيد ز مسجد آن زمان
entries.english: None dared to say “Come now, preach!” or to go forth from the mosque during that time.

entries.farsi: هيبتى بنشسته بد بر خاص و عام / ‏پر شده نور خدا آن صحن و بام
entries.english: Awe had settled on high and low: the court and roof had become filled with the Light of God.

entries.farsi: هر كه بينا ناظر نورش بدى / ‏كور ز آن خورشيد هم گرم آمدى
entries.english: Whoever possessed vision was beholding His Light; the blind man too was being heated by that Sun.

entries.farsi: پس ز گرمى فهم كردى چشم كور / كه بر آمد آفتابى بى‏فتور
entries.english: Hence, by reason of the heat, the blind man’s eye perceived that there had arisen a Sun whose strength does not fail.

entries.farsi: ليك اين گرمى گشايد ديده را / تا ببيند عين هر بشنيده را
entries.english: But this heat opens the eye, that it may see the very substance of everything heard.

entries.farsi: گرمى‏اش را ضجرتى و حالتى / ‏ز آن تبش دل را گشادى فسحتى
entries.english: Its heat has a grievous agitation and emotion, from that glow there comes to the heart a joyous freedom, an expansion.

entries.farsi: كور چون شد گرم از نور قدم / ‏از فرح گويد كه من بينا شدم
entries.english: When the blind man is heated by the Light of Eternity, from gladness he says, “I have become seeing.”

entries.farsi: اين نصيب كور باشد ز آفتاب / ‏صد چنين و اهلل اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: You are very drunk, but, O Bu ’l-Hasan, there is a bit of way to seeing.

entries.farsi: سخت خوش مستى ولى اى بو الحسن / ‏پاره‏اى راه است تا بينا شدن
entries.english: This is the blind man’s portion from the Sun, a hundred such; and God best know what is right.

entries.farsi: و انكه او آن نور را بينا بود / شرح او كى كار بو سينا بود
entries.english: And he that has vision of that Light— how should the explanation of him be a task of Bu Sina?

entries.farsi: ور شود صد تو كه باشد اين زبان / ‏كه بجنباند به كف پرده‏ى عيان
entries.english: If it be hundredfold, who is this tongue that it should move with its hand the veil of clairvoyance?

entries.farsi: واى بر وى گر بسايد پرده را / تيغ اللهى كند دستش جدا
entries.english: Woe to it if it touches the veil! The Divine sword severs its hand.

entries.farsi: دست چه بود خود سرش را بر كند / آن سرى كز جهل سرها مى‏كند
entries.english: What of the hand? It rends off even its head— the head that from ignorance puts forth many a head.

entries.farsi: اين به تقدير سخن گفتم ترا / ور نه خود دستش كجا و آن كجا
entries.english: I have said this to you, speaking hypothetically; otherwise, indeed, how far is its hand from being able to do that!

entries.farsi: خاله را خايه بدى خالو شدى / ‏اين به تقدير آمده‏ست ار او بدى
entries.english: If the aunt had balls, she would have been an uncle: this is hypothetical—“if there were.”

entries.farsi: از زبان تا چشم كاو پاك از شك است / ‏صد هزاران ساله گويم اندك است
entries.english: I say that between the tongue and the eye that is free from doubt there is a hundred thousand years’, it is little.

entries.farsi: هين مشو نوميد نور از آسمان / ‏حق چو خواهد مى‏رسد در يك زمان
entries.english: Now come, do not despair! When God wills, light arrives from heaven in a single moment.

entries.farsi: اختر گردون ظلم را ناسخ است / ‏اختر حق در صفاتش راسخ است
entries.english: At every instant His power causes a hundred influences from the stars to reach the mines.

entries.farsi: صد اثر در كانها از اختران / ‏مى‏رساند قدرتش در هر زمان
entries.english: The star of heaven deletes the darkness; the star of God is fixed in His Attributes.

entries.farsi: چرخ پانصد ساله راه اى مستعين / ‏در اثر نزديك آمد با زمين
entries.english: O you that seek help, the celestial sphere, of five hundred years’ journey, is in effect near to the earth.

entries.farsi: سه هزاران سال و پانصد تا زحل / ‏دم‏به‏دم خاصيتش آرد عمل
entries.english: It is three thousand five hundred years to Saturn; his special property acts incessantly.

entries.farsi: درهمش آرد چو سايه در اياب / ‏طول سايه چيست پيش آفتاب
entries.english: He rolls it up like a shadow at the return: in the sun’s presence what is the length of the shadow?

entries.farsi: وز نفوس پاك اختروش مدد / سوى اخترهاى گردون مى‏رسد
entries.english: And from the pure star like souls replenishment is ever coming to the stars of heaven.

entries.farsi: ظاهر آن اختران قوام ما / باطن ما گشته قوام سما
entries.english: The outward of those stars is our ruler; our inward has become the ruler of the sky.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه حكما گويند آدمى عالم صغرى است و حكماى الهى گويند آدمى عالم كبرى است زيرا آن علم حكما بر صورت آدمى مقصور بود و علم اين حكما در حقيقت آدمى موصول بود
sections.title_en: Explaining that philosophers say that Man is the microcosm, theosophists say that Man is the macrocosm, the reason being that philosophy is confined to the phenomenal form
of Man, whereas theosophy is connected with the essential truth of his true nature.

entries.farsi: پس به صورت عالم اصغر تويى / ‏پس به معنى عالم اكبر تويى
entries.english: Therefore in form you are the microcosm, therefore in reality you are the macrocosm.

entries.farsi: ظاهر آن شاخ اصل ميوه است / ‏باطنا بهر ثمر شد شاخ هست
entries.english: Externally the branch is the origin of the fruit; intrinsically the branch came into existence for the sake of the fruit.

entries.farsi: پس به معنى آن شجر از ميوه زاد / گر به صورت از شجر بودش والد
entries.english: If there had not been desire and hope of the fruit, how should the gardener have planted the root of the tree?

entries.farsi: گر نبودى ميل و اوميد ثمر / كى نشاندى باغبان بيخ شجر
entries.english: Therefore in reality the tree was born of the fruit, if in appearance it was generated by the tree.

entries.farsi: مصطفى زين گفت كادم و انبيا / خلف من باشند در زير لوا
entries.english: Hence Mustafa said, “Adam and the prophets are behind me under banner.”

entries.farsi: بهر اين فرموده است آن ذو فنون / ‏رمز نحن الاخرون السابقون
entries.english: For this reason that master of sorts of knowledge has uttered the allegorical saying, “We are the last and the foremost.”

entries.farsi: گر به صورت من ز آدم زاده‏ام / ‏من به معنى جد جد افتاده‏ام
entries.english: “If in appearance I am born of Adam, in reality I am the forefather of forefather,

entries.farsi: كز براى من بدش سجده‏ى ملك / ‏وز پى من رفت بر هفتم فلك
entries.english: Since the worship of the angels was to him for my sake, and he ascended to the Seventh Heaven on my account

entries.farsi: پس ز من زاييد در معنى پدر / پس ز ميوه زاد در معنى شجر
entries.english: Therefore in reality the Father was born of me, therefore in reality the tree was born of the fruit.”

entries.farsi: اول فكر آخر آمد در عمل / ‏خاصه فكرى كاو بود وصف ازل
entries.english: The thought, which is first, comes last into actuality, in particular the thought that is eternal.

entries.farsi: حاصل اندر يك زمان از آسمان / ‏مى‏رود مى‏آيد ايدر كاروان
entries.english: To sum up, in a single moment the caravan is going from Heaven and coming here.

entries.farsi: نيست بر اين كاروان اين ره دراز / كى مفازه زفت آيد با مفاز
entries.english: This way is not long for this caravan: how should the desert show itself formidable to him who has been granted success?

entries.farsi: دل به كعبه مى‏رود در هر زمان / ‏جسم طبع دل بگيرد ز امتنان
entries.english: The heart is faring to the Ka‘ba at every moment, and through bounty the body assumes the nature of the heart.

entries.farsi: اين دراز و كوتهى مر جسم راست / ‏چه دراز و كوته آن جا كه خداست
entries.english: This length and shortness appertains to the body: where God is, what is “long” and “short”?

entries.farsi: چون خدا مر جسم را تبديل كرد / رفتنش بى‏فرسخ و بى‏ميل كرد
entries.english: When God has transmuted the body, He makes its faring to be without league or mile.

entries.farsi: صد اميد است اين زمان بردار گام / ‏عاشقانه اى فتى خل الكالم
entries.english: There are a hundred hopes at this time. Step, O youth, like a lover and relinquish disputation.

entries.farsi: گر چه پيله‏ى چشم بر هم مى‏زنى / ‏در سفينه خفته‏اى ره مى‏كنى
entries.english: Albeit you are closing your eyelids, you are asleep in the ship and voyaging.

sections.title_fa: ‏تفسير اين حديث كه مثل امتى كمثل سفينه نوح من تمسك بها نجا و من تخلف عنها غرق
sections.title_en: Exposition of the Hadíth, “The parable of my community is the parable of the Ship of Noah: whoso shall cling to it is saved, and whoso shall hold back from it is drowned.”

entries.farsi: بهر اين فرمود پيغمبر كه من / ‏همچو كشتى‏ام به طوفان زمن
entries.english: On this account the Prophet said, “I am as the Ship in the Flood of Time.

entries.farsi: ما و اصحابيم چون كشتى نوح / ‏هر كه دست اندر زند يابد فتوح
entries.english: I and my Companions are as the Ship of Noah: whoever clings will gain graces.”

entries.farsi: چون كه با شيخى تو دور از زشتيى / ‏روز و شب سيارى و در كشتيى
entries.english: When you are with the Shaykh you are far removed from wickedness: day and night you are a traveller and in a ship.

entries.farsi: در پناه جان جان بخشى توى / ‏كشتى اندر خفته‏اى ره مى‏روى
entries.english: You are under the protection of a life-giving spirit: you are asleep in the ship; you are going on the way.

entries.farsi: مگسل از پيغمبر ايام خويش / ‏تكيه كم كن بر فن و بر كام خويش
entries.english: Do not break with the prophet of your days: do not rely on your own skill and footsteps.

entries.farsi: گر چه شيرى چون روى ره بى‏دليل / ‏خويش بين و در ضاللى و ذليل
entries.english: Lion though you are, you are self-conceited and in error and contemptible when you go on the way without a guide.

entries.farsi: هين مپر الا كه با پرهاى شيخ / ‏تا ببينى عون لشكرهاى شيخ
entries.english: Beware! Do not fly but with the wings of the Shaykh, that you may see the aid of the armies of the Shaykh.

entries.farsi: يك زمانى موج لطفش بال تست / ‏آتش قهرش دمى حمال تست
entries.english: At one time the wave of his mercy is your wing, at another moment the fire of his wrath is your carrier.

entries.farsi: قهر او را ضد لطفش كم شمر / اتحاد هر دو بين اندر اثر
entries.english: Do not reckon his wrath to be the contrary of his mercy: behold the oneness of both in the effect.

entries.farsi: ك زمان چون خاك سبزت مى‏كند / يك زمان پر باد و گبزت مى‏كند
entries.english: At one time he will make you green like the earth, at another time he will make you full of wind, and big.

entries.farsi: جسم عارف را دهد وصف جماد / تا بر او رويد گل و نسرين شاد
entries.english: He gives the quality of inorganic things to the body of the knower, in order that gay roses and eglantines may grow on it;

entries.farsi: ليك او بيند نبيند غير او / جز به مغز پاك ندهد خلد بو
entries.english: But he alone sees, none sees but he: Paradise yields no scent but to the purified brain.

entries.farsi: مغز را خالى كن از انكار يار / تا كه ريحان يابد از گلزار يار
entries.english: Empty your brain of disbelief in the Friend, that it may feel sweet odours from the rose-garden of the Friend;

entries.farsi: تا بيابى بوى خلد از يار من / ‏چون محمد بوى رحمن از يمن
entries.english: So that you may feel the scent of Paradise from my Friend, as Mohammed the scent of the Merciful from Yemen

entries.farsi: در صف معراجيان گر بيستى / ‏چون براقت بر كشاند نيستى
entries.english: If you stand in the rank of those who make the ascension, not-being will bear you aloft, like Buraq.

entries.farsi: نه چو معراج زمينى تا قمر / بلكه چون معراج كلكى تا شكر
entries.english: It is not like the ascension of a piece of earth to the moon; nay, but like the ascension of a cane to sugar.

entries.farsi: نه چو معراج بخارى تا سما / ‏بل چو معراج جنينى تا نهى
entries.english: It is not like the ascension of a vapour to the sky; nay, but like the ascension of an embryo to rationality.

entries.farsi: خوش براقى گشت خنگ نيستى / ‏سوى هستى آردت گر بيستى
entries.english: The steed of not-being became a goodly Buraq: it brings you to existence, if you are non-existent.

entries.farsi: كوه و درياها سمش مس مى‏كند / تا جهان حس را پس مى‏كند
entries.english: Its hoof brushes the mountains and seas till it puts the world of sense perception behind.

entries.farsi: پا بكش در كشتى و مى‏رو دوان / ‏چون سوى معشوق جان جان روان
entries.english: Set your foot into the ship and keep going quickly, like the soul going towards the soul’s Beloved.

entries.farsi: دست نه و پاى نه رو تا قدم / ‏آن چنان كه تاخت جانها از عدم
entries.english: No hands and no feet, go to Eternity in the same fashion as that in which the spirits sped from non-existence.

entries.farsi: بر دريدى در سخن پرده‏ى قياس / ‏گر نبودى سمع سامع را نعاس
entries.english: If there had not been somnolence in the hearer’s hearing, the veil of logical reasoning would have been torn asunder in the discourse.

entries.farsi: اى فلك بر گفت او گوهر بيار / از جهان او جهانا شرم دار
entries.english: O Heaven, shower pearls on his advice! O World, have shame of his world!

entries.farsi: پس نثارى كرده باشى بهر خود / چون كه هر سرمايه‏ى تو صد شود
entries.english: If you will shower, your substance will become hundredfold: your inorganic will become seeing and speaking.

entries.farsi: گر ببارى گوهرت صد تا شود / جامدت بيننده و گويا شود
entries.english: Therefore you will have scattered a largesse for your own sake, inasmuch as every stock of yours will be increased a hundredfold.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى هديه فرستادن بلقيس از شهر سبا سوى سليمان عليه السلام
sections.title_en: Story of Bilqís sending a gift from the city of Saba to Solomon, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: هديه‏ى بلقيس چل استر بده‏ست / ‏بار آنها جمله خشت زر بده‏ست
entries.english: The gift of Bilqís was forty mules: their whole load consisted of bricks of gold.

entries.farsi: بر سر زر تا چهل منزل براند / تا كه زر را در نظر آبى نماند
entries.english: When he reached the open plain, belonging to Solomon, he saw that its carpet was entirely of solid gold.

entries.farsi: چون به صحراى سليمانى رسيد / فرش آن را جمله زر پخته ديد
entries.english: He rode on gold for the distance of forty stages, till gold had no more esteem in his sight.

entries.farsi: بارها گفتند زر را وابريم / ‏سوى مخزن ما چه بيگار اندريم
entries.english: Times they said, “Let us take the gold back to the treasury: what a quest are we in!

entries.farsi: عرصه‏اى كش خاك زر ده دهى است / ‏زر به هديه بردن آن جا ابلهى است
entries.english: A spacious land of which the soil is pure gold— to bring gold thither as a gift is folly.”

entries.farsi: اى ببرده عقل هديه تا اله / ‏عقل آن جا كمتر است از خاك راه
entries.english: O you who have brought intelligence to God as a gift, there intelligence is less than the dust of the road.

entries.farsi: چون كساد هديه آن جا شد پديد / شرمساريشان همى واپس كشيد
entries.english: When the worthlessness of the gift became apparent there, shamefacedness was drawing them back;

entries.farsi: باز گفتند ار كساد و گر روا / چيست بر ما بنده فرمانيم ما
entries.english: Again they said, “Whether it is worthless or valuable, what matter to us? We are slaves to the command.

entries.farsi: گر زر و گر خاك ما را بردنى است / ‏امر فرمانده بجا آوردنى است
entries.english: Whether we have to bring gold or earth, the command of the one who gives the command is to be executed.

entries.farsi: گر بفرمايند كه واپس بريد / هم به فرمان تحفه را باز آوريد
entries.english: If they command you to bring it back, take the gift back according to the command.”

entries.farsi: خنده‏ش آمد چون سليمان آن بديد / كز شما من كى طلب كردم ثريد
entries.english: When Solomon beheld that, he laughed, saying, “When did I seek broken bread (tharid)from you?

entries.farsi: كه مرا از غيب نادر هديه‏هاست / ‏كه بشر آن را نيارد نيز خواست
entries.english: I do not bid you bestow gifts on me; nay, I bid you be worthy of the gifts;

entries.farsi: من نمى‏گويم مرا هديه دهيد / بلكه گفتم اليق هديه شويد
entries.english: For I have rare gifts from the Unseen, which human beings dare not even ask for.

entries.farsi: مى‏پرستيد اخترى كاو زر كند / رو به او آريد كاو اختر كند
entries.english: You worship the star that makes gold: turn your faces towards Him that makes the star.

entries.farsi: آفتاب از امر حق طباخ ماست / ‏ابلهى باشد كه گوييم او خداست
entries.english: You worship the sun in heaven, having despised the Spirit of high price.

entries.farsi: مى‏پرستيد آفتاب چرخ را / خوار كرده جان عالى نرخ را
entries.english: The sun, by command of God, is our cook: it is folly that we should say it is God.

entries.farsi: آفتابت گر بگيرد چون كنى / ‏آن سياهى زو تو چون بيرون كنى
entries.english: If your sun be eclipsed, what will you do? How will you expel that blackness from it?

entries.farsi: نه به درگاه خدا آرى صداع / ‏كه سياهى را ببر وا ده شعاع
entries.english: Will not you bring your headache to the court of God, saying, ‘Take the blackness away, give back the radiance!’

entries.farsi: گر كشندت نيم شب خورشيد كو / تا بنالى يا امان خواهى از او
entries.english: If they would kill you at midnight, where is the sun, that you should wail and beg protection of it?

entries.farsi: حادثات اغلب به شب واقع شود / و آن زمان معبود تو غايب بود
entries.english: Calamities, for the most part, happen in the night; and at that time the object of your worship is absent.

entries.farsi: سوى حق گر ز آستانه خم شوى / ‏وا رهى از اختران محرم شوى
entries.english: If you sincerely bow to God, you will be delivered from the stars: you will become intimate.

entries.farsi: چون شوى محرم گشايم با تو لب / ‏تا ببينى آفتابى نيم شب
entries.english: When you become intimate, I will open my lips with you, that you may behold a Sun at midnight.

entries.farsi: جز روان پاك او را شرق نه / ‏در طلوعش روز و شب را فرق نه
entries.english: It has no location but the pure spirit: in its rising, there is no difference between day and night.

entries.farsi: روز آن باشد كه او شارق شود / شب نماند شب چو او بارق شود
entries.english: It is day when it rises; when it begins to shine, night is night no more.

entries.farsi: چون نمايد ذره پيش آفتاب / ‏همچنان است آفتاب اندر لباب
entries.english: As the mote appears in the presence of the sun, even such is the sun in the pure substance.

entries.farsi: آفتابى را كه رخشان مى‏شود / ديده پيشش كند و حيران مى‏شود
entries.english: The sun that becomes resplendent, and before which the sight is blunted and dazzled—

entries.farsi: همچو ذره بينى‏اش در نور عرش / ‏پيش نور بى‏حد موفور عرش
entries.english: You will see it as a mote in the light of the Divine Throne, beside the illimitable abounding light of the Divine Throne.

entries.farsi: خوار و مسكين بينى او را بى‏قرار / ديده را قوت شده از كردگار
entries.english: You will deem it base and lowly and impermanent, strength has come to your eye from the Creator.”

entries.farsi: كيميايى كه از او يك ما ثرى / ‏بر دخان افتاد گشت آن اخترى
entries.english: The Philosophers’ Stone from which a single impression fell on the vapour and it became a star;

entries.farsi: بو العجب ميناگرى كز يك عمل / ‏بست چندين خاصيت را بر زحل
entries.english: The unique elixir of which half a gleam struck upon darkness and made it the sun;

entries.farsi: نادر اكسيرى كه از وى نيم تاب / ‏بر ظالمى زد بكردش آفتاب
entries.english: The marvellous alchemist who by a single operation fastened all these properties on Saturn

entries.farsi: باقى اخترها و گوهرهاى جان / ‏هم بر اين مقياس اى طالب بدان
entries.english: Know, O seeker that the remaining planets and the spiritual substances are according to the same standard.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى حسى زبون آفتاب / ‏ديده‏ى ربانيى جو و بياب
entries.english: The sensuous eye is subject to the sun: seek and find a divine eye,

entries.farsi: تا زبون گردد به پيش آن نظر / شعشعات آفتاب با شرر
entries.english: In order that the beams of the flaming sun may become subject before that vision;

entries.farsi: كان نظر نورى و اين نارى بود / نار پيش نور بس تارى بود
entries.english: For that vision is luminous, while these are igneous: fire is very dark in comparison with light.

sections.title_fa: ‏كرامات و نور شيخ عبد اهلل مغربى قدس سره
sections.title_en: The miraculous gifts and illumination of Shaykh ‘Abdullah Maghribi, may God sanctify his spirit.

entries.farsi: گفت عبد اهلل شيخ مغربى / شصت سال از شب نديدم من شبى
entries.english: Shaykh ‘Abdullah Maghribi said, “During sixty years I never perceived in night the quality of night.

entries.farsi: من نديدم ظلمتى در شصت سال / ‏نه به روز و نه به شب نه ز اعتالل
entries.english: During sixty years I never experienced any darkness, neither by day nor by night nor from infirmity.”

entries.farsi: صوفيان گفتند صدق قال او / شب همى‏رفتيم در دنبال او
entries.english: The Sufis declared his words to be true: “During the night we would follow him

entries.farsi: در بيابانهاى پر از خار و گو / او چو ماه بدر ما را پيش رو
entries.english: Into deserts filled with thorns and ditches, he going in front of us like the full moon.

entries.farsi: روى پس ناكرده مى‏گفتى به شب / ‏هين گو آمد ميل كن در سوى چپ
entries.english: Without looking behind him, he would say, at night-time, ‘Listen! Here is a ditch: turn to the left!’

entries.farsi: باز گفتى بعد يك دم سوى راست / ‏ميل كن زيرا كه خارى پيش پاست
entries.english: Then, after a little while, he would say, ‘Turn to the right, because a thorn is before your feet.’

entries.farsi: روز گشتى پاش را ما پاى بوس / ‏گشته و پايش چو پاهاى عروس
entries.english: Day would break: we would come to kiss his foot, and his foot would be like the feet of a bride,

entries.farsi: مغربى را مشرقى كرده خداى / ‏كرده مغرب را چو مشرق نورزاى
entries.english: No trace of earth or mud on it, not a scratch from thorns or bruise from stones.”

entries.farsi: نه ز خاك و نه ز گل بر وى اثر نه از / خراش خار و آسيب حجر
entries.english: God made the Maghribi a Mashriqí: He made the place of sunset light-producing like the place of sunrise.

entries.farsi: نور اين شمس شموسى فارس است / روز خاص و عام را او حارس است
entries.english: The light of this one who belongs to the Sun of suns is riding: by day he is guarding high and low.

entries.farsi: چون نباشد حارس آن نور مجيد / كه هزاران آفتاب آرد پديد
entries.english: How should that glorious light, which brings thousands of suns into view, not be a guardian?

entries.farsi: تو به نور او همى‏رو در امان / ‏در ميان اژدها و كژدمان
entries.english: By his light do you walk always in safety amidst dragons and scorpions.

entries.farsi: پيش پيشت مى‏رود آن نور پاك / ‏مى‏كند هر ره زنى را چاك چاك
entries.english: That holy light is going in front of you and tearing every highwayman to pieces.

entries.farsi: يوم ال يخزى النَّبىّ راست دان / ‏نور يسعى بين ايديهم بخوان
entries.english: Know rightly on the Day when He will not put the Prophet to shame; read light shall run before them.

entries.farsi: گر چه گردد در قيامت آن فزون / ‏از خدا اينجا بخواهيد آزمون
entries.english: Although that will be increased at the Resurrection, beg of God trial here;

entries.farsi: كاو ببخشد هم به ميغ و هم به ماغ / ‏نور جان و اهلل اعلم بالبالغ
entries.english: For He bestows spiritual light both on cloud and mist, and God best knows how to impart.

sections.title_fa: باز گردانيدن سليمان عليه السلام رسوالن بلقيس را با آن هديه‏ها كه آورده بودند سوى بلقيس و ‏دعوت‏كردن بلقيس را به ايمان و ترك آفتاب پرستى
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, bade the envoys of Bilqis return to her with the gifts which they had brought; and how he called Bilqis to the Faith and to abandon sun-worship.

entries.farsi: باز گرديد اى رسوالن خجل / ‏زر شما را دل به من آريد دل
entries.english: “O shamefaced envoys turn back! The gold is yours: bring unto me the heart, the heart!

entries.farsi: اين زر من بر سر آن زر نهيد / كورى تن فرج استر را دهيد
entries.english: Lay this gold of mine on the top of that gold: tie all the gold to a mule’s vulva.”

entries.farsi: فرج استر اليق حلقه‏ى زر است / ‏زر عاشق روى زرد اصفر است
entries.english: The mule’s vulva is worthy of the gold ring; the lover’s gold is the pallid yellow countenance;

entries.farsi: كه نظرگاه خداوند است آن / كز نظر انداز خورشيد است كان
entries.english: For that is the object of the Lord’s regard, while the mine results from the sun’s casting looks.

entries.farsi: كو نظرگاه شعاع آفتاب / ‏كو نظرگاه خداوند لباب
entries.english: How can the sunbeams’ object of regard be compared with an object of regard to the Lord of the quintessence’?

entries.farsi: از گرفت من ز جان اسپر كنيد / گر چه اكنون هم گرفتار منيد
entries.english: “Make of your souls a shield against my taking captive, though you are my captives even now.”

entries.farsi: مرغ فتنه‏ى دانه بر بام است او / پر گشاده بسته‏ى دام است او
entries.english: The bird tempted by the bait is on the roof: with wings outspread, it is imprisoned in the trap.

entries.farsi: چون به دانه داد او دل را به جان / ‏ناگرفته مر و را بگرفته دان
entries.english: Inasmuch as with its soul it has given its heart to the bait, deem it caught, though apparently it is still free.

entries.farsi: آن نظرها كه به دانه مى‏كند / آن گره دان كاو به پا بر مى‏زند
entries.english: Deem the looks which it is directing to the bait to be the knot that it is tying on its legs.

entries.farsi: دانه گويد گر تو مى‏دزدى نظر / من همى‏دزدم ز تو صبر و مقر
entries.english: The bait says, “If you are stealing your looks I am stealing patience and constancy from you.

entries.farsi: چون كشيدت آن نظر اندر پى‏ام / ‏پس بدانى كز تو من غافل نى‏ام
entries.english: When those looks have drawn you after me, then you will know that I am not inattentive to you.”

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى عطارى كه سنگ ترازوى او گل سر شوى بود و دزديدن مشترى گل خوار از آن گل هنگام سنجيدن شكر
sections.title_en: Story of the druggist whose balance-weight was clay for washing the head; and how a customer, who was a clay-eater, stole some of that clay covertly and secretly, whilst sugar was being weighed.

entries.farsi: پيش عطارى يكى گل خوار رفت / ‏تا خرد ابلوج قند خاص زفت
entries.english: A certain clay-eater went to a druggist to buy fine hard sugar-loaf.

entries.farsi: پس بر عطار طرار دو دل / ‏موضع سنگ ترازو بود گل
entries.english: Now, at the druggist’s, a crafty vigilant man, in place of the balance-weight there was clay.

entries.farsi: گفت گل سنگ ترازوى من است / ‏گر ترا ميل شكر بخريدن است
entries.english: He said, “If you want to buy sugar, my balance-weight is clay.”

entries.farsi: گفت هستم در مهمى قند جو / سنگ ميزان هر چه خواهى باش گو
entries.english: He said, “I am requiring sugar for an urgent affair: let the balance-weight be whatever you wish.”

entries.farsi: گفت با خود پيش آن كه گل خور است / ‏سنگ چه بود گل نكوتر از زر است
entries.english: To himself he said, “What does the weight matter to one that eats clay? Clay is better than gold.”

entries.farsi: همچو آن دالله كه گفت اى پسر / نو عروسى يافتم بس خوب فر
entries.english: As the go-between (dallala) who said, “O son, I have found a very beautiful new bride.

entries.farsi: سخت زيبا ليك هم يك چيز هست / ‏كان ستيره دختر حلواگر است
entries.english: Exceedingly pretty, but there is just one thing, that the lady is a confectioner’s daughter.”

entries.farsi: گفت بهتر اين چنين خود گر بود / دختر او چرب و شيرين‏تر بود
entries.english: “Better,” said he; “if it is indeed so, his daughter will be fatter and sweeter.”

entries.farsi: گر ندارى سنگ و سنگت از گل است / ‏اين به و به گل مرا ميوه‏ى دل است
entries.english: “If you have no weight and your weight is of clay, this is better and better: clay is the fruit of my heart.”

entries.farsi: اندر آن كفه‏ى ترازو ز اعتداد / او بجاى سنگ آن گل را نهاد
entries.english: He placed the clay, because of its being ready, in one scale of the balance instead of the weight;

entries.farsi: پس براى كفه‏ى ديگر به دست / ‏هم به قدر آن شكر را مى‏شكست
entries.english: Then, for the other scale, he was breaking with his hand the equivalent amount of sugar.

entries.farsi: چون نبودش تيشه‏اى او دير ماند / مشترى را منتظر آن جا نشاند
entries.english: Since he had no pick-axe, he took a long time and made the customer sit waiting.

entries.farsi: رويش آن سو بود، گل خور ناشكفت / ‏گل از او پوشيده دزديدن گرفت
entries.english: His face was towards that, the clay-eater, unable to restrain himself, began covertly to steal the clay from him,

entries.farsi: ترس ترسان كه نيايد ناگهان / ‏چشم او بر من فتد از امتحان
entries.english: Terribly frightened lest his eye should fall upon him of a sudden for the purpose of testing

entries.farsi: ديد عطار آن و خود مشغول كرد / كه فزون‏تر دزد هين اى روى زرد
entries.english: The druggist saw it, but made himself busy, saying, “Come, steal more, O pale-faced one!

entries.farsi: گر بدزدى و ز گل من مى‏برى / ‏رو كه هم از پهلوى خود مى‏خورى
entries.english: If you will be a thief and take some of my clay, go on, for you are eating out of your own side.

entries.farsi: تو همى‏ترسى ز من ليك از خرى / ‏من همى‏ترسم كه تو كمتر خورى
entries.english: You are afraid of me, but because you are an ass: I am afraid you will eat less.

entries.farsi: گر چه مشغولم چنان احمق نيم / كه شكر افزون كشى تو از نى‏ام
entries.english: Though I am occupied, I am not such a fool that you should get too much of my sugar-cane.

entries.farsi: چون ببينى مر شكر را ز آزمود / پس بدانى احمق و غافل كه بود
entries.english: When you see by experience the sugar, then you will know who was foolish and careless.”

entries.farsi: مرغ ز آن دانه نظر خوش مى‏كند / دانه هم از دور راهش مى‏زند
entries.english: The bird looks pleased at the bait; still, the bait, at a distance, is waylaying it.

entries.farsi: كز زناى چشم حظى مى‏برى / ‏نه كباب از پهلوى خود مى‏خورى
entries.english: If you are deriving some pleasure from the eye’s greed, are not you eating roast-meat from your own side?

entries.farsi: اين نظر از دور چون تير است و سم / ‏عشقت افزون مى‏شود صبر تو كم
entries.english: This looking from a distance is like arrows and poison: your fond passion is increased and your self-restraint diminished.

entries.farsi: مال دنيا دام مرغان ضعيف / ‏ملك عقبى دام مرغان شريف
entries.english: Worldly riches are a trap for the weak birds; the kingdom of the next world is a trap for the noble birds,

entries.farsi: تا بدين ملكى كه او دامى است ژرف / ‏در شكار آرند مرغان شگرف
entries.english: To the end that by means of this kingdom, which is a deep trap, the great birds may be ensnared.

entries.farsi: من سليمان مى‏نخواهم ملكتان / ‏بلكه من برهانم از هر هلكتان
entries.english: “I, Solomon, do not desire your kingdom; nay, but I will deliver you from every destruction;

entries.farsi: كاين زمان هستيد خود مملوك ملك / ‏مالك ملك آن كه بجهيد او ز هلك
entries.english: For at this time you are indeed slaves to the kingdom; the owner of the kingdom is he that escaped from destruction.”

entries.farsi: اى تو بنده‏ى اين جهان محبوس جان / ‏چند گويى خويش را خواجه‏ى جهان
entries.english: Preposterously, O prisoner of this world, you have named yourself prince of this world.

entries.farsi: باژگونه اى اسير اين جهان / ‏نام خود كردى امير اين جهان
entries.english: O you slave of this world, you whose spirit is imprisoned, how long will you call yourself lord of the world?

sections.title_fa: دل دارى كردن و نواختن سليمان عليه السلام مر آن رسوالن را و دفع وحشت و آزار از دل ايشان و ‏عذر قبول ناكردن هديه شرح كردن با ايشان
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, showed affection and kindness to the envoys and removed resentment and injury from their hearts and explained to them the reason for declining the gift.

entries.farsi: اى رسوالن مى‏فرستمتان رسول / رد من بهتر شما را از قبول
entries.english: “O envoys, I will send you as envoys: my refusal is better for you than acceptance.

entries.farsi: تا بداند كه به زر طامع نه‏ايم / ‏ما زر از زر آفرين آورده‏ايم
entries.english: Relate to Bilqis what marvellous things you have seen concerning the desert of gold,

entries.farsi: پيش بلقيس آن چه ديديد از عجب / ‏باز گوييد از بيابان ذهب
entries.english: That she may know we do not covet gold: we have gotten gold from the gold-Creator,

entries.farsi: آن كه گر خواهد همه خاك زمين / ‏سر به سر زر گردد و در ثمين
entries.english: At whose will the whole earth’s soil from end to end would become gold and precious pearls.”

entries.farsi: حق براى آن كند اى زر گزين / ‏روز محشر اين زمين را نقره‏گين
entries.english: On that account, O you who choose gold, God will make this earth silver on the Day of Resurrection.

entries.farsi: فارغيم از زر كه ما بس پر فنيم / ‏خاكيان را سر به سر زرين كنيم
entries.english: “We have no need of gold, for we are very skilful: we make earthly beings entirely golden.

entries.farsi: از شما كى كديه‏ى زر مى‏كنيم / ‏ما شما را كيمياگر مى‏كنيم
entries.english: How shall we beg gold of you? We make you alchemists.

entries.farsi: ترك آن گيريد گر ملك سباست / ‏كه برون آب و گل بس ملكهاست
entries.english: Abandon that, if it is the kingdom of Saba, for beyond water and earth there are many kingdoms.”

entries.farsi: تخته بند است آن كه تختش خوانده‏اى / صدر پندارى و بر در مانده‏اى
entries.english: That which you have called a throne is a splint- bandage: you deem the seat of honour, but you have remained at the door.

entries.farsi: پادشاهى نيستت بر ريش خود / پادشاهى چون كنى بر نيك و بد
entries.english: You have not sovereignty over your own beard, how will you exercise sovereignty over good and evil?

entries.farsi: بى‏مراد تو شود ريشت سپيد / شرم دار از ريش خود اى كژ اميد
entries.english: Without your wish, your beard grows white: be ashamed of your bend, O you whose hopes are perverse.

entries.farsi: مالك الملك است هر كش سر نهد / بى‏جهان خاك صد ملكش دهد
entries.english: He is the Possessor of the Kingdom: whoever lays his head before Him, to him He gives a hundred kingdoms without the terrestrial world;

entries.farsi: ليك ذوق سجده‏اى پيش خدا / خوشتر آيد از دو صد دولت ترا
entries.english: But the savour of a single prostration before God will be sweeter to you than two hundred empires:

entries.farsi: پس بنالى كه نخواهم ملكها / ملك آن سجده مسلم كن مرا
entries.english: Then you will cry, “I desire not kingdoms: commit unto me the kingdom of that prostration.”

entries.farsi: پادشاهان جهان از بد رگى / ‏بو نبردند از شراب بندگى
entries.english: The kings of the world, because of their evil nature, got no scent of the wine of service;

entries.farsi: ور نه ادهم‏وار سر گردان و دنگ / ‏ملك را بر هم زدندى بى‏درنگ
entries.english: Otherwise, dizzy and dumbfounded like Adham, without delay they would have dashed their sovereignty to pieces.

entries.farsi: ليك حق بهر ثبات اين جهان / ‏مهرشان بنهاد بر چشم و دهان
entries.english: But, for the maintenance of this world, God set a seal upon their eyes and mouths,

entries.farsi: تا شود شيرين بر ايشان تخت و تاج / ‏كه ستانيم از جهان داران خراج
entries.english: To the end that throne and crown should be sweet to them, “for” “we will exact tribute from the rulers of the world.”

entries.farsi: از خراج ار جمع آرى زر چو ريگ / ‏آخر آن از تو بماند مرده ريگ
entries.english: If by way of tribute you amass gold as sand, at last it will be left behind you as an inheritance.

entries.farsi: همره جانت نگردد ملك و زر / زر بده سرمه ستان بهر نظر
entries.english: Sovereignty and gold will not accompany your spirit on its journey: give your gold away, get eye salve for your sight,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى كاين جهان چاهى است تنگ / ‏يوسفانه آن رسن آرى به چنگ
entries.english: In order that you may see that this world is a narrow well, and that, like Joseph, you may grasp that rope,

entries.farsi: تا بگويد چون ز چاه آيى به بام / ‏جان كه يا بشراى هذا لى غالم
entries.english: So that, when you come from the well to the roof, the Soul will say, “Oh, good news for me! This is a youth for me.”

entries.farsi: هست در چاه انعكاسات نظر / كمترين آن كه نمايد سنگ زر
entries.english: In the well there are optical inversions, the least that stones appear to be gold.

entries.farsi: وقت بازى كودكان را ز اختالل / ‏مى‏نمايد آن خزفها زر و مال
entries.english: To children at play-time, from infirmity, those potsherds appear to be gold and riches.

entries.farsi: عارفانش كيمياگر گشته‏اند / تا كه شد كانها بر ايشان نژند
entries.english: His gnostics have become alchemists, so that mines have become worthless in their eyes.

sections.title_fa: ديدن درويش جماعت مشايخ را در خواب و در خواست كردن روزى حالل بى‏مشغول شدن به كسب و از ‏عبادت ماندن و ارشاد ايشان او را و ميوه‏هاى تلخ و ترش كوهى بر وى شيرين شدن به داد آن مشايخ
sections.title_en: How a dervish saw in dream a company of Shaykhs and begged for a daily portion of lawful food

without being occupied with earning and being incapacitated from devotional service; and how they directed him, and how the sour and bitter mountain-fruit became sweet to him through the bounty of those Shaykhs.

entries.farsi: آن يكى درويش گفت اندر سمر / خضريان را من بديدم خواب در
entries.english: A certain dervish said in the night-talk, “I saw in dream those connected with Khizr.

entries.farsi: گفتم ايشان را كه روزى حالل / ‏از كجا نوشم كه نبود آن وبال
entries.english: I said to them, ‘Where shall I eat a daily portion of lawful food that is not harmful?’

entries.farsi: مر مرا سوى كهستان راندند / ميوه‏ها ز آن بيشه مى‏افشاندند
entries.english: They took me along towards the mountainous country: they were shaking down the fruit from the forest,

entries.farsi: كه خدا شيرين بكرد آن ميوه را / در دهان تو به همتهاى ما
entries.english: Saying, ‘God has made the fruit sweet in your mouth because of our benedictions.

entries.farsi: هين بخور پاك و حالل و بى‏حساب / ‏بى‏صداع و نقل و بالا و نشيب
entries.english: Come, eat clean and lawful, and free of reckoning, without trouble and change of place and up and down.’

entries.farsi: پس مرا ز آن رزق نطقى رو نمود / ذوق گفت من خردها مى‏ربود
entries.english: Then from that daily provision there appeared in me a speech: savour of my words was transporting minds.

entries.farsi: گفتم اين فتنه‏ست اى رب جهان / ‏بخششى ده از همه خلقان نهان
entries.english: I said, ‘this is a temptation: O Lord of the world, bestow a gift hidden from all creatures!’

entries.farsi: شد سخن از من دل خوش يافتم / ‏چون انار از ذوق مى‏بشكافتم
entries.english: Speech departed from me; I gained a joyous heart: I was bursting with rapture, like the pomegranate;

entries.farsi: گفتم ار چيزى نباشد در بهشت / ‏غير اين شادى كه دارم در سرشت
entries.english: I said, ‘If there be nothing in Paradise but this delight which I have within my nature,

entries.farsi: هيچ نعمت آرزو نايد دگر / زين نپردازم به جوز و نيشكر
entries.english: No other blessing will be desired: I will not be diverted from this by the houris and sugar-cane.’

entries.farsi: مانده بود از كسب يك دو حبه‏ام / ‏دوخته در آستين جبه‏ام
entries.english: Of my earnings one or two small pieces had remained with me, sewn in the sleeve of my jacket (jubba).

sections.title_fa: نيت كردن او كه اين زر بدهم بدان هيزم كش چون من روزى يافتم به كرامات مشايخ و رنجيدن آن هيزم كش از ضمير و نيت او
sections.title_en: How he formed an intention, saying, ‘I will give this money to that carrier of firewood,
since I have obtained daily provision through the miraculous gifts of the Shaykhs’; and how the carrier of firewood was offended by his secret thought and intention.

entries.farsi: آن يكى درويش هيزم مى‏كشيد / خسته و مانده ز بيشه در رسيد
entries.english: A poor man was carrying firewood: he approached, weary and exhausted, from the forest.

entries.farsi: پس بگفتم من ز روزى فارغم / زين سپس از بهر رزقم نيست غم
entries.english: So I said, ‘I am independent of daily bread: henceforth I have no anxiety for the daily portion.

entries.farsi: ميوه‏ى مكروه بر من خوش شده است / ‏رزق خاصى جسم را آمد به دست
entries.english: The loathed fruit has become sweet to me: a special provision for my body has come to hand.

entries.farsi: چون كه من فارغ شده‏ستم از گلو / حبه اى چند است اين بدهم بدو
entries.english: Since I have been freed from the gullet, here are some small pieces of money: I will give him these.

entries.farsi: بدهم اين زر را بدين تكليف كش / ‏تا دو سه روزك شود از قوت خوش
entries.english: I will give this money to this toil-worn man, that for two or three brief days he may be made happy by food.’

entries.farsi: خود ضميرم را همى‏دانست او / ز انكه سمعش داشت نور از شمع هو
entries.english: He knew my thoughts, because his hearing had illumination from the candle of Hu.

entries.farsi: بود پيشش سر هر انديشه‏اى / ‏چون چراغى در درون شيشه‏اى
entries.english: To him the secret of every thought was as a lamp within a glass.

entries.farsi: هيچ پنهان مى‏نشد از وى ضمير / ‏بود بر مضمون دلها او امير
entries.english: No mental conception was hidden from him: he was ruler over the contents of hearts.

entries.farsi: كه چنين انديشى از بهر ملوك / كيف تلقى الرزق ان لم يرزقوك
entries.english: Therefore that wondrous man was muttering to himself under his breath in answer to my thought,

entries.farsi: پس همى‏منگيد با خود زير لب / ‏در جواب فكرتم آن بو العجب
entries.english: ‘You think so concerning the kings: how should you meet the daily provision unless they provide you?’

entries.farsi: من نمى‏كردم سخن را فهم ليك / ‏بر دلم مى‏زد عتابش نيك نيك
entries.english: I did not understand his words, but his rebuke smote my heart mightily.

entries.farsi: سوى من آمد به هيبت همچو شير / تنگ هيزم را ز خود بنهاد زير
entries.english: He approached me with awful manner, like a lion, and laid down his bundle of firewood.

entries.farsi: پرتو حالى كه او هيزم نهاد / لرزه بر هر هفت عضو من فتاد
entries.english: The influence of the ecstatic state in which he laid down the firewood, a trembling fell upon all my seven limbs.

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب گر ترا خاصان هى‏اند / كه مبارك دعوت و فرخ پى‏اند
entries.english: He said, ‘O Lord, if You have elect ones whose prayers are blessed and whose feet are auspicious,

entries.farsi: لطف تو خواهم كه ميناگر شود / اين زمان اين تنگ هيزم زر شود
entries.english: I entreat that Your grace may become an alchemist and that this bundle of firewood may be turned into gold at this moment.’

entries.farsi: در زمان ديدم كه زر شد هيزمش / ‏همچو آتش بر زمين مى‏تافت خوش
entries.english: Immediately I saw that his firewood was turned into gold, gleaming brightly on the ground, like fire.

entries.farsi: من در آن بى‏خود شدم تا دير گه / ‏چون كه با خويش آمدم من از وله
entries.english: Thereat I became beside myself for a long while. When I came to myself out of bewilderment,

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفت اى خدا گر آن كبار / بس غيورند و گريزان ز اشتهار
entries.english: He said afterwards, ‘O God, if those great ones are very jealous and are fleeing from celebrity,

entries.farsi: باز اين را بند هيزم ساز زود / بى‏توقف هم بر آن حالى كه بود
entries.english: At once, without delay, make this a bundle of firewood again, just as it was.’

entries.farsi: بعد از آن برداشت هيزم را و رفت / ‏سوى شهر از پيش من او تيز و تفت
entries.english: Immediately those branches of gold turned into firewood: the intellect and the sight were intoxicated at his work.

entries.farsi: در زمان هيزم شد آن اغصان زر / مست شد در كار او عقل و نظر
entries.english: After that, he took up the firewood and went from me in hot haste towards the town.

entries.farsi: خواستم تا در پى آن شه روم / ‏پرسم از وى مشكالت و بشنوم
entries.english: I wished to follow that king and ask him about difficulties and hear;

entries.farsi: بسته كرد آن هيبت او مر مرا / پيش خاصان ره نباشد عامه را
entries.english: the awe made me bound: the ordinary have no way to the presence of the elect.”

entries.farsi: ور كسى را ره شود گو سر فشان / ‏كان بود از رحمت و از جذبشان
entries.english: And if the way become to any one, let him offer his head, for that comes from their mercy and their drawing.

entries.farsi: نه چو آن ابله كه يابد قرب شاه / ‏سهل و آسان در فتد آن دم ز راه
entries.english: Therefore, when you gain companionship with the siddíq, deem that Divine favour to be a precious opportunity.

entries.farsi: پس غنيمت دار آن توفيق را / چون بيابى صحبت صديق را
entries.english: Not like the fool who wins the favour of the King, and then lightly and easily falls away from the path.

entries.farsi: چون ز قربانى دهندش بيشتر / پس بگويد ران گاو است اين مگر
entries.english: When more of favour is bestowed on him, then he says, “Surely this is the thigh of an ox.”

entries.farsi: بذل شاهانه‏ست اين بى‏رشوتى / ‏بخشش محض است اين از رحمتى
entries.english: This does not consist of the thigh of an ox, O deviser of falsehood: to you it appears to be the thigh of an ox because you are an ass.

entries.farsi: نيست اين از ران گاو اى مفترى / ران گاوت مى‏نمايد از خرى
entries.english: This is a royal gift devoid of any corruption: this is pure kindness from a mercy,

sections.title_fa: ‏تحريض سليمان عليه السلام مر رسوالن را بر تعجيل هجرت بلقيس بهر ايمان
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, urged the envoys to hasten the emigration of Bilqis for the Faith’s sake.

entries.farsi: همچنان كه شه سليمان در نبرد / جذب خيل و لشكر بلقيس كرد
entries.english: Even as in warfare King Solomon drew the cavalry and foot-soldiers of Bilqis,

entries.farsi: سوى ساحل مى‏فشاند بى‏خطر / جوش موجش هر زمانى صد گهر
entries.english: Saying, “O honoured men, come quickly, quickly, for the waves have risen from the sea of bounty.

entries.farsi: كه بياييد اى عزيزان زود زود / كه بر آمد موجها از بحر جود
entries.english: At every moment the surge of its waves is scattering shoreward a hundred pearls without danger.

entries.farsi: الصال گفتيم اى اهل رشاد / كاين زمان رضوان در جنت گشاد
entries.english: We cry welcome, O people of righteousness, for now Rizwan has opened the gate of Paradise.”

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت اى پيكان رويد / سوى بلقيس و بدين دين بگرويد
entries.english: Then Solomon said, “O couriers, go to Bilqis and believe in this Religion.

entries.farsi: هين بيا اى طالب دولت شتاب / ‏كه فتوح است اين زمان و فتح باب
entries.english: Then bid her come hither with all speed, for truly God invites to the peace.”

entries.farsi: پس بگوييدش بيا اينجا تمام / ‏زود كه ان اللهَّ يدعو بالسلام
entries.english: Listen, come speedily, O seeker of happiness, for now is manifestations and the opening of the door.

entries.farsi: اى كه تو طالب نه اى تو هم بيا / تا طلب يابى ازين يار وفا
entries.english: O you who are not a seeker, come you also, that you may gain seeking from this faithful Friend.

sections.title_fa: ‏سبب هجرت ابراهيم ادهم قدس اللهَّ سره و ترك ملك خراسان
sections.title_en: The cause of the emigration of Adham, may God sanctify his spirit, and his abandoning the kingdom of Khurasan.

entries.farsi: ملك بر هم زن تو ادهم‏وار زود / تا بيابى همچو او ملك خلود
entries.english: Quickly dash to pieces the kingdom, Adham, that like him you may gain the kingdom of everlasting life.

entries.farsi: خفته بود آن شه شبانه بر سرير / حارسان بر بام اندر دار و گير
entries.english: At night that king was asleep on his throne, on the roof the guards were exercising authority.

entries.farsi: قصد شه از حارسان آن هم نبود / كه كند ز آن دفع دزدان و رنود
entries.english: The king’s purpose in the guards was not that he might thereby keep off robbers and ne’er-do-wells.

entries.farsi: او همى‏دانست كان كاو عادل است / ‏فارغ است از واقعه ايمن دل است
entries.english: He knew that the man who is just is free from attack and secure in his heart.

entries.farsi: عدل باشد پاسبان كامها / نه به شب چوبك زنان بر بامها
entries.english: Justice is the guardian of pleasures; not men who beat their rattles on the roofs at night.

entries.farsi: ليك بد مقصودش از بانگ رباب / ‏همچو مشتاقان خيال آن خطاب
entries.english: But his object in the sound of the rebeck was, like ardent lovers, the phantasy of that allocution;

entries.farsi: ناله‏ى سرنا و تهديد دهل / ‏چيزكى ماند بدان ناقور كل
entries.english: The shrill noise of the clarion and the menace of the drum somewhat resemble that universal trumpet.

entries.farsi: پس حكيمان گفته‏اند اين لحنها / از دوار چرخ بگرفتيم ما
entries.english: Hence philosophers have said that we received these harmonies from the revolution of the sphere,

entries.farsi: بانگ گردشهاى چرخ است اين كه خلق / ‏مى‏سرايندش به طنبور و به حلق
entries.english: This which people sing with guitar and throat is the sound of the revolutions of the sphere;

entries.farsi: مومنان گويند كاثار بهشت / ‏نغز گردانيد هر آواز زشت
entries.english: The true believers say that the influences of Paradise made every unpleasant sound to be beautiful.

entries.farsi: ما همه اجزاى آدم بوده‏ايم / ‏در بهشت آن لحنها بشنوده‏ايم
entries.english: We all have been parts of Adam; we have heard those melodies in Paradise.

entries.farsi: ليك چون آميخت با خاك كرب / ‏كى دهند اين زير و اين بم آن طرب
entries.english: Although the water and earth have caused a doubt to fall upon us, something of those comes to our memory;

entries.farsi: گر چه بر ما ريخت آب و گل شكى / ‏يادمان آمد از آنها چيزكى
entries.english: But since it is mingled with the earth of sorrow, how should this treble and bass give the same delight?

entries.farsi: آب چون آميخت با بول و گميز / گشت ز آميزش مزاجش تلخ و تيز
entries.english: When water is mingled with horse and camel urine, its temperament is made bitter and acid by the commixture.

entries.farsi: چيزكى از آب هستش در جسد / بول گيرش آتشى را مى‏كشد
entries.english: There is a small quantity of water in his body: suppose it is urine, it will extinguish a fire.

entries.farsi: گر نجس شد آب اين طبعش بماند / كاتش غم را به طبع خود نشاند
entries.english: If the water has been defiled, this natural property of it remains, for by its nature it allays the fire of grief.

entries.farsi: پس غذاى عاشقان آمد سماع / ‏كه در او باشد خيال اجتماع
entries.english: Therefore sema is the food of lovers, since therein is the phantasy of composure.

entries.farsi: قوتى گيرد خيالات ضمير / بلكه صورت گردد از بانگ و صفير
entries.english: From sounds and piping the mental images gather strength; no, they become forms.

entries.farsi: آتش عشق از نواها گشت تيز / آن چنان كه آتش آن جوز ريز
entries.english: The fire of love is made keen by melodies, just as the fire of the man who dropped walnuts.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن مرد تشنه كه از سر جوز بن جوز مى‏ريخت در جوى آب كه در گو بود و به آب نمى‏رسيد تا به افتادن جوز بانگ آب بشنود و او را چون سماع خوش بانگ آب اندر طرب مى‏آورد
sections.title_en: Story of the thirsty man who dropped walnuts from the top of a walnut-tree into the water-brook
that was in the hollow, without reaching the water, in order that he might hear the sound made by the walnuts falling on the water, which thrilled him with joy as sweet music.

entries.farsi: در نغولى بود آب آن تشنه راند / بر درخت جوز جوزى مى‏فشاند
entries.english: The water was in a deep place: the thirsty man went up the tree and scattered the walnuts one by one.

entries.farsi: مى‏فتاد از جوز بن جوز اندر آب / ‏بانگ مى‏آمد همى‏ديد او حباب
entries.english: The walnuts were falling from the walnut-tree into the water: the sound was coming, and he was seeing the bubbles.

entries.farsi: عاقلى گفتش كه بگذار اى فتى / جوزها خود تشنگى آرد ترا
entries.english: A sensible person said to him, “Leave off, O youth: truly the walnuts will bring thirst to you.

entries.farsi: بيشتر در آب مى‏افتد ثمر / آب در پستى است از تو دور در
entries.english: The more the fruit falls into the water— the water is below at a distance from you,

entries.farsi: تا تو از بالا فرو آيى به زور / آب جويش برده باشد تا به دور
entries.english: The river-water will have carried it far away before you with effort come down from the top.”

entries.farsi: گفت قصدم زين فشاندن جوز نيست / ‏تيزتر بنگر بر اين ظاهر مه‏ايست
entries.english: He replied, “My purpose in this scattering is not the walnuts: look more keenly, do not stop at this superficial.

entries.farsi: قصد من آن است كايد بانگ آب / ‏هم ببينم بر سر آب اين حباب
entries.english: My purpose is that the sound of the water should come; also, that I should see these bubbles on the surface of the water.”

entries.farsi: تشنه را خود شغل چه بود در جهان / ‏گرد پاى حوض گشتن جاودان
entries.english: What, indeed, is the thirsty man’s business in the world? To circle for ever round the base of the tank,

entries.farsi: گرد جو و گرد آب و بانگ آب / ‏همچو حاجى طايف كعبه‏ى صواب
entries.english: Round the channel and round the Water and the sound of the Water, like a pilgrim circumambulating the Ka‘ba of Truth

entries.farsi: همچنان مقصود من زين مثنوى / ‏اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين توى
entries.english: Even so, in this Masnavi you, O Ziya ’l-Haqq Husamu’din, are my object.

entries.farsi: مثنوى اندر فروع و در اصول / جمله آن تست كرده ستى قبول
entries.english: The whole Masnasvi in its branches and roots is yours: you have accepted.

entries.farsi: در قبول آرند شاهان نيك و بد / چون قبول آرند نبود بيش رد
entries.english: Kings accept good and bad: when they accept, it is reprobate no more.

entries.farsi: چون نهالى كاشتى آبش بده / ‏چون گشادش داده‏اى بگشا گره
entries.english: Since you have planted the sapling, give it water. Since you have given it freedom, untie the knots.

entries.farsi: قصدم از الفاظ او راز تو است / ‏قصدم از انشايش آواز تو است
entries.english: In its expressions my object is your mystery; in composing it my object is your voice.

entries.farsi: پيش من آوازت آواز خداست / عاشق از معشوق حاشا كه جداست
entries.english: To me your voice is the voice of God: Heaven forbid that the lover is separate from the Beloved.

entries.farsi: اتصالى بى‏تكيف بى‏قياس / هست رب الناس را با جان ناس
entries.english: There is a union beyond description or analogy between the Lord of Man and the spirit of Man.

entries.farsi: ليك گفتم ناس من نسناس نى / ‏ناس غير جان جان اشناس نى
entries.english: But I said nas, not nasnas; nas is none but the spirit that knows the Spirit.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ خوانده‏اى / ‏ليك جسمى در تجزى مانده‏اى
entries.english: Nas is Man, and where is Manhood? You have never seen the head of Man: you are a tail.

entries.farsi: ناس مردم باشد و كو مردمى / ‏تو سر مردم نديده ستى دمى
entries.english: You have recited you didst not throw when you threw but you are a body: you have remained in division.

entries.farsi: ملك جسمت را چو بلقيس اى غبى / ‏ترك كن بهر سليمان نبى
entries.english: Like Bilqís, O foolish one, abandon the kingdom of your body for the sake of the prophet Solomon.

entries.farsi: مى‏كنم ال حول نه از گفت خويش / ‏بلكه از وسواس آن انديشه كيش
entries.english: I am crying “la hawl,” not on account of my own words, nay, but on account of the false suggestions of the person accustomed to think,

entries.farsi: كاو خيالى مى‏كند در گفت من / ‏در دل از وسواس و انكارات ظن
entries.english: Who is conceiving in his heart a vain fancy about my words, from the false suggestions and incredulities of thought?

entries.farsi: مى‏كنم ال حول يعنى چاره نيست / ‏چون ترا در دل به ضدم گفتنى است
entries.english: I am crying “la hawl,” that is, “there is no help,” because in your heart there is a contradiction of me.

entries.farsi: چون كه گفت من گرفتت در گلو / من خمش كردم تو آن خود بگو
entries.english: Since my words have stuck in your throat, I am silent: do you speak your own words.

entries.farsi: آن يكى نايى خوش نى مى‏زدست / ‏ناگهان از مقعدش بادى بجست
entries.english: A sweet flute-player was playing the flute: when he suddenly farted.

entries.farsi: ناى را بر كون نهاد او كه ز من / ‏گر تو بهتر مى‏زنى بستان بزن
entries.english: He put the ney to his ass, saying, “If you play better than I, take it and play!”

entries.farsi: اى مسلمان خود ادب اندر طلب / ‏نيست الا حمل از هر بى‏ادب
entries.english: O Musalman, in the quest, good manners is indeed nothing but forbearance with every one that is unmannerly.

entries.farsi: هر كه را بينى شكايت مى‏كند / كه فالن كس راست طبع و خوى بد
entries.english: When you see any one complaining of such and such a person’s ill-nature and bad temper,

entries.farsi: اين شكايت گر، بدان كه بد خو است / ‏كه مر آن بد خوى را او بد گو است
entries.english: Know that the complainant is bad-tempered, forasmuch as he speaks ill of that bad-tempered person,

entries.farsi: ز انكه خوش خو آن بود كاو در خمول / ‏باشد از بد خو و بد طبعان حمول
entries.english: Because he is good-tempered who is quietly forbearing towards the bad-tempered and ill-natured

entries.farsi: ليك در شيخ آن گله ز امر خداست / ‏نه پى خشم و ممارات و هواست
entries.english: But in the Shaykh, the complaint is by the command of God; it is not in consequence of anger and contentiousness and vain desire.

entries.farsi: آن شكايت نيست هست اصالح جان / ‏چون شكايت كردن پيغمبران
entries.english: It is not a complaint; it is spiritual correction, like the complaints made by the prophets.

entries.farsi: ناحمولى انبيا از امر دان / ‏ور نه حمال است بد را حلمشان
entries.english: Know that the intolerance of the prophets is by command; otherwise, their clemency is exceedingly tolerant of evil.

entries.farsi: اى سليمان در ميان زاغ و باز / حلم حق شو با همه مرغان بساز
entries.english: They mortified their nature in toleration of evil; if there be intolerance, it is Divine.

entries.farsi: طبع را كشتند در حمل بدى / ‏ناحمولى گر بود هست ايزدى
entries.english: O Solomon, amidst the crows and falcons is you the clemency of God: consort with all the birds.

entries.farsi: اى دو صد بلقيس حلمت را زبون / ‏كه اهد قومى انهم ال يعلمون
entries.english: Oh, two hundred Bilqís are abased before your clemency, for, “guide my people, truly they know not.”

sections.title_fa: ‏تهديد فرستادن سليمان عليه السلام پيش بلقيس كه اصرار مينديش بر شرك و تاخير مكن
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, sent a threatening message to Bilqis, saying, “Do not think to persist in polytheism and do not make delay.”

entries.farsi: هين بيا بلقيس ور نه بد شود / لشكرت خصمت شود مرتد شود
entries.english: “Listen, Bilqis, come! Else, it will be bad: your army will become your enemy and will revolt.

entries.farsi: پرده دار تو درت را بر كند / جان تو با تو به جان خصمى كند
entries.english: Your chamberlain will destroy your door: your soul with its soul will act as an enemy towards you.”

entries.farsi: باد را ديدى كه با عادان چه كرد / آب را ديدى كه در طوفان چه كرد
entries.english: All the atoms of earth and heaven are God’s army, on putting it to the test.

entries.farsi: جمله ذرات زمين و آسمان / ‏لشكر حقند گاه امتحان
entries.english: You have seen what the wind did to the people of ‘Ad, you have seen what the water did at the Deluge;

entries.farsi: آن چه بر فرعون زد آن بحر كين / ‏و انچه با قارون نمودست اين زمين
entries.english: How that vengeful Sea dashed on Pharaoh, and how this Earth behaved to Qarun;

entries.farsi: و انچه آن بابيل با آن پيل كرد / و انچه پشه كله‏ى نمرود خورد
entries.english: And what those swifts (babil) did to the Elephant, and how the gnat devoured the skull of Nimrod;

entries.farsi: و انكه سنگ انداخت داودى به دست / ‏گشت ششصد پاره و لشكر شكست
entries.english: And how a David hurled with his hand a stone became six hundred pieces and shattered an army,

entries.farsi: سنگ مى‏باريد بر اعداى لوط / تا كه در آب سيه خوردند غوط
entries.english: Stones rained upon the enemies of Lot, so that they were sub merged in the black water,

entries.farsi: گر بگويم از جمادات جهان / عاقالنه يارى پيغمبران
entries.english: If I relate the help given rationally to the prophets by the inanimate things of the world,

entries.farsi: نوى چندان شود كه چل شتر / گر كشد عاجز شود از بار پر
entries.english: The Masnavi will become of such extent that, if forty camels carry it, they will be unable to bear the full load.

entries.farsi: دست بر كافر گواهى مى‏دهد / لشكر حق مى‏شود سر مى‏نهد
entries.english: The hand will give testimony against the infidel will become an army of God, and will submit.

entries.farsi: اى نموده ضد حق در فعل درس / ‏در ميان لشكر اويى بترس
entries.english: O you that in your actions have studied to oppose God, you are in the midst of His army: be afraid!

entries.farsi: زو جزوت لشكر او در وفاق / مر ترا اكنون مطيعند از نفاق
entries.english: Every part of you is an army of God in accord they are obedient to you now, not sincerely’.

entries.farsi: گر بگويد چشم را كاو را فشار / درد چشم از تو بر آرد صد دمار
entries.english: If He say to the eye, “Squeeze him”, eye-ache will wreak upon you a hundred vengeances;

entries.farsi: ور به دندان گويد او بنما وبال / ‏پس ببينى تو ز دندان گوشمال
entries.english: And if He says to the teeth, ‘Plague,” then you will suffer chastisement from your teeth.

entries.farsi: باز كن طب را بخوان باب العلل / ‏تا ببينى لشكر تن را عمل
entries.english: Open the Medicine and read the chapter on diseases, that you may see what is done by the army of the body.

entries.farsi: چون كه جان جان هر چيزى وى است / ‏دشمنى با جان جان آسان كى است
entries.english: Since He is the Soul of the soul of everything, how is it a light matter to be hostile to the Soul of the soul?

entries.farsi: خود رها كن لشكر ديو و پرى / ‏كز ميان جان كنندم صفدرى
entries.english: “Let alone the army of demons and genies who, from the core of their hearts, cleave the ranks for me.

entries.farsi: ملك را بگذار بلقيس از نخست / ‏چون مرا يابى همه ملك آن تست
entries.english: First, O Bilqis relinquish your kingdom: when you gain me, all the kingdom is yours.

entries.farsi: خود بدانى چون بر من آمدى / ‏كه تو بى‏من نقش گرمابه بدى
entries.english: When you have come to me, you yourself will know that without me you were a picture in the bath-house.”

entries.farsi: نقش اگر خود نقش سلطان يا غنى است / ‏صورت است از جان خود بى‏چاشنى است
entries.english: Even if the picture be the picture of a sultan or a rich man, it is a form: it has no savour of its own spirit.

entries.farsi: اى تو در پيكار خود را باخته / ‏ديگران را تو ز خود نشناخته
entries.english: Its beauty is for others: its eyes and mouth are open in vain.

entries.farsi: زينت او از براى ديگران / ‏باز كرده بى‏هده چشم و دهان
entries.english: O you who have devoted yourself in strife, you have not known others from yourself.

entries.farsi: اين تو كى باشى كه تو آن اوحدى / ‏كه خوش و زيبا و سر مست خودى
entries.english: You stop at every form that you come to, saying, “I am this.” By God, you are not that.

entries.farsi: تو به هر صورت كه آيى بيستى / ‏كه منم اين و اهلل آن تو نيستى
entries.english: You are left alone by people for a single moment; you remain up to the throat in grief and anxiety.

entries.farsi: يك زمان تنها بمانى تو ز خلق / ‏در غم و انديشه مانى تا به حلق
