entries.english: How are you this? You are that Unique One, for you are fair and lovely and intoxicated with yourself.

entries.farsi: مرغ خويشى صيد خويشى دام خويش / صدر خويشى فرش خويشى بام خويش
entries.english: You are your own bird your own prey, and your own snare; you are your own seat of honour, your own floor, and your own roof.

entries.farsi: جوهر آن باشد كه قايم با خود است / ‏آن عرض باشد كه فرع او شده‏ست
entries.english: The substance is that which subsists in itself; the accident is that which has become a derivative of it.

entries.farsi: چيست اندر خم كه اندر نهر نيست / ‏چيست اندر خانه كاندر شهر نيست
entries.english: If you are born of Adam, sit like him and behold all his progeny in yourself

entries.farsi: گر تو آدم زاده‏اى چون او نشين / ‏جمله ذريات را در خود ببين
entries.english: What is in the jar that is not in the river? What is in the house that is not in the city?

entries.farsi: اين جهان خم است و دل چون جوى آب / ‏اين جهان حجره‏ست و دل شهر عجاب
entries.english: This world is the jar, and the heart is like the river; this world is the chamber, and the heart is the wonderful city.

sections.title_fa: پيدا كردن سليمان عليه السلام كه مرا خالصا المر اللهَّ جهد است در ايمان تو، يك ذره غرضى نيست ‏َّمرا نه در نفس تو و حسن تو و نه در ملك تو، خود بينى چون چشم جان باز شود بنور الله
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, explained, saying, “My labour in your Faith is purely for God’s sake:
I have not one atom of self-interest, either as regards your person or your beauty or your kingdom. You yourself will see when the eye of your spirit is opened by the light of God.”

entries.farsi: هين بيا كه من رسولم دعوتى / ‏چون اجل شهوت كشم نه شهوتى
entries.english: “Listen; come, for I am a Messenger sent to call: like Death, I am the slayer of lust, I am not given to lust.

entries.farsi: ور بود شهوت امير شهوتم / ‏نه اسير شهوت روى بتم
entries.english: And if there be lust, I am the ruler of lust: l am not captive to lust for the face of an idol.

entries.farsi: بت شكن بوده‏ست اصل اصل ما / چون خليل حق و جمله‏ى انبيا
entries.english: My deepest nature is a breaker of idols, like the Friend of God and all the prophets.

entries.farsi: احمد و بو جهل در بت خانه رفت / ‏زين شدن تا آن شدن فرقى است زفت
entries.english: O slave, if I enter the idol-temple, the idol will prostrate itself, not I, in adoration.”

entries.farsi: گر در آييم اى رهى در بتكده / ‏بت سجود آرد نه ما در معبده
entries.english: Ahmad and Bu Jahl went into the idol- temple; there is a great difference between this going and that going.

entries.farsi: اين در آيد سر نهند او را بتان / ‏آن در آيد سر نهد چون امتان
entries.english: This one enters, the idols lay down their heads before him; that one enters and lays down his head, like the peoples.

entries.farsi: اين جهان شهوتى بت خانه‏اى است / انبيا و كافران را النه‏اى است
entries.english: This world, associated with lust, is an idol-temple: it is a nest for the prophets and the infidels,

entries.farsi: ليك شهوت بنده‏ى پاكان بود / زر نسوزد ز انكه نقد كان بود
entries.english: But lust is the slave of holy men: gold does not burn, because it is sterling coin from the mine.

entries.farsi: كافران قلبند و پاكان همچو زر / اندر اين بوته درند اين دو نفر
entries.english: The infidels are alloy, while the holy men are as gold: both these persons are within this crucible.

entries.farsi: قلب چون آمد سيه شد در زمان / ‏زر در آمد شد زرى او عيان
entries.english: When the alloy came, it became black at once; the gold came in, its goldenness was made manifest.

entries.farsi: دست و پا انداخت زر در بوته خوش / ‏در رخ آتش همى‏خندد رگش
entries.english: The gold gladly cast hands and feet into the crucible: its vein laughs in the face of the fire.

entries.farsi: جسم ما رو پوش ما شد در جهان / ‏ما چو دريا زير اين كه در نهان
entries.english: Our body is our veil in the world: we are like a sea hidden beneath this straw.

entries.farsi: شاه دين را منگر اى نادان به طين / ‏كاين نظر كرده است ابليس لعين
entries.english: O fool, do not regard the king of the Religion as clay; for the accursed Iblis took this view.

entries.farsi: كى توان اندود اين خورشيد را / با كف گل تو بگو آخر مرا
entries.english: How is it possible to daub this sun with a handful of earth? Pray, tell me!

entries.farsi: گر بريزى خاك و صد خاكسترش / ‏بر سر نور او بر آيد بر سرش
entries.english: Though you pour earth and a hundred ashes over its light, it will come up above them.

entries.farsi: كه كه باشد كاو بپوشد روى آب / ‏طين كه باشد كاو بپوشد آفتاب
entries.english: Who is straw that it should cover the face of the water? Who is clay that it should cover the sun?

entries.farsi: خيز بلقيسا چو ادهم شاهوار / دود از اين ملك دو سه روزه بر آر
entries.english: O Bilqis arise royally, like Adham: raise the smoke from this kingdom of two or three days’ duration.

sections.title_fa: ‏باقى قصه‏ى ابراهيم ادهم رحمه اللهَّ عليه
sections.title_en: The remainder of the story of Ibrahim son of Adham, may God sanctify his spirit.

entries.farsi: بر سر تختى شنيد آن نيك نام / ‏طق طقى و هاى و هويى شب ز بام
entries.english: On a throne, that man of good name heard at night a noise of tramping and shrill cries from the roof.

entries.farsi: گامهاى تند بر بام سرا / گفت با خود اين چنين زهره كه را
entries.english: (He heard) loud footsteps on the roof of the palace, and said to himself, “Who dares to do this?”

entries.farsi: بانگ زد بر روزن قصر او كه كيست / ‏اين نباشد آدمى مانا پرى است
entries.english: He shouted, at the palace-window, “Who is it? This is not a man, belike it is a genie.

entries.farsi: سر فرو كردند قومى بو العجب / ‏ما همى‏گرديم شب بهر طلب
entries.english: A wondrous folk put their heads down, “We are going round by night for the purpose of search.”

entries.farsi: هين چه مى‏جوييد گفتند اشتران / ‏گفت اشتر بام بر كى جست هان
entries.english: “Eh, what are you seeking?” “Camels,” they replied. He said, “Take heed! Whoever sought camel on a roof?”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش كه تو بر تخت جاه / ‏چون همى‏جويى مالقات اله
entries.english: Then they said to him, “How are you seeking to meet with God on the throne of state?”

entries.farsi: خود همان بد ديگر او را كس نديد / چون پرى از آدمى شد ناپديد
entries.english: That was all. None saw him again: he vanished like a genie from man.

entries.farsi: معنى‏اش پنهان و او در پيش خلق / ‏خلق كى بينند غير ريش و دلق
entries.english: His reality was hidden, though he was in people’s presence: how should the people see anything but beard and frock?

entries.farsi: چون ز چشم خويش و خلقان دور شد / همچو عنقا در جهان مشهور شد
entries.english: When he became far from his own and the people’s eyes, he became renowned in the world, like the ‘Anqa.

entries.farsi: جان هر مرغى كه آمد سوى قاف / ‏جمله‏ى عالم از او الفند الف
entries.english: Whenever the soul of any bird has come to Qaf, the entire world boasts and brags on account of it.

entries.farsi: روحهاى مرده جمله پر زدند / مردگان از گور تن سر بر زدند
entries.english: When this orient light reached Saba, a tumult arose in Bilqis and her people.

entries.farsi: چون رسيد اندر سبا اين نور شرق / ‏غلغلى افتاد در بلقيس و خلق
entries.english: All the dead spirits took wing: the dead put forth their heads from the grave, the body.

entries.farsi: ز ان ندا دينها همى‏گردند گبز / شاخ و برگ دل همى‏گردند سبز
entries.english: They gave the good news to one another, saying, “Listen! Lo, a voice is coming from Heaven.”

entries.farsi: يك دگر را مژده مى‏دادند هان / ‏نك ندايى مى‏رسد از آسمان
entries.english: At that voice religions wax great; the leaves and boughs of the heart become green.

entries.farsi: از سليمان آن نفس چون نفخ صور / مردگان را وارهانيد از قبور
entries.english: Like the blast of the trumpet that breath from Solomon delivered the dead from the tombs.

entries.farsi: مر ترا بادا سعادت بعد از اين / اين گذشت اللهَّ اعلم باليقين
entries.english: May felicity be yours after this! This is past. God best knows the certain truth.

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى اهل سبا و نصيحت و ارشاد سليمان عليه السلام آل بلقيس را هر يكى را اندر خورد مشكالت دين و دل او و صيد كردن هر جنس مرغ ضميرى به صفير آن جنس مرغ و طعمه‏ى او
sections.title_en: The rest of the story of the people of Saba, and of the admonition and guidance given by Solomon, on whom be peace, to the kinsfolk of Bilqis—to everyone suitable to his religious and spiritual difficulties;
and how he caught each sort of conceptional bird with the whistle and bait proper for that sort of bird.

entries.farsi: قصه گويم از سبا مشتاق‏وار / چون صبا آمد به سوى الله‏زار
entries.english: I will tell the story of Saba in lover’s style. When the Zephyr came towards the tulip-field,

entries.farsi: القت الاشباح يوم وصلها / عادت الاوالد صوب اصلها
entries.english: The bodies met the day of their union: the children turned again in the direction of their home.

entries.farsi: أمة العشق الخفى فى الامم / ‏مثل جود حوله لوم السقم
entries.english: Amongst the communities the community of secret Love is like a liberality surrounded by the meanness of distemper.

entries.farsi: ذله الارواح من اشباحها / عزه الاشباح من ارواحها
entries.english: The baseness of spirits is from their bodies; the nobility of bodies is from their spirits.

entries.farsi: ايها العشاق السقيا لكم / ‏أنتم الباقون و البقيا لكم
entries.english: O lovers, the draught is given to you. You are the everlasting: everlastingness is bestowed on you.

entries.farsi: ايها السالون قوموا و اعشقوا / ذاك ريح يوسف فاستنشقوا
entries.english: O you that are forgetful arise and love! That is the wind of Joseph: smell!

entries.farsi: منطق الطير سليمانى بيا / بانگ هر مرغى كه آيد مى‏سرا
entries.english: Come, O bird-speech of Solomon, sing the song of every bird that comes.

entries.farsi: چون به مرغانت فرستاده‏ست حق / ‏لحن هر مرغى بداده‏ستت سبق
entries.english: Since God has sent you to the birds, He has instructed you in the note of every bird.

entries.farsi: مرغ جبرى را زبان جبر گو / مرغ پر اشكسته را از صبر گو
entries.english: To the necessitarian bird speak the language of necessitarianism; to the bird whose wings are broken speak of patience.

entries.farsi: مرغ صابر را تو خوش دار و معاف / ‏مرغ عنقا را بخوان اوصاف قاف
entries.english: Keep the patient bird happy and free from harm; to the bird ‘Anqa recite the descriptions of Mount Qaf.

entries.farsi: مر كبوتر را حذر فرما ز باز / باز را از حلم گو و احتراز
entries.english: Bid the pigeon to beware of the falcon; to the falcon speak of forbearance and being on its guard.

entries.farsi: كبك جنگى را بياموزان تو صلح / ‏مر خروسان را نما اشراط صبح
entries.english: And as for the bat that is left destitute (of spiritual illumination), make it to consort and to be familiar with the Light.

entries.farsi: و آن خفاشى را كه ماند او بى‏نوا / مى‏كنش با نور جفت و آشنا
entries.english: Cause the warlike partridge to learn peace; to the cocks display the signs of dawn.

entries.farsi: همچنان مى‏رو ز هدهد تا عقاب / ‏ره نما و اهلل اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: Even so proceed from the hoopoe to the eagle, and show the way. And God best knows the right course.

sections.title_fa: آزادشدن بلقيس از ملك و مست شدن او از شوق ايمان و التفات همت او از همه ملك منقطع شدن ‏وقت هجرت الا از تخت
sections.title_en: How Bilqis was freed from her kingdom and was intoxicated with longing for the Faith, and how at the moment of her emigration the regard of her desire became severed
from the whole of her kingdom except from her throne.

entries.farsi: جز مگر مرغى كه بد بى‏جان و پر / يا چو ماهى گنگ بود از اصل و كر
entries.english: When Solomon uttered a single whistling note to the birds of Saba he ensnared them all,

entries.farsi: چون سليمان سوى مرغان سبا / يك صفيرى كرد بست آن جمله را
entries.english: Except, maybe, the bird that was without spirit or wings, or was dumb and deaf, like a fish, from the beginning.

entries.farsi: نى غلط گفتم كه كر گر سر نهد / پيش وحى كبريا سمعش دهد
entries.english: No, I have spoken wrongly, for if the deaf one lay his head before the inspiration of the Divine Majesty, it will give to him hearing.

entries.farsi: چون كه بلقيس از دل و جان عزم كرد / بر زمان رفته هم افسوس خورد
entries.english: When Bilqis set out with heart and soul, she felt remorse too for the bygone time,

entries.farsi: ترك مال و ملك كرد او آن چنان / ‏كه بترك نام و ننگ آن عاشقان
entries.english: She took leave of her kingdom and riches in the same way as those lovers take leave of honour and disgrace.

entries.farsi: آن غالمان و كنيزان بناز / پيش چشمش همچو پوسيده پياز
entries.english: Those charming pages and handmaidens to her eye as a rotten onion

entries.farsi: باغها و قصرها و آب رود / پيش چشم از عشق گلخن مى‏نمود
entries.english: For love’s sake, orchards and palaces and river-water seemed to her eye a dunghill.

entries.farsi: عشق در هنگام استيال و خشم / ‏زشت گرداند لطيفان را به چشم
entries.english: Love, in the hour of domination and anger, makes the pleasing ones to become hideous to the eye.

entries.farsi: هر زمرد را نمايد گندنا / غيرت عشق اين بود معنى ال
entries.english: Love’s jealousy causes every emerald to appear as a leek: this is the meaning of La’.

entries.farsi: ال اله الا هو اين است اى پناه / ‏كه نمايد مه ترا ديگ سياه
entries.english: O protection , “There is no god but He” is that the moon should seem to you a black kettle.

entries.farsi: هيچ مال و هيچ مخزن هيچ رخت / ‏مى‏دريغش نامد الا جز كه تخت
entries.english: No wealth, no treasury, and no goods or gear were being grudged by her except her throne.

entries.farsi: پس سليمان از دلش آگاه شد / كز دل او تا دل او راه شد
entries.english: Then Solomon became aware of her heart, for the way was open from his heart to hers.

entries.farsi: آن كسى كه بانگ موران بشنود / هم فغان سر دوران بشنود
entries.english: He that hears the voice of ants will also hear the cry from the inmost soul of them that are afar.

entries.farsi: آن كه گويد راز قالَتْ نملة / ‏هم بداند راز اين طاق كهن
entries.english: He that declares the mystery of “an ant said ” will also know the mystery of this ancient dome

entries.farsi: ديد از دورش كه آن تسليم كيش / ‏تلخش آمد فرقت آن تخت خويش
entries.english: From afar he discerned that to her who was following the path of resignation it was bitter to part with her throne.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم آن سبب گردد دراز / كه چرا بودش به تخت آن عشق و ساز
entries.english: If I explain the reason why she had that love and complaisance to her throne, it will become long.

entries.farsi: گر چه اين كلك قلم خود بى‏حسى است / ‏نيست جنس كاتب او را مونسى است
entries.english: Although this reed-pen is in fact an insensible thing and is not homogeneous with the writer, it is a familiar friend to him.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر آلت پيشه‏ورى / ‏هست بى‏جان مونس جاناورى
entries.english: Likewise, every tool of a craftsman is, lifeless, the familiar friend of the spirit of Man.

entries.farsi: اين سبب را من معين گفتمى / ‏گر نبودى چشم فهمت را نمى
entries.english: This reason I would have explained precisely, if there were not some moisture in the eye of your understanding.

entries.farsi: از بزرگى تخت كز حد مى‏فزود / نقل كردن تخت را امكان نبود
entries.english: There was no possibility of transporting the throne because of its hugeness which exceeded bounds.

entries.farsi: خرده كارى بود و تفريقش خطر / همچو اوصال بدن با همدگر
entries.english: It was filigree work, and there was danger in taking it to pieces, like the limbs of the body with one another.

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت گر چه فى الاخير / سرد خواهد شد بر او تاج و سرير
entries.english: Therefore Solomon said, “Although in the end the diadem and throne will become chilling to her”—

entries.farsi: چون بر آيد گوهر از قعر بحار / بنگرى اندر كف و خاشاك خوار
entries.english: When the spirit puts forth its head from the Unity, in comparison with its splendour the body has no splendour;

entries.farsi: چون ز وحدت جان برون آرد سرى / ‏جسم را با فر او نبود فرى
entries.english: When the pearl comes up from the depths of the seas, you will look with contempt on the foam and sticks and straws.

entries.farsi: سر بر آرد آفتاب با شرر / دم عقرب را كه سازد مستقر
entries.english: The flaming sun lifts up its head, who will make Scorpio’s tail his resting-place?—

entries.farsi: ليك خود با اين همه بر نقد حال / ‏جست بايد تخت او را انتقال
entries.english: “Yet, notwithstanding all this, in the actual case transporting her throne must be sought,

entries.farsi: تا نگردد خسته هنگام لقا / كودكانه حاجتش گردد روا
entries.english: In order that she may not feel hurt at the time of meeting, and that her wish may be fulfilled, like children.

entries.farsi: هست بر ما سهل و او را بس عزيز / تا بود بر خوان حوران ديو نيز
entries.english: It is lightly esteemed by me, but it is exceedingly dear to her: that the devil too may be at the table of the houris.

entries.farsi: عبرت جانش شود آن تخت ناز / همچو دلق و چارقى پيش اياز
entries.english: That throne of delight will become a lesson to her soul, like the frock and shoes in the presence of Ayaz

entries.farsi: تا بداند در چه بود آن مبتال / از كجاها در رسيد او تا كجا
entries.english: So that the afflicted one may know in what she was and from what places to what a place she has arrived.”

entries.farsi: خاك را و نطفه را و مضغه را / پيش چشم ما همى‏دارد خدا
entries.english: God is ever keeping the clay and semen and piece of flesh before our eyes,

entries.farsi: كز كجا آوردمت اى بد نيت / ‏كه از آن آيد همى خفريقى‏ات
entries.english: To say, “O man of evil intention, whence did I bring you, that you have a disgust at it?

entries.farsi: تو بر آن عاشق بدى در دور آن / ‏منكر اين فضل بودى آن زمان
entries.english: You were in love with that in the period thereof: at that time you were denying this grace.”

entries.farsi: اين كرم چون دفع آن انكار تست / ‏كه ميان خاك مى‏كردى نخست
entries.english: Inasmuch as this bounty is the rebutting the denial which you did make in the beginning amid the clay,

entries.farsi: حجت انكار شد انشار تو / از دوا بدتر شد اين بيمار تو
entries.english: Your having been brought to life is the argument against denial; your illness is made worse by the medicine.

entries.farsi: خاك را تصوير اين كار از كجا / نطفه را خصمى و انكار از كجا
entries.english: Whence should clay have the imagining this thing? When should semen conceive opposition and denial?

entries.farsi: از جمادى چون كه انكارت برست / ‏هم از اين انكار حشرت شد درست
entries.english: Since at that moment you were devoid of heart and spirit, you were denying reflection and denial.

entries.farsi: پس مثال تو چو آن حلقه زنى است / ‏كز درونش خواجه گويد خواجه نيست
entries.english: Since your denial arose from the state of lifelessness, so by this denial your resurrection is established.

entries.farsi: چون در آن دم بى‏دل و بى‏سر بدى / ‏فكرت و انكار را منكر بدى
entries.english: Hence the parable of you is like the person who knocks at the door, and the master replies to him from within, saying, “The master is not.”

entries.farsi: حلقه زن زين نيست دريابد كه هست / ‏پس ز حلقه بر ندارد هيچ دست
entries.english: From this “is not” the person knocking perceives that he is, and consequently does not take his hand off the door- ring at all.

entries.farsi: پس هم انكارت مبين مى‏كند / كز جماد او حشر صد فن مى‏كند
entries.english: Therefore the very fact of your denial is making it clear that He brings about manifold resurrections from lifeless matter.

entries.farsi: آب و گل مى‏گفت خود انكار نيست / ‏بانگ مى‏زد بى‏خبر كه اخبار نيست
entries.english: How much artifice passed O you denial, till the water and day produced denial from Hal ata!’

entries.farsi: چند صنعت رفت اى انكار تا / ‏آب و گل انكار زاد از هَلْ أتى
entries.english: The water and clay was really saying, “There is no denial”: it was crying, ‘There is no affirmation,” unaware.

entries.farsi: من بگويم شرح اين از صد طريق / گفت عفريتى كه تختش را به فن
entries.english: I would expound this in a hundred ways, but the mind would stumble at the subtle discourse.

sections.title_fa: چاره كردن سليمان عليه السلام در احضار تخت بلقيس از سبا
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, devised a plan for bringing the throne of Bilqis from Saba.

entries.farsi: ‏حاضر آرم تا تو زين مجلس شدن / گفت آصف من به اسم اعظمش
entries.english: A certain demon said, “By art I will bring her throne here before your departure from this council.”

entries.farsi: ‏حاضر آرم پيش تو در يك دمش / گر چه عفريت اوستاد سحر بود
entries.english: Asaf said, “By means of the greatest Name I will bring it here into your presence in a single moment.”

entries.farsi: ليك آن از نفخ آصف رو نمود / حاضر آمد تخت بلقيس آن زمان
entries.english: Though the Demon was a master of magic, yet that was displayed by the breath of Asaf.

entries.farsi: ‏ليك ز آصف نز فن عفريتيان / گفت حمد اللهَّ بر اين و صد چنين
entries.english: The throne of Bilqis came into the presence instantly, but through Maf, not through the art of them that have the nature of Demons.

entries.farsi: ‏كه بديده‏ستم ز رب العالمين / پس نظر كرد آن سليمان سوى تخت
entries.english: He said, “Praise to God for this and a hundred such which I have seen from the Lord of created beings.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى گول گيرى اى درخت / پيش چوب و پيش سنگ نقش كند
entries.english: Then Solomon turned his eyes towards the throne. “Yes,” he said, “you are one that catches fools, O tree!”

entries.farsi: اى بسا گوالن كه سرها مى‏نهند / ساجد و مسجود از جان بى‏خبر
entries.english: Oh, many are the fools that lay down their heads before wood and graven stone.

entries.farsi: ديده از جان جنبشى و اندك اثر / ديده در وقتى كه شد حيران و دنگ
entries.english: The worshipper and the object of worship are ignorant of the spirit; he has felt a movement and a slight effect of the spirit.

entries.farsi: ‏كه سخن گفت و اشارت كرد سنگ / نرد خدمت چون به ناموضع بباخت
entries.english: He has felt, at the moment when he became rapt and bewildered, that the stone spoke and made signs.

entries.farsi: استخوانى سوى سگ انداخت زود / گفت گر چه نيست آن سگ بر قوام
entries.english: When the wretched man bestowed his devotion in the wrong place and deemed the lion of stone to be a lion,

entries.farsi: ‏شير سنگين را شقى شيرى شناخت / از كرم شير حقيقى كرد جود
entries.english: The real Lion, from kindness, showed munificence and at once threw a bone to the dog,

entries.farsi: ‏ليك ما را استخوان لطفى است عام
entries.english: And said, “Although the dog is not in order, yet as regards me the bone is a bounty of which all partake.”

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى يارى خواستن حليمه از بتان چون عقيب فطام، مصطفى را عليه السلام گم كرد و لرزيدن و ‏سجده‏ى بتان و گواهى دادن ايشان بر عظمت كار مصطفى صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله و سلم
sections.title_en: Story of Halima’s asking help of the idols when she lost Mustafa—on whom be peace—
after he was weaned, and how the idols trembled and prostrated themselves and bore witness to the grandeur of Mohammed’s estate—may God bless and save him!

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى راز حليمه گويمت / ‏تا زدايد داستان او غمت
entries.english: I will tell you the story of Halima’s mystic experience, that her tale may clear away your trouble.

entries.farsi: مصطفى را چون ز شير او باز كرد / بر كفش برداشت چون ريحان و ورد
entries.english: When she parted Mustafa from milk, she took him up on the palm of her hand as sweet basil and roses,

entries.farsi: مى‏گريزانيدش از هر نيك و بد / تا سپارد آن شهنشه را به جد
entries.english: Causing him to avoid every good or evil, that she might commit that emperor to (the care of) his grandsire.

entries.farsi: چون همى‏آورد امانت را ز بيم / ‏شد به كعبه و آمد او اندر حطيم
entries.english: Since she was bringing the trust in fear, she went to the Ka‘ba and came into the Hatím.

entries.farsi: از هوا بشنيد بانگى كاى حطيم / ‏تافت بر تو آفتابى بس عظيم
entries.english: From the air she heard a cry—“O Hatím, an exceedingly mighty Sun has shone upon you.

entries.farsi: اى حطيم امروز آيد بر تو زود / صد هزاران نور از خورشيد جود
entries.english: O Hatím, to-day there will suddenly come upon you a hundred thousand beams from the Sun of munificence.

entries.farsi: اى حطيم امروز آرد در تو رخت / ‏محتشم شاهى كه پيك اوست بخت
entries.english: O Hatím, to-day there will march into you with pomp a glorious King, whose harbinger is Fortune.

entries.farsi: اى حطيم امروز بى‏شك از نوى / ‏منزل جانهاى بالايى شوى
entries.english: O Hatím, to-day without doubt you will become anew the abode of exalted spirits.

entries.farsi: جان پاكان طلب طلب و جوق جوق / ‏آيدت از هر نواحى مست شوق
entries.english: The spirits of the holy will come to you from every quarter in troops and multitudes, drunk with desire.”

entries.farsi: گشت حيران آن حليمه ز آن صدا / نه كسى در پيش نه سوى قفا
entries.english: Halima was bewildered by that voice: neither in front nor behind was any one.

entries.farsi: شش جهت خالى ز صورت وين ندا / شد پياپى آن ندا را جان فدا
entries.english: The six directions were empty of form, and this cry was continuous— may the soul be a ransom for that cry!

entries.farsi: مصطفى را بر زمين بنهاد او / تا كند آن بانگ خوش را جستجو
entries.english: She laid Mustafa on the earth that she might search after the sweet sound.

entries.farsi: چشم مى‏انداخت آن دم سو به سو / كه كجاى است آن شه اسرار گو
entries.english: Then she cast her eye to and fro, saying, “Where is that king that tells of mysteries?

entries.farsi: كاين چنين بانگ بلند از چپ و راست / ‏مى‏رسد يا رب رساننده كجاست
entries.english: For such a loud sound is arriving from left and right. O Lord, where is he that causes it to arrive?”

entries.farsi: چون نديد او خيره و نوميد شد / جسم لرزان همچو شاخ بيد شد
entries.english: When she did not see, she became distraught and despairing: her body began to tremble like the willow-bough.

entries.farsi: باز آمد سوى آن طفل رشيد / مصطفى را بر مكان خود نديد
entries.english: She came back towards that righteous child: she did not see Mustafa in his place.

entries.farsi: حيرت اندر حيرت آمد بر دلش / ‏گشت بس تاريك از غم منزلش
entries.english: Bewilderment on bewilderment fell upon her heart: from grief her abode became very dark.

entries.farsi: سوى منزلها دويد و بانگ داشت / ‏كه كه بر دردانه‏ام غارت گماشت
entries.english: She ran to the dwellings and raised an outcry, saying, “Who has carried off my single pearl?”

entries.farsi: مكيان گفتند ما را علم نيست / ‏ما ندانستيم كانجا كودكى است
entries.english: The Meccans said, “We have no knowledge: we knew not that a child was there.”

entries.farsi: ريخت چندان اشك و كرد او بس فغان / ‏كه از او گريان شدند آن ديگران
entries.english: She shed so many tears and made much lamentation that those others began to weep because of her.

entries.farsi: سينه كوبان آن چنان بگريست خوش / ‏كاختران گريان شدند از گريه‏اش
entries.english: Beating her breast, she wept so well that the stars were made to weep by her weeping.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت آن پير عرب كه داللت كرد حليمه را به استعانت بتان
sections.title_en: Story of the old Arab who directed Halima to seek help from the idols

entries.farsi: كه چنين آتش ز دل افروختى / ‏اين جگرها را ز ماتم سوختى
entries.english: An old man with a staff approached her, saying, “Why, what has befallen you, O Halima,

entries.farsi: پير مردى پيشش آمد با عصا / كاى حليمه چه فتاد آخر ترا
entries.english: That you did let such a fire blaze from your heart and consume these bowels with mourning?”

entries.farsi: گفت احمد را رضيعم معتمد / پس بياوردم كه بسپارم به جد
entries.english: She replied, “I am Ahmad’s trusted foster-mother, so I brought him to hand him over to his grandsire.

entries.farsi: من چو آن الحان شنيدم از هوا / طفل را بنهادم آن جا ز آن صدا
entries.english: When I arrived in the Hatím, voices were coming and I was hearing from the air.

entries.farsi: چون رسيدم در حطيم آوازها / مى‏رسيد و مى‏شنيدم از هوا
entries.english: When I heard from the air those melodious strains, because of that sound I laid down the infant there,

entries.farsi: تا ببينم اين ندا آواز كيست / ‏كه ندايى بس لطيف و بس شهى است
entries.english: To see whose voice is this cry, for it is a very beautiful cry and very delightful

entries.farsi: نه از كسى ديدم به گرد خود نشان / ‏نه ندا مى‏منقطع شد يك زمان
entries.english: I saw no sign of any one around me: the cry was not ceasing for one moment.

entries.farsi: چون كه وا گشتم ز حيرتهاى دل / ‏طفل را آن جا نديدم واى دل
entries.english: When I returned from the bewilderments of my heart, I did not see the child there: alas for my heart!”

entries.farsi: گفتش اى فرزند تو انده مدار / كه نمايم مر ترا يك شهريار
entries.english: He said, “O daughter, do not grieve, for I will show you a queen,

entries.farsi: كه بگويد گر بخواهد حال طفل / ‏او بداند منزل و ترحال طفل
entries.english: Who, if she wishes, will tell what has happened to the child: she knows the dwelling-place of the child and his setting-out.”

entries.farsi: پس حليمه گفت اى جانم فدا / مر ترا اى شيخ خوب خوش ندا
entries.english: Then Halima said, “Oh, my soul is a ransom for you, O goodly and fair-spoken Shaykh!

entries.farsi: هين مرا بنماى آن شاه نظر / كش بود از حال طفل من خبر
entries.english: Come; show me that queen of clairvoyance who has knowledge of what has happened to my child.”

entries.farsi: برد او را پيش عزى كاين صنم / ‏هست در اخبار غيبى مغتنم
entries.english: He brought her to Uzza, saying, “This idol is greatly prized for information concerning the Unseen.

entries.farsi: ما هزاران گم شده زو يافتيم / ‏چون به خدمت سوى او بشتافتيم
entries.english: Through her we have found thousands that were lost, when we hastened towards her in devotion.”

entries.farsi: پير كرد او را سجود و گفت زود / اى خداوند عرب اى بحر جود
entries.english: The old man prostrated himself before her and said at once, “O Sovereign of the Arabs, O sea of munificence!”

entries.farsi: بر عرب حق است از اكرام تو / فرض گشته تا عرب شد رام تو
entries.english: He said, “O Uzza, you have done many favours, so that we have been delivered from snares.

entries.farsi: گفت اى عزى تو بس اكرامها / كرده‏اى تا رسته‏ايم از دامها
entries.english: On account of your favour the duty has become obligatory to the Arabs, so that the Arabs have submitted to you.

entries.farsi: اين حليمه‏ى سعدى از اوميد تو / آمد اندر ظل شاخ بيد تو
entries.english: In hope of you this Halima of Sa‘d has come into the shadow of your willow-bough,

entries.farsi: كه از او فرزند طفلى گم شده ست / ‏نام آن كودك محمد آمده ست
entries.english: For an infant child of hers is lost: the name of that child is Mohammed.”

entries.farsi: چون محمد گفت اين جمله بتان / ‏سر نگون گشتند و ساجد آن زمان
entries.english: When he said “Mohammed,” all those idols immediately fell headlong and prostrate,

entries.farsi: كه برو اى پير اين چه جست و جوست / ‏آن محمد را كه عزل ما از اوست
entries.english: Saying, “Be gone, O old man! What is this search after that Mohammed by whom we are deposed?

entries.farsi: ما نگون و سنگسار آييم از او / ما كساد و بى‏عيار آييم از او
entries.english: By him we are overthrown and reduced to a collection of stones; by him we are made unsaleable and valueless.

entries.farsi: آن خيالاتى كه ديدندى ز ما / وقت فترت گاه گاه اهل هوا
entries.english: Those phantoms which the followers of vain opinion used to see from us at times during the Fatra (interval)

entries.farsi: گم شود چون بارگاه او رسيد / آب آمد مر تيمم را دريد
entries.english: Will disappear now that his royal court has arrived: the water is come and has torn up the ablution with sand

entries.farsi: دور شو اى پير فتنه كم فروز / هين ز رشك احمدى ما را مسوز
entries.english: Get you far off, O old man! Do not kindle mischief! Listen; do not burn us with Ahmad’s jealousy!

entries.farsi: اين چه دم اژدها افشردن است / ‏هيچ دانى چه خبر آوردن است
entries.english: Get you far off, for God’s sake, O old man, lest you be burnt by the fire of Fore-ordainment.

entries.farsi: دور شو بهر خدا اى پير تو / تا نسوزى ز آتش تقدير تو
entries.english: What squeezing of the dragon’s tail is this? Do you know at all what the announcement is?

entries.farsi: زين خبر جوشد دل دريا و كان / ‏زين خبر لرزان شود هفت آسمان
entries.english: At this news the heart of sea and mine will surge; at this news the seven heavens will tremble.”

entries.farsi: چون شنيد از سنگها پير اين سخن / ‏پس عصا انداخت آن پير كهن
entries.english: When the old man heard these words from the stones, the ancient old man let his staff drop;

entries.farsi: پس ز لرزه و خوف و بيم آن ندا / ‏پير دندانها بهم بر مى‏زدى
entries.english: Then, from tremor and fear and dread caused by that proclamation, the old man was striking his teeth together.

entries.farsi: آن چنانك اندر زمستان مرد عور / او همى‏لرزيد و مى‏گفت اى ثبور
entries.english: Even as a naked man in winter, he was shuddering and saying, “O destruction!”

entries.farsi: چون در آن حالت بديد او پير را / ز آن عجب گم كرد زن تدبير را
entries.english: When she saw the old man in such a state, in consequence of that marvel the woman lost deliberation

entries.farsi: گفت پيرا گر چه من در محنتم / ‏حيرت اندر حيرت اندر حيرتم
entries.english: She said, “O old man, though I am in affliction, I am in bewildered.

entries.farsi: ساعتى با دم خطيبى مى‏كند / ساعتى سنگم اديبى مى‏كند
entries.english: At one moment the wind is making a speech to me, at another moment the stones are schooling me.

entries.farsi: باد با حرفم سخنها مى‏دهد / سنگ و كوهم فهم اشيا مى‏دهد
entries.english: The wind addresses me with articulate words, the stones and mountains give me intelligence of things.

entries.farsi: از كه نالم با كه گويم اين گله / ‏من شدم سودايى اكنون صد دله
entries.english: Once they of the Invisible carried off my child—they of the Invisible, the green-winged ones of Heaven.

entries.farsi: غيرتش از شرح غيبم لب ببست / ‏اين قدر گويم كه طفلم گم شده‏ست
entries.english: Of whom shall I complain? To whom shall I tell this plaint? I am become crazy and in a hundred minds.

entries.farsi: گاه طفلم را ربوده غيبيان / ‏غيبيان سبز پر آسمان
entries.english: His jealousy has closed my lips to unfold the mystery: I say this much, that my child is lost.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم چيز ديگر من كنون / ‏خلق بندندم به زنجير جنون
entries.english: If I should say anything else now, the people would bind me in chains as though I were mad.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيرش كاى حليمه شاد باش / ‏سجده‏ى شكر آر و رو را كم خراش
entries.english: The old man said to her, “O Halima, rejoice; bow down in thanksgiving and do not tear your face.

entries.farsi: غم مخور ياوه نگردد او ز تو / بلكه عالم ياوه گردد اندر او
entries.english: Do not grieve: he will not become lost to you; nay, but the world will become lost in him.

entries.farsi: هر زمان از رشك غيرت پيش و پس / ‏صد هزاران پاسبان است و حرس
entries.english: Before and behind there are always hundreds of thousands of keepers and guardians in jealous emulation.

entries.farsi: آن نديدى كان بتان ذو فنون / ‏چون شدند از نام طفلت سر نگون
entries.english: Did not you see how those idols with all their arts fell headlong at the name of your child?

entries.farsi: اين عجب قرنى است بر روى زمين / ‏پير گشتم من نديدم جنس اين
entries.english: This is a marvellous epoch on the face of the earth: I have grown old, and I have not seen anything of this kind.”

entries.farsi: زين رسالت سنگها چون ناله داشت / ‏تا چه خواهد بر گنه كاران گماشت
entries.english: Since the stones bewailed this mission, think what it will set over sinners!

entries.farsi: سنگ بى‏جرم است در معبودى‏اش / ‏تو نه‏اى مضطر كه بنده بودى‏اش
entries.english: The stone is guiltless in respect of being an object of worship; you are not under compulsion in worshipping it.

entries.farsi: او كه مضطر اين چنين ترسان شده‏ست / تا كه بر مجرم چها خواهند بست
entries.english: That one that was under compulsion has become so afraid: consider what things will be fastened upon the guilty!

sections.title_fa: خبر يافتن جد مصطفى عبد المطلب از گم كردن حليمه محمد را عليه الصالة و السلام و طالب شدن او ‏گرد شهر و ناليدن او بر در كعبه و از حق درخواستن و يافتن او محمد را عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How ‘Abdu ’l-Muttalib, the grandfather of Mustafa, got news of Halima’s having lost Mohammed,
on whom be peace, and searched for him round the city and made lamentation at the door of the Ka‘ba and besought God and found him, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: چون خبر يابيد جد مصطفى / از حليمه وز فغانش برمال
entries.english: When the grandfather of Mustafa got the news of Halima and her outcry in public

entries.farsi: و ز چنان بانگ بلند و نعره‏ها / كه به ميلى مى‏رسيد از وى صدا
entries.english: And of such loud screams and shrieks that the echo of them was reaching a mile,

entries.farsi: زود عبد المطلب دانست چيست / ‏دست بر سينه همى‏زد مى‏گريست
entries.english: Abdu l-Muttalib at once knew what the matter was: he beat his hands on his breast and wept.

entries.farsi: آمد از غم بر در كعبه به سوز / كاى خبير از سر شب و ز راز روز
entries.english: In his grief he came ardently to the door of the Ka‘ba, saying, “O You that knows the secret of night and the mystery of day,

entries.farsi: خويشتن را من نمى‏بينم فنى / ‏تا بود هم راز تو همچون منى
entries.english: I see not any accomplishment in myself, that one like me should be Your confidant.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را من نمى‏بينم هنر / تا شوم مقبول اين مسعود در
entries.english: I see not any merit in myself, that I should be accepted of this auspicious door,

entries.farsi: يا سر و سجده‏ى مرا قدرى بود / يا به اشكم دو لبى خندان شود
entries.english: Or that my head and my prostration should have any worth or that because of my tears any fortune should smile;

entries.farsi: ليك در سيماى آن در يتيم / ‏ديده‏ام آثار لطفت اى كريم
entries.english: But in the countenance of that unique Pearl I have beheld the signs of Your grace, O Bounteous One;

entries.farsi: كه نمى‏ماند به ما گر چه ز ماست / ‏ما همه مسيم و احمد كيمياست
entries.english: For he does not resemble us, though he is of us: we all are the copper, while Ahmad is the Elixir

entries.farsi: آن عجايبها كه من ديدم بر او / من نديدم بر ولى و بر عدو
entries.english: The wondrous things that I have seen in him I have not seen in friend or enemy.

entries.farsi: آن كه فضل تو در اين طفليش داد / كس نشان ندهد به صد ساله جهاد
entries.english: None, with a hundred years’ endeavour, would indicate that which Your bounty has bestowed on him in childhood.

entries.farsi: چون يقين ديدم عنايتهاى تو / بر وى او دريست از درياى تو
entries.english: Since I saw with certainty Your favours towards him, he is a pearl of Your sea.

entries.farsi: من هم او را مى‏شفيع آرم به تو / حال او اى حال دان با من بگو
entries.english: Him I bring to plead with You: tell me his plight, O You who knows (every) plight!”

entries.farsi: از درون كعبه آمد بانگ زود / كه هم اكنون رخ به تو خواهد نمود
entries.english: From within the Ka‘ba came at once a cry, “Even now he will show his face unto you.

entries.farsi: با دو صد اقبال او محظوظ ماست / ‏با دو صد طلب ملك محفوظ ماست
entries.english: He is blessed by Us with two hundred felicities; he is guarded by Us with two hundred troops of angels.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش را شهره‏ى كيهان كنيم / ‏باطنش را از همه پنهان كنيم
entries.english: We make his outward celebrated in the world; We make his inward to be hidden from all.

entries.farsi: زر كان بود آب و گل ما زرگريم / ‏كه گهش خلخال و گه خاتم بريم
entries.english: The water and clay was gold of the mine: We are the goldsmith; for We carve it now into an anklet, now into a seal.

entries.farsi: گه حمايلهاى شمشيرش كنيم / ‏گاه بند گردن شيرش كنيم
entries.english: Now We make it the shoulder-belt for a sword, now the chain on the neck of a lion.

entries.farsi: عشقها داريم با اين خاك ما / ز انكه افتاده‏ست در قعده‏ى رضا
entries.english: Now We fashion from it the ball of a throne, now the diadem on the heads that seek empire.

entries.farsi: گه ترنج تخت بر سازيم از او / گاه تاج فرق‏هاى ملك جو
entries.english: We have great affections towards this earth, because it lies in the posture of acquiescence.

entries.farsi: گه چنين شاهى از او پيدا كنيم / ‏گه هم او را پيش شه شيدا كنيم
entries.english: Now We produce from it a king like this; now We make it frenzied in the presence of the king:

entries.farsi: صد هزاران عاشق و معشوق از او / در فغان و در نفير و جستجو
entries.english: On account of him hundreds of thousands of lovers and loved ones are in lamentation and outcry and search.

entries.farsi: كار ما اين است بر كورى آن / كه به كار ما ندارد ميل جان
entries.english: This is Our work, to the confusion of that one who has no spiritual inclination towards Our work.

entries.farsi: اين فضيلت خاك را ز آن رو دهيم / ‏كه نواله پيش بى‏برگان نهيم
entries.english: We confer this eminence on the earth for the same reason that We place a portion of food before the destitute,

entries.farsi: ز انكه دارد خاك شكل اغبرى / ‏و ز درون دارد صفات انورى
entries.english: Because the earth has the gray brown external form, while inwardly it has the qualities of luminosity.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش با باطنش گشته به جنگ / ‏باطنش چون گوهر و ظاهر چو سنگ
entries.english: Its outward has come to be at war with its inward: its inward is like a jewel and its outward like a stone.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش گويد كه ما اينيم و بس / ‏باطنش گويد نكو بين پيش و پس
entries.english: Its outward says, ‘We are this, and no more’; its inward says, ‘Look well before and behind!’

entries.farsi: ظاهرش با باطنش در چالش‏اند / الجرم زين صبر نصرت مى‏كشند
entries.english: Its outward is denying that the inward is nothing; its inward says, ‘We will show: wait and see!’

entries.farsi: ظاهرش منكر كه باطن هيچ نيست / ‏باطنش گويد كه بنماييم بيست
entries.english: Its outward and its inward are in strife: necessarily they are drawing aid from this patient endurance.

entries.farsi: ز انكه ظاهر خاك اندوه و بكاست / ‏در درونش صد هزاران خنده‏هاست
entries.english: We make the forms from this sour-faced earth: We make manifest its hidden laughter,

entries.farsi: زين ترش رو خاك صورتها كنيم / ‏خنده‏ى پنهانش را پيدا كنيم
entries.english: For outwardly the earth is sorrow and tears, within it there are hundreds of thousands of kinds of laughter

entries.farsi: كاشف السريم و كار ما همين / ‏كاين نهانها را بر آريم از كمين
entries.english: We are the Revealer of the mystery, and Our work is just this, that We bring forth these hidden things from concealment.

entries.farsi: گر چه دزد از منكرى تن مى‏زند / شحنه آن از عصر پيدا مى‏كند
entries.english: Although the thief is mute in denial, the magistrate brings it to light by torture.

entries.farsi: فضل‏ها دزديده‏اند اين خاكها / تا مقر آريمشان از ابتال
entries.english: These earths have stolen favours, so that through affliction We may bring them to confess.

entries.farsi: بس عجب فرزند كاو را بوده است / ‏ليك احمد بر همه افزوده است
entries.english: Many are the wondrous child that it has had, but Ahmad has surpassed them all.

entries.farsi: شد زمين و آسمان خندان و شاد / كاين چنين شاهى ز ما دو جفت زاد
entries.english: Earth and Heaven laugh and rejoice, saying, ‘from us two joined in wedlock such a king is born!’

entries.farsi: مى‏شكافد آسمان از شادى‏اش / ‏خاك چون سوسن شده ز آزادى‏اش
entries.english: Heaven is bursting for joy of him; earth is become like the lily on account of his purity.

entries.farsi: ظاهرت با باطنت اى خاك خوش / ‏چون كه در جنگند و اندر كش مكش
entries.english: Since your outward and your inward, O fair earth, are at war and in struggling—

entries.farsi: هر كه با خود بهر حق باشد به جنگ / ‏تا شود معنيش خصم بو و رنگ
entries.english: Whoever is at war with himself for God’s sake, to the end that his reality may become the opponent of scent and colour,

entries.farsi: ظلمتش با نور او شد در قتال / ‏آفتاب جانش را نبود زوال
entries.english: His darkness is in combat with his light, the sun of his spirit will never set.

entries.farsi: هر كه كوشد بهر ما در امتحان / ‏پشت زير پايش آرد آسمان
entries.english: Whoso shall strive in tribulation for Our sake, Heaven will put its back under his feet.”

entries.farsi: ظاهرت از تيرگى افغان كنان / باطن تو گلستان در گلستان
entries.english: Your outward is wailing because of the darkness; your inward is roses within roses.

entries.farsi: قاصد او چون صوفيان رو ترش / ‏تا نياميزند با هر نور كش
entries.english: It is purposely like Sufis sour-faced in order that they may not mix with every one that quenches the (inner) light.

entries.farsi: عارفان رو ترش چون خار پشت / ‏عيش پنهان كرده در خار درشت
entries.english: Like the hedgehog, the sour-faced knowers have hidden their pleasures in rough prickles.

entries.farsi: باغ پنهان گرد باغ آن خار فاش / ‏كاى عدوى دزد زين در دور باش
entries.english: The orchard is hidden, around the orchard those thorns are plainly seen, saying, “O thievish foe, keep far from this gate!”

entries.farsi: خار پشتا خار حارس كرده‏اى / ‏سر چو صوفى در گريبان برده‏اى
entries.english: O hedgehog, you have made the prickles your guardian and, like a Sufi, have buried your head in your bosom,

entries.farsi: تا كسى در چار دانگ عيش تو / گم شود زين گل رخان خار خو
entries.english: That none of these rose-cheeked thorn-natured ones may encounter a cent of your pleasure.

entries.farsi: طفل تو گر چه كه كودك خوبده ست / ‏هر دو عالم خود طفيل او بده ست
entries.english: “Though your infant is childish, verily both the worlds are his parasites.

entries.farsi: ما جهانى را بدو زنده كنيم / ‏چرخ را در خدمتش بنده كنيم
entries.english: We make a world living through him; We make Heaven a slave in his service.”

entries.farsi: گفت عبد المطلب كاين دم كجاست / ‏اى عليم السر نشان ده راه راست
entries.english: ‘Abdu ’l-Muttalib said, “Where is he now? O You that knows the secret, point out the right way!”

sections.title_fa: نشان خواستن عبد المطلب از موضع محمد عليه الصالة و السلام كه كجاش يابم و جواب آمدن از ‏اندرون كعبه و نشان يافتن
sections.title_en: How Abdu l-Muttalib asked for a clue to the place where Mohammed was— peace be upon him!—saying, “Where shall I find him?” and how he was answered from within the Ka‘ba and obtained the clue.

entries.farsi: از درون كعبه آوازش رسيد / گفت اى جوينده آن طفل رشيد
entries.english: A voice reached him from within the Ka‘ba. It said, “O seeker, that righteous child

entries.farsi: در فالن وادى است زير آن درخت / ‏پس روان شد زود پير نيك بخت
entries.english: Is in such and such a wadi beneath yonder tree.” Then the good-fortuned old man at once set out.

entries.farsi: در ركاب او اميران قريش / ‏ز انكه جدش بود ز اعيان قريش
entries.english: At his stirrup the princes of Quraysh, for his grandfather was one of the notables of Quraysh.

entries.farsi: تا به پشت آدم اسالفش همه / ‏مهتران بزم و رزم و ملحمه
entries.english: All his ancestors to the loins of Adam lords in feast and fray and the carnage of battle

entries.farsi: اين نسب خود پوست او را بوده است / ‏كز شهنشاهان مه پالوده است
entries.english: This lineage is only to his husk, which is strained pure from mighty emperors.

entries.farsi: مغز او خود از نسب دور است و پاك / ‏نيست جنسش از سمك كس تا سماك
entries.english: His kernel, in truth, is remote from lineage, and unsoiled: none is its congener from the Fish to Arcturus.

entries.farsi: نور حق را كس نجويد زاد و بود / خلعت حق را چه حاجت تار و پود
entries.english: None seeks the birth and existence of the Light of God: what need have warp and woof has God’s robe of honour?

entries.farsi: كمترين خلعت كه بدهد در ثواب / بر فزايد بر طراز آفتاب
entries.english: The meanest robe of honour that He bestows in recompense excels the embroidered raiment of the sun.

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى دعوت رحمت بلقيس را
sections.title_en: The rest of the story of Mercy’s calling Bilqis.

entries.farsi: خيز بلقيسا بيا و ملك بين / ‏بر لب درياى يزدان در بچين
entries.english: “Arise, O Bilqis! Come and behold the Kingdom! Gather pearls on the shore of God’s Sea!

entries.farsi: خواهرانت را ز بخششهاى راد / هيچ مى‏دانى كه آن سلطان چه داد
entries.english: Your sisters are dwelling in the glorious Heaven: why do you behave like a sultan on account of a carcass?

entries.farsi: خواهرانت ساكن چرخ سنى / ‏تو به مردارى چه سلطانى كنى
entries.english: Do you know at all what noble gifts that Sultan gave to your sisters?

entries.farsi: تو ز شادى چون گرفتى طبل زن / ‏كه منم شاه و رئيس گولخن
entries.english: How did you jubilantly take drummers, proclaiming, ‘I am queen and mistress of the bath-stove’?”

sections.title_fa: مثل قانع شدن آدمى به دنيا و حرص او در طلب و غفلت او از دولت روحانيان كه ابناى جنس وى‏اند َنعره زنان كه يا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يَعْلَمُون
sections.title_en: Parable of Man’s being contented with (the goods of) this world, and his greed in seeking and his indifference to the high and blessed estate of the spiritual who are his congeners crying,
“Oh, would that my people might know!”

entries.farsi: آن سگى در كو گداى كور ديد / حمله مى‏آورد و دلقش مى‏دريد
entries.english: A dog saw a blind beggar in the street, and was rushing at him and tearing his cloak.

entries.farsi: كور گفتش آخر آن ياران تو / بر كه‏اند اين دم شكارى صيد جو
entries.english: We have related this, but it is repeated once again in order to strengthen the story.

entries.farsi: گفته‏ايم اين را ولى بارى دگر / شد مكرر بهر تاكيد خبر
entries.english: The blind man said to it, “Why, at this moment your friends are hunting and seeking prey on the mountain.

entries.farsi: قوم تو در كوه مى‏گيرند گور / در ميان كوى مى‏گيرى تو كور
entries.english: Your kinsfolk are catching wild asses in the mountains: you are catching blind men in the streets.”

entries.farsi: ترك اين تزوير گو شيخ نفور / آب شورى جمع كرده چند كور
entries.english: O recalcitrant Shaykh, abandon this imposture: you are briny water, having gathered some blind men,

entries.farsi: كاين مريدان من و من آب شور / مى‏خورند از من همى‏گردند كور
entries.english: Saying, “These are my disciples, and I am briny water: they drink of me and become blind.”

entries.farsi: آب خود شيرين كن از بحر لدن / ‏آب بد را دام اين كوران مكن
entries.english: Sweeten your water with the esoteric Sea: do not make the foul water a snare for these blind ones.

entries.farsi: خيز شيران خدا بين گور گير / تو چو سگ چونى به زرقى كور گير
entries.english: Arise; behold the lions of God who catch the wild ass: how are you, like a dog, catching the blind with hypocrisy?

entries.farsi: گور چه از صيد غير دوست دور / جمله شير و شير گير و مست نور
entries.english: What wild ass? They are far from hunting anything but the Beloved. They all are lions and lion-catchers and intoxicated with the Light.

entries.farsi: در نظاره‏ى صيد و صيادى شه / ‏كرده ترك صيد و مرده در وله
entries.english: In contemplation of the chase and hunting of the King, they have abandoned the chase and have become dead in bewilderment.

entries.farsi: همچو مرغ مرده‏شان بگرفته يار / تا كند او جنس ايشان را شكار
entries.english: The Friend has taken them, like a dead bird that He may hunt down their congeners.

entries.farsi: مرغ مرده مضطر اندر وصل و بين / ‏خوانده اى القلب بين اصبعين
entries.english: The dead bird is compelled in respect of being united or separated: you have read, “The heart is between two fingers of the Merciful God.”

entries.farsi: مرغ مرده‏اش را هر آن كه شد شكار / چون ببيند شد شكار شهريار
entries.english: Every one that has fallen a prey to His dead bird, when he sees he has fallen a prey to the King

entries.farsi: هر كه او زين مرغ مرده سر بتافت / ‏دست آن صياد را هرگز نيافت
entries.english: Whoever turned his head away from this dead bird never gained the hand of that Hunter.

entries.farsi: گويد او منگر به مردارى من / ‏عشق شه بين در نگهدارى من
entries.english: It says, “Do not regard my being a carcass: see the King’s love in preserving me.

entries.farsi: من نه مردارم مرا شه كشته است / ‏صورت من شبه مرده گشته است
entries.english: I am not a carcass: the King has killed me: my appearance has become like the dead.

entries.farsi: جنبش فانيم بيرون شد ز پوست / ‏جنبشم باقى است اكنون چون از اوست
entries.english: My former motion was by means of wing and pinion: now my motion proceeds from the hand of the Judge.

entries.farsi: جنبشم زين پيش بود از بال و پر / جنبشم اكنون ز دست دادگر
entries.english: My perishable motion has gone forth from my skin: now my motion is everlasting, since it proceeds from Him.

entries.farsi: هر كه كژ جنبد به پيش جنبشم / ‏گر چه سيمرغ است زارش مى‏كشم
entries.english: If anyone moves crookedly in the presence of my motion, I will kill him miserably, though he is the Simurgh.

entries.farsi: هين مرا مرده مبين گر زنده‏اى / ‏در كف شاهم نگر گر بنده‏اى
entries.english: Beware! If you are alive, do not deem me dead; if you are a slave; regard me in the hand of the King.

entries.farsi: كى بمانم مرده در قبضه‏ى خدا / بر كف عيسى مدار اين هم روا
entries.english: Jesus, by his grace, made the dead to be living: I am in the hand of the Creator of Jesus.

entries.farsi: مرده زنده كرد عيسى از كرم / ‏من به كف خالق عيسى درم
entries.english: How should I remain dead in the grasp of God? Likewise, do not hold this to be possible in the hand of ‘Jesus.’

entries.farsi: شد ز عيسى زنده ليكن باز مرد / شاد آن كاو جان بدين عيسى سپرد
entries.english: I am ‘Jesus’; but every one that has gained life from my breath will remain unto everlasting.

entries.farsi: عيسى‏ام ليكن هر آن كاو يافت جان / ‏از دم من او بماند جاودان
entries.english: He was made living by Jesus, but died again. Happy is he that gave up his life to this ‘Jesus.’

entries.farsi: من عصايم در كف موساى خويش / ‏موسيم پنهان و من پيدا به پيش
entries.english: I am the staff in the hand of my ‘Moses’: my ‘Moses’ is hidden, while I am visible in presence.

entries.farsi: اين عصا را اى پسر تنها مبين / ‏كه عصا بى‏كف حق نبود چنين
entries.english: For the true believers I become a bridge across the sea; for Pharaoh, again, I become a dragon.”

entries.farsi: بر مسلمانان پل دريا شوم / ‏باز بر فرعون اژدرها شوم
entries.english: O son, do not regard this staff alone, for the staff would not be like this without the hand of God.

entries.farsi: موج طوفان هم عصا بد كاو ز درد / طنطنه‏ى جادو پرستان را بخورد
entries.english: The waves of the Flood too were a staff which, from being aggrieved, devoured the pomp of the votaries of magic.

entries.farsi: گر عصاهاى خدا را بشمرم / ‏زرق اين فرعونيان را بر درم
entries.english: If I should enumerate the staves of God, I should tear to pieces the hypocrisy of these followers of Pharaoh;

entries.farsi: ليك زين شيرين گياه زهرمند / ترك كن تا چند روزى مى‏چرند
entries.english: But leave them to browse on this sweet poisonous grass for a few days.

entries.farsi: گر نباشد جاه فرعون و سرى / ‏از كجا يابد جهنم پرورى
entries.english: If there is not the power and dominion of Pharaoh, from where shall Hell obtain nutrition?

entries.farsi: فربهش كن آن گهش كش اى قصاب / ‏ز انكه بى‏برگند در دوزخ كالب
entries.english: Fatten him, and then kill him, O Butcher; for the dogs in Hell are without food.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى خصم و دشمن در جهان / ‏پس بمردى خشم اندر مردمان
entries.english: If there were no adversary and enemy in the world, then the anger in men would die.

entries.farsi: دوزخ آن خشم است خصمى بايدش / ‏تا زيد ور نى رحيمى بكشدش
entries.english: That anger is Hell: it needs an adversary that it may live; else Mercy would kill it.

entries.farsi: پس بماندى لطف بى‏قهر و بدى / ‏پس كمال پادشاهى كى بدى
entries.english: Then clemency would remain without any vengeance or evil: then how would the perfection of Kingship be?

entries.farsi: تو اگر خواهى بكن هم ريش‏خند / چند خواهى زيست اى مردار چند
entries.english: Those disbelievers have made a laughing-stock of the parables and clear exposition of them that glorify.

entries.farsi: ريش‏خندى كرده‏اند آن منكران / ‏بر مثلها و بيان ذاكران
entries.english: Make a laughing-stock, if you wish: how long will you live, O carcass, how long?

entries.farsi: شاد باشيد اى محبان در نياز / بر همين در كه شود امروز باز
entries.english: Rejoice, O lovers, in supplication at this same door, for it is opened today.

entries.farsi: هر حويجى باشدش كردى دگر / در ميان باغ از سير و كبر
entries.english: Every pot-herb, garlic and caper, has a different bed in the garden.

entries.farsi: تو كه كرد زعفرانى زعفران / ‏باش و آميزش مكن با ديگران
entries.english: Each with its own kind in its own bed drinks moisture for the purpose of becoming mature.

entries.farsi: هر يكى با جنس خود در كرد خود / از براى پختگى نم مى‏خورد
entries.english: You, who are a saffron-bed, be saffron and do not mix with the others.

entries.farsi: آب مى‏خور زعفرانا تا رسى / ‏زعفرانى اندر آن حلوا رسى
entries.english: Drink the water, O saffron that you may attain to maturity: you are saffron, you will attain to that halwá.

entries.farsi: در مكن در كرد شلغم پوز خويش / كه نگردد با تو او هم طبع و كيش
entries.english: Do not put your muzzle into the bed of turnips, for it will not agree with you in nature and habit.

entries.farsi: تو به كردى او به كردى مودعه / ‏ز انكه ارض اللهَّ آمد واسعه
entries.english: You are planted in one bed, it in another bed, because God’s earth is spacious,

entries.farsi: خاصه آن ارضى كه از پهناورى / ‏در سفر گم مى‏شود ديو و پرى
entries.english: Particularly that earth where, on account of its breadth, demon and genie are lost in their journey

entries.farsi: اندر آن بحر و بيابان و جبال / ‏منقطع مى‏گردد اوهام و خيال
entries.english: In those seas and deserts and mountains imagination and fancy fail entirely.

entries.farsi: اين بيابان در بيابانهاى او / همچو اندر بحر پر يك تاى مو
entries.english: In the deserts, this desert is like a single hair in a full sea.

entries.farsi: آب استاده كه سير استش نهان / ‏تازه‏تر خوشتر ز جوهاى روان
entries.english: The still water whose course is hidden is fresher and sweeter than running brooks,

entries.farsi: كاو درون خويش چون جان و روان / ‏سير پنهان دارد و پاى روان
entries.english: For, like the spirit and the soul, it has within itself a hidden course and a moving foot.

entries.farsi: مستمع خفته ست كوته كن خطاب / ‏اى خطيب اين نقش كم كن تو بر آب
entries.english: The auditor is asleep: cut short the address: O preacher, do not draw this picture on water.

entries.farsi: خيز بلقيسا كه بازارى است تيز / زين خسيسان كساد افكن گريز
entries.english: Arise, O Bilqís, for it is a keen market: flee from these vile wretches who ruin trade.

entries.farsi: خيز بلقيسا كنون با اختيار / پيش از آن كه مرگ آرد گير و دار
entries.english: O Bilqís, arise now with free-will, before Death appears in his sovereign might.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گوشت كشد مرگ آن چنان / ‏كه چو دزد آيى به شحنه جان كنان
entries.english: After that, Death will pull your ear in such wise that you will come in agony, like a thief to the magistrate.

entries.farsi: زين خران تا چند باشى نعل دزد / گر همى‏دزدى بيا و لعل دزد
entries.english: How long will you be stealing shoes from these asses? If you are going to steal, come and steal a ruby!

entries.farsi: خواهرانت يافته ملك خلود / تو گرفته ملكت كور و كبود
entries.english: Your sisters have gained the kingdom of everlasting life; you have won the kingdom of misery.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن را كز اين ملكت بجست / ‏كه اجل اين ملك را ويران گر است
entries.english: Oh, happy he that escaped from this kingdom, for Death makes this kingdom desolate.

entries.farsi: خيز بلقيسا بيا بارى ببين / ‏ملكت شاهان و سلطانان دين
entries.english: Arise, O Bilqís! Come; behold for once the kingdom of the Shahs and Sultans of the Religion.

entries.farsi: شسته در باطن ميان گلستان / ‏ظاهرا حادى ميان دوستان
entries.english: He is seated inwardly amidst the rose-garden; outwardly he is acting as a hadi amongst his friends.

entries.farsi: بوستان با او روان هر جا رود / ليك آن از خلق پنهان مى‏شود
entries.english: The garden is going with him wherever he goes, but it is being concealed from the people.

entries.farsi: طوف مى‏كن بر فلك بى‏پر و بال / ‏همچو خورشيد و چو بدر و چون هالل
entries.english: The fruit is making entreaty, saying, “Eat me”; the Water of Life is come, saying, and “Drink me.”

entries.farsi: ميوه‏ها البه‏كنان كز من بچر / آب حيوان آمده كز من بخور
entries.english: Make a circuit of heaven without wing and pinion, like the sun and like the full-moon and like the new moon.

entries.farsi: چون روان باشى روان و پاى نى / ‏مى‏خورى صد لوت و لقمه خاى نى
entries.english: You will be moving, like the spirit, and no foot; you will be eating a hundred dainties, and none chewing a morsel.

entries.farsi: نه نهنگ غم زند بر كشتى‏ات / ‏نه پديد آيد ز مردن زشتى‏ات
entries.english: Neither will the leviathan, Pain, dash against your ship, nor will ugliness appear in you from dying.

entries.farsi: هم تو شاه و هم تو لشكر هم تو تخت / ‏هم تو نيكو بخت باشى هم تو بخت
entries.english: You will be sovereign, army, and throne, all together: you will be both the fortunate and Fortune.

entries.farsi: گر تو نيكو بختى و سلطان زفت / ‏بخت غير تست روزى بخت رفت
entries.english: If you are fortunate and a powerful monarch, Fortune is other than you: one day Fortune goes,

entries.farsi: تو بماندى چون گدايان بى‏نوا / دولت خود هم تو باش اى مجتبى
entries.english: And you are left destitute like beggars. Be you your own fortune, O elect one!

entries.farsi: چون تو باشى بخت خود اى معنوى / ‏پس تو كه بختى ز خود كى گم شوى
entries.english: When you are your own fortune, O man of Reality, then how will you, who art Fortune, lose yourself?

entries.farsi: تو ز خود كى گم شوى اى خوش خصال / ‏چون كه عين تو ترا شد ملك و مال
entries.english: How will you lose yourself, O man with goodly qualities, when your Essence has become your kingdom and riches?

sections.title_fa: بقيه قصه‏ى عمارت كردن سليمان عليه السلام مسجد اقصى را به تعليم و وحى خدا جهت حكمتهايى كه او داند و معاونت ماليكه و ديو و پرى و آدمى آشكارا
sections.title_en: The rest of the story of Solomon, on whom be peace: how he built the Farther Mosque
by instruction and inspiration from God, for wise purposes which He knows; and how angels, demons, genies, and men lent visible aid.

entries.farsi: اى سليمان مسجد اقصى بساز / لشكر بلقيس آمد در نماز
entries.english: “O Solomon, build the Farther Mosque, the army of Bilqis has come into the prayer.”

entries.farsi: چون كه او بنياد آن مسجد نهاد / جن و انس آمد بدن در كار داد
entries.english: When he laid the foundation of that Mosque, genies and men came and threw themselves into the work,

entries.farsi: يك گروه از عشق و قومى بى‏مراد / همچنان كه در ره طاعت عباد
entries.english: One party from love, and another company unwillingly, just as God’s servants in the way of obedience.

entries.farsi: خلق ديوانند و شهوت سلسله / ‏مى‏كشدشان سوى دكان و غله
entries.english: The folk are demons, and desire is the chain dragging them to shop and crops.

entries.farsi: هست اين زنجير از خوف و وله / ‏تو مبين اين خلق را بى‏سلسله
entries.english: This chain is of being afraid and crazed: do not regard these folk as unchained.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشاندشان سوى كسب و شكار / مى‏كشاندشان سوى كان و بحار
entries.english: It drags them to earning and hunting; it drags them to the mine and the seas.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشدشان سوى نيك و سوى بد / گفت حق فى جيدها حبل المسد
entries.english: It drags them to good and to evil: God has said, “On her neck a cord of palm-fibre.

entries.farsi: قد جعلنا الحبل فى اعناقهم / ‏و اتخذنا الحبل من اخالقهم
entries.english: We have put the cordon their necks: We have made the cord of their natural dispositions.

entries.farsi: حرص تو در كار بد چون آتش است / ‏اخگر از رنگ خوش آتش خوش است
entries.english: There is none ever, defiled or recovered, but his fortune is on his neck.”

entries.farsi: ليس من مستقذر مستنقه / ‏قط الا طايره فى عنقه
entries.english: Your greed for evil-doing is like fire: the live coal is pleasing by the fire’s pleasing hue.

entries.farsi: آن سياهى فحم در آتش نهان / ‏چون كه آتش شد سياهى شد عيان
entries.english: The blackness of the coal is hidden in the fire: when the fire is gone, the blackness becomes evident.

entries.farsi: اخگر از حرص تو شد فحم سياه / ‏حرص چون شد ماند آن فحم تباه
entries.english: By your greed the black coal is made live: when the greed is gone, that vicious coal remains.

entries.farsi: آن زمان آن فحم اخگر مى‏نمود / آن نه حسن كار نار حرص بود
entries.english: At that time the coal appeared to be live; that was not the goodness of action: it was the fire of greed.

entries.farsi: حرص كارت را بياراييده بود / حرص رفت و ماند كار تو كبود
entries.english: Greed had embellished your action: greed departed, and your action was left in squalor

entries.farsi: غوله‏اى را كه بر آراييد غول / ‏پخته پندارد كسى كه هست گول
entries.english: One who is foolish will think ripe the ghawla which the ghouls deck out.

entries.farsi: آزمايش چون نمايد جان او / كند گردد ز آزمون دندان او
entries.english: When his soul makes trial, its teeth are blunted by the experiment.

entries.farsi: از هوس آن دام دانه مى‏نمود / عكس غول حرص و آن خود خام بود
entries.english: From vain desire, the reflection of the ghoul, greed, was causing the trap to appear a berry, though in truth it was unripe.

entries.farsi: حرص اندر كار دين و خير جو / چون نماند حرص باشد نغز رو
entries.english: Seek greed in the practice of religion and in good works: they are beautiful, when the greed remains not.

entries.farsi: خيرها نغزند نه از عكس غير / تاب حرص ار رفت ماند تاب خير
entries.english: Good works are beautiful, not through the re flexion of any other thing: if the glow of greed is gone, the glow of good remains;

entries.farsi: تاب حرص از كار دنيا چون برفت / ‏فحم باشد مانده از اخگر به تفت
entries.english: When the glow of greed is gone from worldly work, of the red-hot coal the black ashes are left.

entries.farsi: كودكان را حرص مى‏آرد غرار / تا شوند از ذوق دل دامن سوار
entries.english: Folly excites greed in children, so that from gleefulness of heart they ride a cock-horse

entries.farsi: چون ز كودك رفت آن حرص بدش / ‏بر دگر اطفال خنده آيدش
entries.english: When that evil greed of his is gone from the child, he begins to laugh at the other children,

entries.farsi: كه چه مى‏كردم چه مى‏ديدم در اين / ‏خل ز عكس حرص بنمود انگبين
entries.english: Saying, “What was I doing? What was I seeing in this?” From the reflection of greed the vinegar appeared to be honey.

entries.farsi: آن بناى انبيا بى‏حرص بود / ز آن چنان پيوسته رونقها فزود
entries.english: That edifice of the prophets was without greed; hence the splendours increased so uninterruptedly.

entries.farsi: اى بسا مسجد بر آورده كرام / ‏ليك نبود مسجد اقصاش نام
entries.english: Oh, many a mosque has the noble erected, but “the Farther Mosque” is not its name.

entries.farsi: كعبه را كه هر دمى عزى فزود / آن ز اخالصات ابراهيم بود
entries.english: The grandeur which at every moment accrued to the Ka’ba— that was from the acts done in pure devotion by Abraham.

entries.farsi: فضل آن مسجد ز خاك و سنگ نيست / ‏ليك در بناش حرص و جنگ نيست
entries.english: The excellence of that mosque is not from earth and stone, but there is no greed or enmity in its builder.

entries.farsi: نه كتبشان مثل كتب ديگران / ‏نه مساجدشان نه كسب و خان و مان
entries.english: Their Books are not as the books of others, nor their mosques nor their means of livelihood nor their houses and homes,

entries.farsi: نه ادبشان نه غضبشان نه نكال / ‏نه نعاس و نه قياس و نه مقال
entries.english: Nor their observance of respect nor their anger nor their chastisement nor their slumber nor their reasoning nor their discourse

entries.farsi: هر يكيشان را يكى فرى دگر / مرغ جانشان طاير از پرى دگر
entries.english: To each one of them belongs a different glory: the bird, their spirit, flies with a different wing.

entries.farsi: دل همى‏لرزد ز ذكر حالشان / ‏قبله‏ى افعال ما افعالشان
entries.english: The heart is trembling at mention of their estate: their actions are the qibla of our actions.

entries.farsi: مرغشان را بيضه‏ها زرين بده ست / ‏نيم شب جانشان سحرگه بين شده ست
entries.english: The eggs laid by their bird are golden: at midnight their spirit has beheld the dawn.

entries.farsi: هر چه گويم من به جان نيكوى قوم / ‏نقص گفتم گشته ناقص گوى قوم
entries.english: Whatever I say with my soul in praise of the company, I have depreciated: I have become a disparager of the company.

entries.farsi: مسجد اقصى بسازيد اى كرام / ‏كه سليمان باز آمد و السلام
entries.english: O you noble, build “the Farther Mosque,” for Solomon has returned—and peace!

entries.farsi: ور ازين ديوان و پريان سر كشند / جمله را امالك در چنبر كشند
entries.english: And if the demons and genies refuse this, the angels will drag them all into bondage.

entries.farsi: ديو يك دم كژ رود از مكر و زرق / ‏تازيانه آيدش بر سر چو برق
entries.english: The demon once make a false step on account of deceit and hypocrisy, the whip comes on his head like lightning.

entries.farsi: چون سليمان شو كه تا ديوان تو / سنگ برند از پى ايوان تو
entries.english: Become like Solomon, in order that your demons may hew stone for your palace.

entries.farsi: چون سليمان باش بى‏وسواس و ريو / تا ترا فرمان برد جنى و ديو
entries.english: Be devoid, like Solomon, of thoughts which tempt to evil-doing and of fraud, that genie and demon may obey your command.

entries.farsi: آن سليمانى دال منسوخ نيست / ‏در سر و سرت سليمانى كنى است
entries.english: This heart is your seal— take heed lest the seal fall a prey to the demon!

entries.farsi: خاتم تو اين دل است و هوش دار / تا نگردد ديو را خاتم شكار
entries.english: Then the demon possessing the seal will always exercise the sway of Solomon over you: beware, and peace!

entries.farsi: پس سليمانى كند بر تو مدام / ‏ديو با خاتم حذر كن و السلام
entries.english: O heart, that sway of Solomon is not abrogated: in your head and inmost consciousness is one that exercises the sway of Solomon.

entries.farsi: دست جنباند چو دست او و ليك / ‏در ميان هر دوشان فرقى است نيك
entries.english: The demon too exercises the sway of Solomon for a time, but how should every weaver weave satin?

entries.farsi: ديو هم وقتى سليمانى كند / ليك هر جوالهه اطلس كى تند
entries.english: He moves his hand like his hand, but there is a good difference between the two of them.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى شاعر و صله دادن شاه و مضاعف كردن آن وزير بو الحسن نام
sections.title_en: Story of the poet and how the king gave him a reward and how the vizier, whose name was Bu ’l-Hasan, made it many times greater.

entries.farsi: شاعرى آورد شعرى پيش شاه / ‏بر اميد خلعت و اكرام و جاه
entries.english: A poet brought a poem before the king in hope of robes of honour and bounty and rank.

entries.farsi: اه مكرم بود فرمودش هزار / از زر سرخ و كرامات و نثار
entries.english: The king was munificent: he ordered him a thousand of red gold and bounties and largesse.

entries.farsi: پس وزيرش گفت كاين اندك بود / ده هزارش هديه وا ده تا رود
entries.english: Then the vizier said to him, “This is little: bestow a gift of ten thousand that he may depart.

entries.farsi: از چنو شاعر پس از تو بحر دست / ‏ده هزارى كه بگفتم اندك است
entries.english: From a poet like him intellect; from you, whose hand is like the ocean, the ten thousand which I mentioned is little.”

entries.farsi: فقه گفت آن شاه را و فلسفه / ‏تا بر آمد عشر خرمن از كفه
entries.english: He argued and reasoned with the king until the tithe on the threshed grain was made up out of the unthreshed ears of corn.

entries.farsi: ده هزارش داد و خلعت در خورش / ‏خانه‏ى شكر و ثنا گشت آن سرش
entries.english: He gave him the ten thousand and the robes of honour suitable to him: his head became a house of thanksgiving and praise.

entries.farsi: پس تفحص كرد كاين سعى كه بود / شاه را اهليت من كى نمود
entries.english: Then he made inquiry, saying, “Whose work was this? Who declared my merit to the king?”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش فالن الدين وزير / آن حسن نام و حسن خلق و ضمير
entries.english: So they told him, “al-Din, the vizier, he whose name is Hasan and whose disposition and heart are good (hasan).”

entries.farsi: در ثناى او يكى شعرى دراز / بر نبشت و سوى خانه رفت باز
entries.english: He wrote a long poem in praise of him and returned home.

entries.farsi: بى‏زبان و لب همان نعماى شاه / ‏مدح شه مى‏كرد و خلعتهاى شاه
entries.english: Without tongue or lip that bounty of the king and those robes of honour bestowed by the king were praising the king.

sections.title_fa: باز آمدن آن شاعر بعد چند سال به اميد همان صله و هزار دينار فرمودن بر قاعده‏ى خويش و گفتن وزير نو هم حسن نام شاه را كه اين سخت بسيار است و ما را خرجهاست و خزينه خالى است و من ‏او را به ده يك آن خشنود كنم
sections.title_en: How after several years the poet came back in the hope of the same reward, and how the king according to his custom ordered a thousand dinars to be given to him,
and how the new vizier, who was also named Hasan, said to the king, “This is very much: we have (great) expenses and the treasury is empty, and I will satisfy him with a tenth of that.”

entries.farsi: بعد سالى چند بهر رزق و كشت / ‏شاعر از فقر و عوز محتاج گشت
entries.english: After some years the poet, on account of poverty and destitution, became in need for daily bread and seed-produce.

entries.farsi: گفت وقت فقر و تنگى دو دست / ‏جست و جوى آزموده بهتر است
entries.english: He said, “At the time of poverty and close-handedness, it is better to seek out one who has been tried.

entries.farsi: درگهى را كازمودم در كرم / ‏حاجت نو را بدان جانب برم
entries.english: The court which I have tried in regard to generosity— I will carry the new request to the same quarter.”

entries.farsi: معنى اللهَّ گفت آن سيبويه / ‏يولهون فى الحوائج هم لديه
entries.english: That Sibawayh said the meaning of Allah they take refuge (yawlahúna) with Him in their needs.

entries.farsi: گفت الهنا فى حوايجنا اليك / ‏و التمسناها وجدناها لديك
entries.english: He said, “We have repaired for succour unto You in our needs and have sought them found them with You.”

entries.farsi: صد هزاران عاقل اندر وقت درد / جمله نالان پيش آن ديان فرد
entries.english: In the hour of affliction hundreds of thousands of intelligent persons are all crying before that unique Judge.

entries.farsi: هيچ ديوانه‏ى فليوى اين كند / بر بخيلى عاجزى كديه تند
entries.english: Would any mad fool do this, continue to beg of a miser incapable?

entries.farsi: گر نديدندى هزاران بار بيش / ‏عاقالن كى جان كشيدنديش پيش
entries.english: Unless the intelligent had experienced more than a thousand times, how should they have betaken themselves to Him?

entries.farsi: بلكه جمله‏ى ماهيان در موجها / جمله‏ى پرندگان بر اوجها
entries.english: No, all the fish in the waves, all the birds in the lofty regions,

entries.farsi: پيل و گرگ و حيدر اشكار نيز / اژدهاى زفت و مور و مار نيز
entries.english: The elephant, the wolf, the hunting lion, the huge dragon, the ant and the snake,

entries.farsi: بلكه خاك و باد و آب و هر شرار / مايه زو يابند هم دى هم بهار
entries.english: No, earth and wind and water and every spark gain subsistence from Him both in December and spring.

entries.farsi: هر دمش البه كند اين آسمان / ‏كه فرو مگذارم اى حق يك زمان
entries.english: This heaven is making entreaty unto Him incessantly— “Do not forsake me, O God, for a single moment!

entries.farsi: استن من عصمت و حفظ تو است / ‏جمله مطوى يمين آن دو دست
entries.english: Your safeguarding and protection is my pillar: all is enfolded in the might of those two Hands.”

entries.farsi: وين زمين گويد كه دارم برقرار / اى كه بر آبم تو كرده ستى سوار
entries.english: And this earth says, “Preserve me, O You who have caused me to ride upon the water.”

entries.farsi: جملگان كيسه از او بر دوختند / دادن حاجت از او آموختند
entries.english: All have sewn up their purses from Him and have learned from Him to give the wants.

entries.farsi: هر نبيى زو بر آورده برات / ‏استعينوا منه صبرا او صالت
entries.english: Every prophet has received from Him the guarantee seek help of Him with patience or prayer.

entries.farsi: هين از او خواهيد نه از غير او / آب در يم جو مجو در خشك جو
entries.english: Come; ask of Him, not of anyone except Him: seek water in the sea, do not seek it in the dry river-bed.

entries.farsi: ور بخواهى از دگر هم او دهد / بر كف ميلش سخا هم او نهد
entries.english: And if you ask of another, it is He that gives; it is He that lays generosity on the open hand of his inclination.

entries.farsi: آن كه معرض را ز زر قارون كند / رو بدو آرى به طاعت چون كند
entries.english: He who with gold makes one that turns away a Qárún how will He do you turn your face towards Him in obedience!

entries.farsi: بار ديگر شاعر از سوداى داد / روى سوى آن شه محسن نهاد
entries.english: The poet, from passionate desire for bounty, set his face a second time towards that beneficent king.

entries.farsi: هديه‏ى شاعر چه باشد شعر نو / پيش محسن آرد و بنهد گرو
entries.english: What is the poet’s offering? A new poem: he brings it to the beneficent and deposits it as his stake.

entries.farsi: محسنان با صد عطا و جود و بر / زر نهاده شاعران را منتظر
entries.english: The beneficent have deposited gold and are waiting for the poets with a hundred gifts and liberalities and kindnesses.

entries.farsi: پيششان شعرى به از صد تنگ شعر / خاصه شاعر كاو گهر آرد ز قعر
entries.english: In their eyes a poem (shi‘r) is better than a hundred bales of silk robes (sha‘r), especially a poet who fetches pearls from the depths.

entries.farsi: آدمى اول حريص نان بود / ز انكه قوت و نان ستون جان بود
entries.english: At first a man is greedy for bread, because food and bread are the pillar of life.

entries.farsi: سوى كسب و سوى غصب و صد حيل / ‏جان نهاده بر كف از حرص و امل
entries.english: On account of greed and expectation he runs every risk in the way of earning his livelihood and seizing property by violence and a hundred devices.

entries.farsi: چون به نادر گشت مستغنى ز نان / ‏عاشق نام است و مدح شاعران
entries.english: When, rarely, he becomes independent of bread, he is in love with fame and the praise of poets,

entries.farsi: تا كه اصل و فصل او را بر دهند / در بيان فضل او منبر نهند
entries.english: In order that they may give fruit to his root and branch and may set up a pulpit to declare his excellence,

entries.farsi: تا كه كر و فر و زر بخشى او / همچو عنبر بو دهد در گفت‏وگو
entries.english: So that his pomp and magnificence and lavishing of gold may yield a perfume, like ambergris, in song.

entries.farsi: خلق ما بر صورت خود كرد حق / ‏وصف ما از وصف او گيرد سبق
entries.english: God created us in His image: our qualities are instructed by His qualities.

entries.farsi: چون كه آن خالق شكر و حمد جوست / ‏آدمى را مدح جويى نيز خوست
entries.english: Inasmuch as the Creator desires thanksgiving and glorification, it is also the nature of man to desire praise,

entries.farsi: خاصه مرد حق كه در فضل است چست / ‏پر شود ز آن باد چون خيك درست
entries.english: Especially the man of God, who is active in excellence,: he becomes filled with that wind, like an undamaged leather bag;

entries.farsi: ور نباشد اهل ز آن باد دروغ / ‏خيك بدريده‏ست كى گيرد فروغ
entries.english: But if he be not worthy, the bag is rent by that wind of falsehood: how should it receive lustre?

entries.farsi: اين مثل از خود نگفتم اى رفيق / ‏سرسرى مشنو چو اهلى و مفيق
entries.english: I have not invented this parable, O comrade: do not hear it silly, if you are worthy and restored to your senses.

entries.farsi: اين پيمبر گفت چون بشنيد قدح / ‏كه چرا فربه شود احمد به مدح
entries.english: The Prophet said this, when he heard vituperation, “Why is Ahmad made fat by praise?”

entries.farsi: رفت شاعر پيش آن شاه و ببرد / شعر اندر شكر احسان كان نمرد
entries.english: The poet went to the king and brought a poem in thanks for beneficence, saying that it never died.

entries.farsi: محسنان مردند و احسانها بماند / اى خنك آن را كه اين مركب براند
entries.english: The beneficent died, and acts of beneficence remained: oh, blest is he that rode this steed!

entries.farsi: ظالمان مردند و ماند آن ظلمها / واى جانى كاو كند مكر و دها
entries.english: The unjust died, and those acts of injustice remained: alas for the soul that practices deceit and fraud!

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر خنك آن را كه او / شد ز دنيا ماند از او فعل نكو
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Blest is he who departed from this world and left good deeds behind him.”

entries.farsi: مرد محسن ليك احسانش نمرد / نزد يزدان دين و احسان نيست خرد
entries.english: The beneficent man died, but his beneficence died not: with God, religion and beneficence are not of small account.

entries.farsi: واى آن كاو مرد و عصيانش نمرد / تا نپندارى به مرگ او جان ببرد
entries.english: Alas for him who died and whose disobedience died not: beware of thinking that by death he saved his soul.

entries.farsi: اين رها كن ز انكه شاعر بر گذر / وام دار است و قوى محتاج زر
entries.english: Dismiss this, for the poet is on the way— in debt and mightily in need of gold.

entries.farsi: برد شاعر شعر سوى شهريار / بر اميد بخشش و احسان يار
entries.english: The poet brought the poem to the king in hope of last year’s donation and benefit—
