entries.farsi: نازنين شعرى پر از در درست / ‏بر اميد و بوى اكرام نخست
entries.english: A charming poem full of flawless pearls, in hope and expectation of the first munificence

entries.farsi: شاه هم بر خوى خود گفتش هزار / چون چنين بد عادت آن شهريار
entries.english: The Shah indeed, according to his habit, ordered a thousand to him, since such was the custom of that monarch;

entries.farsi: ليك اين بار آن وزير پر ز جود / بر براق عز ز دنيا رفته بود
entries.english: But, on this occasion, the bountiful vizier had departed from the present life, on the Buraq of glory,

entries.farsi: بر مقام او وزير نو رئيس / ‏گشته ليكن سخت بى‏رحم و خسيس
entries.english: And in his place a new vizier had assumed authority; but very pitiless and mean.

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه خرجها داريم ما / شاعرى را نبود اين بخشش جزا
entries.english: He said, “O king, we have outlays: this donation is not the reward for a poet.

entries.farsi: من به ربع عشر اين اى مغتنم / ‏مرد شاعر را خوش و راضى كنم
entries.english: With a fortieth part of this, O you eagerly sought, I will make the poet man happy and content.”

entries.farsi: خلق گفتندش كه او را پيش دست / ‏ده هزاران زين دالور برده است
entries.english: The people said to him, “He carried away a sum of ten thousand in ready money from this valiant.

entries.farsi: بعد شكر كلك خوايى چون كند / بعد سلطانى گدايى چون كند
entries.english: After sugar, how should he chew cane? After having been a sultan, how should he practice beggary?”

entries.farsi: آن گه ار خاكش دهم از راه من / ‏در ربايد همچو گلبرگ از چمن
entries.english: He replied, “I will squeeze him in torment, that he may be made wretched and worn out by waiting;

entries.farsi: گفت بفشارم و را اندر فشار / تا شود زار و نزار از انتظار
entries.english: Then, if I give him earth from the road, he will snatch it as rose-leaves from the garden.

entries.farsi: اين بمن بگذار كه استادم در اين / ‏گر تقاضاگر بود هم آتشين
entries.english: Leave this to me, for I am expert in this, even if the claimant be fiery.

entries.farsi: از ثريا گر بپرد تا ثرى / نرم گردد چون ببيند او مرا
entries.english: Though he fly from the Pleiades to the earth, he will become meek when he sees me.

entries.farsi: گفت سلطانش برو فرمان تراست / ‏ليك شادش كن كه نيكو گوى ماست
entries.english: The king said to him, “Go: ’it is for you to command; but make him happy, for he is my eulogist.”

entries.farsi: گفت او را و دو صد اوميد ليس / ‏تو به من بگذار و اين بر من نويس
entries.english: He said, “Leave him and two hundred lickers-up of hope to me, and write this against me.”

entries.farsi: پس فگندش صاحب اندر انتظار / شد زمستان و دى و آمد بهار
entries.english: Then the minister threw him into expectation: winter and December passed and spring came.

entries.farsi: شاعر اندر انتظارش پير شد / پس زبون اين غم و تدبير شد
entries.english: In expectation of it the poet grew old; then he was crushed by this anxiety and making shift to provide,

entries.farsi: انتظارم كشت بارى گو برو / تا رهد اين جان مسكين از گرو
entries.english: And said, “If there is no gold, please give me abuse; so that my soul may be delivered I may be your slave.

entries.farsi: گفت اگر زر نه كه دشنامم دهى / ‏تا رهد جانم ترا باشم رهى
entries.english: Expectation has killed me: at least bid me go, that this wretched soul may be delivered from bondage.”

entries.farsi: بعد از آنش داد ربع عشر آن / ‏ماند شاعر اندر انديشه‏ى گران
entries.english: After that, he gave him the fortieth part of that: the poet remained in heavy thought,

entries.farsi: كان چنان نقد و چنان بسيار بود / اين كه دير اشكفت دسته‏ى خار بود
entries.english: “That was so promptly paid and was so much: this one that blossomed late was a handful of thorns.”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش كه آن دستور راد / رفت از دنيا خدا مزدت دهاد
entries.english: Then they said to him, “That generous vizier has departed from this life: may God reward you!

entries.farsi: كه مضاعف زو همى‏شد آن عطا / كم همى‏افتاد بخشش را خطا
entries.english: For those gifts were always multiplied by him: there was no fault to be found with the donations;

entries.farsi: اين زمان او رفت و احسان را ببرد / او نمرد الحق بلى احسان بمرد
entries.english: Now, he is gone and has taken beneficence away: he is not dead; beneficence is dead, yes, truly.

entries.farsi: رفت از ما صاحب راد و رشيد / صاحب سالخ درويشان رسيد
entries.english: The generous and upright minister is gone from us; the minister who is a flayer of the poor has arrived.

entries.farsi: رو بگير اين را و ز اينجا شب گريز / تا نگيرد با تو اين صاحب ستيز
entries.english: Go, take this and flee from here by night, lest this minister pick a quarrel with you.

entries.farsi: ما به صد حيلت از او اين هديه را / بستديم اى بى‏خبر از جهد ما
entries.english: We have obtained this gift from him by a hundred devices, O you who are ignorant of our exertions.”

entries.farsi: رو به ايشان كرد و گفت اى مشفقان / ‏از كجا آمد بگوييد اين عوان
entries.english: He turned his face to them and said, “O kindly men, tell, from where did this myrmidon come?

entries.farsi: چيست نام اين وزير جامه كن / ‏قوم گفتندش كه نامش هم حسن
entries.english: What is the name of this vizier who tears off the clothes?” The company said to him, “His name too is Hasan.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب نام آن و نام اين / ‏چون يكى آمد دريغ اى رب دين
entries.english: He cried, “O Lord, how are the names of that one and this one the same? Alas, O Lord of the Judgement!

entries.farsi: آن حسن نامى كه از يك كلك او / صد وزير و صاحب آيد جود خو
entries.english: That Hasan by name that by a single pen of his a hundred viziers and ministers are disposed to liberality

entries.farsi: اين حسن كز ريش زشت اين حسن / ‏مى‏توان بافيد اى جان صد رسن
entries.english: This Hasan that from the ugly beard of this Hasan you canst weave, O soul, a hundred ropes.”

entries.farsi: بر چنين صاحب چو شه اصغا كند / شاه و ملكش را ابد رسوا كند
entries.english: When a king listens to such a minister, he disgraces the king and his kingdom unto everlasting.

sections.title_fa: ‏مانستن بد رايى اين وزير دون در افساد مروت شاه به وزير فرعون يعنى هامان در افساد قابليت فرعون
sections.title_en: The resemblance of the bad judgement of this base vizier in corrupting the king’s generosity to the vizier of Pharaoh, namely, Haman, in corrupting the readiness of Pharaoh to receive

entries.farsi: چند آن فرعون مى‏شد نرم و رام / ‏چون شنيدى او ز موسى آن كالم
entries.english: How many a time did Pharaoh soften and become submissive when he was hearing that Word from Moses!—

entries.farsi: آن كالمى كه بدادى سنگ شير / از خوشى آن كالم بى‏نظير
entries.english: That Word that from the sweetness of that incomparable Word the rock would have yielded milk.

entries.farsi: چون به هامان كه وزيرش بود او / مشورت كردى كه كينش بود خو
entries.english: Whenever he took counsel with Haman, who was his vizier and whose nature it was to hate,

entries.farsi: پس بگفتى تا كنون بودى خديو / بنده گردى ژنده پوشى را به ريو
entries.english: Then he would say, “Until now you have been the Khedive: will you become, through deception, the slave to a wearer of rags?”

entries.farsi: همچو سنگ منجنيقى آمدى / ‏آن سخن بر شيشه خانه‏ى او زدى
entries.english: Those words would come like a stone shot by a catapult and strike upon his glass house.

entries.farsi: هر چه صد روز آن كليم خوش خطاب / ‏ساختى در يك دم او كردى خراب
entries.english: All that the Kalím of sweet address built up in a hundred days he would destroy in one moment.

entries.farsi: عقل تو دستور و مغلوب هواست / ‏در وجودت ره زن راه خداست
entries.english: Your intellect is the vizier and is overcome by sensuality: in your being it is a brigand on the Way to God.

entries.farsi: ناصحى ربانيى پندت دهد / آن سخن را او به فن طرحى نهد
entries.english: A godly monitor give you good advice, it will artfully put those words aside,

entries.farsi: كاين نه بر جاى است هين از جا مشو / نيست چندان با خود آ شيدا مشو
entries.english: Saying, “These are not well-founded: take heed, don’t be carried away; they are not so much: come to yourself, don’t be crazed.”

entries.farsi: شاد آن شاهى كه او را دست‏گير / باشد اندر كار چون آصف وزير
entries.english: Alas for the king whose vizier is this: the place of them both is vengeful Hell.

entries.farsi: واى آن شه كه وزيرش اين بود / جاى هر دو دوزخ پر كين بود
entries.english: Happy is the king whose helper in affairs is a vizier like Asaf.

entries.farsi: شاه عادل چون قرين او شود / نام آن نُورٌ عَلى‏ نُورٍ بود
entries.english: When the just king is associated with him, his name is light upon light.

entries.farsi: چون سليمان شاه و چون آصف وزير / نور بر نور است و عنبر بر عبير
entries.english: A king like Solomon and a vizier like Asaf are light upon light and ambergris upon mixed perfumes (‘abir).

entries.farsi: شاه فرعون و چو هامانش وزير / هر دو را نبود ز بد بختى گزير
entries.english: The king Pharaoh and his vizier like Haman, ill-fortune is inevitable for both.

entries.farsi: پس بود ظلمات بعضى فوق بعض / ‏نه خرد يار و نه دولت روز عرض
entries.english: Then it is darkness, one part over another: neither intellect nor fortune shall be their friend on the Day of Judgement.

entries.farsi: من نديدم جز شقاوت در لئام / گر تو ديده‏ستى رسان از من سالم
entries.english: I have not seen anything but misery in the vile: if you have seen, convey the salaam from me.

entries.farsi: همچو جان باشد شه و صاحب چو عقل / ‏عقل فاسد روح را آرد به نقل
entries.english: The king is as the spirit, and the vizier as the intellect: the corrupt intellect brings the spirit into movement.

entries.farsi: آن فرشته‏ى عقل چون هاروت شد / سحر آموز دو صد طاغوت شد
entries.english: When the angelical intellect became a Harut, it became the teacher in magic to two hundred devils.

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى را وزير خود مگير / عقل كل را ساز اى سلطان وزير
entries.english: Do not take the particular intellect as your vizier: make the Universal Intellect your vizier, O king.

entries.farsi: مر هوا را تو وزير خود مساز / كه بر آيد جان پاكت از نماز
entries.english: Do not make sensuality your vizier, else your pure spirit will cease from prayer,

entries.farsi: عقل را دو ديده در پايان كار / بهر آن گل مى‏كشد او رنج خار
entries.english: For this sensuality is full of greed and sees the immediate present, the Intellect takes thought for the Day of Judgement.

entries.farsi: كاين هوا پر حرص و حالى بين بود / عقل را انديشه يوم دين بود
entries.english: The two eyes of the Intellect are on the end of things: it endures the pain of the thorn for the sake of that Rose

entries.farsi: كه نفرسايد نريزد در خزان / ‏باد هر خرطوم اخشم دور از آن
entries.english: Which does not fade and drop in autumn— far from it be the wind of every nose that cannot smell!

sections.title_fa: نشستن ديو بر مقام سليمان عليه السلام و تشبه كردن او به كارهاى سليمان و فرق ظاهر ميان هر ‏دو سليمان و ديو خويشتن را سليمان بن داود نام كردن
sections.title_en: How the Demon sat on the place of Solomon, on whom be peace, and imitated his actions;
and concerning the manifest difference between the two Solomons, and how the Demon called himself Solomon son of David.

entries.farsi: ور چه عقلت هست با عقل دگر / يار باش و مشورت كن اى پدر
entries.english: Even if you have intellect, associate and consult with another intellect, O father.

entries.farsi: با دو عقل از بس بالها وارهى / ‏پاى خود بر اوج گردونها نهى
entries.english: With two intellects you will be delivered from many afflictions: you will plant your foot on the summit of the heavens.

entries.farsi: ديو گر خود را سليمان نام كرد / ملك برد و مملكت را رام كرد
entries.english: If the Demon called himself Solomon and won the kingdom and made the empire subject,

entries.farsi: صورت كار سليمان ديده بود / صورت اندر سر ديوى مى‏نمود
entries.english: He had seen the form of Solomon’s action; within the form the spirit of the demon was appearing.

entries.farsi: خلق گفتند اين سليمان بى‏صفاست / ‏از سليمان تا سليمان فرق‏هاست
entries.english: The people said, “This Solomon is without excellence: there are differences between Solomon and Solomon.”

entries.farsi: او چو بيدارى است اين همچون وسن / ‏همچنان كه آن حسن با اين حسن
entries.english: He is like wakefulness; this one is like sleep; as between that Hasan and this Hasan.

entries.farsi: ديو مى‏گفتى كه حق بر شكل من / ‏صورتى كرده ست خوش بر اهرمن
entries.english: The Demon would reply, “God has bestowed on Ahriman a pleasing form in the likeness of me.

entries.farsi: ديو را حق صورت من داده است / ‏تا نيندازد شما را او به شست
entries.english: God has given my aspect to the Devil: let him not cast you into his net!

entries.farsi: ديوشان از مكر اين مى‏گفت ليك / ‏مى‏نمود اين عكس در دلهاى نيك
entries.english: If he appear with the pretence, beware! Do not have regard to his form.”

entries.farsi: گر پديد آيد به دعوى زينهار / صورت او را مداريد اعتبار
entries.english: The Demon was saying this to them from guile, but in good hearts the reverse of this was apparent.

entries.farsi: نيست بازى با مميز خاصه او / كه بود تمييز و عقلش غيب گو
entries.english: There are no playing tricks with the discerning man, especially him whose discernment and intelligence speak of the Unseen.

entries.farsi: هيچ سحر و هيچ تلبيس و دغل / ‏مى‏نبندد پرده بر اهل دول
entries.english: No magic and no imposture and fraud will bind a veil upon the owners of empire.

entries.farsi: پس همى‏گفتند با خود در جواب / ‏باژگونه مى‏روى اى كج خطاب
entries.english: Hence they were saying to themselves in reply, “You are going upside down, O you who are addressed falsely.

entries.farsi: باژگونه رفت خواهى همچنين / ‏سوى دوزخ اسفل اندر سافلين
entries.english: Upside down likewise you will go towards hell, the lowest among the low.

entries.farsi: او اگر معزول گشته است و فقير / هست در پيشانى‏اش بدر منير
entries.english: If he has been deposed and reduced to poverty, the radiant full-moon is on his forehead.

entries.farsi: تو اگر انگشترى را برده‏اى / ‏دوزخى چون زمهرير افسرده‏اى
entries.english: If you have carried off the signet-ring, you are a Hell frozen like piercing cold.

entries.farsi: ما به بوش و عارض و طاق و طرنب / ‏سر كجا كه خود همى‏ننهيم سنب
entries.english: On account of ostentation and vain show and pomp and grandeur how the head? For we will not lay even a hoof

entries.farsi: ور به غفلت ما نهيم او را جبين / ‏پنجه‏ى مانع بر آيد از زمين
entries.english: And if heedlessly we should lay the forehead to him, a preventing hand will rise up from the earth,

entries.farsi: كه منه آن سر مر اين سر زير را / هين مكن سجده مر اين ادبار را
entries.english: ‘Do not lay the head before this headlong-fallen one; beware, do not bow down to this ill-fated wretch!’”

entries.farsi: كردمى من شرح اين بس جان فزا / گر نبودى غيرت و رشك خدا
entries.english: I would have given a very soul-quickening exposition of this, were it not for the indignation and jealousy of God.

entries.farsi: هم قناعت كن تو بپذير اين قدر / تا بگويم شرح اين وقتى دگر
entries.english: Still, be content and accept this amount, that I may explain this at another time.

entries.farsi: نام خود كرده سليمان نبى / ‏روى پوشى مى‏كند بر هر صبى
entries.english: He, having called himself by the name of the prophet Solomon, makes it a mask to deceive every boy.

entries.farsi: در گذر از صورت و از نام خيز / از لقب و ز نام در معنى گريز
entries.english: Pass on from the form and rise beyond the name: flee from title and from name into reality.

entries.farsi: پس بپرس از حد او و ز فعل او / در ميان حد و فعل او را بجو
entries.english: Inquire, then, about his degree and his actions: in the midst of his degree and actions seek him.

sections.title_fa: در آمدن سليمان عليه السلام هر روز در مسجد اقصى بعد از تمام شدن جهت عبادت و ارشاد عابدان و معتكفان و رستن عقاقير در مسجد
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, entered the Farther Mosque daily, after its completion,
for the purpose of worshipping and directing the worshippers and devotees; and how medicinal herbs grew in the Mosque.

entries.farsi: هر صباحى چون سليمان آمدى / ‏خاضع اندر مسجد اقصى شدى
entries.english: Every morning, when Solomon came and made supplication in the Farther Mosque

entries.farsi: نو گياهى رسته ديدى اندر او / پس بگفتى نام و نفع خود بگو
entries.english: He saw that a new plant had grown there; then he would say, “Tell your name and use.

entries.farsi: تو چه دارويى چيى نامت چى است / ‏تو زيان كى و نفعت بر كى است
entries.english: What medicine are you? What are you? What is your name? To whom are you hurtful and for whom is your usefulness?”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتى هر گياهى فعل و نام / ‏كه من آن را جانم و اين را حمام
entries.english: Then every plant would tell its effect and name, saying “I am life to that one, and death to this one.

entries.farsi: من مر اين را زهرم و او را شكر / نام من اين است بر لوح از قدر
entries.english: I am poison to this one, and sugar to that one: this is my name on the Tablet by the Divine decree.”

entries.farsi: پس طبيبان از سليمان ز آن گيا / عالم و دانا شدندى مقتدا
entries.english: Then from Solomon about those plants the physicians became learned and wise authorities,

entries.farsi: تا كتبهاى طبيبى ساختند / جسم را از رنج مى‏پرداختند
entries.english: So that they compiled medical books and were relieving the body from pain.

entries.farsi: اين نجوم و طب وحى انبياست / ‏عقل و حس را سوى بى‏سوره كجاست
entries.english: This astronomy and medicine is Divine inspiration to the prophets: where is the way for intellect and sense towards that which is without direction?

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى عقل استخراج نيست / ‏جز پذيراى فن و محتاج نيست
entries.english: The particular intellect is not the intellect of production: it is only the receiver of science and is in need.

entries.farsi: قابل تعليم و فهم است اين خرد / ليك صاحب وحى تعليمش دهد
entries.english: This intellect is capable of being taught and of apprehending, but the man possessed of Divine inspiration gives it the teaching.

entries.farsi: جمله حرفتها يقين از وحى بود / اول او ليك عقل آن را فزود
entries.english: Assuredly, in their beginning, all trades were from Divine inspiration, but the intellect added to them.

entries.farsi: هيچ حرفت را ببين كاين عقل ما / تاند او آموختن بى‏اوستا
entries.english: Consider whether this intellect of ours can learn any trade without a master.

entries.farsi: گر چه اندر مكر موى اشكاف بد / هيچ پيشه رام بى‏استا نشد
entries.english: Although it was hair-splitting in contrivance, no trade was subdued without a master.

entries.farsi: دانش پيشه از اين عقل ار بدى / ‏پيشه‏ى بى‏اوستا حاصل شدى
entries.english: If knowledge of a trade were from this intellect, any trade would be acquired without a master.

sections.title_fa: آموختن پيشه‏ى گوركنى قابيل از زاغ پيش از آن كه در عالم علم گوركنى و گور بود
sections.title_en: How Qabil (Cain) learned the trade of grave-digging from the crow (raven), before knowledge of grave-digging and graves existed in the world.

entries.farsi: كندن گورى كه كمتر پيشه بود / كى ز فكر و حيله و انديشه بود
entries.english: When was grave-digging, which was the meanest trade, from thought and cunning and meditation?

entries.farsi: كه كجا غايب كنم اين كشته را / اين به خون و خاك در آغشته را
entries.english: If Qabil had possessed this understanding, how should he have placed Abel on his head?—

entries.farsi: گر بدى اين فهم مر قابيل را / كى نهادى بر سر او هابيل را
entries.english: Saying, “Where shall I hide this murdered one, this man stained with blood and earth?”

entries.farsi: ديد زاغى زاغ مرده در دهان / ‏بر گرفته تيز مى‏آمد چنان
entries.english: He spied a crow which had taken up a dead crow in its mouth and was approaching so quickly.

entries.farsi: از هوا زير آمد و شد او به فن / ‏از پى تعليم او را گور كن
entries.english: It came down from the air and began skillfully to dig a grave for it for the purpose of teaching.

entries.farsi: پس به چنگال از زمين انگيخت گرد / زود زاغ مرده را در گور كرد
entries.english: Then with its talons it raised dust from the ground and speedily put the dead crow in the grave.

entries.farsi: دفن كردش پس بپوشيدش به خاك / ‏زاغ از الهام حق بد علمناك
entries.english: It buried it, and then it covered it with earth: the crow was endowed with knowledge through the inspiration of God.

entries.farsi: گفت قابيل آه شه بر عقل من / ‏كه بود زاغى ز من افزون به فن
entries.english: Qabil cried, “Oh, fie on my intellect! For a crow is superior to me in skill.”

entries.farsi: عقل كل را گفت ما زاغَ البصر / عقل جزوى مى‏كند هر سو نظر
entries.english: Concerning the Universal Intellect He has said, “The sight did not rove,” the particular intellect is looking in every direction.

entries.farsi: جان كه او دنباله‏ى زاغان پرد / زاغ او را سوى گورستان برد
entries.english: The Intellect whose sight does not rove is the light of the elect; the crow-intellect is the sexton for the dead.

entries.farsi: عقل ما زاغَ است نور خاصگان / عقل زاغ استاد گور مردگان
entries.english: The spirit that flies after crows— the crow carries it towards the graveyard.

entries.farsi: هين مدو اندر پى نفس چو زاغ / ‏كاو به گورستان برد نه سوى باغ
entries.english: Beware! Do not run in pursuit of the crow-like fleshly soul, for it carries to the graveyard, not towards the orchard.

entries.farsi: گر روى رو در پى عنقاى دل / ‏سوى قاف و مسجد اقصاى دل
entries.english: If you go, go in pursuit of the Anqa of the heart, towards the Qaf and Farther Mosque of the heart.

entries.farsi: نو گياهى هر دم از سوداى تو / مى‏دمد در مسجد اقصاى تو
entries.english: Every moment from your cogitation a new plant is growing in your Farther Mosque.

entries.farsi: تو سليمان‏وار داد او بده / ‏پى بر از وى پاى رد بر وى منه
entries.english: Do you, like Solomon, give it its due: investigate it, do not lay upon it the foot of rejection,

entries.farsi: ز انكه حال اين زمين با ثبات / ‏باز گويد با تو انواع نبات
entries.english: Because the various sorts of plants declare to you the state of this firm-set earth

entries.farsi: در زمين گر نيشكر ور خود نى است / ‏ترجمان هر زمين نبت وى است
entries.english: Whether in the earth there are sugar-canes or only reeds, every earth is interpreted by its plants.

entries.farsi: پس زمين دل كه نبتش فكر بود / فكرها اسرار دل را وانمود
entries.english: Therefore the heart’s soil, whereof thought was the plant— thoughts have revealed the heart’s secrets.

entries.farsi: گر سخن كش يابم اندر انجمن / ‏صد هزاران گل برويم چون چمن
entries.english: If I find in the company him that draws the discourse, I, like the garden, will grow hundreds of thousands of roses;

entries.farsi: جنبش هر كس به سوى جاذب است / ‏جذب صادق نه چو جذب كاذب است
entries.english: And if at that time I find the scoundrel who kills the discourse, the deep sayings will flee, like a thief, from my heart.

entries.farsi: ور سخن كش يابم آن دم زن به مزد / مى‏گريزد نكته‏ها از دل چو دزد
entries.english: The movement of everyone is towards the Drawer: the true drawing is not like the false drawing.

entries.farsi: مى‏روى گه گمره و گه در رشد / رشته‏اى پيدا نه و آن كت مى‏كشد
entries.english: Sometimes you are going astray, sometimes aright: the cord is not visible, nor He who is drawing you.

entries.farsi: اشتر كورى مهار تو رهين / تو كشش مى‏بين مهارت را مبين
entries.english: You are a blind camel, and your toggle is in keeping: do you regard the act of drawing, do not regard your toggle.

entries.farsi: گر شدى محسوس جذاب و مهار / پس نماندى اين جهان دار الغرار
entries.english: If the Drawer and the toggle became perceptible, then this world would no longer remain the abode of heedlessness.

entries.farsi: گبر ديدى كاو پى سگ مى‏رود / سخره‏ى ديو ستنبه مى‏شود
entries.english: The infidel saw that he was going after a cur and was being made subject to the hideous Devil,

entries.farsi: در پى او كى شدى مانند هيز / پاى خود را وا كشيدى گبر نيز
entries.english: How should he go at its heels like a catamite? The infidel too would step back.

entries.farsi: گاو گر واقف ز قصابان بدى / ‏كى پى ايشان بدان دكان شدى
entries.english: If the cow were acquainted with the butchers, how should she follow them to that shop?

entries.farsi: يا بخوردى از كف ايشان سبوس / ‏يا بدادى شيرشان از چاپلوس
entries.english: Or eat bran from their hands, or give them milk on account of coaxing?

entries.farsi: ور بخوردى كى علف هضمش شدى / ‏گر ز مقصود علف واقف بدى
entries.english: And if she ate, how should the fodder be digested by her, if she were aware of the purpose of the fodder?

entries.farsi: پس ستون اين جهان خود غفلت است / ‏چيست دولت كاين دوادو بالت است
entries.english: Heedlessness, then, is in sooth the pillar of this world: what is dawlat (worldly fortune)? For this dawádaw (running to and fro) is accompa­ nied by lat (blows).

entries.farsi: اولش دو دو به آخر لت بخور / جز در اين ويرانه نبود مرگ خر
entries.english: The beginning thereof is daw, daw (run, run); in the end lat khwar: the death of the ass is not except in this wilderness.

entries.farsi: تو به جد كارى كه بگرفتى به دست / ‏عيبش اين دم بر تو پوشيده شده‏ست
entries.english: Whenever you have earnestly taken a work in hand, its faultiness has become veiled to you at this moment.

entries.farsi: ز آن همى تانى بدادن تن به كار / كه بپوشيد از تو عيبش كردگار
entries.english: You are able to give yourself up to the work, because the Creator veils its faultiness from you.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر فكر كه گرمى در آن / ‏عيب آن فكرت شده ست از تو نهان
entries.english: Likewise, every thought in which you are hot, the faultiness of that thought of yours has become hidden from you.

entries.farsi: بر تو گر پيدا شدى زو عيب و شين / ‏زو رميدى جانت بُعْدَ المشرقين
entries.english: If its faultiness and disgrace were made visible to you, your soul would flee from it the distance between east and west.

entries.farsi: حال كاخر زو پشيمان مى‏شوى / ‏گر بود اين حالت اول كى دوى
entries.english: The state in which at last you repent of it— if this should be your state at first, how wouldst you run?

entries.farsi: پس بپوشيد اول آن بر جان ما / تا كنيم آن كار بر وفق قضا
entries.english: Therefore He at first veiled that from our souls, in order that we might perform that action in accordance with the Divine destiny.

entries.farsi: اين پشيمانى قضاى ديگر است / ‏اين پشيمانى بهل حق را پرست
entries.english: When the Divine destiny brought its ordainment into view, the eye was opened, so that repentance arrived.

entries.farsi: چون قضا آورد حكم خود پديد / چشم وا شد تا پشيمانى رسيد
entries.english: This repentance is another Divine destiny: abandon this repentance, worship God!

entries.farsi: ور كنى عادت پشيمان خور شوى / ‏زين پشيمانى پشيمان‏تر شوى
entries.english: And if you make a habit and become addicted to repentance, because of this repentance you will become more repentant.

entries.farsi: ترك اين فكر و پشيمانى بگو / حال و يار و كار نيكوتر بجو
entries.english: One half of your life will pass in distraction and the other half will pass in repentance.

entries.farsi: نيم عمرت در پريشانى رود / نيم ديگر در پشيمانى رود
entries.english: Take leave of this thought and repentance: seek a better state and friend and work.

entries.farsi: ور ندارى كار نيكوتر به دست / ‏پس پشيمانيت بر فوت چه است
entries.english: And if you have no better work in hand, then for the omission of what is your repentance?

entries.farsi: گر همى‏دانى ره نيكو پرست / ‏ور ندانى چون بدانى كاين بد است
entries.english: If you know the good way, worship; and if you do not know, how do you know that this way is evil?

entries.farsi: بد ندانى تا ندانى نيك را / ‏ضد را از ضد توان ديد اى فتى
entries.english: You do not know evil till you knows well: from contrary is it possible to discern contrary, O youth.

entries.farsi: چون بدى عاجز پشيمانى ز چيست / ‏عاجزى را باز جو كز جذب كيست
entries.english: Since you were rendered impotent to abandon the thought of this, at that time you were also impotent to commit sin.

entries.farsi: چون ز ترك فكر اين عاجز شدى / ‏از گنه آن گاه هم عاجز بدى
entries.english: Since you were impotent, on account of what is your repentance? Inquire concerning impotence, by whose pull is it?

entries.farsi: عاجزى بى‏قادرى اندر جهان / كس نديده ست و نباشد اين بدان
entries.english: No one has seen impotence in the world without power, nor will it be. Know this.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر آرزو كه مى‏برى / ‏تو ز عيب آن حجابى اندرى
entries.english: Similarly, every desire that you cherish, you are debarred from its faultiness;

entries.farsi: ور نمودى علت آن آرزو / خود رميدى جان تو ز آن جستجو
entries.english: And if the viciousness of that desire had been shown, your soul of its own accord would have recoiled from seeking.

entries.farsi: گر نمودى عيب آن كار او ترا / كس نبردى كش كشان آن سو ترا
entries.english: If He had shown you the faultiness of that work, no one, dragging along, would have taken you in that direction;

entries.farsi: و آن دگر كارى كز آن هستى نفور / ز آن بود كه عيبش آمد در ظهور
entries.english: And that other work from which you are exceedingly averse, the reason is that its faultiness has come into clear view.

entries.farsi: اى خداى راز دان خوش سخن / ‏عيب كار بد ز ما پنهان مكن
entries.english: O God who knows the secret and who art gracious in speech, do not hide from us the faultiness of the evil work;

entries.farsi: عيب كار نيك را منما به ما / تا نگرديم از روش سرد و هبا
entries.english: Do not show unto us the faultiness of the good work, lest we become cold and distracted from journeying.

entries.farsi: هم بر آن عادت سليمان سنى / ‏رفت در مسجد ميان روشنى
entries.english: According to that habit, the exalted Solomon went into the Mosque in the brightness.

entries.farsi: دل ببيند سر بدان چشم صفى / ‏آن حشايش كه شد از عامه خفى
entries.english: The king was seeking the daily rule of seeing the new plants in the Mosque.

entries.farsi: قاعده‏ى هر روز را مى‏جست شاه / ‏كه ببيند مسجد اندر نو گياه
entries.english: The heart with that pure eye secretly sees the herbs that are invisible to the vulgar.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى صوفى كه در ميان گلستان سر بر زانو مراقب بود يارانش گفتند سر بر آور تفرج كن بر ‏گلستان و رياحين و مرغان و آثار رحمه اللهَّ تعالى
sections.title_en: Story of the Sufi who, head on knee, was engaged in meditation in the garden: his friends said to him, “Lift up your head and enjoy the garden and the sweet herbs
and the birds and the marks of the mercy of God most High.”

entries.farsi: صوفيى در باغ از بهر گشاد / صوفيانه روى بر زانو نهاد
entries.english: In the orchard a certain Sufi laid his face in Sufi fashion upon his knee for the sake of revelation;

entries.farsi: پس فرو رفت او به خود اندر نغول / ‏شد ملول از صورت خوابش فضول
entries.english: Then he sank deep down into himself. An impertinent fellow was annoyed by his semblance of slumber.

entries.farsi: كه چه خسبى آخر اندر رز نگر / اين درختان بين و آثار و خضر
entries.english: “Why,” said he, “do you sleep? Nay, look at the vines; behold these trees and marks and green plants.

entries.farsi: امر حق بشنو كه گفته ست انظروا / سوى اين آثار رحمت آر رو
entries.english: Listen to the command of God, for He has said, ‘Look’: turn your face towards these marks of mercy.”

entries.farsi: گفت آثارش دل است اى بو الهوس / ‏آن برون آثار آثار است و بس
entries.english: He replied, “O men of vanity, its marks are the heart: that without is only the mark of the marks.”

entries.farsi: باغها و سبزه‏ها در عين جان / ‏بر برون عكسش چو در آب روان
entries.english: The orchards and verdure are in the very essence of the soul: the reflection thereof upon without is as in running water.

entries.farsi: آن خيال باغ باشد اندر آب / ‏كه كند از لطف آب آن اضطراب
entries.english: In the water there is the phantom of the orchard, which quivers on account of the subtle quality of the water.

entries.farsi: باغها و ميوه‏ها اندر دل است / ‏عكس لطف آن بر اين آب و گل است
entries.english: The orchards and fruits are within the heart: the reflection of their beauty is upon this water and earth.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى عكس آن سرو سرور / پس نخواندى ايزدش دار الغرور
entries.english: If it were not the reflection of that delectable cypress, then God would not have called it the abode of deception.

entries.farsi: اين غرور آن است يعنى اين خيال / ‏هست از عكس دل و جان رجال
entries.english: This deception is that: i.e. this phantom exists from the reflection of the heart and spirit of the (holy) men.

entries.farsi: جمله مغروران بر اين عكس آمده / ‏بر گمانى كاين بود جنت‏كده
entries.english: All the deceived ones come to this reflection in the opinion that this is the place of Paradise.

entries.farsi: مى‏گريزند از اصول باغها / بر خيالى مى‏كنند آن الغها
entries.english: They are fleeing from the origins of the orchards; they are making merry over a phantom.

entries.farsi: چون كه خواب غفلت آيدشان به سر / راست بينند و چه سود است آن نظر
entries.english: When their heedless sleep comes to an end, they see truly— but what use is that sight?

entries.farsi: پس به گورستان غريو افتاد و آه / ‏تا قيامت زين غلط وا حسرتاه
entries.english: Then in the graveyard arises uproar and lament: on account of this mistake “alas” till the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن را كه پيش از مرگ مرد / يعنى او از اصل اين رز بوى برد
entries.english: Oh, happy he that died before death, i.e. he got scent of the origin of this vineyard.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى رستن خروب در گوشه‏ى مسجد اقصى و غمگين شدن سليمان عليه السلام از آن چون به ‏سخن آمد با او و خاصيت و نام خود بگفت
sections.title_en: Story of the growing of the carob in a nook of the Farther Mosque, and how Solomon,
on whom be peace, was grieved thereat, when it began to talk with him and told its characteristic property and its name

entries.farsi: پس سليمان ديد اندر گوشه‏اى / ‏نو گياهى رسته همچون خوشه‏اى
entries.english: Then Solomon saw that a new plant had grown, like an ear of wheat, in a nook.

entries.farsi: ديد بس نادر گياهى سبز و تر / مى‏ربود آن سبزى‏اش نور از بصر
entries.english: He saw a very uncommon plant, green and fresh: its greenness took away the light from the sight.

entries.farsi: پس سالمش كرد در حال آن حشيش / ‏او جوابش گفت و بشكفت از خوشيش
entries.english: Then that herb at once saluted him: he answered it and marvelled at its beauty.

entries.farsi: گفت نامت چيست بر گو بى‏دهان / ‏گفت خروب است اى شاه جهان
entries.english: I said, “What is your name? Say without mouth.” It said, ‘‘It is ‘carob,’ O king of the world.’’

entries.farsi: گفت اندر تو چه خاصيت بود / گفت من رستم مكان ويران شود
entries.english: He said, “What special property is in you?” It replied, “I have grown, the place becomes desolate.

entries.farsi: من كه خروبم خراب منزلم / ‏هادم بنياد اين آب و گلم
entries.english: I, who am carob (kharrub), am the ruin (kharàb) of the abode: I am the destroyer of the building of this water and clay.”

entries.farsi: پس سليمان آن زمان دانست زود / كه اجل آمد سفر خواهد نمود
entries.english: Then at that moment Solomon immediately understood that the appointed term was come and that the departure would appear

entries.farsi: تا كه من باشم وجود من بود / مسجد اقصى مخلخل كى شود
entries.english: He said, “So long as I exist, assuredly this Mosque will not be damaged by the banes of the earth.

entries.farsi: گفت تا من هستم اين مسجد يقين / ‏در خلل نايد ز آفات زمين
entries.english: While I am and my existence continues, how should the Farther Mosque become split open with cracks?”

entries.farsi: پس كه هدم مسجد ما بى‏گمان / ‏نبود الا بعد مرگ ما بدان
entries.english: Know, then, that without doubt the ruin of our mosque does not occur except after our death.

entries.farsi: مسجد است آن دل كه جسمش ساجد است / ‏يار بد خروب هر جا مسجد است
entries.english: The mosque is the heart to which the body bows down: wherever the mosque is, the bad companion is the carob.

entries.farsi: يار بد چون رست در تو مهر او / هين از او بگريز و كم كن گفت‏وگو
entries.english: When love for a bad companion has grown in you, beware, flee from him and do not converse.

entries.farsi: بر كن از بيخش كه گر سر بر زند / مر ترا و مسجدت را بر كند
entries.english: Tear it up by the root, for if it shoots up its head it will demolish you and your mosque.

entries.farsi: عاشقا خروب تو آمد كژى / ‏همچو طفالن سوى كژ چون مى‏غژى
entries.english: O lover, your carob is falseness: why do you creep, like children, towards the false?

entries.farsi: خويش مجرم دان و مجرم گو مترس / ‏تا ندزدد از تو آن استاد درس
entries.english: Know yourself a sinner and call yourself a sinner—do not be afraid— so that that Master may not steal the lesson from you.

entries.farsi: چون بگويى جاهلم تعليم ده / ‏اين چنين انصاف از ناموس به
entries.english: When you say, “I am ignorant; give instruction,” such fair-dealing is better than reputation.

entries.farsi: از پدر آموز اى روشن جبين / ‏رَبَّنا گفت و ظَ لَمْنا پيش از اين
entries.english: Learn from your father, O clear-browed man: he said heretofore, “O our Lord” and “We have done wrong.”

entries.farsi: نه بهانه كرد و نه تزوير ساخت / ‏نه لواى مكر و حيلت بر فراخت
entries.english: He made no excuse, nor did he invent falsehood nor lift up the banner of deceit and evasion.

entries.farsi: باز آن ابليس بحث آغاز كرد / كه بدم من سرخ رو كرديم زرد
entries.english: That Iblis, on the other hand, began to dispute, saying, “I was red-faced: You have made me yellow.

entries.farsi: رنگ رنگ تست صباغم تويى / ‏اصل جرم و آفت و داغم تويى
entries.english: The colour is Your colour: You are my dyer, You are the origin of my sin and bane and brand.”

entries.farsi: هين بخوان رَبِّ بِما أغويتني / ‏تا نگردى جبرى و كژ كم تنى
entries.english: Beware! Recite because You have seduced me, in order that you may not become a necessitarian and may not weave untruth.

entries.farsi: بر درخت جبر تا كى بر جهى / ‏اختيار خويش را يك سو نهى
entries.english: How long will you leap up the tree of necessitarianism and lay your free-will aside,

entries.farsi: همچو آن ابليس و ذريات او / با خدا در جنگ و اندر گفت‏وگو
entries.english: Like that Iblis and his progeny, in battle and argument with God?

entries.farsi: چون بود اكراه با چندان خوشى / ‏كه تو در عصيان همى دامن كشى
entries.english: How should there be compulsion when you are trailing your skirt into sin with such complacence?

entries.farsi: آن چنان خوش كس رود در مكرهى / ‏كس چنان رقصان دود در گمرهى
entries.english: Does anyone under compulsion walk so complacently? Does anyone, having lost his way’, go dancing like that?

entries.farsi: بيست مرده جنگ مى‏كردى در آن / ‏كت همى‏دادند پند آن ديگران
entries.english: You were fighting like twenty men in the matter concerning which those others were giving you good advice.

entries.farsi: كه صواب اين است و راه اين است و بس / ‏كى زند طعنه مرا جز هيچ كس
entries.english: You said, “This is right and this is the only way: how should anyone but a nobody rail at me?”

entries.farsi: كى چنين گويد كسى كو مكره است / ‏چون چنين جنگد كسى كاو بى‏ره ست
entries.english: How should one who is compelled speak thus? How should one who has lost his way wrangle like this?

entries.farsi: هر چه نفست خواست دارى اختيار / هر چه عقلت خواست آرى اضطرار
entries.english: Whatever your fleshly soul desires, you have free-will; whatever your reason desires, you plead necessity.

entries.farsi: داند او كاو نيك بخت و محرم است / ‏زيركى ز ابليس و عشق از آدم است
entries.english: He that is blessed and familiar knows that intelligence is of Iblis, while love is of Adam.

entries.farsi: زيركى سباحى آمد در بحار / كم رهد غرق است او پايان كار
entries.english: Intelligence is swimming in the seas: he is not saved: he is drowned at the end of the business.

entries.farsi: هل سباحت را رها كن كبر و كين / ‏نيست جيحون نيست جو درياست اين
entries.english: Leave off swimming, let pride and enmity go: this is not a Oxus or a river, it is an ocean;

entries.farsi: و آن گهان درياى ژرف بى‏پناه / ‏در ربايد هفت دريا را چو كاه
entries.english: And, moreover, the deep Ocean without refuge: it sweeps away the seven seas like straw.

entries.farsi: عشق چون كشتى بود بهر خواص / كم بود آفت بود اغلب خالص
entries.english: Love is as a ship for the elect: seldom is calamity; for the most part it is deliverance.

entries.farsi: زيركى بفروش و حيرانى بخر / زيركى ظن است و حيرانى نظر
entries.english: Sell intelligence and buy bewilderment: intelligence is opinion, while bewilderment is vision.

entries.farsi: عقل قربان كن به پيش مصطفى / ‏حَسْ بِيَ اللهَُّ گو كه اهلل‏ام كفى
entries.english: Sacrifice your understanding in the presence of Mustafa say, “hasbiya ‘llah for God suffices me.”

entries.farsi: همچو كنعان سر ز كشتى وامكش / كه غرورش داد نفس زيركش
entries.english: Do not draw back your head from the ship, like Canaan, whom his intelligent soul deluded,

entries.farsi: كه بر آيم بر سر كوه مشيد / منت نوحم چرا بايد كشيد
entries.english: Saying, “I will go up to the top of the lofty mountain: why must I bear gratitude to Noah?”

entries.farsi: چون رمى از منتش اى بى‏رشد / كه خدا هم منت او مى‏كشد
entries.english: How should you recoil from being grateful to him, O unrighteous one, when even God bears gratitude to him?

entries.farsi: چون نباشد منتش بر جان ما / چون كه شكر و منتش گويد خدا
entries.english: How should gratitude to him not be on our souls, when God gives him words of thankful praise and gratitude?

entries.farsi: تو چه دانى اى غراره‏ى پر حسد / كه نهادن منت او را مى‏رسد
entries.english: What do you know, O sack full of envy? Even God bears gratitude to him.

entries.farsi: كاشكى او آشنا ناموختى / ‏تا طمع در نوح و كشتى دوختى
entries.english: Would that he (one like Kan’án) had not learned to swim, so that he might have fixed his hope on Noah and the ark!

entries.farsi: كاش چون طفل از حيل جاهل بدى / ‏تا چو طفالن چنگ در مادر زدى
entries.english: Would that, like a child, he had been ignorant of devices, so that, like children, he might have clung to his mother,

entries.farsi: يا به علم نقل كم بودى ملى / ‏علم وحى دل ربودى از ولى
entries.english: Or that he had not been filled with traditional knowledge, had carried away from a saint the knowledge divinely revealed to the heart!

entries.farsi: با چنين نورى چو پيش آرى كتاب / ‏جان وحى آساى تو آرد عتاب
entries.english: When you bring forward a book with such a light, your soul, that resembles inspiration, reproaches.

entries.farsi: چون تيمم با وجود آب دان / ‏علم نقلى با دم قطب زمان
entries.english: Know that beside the breath of the Qutb of the time traditional knowledge is like performing the ritual ablution with sand when there is water.

entries.farsi: خويش ابله كن تبع مى‏رو سپس / ‏رستگى زين ابلهى يابى و بس
entries.english: Make yourself foolish and follow behind: only by means of this foolishness will you gain deliverance.

entries.farsi: اكثر اهل الجنة البله اى پدر / بهر اين گفته ست سلطان البشر
entries.english: On this account, O father, the Sultan of mankind has said, “Most of the people of Paradise are the foolish.”

entries.farsi: زيركى چون كبر و باد انگيز تست / ‏ابلهى شو تا بماند دل درست
entries.english: Since, intelligence is the exciter of pride and vanity in you, become a fool in order that your heart may remain sound—

entries.farsi: ابلهى نه كاو به مسخرگى دو توست / ابلهى كاو واله و حيران هوست
entries.english: Not the fool that is bent double (abases himself) in buffoonery, the fool that is distraught and bewildered in Him.

entries.farsi: ابلهانند آن زنان دست بر / از كف ابله وز رخ يوسف نذر
entries.english: The foolish are those women who cut their hands—foolish in respect of their hands, giving notice to beware of the face of Joseph.

entries.farsi: عقل را قربان كن اندر عشق دوست / ‏عقلها بارى از آن سوى است كاوست
entries.english: Sacrifice your intellect in love for the Friend: anyhow, intellects are from the quarter where He is.

entries.farsi: عقلها آن سو فرستاده عقول / ‏مانده اين سو كه نه معشوق است گول
entries.english: The intelligent have sent their intellects to that quarter: the dolt has remained in this quarter where the Beloved is not.

entries.farsi: زين سر از حيرت گر اين عقلت رود / هر سر مويت سر و عقلى شود
entries.english: If, from bewilderment, this intellect of yours goes out of this head, every head of your hair will become head and intellect.

entries.farsi: نيست آن سو رنج فكرت بر دماغ / ‏كه دماغ و عقل رويد دشت و باغ
entries.english: In that quarter the trouble of thinking is not on the brain, for the brain and intellect produce fields and orchards.

entries.farsi: سوى دشت از دشت نكته بشنوى / ‏سوى باغ آيى شود نخلت روى
entries.english: If you turn towards the field, you will hear from the field a subtle discourse; you come to the orchard, your palm- tree will become fresh and flourishing.

entries.farsi: اندر اين ره ترك كن طاق و طرنب / ‏تا قالووزت نجنبد تو مجنب
entries.english: In this Way abandon ostentation: do not move unless your guide moves.

entries.farsi: هر كه او بى‏سر بجنبد دم بود / جنبشش چون جنبش كژدم بود
entries.english: Anyone who moves without the head is a tail: his movement is like the movement of the scorpion.

entries.farsi: كژرو و شب كور و زشت و زهرناك / ‏پيشه‏ى او خستن اجسام پاك
entries.english: Going crookedly, night-blind and ugly and venomous— his trade is the wounding of the pure bodies.

entries.farsi: خود صالح اوست آن سر كوفتن / ‏تا رهد جان ريزه‏اش ز آن شوم تن
entries.english: Beat the head of him whose inmost spirit is this, and whose permanent nature and disposition is this.

entries.farsi: سر بكوب آن را كه سرش اين بود / خلق و خوى مستمرش اين بود
entries.english: In sooth it is good for him to beat this head, so that his puny-soul may be delivered from that ill-starred body.

entries.farsi: واستان از دست ديوانه سالح / ‏تا ز تو راضى شود عدل و صالح
entries.english: Take away the weapons from the madman’s hand, that Justice and Goodness may be satisfied with you.

entries.farsi: چون سالحش هست و عقلش نه، ببند / دست او را ور نه آرد صد گزند
entries.english: Since he has weapons and has no understanding, shackle his hand; otherwise he will inflict a hundred injuries.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه حصول علم و مال و جاه مر بد گوهران را فضيحت اوست و چون شمشيرى است كه ‏افتاده ست به دست راه زن
sections.title_en: Explaining that the acquisition of knowledge and wealth and rank by men of evil nature is the exposing him to shame and is like a sword that has fallen into the hand of a brigand.

entries.farsi: بد گهر را علم و فن آموختن / ‏دادن تيغ است دست راه زن
entries.english: To teach the evil-natured man knowledge and skill is to put a sword in the hand of a brigand.

entries.farsi: تيغ دادن در كف زنگى مست / ‏به كه آيد علم ناكس را به دست
entries.english: It is better to put a sword in the hand of an intoxicated Negro than that knowledge should come into the possession of a worthless person.

entries.farsi: علم و مال و منصب و جاه و قران / ‏فتنه آمد در كف بد گوهران
entries.english: Knowledge and wealth and office and rank and fortune are a mischief in the hands of the evil-natured.

entries.farsi: پس غزا زين فرض شد بر مومنان / ‏تا ستانند از كف مجنون سنان
entries.english: Therefore the Holy War was made obligatory on the true believers for this purpose, that they might take the spear-point from the hand of the madman.

entries.farsi: جان او مجنون تنش شمشير او / واستان شمشير را ز آن زشت خو
entries.english: His spirit is the madman, and his body is his sword: take away the sword from that wicked man!

entries.farsi: آن چه منصب مى‏كند با جاهالن / ‏از فضيحت كى كند صد ارسالن
entries.english: How should a hundred lions inflict the shame which office inflicts upon the ignorant?

entries.farsi: عيب او مخفى است چون آلت بيافت / ‏مارش از سوراخ بر صحرا شتافت
entries.english: His vice is hidden, when he got the instrument, his snake, from its hole, sped along the plain.

entries.farsi: جمله صحرا مار و كژدم پر شود / چون كه جاهل شاه حكم مر شود
entries.english: The entire plain is filled with snakes and scorpions when the ignorant man becomes king of the bitter decree.

entries.farsi: مال و منصب ناكسى كارد به دست / ‏طالب رسوايى خويش او شده‏ست
entries.english: The worthless person who acquires wealth and office has become the seeker of his own disgrace.

entries.farsi: شاه را در خانه‏ى بى‏ذق نهد / اين چنين باشد عطا كاحمق دهد
entries.english: Either he behaves stingily and gives few presents, or he shows generosity and bestows in the wrong place.

entries.farsi: يا كند بخل و عطاها كم دهد / يا سخا آرد به ناموضع نهد
entries.english: He puts the king in the house of the pawn: the gifts which a fool makes are like this.

entries.farsi: حكم چون در دست گم راهى فتاد / جاه پنداريد در چاهى فتاد
entries.english: When authority falls into the hands of one who has lost the way, he deems it to be a high position (jáh), he has fallen into a pit (cháh).

entries.farsi: ره نمى‏داند قالووزى كند / جان زشت او جهان سوزى كند
entries.english: He does not know the way, he acts as guide: his wicked spirit makes a world-conflagration.

entries.farsi: طفل راه فقر چون پيرى گرفت / ‏پى روان را غول ادبارى گرفت
entries.english: When a child in the Way of poverty assumes the part of an Elder, the evil ghoul seizes those who follow.

entries.farsi: كه بيا كه ماه بنمايم ترا / ماه را هرگز نديد آن بى‏صفا
entries.english: “Come,” says he, “for I will show you the moon”; that impure one never saw the moon.

entries.farsi: چون نمايى چون نديده ستى به عمر / عكس مه در آب هم اى خام غمر
entries.english: How will you show when during your life you have not seen even the reflection of the moon in the water, O half-baked dunce?

entries.farsi: احمقان سرور شده‏ستند و ز بيم / ‏عاقالن سرها كشيده در گليم
entries.english: The foolish have become leaders, and from fear the wise have drawn their heads into the cloak.

sections.title_fa: ُتفسير يا أَيُّهَا الْمُزَّمِّل
sections.title_en: Commentary on “O you that wrap yourself.” (muzzammil)

entries.farsi: خواند مزمل نبى را زين سبب / ‏كه برون آى از گليم اى بو الهرب
entries.english: For this reason He called the Prophet muzzammil, saying, “Come forth from the cloak, O you who are fond of taking flight.

entries.farsi: سر مكش اندر گليم و رو مپوش / ‏كه جهان جسمى است سر گردان تو هوش
entries.english: Do not draw your head into the cloak and do not cover your face, for the world is a reeling body: you are the intelligence.

entries.farsi: هين مشو پنهان ز ننگ مدعى / ‏كه تو دارى شمع وحى شعشعى
entries.english: Listen; do not hide on account of the disgrace of the adversary, since you have the resplendent candle of the Revelation.

entries.farsi: هين قُمِ اللَّيْلَ كه شمعى اى همام / ‏شمع اندر شب بود اندر قيام
entries.english: Listen; stand up during the night, for you are a candle, O prince: at night a candle stands up.

entries.farsi: بى‏فروغت روز روشن هم شب است / ‏بى‏پناهت شير اسير ارنب است
entries.english: Without your radiance, even the bright day is night: without your protection, the lion is captive to the hare.

entries.farsi: باش كشتيبان در اين بحر صفا / ‏كه تو نوح ثانيى اى مصطفى
entries.english: Be the captain of the ship in this sea of purity, for you are a second Noah, O Mustafa.

entries.farsi: ره شناسى مى‏ببايد با لباب / ‏هر رهى را خاصه اندر راه آب
entries.english: An expert guide, with understanding, is needed for every road, especially in the road on the water.

entries.farsi: خيز بنگر كاروان ره زده / ‏هر طرف غولى است كشتيبان شده
entries.english: Arise! Look upon the waylaid caravan: everywhere a ghoul has become captain of the ship.

entries.farsi: خضر وقتى غوث هر كشتى توى / ‏همچو روح اللهَّ مكن تنها روى
entries.english: You are the Khizr of the time and the Deliverer of every ship: do not, like the Spirit of God, practice solitude.

entries.farsi: وقت خلوت نيست اندر جمع آى / ‏اى هدى چون كوه قاف و تو هماى
entries.english: In the sight of this assembly you are as the candle of heaven: stop separating yourself and adopting seclusion.

entries.farsi: پيش اين جمعى چو شمع آسمان / ‏انقطاع و خلوت آرى را بمان
entries.english: It is not the time for seclusion: come into the assembly, O you who are the humay, while the guidance is like Mount Qaf.

entries.farsi: بدر بر صدر فلك شد شب روان / ‏سير را نگذارد از بانگ سگان
entries.english: The full-moon is moving by night on the upper part of the sky: it does not cease from its journey because of the outcry of the dogs.

entries.farsi: طاعنان همچون سگان بر بدر تو / بانگ مى‏دارند سوى صدر تو
entries.english: The scoffers keep making an outcry, like dogs, at your full-moon in the direction of your high place.

entries.farsi: اين سگان كرند ز امر أنصتوا / از سفه وعوع كنان بر بدر تو
entries.english: These dogs are deaf to the command, ‘Be silent’: from folly howling at your full-moon.

entries.farsi: هين بمگذار اى شفا رنجور را / تو ز خشم كر عصاى كور را
entries.english: Listen, O the cure for the sick, do not, on account of anger against the deaf, let go the staff of the blind.

entries.farsi: نه تو گفتى قايد اعمى به راه / ‏صد ثواب و اجر يابد از اله
entries.english: Did not you say?—‘He that leads a blind man on the way gains a hundred recompenses and rewards from God.

entries.farsi: هر كه او چل گام كورى را كشد / گشت آمرزيده و يابد رشد
entries.english: Whoever leads a blind man forty steps is pardoned and will find salvation.

entries.farsi: پس بكش تو زين جهان بى‏قرار / جوق كوران را قطار اندر قطار
entries.english: Lead away from this impermanent world the multitude of the blind, file on file.

entries.farsi: كار هادى اين بود تو هاديى / ‏ماتم آخر زمان را شاديى
entries.english: This is the business of a guide: you are the Guide; you are the joy for the sorrow of the last time.

entries.farsi: هين روان كن اى امام المتقين / ‏اين خيال انديشگان را تا يقين
entries.english: Listen, O Imam of the God-fearing, cause these thinkers of vain fancies to go till certainty.

entries.farsi: هر كه در مكر تو دارد دل گرو / گردنش را من زنم تو شاد رو
entries.english: Whoever has his heart in pawn to plotting against you, I will smite his neck: do you advance joyously.

entries.farsi: بر سر كوريش كوريها نهم / ‏او شكر پندارد و زهرش دهم
entries.english: I will lay blindness on the top of his blindness: he will deem it sugar, and I will give him poison.

entries.farsi: عقلها از نور من افروختند / مكرها از مكر من آموختند
entries.english: Intellects have been kindled by My light; plots have been learned from My plotting.

entries.farsi: چيست خود آالجق آن تركمان / ‏پيش پاى نره پيالن جهان
entries.english: What indeed is the Turcoman’s tent of black felt before the feet of the male elephants of this world?

entries.farsi: آن چراغ او به پيش صرصرم / ‏خود چه باشد اى مهين پيغمبرم
entries.english: Before My sarsar wind what in sooth is that lamp of his, O My greatest prophet?

entries.farsi: خيز در دم تو به صور سهمناك / ‏تا هزاران مرده بر رويد ز خاك
entries.english: Do you arise and blow on the terrible trumpet that your sands of the dead may spring up from the earth.

entries.farsi: چون تو اسرافيل وقتى راست خيز / رستخيزى ساز پيش از رستخيز
entries.english: Since you are the upright-rising Seraphiel of the time, make a resurrection before the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: هر كه گويد كو قيامت اى صنم / ‏خويش بنما كه قيامت نك منم
entries.english: O beloved, if anyone say, ‘Where is the Resurrection?’ show yourself, saying, ‘Behold, I am the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: در نگر اى سايل محنت زده / ‏زين قيامت صد جهان افزون شده
entries.english: Look, O questioner who is stricken with tribulation, that from this resurrection a hundred worlds have grown!’

entries.farsi: ور نباشد اهل اين ذكر و قنوت / ‏پس جواب الاحمق اى سلطان سكوت
entries.english: And if he be not fit for this praise and humble supplication, then, O Sultan, the reply to a fool is silence.

entries.farsi: ز آسمان حق سكوت آيد جواب / ‏چون بود جانا دعا نامستجاب
entries.english: From God’s Heaven silence comes in reply when, O soul, the prayer is unanswered.”

entries.farsi: اى دريغا وقت خرمنگاه شد / ليك روز از بخت ما بى‏گاه شد
entries.english: Oh, alas, it is harvest-time, but by our fortune the day has become late.

entries.farsi: وقت تنگ است و فراخى اين كالم / ‏تنگ مى‏آيد بر او عمر دوام
entries.english: Time is pressing, and the amplitude of this discussion a perpetual life will be restricted for it.

entries.farsi: نيزه بازى اندر اين كوهاى تنگ / ‏نيزه بازان را همى‏آرد به ننگ
entries.english: To dart the lance in these narrow lanes brings to disgrace those who dart the lance.

entries.farsi: وقت تنگ و خاطر و فهم عوام / ‏تنگ‏تر صد ره ز وقت است اى غالم
entries.english: The time is narrow, and the mind and understanding of the vulgar is narrower a hundredfold than the time, O youth.

entries.farsi: چون جواب احمق آمد خامشى / ‏اين درازى در سخن چون مى‏كشى
entries.english: Inasmuch as silence is the reply to the fool, how are you thus prolonging the discourse?

entries.farsi: از كمال رحمت و موج كرم / ‏مى‏دهد هر شوره را باران و نم
entries.english: He, from the perfection of His mercy and the waves of His bounty, bestows rain and moisture on every barren soil.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه ترك الجواب جواب مقرر اين سخن كه جواب الاحمق سكوت، شرح اين هر دو در اين قصه است كه گفته مى‏آيد
sections.title_en: Showing that, “Omission to reply is a reply,” confirms the saying that silence is the reply to the fool. The explanation of both these is in the story which will now be related.

entries.farsi: بود شاهى بود او را بنده‏اى / ‏مرده عقلى بود و شهوت زنده‏اى
entries.english: There was a king: he had a slave; he was one whose reason was dead and whose lust was alive.

entries.farsi: خرده‏هاى خدمتش بگذاشتى / ‏بد سگاليدى نكو پنداشتى
entries.english: He would neglect the niceties of service to him: he was thinking evil and deeming good.

entries.farsi: گفت شاهنشه جرائش كم كنيد / ور بجنگد نامش از خط بر زنيد
entries.english: The monarch said, “Reduce his allowance, and if he wrangle strike his name off the roll.”

entries.farsi: عقل او كم بود و حرص او فزون / ‏چون جرا كم ديد شد تند و حزون
entries.english: His reason was deficient, his cupidity excessive: when he saw the allowance reduced he became violent and refractory.

entries.farsi: عقل بودى گرد خود كردى طواف / ‏تا بديدى جرم خود گشتى معاف
entries.english: Had there been reason, he would have made a circuit round himself, in order that he might see his offence and become forgiven.

entries.farsi: پس بگويد خر كه يك بندم بس است / ‏خود مدان كان دو ز فعل آن خس است
entries.english: When, on account of asininity, a tethered ass becomes violent, both his legs will be shackled in addition.

entries.farsi: چون خرى پا بسته تندد از خرى / ‏هر دو پايش بسته گردد بر سرى
entries.english: Then the ass will say, “One tether is enough for me”; in truth do not think, for those two are from the action of that vile creature.

sections.title_fa: در تفسير اين حديث مصطفى عليه الصاله و السلام كه ان اللهَّ تعالى خلق المالئكة و ركب فيهم العقل و خلق البهائم و ركب فيها الشهوة و خلق بنى آدم و ركب فيهم العقل و الشهوة فمن غلب عقله ‏شهوته فهو اعلى من المالئكة و من غلب شهوته عقله فهو ادنى من البهائم

sections.title_en: In exposition of the following Hadíth of Mustafa, on whom be peace: “Truly, the most High God created the angels and set reason in them, and He created the beasts and set lust in them, and He created the sons of Adam and set in them reason and lust; and he whose reason prevails over his lust is higher than the angels, and he whose lust prevails over his reason is lower than the beasts.”

entries.farsi: در حديث آمد كه يزدان مجيد / خلق عالم را سه گونه آفريد
entries.english: It is related in the Hadíth that the majestic God created the creatures of the world three kinds.

entries.farsi: يك گره را جمله عقل و علم و جود / آن فرشته ست او نداند جز سجود
entries.english: One class entirely reason and knowledge and munificence; that is the angel: he knows nothing but prostration in worship.

entries.farsi: نيست اندر عنصرش حرص و هوا / نور مطلق زنده از عشق خدا
entries.english: In his original nature is no concupiscence and sensuality: he is absolute light, living through love of God.

entries.farsi: يك گروه ديگر از دانش تهى / ‏همچو حيوان از علف در فربهى
entries.english: Another class is devoid of knowledge, like the animal in fatness from fodder.

entries.farsi: او نبيند جز كه اصطبل و علف / ‏از شقاوت غافل است و از شرف
entries.english: It sees nothing but stable and fodder: it is heedless of misery and glory.

entries.farsi: اين سوم هست آدمى زاد و بشر / نيم او ز افرشته و نيميش خر
entries.english: The third is Adam’s descendant and Man: half of him is of the angel and half of him is ass.

entries.farsi: نيم خر خود مايل سفلى بود / نيم ديگر مايل عقلى بود
entries.english: The ass-half, indeed, inclines to that which is low; the other half inclines to that which is rational.

entries.farsi: آن دو قوم آسوده از جنگ و حراب / ‏وين بشر با دو مخالف در عذاب
entries.english: Those two classes are at rest from war and combat, while this Man is in torment with two adversaries.

entries.farsi: وين بشر هم ز امتحان قسمت شدند / آدمى شكلند و سه امت شدند
entries.english: And, moreover, this Human, through probation, has been divided: they are of human shape, but they have become three communities.

entries.farsi: يك گره مستغرق مطلق شدند / همچو عيسى با ملك ملحق شدند
entries.english: One party has become submerged absolutely and, like Jesus, have attained unto the angel.

entries.farsi: نقش آدم ليك معنى جبرئيل / ‏رسته از خشم و هوا و قال و قيل
entries.english: The form Adam, but the reality is Gabriel: he has been delivered from anger and sensual passion and disputation.

entries.farsi: از رياضت رسته و ز زهد و جهاد / گوييا از آدمى او خود نزاد
entries.english: He has been delivered from discipline and asceticism and self-mortification: you would say he was not even born of a child of Adam.

entries.farsi: قسم ديگر با خران ملحق شدند / خشم محض و شهوت مطلق شدند
entries.english: The second sort have attained unto asses: they have become pure anger and absolute lust.

entries.farsi: وصف جبريلى در ايشان بود رفت / ‏تنگ بود آن خانه و آن وصف زفت
entries.english: The qualities of Gabriel were in them and departed: that house was narrow, and those qualities grand.

entries.farsi: مرده گردد شخص كاو بى‏جان شود / خر شود چون جان او بى‏آن شود
entries.english: The person who is deprived of spirit becomes dead: when his spirit is deprived of those, he becomes an ass,

entries.farsi: ز انكه جانى كان ندارد هست پست / ‏اين سخن حق است و صوفى گفته است
entries.english: Because the spirit that has not those is vile: this word is true, and the Sufi has said.

entries.farsi: او ز حيوانها فزون‏تر جان كند / در جهان باريك كاريها كند
entries.english: He suffers more anxiety than the beasts; he practices subtle arts in the world.

entries.farsi: مكر و تلبيسى كه او داند تنيد / آن ز حيوان دگر نايد پديد
entries.english: The cunning and imposture which he knows how to spin— that is not produced by any other animal

entries.farsi: جامه‏هاى زركشى را بافتن / ‏درها از قعر دريا يافتن
entries.english: To weave gold-embroidered robes, to win pearls from the bottom of the sea,

entries.farsi: خرده كاريهاى علم هندسه / ‏يا نجوم و علم طب و فلسفه
entries.english: The fine artifices of geometry or astronomy, and the science of medicine and philosophy—

entries.farsi: اين همه علم بناى آخور است / ‏كه عماد بود گاو و اشتر است
entries.english: Which are connected only with this world and have no way up to the Seventh Heaven.

entries.farsi: كه تعلق با همين دنياستش / ‏ره به هفتم آسمان بر نيستش
entries.english: All this is the science of building the stable which is the pillar of the existence of the ox and the camel.

entries.farsi: بهر استبقاى حيوان چند روز / نام آن كردند اين گيجان رموز
entries.english: For the sake of preserving the animal for a few days, these crazy fools have given to those the name of “mysteries.”

entries.farsi: علم راه حق و علم منزلش / ‏صاحب دل داند آن را يا دلش
entries.english: The knowledge of the Way to God and the knowledge of His dwelling place— that only the owner of the heart knows, or his heart.

entries.farsi: نام كَالأَْنْعامِ كرد آن قوم را / ز انكه نسبت كو به يقظه نوم را
entries.english: He, then, created in this composite fashion the goodly animal and made him familiar with knowledge.

entries.farsi: پس در اين تركيب حيوان لطيف / ‏آفريد و كرد با دانش اليف
entries.english: That class He named “like the cattle,” for where is the resemblance between waking and sleep?

entries.farsi: روح حيوانى ندارد غير نوم / ‏حسهاى منعكس دارند قوم
entries.english: The animal spirit has nothing but sleep: the class of men possesses inverted sense-perceptions.

entries.farsi: يقظه آمد نوم حيوانى نماند / انعكاس حس خود از لوح خواند
entries.english: Waking comes, the animal sleep is no more, and he reads the inversion of his senses from the tablet.

entries.farsi: همچو حس آن كه خواب او را ربود / چون شد او بيدار عكسيت نمود
entries.english: Like the sense-perceptions of one whom sleep has seized: when he awakes, the inverted quality becomes apparent.

entries.farsi: الجرم اسفل بود از سافلين / ‏ترك او كن ال أُحِبُّ اآلفلين
entries.english: Necessarily, he is the lowest of the low. Take leave of him: I love not them that sink.

sections.title_fa: ًدر تفسير اين آيت كه وَ أَمَّا الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ فَزادَتْهُمْ رِجْ ساًو قوله يُضِ لُّ بِهِ كَثِيراً وَ يَهْدِي بِهِ كَثِيرا
sections.title_en: In exposition of the following Verse: “and as for those in whose hearts is a disease, it added unto their uncleanness”; and of His Word: “thereby He lets many be led astray,
and thereby He lets many be guided aright.”

entries.farsi: ز انكه استعداد تيديل و نبرد / بودش از پستى و آن را فوت كرد
entries.english: Because he possessed the capacity for transforming himself and striving from lowness, but lost it

entries.farsi: باز حيوان را چو استعداد نيست / ‏عذر او اندر بهيمى روشنى است
entries.english: Again, since the animal does not possess capacity, its lack of accountability is evident.

entries.farsi: زو چو استعداد شد كان رهبر است / ‏هر غذايى كاو خورد مغز خر است
entries.english: When the capacity, which is the guide, is gone from him, every nutriment that he eats is the brain of an ass

entries.farsi: گر بالذر خورد او افيون شود / سكته و بى‏عقلى‏اش افزون شود
entries.english: If he eats fruit, it becomes opium: his apoplexy and dementia are increased.

entries.farsi: ماند يك قسم دگر اندر جهاد / نيم حيوان نيم حى با رشاد
entries.english: There remains another sort in warfare: half animal, half alive and endowed with good guidance.

entries.farsi: روز و شب در جنگ و اندر كش مكش / ‏كرده چاليش آخرش با اولش
entries.english: Day and night in strife and mutual struggle, his last battles with his first.

sections.title_fa: چاليش عقل با نفس همچون تنازع مجنون با ناقه، ميل مجنون سوى حره ميل ناقه واپس سوى ‏كرده، چنان كه گفت مجنون هوى ناقتى خلفى و قدامى الهوى و انى و اياها خملتلفان‏

sections.title_en: The battle of the reason against the flesh is like the contention of Majnun with his she camel: Majnun’s inclination is towards the noble woman, while the she camel’s inclination is back towards her foal, as Majnun said: “My she-camel’s love is behind me, while my love is in front of me; and verily I and she are discordant.”

entries.farsi: همچو مجنون‏اند و چون ناقه‏اش يقين / ‏مى‏كشد آن پيش و اين واپس به كين
entries.english: Assuredly they are like Majnun and his she-camel: that one is pulling forward and this one backward in enmity.

entries.farsi: ميل مجنون پيش آن ليلى روان / ‏ميل ناقه پس پى كره دوان
entries.english: Majnun’s desire is speeding to the presence of that Layla; the she camel’s desire is running back after her foal.

entries.farsi: يك دم ار مجنون ز خود غافل بدى / ‏ناقه گرديدى و واپس آمدى
entries.english: If Majnun forgot himself for one moment, the she-camel would turn and go back.

entries.farsi: عشق و سودا چون كه پر بودش بدن / ‏مى‏نبودش چاره از بى‏خود شدن
entries.english: Since his body was full of love and passion, he had no resource but to become beside himself.

entries.farsi: آن كه او باشد مراقب عقل بود / عقل را سوداى ليلى در ربود
entries.english: That which is regardful was reason: passion for Layla carried reason away.

entries.farsi: ليك ناقه بس مراقب بود و چست / ‏چون بديدى او مهار خويش سست
entries.english: But the she-camel was very regardful and alert: whenever she saw her toggle slack

entries.farsi: فهم كردى زو كه غافل گشت و دنگ / ‏رو سپس كردى به كره بى‏درنگ
entries.english: She would at once perceive that he had become heedless and dazed, and would turn her face back to the foal without delay.

entries.farsi: چون به خود باز آمدى ديدى ز جا / كاو سپس رفته ست بس فرسنگ‏ها
entries.english: When he came to himself again, he would see on the spot that she had gone back many leagues.

entries.farsi: در سه روزه ره بدين احوالها / ماند مجنون در تردد سالها
entries.english: In these conditions Majnun remained going to and fro for years on a three days’ journey.

entries.farsi: گفت اى ناقه چو هر دو عاشقيم / ‏ما دو ضد پس همره نالايقيم
entries.english: He said, “O camel, since we both are lovers, therefore we two contraries are unsuitable fellow-travellers.

entries.farsi: نيستت بر وفق من مهر و مهار / كرد بايد از تو عزلت اختيار
entries.english: Your affection and toggle are not in accord with me: it behooves to choose parting from your companionship.”

entries.farsi: اين دو همره همدگر را راه زن / ‏گمره آن جان كاو فرو نايد ز تن
entries.english: These two fellow-travellers are brigands waylaying each other: lost is the spirit that does not dismount from the body.

entries.farsi: جان ز هجر عرش اندر فاقه‏اى / ‏تن ز عشق خار بن چون ناقه‏اى
entries.english: The spirit, because of separation from the highest Heaven, is in a want; the body, on account of passion for the thorn-shrub, is like a she-camel.

entries.farsi: جان گشايد سوى بالا بالها / در زده تن در زمين چنگالها
entries.english: The spirit unfolds its wings upwards; the body has stuck its claws in the earth.

entries.farsi: تا تو با من باشى اى مرده‏ى وطن / ‏پس ز ليلى دور ماند جان من
entries.english: “So long as you art with me, O you who art mortally enamoured of your home, then my spirit will remain far from Layla.

entries.farsi: خطوتينى بود اين ره تا وصال / مانده‏ام در ره ز شستت شصت سال
entries.english: From experiences of this kind my life-time, for many years, has gone, like the people of Moses in the desert.

entries.farsi: روزگارم رفت زين گون حالها / همچو تيه و قوم موسى سالها
entries.english: This journey to union was a matter of two steps: because of your noose I have remained sixty years on the way.

entries.farsi: راه نزديك و بماندم سخت دير / سير گشتم زين سوارى سير سير
entries.english: The way is near, but I have tarried very late: I have become sick of this riding, sick, sick.”

entries.farsi: سر نگون خود را ز اشتر در فكند / گفت سوزيدم ز غم تا چند چند
entries.english: He threw himself headlong from the camel. He said, “I am consumed with grief: how long, how long?”

entries.farsi: تنگ شد بر وى بيابان فراخ / خويشتن افكند اندر سنگالخ
entries.english: The wide desert became narrow for him: he flung himself on the stony place.

entries.farsi: آن چنان افكند خود را سخت زير / كه مخلخل گشت جسم آن دلير
entries.english: He flung himself down so violently that the body of that courageous man was cracked.

entries.farsi: چون چنان افكند خود را سوى پست / ‏از قضا آن لحظه پايش هم شكست
entries.english: When he flung himself to the ground thus, at that moment also by destiny his leg broke.

entries.farsi: پاى را بر بست گفتا گو شوم / ‏در خم چوگانش غلطان مى‏روم
entries.english: He tied up his leg and said, “I will become a ball, I will go rolling along in the curve of His bat.”

entries.farsi: زين كند نفرين حكيم خوش دهن / ‏بر سوارى كاو فرو نايد ز تن
entries.english: For this cause the sweet-mouthed Sage utters a curse on the rider who does not dismount from the body.

entries.farsi: عشق مولى كى كم از ليلى بود / گوى گشتن بهر او اولى بود
entries.english: How should love for the Lord be inferior to love for Layla? To become a ball for His sake is more worthy.

entries.farsi: گوى شو مى‏گرد بر پهلوى صدق / ‏غلط غلطان در خم چوگان عشق
entries.english: Become a ball, turn on the side which is sincerity, rolling, rolling in the curve of the bat of Love,

entries.farsi: كاين سفر زين پس بود جذب خدا / و آن سفر بر ناقه باشد سير ما
entries.english: For henceforth this journey is the pull of God, while that journey on the she-camel is our progression.

entries.farsi: اين چنين سيرى است مستثنى ز جنس / ‏كان فزود از اجتهاد جن و انس
entries.english: Such is the extraordinary mode of progression which transcends the utmost exertion of the Jinn and mankind.

entries.farsi: اين چنين جذبى است نى هر جذب عام / ‏كه نهادش فضل احمد و السلام
entries.english: Such is the pull—not every common pull— to which Ahmad awarded the pre-eminence. And farewell!

sections.title_fa: ‏نوشتن آن غالم قصه‏ى شكايت نقصان اجرى سوى پادشاه
sections.title_en: How the slave wrote to the King a statement complaining of the reduction of his allowance

entries.farsi: قصه كوته كن براى آن غالم / ‏كه سوى شه بر نوشته ست او پيام
entries.english: Cut short the discourse for the sake of the slave who has written a message to the King

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى پر جنگ و پر هستى و كين / ‏مى‏فرستد پيش شاه نازنين
entries.english: He is sending to the gracious King a statement filled with wrangling and self-conceit and hatred.

entries.farsi: كالبد نامه است اندر وى نگر / هست اليق شاه را آن گه ببر
entries.english: The body is a letter: look into it whether it is worthy of the King; then take it.

entries.farsi: گوشه‏اى رو نامه را بگشا بخوان / ‏بين كه حرفش هست در خورد شهان
entries.english: Go into a corner, open the letter, read, and see whether its words are suitable to kings.

entries.farsi: گر نباشد در خور آن را پاره كن / ‏نامه‏ى ديگر نويس و چاره كن
entries.english: If it be not suitable, tear it in pieces and write another letter and remedy.

entries.farsi: ليك فتح نامه‏ى تن زپ مدان / ‏ور نه هر كس سر دل ديدى عيان
entries.english: But do not think it is easy to open the letter which is the body; otherwise everyone would plainly see the secret of the heart.

entries.farsi: نامه بگشادن چه دشوار است و صعب / ‏كار مردان است نه طفالن كعب
entries.english: How hard and difficult is it to open the letter! It is a task for men, not for children playing at knuckle

entries.farsi: جمله بر فهرست قانع گشته‏ايم / ‏ز انكه در حرص و هوا آغشته‏ايم
entries.english: We have all become satisfied with the table of contents, because we are steeped in cupidity and vain desire.

entries.farsi: باشد آن فهرست دامى عامه را / تا چنان دانند متن نامه را
entries.english: The table of contents is a snare for the ordinary that they may think the text of the scroll is like that.

entries.farsi: باز كن سر نامه را گردن متاب / ‏زين سخن و اهلل اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: Open the title-page, do not turn your neck aside from these words— and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: هست آن عنوان چو اقرار زبان / ‏متن نامه‏ى سينه را كن امتحان
entries.english: That title is like a declaration made by the tongue: examine the text of the scroll, namely, the bosom,

entries.farsi: كه موافق هست با اقرار تو / تا منافق‏وار نبود كار تو
entries.english: Whether it is in agreement with your declaration, in order that your actions may not be hypocritical.

entries.farsi: چون جوال بس گرانى مى‏برى / ‏ز آن نبايد كم كه در وى بنگرى
entries.english: When you are carrying a very heavy sack, you must not fail to look into it,

entries.farsi: كه چه دارى در جوال از تلخ و خوش / ‏گر همى‏ارزد كشيدن را بكش
entries.english: What of sour and sweet you have in the sack. If it is worth bringing along, bring it;

entries.farsi: ور نه خالى كن جوالت را ز سنگ / ‏باز خر خود را از اين بيگار و ننگ
entries.english: Otherwise, empty your sack of the stones, and redeem yourself from this fruitless toil and disgrace.

entries.farsi: در جوال آن كن كه مى‏بايد كشيد / سوى سلطانان و شاهان رشيد
entries.english: Put in the sack that which must be brought to righteous sultans and kings.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن فقيه با دستار بزرگ و آن كه بربود دستارش و بانگ مى‏زد كه باز كن ببين كه چه مى‏برى آن گه ببر
sections.title_en: Story of the divine with a big turban and the man who carried it off, and how he shouted,
“Undo it and see what you are taking: then take it!”

entries.farsi: يك فقيهى ژنده‏ها در چيده بود / در عمامه‏ى خويش در پيچيده بود
entries.english: A certain divine had collected some old rags and wound them in his turban,

entries.farsi: تا شود زفت و نمايد آن عظيم / ‏چون در آيد سوى محفل در حطيم
entries.english: In order that it might become big and look grand when he came into the assembly in the Hatím

entries.farsi: ظاهر دستار چون حله‏ى بهشت / ‏چون منافق اندرون رسوا و زشت
entries.english: He had clipped the rags from garments and outwardly embellished the turban with them.

entries.farsi: ژنده‏ها از جامه‏ها پيراسته / ‏ظاهرا دستار از آن آراسته
entries.english: The exterior of the turban was like a robe of Paradise, it was shameful and ugly within, like the hypocrite.

entries.farsi: پاره پاره‏ى دلق و پنبه و پوستين / ‏در درون آن عمامه بد دفين
entries.english: Shreds of dervish-cloak (dalq) and cotton and fur were buried inside that turban.

entries.farsi: روى سوى مدرسه كرده صبوح / ‏تا بدين ناموس يابد او فتوح
entries.english: He had set his face towards the college at dawn, that by means of this false dignity he might gain blessings.

entries.farsi: در ره تاريك مردى جامه كن / ‏منتظر استاده بود از بهر فن
entries.english: A clothes-robber stood waiting on the dark road to practice his craft.

entries.farsi: در ربود او از سرش دستار را / پس دوان شد تا بسازد كار را
entries.english: He snatched the turban from his head, and then started to run in order that he might settle the business.

entries.farsi: اين چنين كه چار پره مى‏پرى / ‏باز كن آن هديه را كه مى‏برى
entries.english: Thereupon the divine shouted at him, saying, “O son, undo the turban, and then take it.

entries.farsi: باز كن آن را به دست خود بمال / ‏آن گهان خواهى ببر كردم حالل
entries.english: Even as you are flying with four wings, undo the gift which you are taking away.

entries.farsi: پس فقيهش بانگ بر زد كاى پسر / باز كن دستار را آن گه ببر
entries.english: Undo it and rub with your hand, then take it if you like: I sanction.”

entries.farsi: چون كه بازش كرد آن كه مى‏گريخت / ‏صد هزاران ژنده اندر ره بريخت
entries.english: When he who was fleeing undid it, a hundred thousand rags dropped on the road.

entries.farsi: ز آن عمامه‏ى زفت نابايست او / ماند يك گز كهنه‏اى در دست او
entries.english: Of that big improper turban of his there remained in his hand a meter of old cloth.

entries.farsi: بر زمين زد خرقه را كاى بى‏عيار / زين دغل ما را بر آوردى ز كار
entries.english: He dashed the rag on the ground, saying, “O worthless man, by this fraud you have put me out of business.”

sections.title_fa: نصيحت دنيا اهل دنيا را به زبان حال و بى‏وفايى خود را نمودن به وفا طمع دارندگان از او
sections.title_en: The World’s mute admonition to human beings and how it displays its faithlessness to those who have hope of its keeping faith.

entries.farsi: همچنين دنيا اگر چه خوش شكفت / ‏بانگ زد هم بى‏وفايى خويش گفت
entries.english: He said, “I defrauded, but I declared to you the matter by way of admonition.”

entries.farsi: گفت بنمودم دغل ليكن ترا / از نصيحت باز گفتم ماجرا
entries.english: Likewise the World, though it blossomed delightfully, at the same time uttered a cry and declared its faithlessness.

entries.farsi: اندر اين كون و فساد اى اوستاد / آن دغل كون و نصيحت آن فساد
entries.english: In this existence and corruption, O master, existence is the fraud and that corruption is the admonition.

entries.farsi: كون مى‏گويد بيا من خوش پى‏ام / ‏و آن فسادش گفته رو من ال شى‏ام
entries.english: Existence says, “Come, I am delectable,” and its corruption says, “Go, I am nothing.”

entries.farsi: اى ز خوبى بهاران لب گزان / ‏بنگر آن سردى و زردى خزان
entries.english: O you that bite your lip at the beauty of spring, look on the coldness and paleness of autumn.

entries.farsi: روز ديدى طلعت خورشيد خوب / ‏مرگ او را ياد كن وقت غروب
entries.english: In the daytime you deemed the countenance of the sun beauteous: remember its death in the moment of setting.

entries.farsi: بدر را ديدى بر اين خوش چار طاق / ‏حسرتش را هم ببين اندر محاق
entries.english: You saw the full-moon on this lovely firmament: observe also its anguish during the interlunar period.

entries.farsi: كودكى از حسن شد موالى خلق / ‏بعد فردا شد خرف رسواى خلق
entries.english: A boy, on account of his beauty, became the lord of the people: after the morrow he became doting and exposed to the scorn of the people.

entries.farsi: گر تن سيمين تنان كردت شكار / بعد پيرى بين تنى چون پنبه‏زار
entries.english: If the body of those in the fresh bloom of youth has made you a prey, after old age behold a body like a cotton plantation.

entries.farsi: اى بديده لوتهاى چرب خيز / فضله‏ى آن را ببين در آب ريز
