entries.english: O you, who have seen rich viands, arise and see the residue thereof in the latrine.

entries.farsi: مر خبث را گو كه آن خوبيت كو / بر طبق آن ذوق و آن نغزى و بو
entries.english: Say to the filth, “Where is that beauty of your— the savour and goodliness and scent in the dish?”

entries.farsi: گويد او آن دانه بد من دام آن / ‏چون شدى تو صيد شد دانه نهان
entries.english: It replies, “That was the bait: I was its trap: since you have become prey, the bait has become hidden.”

entries.farsi: بس انامل رشك استادان شده / ‏در صناعت عاقبت لرزان شده
entries.english: Many fingers that in handicraft were the envy of master craftsmen have at last become trembling.

entries.farsi: نرگس چشم خمار همچو جان / آخر اعمش بين و آب از وى چكان
entries.english: The soul-like intoxicating narcissus-eye— sees it dimmed at last and water trickling from it.

entries.farsi: حيدرى كاندر صف شيران رود / آخر او مغلوب موشى مى‏شود
entries.english: The lion that advances into the ranks of lions— at last he is conquered by a mouse.

entries.farsi: طبع تيز دور بين محترف / ‏چون خر پيرش ببين آخر خرف
entries.english: The acute, far-seeing, artful genius— beholds it at last imbecile as an old ass.

entries.farsi: زلف جعد مشكبار عقل بر / آخرا چون دم زشت خنگ خر
entries.english: The curly lock that sheds musk and takes away the reason— at last it is like the ugly white tail of a donkey.

entries.farsi: خوش ببين كونش ز اول با گشاد / و آخر آن رسوايى‏اش بين و فساد
entries.english: Observe its existence, at first pleasing and joyous; and observe its shamefulness and corruption in the end;

entries.farsi: ز انكه او بنمود پيدا دام را / پيش تو بر كند سبلت خام را
entries.english: For it showed the snare plainly: it plucked out the fool’s moustache in your presence.

entries.farsi: پس مگو دنيا به تزويرم فريفت / ‏ور نه عقل من ز دامش مى‏گريخت
entries.english: Do not say, then, “The World deceived me by its imposture; otherwise, my reason would have fled from its snare.”

entries.farsi: طوق زرين و حمايل بين هله / ‏غل و زنجيرى شده ست و سلسله
entries.english: Come now; see the golden collar and shoulder-belt have become a shackle and chain.

entries.farsi: هر كه آخر بين‏تر او مسعودتر / هر كه آخور بين‏تر او مطرودتر
entries.english: Reckon every particle of the World like this: bring its beginning and its end into consideration.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر جزو عالم مى‏شمر / اول و آخر در آرش در نظر
entries.english: The more anyone regards the end (ákhir) the more blessed he is; the more anyone regards the stable (ákhur) the more banned he is.

entries.farsi: روى هر يك چون مه فاخر ببين / چون كه اول ديده شد آخر ببين
entries.english: Regard every one’s face as the glorious moon: when the beginning has been seen, see the end,

entries.farsi: تا نباشى همچو ابليس اعورى / ‏نيم بيند نيم نه چون ابترى
entries.english: Lest you become a man blind of one eye, like Iblis: he, like a person docked, sees half and not half.

entries.farsi: ديد طين آدم و دينش نديد / اين جهان ديد آن جهان بينش نديد
entries.english: He saw the clay (tin) of Adam but did not see his obedience to God (din): he saw in him this world but did not see that which beholds yonder world.

entries.farsi: فضل مردان بر زنان اى بو شجاع / ‏نيست بهر قوت و كسب و ضياع
entries.english: The superiority of men to women, O valorous one, is not on account of strength and money-making and landed estates;

entries.farsi: ور نه شير و پيل را بر آدمى / ‏فضل بودى بهر قوت اى عمى
entries.english: Otherwise the lion and elephant because of strength would be superior to the human being, O blind one.

entries.farsi: فضل مردان بر زن اى حالى پرست / ‏ز آن بود كه مرد پايان بين‏تر است
entries.english: The superiority of men to women, O time-server, is because man is more regardful of the end.

entries.farsi: مرد كاندر عاقبت بينى خم است / ‏او ز اهل عاقبت چون زن كم است
entries.english: The man who is crooked in respect of seeing the end, he, like a woman, is inferior to those acquainted with the end.

entries.farsi: از جهان دو بانگ مى‏آيد به ضد / تا كدامين را تو باشى مستعد
entries.english: From the World are coming two cries in opposition: for which you are adapted.

entries.farsi: آن يكى بانگش نشور اتقيا / و آن يكى بانگش فريب اشقيا
entries.english: Its one cry is the quickening the devout with life; and its other cry is the cajoling the graceless.

entries.farsi: من شكوفه‏ى خارم اى خوش گرم‏دار / گل بريزد من بمانم شاخ خار
entries.english: “I am the thorn-blossom, O sweet cherisher: the flower will drop and I shall remain a thorn-bough.”

entries.farsi: بانگ اشكوفه‏ش كه اينك گل فروش / ‏بانگ خار او كه سوى ما مكوش
entries.english: The cry of its blossom is, “Here is the flower-seller!” The cry of its thorn is, “Do not strive towards me.”

entries.farsi: اين پذيرفتى بماندى ز آن دگر / كه محب از ضد محبوب است كر
entries.english: You have accepted this, you are left by the other, for a lover is deaf to the contrary of the object loved.

entries.farsi: آن يكى بانگ اين كه اينك حاضرم / ‏بانگ ديگر بنگر اندر آخرم
entries.english: The one cry is this, “Here am I, ready”; the other cry is, “Look upon my latter end.

entries.farsi: حاضرى‏ام هست چون مكر و كمين / ‏نقش آخر ز آينه‏ى اول ببين
entries.english: My readiness is like guile and ambush: behold the image of the end in the mirror of the beginning.”

entries.farsi: چون يكى زين دو جوال اندر شدى / ‏آن دگر را ضد و نادر خور شدى
entries.english: When you have gone into one of these two sacks, you have become contrary and unsuitable to the other.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن كاو ز اول آن شنيد / كش عقول و مسمع مردان شنيد
entries.english: Oh, happy is he who, from the first, heard that which the intelligences and the ears of men have heard.

entries.farsi: خانه خالى يافت و جارا او گرفت / ‏غير آنش كژ نمايد يا شگفت
entries.english: It has found the house empty and taken abode, all else appears to him perverted or wonderful.

entries.farsi: كوزه‏ى نو كاو به خود بولى كشيد / آن خبث را آب نتواند بريد
entries.english: The new pot that has drawn to itself some urine: water cannot detach that filth.

entries.farsi: در جهان هر چيز چيزى مى‏كشد / كفر كافر را و مرشد را رشد
entries.english: Everything in the world draws something: infidelity the infidel and righteousness him who is guided aright.

entries.farsi: كهربا هم هست و مغناطيس هست / ‏تا تو آهن يا كهى آيى به شست
entries.english: There is both the amber and the magnet: whether you art iron or straw you will come to the hook.

entries.farsi: برد مغناطيست ار تو آهنى / ‏ور كهى بر كهربا بر مى‏تنى
entries.english: The magnet carries you off if you are iron; and if you are straw, you will be in contact with the amber.

entries.farsi: آن يكى چون نيست با اخيار يار / الجرم شد پهلوى فجار جار
entries.english: When any one is not associated with the good, he inevitably becomes a neighbour to the wicked.

entries.farsi: هست موسى پيش قبطى بس ذميم / هست هامان پيش سبطى بس رجيم
entries.english: Moses is very despicable in the eyes of the Egyptian; Haman is exceedingly accursed in the eyes of the Israelite.

entries.farsi: جان هامان جاذب قبطى شده / ‏جان موسى طالب سبطى شده
entries.english: The spirit of Haman has drawn the Egyptian; the spirit of Moses has sought the Israelite.

entries.farsi: معده‏ى خر كه كشد در اجتذاب / ‏معده‏ى آدم جذوب گندم آب
entries.english: The belly of the ass draws straw at the swallowing; the belly of Adam is an attractor of wheat-broth.

entries.farsi: گر تو نشناسى كسى را از ظالم / ‏بنگر اوراك اوش سازيده ست امام
entries.english: If, on account of the darkness, you do not recognise a person, look at him whom he has made his imam;

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه عارف را غذايى است از نور حق كه ابيت عند ربى يطعمنى و يسقينى و قوله الجوع طعام ‏َّاللهَّ يحيى به ابدان الصديقين اى فى الجوع يصل طعام الله
sections.title_en: Explaining that the gnostic has a nutriment of the Light of God, for, “I pass the night with my Lord: He gives me meat and drink”; and “Hunger is God’s food whereby He revives the bodies of the siddíqs,”
that is, “in hunger God’s food reaches.”

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر كره پى مادر رود / تا بدان جنسيت‏اش پيدا شود
entries.english: For every foal goes after its dam, so that thereby its being a congener becomes apparent

entries.farsi: آدمى را شير از سينه رسد / شير خر از نيم زيرينه رسد
entries.english: The human creature’s milk comes from the breast; the milk of the ass comes from the under-half.

entries.farsi: عدل قسام است و قسمت كردنى است / ‏اين عجب كه جبر نى و ظلم نيست
entries.english: It is the Justice of the Dispenser, it is an act of dispensation: the wonder is this, that there is neither compulsion nor injustice.

entries.farsi: جبر بودى كى پشيمانى بدى / ‏ظلم بودى كى نگهبانى بدى
entries.english: Were there compulsion, how would there be repentance? Were there injustice, how would there be protection?

entries.farsi: روز آخر شد سبق فردا بود / راز ما را روز كى گنجا بود
entries.english: The day is ended: the lesson will be to-morrow: how should the day contain our mystery?

entries.farsi: اى بكرده اعتماد واثقى / ‏بر دم و بر چاپلوس فاسقى
entries.english: O you who have put firm confidence in the breath and flattery of a scoundrel,

entries.farsi: قبه‏اى بر ساخته ستى از حباب / ‏آخر آن خيمه‏ست بس واهى طناب
entries.english: You have raised up a tent of bubbles: in the end that tent has exceedingly weak ropes.

entries.farsi: زرق چون برق است و اندر نور آن / ‏راه نتوانند ديدن ره روان
entries.english: Hypocrisy is like lightning, and in its gleam the travellers cannot see the way.

entries.farsi: اين جهان و اهل او بى‏حاصلند / هر دو اندر بى‏وفايى يك دلند
entries.english: This world and its people are good-for-nothing: both are unanimous in respect of faithlessness.

entries.farsi: زاده‏ى دنيا چو دنيا بى‏وفاست / گر چه رو آرد به تو آن رو قفاست
entries.english: The son of the world is faithless like the world: though he turns the face towards you, that face is the nape.

entries.farsi: اهل آن عالم چو آن عالم ز بر / تا ابد در عهد و پيمان مستمر
entries.english: The people of that world, like that world, on account of goodness continue forever in covenant and promise.

entries.farsi: خود دو پيغمبر به هم كى ضد شدند / معجزات از همدگر كى بستدند
entries.english: When, in truth, did two prophets oppose each other? When did they wrest evidential miracles from one another?

entries.farsi: كى شود پژمرده ميوه‏ى آن جهان / ‏شادى عقلى نگردد اندهان
entries.english: How should the fruit of that world become stale? Intellectual joy does not turn into sorrows.

entries.farsi: نفس بى‏عهد است ز آن رو كشتنى است / ‏او دنى و قبله‏گاه او دنى است
entries.english: The fleshly soul is faithful; for that reason it ought to be killed: it is base, and base is the spot to which its desires are directed.

entries.farsi: نفسها را اليق است اين انجمن / ‏مرده را در خور بود گور و كفن
entries.english: This assembly is well-adapted for fleshly souls: the grave and shroud are suitable to the dead.

entries.farsi: نفس اگر چه زيرك است و خرده‏دان / ‏قبله‏اش دنياست او را مرده دان
entries.english: Although the fleshly soul is sagacious and acute, its qibla is this world, regard it as dead.

entries.farsi: آب وحى حق بدين مرده رسيد / شد ز خاك مرده‏اى زنده پديد
entries.english: The water of God’s inspiration has reached this dead, the living comes into view from the tomb of a corpse.

entries.farsi: تا نيايد وحى تو غره مباش / ‏تو بدان گلگونه‏ى طال بقاش
entries.english: Until inspiration comes, do not be duped by that rouge of “May his life be long!”

entries.farsi: بانگ و صيتى جو كه آن خامل نشد / تاب خورشيدى كه آن آفل نشد
entries.english: Seek the applause and renown that does not die away, the splendour of the sun that does not sink.

entries.farsi: آن هنرهاى دقيق و قال و قيل / ‏قوم فرعون‏اند اجل چون آب نيل
entries.english: Those abstruse sciences and disputations are the people of Pharaoh: Death is like the water of the Nile.

entries.farsi: رونق و طاق و طرنب و سحرشان / ‏گر چه خلقان را كشد گردن كشان
entries.english: Although their brilliance and pomp and show and enchantment drag the people along by the scruff of the neck,

entries.farsi: سحرهاى ساحران دان جمله را / مرگ چوبى دان كه آن گشت اژدها
entries.english: Know that all is the enchantments of the magicians; know that Death is the rod which became a dragon.

entries.farsi: جادويى‏ها را همه يك لقمه كرد / يك جهان پر شب بدان را صبح خورد
entries.english: It made one mouthful of all sorceries. There was a world filled with night: the dawn devoured it.

entries.farsi: نور از آن خوردن نشد افزون و بيش / ‏بل همان سان است كاو بوده ست پيش
entries.english: The light is not made greater and more by that devouring; nay, it is just the same as it has been before.

entries.farsi: در اثر افزون شد و در ذات نى / ‏ذات را افزونى و آفات نى
entries.english: It is increased in respect of the effect, but not in respect of its essence: the essence has no increase or diminution.

entries.farsi: حق ز ايجاد جهان افزون نشد / آن چه اول آن نبود اكنون نشد
entries.english: God was not increased by bringing the world into existence: that which He was not formerly He has not become now;

entries.farsi: ليك افزون گشت اثر ز ايجاد خلق / ‏در ميان اين دو افزونى است فرق
entries.english: But the effect was increased by bringing created things into existence: there is difference between these two increases.

entries.farsi: هست افزونى اثر اظهار او / تا پديد آيد صفات و كار او
entries.english: The increase of the effect is His manifestation, in order that His attributes and action may be made visible.

entries.farsi: هست افزونى هر ذاتى دليل / ‏كاو بود حادث به علتها عليل
entries.english: The increase of any essence is a proof that it is originated and subject to causes.

sections.title_fa: ‏تفسير فَأَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِ هِ خِيفَةً مُوسى‏ قُلْنا ال تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الأَْعْ لى
sections.title_en: Commentary on “Moses conceived a fear in his heart: We said, ‘Fear not, truly you will be the superior.’”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى سحر هم حيران كنى است / ‏چون كنم كاين خلق را تمييز نيست
entries.english: Moses said, “Magic too is a bewildering thing: how shall I act?— for this people have no discernment.”

entries.farsi: گفت حق تمييز را پيدا كنم / گر چه چون دريا بر آوردند كف
entries.english: God said, “I will produce discernment, I will make the undiscerning mind able to perceive.

entries.farsi: ‏موسيا تو غالب آيى ال تخف / بود اندر عهد خود سحر افتخار
entries.english: Although they have raised up foam, like the sea, you, O Moses, will prevail: fear not!”

entries.farsi: چون عصا شد مار آنها گشت عار / هر كسى را دعوى حسن و نمك
entries.english: Magic was glorious in its own time: when the rod became a dragon, those were disgraced.

entries.farsi: ‏سنگ مرگ آمد نمكها را محك / سحر رفت و معجزه‏ى موسى گذشت
entries.english: Every one pretends to excellence and elegance: the stone of Death is the touchstone for elegances.

entries.farsi: بانگ طشت دين بجز رفعت چه ماند / چون محك پنهان شده‏ست از مرد و زن
entries.english: Magic is gone and the miracle of Moses is past: as regards both, the bowl has fallen from the roof of being.

entries.farsi: ‏هر دو را از بام بود افتاد طشت / بانگ طشت سحر جز لعنت چه ماند
entries.english: What has the noise of the bowl of magic left behind but execration? What has the noise of the bowl of religion left behind but sublimity?

entries.farsi: ‏در صف آ اى قلب و اكنون الف‏زن / وقت الف است محك چون غايب است
entries.english: Since the touchstone has become hidden from man and woman, O adulterated coin, come now into line and brag!

entries.farsi: قلب مى‏گويد ز نخوت هر دمم / زر همى‏گويد بلى اى خواجه‏تاش
entries.english: It is the time for you to brag. Since the touchstone is absent, they will pass you in honour from hand to hand.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏برندت از عزيزى دست دست / قلب مى‏گويد ز نخوت هر دمم
entries.english: The adulterated coin is ever saying to me arrogantly, “O pure gold, how am I inferior to you?”

entries.farsi: ‏ليك مى‏آيد محك آماده باش / مرگ تن هديه‏ست بر اصحاب راز
entries.english: The gold says, “Yes, O fellow-servant; but the touchstone is coming: be prepared.”

entries.farsi: زر خالص را چه نقصان است گاز / قلب اگر در خويش آخر بين بدى
entries.english: The death of the body is a gift to the adepts of the mystery: what damage is the scissors to pure gold?

entries.farsi: ‏آن سيه كاخر شد او اول شدى / چون شدى اول سيه اندر لقا
entries.english: If the adulterated coin had seen the end in regard to itself, it would have become at first the black which it became in the end.

entries.farsi: دور بودى از نفاق و از شقا / كيمياى فضل را طالب بدى
entries.english: Since it would have become black at first, in confrontation it would have been far from duplicity and damnation.

entries.farsi: ‏عقل او بر زرق او غالب بدى / چون شكسته دل شدى از حال خويش
entries.english: It would have sought the elixir of grace; its reason would have prevailed over its hypocrisy.

entries.farsi: ‏جابر اشكستگان ديدى به پيش / عاقبت را ديد و او اشكسته شد
entries.english: Since it would have become broken-hearted on account of its state, it would have seen before it Him who mends them that are broken.

entries.farsi: از شكسته بند در دم بسته شد / فضل مسها را سوى اكسير راند
entries.english: It saw the end and became broken, it was at once bandaged by the Bone-setter.

entries.farsi: آن زر اندود از كرم محروم ماند / اى زر اندوده مكن دعوى ببين
entries.english: The grace impelled the pieces of copper towards the elixir; the gilt remained deprived of bounty.

entries.farsi: ‏كه نماند مشتريت اعمى چنين / نور محشر چشمشان بينا كند
entries.english: O gilt one; do not make pretensions: recognise that your purchaser will not remain so blind.

entries.farsi: چشم بندى ترا رسوا كند / بنگر آنها را كه آخر ديده‏اند
entries.english: The light of the place of congregation will cause their eyes to see and will expose your blindfolding.

entries.farsi: حسرت جانها و رشك ديده‏اند / بنگر آنها را كه حالى ديده‏اند
entries.english: Look at those who have seen the end: they are the amazement of souls and the envy of the eye.

entries.farsi: سر فاسد ز اصل سر ببريده‏اند / پيش حالى بين كه در جهل است و شك
entries.english: Look at those who have seen the present: their inmost self is corrupt; they are radically decapitated.

entries.farsi: ‏صبح صادق صبح كاذب هر دو يك / صبح كاذب صد هزاران كاروان
entries.english: To the seer of the present, who is in ignorance and doubt, both the true dawn and the false dawn are one.

entries.farsi: ‏داد بر باد هالكت اى جوان / نيست نقدى كش غلط انداز نيست
entries.english: The false dawn has given a hundred thousand caravans to the wind of destruction, O youth.

entries.farsi: ‏واى آن جان كش محك و گاز نيست / بو مسيلم گفت خود من احمدم
entries.english: There is no genuine money that has not a deceptive counterfeit: alas for the soul that does not possess the touchstone and scissors!

sections.title_fa: ‏زجر مدعى از دعوى و امر كردن او را به متابعت
sections.title_en: Warning the pretender to shun pretension and enjoining him to follow.

entries.farsi: ‏دين احمد را به فن بر هم زدم / بو مسيلم را بگو كم كن بطر
entries.english: Bu Musaylim said, “I myself am Ahmad: I have cunningly confounded the religion of Ahmad.”

entries.farsi: غره‏ى اول مشو آخر نگر / اين قالووزى مكن از حرص جمع
entries.english: Say to Bu Musaylim, “Do not behave with insolence: be not deluded by the beginning, regard the end.

entries.farsi: ‏پس روى كن تا رود در پيش شمع / شمع مقصد را نمايد همچو ماه
entries.english: Do not act thus as a guide from greed for amassing: follow behind, in order that the Candle may go in front.”

entries.farsi: ‏كاين طرف دانه ست يا خود دامگاه / گر بخواهى ور نخواهى با چراغ
entries.english: The Candle, like the moon, shows the destination, and whether in this direction there is the grain or the place for the snare.

entries.farsi: ‏ديده گردد نقش باز و نقش زاغ / ور نه اين زاغان دغل افروختند
entries.english: Whether you will or not, with the Lantern the form of falcon and the form of crow become visible.

entries.farsi: بانگ بازان سپيد آموختند / بانگ هدهد گر بياموزد فتى
entries.english: Otherwise, these crows have lit fraud: they have learned the cry of the white falcons.

entries.farsi: راز هدهد كو و پيغام سبا / بانگ بر رسته ز بر بسته بدان
entries.english: If a man learns the cry of the hoopoe, where is the mystery of the hoopoe and the message from Saba?

entries.farsi: ‏تاج شاهان را ز تاج هدهدان / حرف درويشان و نكته‏ى عارفان
entries.english: Know the natural cry from the artificial one, the crown of kings from the crown of hoopoes.

entries.farsi: بسته‏اند اين بى‏حيايان بر زبان / هر هالك امت پيشين كه بود
entries.english: These shameless persons have attached to their tongues the speech of dervishes and the deep sayings of gnostics.

entries.farsi: ز انكه چندل را گمان بردند عود / بودشان تمييز كان مظهر كند
entries.english: Every destruction of an olden people that there was— because they deemed sandal-wood to be wood.

entries.farsi: ليك حرص و آز كور و كر كند / كورى كوران ز رحمت دور نيست
entries.english: They had the discernment that should make that evident, but greed and cupidity make blind and deaf.

entries.farsi: ‏كورى حرص است كان معذور نيست / چار ميخ شه ز رحمت دور نى
entries.english: The blindness of the blind is not far from mercy; it is the blindness of greed that is inexcusable.

entries.farsi: ‏چار ميخ حاسدى مغفور نى / ماهيا آخر نگر بنگر به شست
entries.english: Crucifixion (tribulation) inflicted by the King is not far from mercy; the crucifixion of envy is not forgiven.

entries.farsi: ‏بد گلويى چشم آخر بينت بست / با دو ديده اول و آخر ببين
entries.english: O fish regard the end; do not regard the hook: evil appetite has bandaged your eye that sees the end.

entries.farsi: ‏هين مباش اعور چو ابليس لعين / اعور آن باشد كه حالى ديد و بس
entries.english: See the beginning and the end with both eyes: beware, do not be one-eyed like the accursed Iblis.

entries.farsi: ‏چون بهايم بى‏خبر از باز پس / چون دو چشم گاو در جرم تلف
entries.english: The one-eyed man is he who saw only the present— ignorant, like the beasts, of after.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو يك چشم است كش نبود شرف / نصف قيمت ارزد آن دو چشم او
entries.english: Since the two eyes of an ox are as one eye in damages for destruction— for it has no excellence—

entries.farsi: كه دو چشمش راست مسند چشم تو / ور كنى يك چشم آدم زاده‏اى
entries.english: Its two eyes are worth a half of its value, inasmuch as your eye is the support for its two eyes.

entries.farsi: ‏نصف قيمت اليق است از جاده‏اى / ز انكه چشم آدمى تنها به خود
entries.english: But if you destroy one eye of a son of Adam, by a statute you must pay half of his value,

entries.farsi: بى‏دو چشم يار كارى مى‏كند / چشم خر چون اولش بى‏آخر است
entries.english: Because the human eye works alone by itself without the two eyes of a friend

entries.farsi: ‏گردو چشمش هست حكمش اعور است / اين سخن پايان ندارد و آن خفيف
entries.english: Since the donkey’s eye the beginning is not accompanied by the end, it is in the same case as the one-eyed man, if it has two eyes.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏نويسد رقعه در طمع رغيف / رفت پيش از نامه پيش مطبخى
entries.english: This topic has no limit—and that light-minded one is writing a letter in hope of loaves.

sections.title_fa: ‏بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى نوشتن آن غالم رقعه به طلب اجرى
sections.title_en: The rest of the story of the slave’s writing a petition for his allowance.

entries.farsi: ‏كاى بخيل از مطبخ شاه سخى / دور از او و ز همت او كاين قدر
entries.english: Before the letter he went to the kitchen-steward and said, “O niggard of the kitchen of the generous king,

entries.farsi: از جرى‏ام آيدش اندر نظر / گفت بهر مصلحت فرموده است
entries.english: ‘It is far from him and from his magnanimity that this amount of my allowance should come into his consideration.”

entries.farsi: ‏نه براى بخل و نه تنگى دست / گفت دهليزى است و اهلل اين سخن
entries.english: He said, “He has ordered for a good object, not on account of stinginess or close-fistedness.”

entries.farsi: ‏پيش شه خاك است هم زر كهن / مطبخى ده گونه حجت بر فراشت
entries.english: “By God,” he replied, “this is a canard: even old gold is as dust in the king’s eyes.”

entries.farsi: او همه رد كرد از حرصى كه داشت / چون جرى كم آمدش در وقت چاشت
entries.english: The steward raised up manifold arguments: he rejected them all because of the greed which he had.

entries.farsi: ‏زد بسى تشنيع او سودى نداشت / گفت قاصد مى‏كنيد اينها شما
entries.english: When, at the time of the forenoon meal, his allowance was reduced, he uttered much revilement, it was of no avail.

entries.farsi: گفت نه كه بنده فرمانيم ما / اين مگير از فرع اين از اصل گير
entries.english: He said, “You are doing these things on purpose.” “No,” said the other, “we obey the command.

entries.farsi: بر كمان كم زن كه از بازوست تير / ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ ابتالست
entries.english: Do not regard this from the branch: regard it from the root; do not strike at the bow, for the arrow is from the arm.

entries.farsi: ‏بر نبى كم نه گنه كان از خداست / آب از سر تيره است اى خيره چشم
entries.english: You did not throw when you threw is a trial: do not lay the fault on the Prophet, for that is from God.

entries.farsi: ‏پيشتر بنگر يكى بگشاى چشم / شد ز خشم و غم درون بقعه‏اى
entries.english: The water is turbid from the source: O you, who are angry in vain, look farther on, open your eye once!”

entries.farsi: ‏سوى شه بنوشت خشمين رقعه‏اى / اندر آن رقعه ثناى شاه گفت
entries.english: By anger and resentment he went into a certain place and wrote an angry letter to the king.

entries.farsi: ‏گوهر جود و سخاى شاه سفت / كاى ز بحر و ابر افزون كف تو
entries.english: In that letter he lauded the king and threaded the pearl of the king’s munificence and generosity,

entries.farsi: در قضاى حاجت حاجات جو / ز انكه ابر آن چه دهد گريان دهد
entries.english: Saying, “O you whose hand exceeds the sea and the clouds in fulfilling the want of the suitor,

entries.farsi: كف تو خندان پياپى خوان نهد / ظاهر رقعه اگر چه مدح بود
entries.english: Because that which the cloud gives, it gives with tears, your hand incessantly lays the dish with smiles.”

entries.farsi: بوى خشم از مدح اثرها مى‏نمود / ز آن همه كار تو بى‏نور است و زشت
entries.english: Though the outward form of the letter was praise, from the praise the scent of anger was showing traces.

entries.farsi: ‏كه تو دورى دور از نور سرشت / رونق كار خسان كاسد شود
entries.english: All your actions are devoid of light and ugly because you are far, far from the light of your original nature.

entries.farsi: همچو ميوه‏ى تازه زو فاسد شود / رونق دنيا بر آرد زو كساد
entries.english: The splendour of the actions of the vile becomes unsaleable, just as fresh fruit soon becomes rotten.

entries.farsi: ز انكه هست از عالم كون و فساد / خوش نگردد از مديحى سينه‏ها
entries.english: The splendour of the present life soon produces becomes of no account, inasmuch as it belongs to the world of generation and corruption.

entries.farsi: چون كه در مداح باشد كينه‏ها / اى دل از كين و كراهت پاك شو
entries.english: Breasts are not gladdened by an encomium when there are feelings of enmity in the encomiast.

entries.farsi: و آن گهان الحمد خوان چالاك شو / بر زبان الحمد و اكراه درون
entries.english: O heart, become purged of enmity and repugnance, and then chant “Glory to God” and be busy.

entries.farsi: ‏از زبان تلبيس باشد يا فسون / و آنگهان گفته خدا كه ننگرم
entries.english: “Glory to God” on your tongue and repugnance within is hypocrisy or guile on the tongue’s part;

entries.farsi: ‏من به ظاهر من به باطن ناظرم / آن يكى با دلق آمد از عراق
entries.english: And moreover God has said, “I do not look to the exterior, I am regarding the interior.”

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن مداح كه از جهت ناموس شكر ممدوح مى‏كرد و بوى اندوه و غم اندرون او و خالقت دلق ‏ظاهر او مى‏نمود كه آن شكرها الف است و دروغ
sections.title_en: Story of the encomiast who from regard for reputation was thanking the object of his praise,
while the scent of his inward grief and pain and the shabbiness of his outward garb showed that those expressions of gratitude were vain and false.

entries.farsi: ‏باز پرسيدند ياران از فراق / گفت آرى بد فراق الا سفر
entries.english: A certain man came from Iraq, in a tattered cloak: his friends inquired concerning separation.

entries.farsi: بود بر من بس مبارك مژده‏ور / كه خليفه داد ده خلعت مرا
entries.english: “Yes,” he replied; “there was separation, but the journey was very blessed and fortunate for me,

entries.farsi: كه قرينش باد صد مدح و ثنا / شكرها و مدحها بر مى‏شمرد
entries.english: For the Caliph gave me ten robes of honour— may a hundred praises and laudations accompany him!”

entries.farsi: تا كه شكر از حد و اندازه ببرد / پس بگفتندش كه احوال نژند
entries.english: He was reciting expressions of gratitude and praise till he carried gratitude beyond bound and limit.

entries.farsi: بر دروغ تو گواهى مى‏دهند / تن برهنه سر برهنه سوخته
entries.english: Then they said to him, “Your wretched guise bears witness to your mendacity.

entries.farsi: شكر را دزديده يا آموخته / كو نشان شكر و حمد مير تو
entries.english: Naked, bare-headed, consumed: you have stolen expressions of gratitude or learned.

entries.farsi: بر سر و بر پاى بى‏توفير تو / گر زبانت مدح آن شه مى‏تند
entries.english: Where are the signs of the gratitude and praise due to your prince on your unplenished head and feet?

entries.farsi: هفت اندامت شكايت مى‏كند / در سخاى آن شه و سلطان جود
entries.english: If your tongue is weaving praise of that king, your seven members are complaining.

entries.farsi: مر ترا كفشى و شلوارى نبود / گفت من ايثار كردم آن چه داد
entries.english: In the generosity of that king and sultan of munificence was there not a pair of shoes and trousers for you?”

entries.farsi: مير تقصيرى نكرد از افتقاد / بستدم جمله‏ى عطاها از امير
entries.english: He replied, “I gave away what he bestowed: the prince left nothing undone in the way of solicitude.

entries.farsi: بخش كردم بر يتيم و بر فقير / مال دادم بستدم عمر دراز
entries.english: I received all the presents from the prince and distributed them among the orphans and the poor.

entries.farsi: در جزا زيرا كه بودم پاك باز / پس بگفتندش مبارك مال رفت
entries.english: I gave the riches away and received long life in return, because I was utterly self-sacrificing.”

entries.farsi: ‏چيست اندر باطنت اين دود و تفت / صد كراهت در درون تو چو خار
entries.english: Then they said to him, “Bless you! The riches are gone: what is this naphtha-smoke within you?

entries.farsi: كى بود انده نشان ابتشار / كو نشان عشق و ايثار و رضا
entries.english: A hundred hatreds like thorns are in your heart: how should grief be the sign of rejoicing?

entries.farsi: ‏گر درست است آن چه گفتى ما مضى / خود گرفتم مال گم شد ميل كو
entries.english: Where are the signs of love and charity and being pleased, if what you have said of what passed is true?

entries.farsi: سيل اگر بگذشت جاى سيل كو / چشم تو گر بد سياه و جان فزا
entries.english: I grant, indeed, that the riches are gone: where is desire? If the torrent has gone by, where is the torrent-bed?

entries.farsi: گر نماند او جان فزا ازرق چرا / كو نشان پاك بازى اى ترش
entries.english: If your eye was black and soul-inspiring, and if it is soul-inspiring no longer, why is it blue?

entries.farsi: ‏بوى الف كژ همى‏آيد خمش / صد نشان باشد درون ايثار را
entries.english: Where the signs of self-sacrifice, O are sour one? The smell of false and empty words is coming: be silent!”

entries.farsi: صد عالمت هست نيكو كار را / مال در ايثار اگر گردد تلف
entries.english: Charity has a hundred signs within (in the heart): the good deed has a hundred tokens.

entries.farsi: ‏در درون صد زندگى آيد خلف / در زمين حق زراعت كردنى
entries.english: If riches be consumed in charity, a hundred lives come into the heart as a substitute.

entries.farsi: پس چه واسع باشد ارض اللهَّ بگو / چون كه اين ارض فنا بى‏ريع نيست
entries.english: A sowing of pure seeds in God’s earth, and then no income!

entries.farsi: ‏تخمهاى پاك آن گه دخل نى / گر نرويد خوشه از روضات هو
entries.english: If the ears of corn grow not from the gardens of Hu, then tell, how should God’s earth be “spacious”?

entries.farsi: ‏چون بود ارض اللهَّ آن مستوسعى است / اين زمين را ريع او خود بى‏حد است
entries.english: Since this earth of mortality is not without produce, how should God’s earth be? That is a spacious place.

entries.farsi: ‏دانه اى را كمترين خود هفصد است / حمد گفتى كو نشان حامدون
entries.english: Verily, the produce of this earth is infinite: even the least for a single seed is seven-hundredfold.

entries.farsi: نه برونت هست اثر نه اندرون / حمد عارف مر خدا را راست است
entries.english: You said, “Glory to God!” Where are the signs of those who glorify? Neither in your exterior nor within is there a trace.

entries.farsi: ‏كه گواه حمد او شد پا و دست / از چه تاريك جسمش بر كشيد
entries.english: The gnostic’s glorification of God is right, for his feet and hands have borne witness to his glorification.

entries.farsi: و ز تگ زندان دنيايش خريد / اطلس تقوى و نور موتلف
entries.english: It has lifted him up from the dark pit of the body and redeemed him from the bottom of the dungeon of this world.

entries.farsi: ‏آيت حمد است او را بر كتف / وا رهيده از جهان عاريه
entries.english: On his shoulder is the sign of glorification— the silken robe of piety and the light which associates itself.

entries.farsi: ساكن گلزار و عَيْنٌ جارية / بر سرير سر عالى همتش
entries.english: He is delivered from the transitory world; he is dwelling in the Rose-garden, and a running fountain.

entries.farsi: جمله سر سبزند و شاد و تازه رو / حمدشان چون حمد گلشن از بهار
entries.english: His sitting-place and home and abode is on the throne of the high-aspiring inmost consciousness, and his station

entries.farsi: ‏مجلس و جاه و مقام و رتبتش / مقعد صدقى كه صديقان در او
entries.english: Is the Seat of sincerity in which all the siddíqs are flourishing and joyous and fresh of countenance.

entries.farsi: صد نشانى دارد و صد گير و دار / بر بهارش چشمه و نخل و گياه
entries.english: Their praise, like the garden’s praise on account of spring, has a hundred signs and a hundred displays.

entries.farsi: ‏و آن گلستان و نگارستان گواه / شاهد شاهد هزاران هر طرف
entries.english: Fountains and palms and herbs and rose-beds and plots of bright-coloured flowers bear witness to its springtide.

entries.farsi: ‏در گواهى همچو گوهر بر صدف / بوى سر بد بيايد از دمت
entries.english: Everywhere thousands of witnesses to the Beloved are in bearing testimony, as the pearl to the oyster-shell.

entries.farsi: ‏وز سر و رو تابد اى الفى غمت / بو شناسانند حاذق در مصاف
entries.english: From your breath comes the smell of a bad conscience, and your pain is reflected from your head and face, O braggart!

entries.farsi: ‏تو به جلدى‏هاى و هو كم كن گزاف / تو مالف از مشك كان بوى پياز
entries.english: In the battle-field there are sagacious ones who know the smell: do not in presumption idly utter the ecstatic cries.

entries.farsi: از دم تو مى‏كند مكشوف راز / گل شكر خوردم همى‏گويى و بوى
entries.english: Do not brag of musk, for that smell of onions is revealing the secret of your breath.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏زند از سير كه يافه مگوى / هست دل ماننده‏ى خانه‏ى كالن
entries.english: You are saying, “I have eaten rose-sugar,” while the smell of garlic is striking and saying, “Don’t talk nonsense.”

entries.farsi: ‏خانه‏ى دل را نهان همسايگان / از شكاف روزن و ديوارها
entries.english: The heart is like unto a great house: the house of the heart has neighbours concealed:

entries.farsi: مطلع گردند بر اسرارها / از شكافى كه ندارد هيچ وهم
entries.english: Through the window-slit and the walls they observe the hidden thoughts—

entries.farsi: ‏صاحب خانه ندارد هيچ سهم / از نبى بر خوان كه ديو و قوم او
entries.english: Through a slit whereof the owner of the house has no conception and in which he has no share

entries.farsi: مى‏برند از حال انسى خفيه بو / از رهى كه انس از آن آگاه نيست
entries.english: Recite from the Qur’an that the Devil and his tribe secretly get scent of the state of humankind,

entries.farsi: ‏با محك اى قلب دون الفى مزن / مر محك را ره بود در نقد و قلب
entries.english: By a way of which humankind are ignorant, because it is not of this sensible or one of these semblances.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه زين محسوس و زين اشباه نيست / در ميان ناقدان زرقى متن
entries.english: Do not devise any fraud amongst the assayers: do not address any idle boast to the touchstone, O base spurious coin.

entries.farsi: ‏كه خدايش كرد امير جسم و قلب / چون شياطين با غليظيهاى خويش
entries.english: The touchstone has a way to the genuine and the spurious coin, for God made him the commander of body and heart.

entries.farsi: ‏واقفند از سر ما و فكر و كيش / مسلكى دارند دزديده درون
entries.english: Since the devils, notwithstanding their grossness, are acquainted with our inmost soul and thought and belief,

entries.farsi: صاحب نقب و شكاف روزنند / پس چرا جانهاى روشن در جهان
entries.english: Have a passage stealthily within, we are overthrown by their thievish practices,

entries.farsi: ‏ما ز دزديهاى ايشان سر نگون / دم‏به‏دم خبط و زيانى مى‏كنند
entries.english: They continually inflict a derangement and damage, for they are masters of the tunnel and window-slit—

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏خبر باشند از حال نهان / در سرايت كمتر از ديوان شدند
entries.english: Why, then, should the illuminated spirits in the world be unaware of our hidden state?

entries.farsi: روحها كه خيمه بر گردون زدند / ديو دزدانه سوى گردون رود
entries.english: Have the spirits that pitched their tent on Heaven become inferior to the devils in respect of permeation?

entries.farsi: از شهاب محرق او مطعون شود / سر نگون از چرخ زير افتد چنان
entries.english: The devil goes like a thief towards Heaven, and he is pierced with a burning meteor:

entries.farsi: از فلكشان سر نگون مى‏افگنند / تو اگر شلى و لنگ و كور و كر
entries.english: He falls down headlong from the sky as the wretched in battle by the blow of the spear-point.

entries.farsi: ‏كه شقى در جنگ از زخم سنان / آن ز رشك روحهاى دل پسند
entries.english: That is caused by the jealousy of the delectable spirits: they cast them headlong from Heaven.

entries.farsi: اين گمان بر روحهاى مه مبر / شرم دار و الف كم زن جان مكن
entries.english: If you are palsied and lame and blind and deaf, do not hold this opinion of the great spirits.

entries.farsi: ‏كه بسى جاسوس هست آن سوى تن
entries.english: Be ashamed and do not utter idle words, do not torment yourself, for there are many spies beyond the body.

sections.title_fa: دريافتن طبيبان الهى امراض دين و دل را در سيماى مريد و بيگانه و لحن گفتار او و رنگ چشم او ‏و بى‏اين همه نيز از راه دل كه انهم جواسيس القلوب فجالسوهم بالصدق
sections.title_en: How the divine physicians detect diseases, religious and spiritual, in the countenance of friend or stranger and in the tones of his speech and the colour of his eyes,
and even without all these, by the way of the heart; for “verily, they are spies on the hearts; therefore behave with sincerity when you sit with them.”

entries.farsi: اين طبيبان بدن دانش‏ورند / بر سقام تو ز تو واقف‏ترند
entries.english: These physicians of the body have knowledge: they are more acquainted with your malady than you are,

entries.farsi: تا ز قاروره همى‏بينند حال / كه ندانى تو از آن رو اعتالل
entries.english: So that they perceive the state from the urine-bottle, though you cannot know your ailment by that means,

entries.farsi: هم ز نبض و هم ز رنگ و هم ز دم / ‏بو برند از تو به هر گونه سقم
entries.english: And from your pulse, complexion, and breath alike they diagnose every kind of disease in you.

entries.farsi: پس طبيبان الهى در جهان / ‏چون ندانند از تو بى‏گفت دهان
entries.english: How, then, should the divine physicians in the world not diagnose in you without word of mouth?

entries.farsi: هم ز نبضت هم ز چشمت هم ز رنگ / ‏صد سقم بينند در تو بى‏درنگ
entries.english: From your pulse and your eyes and your complexion alike they immediately discern a hundred maladies in you.

entries.farsi: اين طبيبان نو آموزند خود / كه بدين آياتشان حاجت بود
entries.english: In truth, it is these newly-taught physicians that have need of these signs.

entries.farsi: كامالن از دور نامت بشنوند / تا به قعر باد و بودت در دوند
entries.english: The perfect will hear your name from afar and quickly penetrate into the deepest ground of your being and existence;

entries.farsi: بلكه پيش از زادن تو سالها / ديده باشندت ترا با حالها
entries.english: Nay, they will have seen you years before your birth — you together with all the circumstances.

sections.title_fa: مژده دادن بايزيد از زادن ابو الحسن خرقانى پيش از سالها و نشان صورت او و سيرت او يك به يك و نوشتن تاريخ نويسان آن را جهت رصد
sections.title_en: How Abu Yazíd Bistami announced the birth of Abu ’l-Hasan Kharraqání—

may God sanctify the spirit of them both years before it took place, and gave a detailed description of his outer and inner characteristics; and how the chroniclers wrote it down for the purpose of observation.

entries.farsi: آن شنيدى داستان بايزيد / كه ز حال بو الحسن پيشين چه ديد
entries.english: Have you heard the story of Bayazid— what he saw beforehand of the state of Bu ’l-Hasan?

entries.farsi: روزى آن سلطان تقوى مى‏گذشت / ‏با مريدان جانب صحرا و دشت
entries.english: One day that sultan of piety was passing with his disciples towards the open country and the plain.

entries.farsi: بوى خوش آمد مر او را ناگهان / ‏در سواد رى ز سوى خارقان
entries.english: Suddenly there came to him, in the district of Rayy, a sweet scent from the direction of Kharaqan.

entries.farsi: هم بدانجا ناله‏ى مشتاق كرد / بوى را از باد استنشاق كرد
entries.english: On the spot he uttered the lamentable cry of one who is yearning, and sniffed the scent from the breeze.

entries.farsi: بوى خوش را عاشقانه مى‏كشيد / جان او از باد باده مى‏چشيد
entries.english: He was inhaling the sweet scent lovingly: his soul was tasting wine from the breeze.

entries.farsi: كوزه‏اى كاو از يخابه پر بود / چون عرق بر ظاهرش پيدا شود
entries.english: When “sweat” appears on the outside of a pot that is full of icy water,

entries.farsi: آن ز سردى هوا آبى شده‏ست / ‏از درون كوزه نم بيرون نجست
entries.english: It has been turned into water by the coldness of the air: the moisture has not escaped from the inside of the pot.

entries.farsi: باد بوى آور مر او را آب گشت / ‏آب هم او را شراب ناب گشت
entries.english: The scent-bearing breeze became water for him; for him too the water became pure wine.

entries.farsi: پس بپرسيدش كه اين احوال خوش / ‏كه برون است از حجاب پنج و شش
entries.english: When the marks of intoxication appeared in him, a disciple questioned him concerning that breath;

entries.farsi: چون در او آثار مستى شد پديد / يك مريد او را از آن دم بر رسيد
entries.english: Then he asked him, “these sweet ecstasies which are beyond the pale of the five and the six?

entries.farsi: گاه سرخ و گاه زرد و گه سپيد / مى‏شود رويت چه حال است و نويد
entries.english: Your face is becoming now red and now yellow and now white: what is the hap and the glad tidings?

entries.farsi: مى‏كشى بوى و به ظاهر نيست گل / ‏بى‏شك از غيب است و از گلزار كل
entries.english: You are inhaling scent, and no flowers are visible: doubtless it is from the Unseen and from the garden of the Universal.

entries.farsi: اى تو كام جان هر خودكامه‏اى / ‏هر دم از غيبت پيام و نامه‏اى
entries.english: O you who are the desire of everyone who follows his own desire, you to whom there is at every moment a message and letter from the Unseen,

entries.farsi: هر دمى يعقوب‏وار از يوسفى / مى‏رسد اندر مشام تو شفا
entries.english: You to whose organ of smell there is coming at every moment, as to Jacob, balm from a Joseph,

entries.farsi: قطره‏اى بر ريز بر ما ز آن سبو / شمه‏اى ز آن گلستان با ما بگو
entries.english: Spill upon us one drop from that pitcher; give us one word that smells of that garden.

entries.farsi: خو نداريم اى جمال مهترى / ‏كه لب ما خشك و تو تنها خورى
entries.english: We are not accustomed, O beauty of majesty, that you should drink alone while our lips are dry.

entries.farsi: اى فلك پيماى چست چست خيز / ز انچه خوردى جرعه‏اى بر ما بريز
entries.english: O nimble, nimbly-rising traverser of Heaven, spill upon us one draught of that which you have drunk.

entries.farsi: كى توان نوشيد اين مى زير دست / ‏مى يقين مر مرد را رسواگر است
entries.english: There is no other Master of the Revels in the world except you: O king, look on the boon-companions!

entries.farsi: مير مجلس نيست در دوران دگر / جز تو اى شه در حريفان در نگر
entries.english: How is it possible to quaff this wine underhand? Certainly wine is the exposer of man.

entries.farsi: بوى را پوشيده و مكنون كند / چشم مست خويشتن را چون كند
entries.english: He may disguise and conceal the scent, how will he hide his intoxicated eye?

entries.farsi: خود نه آن بوى است اين كاندر جهان / ‏صد هزاران پرده‏اش دارد نهان
entries.english: In truth this is not a scent that thousands of veils will keep hidden in the world.

entries.farsi: پر شد از تيزى او صحرا و دشت / ‏دشت چه كز نه فلك هم بر گذشت
entries.english: The desert and plain are filled with its pungency. What plain? For it has passed even beyond the nine spheres.

entries.farsi: اين سر خم را به كهگل در مگير / كاين برهنه نيست خود پوشش پذير
entries.english: Do not daub the head of the jar with mortar, for indeed this naked one does not admit of covering.

entries.farsi: لطف كن اى راز دان رازگو / آن چه بازت صيد كردش باز گو
entries.english: Show kindness: O you who knows and can tell the mystery, declare that which your falcon has made its prey.”

entries.farsi: گفت بوى بو العجب آمد به من / ‏همچنان كه مر نبى را از يمن
entries.english: He said, “A marvellous scent is come to me, even as for the Prophet’s sake from Yemen;

entries.farsi: كه محمد گفت بر دست صبا / از يمن مى‏آيدم بوى خدا
entries.english: For Mohammed said, ‘the scent of God is coming to me from Yemen, on the hand of the zephyr.’”

entries.farsi: بوى رامين مى‏رسد از جان ويس / ‏بوى يزدان مى‏رسد هم از اويس
entries.english: The scent of Ramin is coming from the soul of Wis; the scent of God, too, is coming from Uways.

entries.farsi: از اويس و از قرن بوى عجب / ‏مر نبى را مست كرد و پر طرب
entries.english: From Uways and from Qaran a wondrous scent made the Prophet drunken and full of rapture.

entries.farsi: چون اويس از خويش فانى گشته بود / آن زمينى آسمانى گشته بود
entries.english: Since Uways had passed away from himself, that earthly one had become heavenly.

entries.farsi: آن هليله‏ى پروريده در شكر / چاشنى تلخيش نبود دگر
entries.english: The almond conserved in sugar— its bitter taste is not any more.

entries.farsi: آن هليله‏ى رسته از ما و منى / ‏نقش دارد از هليله طعم نى
entries.english: The almond that is freed from egoism has the appearance of almond, not the flavour.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد باز گرد / تا چه گفت از وحى غيب آن شير مرد
entries.english: This topic has no end. Return, that what that holy man said, by inspiration from the World Unseen.

sections.title_fa: ‏قول رسول صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله و سلم انى الجد نفس الرحمن من قبل اليمن
sections.title_en: The words of the Prophet, may God bless and save him, “Truly, I feel the Breath of the Merciful from the direction of Yemen.”

entries.farsi: گفت زين سو بوى يارى مى‏رسد / كاندر اين ده شهريارى مى‏رسد
entries.english: He said, “The scent of a friend is coming from this quarter, for a monarch is coming into this village.

entries.farsi: بعد چندين سال مى‏زايد شهى / ‏مى‏زند بر آسمانها خرگهى
entries.english: After such and such a number of years a king will be born: he will pitch a tent above the heavens.

entries.farsi: رويش از گلزار حق گلگون بود / از من او اندر مقام افزون بود
entries.english: His face will be coloured with roses from God’s rose garden: he will surpass me in station.”

entries.farsi: چيست نامش گفت نامش بو الحسن / ‏حليه‏اش وا گفت ز ابرو و ذقن
entries.english: “What is his name?” He replied, “His name is Bu ’l- Hasan,” and described his features—his eyebrows and chin;

entries.farsi: قد او و رنگ او و شكل او / يك به يك وا گفت از گيسو و رو
entries.english: He described his height and his complexion and his figure and spoke in detail of his locks of hair and his face.

entries.farsi: حليه‏هاى روح او را هم نمود / از صفات و از طريقه و جا و بود
entries.english: He also declared his spiritual features— his qualities and the way and his rank and estate.

entries.farsi: حليه‏ى تن همچو تن عاريتى است / ‏دل بر آن كم نه كه آن يك ساعتى است
entries.english: The bodily features, like the body, are borrowed: set not your heart on them, for they are lasting one hour.

entries.farsi: حليه‏ى روح طبيعى هم فناست / ‏حليه‏ى آن جان طلب كان بر سماست
entries.english: The features of the natural spirit also are perishable: seek the features of that spirit which is above the sky.

entries.farsi: جسم او همچون چراغى بر زمين / ‏نور او بالاى سقف هفتمين
entries.english: Its body is on the earth, like a lamp, its light is above the Seventh Roof.

entries.farsi: آن شعاع آفتاب اندر وثاق / ‏قرص او اندر چهارم چار طاق
entries.english: Those rays of the sun are in the house; their orb is in the Fourth Dome.

entries.farsi: نقش گل در زير بينى بهر الغ / ‏بوى گل بر سقف و ايوان دماغ
entries.english: The form of the rose is beneath the nose for idle pleasure’s sake, the scent of the rose is on the roof and palace of the brain.

entries.farsi: مرد خفته در عدن ديده فرق / ‏عكس آن بر جسم افتاده عرق
entries.english: A man asleep sees terror at Aden: the reflection thereof appears as sweat on his body.

entries.farsi: پيرهن در مصر رهن يك حريص / ‏پر شده كنعان ز بوى آن قميص
entries.english: The shirt was in Egypt in the keeping of one exceedingly careful: Canaan was filled with the scent of that shirt.

entries.farsi: بر نبشتند آن زمان تاريخ را / از كباب آراستند آن سيخ را
entries.english: Thereupon they wrote down the date: they adorned the spit with the meat for roasting.

entries.farsi: چون رسيد آن وقت و آن تاريخ راست / ‏زاده شد آن شاه و نرد ملك باخت
entries.english: When the right time and date arrived, that king was born and played the dice of empire.

entries.farsi: از پس آن سالها آمد پديد / بو الحسن بعد وفات بايزيد
entries.english: After those years, Bu ’l-Hasan appeared after the death of Bayazid.

entries.farsi: جمله‏ى خوهاى او ز امساك و جود / آن چنان آمد كه آن شه گفته بود
entries.english: All his dispositions, of withholding tenaciously or bestowing liberally, proved to be such as that king had foretold.

entries.farsi: لوح محفوظ است او را پيشوا / از چه محفوظ است محفوظ از خطا
entries.english: His guide is “the guarded tablet.” From what is it guarded? It is guarded from error.

entries.farsi: نه نجوم است و نه رمل است و نه خواب / ‏وحى حق و اهلل اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: The inspiration of God is not astrology or geomancy or dreams— and God best knows what is right.

entries.farsi: از پى رو پوش عامه در بيان / ‏وحى دل گويند آن را صوفيان
entries.english: The Sufis in explaining call it the inspiration of the heart, in order to disguise from the vulgar.

entries.farsi: وحى دل گيرش كه منظر گاه اوست / ‏چون خطا باشد چو دل آگاه اوست
entries.english: Take it to be the inspiration of the heart, for it is the place where He is seen: how should there be error when the heart is aware of Him?

entries.farsi: مومنا بنظر به نور اللهَّ شدى / ‏از خطا و سهو ايمن آمدى
entries.english: O true believer, you have become seeing by the light of God: you have become secure from error and inadvertence.

sections.title_fa: ‏نقصان اجراى جان و دل صوفى از طعام الل
sections.title_en: The reduction of the allowance of God’s food for the soul and heart of the Sufi

entries.farsi: صوفيى از فقر چون در غم شود / عين فقرش دايه و مطعم شود
entries.english: How should a Sufi be grieved on account of poverty? The very essence of poverty becomes his nurse and his food,

entries.farsi: ز انكه جنت از مكاره رسته است / ‏رحم قسم عاجزى اشكسته است
entries.english: Because Paradise has grown from thing disliked, and Mercy is the portion of one who is helpless and broken.

entries.farsi: آن كه سرها بشكند او از علو / رحم حق و خلق نايد سوى او
entries.english: He that haughtily breaks the heads, the mercy of God and His creatures does not come not towards him.

entries.farsi: اين سخن آخر ندارد و آن جوان / ‏از كمى اجراى نان شد ناتوان
entries.english: This topic has no end, and that youth has been deprived of strength by the reduction of his bread-allowance.

entries.farsi: شاد آن صوفى كه رزقش كم شود / آن شبه‏ش در گردد و او يم شود
entries.english: Happy is the Sufi whose daily bread is reduced: his bead becomes a pearl, and he becomes the Sea.

entries.farsi: ز آن جراى خاص هرك آگاه شد / او سزاى قرب و اجرى گاه شد
entries.english: Whosoever has become acquainted with that choice (spiritual) allowance, he has become worthy of approach and of the Source of allowance.

entries.farsi: ز آن جراى روح چون نقصان شود / جانش از نقصان آن لرزان شود
entries.english: When there is a reduction of that spiritual allowance, his spirit trembles on account of its reduction;

entries.farsi: پس بداند كه خطايى رفته است / ‏كه سمن زار رضا آشفته است
entries.english: Then he knows that a fault has been committed which has ruffled the jasmine-bed of approbation,

entries.farsi: همچنانك آن شخص از نقصان كشت / ‏رقعه سوى صاحب خرمن نبشت
entries.english: Just as that person, on account of the deficiency of his crop, wrote a letter to the owner of the harvest

entries.farsi: رقعه‏اش بردند پيش مير داد / خواند آن رقعه جوابى وا نداد
entries.english: They brought his letter to the lord of justice: he read the letter and returned no answer.

entries.farsi: گفت او را نيست الا درد لوت / ‏پس جواب احمق اوليتر سكوت
entries.english: He said, “He has no care but for delicacies: silence, then, is the best answer to a fool.

entries.farsi: نيستش درد فراق و وصل هيچ / ‏بند فرع است او نجويد اصل هيچ
entries.english: He has no care at all for separation or union: he is confined to the branch; he does not seek the root at all.

entries.farsi: آسمانها و زمين يك سيب دان / ‏كز درخت قدرت حق شد عيان
entries.english: He is a fool and dead in egoism, for because of his anxious care for the branch he has no leisure for the root.”

entries.farsi: احمق است و مرده‏ى ما و منى / ‏كز غم فرعش فراغ اصل نى
entries.english: Deem the skies and the earth to be an apple that appeared from the tree of Divine Power.

entries.farsi: آن يكى كرمى دگر در سيب هم / ‏ليك جانش از برون صاحب علم
entries.english: You are as a worm in the midst of the apple and art ignorant of the tree and the gardener.

entries.farsi: تو چو كرمى در ميان سيب در / و ز درخت و باغبانى بى‏خبر
entries.english: The other worm too is in the apple, but its spirit is outside, bearing the banner aloft.

entries.farsi: بر دريده جنبش او پرده‏ها / صورتش كرم است و معنى اژدها
entries.english: Its movement splits the apple asunder: the apple cannot endure that shock.

entries.farsi: جنبش او واشكافد سيب را / بر نتابد سيب آن آسيب را
entries.english: Its movement has rent veils: its form is a worm, but its reality is a dragon.

entries.farsi: آتشى كاول ز آهن مى‏جهد / او قدم بس سست بيرون مى‏نهد
entries.english: The fire that first darts from the steel puts forth its foot very feebly

entries.farsi: دايه‏اش پنبه‏ست اول ليك اخير / مى‏رساند شعله‏ها او تا اثير
entries.english: Cotton is its nurse at first, but in the end it carries its flames up to the ether.

entries.farsi: مرد اول بسته‏ى خواب و خور است / ‏آخر الامر از ماليك برتر است
entries.english: At first, man is in bondage to sleep and food; ultimately he is higher than the angels.

entries.farsi: در پناه پنبه و كبريتها / شعله و نورش بر آيد بر سها
entries.english: Under the protection of cotton and sulphur matches his flame and light rises above Suha

entries.farsi: عالم تاريك روشن مى‏كند / كنده‏ى آهن به سوزن مى‏كند
entries.english: He illuminates the dark world: he tears the iron fetter (in pieces) with a needle.

entries.farsi: گر چه آتش نيز هم جسمانى است / ‏نه ز روح است و نه از روحانى است
entries.english: Though the fire too is connected with the body, is it not derived from the spirit and the spiritual?

entries.farsi: جسم را نبود از آن عز بهره‏اى / ‏جسم پيش بحر جان چون قطره‏اى
entries.english: The body has no share in that glory: the body is as a drop of water in comparison with the sea of the spirit.

entries.farsi: جسم از جان روز افزون مى‏شود / چون رود جان جسم بين چون مى‏شود
entries.english: The days of the body, are increased by the spirit: mark what becomes of the body when the spirit goes.

entries.farsi: حد جسمت يك دو گز خود بيش نيست / ‏جان تو تا آسمان جوالن كنى است
entries.english: The range of your body is an ell or two, no more: your spirit is a maker of swift flights to heaven.

entries.farsi: تا به بغداد و سمرقند اى همام / ‏روح را اندر تصور نيم گام
entries.english: In the spirit’s imagination, O prince, it is half a step to Baghdad and Samarkand.

entries.farsi: دو درم سنگ است پيه چشمتان / ‏نور روحش تا عنان آسمان
entries.english: The fat of your eye is two dirham’s in weight: the light of its spirit to the lofty region of the sky.

entries.farsi: نور بى‏اين چشم مى‏بيند به خواب / ‏چشم بى‏اين نور چه بود جز خراب
entries.english: The light sees in dream without this eye: without this light what would the eye be but ruined?

entries.farsi: جان ز ريش و سبلت تن فارغ است / ليك تن بى‏جان بود مردار و پست
entries.english: The spirit is unconcerned with the heard and moustache of the body, but without the spirit the body is a carcass and vile.

entries.farsi: بار نامه‏ى روح حيوانى است اين / ‏پيشتر رو روح انسانى ببين
entries.english: Such is the magnificence of the animal spirit: advance farther, behold the human spirit.

entries.farsi: بگذر از انسان هم و از قال و قيل / ‏تا لب درياى جان جبرئيل
entries.english: Pass beyond Man and disputation unto the shore of the sea of the spirit of Gabriel.

entries.farsi: بعد از آنت جان احمد لب گزد / جبرئيل از بيم تو واپس خزد
entries.english: After that, the, spirit of Ahmad will bite your lip, and Gabriel will creep back in fear of you,

entries.farsi: گويد ار آيم به قدر يك كمان / ‏من به سوى تو بسوزم در زمان
entries.english: And will say, “If I come one bow’s length towards you, I shall be instantly consumed.”

sections.title_fa: ‏آشفتن آن غالم از نارسيدن جواب رقعه از قبل پادشاه
sections.title_en: How the slave was indignant because no reply to his letter arrived from the king.

entries.farsi: اين بيابان خود ندارد پا و سر / بى‏جواب نامه خسته‏ست آن پسر
entries.english: Truly this desert has no head or foot. That youth, without a reply to his letter, is aggrieved

entries.farsi: كاى عجب چونم نداد آن شه جواب / ‏يا خيانت كرد رقعه بر ز تاب
entries.english: And says, “Oh, it is a wonder. How did the king give me no reply? Or the carrier of the letter behaved treacherously because of the torment,

entries.farsi: رقعه پنهان كرد و ننمود آن به شاه / كاو منافق بود و آبى زير كاه
entries.english: And concealed the letter and did not show it to the king; for he was a hypocrite and a piece of water beneath straw.

entries.farsi: رقعه‏ى ديگر نويسم ز آزمون / ‏ديگرى جويم رسول ذو فنون
entries.english: I will write another letter by way of test and seek another accomplished messenger.”

entries.farsi: بر امير و مطبخى و نامه بر / عيب بنهاده ز جهل آن بى‏خبر
entries.english: That heedless man ignorantly puts the blame on the Amir and the steward and the letter-carrier.

entries.farsi: هيچ گرد خود نمى‏گردد كه من / ‏كژروى كردم چو اندر دين شمن
entries.english: Never does he go round about himself and say, “I have acted perversely, like the idolater in religion.”

sections.title_fa: كژ وزيدن باد بر سليمان عليه السلام به سبب زلت او
sections.title_en: How the wind blew perversely against Solomon, on whom be peace, because of his lapse.

entries.farsi: باد بر تخت سليمان رفت كژ / پس سليمان گفت بادا كژ مغژ
entries.english: The wind moved perversely against Solomon’s throne. Then Solomon said, “O wind, do not creep perversely.”

entries.farsi: باد هم گفت اى سليمان كژ مرو / ور روى كژ از كژم خشمين مشو
entries.english: The wind too said, “Do not move perversely, O Solomon; and if you move perversely, be not angry at my perverseness.

entries.farsi: اين ترازو بهر اين بنهاد حق / ‏تا رود انصاف ما را در سبق
entries.english: God set up these scales for the purpose that justice might be done to us in eternity.

entries.farsi: از ترازو كم كنى من كم كنم / ‏تا تو با من روشنى من روشنم
entries.english: You give short measure, I will give short measure; so long as you are honest with me, I am honest.”

entries.farsi: همچنين تاج سليمان ميل كرد / روز روشن را بر او چون ليل كرد
entries.english: Likewise, Solomon’s tiara swerved to one side and made the bright day as night to him.

entries.farsi: گفت تاجا كژ مشو بر فرق من / ‏آفتابا كم مشو از شرق من
entries.english: He said, “O tiara, do not become awry on my head: O sun, do not decline from my orient.”

entries.farsi: راست مى‏كرد او به دست آن تاج را / ‏باز كژ مى‏شد بر او تاج اى فتى
entries.english: He was putting the tiara straight with his hand, the tiara always became awry for him again, O youth.

entries.farsi: هشت بارش راست كرد و گشت كژ / گفت تاجا چيست آخر كژ مغژ
entries.english: Eight times he straightened it, and it became awry. He said, “Why, what is the matter, O tiara? Do not sag crookedly.”

entries.farsi: گفت اگر صد ره كنى تو راست من / ‏كژ روم چون كژ روى اى موتمن
entries.english: It replied, “If you put me straight a hundred times, I go awry since you go awry, O trusted one.”

entries.farsi: پس سليمان اندرونه راست كرد / دل بر آن شهوت كه بودش كرد سرد
entries.english: Then Solomon put straight his inward part: he made his heart cold to the lust which it had.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن تاجش همان دم راست شد / آن چنان كه تاج را مى‏خواست شد
entries.english: Thereupon his tiara immediately became straight and such as he wished it to be.

entries.farsi: بعد از آنش كژ همى‏كرد او به قصد / تاج وا مى‏گشت تارك جو به قصد
entries.english: Afterwards he was purposely making it awry, the tiara always returned purposely, seeking the crown of his head.

entries.farsi: هشت كرت كژ بكرد آن مهترش / ‏راست مى‏شد تاج بر فرق سرش
entries.english: Eight times did that prince make it awry, and did it become straight on the crown of his head.

entries.farsi: تاج ناطق گشت كاى شه ناز كن / ‏چون فشاندى پر ز گل پرواز كن
entries.english: The tiara began to speak, saying, “O king, display pride: since you have shaken your wings free from the clay, take flight.

entries.farsi: نيست دستورى كز اين من بگذرم / ‏پرده‏هاى غيب اين بر هم درم
entries.english: I have no permission to pass beyond this and tear to pieces the veils of the mystery of this.

entries.farsi: بر دهانم نه تو دست خود ببند / مر دهانم را ز گفت ناپسند
entries.english: Lay your hand on my mouth: shut my mouth from unacceptable speech.”

entries.farsi: پس ترا هر غم كه پيش آيد ز درد / بر كسى تهمت منه بر خويش گرد
entries.english: Do not you, then, whatever grief befalls you, resentfully accuse any one: turn upon yourself.

entries.farsi: ظن مبر بر ديگرى اى دوستكام / ‏آن مكن كه مى‏سگاليد آن غالم
entries.english: Do not think evil of another, O you who gratify the desire of your friend: do not do that which that slave was meditating—

entries.farsi: گاه جنگش با رسول و مطبخى / ‏گاه خشمش با شهنشاه سخى
entries.english: Now his quarrel with the messenger and the steward, now his anger against the generous emperor.

entries.farsi: همچو فرعونى كه موسى هشته بود / طفلكان خلق را سر مى‏ربود
entries.english: You are like Pharaoh, who had left Moses and was taking off the heads of the people’s babes:

entries.farsi: آن عدو در خانه‏ى آن كوردل / ‏او شده اطفال را گردن گسل
entries.english: The enemy was in the house of that blind-hearted man, he was cutting the necks of the children.

entries.farsi: تو هم از بيرون بدى با ديگران / ‏و اندرون خوش گشته با نفس گران
entries.english: You also are bad to others outside, while you have become complaisant to the grievous self within.

entries.farsi: خود عدويت اوست قندش مى‏دهى / ‏و ز برون تهمت به هر كس مى‏نهى
entries.english: It is your enemy indeed; you are giving it candy, while outside you are accusing every one.

entries.farsi: همچو فرعونى تو كور و كوردل / ‏با عدو خوش بى‏گناهان را مذل
entries.english: You are like Pharaoh, blind and blind-hearted: complaisant to your enemy and treating the guiltless with ignominy.

entries.farsi: عقل او بر عقل شاهان مى‏فزود / حكم حق بى‏عقل و كورش كرده بود
entries.english: How long, O Pharaoh, will you slay the innocent and pamper your noxious body?

entries.farsi: چند فرعونا كشى بى‏جرم را / مى‏نوازى مر تن پر غرم را
entries.english: His understanding was superior to that of kings: God’s ordainment had made him without understanding and blind.

entries.farsi: مهر حق بر چشم و بر گوش خرد / گر فالطون است حيوانش كند
entries.english: God’s seal upon the eye and ear of the intelligence makes him an animal, if he is a Plato.

entries.farsi: حكم حق بر لوح مى‏آيد پديد / آن چنان كه حكم غيب بايزيد
entries.english: God’s ordainment comes into view on the tablet in such wise as Bayazid’s prediction of the future event.

sections.title_fa: شنيدن شيخ ابو الحسن خرقانى خبر دادن بايزيد را از بود او و احوال او
sections.title_en: How Shaykh Abu ’l-Hasan, may God be well-pleased with him, heard Bayazid’s announcement of his coming into existence and of what should happen to him.

entries.farsi: همچنان آمد كه او فرموده بود / بو الحسن از مردمان آن را شنود
entries.english: It came to pass just as he had said. Bu ’l-Hasan heard from the people that,

entries.farsi: كه حسن باشد مريد و امتم / ‏درس گيرد هر صباح از تربتم
entries.english: “Hasan will be my disciple and my true follower, and will receive lessons from my tomb at every dawn.”

entries.farsi: گفت من هم نيز خوابش ديده‏ام / ‏و ز روان شيخ اين بشنيده‏ام
entries.english: He said, “I have also seen him in a dream and have heard this from the spirit of the Shaykh.”

entries.farsi: هر صباحى رو نهادى سوى گور / ايستادى تا ضحى اندر حضور
entries.english: Every dawn he would set his face towards the grave and stand in attention till the forenoon,

entries.farsi: يا مثال شيخ پيشش آمدى / ‏يا كه بى‏گفتى شكالش حل شدى
entries.english: And either the apparition of the Shaykh would come to him, or without anything spoken his difficulty would be solved,

entries.farsi: تا يكى روزى بيامد با سعود / گورها را برف نو پوشيده بود
entries.english: Till one day he came auspiciously: the graves were covered with new-fallen snow.

entries.farsi: توى بر تو برفها همچون علم / ‏قبه قبه ديد و شد جانش به غم
entries.english: He saw the snows, wreath on wreath like flags, mound on mound; and his soul was grieved.

entries.farsi: بانگش آمد از حظيره‏ى شيخ حى / ‏ها انا ادعوك كى تسعى الى
entries.english: From the shrine of the living Shaykh came to him a cry, “Listen, I call you that you may run to me.

entries.farsi: هين بيا اين سو بر آوازم شتاب / ‏عالم ار برف است روى از من متاب
entries.english: Hey, come quickly in this direction, towards my voice: if the world is snow, do not turn your face away from me.”

entries.farsi: حال او ز آن روز شد خوب و بديد / آن عجايب را كه اول مى‏شنيد
entries.english: From that day his state became excellent, and he saw those wondrous things which at first he was hearing.

sections.title_fa: ‏ى اول نيافت‏ى ديگر نوشنت آن غالم پيش شاه چون جواب آن رقعه‏رقعه
sections.title_en: How the slave wrote another letter to the king when he received no reply to the first letter.

entries.farsi: نامه‏ى ديگر نوشت آن بد گمان / ‏پر ز تشنيع و نفير و پر فغان
entries.english: That evil-thinking one wrote another letter, full of vituperation and clamour and loud complaint.

entries.farsi: كه يكى رقعه نبشتم پيش شه / ‏اى عجب آن جا رسيد و يافت ره
entries.english: He said, “I wrote a letter to the king; oh, I wonder if it arrived there and found its way.”

entries.farsi: آن دگر را خواند هم آن خوب خد / هم نداد او را جواب و تن بزد
entries.english: The fair-cheeked read that second one also, and as before he gave him no reply and kept silence.

entries.farsi: خشك مى‏آورد او را شهريار / او مكرر كرد رقعه پنج بار
entries.english: The king was withholding all favour from him: he repeated the letter five times.

entries.farsi: گفت حاجب آخر او بنده‏ى شماست / ‏گر جوابش بر نويسى هم رواست
entries.english: “After all,” said the chamberlain, “he is your slave: if you write a reply to him, it is fitting.

entries.farsi: از شهى تو چه كم گردد اگر / بر غالم و بنده اندازى نظر
entries.english: What diminution of your sovereignty will occur if you cast looks on your slave and servant?”

entries.farsi: گفت اين سهل است اما احمق است / ‏مرد احمق زشت و مردود حق است
entries.english: He said, “This is easy; but he is fool: a foolish man is foul and rejected of God.

entries.farsi: گر چه آمرزم گناه و زلتش / ‏هم كند بر من سرايت علتش
entries.english: Though I pardon his sin and fault, his disease will infect me also.

entries.farsi: صد كس از گرگين همه گرگين شوند / گر كم عقلى مبادا گبر را
entries.english: From an itchy person a whole hundred become itchy, especially this loathsome reprobate itch

entries.farsi: شهر شد ويرانه از بومى او / از گر آن احمقان طوفان نوح
entries.english: May the itch, lack of intelligence, not befall the infidel His ill-starredness keeps the cloud rainless.

entries.farsi: شوم او بى‏آب دارد ابر را / نم نبارد ابر از شومى او
entries.english: On account of his ill-starredness the cloud sheds no moisture: by his owlishness the city is made a desert.

entries.farsi: ‏كرد ويران عالمى را در فضوح / گفت پيغمبر كه احمق هر كه هست
entries.english: Because of the itch of those foolish ones the Flood of Noah devastated a whole world in disgrace.

entries.farsi: ‏او عدوى ماست و غول ره زن است / هر كه او عاقل بود او جان ماست
entries.english: The Prophet said, ‘Whoever is foolish, he is our enemy and a ghoul who waylays.

entries.farsi: ‏روح او و ريح او ريحان ماست / عقل دشنامم دهد من راضيم
entries.english: Whoso is intelligent, he is our soul: his breeze and wind is our sweet basil.’

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه فيضى دارد از فياضيم / نبود آن دشنام او بى‏فايده
entries.english: Intelligence revile me, I am well-pleased, because it possesses something that has emanated from my emanative activity.

entries.farsi: ‏من از آن حلواى او اندر تبم / اين يقين دان گر لطيف و روشنى
entries.english: Its revilement is not without use, its hospitality is not without a table;

entries.farsi: ‏نبود آن مهمانى‏اش بى‏مايده / احمق ار حلوا نهد اندر لبم
entries.english: If the fool put sweetmeat on my lip, I am in a fever from his sweetmeat.”

entries.farsi: ‏نيست بوسه‏ى كون خر را چاشنى / سبلتت گنده كند بى‏فايده
entries.english: If you are goodly and enlightened, know this for sure, kissing the arse of an ass has no savour.

entries.farsi: ‏جامه از ديگش سيه بى‏مايده / مايده عقل است نى نان و شوا
entries.english: He uselessly makes your moustache fetid; your dress is blackened by his kettle without a table.

entries.farsi: نور عقل است اى پسر جان را غذا / نيست غير نور آدم را خورش
entries.english: Intelligence is the table, not bread and roast-meat: the light of intelligence, O son, is the nutriment for the soul.

entries.farsi: ‏از جز آن جان نيابد پرورش / زين خورشها اندك اندك باز بر
entries.english: Man has no food but the light: the soul does not obtain nourishment from aught but that.

entries.farsi: كاين غذاى خر بود نه آن حر / تا غذاى اصل را قابل شوى
entries.english: Little by little cut off from these foods— for these are the nutriment of an ass, not that of a free man—

entries.farsi: ‏لقمه‏هاى نور را آكل شوى / عكس آن نور است كاين نان نان شده‏ست
entries.english: So that you may become capable of the original nutriment and may eat habitually the dainty morsels of the light

entries.farsi: ‏فيض آن جان است كاين جان جان شده‏ست / چون خورى يك بار از مأكول نور
entries.english: It is the reflection of that light that this bread has become bread; it is the overflowing of that soul that this soul has become soul.

entries.farsi: خاك ريزى بر سر نان و تنور / عقل دو عقل است اول مكسبى
entries.english: When you eat once of the light you will pour earth over the bread and oven

entries.farsi: كه در آموزى چو در مكتب صبى / از كتاب و اوستاد و فكر و ذكر
entries.english: Intelligence consists of two intelligences; the former is the acquired one which you learn, like a boy at school,

entries.farsi: از معانى و ز علوم خوب و بكر / عقل تو افزون شود بر ديگران
entries.english: From book and teacher and reflection and memory, and from concepts, and from excellent and virgin sciences

entries.farsi: ‏ليك تو باشى ز حفظ آن گران / لوح حافظ باشى اندر دور و گشت
entries.english: Your intelligence becomes superior to others; but through preserving that you are heavily burdened.

entries.farsi: چشمه‏ى آن در ميان جان بود / چون ز سينه آب دانش جوش كرد
entries.english: You, in wandering and going about, are a preserving tablet; the preserved tablet is he that has passed beyond this.

entries.farsi: ‏لوح محفوظ اوست كاو زين در گذشت / عقل ديگر بخشش يزدان بود
entries.english: The other intelligence is the gift of God: its fountain is in the midst of the soul.

entries.farsi: نه شود گنده نه ديرينه نه زرد / ور ره نبعش بود بسته چه غم
entries.english: When the water of knowledge gushes from the breast, it does not become fetid, old or yellow;

entries.farsi: ‏كاو همى‏جوشد ز خانه دم‏به‏دم / عقل تحصيلى مثال جويها
entries.english: And if its way of issue be stopped, what harm? For it gushes continually from the house

entries.farsi: از درون خويشتن جو چشمه را
entries.english: The acquired intelligence is like the conduits which run into a house from the streets:

entries.farsi: كان رود در خانه‏اى از كويها / راه آبش بسته شد شد بى‏نوا
entries.english: Its water-way is blocked, it is without any supply. Seek the fountain from within yourself!

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى آن كه كسى با كسى مشورت مى‏كرد گفتش مشورت با ديگرى كن كه من عدوى توام
sections.title_en: Story that someone was consulting another, who said, “Consult someone else, for I am your enemy.”

entries.farsi: مشورت مى‏كرد شخصى با كسى / ‏كز تردد وا رهد وز محبسى
entries.english: A certain person was consulting someone that he might be delivered from perplexity and from a quandary.

entries.farsi: رو كسى جو كه ترا او هست دوست / ‏دوست بهر دوست ال شك خير جوست
entries.english: “O man of good name,” he replied; “seek another, not me, and explain to him the matter for consultation.

entries.farsi: من عدويم مر ترا با من مپيچ / ‏نبود از راى عدو پيروز هيچ
entries.english: I am an enemy to you: do not attach yourself to me; one is never successful from the counsel of an enemy.

entries.farsi: گفت اى خوشنام غير من بجو / ماجراى مشورت با او بگو
entries.english: Go; seek one who is a friend to you: undoubtedly a friend seeks good for his friend.

entries.farsi: من عدويم چاره نبود كز منى / ‏كژ روم با تو نمايم دشمنى
entries.english: I am an enemy: it is inevitable that from egoism I should go crookedly and show enmity towards you.

entries.farsi: حارسى از گرگ جستن شرط نيست / جستن از غير محل ناجستنى است
entries.english: It is not a condition to demand of a wolf the task of a watchman: to demand from the wrong place is a negation of the demand.

entries.farsi: من ترا بى‏هيچ شكى دشمنم / ‏من ترا كى ره نمايم ره زنم
entries.english: Without any doubt I am an enemy to you: how should I show you the way? I am a highwayman.

entries.farsi: هر كه باشد همنشين دوستان / ‏هست در گلخن ميان بوستان
entries.english: Whoever is sitting with friends is amidst a flower-garden in a bath-furnace.

entries.farsi: هر كه با دشمن نشيند در زمن / ‏هست او در بوستان در گولخن
entries.english: Whoever in the world sits with an enemy, he is in a bath-furnace in a flower-garden.

entries.farsi: دوست را مازار از ما و منت / ‏تا نگردد دوست خصم و دشمنت
entries.english: Vex not your friend by egoism, lest your friend become your adversary and enemy.

entries.farsi: تا هماره دوست بينى در نظر / در دلت نايد ز كين ناخوش صور
entries.english: Do good to the people for your God’s sake or for the peace of your own soul,

entries.farsi: خير كن با خلق بهر ايزدت / ‏يا براى راحت جان خودت
entries.english: That you may always see friendly in your sight, and that ugly ideas arising from hatred may not come into your heart.

entries.farsi: چون كه كردى دشمنى پرهيز كن / ‏مشورت با يار مهر انگيز كن
entries.english: Since you have behaved with enmity, abstain: consult a friend who arouses affection.”

entries.farsi: گفت مى‏دانم ترا اى بو الحسن / ‏كه تويى ديرينه دشمن دار من
entries.english: He replied, “I know you, O Bu ’l-Hasan, to be one who has long deemed me an enemy;

entries.farsi: ليك مرد عاقلى و معنوى / ‏عقل تو نگذاردت كه كژ روى
entries.english: But you are a reasonable and spiritual man: your reason will not allow you to go crookedly.”

entries.farsi: طبع خواهد تا كشد از خصم كين / ‏عقل بر نفس است بند آهنين
entries.english: The nature desires to take revenge on its adversary: the reason is an iron chain upon the flesh.

entries.farsi: آيد و منعش كند واداردش / ‏عقل چون شحنه‏ست در نيك و بدش
entries.english: It comes and prevents it and restrains it: the reason is like a police-inspector for it in its good and evil.

entries.farsi: عقل ايمانى چو شحنه‏ى عادل است / ‏پاسبان و حاكم شهر دل است
entries.english: The reason that is allied to Faith is like a just police-inspector: it is the guardian and magistrate of the city of the heart.

entries.farsi: همچو گربه باشد او بيدار هوش / ‏دزد در سوراخ ماند همچو موش
entries.english: It is mentally alert like a cat: the thief remains in the hole, like a mouse.

entries.farsi: در هر آن جا كه بر آرد موش دست / ‏نيست گربه يا كه نقش گربه است
entries.english: Wherever the mouse gets the upper hand, no cat is there, or there is the form of a cat.

entries.farsi: گربه‏ى چه شير شير افكن بود / عقل ايمانى كه اندر تن بود
entries.english: What cat? The Faith-regarding reason which is in the body is the lion that overthrows the lions.

entries.farsi: غره‏ى او حاكم درندگان / ‏نعره‏ى او مانع چرندگان
entries.english: Its roar is the magistrate of the tearing animals; its shout is the preventer of the browsing animals.

entries.farsi: شهر پر دزد است و پر جامه كنى / ‏خواه شحنه باش گو و خواه نى
entries.english: The city is full of thieves and clothes-robbers, let there be a police-inspector if you will, or let there be none.

sections.title_fa: امير كردن رسول عليه الصالة و السلام جوان هذيلى را بر سريه اى كه در آن پيران و جنگ آزمودگان بودند
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, appointed a youth of Hudhayl to be commander of an expeditionary force in which there were elders and veteran warriors.

entries.farsi: يك جوانى را گزيد او از هذيل / ‏مير لشكر كردش و سالار خيل
entries.english: The Prophet was sending a force to fight against the unbelievers and repel insolence.

entries.farsi: يك سريه مى‏فرستادى رسول / ‏بهر جنگ كافر و دفع فضول
entries.english: He chose a youth of Hudhayl and appointed him commander of the army and leader of the cavalry.

entries.farsi: اصل لشكر بى‏گمان سرور بود / قوم بى‏سرور تن بى‏سر بود
entries.english: The foundation of an army is unquestionably the chief in command: a people without a chief are a body without a head.

entries.farsi: اين همه كه مرده و پژمرده‏اى / ز آن بود كه ترك سرور كرده‏اى
entries.english: That you are dead and decrepit— all this is because you have abandoned the chief.

entries.farsi: از كسل و ز بخل و ز ما و منى / ‏مى‏كشى سر خويش را سر مى‏كنى
entries.english: On account of laziness and avarice and egoism you are drawing your head back and making yourself the head.

entries.farsi: صاحبش در پى دوان كاى خيره‏سر / هر طرف گرگى است اندر قصد خر
entries.english: Like the beast that flees from the burden: it takes its head into the mountains.

entries.farsi: همچو استورى كه بگريزد ز بار / او سر خود گيرد اندر كوهسار
entries.english: Its master is running after it, crying, “O giddy-headed one, on every side there is a wolf in quest of an ass.

entries.farsi: گر ز چشمم اين زمان غايب شوى / ‏پيشت آيد هر طرف گرگ قوى
entries.english: If you disappear now from mine eye, the mighty wolf will approach you from every direction.

entries.farsi: آن مگير آخر بمانى از علف / ‏آتش از بى‏هيزمى گردد تلف
entries.english: He will chew your bones like sugar, so that you will never see life again.

entries.farsi: استخوانت را بخايد چون شكر / كه نبينى زندگانى را دگر
entries.english: Do not suppose that; at any rate you will be left without fodder: fire is extinguished by lack of faggots.

entries.farsi: هين بمگريز از تصرف كردنم / ‏و ز گرانى بار كه جانت منم
entries.english: Beware! Do not flee from my control and from the heaviness of the burden, for I am your soul.”

entries.farsi: تو ستورى هم كه نفست غالب است / ‏حكم غالب را بود اى خود پرست
entries.english: You also are a beast, for your carnal soul predominates: the predominant determines, O worshipper of self.
