entries.farsi: خر نخواندت اسب خواندت ذو الجلال / ‏اسب تازى را عرب گويد تعال
entries.english: The Almighty did not call you an ass, He called you a horse: the Arabs say to the Arab horse “ta‘ál” (“come”).

entries.farsi: قُلْ تَعالَوْا گفت از جذب كرم / تا رياضتتان دهم من رايضم
entries.english: Mustafa was God’s stable-overseer for the beasts, the iniquitous carnal soul.

entries.farsi: مير آخور بود حق را مصطفى / بهر استوران نفس پر جفا
entries.english: Moved by lovingkindness, He said, “Say, ‘ta‘álaw (come), to the end that I may train you: I am the trainer.’”

entries.farsi: نفسها را تا مروض كرده‏ام / ‏زين ستوران بس لگدها خورده‏ام
entries.english: “Since I have trained the carnal souls, I have suffered many kicks from these beasts.

entries.farsi: هر كجا باشد رياضت باره‏اى / ‏از لگدهايش نباشد چاره‏اى
entries.english: Wherever there is one fond of training, he has no means of avoiding kicks.

entries.farsi: الجرم اغلب بال بر انبياست / ‏كه رياضت دادن خامان بالست
entries.english: Of necessity the most affliction falls on the prophets, for it is an affliction to give training to the raw.

entries.farsi: سكسكانيد از دمم يرغا رويد / تا يواش و مركب سلطان شويد
entries.english: You are stumbling along: at my word go at a jog-trot that you may become gentle and be suitable for the king to ride.

entries.farsi: قُلْ تَعالَوْا قُلْ تَعالَوْا گفت رب / ‏اى ستوران رميده از ادب
entries.english: The Lord said, ‘Say, Come, say, Come, O beasts that have shied away from discipline.

entries.farsi: گر نيايند اى نبى غمگين مشو / ز آن دو بى‏تمكين تو پر از كين مشو
entries.english: If they come not, O Prophet, be not grieved: be not full of hatred on account of those two without steadfastness.’”

entries.farsi: گوش بعضى زين تعالواها كر است / ‏هر ستورى را صطبلى ديگر است
entries.english: The ears of some are deaf to these ta‘álaw: every beast has a different stable.

entries.farsi: منهزم گردند بعضى زين ندا / هست هر اسبى طويله‏ى او جدا
entries.english: Some are put to flight by this call: the stall of every horse is separate.

entries.farsi: منقبض گردند بعضى زين قصص / ‏ز انكه هر مرغى جدا دارد قفص
entries.english: Some are chagrined by this story, for every bird has a separate cage.

entries.farsi: خود ماليك نيز ناهمتا بدند / زين سبب بر آسمان صف صف شدند
entries.english: Even the angels too were not peers: for this reason they formed diverse ranks in Heaven.

entries.farsi: كودكان گر چه به يك مكتب درند / در سبق هر يك ز يك بالاترند
entries.english: Children, though they are at one school, surpass each other in lessons.

entries.farsi: مشرقى و مغربى را حسهاست / ‏منصب ديدار حس چشم راست
entries.english: Sense-perceptions are possessed by Easterner and Westerner, the function of sight belongs to the ocular sense.

entries.farsi: باز صف گوشها را منصبى / ‏در سماع جان و اخبار و نبى
entries.english: Though a hundred thousand ears are arrayed in rank, they all are in need of the clear eye.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران گوشها گر صف زنند / جمله محتاجان چشم روشنند
entries.english: Again, the ranks of ears have a function in respect of hearing the Spirit and the Traditions and the Revelation.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران چشم را آن راه نيست / ‏هيچ چشمى از سماع آگاه نيست
entries.english: A hundred thousand eyes do not possess that avenue: no eye is acquainted with hearing.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر حس يك يك مى‏شمر / هر يكى معزول از آن كار دگر
entries.english: Similarly, enumerate each sense, one by one: each one is removed from the work of another.

entries.farsi: پنج حس ظاهر و پنج اندرون / ‏ده صف‏اند اندر قيام الصافون
entries.english: The five external and the five internal senses are in ten ranks in the standing posture of the ranked.

entries.farsi: هر كسى كاو از صف دين سركش است / مى‏رود سوى صفى كان ناخوش است
entries.english: Anyone who draws back from the rank of religion will go into the rank that is behind.

entries.farsi: تو ز گفتار تَعالَوْا كم مكن / ‏كيمياى بس شگرف است اين سخن
entries.english: Do not make little of the Word ta‘álaw (come): this Word is an exceedingly great elixir.

entries.farsi: گر مسى گردد ز گفتارت نفير / كيميا را هيچ از وى وامگير
entries.english: If a copper turn away in repugnance from your saying, by no means withhold the elixir from him.

entries.farsi: اين زمان گر بست نفس ساحرش / ‏گفت تو سودش كند در آخرش
entries.english: If his magician-like soul has bound him at the present time, your saying will profit him in his latter end.

entries.farsi: خواجه باز آ از منى و از سرى / ‏سرورى جو كم طلب كن سرورى
entries.english: O slave, say, Come, say, Come: take heed, for verily God invites unto the Peace.

entries.farsi: قُلْ تَعالَوْا قُلْ تَعالَوْا اى غالم / ‏هين كه ان اللهَّ يدعو للسالم
entries.english: Then come back, sire, from egoism and headship: seek a leader, do not desire leadership.

sections.title_fa: ‏اعتراض كردن معترضى بر رسول عليه الصالة و السلام بر امير كردن آن هذيلى
sections.title_en: How an objector objected to the Prophet’s—on whom be peace— appointing the man of Hudhayl to be commander.

entries.farsi: چون پيمبر سرورى كرد از هذيل / ‏از براى لشكر منصور خيل
entries.english: When the Prophet appointed a leader from Hudhayl for the army whose troops were divinely aided,

entries.farsi: بو الفضولى از حسد طاقت نداشت / ‏اعتراض و ال نسلم بر فراشت
entries.english: An insolent fellow, through envy, could not endure: he raised objection and opposition.

entries.farsi: خلق را بنگر كه چون ظلمانى‏اند / در متاع فانيى چون فانى‏اند
entries.english: Behold humankind, how dark they are, and how they are perishing in a perishable piece of goods.

entries.farsi: از تكبر جمله اندر تفرقه / ‏مرده از جان زنده اندر مخرقه
entries.english: On account of pride they all are in separation, dead to the spirit, living in deception.

entries.farsi: اين عجب كه جان به زندان اندر است / ‏و آنگهى مفتاح زندانش به دست
entries.english: It is wonderful that the spirit is in prison, and then the key of the prison is in its hand!

entries.farsi: پاى تا سر غرق سرگين آن جوان / مى‏زند بر دامنش جوى روان
entries.english: That youth is plunged in dung from head to foot; the flowing river is touching his skirt!

entries.farsi: دايما پهلو به پهلو بى‏قرار / پهلوى آرامگاه و پشت دار
entries.english: Always moving restlessly from side to side beside the place of repose and the couch where he might recline!

entries.farsi: نور پنهان است و جستجو گواه / ‏كز گزافه دل نمى‏جويد پناه
entries.english: The light is hidden, and search is the evidence, for the heart does not seek shelter in vain.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى حبس دنيا را مناص / ‏نه بدى وحشت نه دل جستى خالص
entries.english: If this world’s prison had no refuge, neither would there be any feeling of aversion nor would the heart seek release.

entries.farsi: وحشتت همچون موكل مى‏كشد / كه بجو اى ضال منهاج رشد
entries.english: Your aversion is dragging you along, like a custodian, saying, “O man astray, seek the path of righteousness.”

entries.farsi: هست منهاج و نهان در مكمن است / ‏يافتش رهن گزافه جستن است
entries.english: The path is there, but it is hidden in a secret place: its discovery is in pawn to seeking in vain.

entries.farsi: تفرقه جويان جمع اندر كمين / ‏تو در اين طالب رخ مطلوب بين
entries.english: Separation is secretly in quest of union: in this seeker do you discern the face of the sought.

entries.farsi: مردگان باغ برجسته ز بن / ‏كان دهنده‏ى زندگى را فهم كن
entries.english: The dead of the orchard spring up from the root, saying, “Perceive the Giver of life!”

entries.farsi: چشم اين زندانيان هر دم به در / كى بدى گر نيستى كس مژده‏ور
entries.english: How should the eyes of these prisoners be always on beyond, if there were no bringer of the good news?

entries.farsi: بر زمين پهلوت را آرام نيست / ز ان كه در خانه لحاف و بسترى است
entries.english: How should there be a hundred thousand befouled ones seeking water, if there were no water in the river?

entries.farsi: صد هزار آلودگان آب جو / كى بدندى گر نبودى آب جو
entries.english: There is no rest for your side upon the earth, know that there is a coverlet and mattress at home.

entries.farsi: بى‏مقر گاهى نباشد بى‏قرار / بى‏خمار اشكن نباشد اين خمار
entries.english: Without a resting-place there would not be the restless; without that which takes away the headache of intoxication, there would not be this headache.

entries.farsi: گفت نه نه يا رسول اللهَّ مكن / ‏سرور لشكر مگر شيخ كهن
entries.english: He said, “Nay, nay, O Messenger of God, do not appoint any but an old Shaykh to be chief of the army.

entries.farsi: يا رسول اللهَّ جوان ار شير زاد / غير مرد پير سر لشكر مباد
entries.english: O Messenger of God, if the youth is lion-born, may none but an old man be head of the army!

entries.farsi: هم تو گفتستى و گفت تو گوا / پير بايد پير بايد پيشوا
entries.english: You too have said, and your word is witness, ‘The leader must be old, must be old.’

entries.farsi: يا رسول اللهَّ در اين لشكر نگر / هست چندين پير و از وى پيشتر
entries.english: O Messenger of God, look on this army, there are so many elders and superior to him.”

entries.farsi: زين درخت آن برگ زردش را مبين / ‏سيبهاى پخته‏ى او را بچين
entries.english: Do not regard the yellow leaves of this tree, pick its ripe apples.

entries.farsi: برگهاى زرد او خود كى تهى است / ‏اين نشان پختگى و كاملى است
entries.english: How, in truth, are its yellow leaves colourless? This is the sign of maturity and perfection.

entries.farsi: برگ زرد ريش و آن موى سپيد / بهر عقل پخته مى‏آرد نويد
entries.english: The yellow leaf of the beard and his white hair bring tidings of joy on account of his mature intelligence.

entries.farsi: برگ بى‏برگى نشان عارفى است / ‏زردى زر سرخ رويى صارفى است
entries.english: The newly-arrived green-coloured leaves signify that this fruit is unripe.

entries.farsi: برگهاى نو رسيده سبزفام / ‏شد نشان آن كه آن ميوه ست خام
entries.english: The provision of leaflessness is the sign of being a gnostic; the yellowness of gold is the money-changer’s ruddy face.

entries.farsi: آن كه او گل عارض است ار نو خط است / ‏او به مكتب گاه مخبر نو خط است
entries.english: If he that is rosy-cheeked has fresh down, he has just begun to learn writing in the school of knowledge.

entries.farsi: حرفهاى خط او كژمژ بود / مزمن عقل است اگر تن مى‏دود
entries.english: The letters of his handwriting are very crooked: he is a cripple in respect of intelligence, though his body moves with agility.

entries.farsi: پاى پير از سرعت ار چه باز ماند / يافت عقل او دو پر بر اوج راند
entries.english: Although an old man’s feet are deprived of rapid movement, his intelligence has gotten two wings and has sped to the zenith.

entries.farsi: گر مثل خواهى به جعفر در نگر / داد حق بر جاى دست و پاش پر
entries.english: If you wish for an example, look at Jafar: God gave him wings instead of hands and feet.

entries.farsi: بگذر از زر كاين سخن شد محتجب / ‏همچو سيماب اين دلم شد مضطرب
entries.english: Cease speaking of gold pallor, for this topic is recondite: this heart of mine has become agitated like quicksilver.

entries.farsi: ز اندرونم صد خموش خوش نفس / ‏دست بر لب مى‏زند يعنى كه بس
entries.english: From within me a hundred sweet-breathing silent ones put their hands on their lips, signifying, “It is enough.”

entries.farsi: خامشى بحر است و گفتن همچو جو / بحر مى‏جويد ترا جو را مجو
entries.english: Silence is the sea, and speech is like the river. The sea is seeking you: do not seek the river.

entries.farsi: از اشارتهاى دريا سر متاب / ‏ختم كن و اهلل اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: Do not turn your head away from the indications given by the sea: conclude—and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: همچنين پيوسته كرد آن بى‏ادب / ‏پيش پيغمبر سخن ز آن سرد لب
entries.english: That irreverent made no pause in the words in this fashion from those cold lips in the presence of the Prophet.

entries.farsi: دست مى‏دادش سخن او بى‏خبر / كه خبر هرزه بود پيش نظر
entries.english: Words were assisting him; he was ignorant that hearsay is mere babble in the presence of sight.

entries.farsi: اين خبرها از نظر خود نايب است / ‏بهر حاضر نيست بهر غايب است
entries.english: Indeed, these matters of hearsay are a substitute for sight: they are not for him who is present, for him who is absent.

entries.farsi: هر كه او اندر نظر موصول شد / اين خبرها پيش او معزول شد
entries.english: Whoever has been caused to attain unto sight, before him these matters of hearsay are idle.

entries.farsi: چون كه با معشوق گشتى همنشين / ‏دفع كن داللگان را بعد از اين
entries.english: When you have sat down beside your beloved, after this banish the dallala (the old women who act as go-betweens)

entries.farsi: هر كه از طفلى گذشت و مرد شد / نامه و دالله بر وى سرد شد
entries.english: When any one has passed beyond childhood and has become a man, the letter and the dallala become irksome to him.

entries.farsi: نامه خواند از پى تعليم را / حرف گويد از پى تفهيم را
entries.english: He reads letters, for the purpose of teaching; he utters words, for the purpose of making understand.

entries.farsi: پيش بينايان خبر گفتن خطاست / ‏كان دليل غفلت و نقصان ماست
entries.english: It is wrong to speak by hearsay in the presence of those who see, for it is a proof of our heedlessness and deficiency.

entries.farsi: پيش بينا شد خموشى نفع تو / بهر اين آمد خطاب أنصتوا
entries.english: In the presence of the seer silence is to your advantage: on this account came the allocution, be silent.

entries.farsi: ور بفرمايد كه اندر كش دراز / همچنين شرمين بگو با امر ساز
entries.english: If he bids you speak, speak gladly, but say little and do not draw out to length;

entries.farsi: گر بفرمايد بگو بر گوى خوش / ‏ليك اندر گو دراز اندر مكش
entries.english: And if he bid you draw them out to length, speak with the same modesty and comply with his command,

entries.farsi: همچنين كه من در اين زيبا فسون / ‏با ضياء الحق حسام الدين كنون
entries.english: Even as I now, in this goodly enchantment, with Ziya ’l-Haqq Husamu’din.

entries.farsi: اى حسام الدين ضياى ذو الجلال / ‏چون كه مى‏بينى چه مى‏جويى مقال
entries.english: When I am cutting short concerning righteousness, he draws me on to speak by a hundred kinds.

entries.farsi: چون كه كوته مى‏كنم من از رشد / او به صد نوعم به گفتن مى‏كشد
entries.english: O Husamu’ddin, Radiance of the Almighty, inasmuch as you see, why do you seek speech?

entries.farsi: اين مگر باشد ز حب مشتهى / اسقنى خمرا و قل لى انها
entries.english: Perchance this demand may arise from love for the Desired One, “Give me wine to drink and tell me that it is.”

entries.farsi: بر دهان تست اين دم جام او / گوش مى‏گويد كه قسم گوش كو
entries.english: At this moment His cup is at your mouth, ear says, “Where is the ear’s portion?”

entries.farsi: قسم تو گرمى است نك گرمى و مست / ‏گفت حرص من از اين افزون‏تر است
entries.english: Your portion is the heat: lo, you are heated and intoxicated. It replied, “My greed is greater than this.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام اعتراض كننده را
sections.title_en: How Mustafa, on whom be peace, answered the objector.

entries.farsi: در حضور مصطفاى قند خو / چون ز حد برد آن عرب از گفت‏وگو
entries.english: When that Arab carried disputation beyond bounds in the presence of sweet-tempered Mustafa,

entries.farsi: آن شه وَ النَّجْ مِ و سلطان عبس / ‏لب گزيد آن سرد دم را گفت بس
entries.english: That king of Wa ’l-Najm and that sultan of Abas bit his lip and said to the silly prater, “Enough!”

entries.farsi: دست مى‏زد بهر منعش بر دهان / ‏چند گويى پيش داناى نهان
entries.english: He was putting his hand on his mouth to prevent him, “How long will you speak in the presence of one who knows the occult?”

entries.farsi: پيش بينا برده‏اى سرگين خشك / ‏كه بخر اين را به جاى ناف مشك
entries.english: You have brought dry ordure to one endowed with vision, saying, “Buy this instead of a musk-bag.”

entries.farsi: بعر را اى گنده مغز گنده مخ / ‏زير بينى بنهى و گويى كه اخ
entries.english: O you of stinking brain and stinking marrow, you place camel’s dung beneath your nose and say, “Oh, delicious!”

entries.farsi: اخ اخى برداشتى اى گيج گاج / ‏تا كه كالاى بدت يابد رواج
entries.english: O squinting crazy fool, you have exclaimed in delight, “Oh, oh,” that your bad wares may find a ready sale,

entries.farsi: تا فريبى آن مشام پاك را / آن چريده‏ى گلشن افلاک را
entries.english: And that you may deceive that pure organ of smell, that which pastures in the celestial rose-garden.

entries.farsi: حلم او خود را اگر چه گول ساخت / ‏خويشتن را اندكى بايد شناخت
entries.english: Though his forbearance has feigned to be stupid, one must know one’s self a little.

entries.farsi: ديگ را گر باز ماند امشب دهن / ‏گربه را هم شرم بايد داشتن
entries.english: If tonight the mouth of the cooking-pot is left open, yet the cat must have discretion.

entries.farsi: خويشتن گر خفته كرد آن خوب فر / سخت بيدار است دستارش مبر
entries.english: If that glorious one has feigned to be asleep, he is very much awake: do not carry off his turban.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران حلم دارند اين گروه / ‏هر يكى حلمى از آنها صد چو كوه
entries.english: How long, O contumacious man devoid of (spiritual) excellence, will you utter these Devil’s enchantments in the presence of God’s elect one?

entries.farsi: چند گويى اى لجوج بى‏صفا / ‏اين فسون ديو پيش مصطفى
entries.english: This company has a hundred thousand forbearances, every one of which is as a hundred mountains.

entries.farsi: حلمشان بيدار را ابله كند / زيرك صد چشم را گمره كند
entries.english: Their forbearance makes a fool of the wary and causes the keen-witted man with a hundred eyes to lose his way.

entries.farsi: حلمشان همچون شراب خوب نغز / نغز نغزك بر رود بالاى مغز
entries.english: Their forbearance, like fine choice wine, mounts by nice degrees up to the brain.

entries.farsi: مست را بين ز آن شراب پر شگفت / ‏همچو فرزين مست كژ رفتن گرفت
entries.english: Behold the man drunken with that marvellous wine: the drunken man has begun to move crookedly like the queen.

entries.farsi: مرد برنا ز آن شراب زود گير / در ميان راه مى‏افتد چو پير
entries.english: From that quickly-catching wine the youth is falling in the middle of the road, like an aged man.

entries.farsi: خاصه اين باده كه از خم بلى است / نه ميى كه مستى او يك شبى است
entries.english: Especially this wine which is from the jar of Bala— not the wine whereof the intoxication lasts one night;

entries.farsi: آنك آن اصحاب كهف از نقل و نقل / ‏سيصد و نه سال گم كردند عقل
entries.english: That from which, at dessert and in migration, the Men of the Cave lost their reason for three hundred and nine years.

entries.farsi: ز آن زنان مصر جامى خورده‏اند / دستها را شرحه شرحه كرده‏اند
entries.english: The women of Egypt drank one cup of that and cut their hands to pieces.

entries.farsi: جعفر طيار ز آن مى بود مست / ‏ز آن گرو مى‏كرد بى‏خود پا و دست
entries.english: The magicians too had the intoxication of Moses: they deemed the gallows to be their beloved.

entries.farsi: ساحران هم سكر موسى داشتند / دار را دل دار مى‏انگاشتند
entries.english: Jafar-i Tayyar was drunk with that wine: therefore, being beside himself, he was pawning his feet and hands.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى سبحانى ما اعظم شانى گفتن بايزيد و اعتراض مريدان و جواب او مر ايشان را نه بطريق ‏گفت زبان بلكه از راه عيان
sections.title_en: Story of Bayazid’s—may God sanctify his spirit—saying, “Glory to me! How grand is my estate!”
and the objection raised by his disciples, and how he gave them an answer to this, not by the way of speech but by the way of vision.

entries.farsi: با مريدان آن فقير محتشم / ‏بايزيد آمد كه نك يزدان منم
entries.english: That venerable dervish, Bayazid, came to his disciples, saying, “Lo, I am God.”

entries.farsi: گفت مستانه عيان آن ذو فنون / ‏ال اله الا انا ها فاعبدون
entries.english: That master of the sciences said plainly in drunken fashion, “Listen, there is no god but I, so worship me.”

entries.farsi: چون گذشت آن حال گفتندش صباح / ‏تو چنين گفتى و اين نبود صالح
entries.english: When that ecstasy had passed, they said to him at dawn, “You said such and such, and this is impiety.”

entries.farsi: گفت اين بار ار كنم من مشغله / ‏كاردها بر من زنيد آن دم هله
entries.english: He said, “This time, if I make a scandal, come on at once and dash knives into me.

entries.farsi: حق منزه از تن و من با تنم / ‏چون چنين گويم ببايد كشتنم
entries.english: God transcends the body, and I am with the body: you must kill me when I say a thing like this.”

entries.farsi: چون وصيت كرد آن آزاد مرد / هر مريدى كاردى آماده كرد
entries.english: When that freeman gave the injunction, each disciple made ready a knife.

entries.farsi: مست گشت او باز از آن سغراق زفت / نقل آمد عقل او آواره شد
entries.english: Again he became intoxicated by that potent flagon: those injunctions vanished from his mind.

entries.farsi: صبح آمد شمع او بى‏چاره شد / عقل چون شحنه‏ست چون سلطان رسيد
entries.english: The Dessert came: his reason became distraught. The Dawn came: his candle became helpless.

entries.farsi: شحنه‏ى بى‏چاره در كنجى خزيد / عقل سايه‏ى حق بود حق آفتاب
entries.english: Reason is like the prefect: when the sultan arrives, the helpless prefect creeps into a corner.

entries.farsi: ‏سايه را با آفتاب او چه تاب / چون پرى غالب شود بر آدمى
entries.english: Reason is the shadow of God: God is the sun: what power has the shadow to resist His sun?

entries.farsi: ‏گم شود از مرد وصف مردمى / هر چه گويد آن پرى گفته بود
entries.english: When a genie prevails over a man, the attributes of humanity disappear from the man.

entries.farsi: زين سرى ز آن آن سرى گفته بود / چون پرى را اين دم و قانون بود
entries.english: Whatsoever he says, that genie will have said it: the one who belongs to this side will have spoken from the one who belongs to yonder side.

entries.farsi: كردگار آن پرى خود چون بود / اوى او رفته پرى خود او شده
entries.english: Since a genie has this influence and rule, how indeed must be the Creator of that genie!

entries.farsi: ‏ترك بى‏الهام تازى گو شده / چون بخود آيد نداند يك لغت
entries.english: His “he” is gone: he has in truth become the genie: the Turk, without Divine inspiration, has become a speaker of Arabic.

entries.farsi: ‏از پرى كى باشدش آخر كمى / شير گير ار خون نره شير خورد
entries.english: When he comes to himself, he does not know a word. Inasmuch as a genie has this essence and quality,

entries.farsi: ‏چون پرى را هست اين ذات و صفت / پس خداوند پرى و آدمى
entries.english: Then how, pray, should the Lord of genie and man have inferiority to the genie?

entries.farsi: تو بگويى او نكرد آن باده كرد / ور سخن پردازد از زر كهن
entries.english: If a pot-valiant fellow has drunk the blood of a fierce lion, you will say that the wine did it, not he;

entries.farsi: ‏تو بگويى باده گفته است آن سخن / باده‏اى را مى بود اين شر و شور
entries.english: And if he fashion words of old gold, you will say that the wine has spoken those words.

entries.farsi: نور حق را نيست آن فرهنگ و زور / كه ترا از تو بكل خالى كند
entries.english: A wine has this disturbance and commotion: has not the Light of God that virtue and potency

entries.farsi: تو شوى پست او سخن عالى كند / گر چه قرآن از لب پيغمبر است
entries.english: To make you entirely empty of self, you should be laid low and He should make the Word lofty?

entries.farsi: ‏هر كه گويد حق نگفت او كافر است / چون هماى بى‏خودى پرواز كرد
entries.english: Though the Qur’an is from the lips of the Prophet — if any one says God did not speak it, he is an infidel.

entries.farsi: آن سخن را بايزيد آغاز كرد / عقل را سيل تحير در ربود
entries.english: When the huma of selflessness took wing, Bayazid began those words.

entries.farsi: ز آن قوى‏تر گفت كاول گفته بود / نيست اندر جبه‏ام الا خدا
entries.english: The flood of bewilderment swept away his reason: he spoke more strongly than he had spoken at first,

entries.farsi: چند جويى بر زمين و بر سما / آن مريدان جمله ديوانه شدند
entries.english: “Within my mantle there is naught but God: how long will you seek on the earth and in heaven?”

entries.farsi: كاردها در جسم پاكش مى‏زدند / هر يكى چون ملحدان گرد كوه
entries.english: All the disciples became frenzied and dashed their knives at his holy body.

entries.farsi: ‏كارد مى‏زد پير خود را بى‏ستوه / هر كه اندر شيخ تيغى مى‏خليد
entries.english: Like the heretics of Girdakuh, everyone was ruthlessly stabbing his spiritual Director.

entries.farsi: باژگونه از تن خود مى‏دريد / يك اثر نه بر تن آن ذو فنون
entries.english: Everyone who plunged a dagger into the Shaykh was reversely making a gash in his own body.

entries.farsi: ‏و آن مريدان خسته و غرقاب خون / هر كه او سوى گلويش زخم برد
entries.english: There was no mark on the body of that possessor of the sciences, while those disciples were wounded and drowned in blood.

entries.farsi: حلق خود ببريده ديد و زار مرد / و انكه او را زخم اندر سينه زد
entries.english: Whoever aimed a blow at his throat saw his own throat cut, and died miserably;

entries.farsi: سينه‏اش بشكافت و شد مرده‏ى ابد / و آن كه آگه بود از آن صاحب قران
entries.english: And whoever inflicted a blow on his breast, his breast was split, and he became dead for ever;

entries.farsi: ‏دل ندادش كه زند زخم گران / نيم دانش دست او را بسته كرد
entries.english: And he that was acquainted with that emperor of high fortune, his heart did not consent to strike a heavy blow,

entries.farsi: جان ببرد الا كه خود را خسته كرد / روز گشت و آن مريدان كاسته
entries.english: Half-knowledge tied his hand; he saved his life and only wounded himself.

entries.farsi: ‏نوحه‏ها از خانه‏شان برخاسته / پيش او آمد هزاران مرد و زن
entries.english: Day broke, and the disciples were thinned: wails of lamentation arose from their house.

entries.farsi: ‏كاى دو عالم درج در يك پيرهن / اين تن تو گر تن مردم بدى
entries.english: Thousands of men and women came to him, saying, “O you in whose single shirt the two worlds are contained,

entries.farsi: با خود اندر ديده‏ى خود خار زد / اى زده بر بى‏خودان تو ذو الفقار
entries.english: If this body of yours were a human body, it would have been destroyed, like a human body, by the daggers.”

entries.farsi: ‏چون تن مردم ز خنجر گم شدى / با خودى با بى‏خودى دوچار زد
entries.english: A self-existent one encountered a selfless one in combat: the self-existent one drove a thorn into his own eye.

entries.farsi: بر تن خود مى‏زنى آن هوش دار / ز انكه بى‏خود فانى است و ايمن است
entries.english: O you who stab the selfless ones with the sword, you are stabbing your own body with it. Beware!

entries.farsi: ‏تا ابد در ايمنى او ساكن است / نقش او فانى و او شد آينه
entries.english: For the selfless one has passed away and is safe: he is dwelling in safety for ever.

entries.farsi: ‏غير نقش روى غير آن جاى نه / گر كنى تف سوى روى خود كنى
entries.english: His form has passed away and he has become a mirror: naught is there but the form of the face of another.

entries.farsi: ‏ور زنى بر آينه بر خود زنى / ور ببينى روى زشت آن هم تويى
entries.english: If you spit, you spit at your own face; and if you strike at the mirror, you strike at yourself;

entries.farsi: ‏ور ببينى عيسى و مريم تويى / او نه اين است و نه آن او ساده است
entries.english: And if you see an ugly face, it is you; and if you see Jesus and Mary, it is you.

entries.farsi: ‏نقش تو در پيش تو بنهاده است / چون رسيد اينجا سخن لب در ببست
entries.english: He is neither this nor that: he is simple: he has placed your image before you.

entries.farsi: ‏چون رسيد اينجا قلم در هم شكست / لب ببند ار چه فصاحت دست داد
entries.english: When the discourse reached this point, it closed its lips; when the pen reached this point, it broke to pieces.

entries.farsi: دم مزن و اهلل اعلم بالرشاد / بر كنار بامى اى مست مدام
entries.english: Close your lips: though eloquence is at your command, do not breathe a word—and God best knows the right way.

entries.farsi: ‏پست بنشين يا فرود آ و السلام / هر زمانى كه شدى تو كامران
entries.english: O you who are drunk with the wine, you are on the edge of the roof: sit down or descend, and peace be with you!

entries.farsi: ‏آن دم خوش را كنار بام دان / بر زمان خوش هراسان باش تو
entries.english: Every moment when you enjoy, deem that delightful moment to be the edge of the roof.

entries.farsi: ترس ترسان رو در آن مكمن هال / ترس جان در وقت شادى از زوال
entries.english: Be trembling for the delightful moment: conceal it like a treasure, do not divulge it.

entries.farsi: همچو گنجش خفيه كن نه فاش تو / تا نيايد بر وال ناگه بال
entries.english: Lest calamity suddenly befall plighted love, take heed, go very fearfully into that place of ambush.

entries.farsi: روح مى‏بيند كه هستش اهتزاز / هر نكالى ناگهان كان آمده ست
entries.english: The spirit’s fear of loss at the moment of enjoyment is departure from the hidden roof-edge.

entries.farsi: ‏ز آن كنار بام غيب است ارتحال / گر نمى‏بينى كنار بام راز
entries.english: If you do not see the mysterious roof-edge, the spirit is seeing, for it is shuddering.

entries.farsi: اعتبار از قوم نوح و قوم لوط
entries.english: Every sudden chastisement that has come to pass has taken place on the edge of the turret of enjoyment.

entries.farsi: ‏بر كنار كنگره‏ى شادى بده ست / جز كنار بام خود نبود سقوط
entries.english: Indeed there is no fall except the edge of the roof: warning from the people of Noah and the people of Lot.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان سبب فصاحت و بسيار گويى آن فضول به خدمت رسول عليه الصالة و السلام
sections.title_en: Explaining the cause of the eloquence and loquacity of that impertinent man in the presence of the Prophet, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: پرتو مستى بى‏حد نبى / ‏چون بزد هم مست و خوش گشت آن غبى
entries.english: When the ray of the Prophet’s boundless intoxication struck, that stupid fellow also became drunken and merry.

entries.farsi: الجرم بسيار گو شد از نشاط / مست ادب بگذاشت آمد در خباط
entries.english: Of course, in consequence of glee he became loquacious: the intoxicated man neglected respect and began to rave.

entries.farsi: نه همه جا بى‏خودى شر مى‏كند / بى‏ادب را مى چنان تر مى‏كند
entries.english: Not on every occasion does selflessness work mischief, wine makes the unmannerly person more so.

entries.farsi: گر بود عاقل نكو فر مى‏شود / ور بود بد خوى بدتر مى‏شود
entries.english: If he be intelligent, he becomes decorous; and if he be evil-natured, he becomes worse.

entries.farsi: ليك اغلب چون بدند و ناپسند / بر همه مى را محرم كرده‏اند
entries.english: But since the majority are evil and reprobate, wine has been forbidden to all.

sections.title_fa: بيان رسول عليه السلام سبب تفضيل و اختيار كردن او آن هذيلى را به اميرى و سر لشكر ‏ى بر پيران و كار ديده‏گان
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, explained the cause of his preferring and choosing
the man of Hudhayl as commander and chief of the army over the heads of the elders and veterans.

entries.farsi: حكم اغلب راست چون غالب بدند / تيغ را از دست ره زن بستدند
entries.english: Cases are decided by the general rule: since the majority are evil, the sword was taken away from the hand of the highwayman.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه اى ظاهر نگر / تو مبين او را جوان و بى‏هنر
entries.english: The Prophet said, “O you who look on externals, do not regard him as a youth and unskilled.

entries.farsi: اى بسا ريش سياه و مرد پير / اى بسا ريش سپيد و دل چو قير
entries.english: Oh, there is many a black beard and the man old; oh, there is many a white beard with a heart as pitch.

entries.farsi: عقل او را آزمودم بارها / كرد پيرى آن جوان در كارها
entries.english: Often have I tested his understanding: that youth has shown age in affairs.

entries.farsi: پير پير عقل باشد اى پسر / نه سپيدى موى اندر ريش و سر
entries.english: O son, the old is the old in understanding: it is not whiteness of the hair in the beard and on the head.

entries.farsi: از بليس او پيرتر خود كى بود / چون كه عقلش نيست او الشى بود
entries.english: How should he be older than Iblis? When he lacks understanding, he is good-for-naught.

entries.farsi: طفل گيرش چون بود عيسى نفس / ‏پاك باشد از غرور و از هوس
entries.english: Suppose he is a child: when he has the breath of Jesus is purged of vainglory and vain desire?

entries.farsi: آن سپيدى مو دليل پختگى است / ‏پيش چشم بسته كش كوته تگى است
entries.english: That whiteness of hair is a proof of maturity to the bandaged eye that has little penetration.

entries.farsi: آن مقلد چون نداند جز دليل / ‏در عالمت جويد او دايم سبيل
entries.english: Since the blind imitator recognises nothing but proof, he continually seeks the way in the sign

entries.farsi: بهر او گفتيم كه تدبير را / چون كه خواهى كرد بگزين پير را
entries.english: For his sake we have said, ‘When you wish to plan, choose an elder.’

entries.farsi: آن كه او از پرده‏ى تقليد جست / ‏او به نور حق ببيند آن چه هست
entries.english: He who has escaped from the separation of blind imitation sees by the light of God that which is.

entries.farsi: نور پاكش بى‏دليل و بى‏بيان / ‏پوست بشكافد در آيد در ميان
entries.english: Without proof and without exposition the pure Light cleaves its skin and enters into the middle.

entries.farsi: پيش ظاهر بين چه قلب و چه سره / ‏او چه داند چيست اندر قوصره
entries.english: To one who regards the appearance, what is the adulterated and genuine coin? How should he know what is in the date-basket?

entries.farsi: اى بسا زر سيه كرده به دود / تا رهد از دست هر دزدى حسود
entries.english: Oh, there is much gold made black with smoke that it may be saved from the hands of every envious thief.

entries.farsi: اى بسا مس زر اندوده به زر / تا فرو شد آن به عقل مختصر
entries.english: Oh, there is much copper gilded with gold, that he may sell it to small understanding.

entries.farsi: ما كه باطن بين جمله‏ى كشوريم / ‏دل ببينيم و به ظاهر ننگريم
entries.english: We, who see the inward of the whole world, see the heart and look not on the outward form.”

entries.farsi: قاضيانى كه به ظاهر مى‏تنند / حكم بر اشكال ظاهر مى‏كنند
entries.english: The cadis who are concerned with the outward form give judgement according to outward appearances.

entries.farsi: چون شهادت گفت و ايمانى نمود / حكم او مومن كند اين قوم زود
entries.english: When he (the suspect) has uttered the profession of the Faith and has shown some true belief, at once these people pronounce him a true believer.

entries.farsi: بس منافق كاندر اين ظاهر گريخت / ‏خون صد مومن به پنهانى بريخت
entries.english: There is many a hypocrite who has taken refuge in this out ward form and has shed the blood of a hundred true believers in secret.

entries.farsi: جهد كن تا پير عقل و دين شوى / ‏تا چو عقل كل تو باطن بين شوى
entries.english: Endeavour to become old in intelligence and religion that you may become, like the Universal Intelligence, a seer of the inward.

entries.farsi: از عدم چون عقل زيبا رو گشاد / خلعتش داد و هزارش نام داد
entries.english: When the beauteous Intelligence unveiled its face from non-existence, He gave it a robe of honour and a thousand names.

entries.farsi: كمترين ز آن نامهاى خوش نفس / ‏اينكه نبود هيچ او محتاج كس
entries.english: Of those sweet-breathing names the least is this; that it is not in need of any one.

entries.farsi: گر به صورت وا نمايد عقل رو / تيره باشد روز پيش نور او
entries.english: If the Intelligence displays its face in visible form, day will be dark beside its light;

entries.farsi: ور مثال احمقى پيدا شود / ظلمت شب پيش او روشن بود
entries.english: And if the shape of foolishness becomes visible, beside it the darkness of night will be radiant,

entries.farsi: كاو ز شب مظلم‏تر و تارى‏تر است / ‏ليك خفاش شقى ظلمت خر است
entries.english: For it is darker and more murky than night; but the miserable bat is a buyer of darkness.

entries.farsi: اندك اندك خوى كن با نور روز / ور نه خفاشى بمانى بى‏فروز
entries.english: Little by little accustom yourself to the daylight; else you will remain a bat deprived of splendour.

entries.farsi: عاشق هر جا شكال و مشكلى است / ‏دشمن هر جا چراغ مقبلى است
entries.english: He is the lover of every place where there is difficulty and perplexing doubt, and the enemy of every place where there is the lamp of felicity

entries.farsi: ظلمت اشكال ز آن جويد دلش / ‏تا كه افزون‏تر نمايد حاصلش
entries.english: His heart seeks the darkness of perplexity in order that his acquirements may seem greater,

entries.farsi: تا ترا مشغول آن مشكل كند / و ز نهاد زشت خود غافل كند
entries.english: So that he may preoccupy you with that difficult question and make you oblivious of his own evil nature.

sections.title_fa: ‏عالمت عاقل تمام و عالمت نيم عاقل و مرد تمام و نيم مرد و عالمت شقى مغرور الشى
sections.title_en: The marks of the wholly intelligent and the half-intelligent and the whole man and the half-man and the deluded worthless wretch doomed to perdition.

entries.farsi: عاقل آن باشد كه او با مشغله است / ‏او دليل و پيشواى قافله است
entries.english: The intelligent man is he who bathes the lamp: he is the guide and leader of the caravan.

entries.farsi: پى رو نور خود است آن پيش رو / تابع خويش است آن بى‏خويش رو
entries.english: That leader is one who goes after his own light: that selfless traveller is the follower of himself.

entries.farsi: مومن خويش است و ايمان آوريد / هم بدان نورى كه جانش زو چريد
entries.english: He is the one that puts faith in himself; and do you too put faith in the light on which his soul has browsed.

entries.farsi: ديگرى كه نيم عاقل آمد او / عاقلى را ديده‏ى خود داند او
entries.english: The other, who is the half-intelligent, deems an intelligent person to be his eye,

entries.farsi: دست در وى زد چو كور اندر دليل / ‏تا بدو بينا شد و چست و جليل
entries.english: And has clutched him as the blind man clutches the guide, so that through him he has become seeing and active and illustrious

entries.farsi: و آن خرى كز عقل جو سنگى نداشت / ‏خود نبودش عقل و عاقل را گذاشت
entries.english: But the ass who had not a single barley-corn’s weight of intelligence, who possessed no intelligence himself and forsook the intelligent,

entries.farsi: ره نداند نه كثير و نه قليل / ‏ننگش آيد آمدن خلف دليل
entries.english: Knows neither much nor little of the way disdains to go behind the guide,

entries.farsi: مى‏رود اندر بيابان دراز / گاه لنگان آيس و گاهى به تاز
entries.english: He is journeying in a long wilderness, now limping in despair and now at a run.

entries.farsi: شمع نه تا پيشواى خود كند / نيم شمعى نه كه نورى كد كند
entries.english: He has neither a candle, that he should make it his leader, nor half a candle, that he should beg a light.

entries.farsi: نيست عقلش تا دم زنده زند / نيم عقلى نه كه خود مرده كند
entries.english: He has neither intelligence, that he should breathe the breath of the living, nor has he a half-intelligence, that he should make himself dead.

entries.farsi: مرده‏ى آن عاقل آيد او تمام / ‏تا بر آيد از نشيب خود به بام
entries.english: He becomes wholly dead in the man of intelligence that he may ascend from his own low place to the roof.

entries.farsi: زنده نى تا هم دم عيسى بود / مرده نى تا دمگه عيسى شود
entries.english: You have not perfect intelligence, make yourself dead under the protection of an intelligent man whose words are living.

entries.farsi: عقل كامل نيست خود را مرده كن / ‏در پناه عاقلى زنده سخن
entries.english: He is not living, that he should breathe in accord with Jesus, nor is he dead, that he should become a channel for the breath of Jesus.

entries.farsi: جان كورش گام هر سو مى‏نهد / عاقبت نجهد ولى بر مى‏جهد
entries.english: His blind spirit is stepping in every direction: it will not escape in the end, but it is leaping up.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن آبگير و صيادان و آن سه ماهى يكى عاقل و يكى نيم عاقل و آن ‏ ديگر مغرور و ابله مغفل الشى و عاقبت هر سه
sections.title_en: Story of the lake and the fishermen and the three fishes, one intelligent and one half intelligent and the third deluded, foolish, heedless and good-for-naught; and the end of all three.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى آن آبگير است اى عنود / كه در او سه ماهى اشگرف بود
entries.english: This, O obstinate man, is the story of the lake in which there were three great fishes.

entries.farsi: در كليله خوانده باشى ليك آن / ‏قشر قصه باشد و اين مغز جان
entries.english: You will have read it in Kalíla, but that is the husk of the story, while this is the spiritual kernel.

entries.farsi: چند صيادى سوى آن آبگير / بر گذشتند و بديدند آن ضمير
entries.english: Some fishermen passed beside the lake and saw that concealed.

entries.farsi: پس شتابيدند تا دام آورند / ماهيان واقف شدند و هوشمند
entries.english: Then they hastened to bring the net: the fishes noticed and became aware.

entries.farsi: آن كه عاقل بود عزم راه كرد / عزم راه مشكل ناخواه كرد
entries.english: The intelligent one resolved to journey, resolved to make the difficult unwelcome journey.

entries.farsi: گفت با اينها ندارم مشورت / ‏كه يقين سستم كنند از مقدرت
entries.english: He said, “I will not consult these, for assuredly they will make me weak in power.

entries.farsi: مهر زاد و بود بر جانشان تند / كاهلى و جهلشان بر من زند
entries.english: Love of their native place and abode holds sway over their souls: their indolence and ignorance will strike on me.”

entries.farsi: مشورت را زنده‏اى بايد نكو / كه ترا زنده كند و آن زنده كو
entries.english: For consultation, some goodly, living person is required, that he may make you living; and where is that living one?

entries.farsi: از دم حب الوطن بگذر مه‏ايست / ‏كه وطن آن سوست جان اين سوى نيست
entries.english: O traveller, take counsel with a traveller, for a woman’s counsel will keep your foot lame.

entries.farsi: اى مسافر با مسافر راى زن / ‏ز انكه پايت لنگ دارد راى‏زن
entries.english: Pass on from the expression, “love of country”; do not stop, for your country is Yonder, O soul: it is not on this side.

entries.farsi: گر وطن خواهى گذر ز آن سوى شط / اين حديث راست را كم خوان غلط
entries.english: If you desire your country, cross to the other bank of the stream: do not misread this true Hadíth.

sections.title_fa: سر خواندن وضو كننده اوراد وضو را
sections.title_en: The inner meaning of the recitation of the ablutionary prayers by one who performs the ritual ablution

entries.farsi: در وضو هر عضو را وردى جدا / آمده‏ست اندر خبر بهر دعا
entries.english: In the ritual ablution, a separate form of prayer for each member of the body has been handed down in Tradition.

entries.farsi: چون كه استنشاق بينى مى‏كنى / ‏بوى جنت خواه از رب غنى
entries.english: When you snuff up water into your nose, beg of the self-sufficient Lord the scent of Paradise,

entries.farsi: تا ترا آن بو كشد سوى جنان / ‏بوى گل باشد دليل گلبنان
entries.english: In order that that scent may lead you towards Paradise: the scent of the rose is the guide to the rose-trees.

entries.farsi: چون كه استنجا كنى ورد و سخن / ‏اين بود يا رب تو زين‏ام پاك كن
entries.english: When you perform the act of ablution, the form of prayer and words is this: “O Lord, cleanse me from this.

entries.farsi: دست من اينجا رسيد اين را بشست / ‏دستم اندر شستن جان است سست
entries.english: My hand has reached this place and washed it, my hand is weak to wash my spirit.

entries.farsi: اى ز تو كس گشته جان ناكسان / ‏دست فضل تست در جانها رسان
entries.english: O You by whom the spirit of the unworthy is made worthy, the hand of Your bounty is reaching the spirits.

entries.farsi: حد من اين بود كردم من لئيم / ‏ز آن سوى حد را نقى كن اى كريم
entries.english: This I, vile, has done was my limit: make clean what beyond the limit, O gracious One.

entries.farsi: از حدث شستم خدايا پوست را / از حوادث تو بشو اين دوست را
entries.english: O God, I have washed my skin clean of ordure: You wash this beloved clean of worldly taints.”

sections.title_fa: شخصى به وقت استنجا مى‏گفت اللَّهم ارحنى رايحه الجنة بجاى آن كه اللَّهم اجعلنى من التوابين و اجعلنى من المتطهرين كه ورد استنجاست و ورد استنجا را به وقت استنشاق مى‏گفت عزيزى بشنيد ‏و اين را طاقت نداشت

sections.title_en: A certain person used to say at the time of ablution, “O God, let me smell the sweet odour of Paradise” instead of “O God, make me one of those who repent much, and make me one of those who purify themselves,” which is the form of prayer in ablution; and he used to recite the formula proper to ablution at the time of rinsing his nose.
A venerable man heard and could not endure it.

entries.farsi: آن يكى در وقت استنجا بگفت / ‏كه مرا با بوى جنت دار جفت
entries.english: A certain one said at the time of ablution, “Unite me with the scent of Paradise!”

entries.farsi: گفت شخصى خوب ورد آورده‏اى / ‏ليك سوراخ دعا گم كرده‏اى
entries.english: A person said, “You have used a good formula, but you have missed the hole for the prayer.

entries.farsi: اين دعا چون ورد بينى بود چون / ‏ورد بينى را تو آوردى به كون
entries.english: Since this prayer was the formula applicable to the nose, why have you applied the nose-formula to the arse?

entries.farsi: رايحه‏ى جنت ز بينى يافت حر / رايحه‏ى جنت كى آيد از دبر
entries.english: One free gets the odour of Paradise from his nose: how should the odour of Paradise come from the rump?”

entries.farsi: اى تواضع برده پيش ابلهان / ‏وى تكبر برده تو پيش شهان
entries.english: O you who have brought humility into the presence of fools, and O you who have brought pride into the presence of kings,

entries.farsi: آن تكبر بر خسان خوب است و چست / ‏هين مرو معكوس عكسش بند تست
entries.english: The pride shown to the base is goodly and fitting. Take heed, do not behave in the reverse manner: the reverse thereof is your bondage.

entries.farsi: از پى سوراخ بينى رست گل / ‏بو وظيفه‏ى بينى آمد اى عتل
entries.english: The rose grew for the sake of the nostrils: sweet scent is the stipend of the nose, O churl.

entries.farsi: بوى گل بهر مشام است اى دلير / جاى آن بو نيست اين سوراخ زير
entries.english: The scent of the rose is for organs of smell, O bold man: this hole below is not the place for that scent.

entries.farsi: كى از اينجا بوى خلد آيد ترا / بو ز موضع جو اگر بايد ترا
entries.english: How should the scent of Paradise come to you from this place? If you require the scent, seek it from its place.

entries.farsi: همچنين حب الوطن باشد درست / ‏تو وطن بشناس اى خواجه نخست
entries.english: Likewise, “love of country” is right, first, O master, know your country.

entries.farsi: گفت آن ماهى زيرك ره كنم / ‏دل ز راى و مشورتشان بر كنم
entries.english: That sagacious fish said, “I will journey, I will withdraw my heart from their advice and counsel.”

entries.farsi: محرم آن آه كمياب است بس / ‏شب رو و پنهان روى كن چون عسس
entries.english: ’It is no time for counsel. Listen, journey! Like ‘Alí, sigh into the well.

entries.farsi: نيست وقت مشورت هين راه كن / ‏چون على تو آه اندر چاه كن
entries.english: Very seldom is there found a fit confidant for that sigh: go by night and let your movement be hidden, like the night-patrol.

entries.farsi: سوى دريا عزم كن زين آبگير / بحر جو و ترك اين گرداب گير
entries.english: Set out from this lake towards the sea: seek the sea and take leave of this whirlpool.

entries.farsi: سينه را پا ساخت مى‏رفت آن حذور / از مقام با خطر تا بحر نور
entries.english: That wary fish made its breast a foot and was going from its perilous abode to the sea of light,

entries.farsi: همچو آهو كز پى او سگ بود / مى‏دود تا در تنش يك رگ بود
entries.english: Like the deer of which a dog is in pursuit and which keeps running so long as there is a single nerve in its body.

entries.farsi: خواب خرگوش و سگ اندر پى خطاست / خواب خود در چشم ترسنده كجاست
entries.english: Hare’s sleep with the dog in pursuit is a sin: how indeed is sleep in the eye of him who has fear?

entries.farsi: رفت آن ماهى ره دريا گرفت / ‏راه دور و پهنه‏ى پهنا گرفت
entries.english: That fish departed and took the way to the sea: it took the far way and the vast expanse.

entries.farsi: رنجها بسيار ديد و عاقبت / ‏رفت آخر سوى امن و عافيت
entries.english: It suffered many afflictions, and in the end it went after all towards safety and welfare.

entries.farsi: خويشتن افكند در درياى ژرف / ‏كه نيابد حد آن را هيچ طرف
entries.english: It cast itself into the deep Sea whose bound no eye can reach.

entries.farsi: پس چو صيادان بياوردند دام / ‏نيم عاقل را از آن شد تلخ كام
entries.english: So when the fishermen brought their net, the half-intelligent was bitterly grieved thereat.

entries.farsi: گفت اه من فوت كردم فرصه را / چون نگشتم همره آن رهنما
entries.english: And said, “Alas, I have lost the opportunity: how did not I accompany that guide?

entries.farsi: ناگهان رفت او و ليكن چون كه رفت / ‏مى‏ببايستم شدن در پى به تفت
entries.english: He went off suddenly, but seeing that he went I ought to have gone after him in hot haste.”

entries.farsi: بر گذشته حسرت آوردن خطاست / ‏باز نايد رفته ياد آن هباست
entries.english: It is wrong to regret the past: what is gone will not come back: to remember it is of no avail.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن مرغ گرفته كه وصيت كرد كه بر گذشته پشيمانى مخور تدارك وقت انديش ‏ و روزگار مبر در پشيمانى
sections.title_en: Story of the captive bird which gave the injunctions: do not feel sorrow for what is past, think about taking precaution for the present (need), and do not spend time in repenting.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مرغى گرفت از مكر و دام / ‏مرغ او را گفت اى خواجه‏ى همام
entries.english: A certain man caught a bird by guile and trap: the bird said to him, “O noble sire,

entries.farsi: تو بسى گاوان و ميشان خورده‏اى / ‏تو بسى اشتر به قربان كرده‏اى
entries.english: You have eaten many oxen and sheep; you have sacrificed many camels;

entries.farsi: تو نگشتى سير از آنها در زمن / ‏هم نگردى سير از اجزاى من
entries.english: You have never in the world been satisfied by them; neither will you be satisfied by my limbs.

entries.farsi: اول آن پند هم در دست تو / ثانيش بر بام كهگل بست تو
entries.english: Let me go, that I may bestow on you three counsels, that you may perceive whether I am wise or foolish.

entries.farsi: هل مرا تا كه سه پندت بر دهم / ‏تا بدانى زيركم يا ابلهم
entries.english: The first of those counsels on your hand, the second of them on your plastered roof,

entries.farsi: و آن سوم پندت دهم من بر درخت / ‏كه از اين سه پند گردى نيك بخت
entries.english: And the third counsel I will give you on a tree, for you will become fortunate through these three counsels.

entries.farsi: آنچ بر دست است اين است آن سخن / ‏كه محالى را ز كس باور مكن
entries.english: That saying which is on your hand, it is this: ‘do not believe an absurdity from any one.’”

entries.farsi: بر كفش چون گفت اول پند زفت / ‏گشت آزاد و بر آن ديوار رفت
entries.english: When it had uttered the first grave counsel on his palm, it became free and went on the wall,

entries.farsi: گفت ديگر بر گذشته غم مخور / چون ز تو بگذشت ز آن حسرت مبر
entries.english: And said, “The second is, ‘do not grieve over the past: when it has passed from you, do not feel regret for it.’”

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش كه در جسمم كتيم / ‏ده درم سنگ است يك در يتيم
entries.english: After that, it said to him, “In my body is concealed a solitary pearl, ten ounces in weight.

entries.farsi: دولت تو بخت فرزندان تو / بود آن گوهر به حق جان تو
entries.english: By your soul’s truth, that jewel was your fortune and the luck of your children.

entries.farsi: فوت كردى در كه روزى‏ات نبود / كه نباشد مثل آن در در وجود
entries.english: You have missed the pearl, for it was not your appointed lot— a pearl the like of which is not in existence.”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه وقت زادن حامله / ‏ناله دارد، خواجه شد در غلغله
entries.english: Even as a woman big with child keeps wailing at the time of birth, so the Khwaja began to cry out clamorously.

entries.farsi: مرغ گفتش نى نصيحت كردمت / ‏كه مبادا بر گذشته‏ى دى غمت
entries.english: The bird said to him, “Did not I admonish you, saying, ‘Let there be no grief in you for what passed yesterday’?

entries.farsi: چون گذشت و رفت غم چون مى‏خورى / ‏يا نكردى فهم پندم يا كرى
entries.english: Since it is past and gone, why are you grieving? Either you did not understand my counsel or you are deaf.

entries.farsi: و آن دوم پندت بگفتم كز ضالل / ‏هيچ تو باور مكن قول محال
entries.english: And the second counsel I gave you,, ‘Do not from lack of understanding put any belief in an absurd statement,’

entries.farsi: من نيم خود سه درمسنگ اى اسد / ده درم سنگ اندرونم چون بود
entries.english: O lion, I myself do not weigh ten ounces: how should the weight of ten ounces be within me?”

entries.farsi: خواجه باز آمد به خود گفتا كه هين / ‏باز گو آن پند خوب سومين
entries.english: The Khwaja came back to himself and said, “Listen, disclose the third excellent counsel.”

entries.farsi: پند گفتن با جهول خوابناك / ‏تخم افكندن بود در شوره خاك
entries.english: “Yes,” said the bird, “you have made good use of those, that I should tell the third counsel in vain!”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى خوش عمل كردى بدان / ‏تا بگويم پند ثالث رايگان
entries.english: To give counsel to a sleepy ignoramus is to scatter seed in nitrous soil.

entries.farsi: چاك حمق و جهل نپذيرد رفو / تخم حكمت كم دهش اى پند گو
entries.english: The rent of folly and ignorance does not admit of being patched up: do not give the seed of wisdom to him, O counsellor.

sections.title_fa: ‏چاره انديشيدن آن ماهى نيم عاقل و خود را مرده كردن
sections.title_en: How the half-intelligent fish devised a means and feigned to be dead.

entries.farsi: گفت ماهى دگر وقت بال / چون كه ماند از سايه‏ى عاقل جدا
entries.english: The second fish said in the hour of tribulation, when he was left sundered from the shadow of the intelligent one,

entries.farsi: كاو سوى دريا شد و از غم عتيق / ‏فوت شد از من چنان نيكو رفيق
entries.english: “He has gone towards the sea and is freed from sorrow: such a good comrade has been lost to me!

entries.farsi: ليك ز آن ننديشم و بر خود زنم / ‏خويشتن را اين زمان مرده كنم
entries.english: But I will not think of that and will attend to myself: at this time I will feign to be dead.

entries.farsi: پس بر آرم اشكم خود بر زبر / پشت زير و مى‏روم بر آب بر
entries.english: Then I will turn my belly upwards and my back downwards and will move on the water.

entries.farsi: مرده گردم خويش بسپارم به آب / ‏مرگ پيش از مرگ امن است از عذاب
entries.english: I will move upon it as weeds move, not by swimming as a person does.

entries.farsi: مى‏روم بر وى چنان كه خس رود / نى بسباحى چنان كه كس رود
entries.english: I will become dead; I will commit myself to the water: to die before death is to be safe from torment.”

entries.farsi: مرگ پيش از مرگ امن است اى فتى / ‏اين چنين فرمود ما را مصطفى
entries.english: To die before death is to be safe, O youth: even so has Mustafa commanded us.

entries.farsi: گفت موتوا كلكم من قبل ان / ‏ياتى الموت تموتوا بالفتن
entries.english: He said, “Die, all of you, before death comes, else you will die with sore afflictions.”

entries.farsi: همچنان مرد و شكم بالا فگند / آب مى‏بردش نشيب و گه بلند
entries.english: He died in that manner and threw his belly upwards: the water was carrying him, now below, now aloft.

entries.farsi: شاد مى‏شد او از آن گفت دريغ / ‏پيش رفت اين بازى‏ام رستم ز تيغ
entries.english: Every one of those pursuers bore great vexation, saying, “Alas, the best fish is dead.”

entries.farsi: هر يكى ز آن قاصدان بس غصه برد / كه دريغا ماهى بهتر بمرد
entries.english: He was made glad by their saying “Alas”, “This trick of mine has come off, I am delivered from the sword.”

entries.farsi: پس گرفتش يك صياد ارجمند / پس بر او تف كرد و بر خاكش فگند
entries.english: Then a worthy fisherman seized him and spat on him and flung him on the ground.

entries.farsi: غلط غلطان رفت پنهان اندر آب / ‏ماند آن احمق همى‏كرد اضطراب
entries.english: He, rolling over and over, went secretly into the water; the foolish one remained, moving to and fro in agitation.

entries.farsi: از چپ و از راست مى‏جست آن سليم / ‏تا به جهد خويش برهاند گليم
entries.english: That simpleton kept leaping about, right and left, in order that he might save his skin by his own efforts.

entries.farsi: دام افكندند و اندر دام ماند / احمقى او را در آن آتش نشاند
entries.english: They cast the net, and he remained in the net: foolishness ensconced him in that fire.

entries.farsi: بر سر آتش به پشت تابه‏اى / ‏با حماقت گشت او هم خوابه‏اى
entries.english: On the top of the fire, on the surface of a frying-pan, he became the bedfellow of Folly.

entries.farsi: او همى‏جوشيد از تف سعير / عقل مى‏گفتش أ لم ياتك نذير
entries.english: He was seething from the heat of the flames: Reason was saving to him, “Did not a warner come to you?”

entries.farsi: او همى‏گفت از شكنجه و ز بال / ‏همچو جان كافران قالُوا بلى
entries.english: He, from the rack of torture and tribulation, was replying like the souls of the unbelievers: they said, “Yea.”

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گفت او كه گر اين بار من / ‏وا رهم زين محنت گردن شكن
entries.english: Then again he was saying, “If this time I escape from this neck-breaking affliction,

entries.farsi: من نسازم جز به دريايى وطن / ‏آب گيرى را نسازم من سكن
entries.english: I will not make my home except in a sea: I will not make a lake my dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: آب بى‏حد جويم و آمن شوم / ‏تا ابد در امن و صحت مى‏روم
entries.english: I will seek the boundless sea and become safe: I will go in safety and welfare forever.”

sections.title_fa: ُبيان آن كه عهد كردن احمق وقت گرفتارى و ندم هيچ وفايى ندارد كه وَ لَوْ رُدُّوا لَعادُوا لِما نُهُوا عَ نْه وَ إِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ ، صبح كاذب وفا ندارد
sections.title_en: Explaining that the promise made by the fool at the moment of seizure and contrition is faithless, for though they should be sent back, they would surely return to that which they were forbidden to do, and verily they are liars. The false dawn keeps not faith.

entries.farsi: عقل مى‏گفتش حماقت با تو است / ‏با حماقت عهد را آيد شكست
entries.english: Reason was saying to him, “Folly is with you: with Folly, the promise will be broken.

entries.farsi: عقل را باشد وفاى عهدها / تو ندارى عقل رو اى خربها
entries.english: The keeping of promises appertains to reason: you have not reason: Go, O you whose value is that of an ass!

entries.farsi: عقل را ياد آيد از پيمان خود / پرده‏ى نسيان بدراند خرد
entries.english: Reason remembers its covenant: understanding rends the veil of forgetfulness.

entries.farsi: از كمى عقل پروانه‏ى خسيس / ‏ياد نارد ز آتش و سوز و حسيس
entries.english: Since you have not reason, forgetfulness is your ruler: it is your enemy and the bringer-to-naught of your devising.”

entries.farsi: چون كه عقلت نيست نسيان مير تست / ‏دشمن و باطل كن تدبير تست
entries.english: From deficiency of reason the wretched moth does not re member the flame and the burning and the sound.

entries.farsi: ضبط و درك و حافظى و يادداشت / ‏عقل را باشد كه عقل آن را فراشت
entries.english: When its wings are burnt, it repents; cupidity and forgetfulness dash it into the flame.

entries.farsi: چون كه پرش سوخت توبه مى‏كند / آز و نسيانش بر آتش مى‏زند
entries.english: Grasp and apprehension and retentiveness and keeping in mind belong to Reason, for Reason has raised those.

entries.farsi: چون كه گوهر نيست تابش چون بود / چون مذكر نيست ايابش چون بود
entries.english: When the pearl is not there, how should its lustre exist? When there is none to remind, how should he turn back?

entries.farsi: اين تمنى هم ز بى‏عقلى اوست / ‏كه نبيند كان حماقت را چه خوست
entries.english: Moreover, this wish arises from his want of reason, for he does not see what the nature of that folly is.

entries.farsi: آن ندامت از نتيجه‏ى رنج بود / نه ز عقل روشن چون گنج بود
entries.english: That contrition was the result of pain, not of Reason which is bright as a treasure.

entries.farsi: چون كه شد رنج آن ندامت شد عدم / ‏مى‏نيرزد خاك آن توبه و ندم
entries.english: When the pain departed, that contrition became naught: that repentance and contrition has not the worth of dust.

entries.farsi: آن ندم از ظلمت غم بست بار / پس كالم الليل يمحوه النهار
entries.english: That contrition burgeoned from the darkness of pain; hence day wipes out the words of night

entries.farsi: چون برفت آن ظلمت غم گشت خوش / ‏هم رود از دل نتيجه و زاده‏اش
entries.english: When the darkness of pain is gone and he has become happy, its result and product also goes from his heart.

entries.farsi: مى‏كند او توبه و پير خرد / بانگ لَوْ رُدُّوا لَعادُوا مى‏زند
entries.english: He is making vows of repentance, whilst the Pir, Reason, is crying, “Though they should be sent back, they would surely return.”

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه وهم قلب عقل است و ستيزه‏ى اوست، بدو ماند و او نيست و قصه‏ى مجاوبات موسى عليه السلام كه صاحب عقل بود با فرعون كه صاحب وهم بود
sections.title_en: Explaining that imagination is the counterfeit of Reason and in opposition to it,

and that though it resembles Reason it is not Reason; and the story of the replies given to each other by Moses, on whom be peace, who was the possessor of Reason, and Pharaoh, who was the possessor of imagination.

entries.farsi: عقل ضد شهوت است اى پهلوان / ‏آن كه شهوت مى‏تند عقلش مخوان
entries.english: Reason is the contrary of sensuality: O brave man, do not call Reason that which is attached to sensuality.

entries.farsi: وهم خوانش آن كه شهوت را گداست / ‏وهم قلب نقد زر عقلهاست
entries.english: That which is a beggar of sensuality—calls it imagination: imagination is the counterfeit of the sterling gold of the rational faculties.

entries.farsi: بى‏محك پيدا نگردد وهم و عقل / ‏هر دو را سوى محك كن زود نقل
entries.english: Without a touchstone, imagination and reason are not clearly distinguished: quickly bring both to the touchstone.

entries.farsi: اين محك قرآن و حال انبيا / چون محك مر قلب را گويد بيا
entries.english: The Qur’an and the state of the Prophets are this touchstone: they, like a touchstone, say to the counterfeit coin, “Come,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى خويش را ز آسيب من / ‏كه نه‏اى اهل فراز و شيب من
entries.english: That by contact with me you may see yourself that you are not worthy of my higher and lower”

entries.farsi: عقل را گر اره‏اى سازد دو نيم / ‏همچو زر باشد در آتش او بسيم
entries.english: If a saw make Reason two halves, it will be smiling like gold in the fire.

entries.farsi: وهم مر فرعون عالم سوز را / عقل مر موساى جان افروز را
entries.english: Imagination belongs to Pharaoh, the world-incendiary; Reason to Moses, the spirit-enkindler.

entries.farsi: رفت موسى بر طريق نيستى / ‏گفت فرعونش بگو تو كيستى
entries.english: Moses went on the way of non-existence: Pharaoh said to him, “Tell me, who are you?”

entries.farsi: گفت من عقلم رسول ذو الجلال / ‏حجه اللهَّ‏ام امانم از ضالل
entries.english: He said, “I am Reason, the messenger of the Almighty: I am the proof of God, I am the protection against error.”

entries.farsi: گفت نى خامش رها كن هاى‏و هو / نسبت و نام قديمت را بگو
entries.english: “Nay,” said he, “hush, cease from outcry: tell your ancient lineage and name.”

entries.farsi: گفت كه نسبت مرا از خاكدانش / ‏نام اصلم كمترين بندگانش
entries.english: “My lineage,” he said, “From His dust-pit; my original name is ‘the meanest of His slaves.’

entries.farsi: بنده زاده‏ى آن خداوند وحيد / زاده از پشت جوارى و عبيد
entries.english: I am the slave-born of that unique Lord— born of the loins of slaves female and male.

entries.farsi: نسبت اصلم ز خاك و آب و گل / ‏آب و گل را داد يزدان جان و دل
entries.english: My original lineage is from earth and water and clay: God gave unto water and clay a soul and heart.

entries.farsi: مرجع اين جسم خاكم هم به خاك / ‏مرجع تو هم به خاك اى سهمناك
entries.english: To earth also will return this earthen body of mine; to earth you likewise will return, O terrible one.

entries.farsi: اصل ما و اصل جمله سركشان / ‏هست از خاكى و آن را صد نشان
entries.english: Our origin and the origin of all the proud is from a piece of earth, and a hundred signs thereof;

entries.farsi: كه مدد از خاك مى‏گيرد تنت / ‏از غذاى خاك پيچد گردنت
entries.english: For your body receives support from the earth, and from earthly nourishment your neck is wrapped in folds

entries.farsi: چون رود جان مى‏شود او باز خاك / ‏اندر آن گور مخوف سهمناك
entries.english: When the spirit departs, it will again become earth in the dreaded and horrible grave.

entries.farsi: هم تو و هم ما و هم اشباه تو / خاك گردند و نماند جاه تو
entries.english: Both you and we and all who resemble you will become earth, and your power will remain no more.”

entries.farsi: بنده‏ى فرعون و بنده‏ى بندگانش / ‏كه از او پرورد اول جسم و جانش
entries.english: He said, “You have a name other than this lineage: truly that name is more proper for you—

entries.farsi: گفت غير اين نسب ناميت هست / ‏مر ترا آن نام خود اوليتر است
entries.english: ‘Slave of Pharaoh and slave of his slaves,’ whose body and soul were first nurtured by him,

entries.farsi: بنده‏ى ياغى طاغى ظلوم / ‏زين وطن بگريخته از فعل شوم
entries.english: A hostile, insolent and unrighteous slave, who fled from this country on account of an ill-omened deed

entries.farsi: خونى و غدارى و حق ناشناس / ‏هم بر اين اوصاف خود مى‏كن قياس
entries.english: You are a murderer and treacherous and ungrateful: from just these qualities, for truth, form a judgement.

entries.farsi: در غريبى خوار و درويش و خلق / ‏كه ندانستى سپاس ما و حق
entries.english: In exile, despised and poor and threadbare, since you did not acknowledge gratitude and obligation to me.”

entries.farsi: گفت حاشا كه بود با آن مليك / ‏در خداوندى كسى ديگر شريك
entries.english: He said, “Far be it that any other person should be a partner in Lordship with that King.

entries.farsi: واحد اندر ملك او را يار نى / ‏بندگانش را جز او سالار نى
entries.english: One: He has no associate in Kingship; His slaves have no master but Him.

entries.farsi: نقش او كردست و نقاش من اوست / ‏غير اگر دعوى كند او ظلم جوست
entries.english: His creatures have no other owner: does anyone claim partnership with Him except one that is doomed to perish?

entries.farsi: نيست خلقش را دگر كس مالكى / ‏شركتش دعوى كند جز هالكى
entries.english: He has made the design, He is my Designer; if another lay claim, he is a seeker of iniquity.

entries.farsi: تو نتانى ابروى من ساختن / ‏چون توانى جان من بشناختن
entries.english: You cannot fashion my eyebrow: how can you know my soul?

entries.farsi: بلكه آن غدار و آن طاغى تويى / ‏كه كنى با حق تو دعوى دويى
entries.english: Nay, it is you who are the traitor and the insolent one, for you claim duality with God.

entries.farsi: من زدم مشتى و ناگاه او فتاد / آن كه جانش خود نبد جانى بداد
entries.english: If I inadvertently killed a ruffian, I killed neither for self ’s sake nor in sport.

entries.farsi: من سگى كشتم تو مرسل زادگان / ‏صد هزاران طفل بى‏جرم و زيان
entries.english: I struck a blow with my fist, and he suddenly fell: one who really had no soul gave up a soul.

entries.farsi: گر بكشتم من عوانى را به سهو / نه براى نفس كشتم نه به لهو
entries.english: I killed a cur: you the children of him who was sent— hundreds of thousands of innocent and harmless babes—

entries.farsi: كشته‏اى و خونشان در گردنت / ‏تا چه آيد بر تو زين خون خوردنت
entries.english: Have killed, and their blood is on your neck: consider what shall come upon you because of this blood-drinking of yours.

entries.farsi: كشته‏اى ذريت يعقوب را / بر اميد قتل من مطلوب را
entries.english: You have killed the progeny of Jacob— those sought after in hope of slaying me.

entries.farsi: كورى تو حق مرا خود بر گزيد / سر نگون شد آن چه نفست مى‏پزيد
entries.english: Despite you God Himself chose me out: that which your soul was concocting was overthrown.”

entries.farsi: گفت اينها را بهل بى‏هيچ شك / ‏اين بود حق من و نان و نمك
entries.english: He said, “Let these things be without any doubt: is it due to me and to the bread and salt

entries.farsi: كه مرا پيش حشر خوارى كنى / ‏روز روشن بر دلم تارى كنى
entries.english: That you should treat me rudely in the presence of the assembled people and make the bright day dark to my heart?”

entries.farsi: گفت خوارى قيامت صعب‏تر / گر ندارى پاس من در خير و شر
entries.english: He said, “The rudely of the Resurrection is more grievous, if you do not pay regard to me in good and evil.

entries.farsi: زخم كيكى را نمى‏تانى كشيد / زخم مارى را تو چون خواهى چشيد
entries.english: You cannot bear the bite of a flea: how will you taste the bite of a snake?

entries.farsi: ظاهرا كار تو ويران مى‏كنم / ‏ليك خارى را گلستان مى‏كنم
entries.english: In appearance I am ruining your work, but I am making a thorn into a rose-garden.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه عمارت در ويرانى است و جمعيت در پراكندگى است و درستى در شكستگى است و مراد ‏در بى‏مرادى است و وجود در عدم است و على هذا بقيه الاضداد و الازواج
sections.title_en: Explaining that cultivation consists in devastation and composure in distraction and wholeness in brokenness and success in failure and existence in non-existence;
and thus the rest of the contraries and pairs

entries.farsi: آن يكى آمد زمين را مى‏شكافت / ‏ابلهى فرياد كرد و بر نتافت
entries.english: A certain man came and was cleaving the soil: a fool cried out and could not control himself,

entries.farsi: كاين زمين را از چه ويران مى‏كنى / ‏مى‏شكافى و پريشان مى‏كنى
entries.english: Saying, ‘Why are you ruining this soil, cleaving and scattering it?’

entries.farsi: گفت اى ابله برو بر من مران / ‏تو عمارت از خرابى باز دان
entries.english: ‘O fool,’ said he, ‘depart, do not interfere with me: recognise cultivation from devastation.

entries.farsi: كى شود گلزار و گندم‏زار اين / تا نگردد زشت و ويران اين زمين
entries.english: How should this become a rose-garden or cornfield till this soil becomes ugly and ruined?

entries.farsi: تا بنشكافى به نشتر ريش چغز / كى شود نيكو و كى گرديد نغز
entries.english: How should it become orchards and crops and leaves and fruit till its arrangement is turned upside down?’

entries.farsi: كى شود بستان و كشت و برگ و بر / تا نگردد نظم او زير و زبر
entries.english: Till you pierce the pus filled ulcer with a lancet, how will it become well and how will you become healthy?

entries.farsi: تا نشويد خلطهايت از دوا / كى رود شورش كجا آيد شفا
entries.english: Till he cleanses your humours with medicine, how will the indisposition be removed? How will a cure be effected?

entries.farsi: پاره پاره كرده درزى جامه را / كس زند آن درزى عالمه را
entries.english: When a tailor cuts a garment piece by piece, will any one strike that expert tailor,

entries.farsi: كه چرا اين اطلس بگزيده را / بر دريدى چه كنم بدريده را
entries.english: Saying, ‘Why have you torn this choice satin? What can I do with a torn?’

entries.farsi: هر بناى كهنه كابادان كنند / نه كه اول كهنه را ويران كنند
entries.english: Whenever they put an old building in good repair, do not they first ruin the old one?

entries.farsi: همچنين نجار و حداد و قصاب / ‏هستشان پيش از عمارتها خراب
entries.english: Likewise the carpenter, the iron-smith and the butcher— with them there is destruction before restorations.

entries.farsi: آن هليله و آن بليله كوفتن / ‏ز آن تلف، گردند معمورى تن
entries.english: The pounding of almond and bastard almond— by reason of that destruction they become the means of restoring the body.

entries.farsi: تا نكوبى گندم اندر آسيا / كى شود آراسته ز آن خوان ما
entries.english: Until you crush wheat in the mill, how will our table be garnished with it?

entries.farsi: آن تقاضا كرد آن نان و نمك / ‏كه ز شستت وارهانم اى سمك
entries.english: That bread and salt demanded that I should deliver you, O fish, from the net.

entries.farsi: گر پذيرى پند موسى وارهى / ‏از چنين شست بد نامنتهى
entries.english: If you accept the counsel of Moses, you will escape from such an evil infinite net.

entries.farsi: بس كه خود را كرده‏اى بنده‏ى هوا / كرمكى را كرده‏اى تو اژدها
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have made yourself the slave of sensuality, you have made a petty worm into a dragon.

entries.farsi: اژدها را اژدها آورده‏ام / ‏تا به اصالح آورم من دم‏به‏دم
entries.english: I have brought a dragon for dragon, that I may correct breath by breath,

entries.farsi: تا دم آن از دم اين بشكند / مار من آن اژدها را بر كند
entries.english: So that the breath of that one may be defeated by the breath of this one, and that my serpent may destroy that dragon.

entries.farsi: گر رضا دادى رهيدى از دو مار / ور نه از جانت بر آرد آن دما
entries.english: If you submit, you are freed from two serpents; otherwise, it will bring your spirit to utter perdition.”

entries.farsi: گفت الحق سخت استا جادويى / كه در افكندى به مكر اينجا دويى
entries.english: He said, “In truth, you are an exceedingly cunning sorcerer, for by craft you have introduced duality here.

entries.farsi: خلق يكدل را تو كردى دو گروه / ‏جادويى رخنه كند در سنگ و كوه
entries.english: You have made the unanimous people into two factions: sorcery makes fissures in rock and mountain.”

entries.farsi: گفت هستم غرق پيغام خدا / جادويى كى ديد با نام خدا
entries.english: He said, “I am submerged in the message of God: who saw sorcery together with the name of God?

entries.farsi: غفلت و كفر است مايه‏ى جادوى / ‏مشعله‏ى دين است جان موسوى
entries.english: The substance of sorcery is forgetfulness and unbelief: the spirit of Moses is the flaming torch of the religion.

entries.farsi: من به جادويان چه مانم اى وقيح / ‏كاز دمم پر رشك مى‏گردد مسيح
entries.english: How do I resemble sorcerers, O impudent one? For the Messiah is becoming jealous of my breath.

entries.farsi: من به جادويان چه مانم اى جنب / ‏كه ز جانم نور مى‏گيرد كتب
entries.english: How do I resemble sorcerers, O polluted one?— for the Books are receiving light from my spirit.

entries.farsi: چون تو با پر هوا بر مى‏پرى / ‏ال جرم بر من گمان آن مى‏برى
entries.english: Since you are soaring on the wings of sensuality, inevitably you bear that thought against me.”

entries.farsi: هر كه را افعال دام و دد بود / بر كريمانش گمان بد بود
entries.english: Every one whose actions are those of wild beasts has ill thoughts against the noble.

entries.farsi: چون تو جزو عالمى هر چون بوى / ‏كل را بر وصف خود بينى غوى
entries.english: Since you are a part of the world, howsoever you are you deem all to be of the same description as yourself, misguided man.

entries.farsi: گر تو بر گردى و بر گردد سرت / ‏خانه را گردنده بيند منظرت
entries.english: If you whirl round and your head whirl round, your sight sees the house whirling round;

entries.farsi: ور تو در كشتى روى بر يم روان / ‏ساحل يم را همى‏بينى دوان
entries.english: And if you embark in a ship moving on the sea, you deem the seashore to be running.

entries.farsi: گر تو باشى تنگدل از ملحمه / ‏تنگ بينى جو دنيا را همه
entries.english: If you are narrow at heart from combat, you deem the whole atmosphere of the world to be narrow;

entries.farsi: ور تو خوش باشى به كام دوستان / ‏اين جهان بنمايدت چون گلستان
entries.english: And if you are happy as your friends would desire, this world seems to you like a garden of roses.

entries.farsi: اى بسا كس رفته تا شام و عراق / ‏او نديده هيچ جز كفر و نفاق
entries.english: How many a one has gone as far as Syria and ‘Iraq and has seen nothing but unbelief and hypocrisy;

entries.farsi: وى بسا كس رفته تا هند و هرى / ‏او نديده جز مگر بيع و شرى
entries.english: And how many a one has gone as far as India and Herat and seen nothing but selling and buying;

entries.farsi: وى بسا كس رفته تركستان و چين / ‏او نديده هيچ جز مكر و كمين
entries.english: And how many a one has gone as far as Turkistan and China and seen nothing but deceit and hidden guile!

entries.farsi: چون ندارد مدركى جز رنگ و بو / جمله‏ى اقليمها را گو بجو
entries.english: Since he has no object of perception save colour and perfume, let him seek all the climes,

entries.farsi: گاو در بغداد آيد ناگهان / ‏بگذرد او زين سران تا آن سران
entries.english: A cow comes suddenly into Baghdad and passes from this side to that side,

entries.farsi: از همه عيش و خوشيها و مزه / ‏او نبيند جز كه قشر خربزه
entries.english: Of all pleasures and joys and delights she will see nothing but the rind of a water-melon.

entries.farsi: كه بود افتاده بر ره يا حشيش / ‏اليق سيران گاوى يا خريش
entries.english: Straw or hay has fallen on the road, suitable to his bovine or asinine disposition.

entries.farsi: خشك بر ميخ طبيعت چون قديد / بسته‏ى اسباب جانش ال يزيد
entries.english: Dry on the nail of nature, like strips of meat,his spirit, bound with secondary causes, does not grow;

entries.farsi: و آن فضاى خرق اسباب و علل / ‏هست ارض اللهَّ اى صدر اجل
entries.english: But the spacious realm where means and causes are torn to shreds is the earth of God, O most honourable sire.

entries.farsi: هر زمان مبدل شود چون نقش جان / نو به نو بيند جهانى در عيان
entries.english: It is ever changing, like a picture: the spirit beholds in clairvoyance a world anew and anew.

entries.farsi: گر بود فردوس و انهار بهشت / چون فسرده‏ى يك صفت شد گشت زشت
entries.english: Though it is Paradise and the rivers of Eden, becomes ugly when it is congealed in one aspect.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه هر حس مدركى را از آدمى نيز مدركاتى ديگر است كه از مدركات آن حس ديگر بى‏خبر است چنان كه هر پيشه‏ور استاد، اعجمى كار آن استاد دگر پيشه‏ور است و بى‏خبرى او از آن كه وظيفه‏ى او نيست دليل نكند كه آن مدركات نيست، اگر چه به حكم حال منكر بود آن را اما از منكرى ‏او اينجا جز بى‏خبرى نمى‏خواهيم در اين مقام

sections.title_en: Explaining that every percipient sense of man has different objects of perception too, of which the other senses are ignorant, as every skilled craftsman is unfamiliar with the work of those skilled in other crafts; and its ignorance of that which is not its business does not prove that those objects of perception are non-existent. Although it virtually denies them,
yet here in this place we only mean by its ‘denial’ its ignorance.

entries.farsi: مدتى حس را بشو ز آب عيان / ‏اين چنين دان جامه شوى صوفيان
entries.english: Your perception is the measure of your vision of the world: your impure senses are the veil of the pure.

entries.farsi: چنبره‏ى ديد جهان ادراك تست / ‏پرده‏ى پاكان حس ناپاك تست
entries.english: Wash your senses for a while with the water of clairvoyance: know that the garment-washing of the Sufis is like this.

entries.farsi: چون شدى تو پاك پرده بر كند / جان پاكان خويش برتو مى‏زند
entries.english: When you have become purified, the spirit of the pure ones will tear off the veil and attach itself to you.

entries.farsi: جمله عالم گر بود نور و صور / چشم را باشد از آن خوبى خبر
entries.english: If the whole world be light and forms, the eye would be aware of that loveliness.

entries.farsi: چشم بستى گوش مى‏آرى به پيش / ‏تا نمايى زلف و رخساره‏ى بتيش
entries.english: You have shut the eye and are bringing forward the ear that you may show unto it the locks and face of an adorable beauty,

entries.farsi: گوش گويد من به صورت نگروم / ‏صورت ار بانگى زند من بشنوم
entries.english: The ear will say, “I do not attend to the form: if the form utters a cry, I will listen.

entries.farsi: عالمم من ليك اندر فن خويش / ‏فن من جز حرف و صوتى نيست بيش
entries.english: I am skilled, but in my own art: my art is a word or sound, no more.”

entries.farsi: هين بيا بينى ببين اين خوب را / نيست در خور بينى اين مطلوب را
entries.english: “Hey, nose, come and see this beauteous one,” the nose is not fit for this purpose.

entries.farsi: گر بود مشك و گالبى بو برم / ‏فن من اين است و علم و مخبرم
entries.english: “If there be any musk or rose-water, I will smell it: this is my art and science and knowledge.

entries.farsi: كى ببينم من رخ آن سيم ساق / ‏هين مكن تكليف ما ليس يطاق
entries.english: How should I see the face of that silver-calved one? Take heed, do not lay as a task that which cannot be done.”

entries.farsi: باز حس كژ نبيند غير كژ / خواه كژ غژ پيش او يا راست غژ
entries.english: Again, the crooked sense has naught but crooked perception, go crookedly into His presence or go straight, as you will.

entries.farsi: چشم احول از يكى ديدن يقين / ‏دان كه معزول است اى خواجه‏ى معين
entries.english: Know for sure that the eye of him who sees double is remote from seeing the Unity, O Khwaja who aides.

entries.farsi: تو كه فرعونى همه مكرى و زرق / ‏مر مرا از خود نمى‏دانى تو فرق
entries.english: You who are a Pharaoh are wholly deceit and hypocrisy: you know no difference between me and yourself.

entries.farsi: منگر از خود در من اى كژ باز تو / تا يكى تو را نبينى تو دو تو
entries.english: Do not regard me from yourself, O false-playing man, that you may not see the single as double.

entries.farsi: بنگر اندر من ز من يك ساعتى / ‏تا وراى كون بينى ساحتى
entries.english: Regard me from me for one moment, that you may behold a spacious region beyond existence,

entries.farsi: وارهى از تنگى و از ننگ و نام / ‏عشق اندر عشق بينى و السلام
entries.english: And may be delivered from distress, dishonour and renown and behold love within love, and peace!

entries.farsi: پس بدانى چون كه رستى از بدن / گوش و بينى چشم مى‏داند شدن
entries.english: Then, when you have been delivered from the body, you will know that ear and nose can become eye.

entries.farsi: راست گفته است آن شه شيرين زبان / ‏چشم گردد مو به موى عارفان
