entries.english: That sweet-tongued king has said with truth that every hair of gnostics becomes an eye.

entries.farsi: چشم را چشمى نبود اول يقين / ‏در رحم بود او جنين گوشتين
entries.english: Certainly the eye had no eye at first: it was an embryo of flesh in the womb.

entries.farsi: علت ديدن مدان پيه اى پسر / ور نه خواب اندر نديدى كس صور
entries.english: Do not deem the fat to be the cause of sight, O son; otherwise none would see forms in dream.

entries.farsi: آن پرى و ديو مى‏بيند شبيه / ‏نيست اندر ديدگاه هر دو پيه
entries.english: The genie and the demon see the like, and there is no fat in the sight-organ of either.

entries.farsi: نور را با پيه خود نسبت نبود / نسبتش بخشيد خالق ودود
entries.english: In fact there was no relationship between light and the fat: the loving Creator gave them relationship.

entries.farsi: آدم است از خاك كى ماند به خاك / ‏جنى است از نار بى‏هيچ اشتراك
entries.english: Adam is of earth, how does he resemble earth? The genie is of fire without any participation;

entries.farsi: نيست مانند آى آتش آن پرى / ‏گر چه اصلش اوست چون مى‏بنگرى
entries.english: The genie is not similar to fire, though when you consider it is his origin.

entries.farsi: مرغ از باد است كى ماند به باد / نامناسب را خدا نسبت بداد
entries.english: The bird is of wind, how does it resemble wind? God gave relationship to the unrelated.

entries.farsi: نسبت اين فرعها با اصلها / هست بى‏چون گر چه دادش وصلها
entries.english: The relation of these derivatives to the originals is ineffable, although He connected them.

entries.farsi: آدمى چون زاده‏ى خاك هباست / ‏اين پسر را با پدر نسبت كجاست
entries.english: Since man is born of dust, where is the relation between this son and his father?

entries.farsi: نسبتى گر هست مخفى از خرد / هست بى‏چون و خرد كى پى برد
entries.english: If there is a relation hidden from the understanding, it is ineffable, and how should the understanding follow its track?

entries.farsi: باد را بى‏چشم اگر بينش نداد / فرق چون مى‏كرد اندر قوم عاد
entries.english: If He did not give the wind vision without eye, how was it making a distinction among the people of Ad?

entries.farsi: چون همى‏دانست مومن از عدو / چون همى‏دانست مى را از كدو
entries.english: How did it know the true believer from the enemy? How did it know the wine from the gourd-shaped goblet?

entries.farsi: آتش نمرود را گر چشم نيست / ‏با خليلش چون تجشم كردنى است
entries.english: If the fire of Nimrod has no eye, how is a taking pains towards Abraham?

entries.farsi: گر نبودى نيل را آن نور و ديد / از چه قبطى را ز سبطى مى‏گزيد
entries.english: If the Nile had not possessed that light and sight, wherefore should it have picked out the Egyptians from the Israelites?

entries.farsi: اين زمين را گر نبودى چشم جان / ‏از چه قارون را فرو خورد آن چنان
entries.english: If mountain and rock had not been endowed with sight, then how should it have become a friend to David?

entries.farsi: گر نه كوه و سنگ با ديدار شد / پس چرا داود را او يار شد
entries.english: If this earth had not possessed a spiritual eye, wherefore should it have swallowed Qárún in such a fashion?

entries.farsi: گر نبودى چشم دل حنانه را / چون بديدى هجر آن فرزانه را
entries.english: If the Moaning Pillar had not possessed the eye of the heart, how should it have seen the separation of that august one?

entries.farsi: سنگ ريزه گر نبودى ديده‏ور / چون گواهى دادى اندر مشت در
entries.english: If the gravel had not been possessed of an eye, how should it have given testimony in the closed fist?

entries.farsi: اى خرد بر كش تو پر و بالها / سوره بر خوان زلزلت زلزالها
entries.english: O intellect, unfold your wings and pinions: read the Sura, when the earth shall be caused to quake with a mighty quaking.

entries.farsi: اين فرستادن مرا پيش تو مير / هست برهانى كه بد مرسل خبير
entries.english: At the Resurrection how should this earth give testimonies concerning good and evil without having seen?

entries.farsi: در قيامت اين زمين بر نيك و بد / كى ز ناديده گواهيها دهد
entries.english: For she will relate her experiences and informations: the earth will reveal her secrets to us.

entries.farsi: كه تحدث حالها و اخبارها / تظهر الارض لنا اسرارها
entries.english: “This sending of me to you, O prince, is a clear evidence that the Sender was aware

entries.farsi: كاين چنين دارو چنين ناسور را / هست در خور از پى ميسور را
entries.english: That such a medicine as this is suitable to such a desperate malady, for the purpose of success.

entries.farsi: واقعاتى ديده بودى پيش از اين / ‏كه خدا خواهد مرا كردن گزين
entries.english: Previously you had seen visions that God would choose me out,

entries.farsi: من عصا و نور بگرفته به دست / ‏شاخ گستاخ ترا خواهم شكست
entries.english: I, having taken the rod and the Light in my hand, would break your insolent horn.

entries.farsi: واقعات سهمگين از بهر اين / ‏گونه گونه مى‏نمودت رب دين
entries.english: On this account the Lord of the Judgement was showing unto you terrible visions of diverse sorts,

entries.farsi: در خور سر بد و طغيان تو / تا بدانى كاوست در خور دان تو
entries.english: Suitable to your evil conscience and your inordinate disobedience, that you might know that He knows what is proper for you;

entries.farsi: تا بدانى كاو حكيم است و خبير / مصلح امراض درمان ناپذير
entries.english: That you might know that He is wise and omniscient and the healer of irremediable maladies.

entries.farsi: تو به تاويالت مى‏گشتى از آن / ‏كور و كر كاين هست از خواب گران
entries.english: Through false interpretations you were made blind and deaf to those visions, saying, ‘This is caused by heavy slumber’;

entries.farsi: و آن طبيب و آن منجم در لمع / ‏ديد تعبيرش بپوشيد از طمع
entries.english: And the physician and the astrologer in flashes saw the explanation of it, but concealed it from greedy motives.

entries.farsi: گفت دور از دولت و از شاهى‏ات / ‏كه در آيد غصه در آگاهى‏ات
entries.english: He said, ‘Far be it from your empire and kingship that anxiety should enter into your consciousness.

entries.farsi: از غذاى مختلف يا از طعام / ‏طبع شوريده همى‏بيند منام
entries.english: When the constitution is indisposed by food that disagrees with it or by rich delicacies, it sees visions in sleep.’

entries.farsi: ز انكه ديد او كه نصيحت جو نه‏اى / ‏تند و خون‏خوارى و مسكين خو نه‏اى
entries.english: Because he perceived that you are not one that desires good counsel, and that you are violent and blood-drinking and not of lowly nature.

entries.farsi: پادشاهان خون كنند از مصلحت / ‏ليك رحمتشان فزون است از عنت
entries.english: Kings shed blood for righteousness’ sake, but their mercy is greater than their severity.

entries.farsi: شاه را بايد كه باشد خوى رب / ‏رحمت او سبق دارد بر غضب
entries.english: The king must have the nature of the Lord: His mercy has precedence over His wrath.

entries.farsi: نه غضب غالب بود مانند ديو / بى‏ضرورت خون كند از بهر ريو
entries.english: Wrath must not prevail, like the Devil, he sheds blood unnecessarily for the purpose of guile;

entries.farsi: نه حليمى مخنث‏وار نيز / كه شود زن روسپى ز آن و كنيز
entries.english: Nor, again, an effeminate mildness, for in consequence of that his wife and handmaids will become harlots.

entries.farsi: ديو خانه كرده بودى سينه را / قبله‏اى سازيده بودى كينه را
entries.english: You had made your breast a house for the Devil, you had made hatred a qibla.

entries.farsi: شاخ تيزت بس جگرها را كه خست / ‏نك عصايم شاخ شوخت را شكست
entries.english: Many are the hearts which your sharp horn has wounded: lo, my rod has broken your forward horn.

sections.title_fa: حمله بردن اين جهانيان بر آن جهانيان و تاختن بردن تا سينور ذر و نسل كه سر حد غيب است و غفلت ايشان از كمين كه چون غازى به غزا نرود كافر تاختن آورد
sections.title_en: How the people of this world attack the people of that world and charge as far as the frontier,
namely, generation and propagation, which is the boundary of the Unseen, and how they are unaware of the ambush; for the infidel makes his assault when the holy warrior does not go to war.

entries.farsi: تا فرو گيرند بر در بند غيب / ‏تا كسى نايد از آن سو پاك جيب
entries.english: The army of the corporeal ones attacked in the direction of the fortress and stronghold of the spiritual ones,

entries.farsi: حمله بردند اسپه جسمانيان / ‏جانب قلعه و دژ روحانيان
entries.english: In order that they might occupy the frontier-pass of the Unseen, so that no pure spirit should come from that quarter

entries.farsi: غازيان حمله‏ى غزا چون كم برند / كافران بر عكس حمله آورند
entries.english: When the holy warriors do not attack in warfare, the infidels on the contrary deliver an attack.

entries.farsi: غازيان غيب چون از حلم خويش / ‏حمله ناوردند بر تو زشت كيش
entries.english: When the holy warriors of the Unseen in their forbearance refrained from delivering an attack on you, man of evil practice,

entries.farsi: حمله بردى سوى در بندان غيب / ‏تا نيايند اين طرف مردان غيب
entries.english: You made an attack towards the frontier-passes of the Unseen, in order that the men of the Unseen should not come in this direction.

entries.farsi: چنگ در صلب و رحمها در زدى / ‏تا كه شارع را بگيرى از بدى
entries.english: You did lay a hand upon the loins and wombs, that you might wickedly occupy the thoroughfare of sexual intercourse.

entries.farsi: سد شدى در بندها را اى لجوج / ‏كورى تو كرد سرهنگى خروج
entries.english: How should you occupy the highway which the Almighty has opened for the purpose of procreation?

entries.farsi: چون بگيرى شه رهى كه ذو الجلال / ‏بر گشاده‏ست از براى انتسال
entries.english: You did block the passes, O rebellious one, in despite of you a captain issued forth.

entries.farsi: نك منم سرهنگ هنگت بشكنم / ‏نك به نامش نام و ننگت بشكنم
entries.english: Lo, I am the captain: I will break your power. Lo, in His name I will break your name and fame.

entries.farsi: تو هال در بندها را سخت بند / چند گاهى بر سبال خود بخند
entries.english: Come now, close the passes tightly! Laugh at your moustache a while!

entries.farsi: سبلتت را بر كند يك يك قدر / تا بدانى كالقدر يعمى الحذر
entries.english: The Divine decree will tear out your moustache piecemeal, that you may know that the Decree makes precaution blind.

entries.farsi: سبلت تو تيزتر يا آن عاد / كه همى‏لرزيد از دمشان بالد
entries.english: Is your moustache fiercer than that of Ad, at whose breath the lands used to tremble?

entries.farsi: تو ستيزه روترى يا آن ثمود / كه نيامد مثل ايشان در وجود
entries.english: Are you more contentious in aspect or Thamud, the like of whom never came into existence?

entries.farsi: صد از اينها گر بگويم تو كرى / ‏بشنوى و ناشنوده آورى
entries.english: Though I tell a hundred of these, you are deaf: you hear and pretend not to have heard.

entries.farsi: توبه كردم از سخن كانگيختم / ‏بى‏سخن من داروت آميختم
entries.english: I repent of the words which I raised up: without words, I have mixed for you a medicine

entries.farsi: كه نهم بر ريش خامت تا پزد / يا بسوزد ريش و ريشه‏ات تا ابد
entries.english: Which I will place upon your raw sore, that it may be assuaged, or that your sore and your beard may be burnt unto everlasting,

entries.farsi: تا بدانى كه خبير است اى عدو / مى‏دهد هر چيز را در خورد او
entries.english: To the end that you may know that He is omniscient, O enemy: He gives to everything that which befits it.

entries.farsi: كى كژى كردى و كى كردى تو شر / كه نديدى اليقش در پى اثر
entries.english: When have you done wrong and when have you wrought evil but you have seen the effect befitting it?

entries.farsi: كى فرستادى دمى بر آسمان / ‏نيكيى كز پى نيامد مثل آن
entries.english: When have you once sent a good deed to Heaven but the like thereof has followed after?

entries.farsi: گر مراقب باشى و بيدار تو / بينى هر دم پاسخ كردار تو
entries.english: If you will be observant and vigilant, you will see at every moment the response to your action.

entries.farsi: چون مراقب باشى و گيرى رسن / ‏حاجتت نايد قيامت آمدن
entries.english: When you are observant and do grasp the cord, you need not the coming of the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: آن كه رمزى را بداند او صحيح / ‏حاجتش نايد كه گويندش صريح
entries.english: He that truly knows an indication does not need to have it plainly declared to him.

entries.farsi: اين بال از كودنى آيد ترا / كه نكردى فهم نكته و رمزها
entries.english: This tribulation befalls you from stupidity in not understanding the subtle hints and indications.

entries.farsi: از بدى چون دل سياه و تيره شد / فهم كن اينجا نشايد خيره شد
entries.english: When your heart has been blackened and darkened by wickedness, understand! One ought not to become besotted here;

entries.farsi: ور نه خود تيرى شود آن تيرگى / ‏در رسد در تو جزاى خيرگى
entries.english: Otherwise, in truth, that darkness will become an arrow, and the penalty of foolishness will overtake you.

entries.farsi: ور نيايد تير از بخشايش است / ‏نه پى ناديدن آاليش است
entries.english: And if the arrow doesn’t come, it is from bounty; due to not seeing the defilement

entries.farsi: هين مراقب باش گر دل بايدت / ‏كز پى هر فعل چيزى زايدت
entries.english: Listen; be observant if you would have a heart, for something is born to you in consequence of every action.

entries.farsi: ور ازين افزون ترا همت بود / از مراقب كار بالاتر رود
entries.english: And if you have an aspiration greater than this, the enterprise goes beyond the observant,

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه تن خاكى آدمى همچون آهن نيكو جوهر قابل آيينه‏شدن است تا در او هم در دنيا بهشت و ‏دوزخ و قيامت و غير آن معاينه بنمايد نه بر طريق خيال
sections.title_en: Explaining that the earthen body of man, like iron of fine substance, is capable of becoming a mirror, so that therein even in this world Paradise and Hell and the Resurrection et cetera are shown by immediate vision, not in the mode of phantasy.

entries.farsi: پس چو آهن گر چه تيره هيكلى / ‏صيقلى كن صيقلى كن صيقلى
entries.english: Then, though you are dark-bodied like iron, make a practice of polishing, polishing, polishing,

entries.farsi: تا دلت آيينه گردد پر صور / اندر او هر سو مليحى سيم بر
entries.english: That your heart may become a mirror full of images, a lovely therein on every side.

entries.farsi: آهن ار چه تيره و بى‏نور بود / صيقلى آن تيرگى از وى زدود
entries.english: Although the iron was dark and devoid of light, polishing cleared away the darkness from it.

entries.farsi: صيقلى ديد آهن و خوش كرد رو / تا كه صورتها توان ديدن در او
entries.english: The iron saw the polishing and made its face fair, so that images could be seen there.

entries.farsi: گر تن خاكى غليظ و تيره است / ‏صيقلش كن ز انكه صيقل‏گيره است
entries.english: If the earthen body is gross and dark, polish it— for it is receptive to the polishing instrument—

entries.farsi: تا در او اشكال غيبى رو دهد / عكس حورى و ملك در وى جهد
entries.english: In order that the forms of the Unseen may appear in it, and that the reflection of houri and angel may dart into it.

entries.farsi: صيقل عقلت بدان داده‏ست حق / ‏كه بدو روشن شود دل را ورق
entries.english: God has given you the polishing instrument, Reason, to the end that thereby the leaf of the heart may be made resplendent.

entries.farsi: صيقلى را بسته‏اى اى بى‏نماز / و آن هوا را كرده‏اى دو دست باز
entries.english: You, O prayerless man, have put the polisher in bonds and have loosed the two hands of sensuality.

entries.farsi: گر هوا را بند بنهاده شود / صيقلى را دست بگشاده شود
entries.english: If bonds be put on sensuality, the hand of the polisher will be untied.

entries.farsi: آهنى كايينه‏ى غيبى بدى / ‏جمله صورتها در او مرسل شدى
entries.english: A piece of iron that became a mirror of the Unseen— all the forms would be shot into it.

entries.farsi: تيره كردى زنگ دادى در نهاد / اين بود يسعون فى الارض الفساد
entries.english: You made your heart dark and did let the rust into your nature: this is they work evil on the earth.

entries.farsi: برمشوران تا شود اين آب صاف / ‏و اندر او بين ماه و اختر در طواف
entries.english: So have you done till now: now do it not. You have made the water turbid: do not make it more.

entries.farsi: تا كنون كردى چنين اكنون مكن / ‏تيره كردى آب را افزون مكن
entries.english: Do not stir it up: let this water become clear, and behold the moon and stars circling there.

entries.farsi: ز انكه مردم هست همچون آب جو / چون شود تيره نبينى قعر او
entries.english: For man is like the water of the river: when it becomes turbid, you cannot see its bottom.

entries.farsi: قعر جو پر گوهر است و پر ز در / هين مكن تيره كه هست اوصاف حر
entries.english: The bottom of the river is full of jewels and full of pearls: take heed, do not make turbid, for it is pure and free.

entries.farsi: جان مردم هست مانند هوا / چون به گرد آميخت شد پرده‏ى سما
entries.english: The spirit of man resembles air: when it is mixed with dust, it veils the sky,

entries.farsi: مانع آيد او ز ديد آفتاب / ‏چون كه گردش رفت شد صافى و ناب
entries.english: And prevents from seeing the sun; when its dust is gone, it becomes pure and undefiled.

entries.farsi: با كمال تيرگى حق واقعات / ‏مى‏نمودت تا روى راه نجات
entries.english: Notwithstanding your complete darkness, God was showing you visions that you might travel the way of deliverance.

sections.title_fa: باز گفتن موسى عليه السلام اسرار فرعون را و واقعات او را ظهر الغيب تا به خبيرى حق ايمان آورد يا گمان برد
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, declared from the Unseen the secret thoughts and visions of Pharaoh, in order that he might truly believe in the omniscience of God or hold that opinion.

entries.farsi: ز آهن تيره به قدرت مى‏نمود / واقعاتى كه در آخر خواست بود
entries.english: From the dark iron He, by His power, was showing forth the visions that should come to pass in the end,

entries.farsi: تا كنى كمتر تو آن ظلم و بدى / ‏آن همى‏ديدى و بدتر مى‏شدى
entries.english: In order that you might lessen that injustice and wickedness: you were seeing those and becoming wicked.

entries.farsi: نقشهاى زشت خوابت مى‏نمود / مى‏رميدى ز آن و آن نقش تو بود
entries.english: He was showing unto you hideous forms in dream: you were shrinking back from them, and they were your form;

entries.farsi: همچو آن زنگى كه در آيينه ديد / روى خود را زشت و بر آيينه ريد
entries.english: Like the Ethiopian who saw in the mirror that his face was ugly, and defecated on the mirror,

entries.farsi: اين حدث بر روى زشتت مى‏كنى / ‏نيست بر من ز انكه هستم روشنى
entries.english: Saying, ‘How ugly you are! You are deserving only of this.’, ‘My ugliness belongs to you, O vile blind one.

entries.farsi: كه چه زشتى اليق اينى و بس / ‏زشتيم آن تو است اى كور خس
entries.english: You are putting this filth on your ugly face: it is not on me, for I have splendour.’

entries.farsi: گاه مى‏ديدى لباست سوخته / ‏گه دهان و چشم تو بر دوخته
entries.english: At one time you were seeing your raiment burnt; at another time your mouth and eyes stitched up;

entries.farsi: گاه حيوان قاصد خونت شده / ‏گه سر خود را به دندان دده
entries.english: Now an animal seeking your blood; now your head in the teeth of a wild beast;

entries.farsi: گه نگون اندر ميان آب ريز / گه غريق سيل خون آميز تيز
entries.english: Now upside down in the midst of a latrine; now sunk in a fierce blood-dyed torrent;

entries.farsi: گه ندات آمد از اين چرخ نقى / ‏كه شقيى و شقيى و شقى
entries.english: Now from this pure heaven came to you a voice crying, ‘You are damned, you are damned, damned’;

entries.farsi: گه ندات آمد صريحا از جبال / ‏كه برو هستى ز اصحاب الشمال
entries.english: Now from the mountains came to you a voice, plainly, go! You are one of the people of the left hand’;

entries.farsi: گه ندا مى‏آمدت از هر جماد / تا ابد فرعون در دوزخ فتاد
entries.english: Now from every inanimate thing was coming to you a voice, ‘Pharaoh is fallen into Hell for evermore’;

entries.farsi: زين بترها كه نمى‏گويم ز شرم / ‏تا نگردد طبع معكوس تو گرم
entries.english: Worse things than these, which from shame I will not tell, lest your perverted nature become hot.

entries.farsi: اندكى گفتم به تو اى ناپذير / ز اندكى دانى كه هستم من خبير
entries.english: I have told you a little, O you who will not accept: from a little you may know that I am acquainted.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را كور مى‏كردى و مات / ‏تا نينديشى ز خواب و واقعات
entries.english: You were making yourself blind and dead, that you might not bethink you of the dreams and visions.

entries.farsi: چند بگريزى نك آمد پيش تو / كورى ادراك مكر انديش تو
entries.english: How long will you flee? Lo, it is come to you in despite of your guile-meditating perception.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان آن كه در توبه باز است
sections.title_en: Explaining that the door of repentance is open

entries.farsi: هين مكن زين پس فراگير احتراز / كه ز بخشايش در توبه است باز
entries.english: Listen, do not act henceforth, take precaution, for through bounty the door of repentance is open.

entries.farsi: تا ز مغرب بر زند سر آفتاب / ‏باز باشد آن در از وى رو متاب
entries.english: From the quarter of the West a door of repentance is open to mankind till the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: توبه را از جانب مغرب درى / ‏باز باشد تا قيامت بر ورى
entries.english: Till the sun lifts up its head from the West, that door is open: do not avert your face from it.

entries.farsi: هست جنت را ز رحمت هشت در / يك در توبه‏ست ز آن هشت اى پسر
entries.english: By the mercy Paradise has eight doors: one of those eight is the door of repentance, O son.

entries.farsi: آن همه گه باز باشد گه فراز / و آن در توبه نباشد جز كه باز
entries.english: All the others are sometimes open, sometimes shut; and never is the door of repentance but open.

entries.farsi: هين غنيمت دار در باز است زود / رخت آن جا كش به كورى حسود
entries.english: Come, seize the opportunity: the door is open: carry your baggage thither at once in despite of the envious.

sections.title_fa: ‏گفتن موسى عليه السلام فرعون را كه از من يك پند قبول كن و چهار فضيلت عوض بستان
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, said to Pharaoh, “Accept one counsel from me and take four excellent qualities as recompense.”

entries.farsi: هين ز من بپذير يك چيز و بيار / پس ز من بستان عوض آن را چهار
entries.english: Come, accept from me one thing and bring, and then take from me four as recompense for that.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى كدام است آن يكى / ‏شرح كن با من از آن يك اندكى
entries.english: He replied, “O Moses, what is that one thing? Explain to me a little about that one thing.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن يك كه بگويى آشكار / كه خدايى نيست غير كردگار
entries.english: “That one thing,” said he, “is that you should say publicly that there is no god but the Maker,

entries.farsi: خالق افلاک و انجم بر عال / مردم و ديو و پرى و مرغ را
entries.english: The Creator of the heavenly spheres and of the stars on high and of man and devil and genie and bird,

entries.farsi: خالق دريا و دشت و كوه و تيه / ‏ملكت او بى‏حد و او بى‏شبيه
entries.english: The Creator of sea and plain and mountain and desert: His sovereignty is without limit and He is without like.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى كدام است آن چهار / كه عوض بدهى مرا بر گو بيار
entries.english: He said, “O Moses, what are those four things that you will give me in recompense? Declare and bring,

entries.farsi: تا بود كز لطف آن وعده‏ى حسن / ‏سست گردد چهار ميخ كفر من
entries.english: That perchance, by the favour of that goodly promise, the crucifixion of my unbelief may be assuaged.

entries.farsi: بو كه از تاثير جوى انگبين / ‏شهد گردد در تنم اين زهر كين
entries.english: Perchance the lock of my hundred kilos weight of unbelief may be opened by those fair and desirable promises.

entries.farsi: بو كه ز آن خوش وعده‏هاى مغتنم / ‏بر گشايد قفل كفر صد منم
entries.english: Perchance, by the effect of the river of honey, this poison of hatred may be turned into honey in my body;

entries.farsi: يا ز عكس جوى آن پاكيزه شير / پرورش يابد دمى عقل اسير
entries.english: Or by the reflection of the river of that pure milk, captive intelligence may be nourished for a moment;

entries.farsi: يا بود كز عكس آن جوهاى خمر / مست گردم بو برم از ذوق امر
entries.english: Or perchance, by the reflection of those rivers of wine, I may be intoxicated and obtain a scent of the delight of the command;

entries.farsi: يا بود كز لطف آن جوهاى آب / ‏تازگى يابد تن شوره‏ى خراب
entries.english: Or perchance, by the favour of those rivers of water, my barren devastated body may gain refreshment—

entries.farsi: شوره‏ام را سبزه‏اى پيدا شود / خار زارم جنت ماوى شود
entries.english: Some verdure may appear on my barren soil, my thorn-brake may become the Garden of abode;

entries.farsi: بو كه از عكس بهشت و چار جو / جان شود از يارى حق يار جو
entries.english: Perchance, by the reflection of Paradise and the four rivers, my spirit, through God’s befriending, may become a seeker of the Friend,

entries.farsi: آن چنانك از عكس دوزخ گشته‏ام / ‏آتش و در قهر حق آغشته‏ام
entries.english: In the same fashion as from the reflection of Hell I have become fire and am steeped in the wrath of God.

entries.farsi: گه ز عكس مار دوزخ همچو مار / گشته‏ام بر اهل جنت زهر بار
entries.english: At one moment, from the reflection of the snake of Hell I have become dropping poison, like a snake, on those who shall dwell in Paradise;

entries.farsi: من ز عكس زمهريرم زمهرير / يا ز عكس آن سعيرم چون سعير
entries.english: At another time, from the reflection of the boiling of the hot water, the water of my oppression has made the people rotten bones.

entries.farsi: گه ز عكس جوشش آب حميم / ‏آب ظلمم كرده خلقان را رميم
entries.english: From the reflection of the cold of hell (zamharír) I am the zamharír; or from the reflection of the sa‘ír I am as the sa‘ír.

entries.farsi: دوزخ درويش و مظلومم كنون / ‏واى آن كه يابمش ناگه زبون
entries.english: I am now the Hell of the poor and oppressed: woe to him whom I suddenly find subject!”

sections.title_fa: ‏شرح كردن موسى عليه السلام آن چهار فضيلت را جهت پاى مزد ايمان فرعون
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, explained those four excellent qualities as a reward for Pharaoh’s coming into the Faith.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى كاولين آن چهار / صحتى باشد تنت را پايدار
entries.english: Moses said, “The first of those four will be constant health for your body:

entries.farsi: اين عللهايى كه در طب گفته‏اند / دور باشد از تنت اى ارجمند
entries.english: These maladies that are described in Medicine will be far from your body, O estimable one.

entries.farsi: ثانيا باشد ترا عمر دراز / كه اجل دارد ز عمرت احتراز
entries.english: Secondly, you will have a long life, for death will be cautious of your life;

entries.farsi: وين نباشد بعد عمر مستوى / ‏كه به ناكام از جهان بيرون روى
entries.english: And after a life uniform this will not be that you will go forth from the world against your will;

entries.farsi: بلكه خواهان اجل چون طفل شير / نه ز رنجى كه ترا دارد اسير
entries.english: Nay, but desiring death as the sucking babe, not on account of the pain that holds you captive

entries.farsi: مرگ جو باشى ولى نه از عجز رنج / ‏بلكه بينى در خراب خانه گنج
entries.english: You will be seeking death, but not from painful infirmity; nay, you will see the treasure in the ruin of the house.

entries.farsi: پس به دست خويش گيرى تيشه‏اى / ‏مى‏زنى بر خانه بى‏انديشه‏اى
entries.english: Therefore with your own hand you will take a pick-axe and smite upon the house without any care;

entries.farsi: كه حجاب گنج بينى خانه را / مانع صد خرمن اين يك دانه را
entries.english: For you will deem the house to be the barrier to the treasure, and this single grain to be the obstacle to a hundred wheat-stacks.

entries.farsi: پس در آتش افكنى اين دانه را / پيش گيرى پيشه‏ى مردانه را
entries.english: This grain, then, you will cast into the fire and adopt the profession that is worthy of a man.”

entries.farsi: اى به يك برگى ز باغى مانده / ‏همچو كرمى برگش از رز رانده
entries.english: O you who because of a single leaf have been left without an orchard, you are like the worm which a leaf has driven away from the vineyard.

entries.farsi: چون كرم اين كرم را بيدار كرد / اژدهاى جهل را اين كرم خورد
entries.english: When Grace awakened this worm, this worm devoured the dragon of ignorance.

entries.farsi: كرم كرمى شد پر از ميوه و درخت / ‏اين چنين تبديل گردد نيك بخت
entries.english: The worm became a vineyard full of fruit and trees: even so is the blessed man transformed.

sections.title_fa: ‏تفسير كنت كنزا مخفيا فاحببت ان اعرف
sections.title_en: Exposition of “I was a hidden treasure, and I desired to be known”

entries.farsi: خانه بر كن كاز عقيق اين يمن / ‏صد هزاران خانه شايد ساختن
entries.english: Demolish the house, for a hundred thousand houses may be made from the cornelian of this Yemen.

entries.farsi: گنج زير خانه است و چاره نيست / ‏از خرابى خانه منديش و مه‏ايست
entries.english: The treasure lies beneath the house, and there is no help: do not be afraid of destroying the house and do not stand still,

entries.farsi: كه هزاران خانه از يك نقد گنج / ‏تان عمارت كرد بى‏تكليف و رنج
entries.english: For from one treasure in hand it is possible to build a thousand houses without suffering toil and pain.

entries.farsi: عاقبت اين خانه خود ويران شود / گنج از زيرش يقين عريان شود
entries.english: In the end this house will fall of itself into ruin and the treasure beneath it will certainly be uncovered;

entries.farsi: ليك آن تو نباشد ز انكه روح / ‏مزد ويران كردن استش آن فتوح
entries.english: But it will not be your, since the spirit receives that gift as wages for destroying.

entries.farsi: چون نكرد آن كارمزدش هست ال / ‏لَيْسَ لِلإِْنْسانِ إِلاَّ ما سعى
entries.english: When it has not done that work, its wages are naught: there is nothing for Man but that which he wrought.

entries.farsi: دست خايى بعد از آن تو كاى دريغ / ‏اين چنين ماهى بد اندر زير ميغ
entries.english: After that, you will gnaw your hand, saying, “Alas, a moon like this was under the cloud.

entries.farsi: من نكردم آن چه گفتند از بهى / ‏گنج رفت و خانه و دستم تهى
entries.english: I did not do the good which they told: the treasure and the house are gone, and my hand is empty.”

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى اجرت گرفتى و كرى / ‏نيست ملك تو به بيعى يا شرى
entries.english: You have rented and hired a house: it is not your property by any act of sale or purchase.

entries.farsi: اين كرى را مدت او تا اجل / ‏تا در اين مدت كنى در وى عمل
entries.english: The period of this hiring is till death, in order that you may work in it during this period.

entries.farsi: پاره دوزى مى‏كنى اندر دكان / ‏زير اين دكان تو مدفون دو كان
entries.english: You are sewing patches in the shop, under this shop of your two mines are buried.

entries.farsi: هست اين دكان كرايى زود باش / ‏تيشه بستان و تكش را مى‏تراش
entries.english: This shop is held on hire: be quick, take the pick-axe and break up its foundation,

entries.farsi: تا كه تيشه ناگهان بر كان نهى / ‏از دكان و پاره دوزى وارهى
entries.english: That of a sudden you may lay the axe on the mine and be delivered from the shop and from patch-sewing.

entries.farsi: پاره دوزى چيست خورد آب و نان / ‏مى‏زنى اين پاره بر دلق گران
entries.english: What is patch-sewing? The drinking of water and the eating of bread: you are applying these patches to the heavy cloak.

entries.farsi: هر زمان مى‏درد اين دلق تنت / ‏پاره بر وى مى‏زنى زين خوردنت
entries.english: This cloak, your body, is always being torn, and you are patching it by this eating and drinking of yours.

entries.farsi: اى ز نسل پادشاه كاميار / با خود آ زين پاره دوزى ننگ دار
entries.english: O you who are of the progeny of the fortunate King, come to yourself, be ashamed of this patch-sewing.

entries.farsi: پاره‏اى بر كن از اين قعر دكان / ‏تا بر آرد سر به پيش تو دو كان
entries.english: Tear a patch from off this shop-floor, in order that two mines may lift up their head before you,

entries.farsi: پس ترا بيرون كند صاحب دكان / ‏وين دكان را بر كند از روى كان
entries.english: Before this lease of the hired house come to an end without your having gained any profit from it.

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كاين مهلت خانه‏ى كرى / ‏آخر آيد تو نخورده زو برى
entries.english: Then the owner of the shop will turn you out and will demolish this shop for the sake of the mine,

entries.farsi: تو ز حسرت گاه بر سر مى‏زنى / ‏گاه ريش خام خود بر مى‏كنى
entries.english: You at one moment will beat your head in remorse and at another tear your foolish beard,

entries.farsi: كاى دريغا آن من بود اين دكان / ‏كور بودم بر نخوردم زين مكان
entries.english: Saying, “Alas, this shop was mine; I was blind and got no profit from this place of abode.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا بود ما را برد باد / تا ابد يا حسرتا شد للعباد
entries.english: Alas, the wind swept our existence away: O sorrow for the servants of God is come unto everlasting.

sections.title_fa: ‏غره شدن آدمى به ذكاوت و تصويرات طبع خويشتن و طلب ناكردن علم غيب كه علم انبياست
sections.title_en: How Man is deluded by the sagacity and imaginations of his nature and does not seek knowledge of the Unseen, which is the knowledge possessed by the prophets

entries.farsi: ديدم اندر خانه من نقش و نگار / بودم اندر عشق خانه بى‏قرار
entries.english: I saw pictures and paintings in the house: I was without self-control in love of the house.

entries.farsi: بودم از گنج نهانى بى‏خبر / ور نه دستنبوى من بودى تبر
entries.english: I was unaware of any hidden treasure; otherwise, the axe would have been the pomander in my hand.

entries.farsi: آه گر داد تبر را دادمى / ‏اين زمان غم را تبرا دادمى
entries.english: Ah, if I had given the axe its due, I should now have given a quittance to grief.

entries.farsi: چشم را بر نقش مى‏انداختم / ‏همچو طفالن عشقها مى‏باختم
entries.english: I was casting my eye on the picture and falling idly in love, like children.”

entries.farsi: پس نكو گفت آن حكيم كاميار / كه تو طفلى خانه پر نقش و نگار
entries.english: That fortunate Sage, then, has said well, “You are a child: the house is full of pictures and paintings.”

entries.farsi: در الهى نامه بس اندرز كرد / كه بر آر از دودمان خويش گرد
entries.english: In the Iláhí-náma he gave many an injunction, saying, “Raise the dust from your own household.”

entries.farsi: بس كن اى موسى بگو وعده‏ى سوم / ‏كه دل من ز اضطرابش گشت گم
entries.english: “Enough; O Moses! Tell the third promise, for my heart has become lost from the agitation caused by it.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى آن سوم ملك دو تو / دو جهانى خالص از خصم و عدو
entries.english: Moses said, “This third is a twofold empire— appertaining to the two worlds, free from adversary and enemy;

entries.farsi: بيشتر ز آن ملك كاكنون داشتى / ‏كان بد اندر جنگ و اين در آشتى
entries.english: Greater than the empire of which you are now in possession; for that was at war, and this at peace.

entries.farsi: آن كه در جنگت چنان ملكى دهد / بنگر اندر صلح خوانت چون نهد
entries.english: He who bestows on you, at war, such an empire as this— consider how He will lay the table for you (when you are) at peace.

entries.farsi: آن كرم كاندر جفا آنهات داد / در وفا بنگر چه باشد افتقاد
entries.english: That bounty which gave you those things in your unrighteousness— consider what will be care in your faithfulness.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى چهارم چيست زود / باز گو صبرم شد و حرصم فزود
entries.english: “O Moses,” said he, “what is the fourth promise? Quickly declare: my patience is gone and my desire has waxed great.”

entries.farsi: گفت چارم آن كه مانى تو جوان / ‏موى همچون قير و رخ چون ارغوان
entries.english: He said, “The fourth is that you will remain young, hair like pitch and cheeks like the arghawán (flower of the Judas-tree).

entries.farsi: رنگ و بو در پيش ما بس كاسد است / ‏ليك تو پستى سخن كرديم پست
entries.english: To us colour and perfume are very worthless, but you are low, we have made our words low.

entries.farsi: افتخار از رنگ و بو و از مكان / ‏هست شادى و فريب كودكان
entries.english: Boasting of colour and perfume and dwelling-place is a joy and deception to children.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان اين خبر كه كلموا الناس على قدر عقولهم ال على قدر عقولكم حتى ال يكذب اللهَّ و رسوله
sections.title_en: [Explanation of the Tradition, “Speak you unto men according to the measure of their understandings, not according to the measure of your understandings,
so that God and His messenger may not be given the lie.”]

entries.farsi: كه برو كتاب تا مرغت خرم / ‏يا مويز و جوز و فستق آورم
entries.english: Since my business happens to be with a child, I must accordingly speak the language suited to children,

entries.farsi: چون كه با كودك سر و كارم فتاد / هم زبان كودكان بايد گشاد
entries.english: Saying, ‘Go to school, that I may buy a bird for you or bring raisins and walnuts and pistachio nuts’

entries.farsi: جز شباب تن نمى‏دانى بگير / اين جوانى را بگير اى خر شعير
entries.english: You know only the youth of the body: take this youthfulness: take the barley, O ass!

entries.farsi: هيچ آژنگى نيفتد بر رخت / ‏تازه ماند آن شباب فرخت
entries.english: No wrinkle will fall upon your face: your fortunate youthfulness will remain fresh.

entries.farsi: نه نژند پيريت آيد به رو / نه قد چون سرو تو گردد دو تو
entries.english: Neither will the withering of old age come over your countenance, nor will your cypress-like figure be double;

entries.farsi: نه شود زور جوانى از تو كم / ‏نه به دندانها خللها يا الم
entries.english: Nor will the strength of youth vanish from you, nor will your teeth decay or be in pain;

entries.farsi: نه كمى در شهوت و طمث و بعال / ‏كه زنان را آيد از ضعفت مالل
entries.english: Nor will there be a deficiency in sexual desire, intercourse, and marital union so that there will be weariness for woman because of your weakness.

entries.farsi: آن چنان بگشايدت فر شباب / ‏كه گشود آن مژده‏ى عكاشه باب
entries.english: The glory of youth will be opened to you in such wise as the good tidings brought by ‘Ukkásha opened the door.

sections.title_fa: قوله عليه السلام من بشرنى بخروج صفر بشرته بالجنة
sections.title_en: The saying of the Prophet, on whom be peace, “Whosoever shall bring me the glad news of the expiration of Safar, I will give him the glad news of Paradise.”

entries.farsi: احمد آخر زمان را انتقال / ‏در ربيع اول آيد بى‏جدال
entries.english: The decease of Ahmad, of the last time, will indisputably occur in the First Rabí‘.

entries.farsi: چون خبر يابد دلش زين وقت نقل / ‏عاشق آن وقت گردد او به عقل
entries.english: When his heart shall gain knowledge of this moment of decease, he will become intellectually in love with that moment,

entries.farsi: چون صفر آيد شود شاد از صفر / كه پس اين ماه مى‏سازم سفر
entries.english: And when Safar comes, he will rejoice on account of Safar, saying, ‘After this month I will make the journey.’”

entries.farsi: هر شبى تا روز زين شوق هدى / ‏اى رفيق راه اعلى مى‏زدى
entries.english: From this longing for guidance he was crying, every night till daybreak, “O most High Companion on the Way!”

entries.farsi: گفت آن كس كه مرا مژده دهد / چون صفر پاى از جهان بيرون نهد
entries.english: He said, “Any person who gives me the good news, when Safar steps forth from this world,

entries.farsi: كه صفر بگذشت و شد ماه ربيع / ‏مژده‏ور باشم مر او را و شفيع
entries.english: That Safar is past and that the month of Rabí is come— for him I will be a bearer of good news and an intercessor.”

entries.farsi: گفت عكاشه صفر بگذشت و رفت / ‏گفت كه جنت ترا اى شير زفت
entries.english: ‘Ukkásha said, “Safar is past and gone.” He said, “O mighty lion, Paradise is yours.”

entries.farsi: ديگرى آمد كه بگذشت آن صفر / گفت عكاشه ببرد از مژده بر
entries.english: Someone else came, saying, “Safar is past.” He said, “‘Ukkásha has borne away the fruit for the good news.”

entries.farsi: پس رجال از نقل عالم شادمان / ‏و ز بقايش شادمان اين كودكان
entries.english: Men, therefore, rejoice in the world’s departing, while these children rejoice in its abiding.

entries.farsi: چون كه آب خوش نديد آن مرغ كور / پيش او كوثر نمايد آب شور
entries.english: Inasmuch as the blind bird did not see the sweet water, the briny water seems to it Kawthar.

entries.farsi: همچنين موسى كرامت مى‏شمرد / كه نگردد صاف اقبال تو درد
entries.english: Thus was Moses enumerating the grace, saying, “The pure of your fortune will not be turned into dregs.”

entries.farsi: گفت احسنت و نكو گفتى و ليك / ‏تا كنم من مشورت با يار نيك
entries.english: He said, “You have done well and spoken well, but that I may take counsel with good friend.”

sections.title_fa: ‏مشورت كردن فرعون با ايسيه در ايمان آوردن به موسى عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh took counsel with Asiya as to believing in Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: باز گفت او اين سخن با ايسيه / ‏گفت جان افشان بر اين اى دل سيه
entries.english: He related these words to Asiya. She said, “Offer up your soul to this, O black-hearted one,

entries.farsi: بس عنايتهاست متن اين مقال / ‏زود درياب اى شه نيكو خصال
entries.english: At the back of this speech are many favours: enjoy quickly, O virtuous king!

entries.farsi: وقت كشت آمد زهى پر سود كشت / ‏اين بگفت و گريه كرد و گرم گشت
entries.english: The hour of sowing is come: bravo, a profitable sowing!” She said this and wept and became hot.

entries.farsi: بر جهيد از جا و گفتا بخ لك / ‏آفتابى تاج گشتت اى كلك
entries.english: She sprang up from her place and said, “Blessed are you! A sun has become a tiara for you, O poor bald man.

entries.farsi: عيب كل را خود بپوشاند كاله / ‏خاصه چون باشد كله خورشيد و ماه
entries.english: A cap in truth covers the defect of the baldpate, especially when the cap is the sun and moon.

entries.farsi: هم در آن مجلس كه بشنيدى تو اين / ‏چون نگفتى آرى و صد آفرين
entries.english: In that very chamber where you heard this, how did not you say ‘Yes’ and a hundred expressions of praise?

entries.farsi: اين سخن در گوش خورشيد ارشدى / ‏سر نگون بر بوى اين زير آمدى
entries.english: If these words had entered into the ear of the sun, it would have come down headlong in hope of this.

entries.farsi: هيچ مى‏دانى چه وعده‏ست و چه داد / مى‏كند ابليس را حق افتقاد
entries.english: Do you understand at all what the promise is and what the gift is? God is showing solicitude for Iblis.

entries.farsi: چون بدين لطف آن كريمت باز خواند / اى عجب چون زهره‏ات بر جاى ماند
entries.english: When that gracious One called you back so kindly, oh, it is a wonder how your heart remained unmoved,

entries.farsi: زهره‏ات ندريد تا ز آن زهره‏ات / ‏بودى اندر هر دو عالم بهره‏ات
entries.english: Your heart was not burst, so that, by means of that heart of yours, there might accrue to you the portion in the two worlds.

entries.farsi: زهره‏اى كز بهره‏ى حق بر درد / چون شهيدان از دو عالم بر خورد
entries.english: The heart that is burst for the sake of God’s portion eats fruit from the two worlds, as the martyrs.

entries.farsi: غافلى هم حكمت است و اين عمى / تا بماند ليك تا اين حد چرا
entries.english: True, heedlessness and this blindness is Divine Wisdom, in order that he may endure; but why to such an extent as this?

entries.farsi: غافلى هم حكمت است و نعمت است / ‏تا نپرد زود سرمايه ز دست
entries.english: True, heedlessness is Divine Wisdom and Bounty, in order that stock-in-trade may not suddenly fly out of hand;

entries.farsi: ليك نى چندان كه ناسورى شود / ز هر جان و عقل رنجورى شود
entries.english: But not so great that it becomes an incurable sore and a poison to the spirit and intellect of one who is sick.

entries.farsi: خود كه يابد اين چنين بازار را / كه به يك گل مى‏خرى گلزار را
entries.english: Who, really, can find bazaars like this where with a single rose you are buying rose-gardens;

entries.farsi: دانه‏اى را صد درختستان عوض / ‏حبه‏اى را آمدت صد كان عوض
entries.english: A hundred groves come to you in exchange for one seed, a hundred mines in exchange for one nickel?

entries.farsi: كان هلل دادن آن حبه است / ‏تا كه كان اللهَّ له آيد به دست
entries.english: Kána lilláh is the giving of that nickel, in order that kána ’lláh lahú may come into hand;

entries.farsi: ز انكه اين هوى ضعيف بى‏قرار / هست شد ز آن هوى رب پايدار
entries.english: For this weak unstable hú was brought into being by the steadfast hú of the Lord.

entries.farsi: هوى فانى چون كه خود با او سپرد / گشت باقى دايم و هرگز نمرد
entries.english: When the hú that passes away has surrendered itself to Him, it becomes everlasting and never dies.

entries.farsi: همچو قطره‏ى خايف از باد و ز خاك / ‏كه فنا گردد بدين هر دو هالك
entries.english: Like a drop of water afraid of wind and earth; for by means of these twain it is made to pass away.

entries.farsi: چون به اصل خود كه دريا بود جست / ‏از تف خورشيد و باد و خاك رست
entries.english: When it has leaped into the sea, which was its source, it is delivered from the heat of the sun and from wind and earth.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش گم گشت در دريا و ليك / ‏ذات او معصوم و پا بر جا و نيك
entries.english: Its outward form has disappeared in the sea, but its essence is inviolate and permanent and goodly.

entries.farsi: هين بده اى قطره خود را بى‏ندم / ‏تا بيابى در بهاى قطره يم
entries.english: Listen, O drop, give yourself up without repenting, that in recompense for the drop you may gain the Ocean.

entries.farsi: هين بده اى قطره خود را اين شرف / ‏در كف دريا شو ايمن از تلف
entries.english: Listen, O drop, bestow upon yourself this honour, and in the hand of the Sea become safe from destruction.

entries.farsi: خود كه را آيد چنين دولت به دست / ‏قطره را بحرى تقاضاگر شده‏ست
entries.english: Whom indeed should fortune like this befall? A Sea has become the suitor for a drop.

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اللهَّ زود بفروش و بخر / قطره‏اى ده بحر پر گوهر ببر
entries.english: In God’s name, in God’s name, sell and buy at once! Give a drop, and take the Sea which is full of pearls.

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اللهَّ هيچ تاخيرى مكن / ‏كه ز بحر لطف آمد اين سخن
entries.english: In God’s name, in God’s name, do not make any postponement, for these words come from the Sea of Grace.

entries.farsi: لطف اندر لطف اين گم مى‏شود / كاسفلى بر چرخ هفتم مى‏شود
entries.english: Grace is lost in this grace that one of the lowest is going up to the Seventh Heaven.

entries.farsi: هين كه يك بازى فتادت بو العجب / ‏هيچ طالب اين نيابد در طلب
entries.english: Listen, for a marvellous falcon has fallen to you: no seeker will find it in search.”

entries.farsi: گفت با هامان بگويم اى ستير / شاه را الزم بود راى وزير
entries.english: He said, “I will tell Haman, O veiled one: the counsel of the vizier is necessary to the king.”

entries.farsi: گفت با هامان مگو اين راز را / كور كمپيرى چه داند باز را
entries.english: She said, “Do not tell Haman this secret: what should a blind decrepit old woman know about a falcon?”

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى باز پادشاه و كمپير زن
sections.title_en: Story of the king’s falcon and the decrepit old woman

entries.farsi: باز اسپيدى به كمپيرى دهى / ‏او ببرد ناخنش بهر بهى
entries.english: You give a white falcon to a decrepit old woman, she will clip its talons for the sake of welfare.

entries.farsi: كه كجا بوده‏ست مادر كه ترا / ناخنان زين سان دراز است اى كيا
entries.english: The blind old woman will blindly clip the talons which are the source of its usefulness in the chase,

entries.farsi: ناخنى كه اصل كار است و شكار / كور كمپيرك ببرد كوروار
entries.english: Saying, “Where has your mother been, that your talons are so long, O prince?”

entries.farsi: ناخن و منقار و پرش را بريد / وقت مهر اين مى‏كند زال پليد
entries.english: She clipped its talons and beak and wings: the filthy old hag does this at the time of affection.

entries.farsi: چون كه تتماجش دهد او كم خورد / خشم گيرد مهرها را بر درد
entries.english: When she gives it stew (tutmaj), it will not eat; she is enraged and tears up her feelings of affection,

entries.farsi: كه چنين تتماج پختم بهر تو / تو تكبر مى‏نمايى و عتو
entries.english: Saying, “I have cooked such stew for you, and you are showing pride and insolence.

entries.farsi: آب تتماجش دهد كاين را بگير / گر نمى‏خواهى كه نوشى ز آن فطير
entries.english: You deserve to be in that trouble and affliction: how should happiness and prosperity be suitable for you?”

entries.farsi: تو سزايى در همان رنج و بال / نعمت و اقبال كى سازد ترا
entries.english: She gives it the stew broth, saying, “Take this, if you do not wish to eat of the pastry.”

entries.farsi: آب تتماجش نگيرد طبع باز / زال بترنجد شود خشمش دراز
entries.english: The falcon’s nature does not accept the stew broth: the old woman frowns, and her anger is prolonged.

entries.farsi: از غضب آن آش سوزان بر سرش / ‏زن فرو ريزد شود كل مغفرش
entries.english: In her rage the woman pours down the burning hot soup on its head: the crown of its head is made bald.

entries.farsi: اشك از آن چشمش فرو ريزد ز سوز / ياد آرد لطف شاه دل فروز
entries.english: On account of the burning pain the tears pour down from its eye: it remembers the kindness of the heart-delighting king.

entries.farsi: ز آن دو چشم نازنين با دالل / ‏كه ز چهره‏ى شاه دارد صد كمال
entries.english: From those two charming coquettish eyes, which possess a hundred perfections from the countenance of the king.

entries.farsi: چشم ما زاغش شده پر زخم زاغ / ‏چشم نيك از چشم بد با درد و داغ
entries.english: Its eye that turned not aside has become full of wounds inflicted by the crow: the good eye is with pain and anguish by the evil eye.

entries.farsi: چشم دريا بسطتى كز بسط او / هر دو عالم مى‏نمايد تار مو
entries.english: An eye with the range of the sea, from the range whereof both the worlds appear a thread of hair

entries.farsi: گر هزاران چرخ در چشمش رود / همچو چشمه پيش قلزم گم شود
entries.english: If thousands of spheres should enter into its eye, they would vanish like a fountain before the ocean.

entries.farsi: چشم بگذشته از اين محسوسها / يافته از غيب بينى بوسها
entries.english: The eye that has passed beyond these objects of sense-perception and won kisses from vision of the Unseen—

entries.farsi: خود نمى‏يابم يكى گوشى كه من / ‏نكته‏اى گويم از آن چشم حسن
entries.english: Verily, I do not find a single ear to which I should tell a mystery concerning that beauteous eye.

entries.farsi: مى‏چكيد آن آب محمود جليل / ‏مى‏ربودى قطره‏اش را جبرئيل
entries.english: The lauded and august water were to trickle, Gabriel would carry off its drops,

entries.farsi: تا بمالد در پر و منقار خويش / ‏گر دهد دستورى‏اش آن خوب كيش
entries.english: That he might rub them on his wings and beak, if that person of goodly practice gives him permission.

entries.farsi: باز گويد خشم كمپير ار فروخت / ‏فر و نور و صبر و علمم را نسوخت
entries.english: The falcon says, “If the anger of the old crone has blazed forth, it has not consumed my glory and splendour and self-denial and knowledge.

entries.farsi: باز جانم باز صد صورت تند / زخم بر ناقه نه بر صالح زند
entries.english: The falcon, my spirit, will still weave a hundred forms: the blow falls on the she-camel, not on Salih.

entries.farsi: صالح از يك دم كه آرد باشكوه / ‏صد چنان ناقه بزايد متن كوه
entries.english: At a single awful breath that Salih heaves, the back of the mountain will bring to birth a hundred such she-camels.”

entries.farsi: دل همى‏گويد خموش و هوش دار / ور نه درانيد غيرت پود و تار
entries.english: Heart is saying, “Be silent and observe discretion; otherwise, the jealousy will end the warp and woof.”

entries.farsi: غيرتش را هست صد حلم نهان / ‏ور نه سوزيدى به يك دم صد جهان
entries.english: His jealousy has a hundred hidden clemencies; else in one moment it would consume a hundred worlds.

entries.farsi: كه كنم با راى هامان مشورت / ‏كاوست پشت ملك و قطب مقدرت
entries.english: Kingly pride seized the place of admonition in him, so that he wrenched his heart away from the bonds of admonition,

entries.farsi: نخوت شاهى گرفتش جاى پند / تا دل خود را ز بند پند كند
entries.english: Saying, “I will take counsel with Haman, for he is the support of the kingdom and the pivot of power.”

entries.farsi: عرق جنسيت چنانش جذب كرد / كان نصيحتها به پيشش گشت سرد
entries.english: The Lord’s veracious witness was the counsellor of Mustafa; Bu Lahab became the counsellor of Bu Jahl.

entries.farsi: مصطفى را رايزن صديق رب / ‏رايزن بو جهل را شد بو لهب
entries.english: The homogeneity rooted in his nature drew him so that those admonishments became irksome to him.

entries.farsi: جنس سوى جنس صد پره پرد / بر خيالش بندها را بر درد
entries.english: Congener flies to congener with a hundred wings and rives bounds asunder in the fancy for him.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى آن زن كه طفل او بر سر ناودان خزيد و خطر افتادن بود و از على عليه السلام چاره جست
sections.title_en: Story of the woman whose child crawled to the top of the water-spout and was in danger of falling; she sought help of ‘Alí Murtadá, may God ennoble his person.

entries.farsi: يك زنى آمد به پيش مرتضى / گفت شد بر ناودان طفلى مرا
entries.english: A woman came to Murtadá and said, “A child belonging to me has gone up on to the water-spout.

entries.farsi: گرش مى‏خوانم نمى‏آيد به دست / ‏ور هلم ترسم كه افتد او به پست
entries.english: If I call it, it will not come to my hands; and if I leave it, I am afraid it will fall to the ground.

entries.farsi: نيست عاقل تا كه دريابد چو ما / گر بگويم كز خطر سوى من آ
entries.english: It is not intelligent, that it should apprehend, like us, if I say, ‘Come to me from the danger.’

entries.farsi: بس نمودم شير و پستان را بدو / او همى‏گرداند از من چشم و رو
entries.english: Moreover, it does not understand signs made by the hand; or if it should understand, it will not listen: this too is bad.

entries.farsi: هم اشارت را نمى‏داند به دست / ‏ور بداند نشنود اين هم بد است
entries.english: Many times have I shown to it the milk and the teat, it always turns its eyes and face away from me.

entries.farsi: از براى حق شماييد اى مهان / ‏دستگير اين جهان و آن جهان
entries.english: For God’s sakes you, O noble ones, are those who give succour in this world and that world—

entries.farsi: زود درمان كن كه مى‏لرزد دلم / ‏كه به درد از ميوه‏ى دل بگسلم
entries.english: Quickly apply the remedy, for my heart is trembling lest I be torn painfully from the fruit of my heart.”

entries.farsi: زن چنان كرد و چو ديد آن طفل او / جنس خود خوش خوش بدو آورد رو
entries.english: He said, “Take another child up to the roof, in order that the boy may see his congener,

entries.farsi: سوى جنس آيد سبك ز آن ناودان / جنس بر جنس است عاشق جاودان
entries.english: And come nimbly from the water-spout to his congener: congener is ever in love with congener.”

entries.farsi: گفت طفلى را بر آور هم به بام / ‏تا ببيند جنس خود را آن غالم
entries.english: The woman did so, and when her child saw its congener, it turned its face towards it with delight

entries.farsi: سوى بام آمد ز متن ناودان / ‏جاذب هر جنس را هم جنس دان
entries.english: And came from the ridge of the water-spout to the roof: know that a congener attracts every congener.

entries.farsi: غژغژان آمد به سوى طفل طفل / وارهيد او از فتادن سوى سفل
entries.english: The child came crawling along to the child: it was saved from falling to below.

entries.farsi: ز آن بود جنس بشر پيغمبران / ‏تا به جنسيت رهند از ناودان
entries.english: The prophets are of humankind for this reason that they, through the homogeneity, may be saved from the water-spout.

entries.farsi: ز انكه جنسيت عجايب جاذبى است / ‏جاذبش جنس است هر جا طالبى است
entries.english: Therefore he called himself a man like you, that you might come to your congener and might not become lost;

entries.farsi: پس بشر فرمود خود را مثلكم / ‏تا به جنس آييد و كم گرديد گم
entries.english: For homogeneity is a wondrous attractor: wherever there is a seeker, his congener is attracting him.

entries.farsi: عيسى و ادريس بر گردون شدند / با ماليك چون كه هم جنس آمدند
entries.english: Jesus and Idrís ascended to heaven, since they were homogeneous with the angels.

entries.farsi: باز آن هاروت و ماروت از بلند / جنس تن بودند ز آن زير آمدند
entries.english: Again, Harut and Marut were homogeneous with the body: hence they descended from on high.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران خوى بد آموخته / ‏ديده‏هاى عقل و دل بر دوخته
entries.english: The infidels have become homogeneous with Satan: their spirits have become disciples of the devils.

entries.farsi: كافران هم جنس شيطان آمده / ‏جانشان شاگرد شيطانان شده
entries.english: They have learned a hundred thousand evil dispositions; they have sewn up the eyes of intellect and heart.

entries.farsi: كمترين خوشان به زشتى آن حسد / آن حسد كه گردن ابليس زد
entries.english: Their least ugly disposition is envy— that envy which smote the neck of Iblis.

entries.farsi: ز آن سگان آموخته حقد و حسد / كه نخواهد خلق را ملك ابد
entries.english: From those curs they have learned hatred and envy, for he does not wish the kingdom everlasting to creatures.

entries.farsi: هر كه را ديد او كمال از چپ و راست / ‏از حسد قولنجش آمد درد خاست
entries.english: When he sees, on left or right, any one perfect, colic comes to him and pain arises from envy,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر بد بخت خرمن سوخته / ‏مى‏نخواهد شمع كس افروخته
entries.english: Because every miserable wretch whose stack has been burnt is unwilling that any one’s candle should be lighted

entries.farsi: هين كمالى دست آور تا تو هم / ‏از كمال ديگران نفتى به غم
entries.english: Listen; bring to hand some perfection, in order that you too may not be aggrieved by the perfection of others.

entries.farsi: از خدا مى‏خواه دفع اين حسد / تا خدايت وارهاند از جسد
entries.english: Beg of God the removal of this envy, that God may deliver you from the body,

entries.farsi: جرعه‏ى مى را خدا آن مى‏دهد / كه بدو مست از دو عالم مى‏رهد
entries.english: And bestow on you an inward occupation, from which you will not become disengaged outwards.

entries.farsi: مر ترا مشغوليى بخشد درون / ‏كه نپردازى از آن سوى برون
entries.english: God gives to a draught of wine such that one intoxicated with it escapes from the two worlds.

entries.farsi: خاصيت بنهاده در كف حشيش / ‏كاو زمانى مى‏رهاند از خوديش
entries.english: He has endowed hashish with the property that, for a time, it delivers him from self-consciousness.

entries.farsi: خواب را يزدان بدان سان مى‏كند / كز دو عالم فكر را بر مى‏كند
entries.english: God makes sleep to be in such a manner that it erases thought of the two worlds.

entries.farsi: كرد مجنون را ز عشق پوستى / ‏كاو بشناسد عدو از دوستى
entries.english: He made Majnun, through love for a skin, to be such that he would not know an enemy from a friend.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران اين چنين مى‏دارد او / كه بر ادراكات تو بگمارد او
entries.english: He has a hundred thousand wines of this sort which He sets over your perceptions.

entries.farsi: هست مى‏هاى شقاوت نفس را / كه ز ره بيرون برد آن نحس را
entries.english: For the carnal soul there are the wines of damnation, which carry that ill-starred one out of the way.

entries.farsi: هست مى‏هاى سعادت عقل را / كه بيابد منزل بى‏نقل را
entries.english: For the intellect there are the wines of felicity, so that it gains the abode whence is no departure.

entries.farsi: خيمه‏ى گردون ز سر مستى خويش / ‏بر كند ز آن سو بگيرد راه پيش
entries.english: Through its intoxication it uproots the tent of the sky and takes the way (leading) onward from that direction.

entries.farsi: هين به هر مستى دال غره مشو / هست عيسى مست حق خر مست جو
entries.english: Listen, be not deceived, O heart, by every intoxication: Jesus is intoxicated with God, the ass is intoxicated with barley.

entries.farsi: اين چنين مى را بجو زين خنبها / مستى‏اش نبود ز كوته دنبها
entries.english: Seek wine like this from these jars: the intoxication by it is not from the bobtailed;

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر معشوق چون خنبى است پر / آن يكى درد و دگر صافى چو در
entries.english: For every object of love is like a full jar, one dregs, and another pure as pearls.

entries.farsi: مى شناسا هين بچش با احتياط / تا ميى يابى منزه ز اختالط
entries.english: O connoisseur of wine, beware, taste with precaution, that you may find a wine free from adulteration.

entries.farsi: هر دو مستى مى‏دهندت ليك اين / ‏مستى‏ات آرد كشان تا رب دين
entries.english: Both will intoxicate you, but this intoxication, drawing, will lead you to the Lord of the Judgement,

entries.farsi: تا رهى از فكر و وسواس و حيل / ‏بى‏عقال اين عقل در رقص الجمل
entries.english: So that you will be delivered from thought and anxiety and expedients, this intellect unshackled at the camel’s ambling pace.

entries.farsi: انبيا چون جنس روحند و ملك / ‏مر ملك را جذب كردند از فلك
entries.english: Since the prophets are homogeneous with spirit and angel, they drew angels from heaven.

entries.farsi: باد جنس آتش است و يار او / كه بود آهنگ هر دو بر علو
entries.english: Wind is the congener and friend of fire, for the tendency of both is upward.

entries.farsi: چون ببندى تو سر كوزه‏ى تهى / ‏در ميان حوض يا جويى نهى
entries.english: When you stop the mouth of an empty pot and put it in a tank or river,

entries.farsi: تا قيامت آن فرو نايد به پست / ‏كه دلش خالى است و در وى باد هست
entries.english: It will not sink till the Resurrection, for its heart is void and there is wind in it.

entries.farsi: ميل بادش چون سوى بالا بود / ظرف خود را هم سوى بالا كشد
entries.english: Since the desire of the wind in it is upward, it draws upward also the vessel containing it.

entries.farsi: باز آن جانها كه جنس انبياست / ‏سوى ايشان كش كشان چون سايه‏هاست
entries.english: Again, the spirits that are homogeneous with the prophets are moving gradually, like shadows, towards them,

entries.farsi: ز انكه عقلش غالب است و بى‏ز شك / ‏عقل جنس آمد به خلقت با ملك
entries.english: Because its intelligence is predominant; and beyond doubt the intelligence is homogeneous in nature with the angel;

entries.farsi: و آن هواى نفس غالب بر عدو / نفس جنس اسفل آمد شد بدو
entries.english: While in the enemy the carnal soul’s concupiscence is predominant: the carnal soul is homogeneous with the lowest and goes to it.

entries.farsi: بود قبطى جنس فرعون ذميم / ‏بود سبطى جنس موساى كليم
entries.english: The Egyptian was a congener of the reprobate Pharaoh; the Israelite was a congener of Moses, the Kalim.

entries.farsi: بود هامان جنس‏تر فرعون را / بر گزيدش برد بر صدر سرا
entries.english: Haman was more congenial to Pharaoh: he chose him out and brought him to the high-seat in the palace.

entries.farsi: الجرم از صدر تا قعرش كشيد / كه ز جنس دوزخند آن دو پليد
entries.english: Inevitably he dragged him from the high-seat to the lowest depth, for those two unclean ones are homogenous with Hell.

entries.farsi: هر دو سوزنده چو دوزخ ضد نور / هر دو چون دوزخ ز نور دل نفور
entries.english: Both, like Hell, are burning and contrary to light: both, like Hell, are exceedingly averse to the light of the heart;

entries.farsi: ز انكه دوزخ گويد اى مومن تو زود / بر گذر كه نورت آتش را ربود
entries.english: For Hell says, “O true believer, pass by quickly, since your light has taken away the Fire.

entries.farsi: بگذر اى مومن كه نورت مى‏كشد / آتشم را چون كه دامن مى‏كشد
entries.english: Pass, O true believer, for your light, when it sweeps by, quenches my fire.”

entries.farsi: مى‏رمد آن دوزخى از نور هم / ‏ز انكه طبع دوزخ استش اى صنم
entries.english: The man destined for Hell, also, is recoiling from the light, because he has the nature of Hell, O worshipful one.

entries.farsi: دوزخ از مومن گريزد آن چنان / ‏كه گريزد مومن از دوزخ به جان
entries.english: Hell flees from the true believer just as the true believer flees with his soul from Hell,

entries.farsi: ز انكه جنس نار نبود نور او / ضد نار آمد حقيقت نور جو
entries.english: Because his light is not homogeneous with the Fire: the seeker of the light is in reality the contrary of the Fire

entries.farsi: در حديث آمد كه مومن در دعا / چون امان خواهد ز دوزخ از خدا
entries.english: It is related in the Hadith that when the true believer prays to God for protection from Hell,

entries.farsi: جاذبه‏ى جنسيت است اكنون ببين / ‏كه تو جنس كيستى از كفر و دين
entries.english: Hell also begs earnestly for protection from him, saying, “O God, keep me far from such-and-such a one!”

entries.farsi: دوزخ از وى هم امان خواهد به جان / ‏كه خدايا دور دارم از فالن
entries.english: ‘It is the attracting power of homogeneity: consider now with whom you are congenial in respect of infidelity or true religion.

entries.farsi: گر به هامان مايلى هامانيى / ‏ور به موسى مايلى سبحانيى
entries.english: If you are inclined towards Haman, you have the nature of Haman, and if you are inclined towards Moses, you are a glorifier of God.

entries.farsi: ور به هر دو مايلى انگيخته / ‏نفس و عقلى هر دوان آميخته
entries.english: And if you are inclined and impelled towards both, you are carnal soul and reason both mingled together.

entries.farsi: هر دو در جنگند هان و هان بكوش / ‏تا شود غالب معانى بر نقوش
entries.english: Both are at war: take heed, take heed, and strive that the spiritual realities may prevail over the forms.

entries.farsi: در جهان جنگ شادى اين بس است / ‏كه ببينى بر عدو هر دم شكست
entries.english: In the world of war it is joy enough that you should always see defeat on the enemy.

entries.farsi: آن ستيزه رو به سختى عاقبت / ‏گفت با هامان براى مشورت
entries.english: Finally that quarrelsome-looking man in his hardness told Haman, for the purpose of consultation.

entries.farsi: وعده‏هاى آن كليم اهلل را / گفت و محرم ساخت آن گمراه را
entries.english: He told the promises of the one with whom God spoke, and made that misguided person his confidant.

sections.title_fa: ‏مشورت كردن فرعون با وزيرش هامان در ايمان آوردن به موسى عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh took counsel with ha vizier, Haman, as to believing in Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت با هامان چو تنهايش بديد / جست هامان و گريبان را دريد
entries.english: He told Haman when he saw him alone: Haman sprang up and rent the bosom of his shirt.

entries.farsi: بانگها زد گريه‏ها كرد آن لعين / ‏كوفت دستار و كله را بر زمين
entries.english: That accursed one uttered loud cries and sobs and beat his turban and cap on the ground,

entries.farsi: كه چگونه گفت اندر روى شاه / ‏اين چنين گستاخ آن حرف تباه
entries.english: Saying, “How dare he say those vain words so impudently in the face of the king?

entries.farsi: جمله عالم را مسخر كرده تو / كار را با بخت چون زر كرده تو
entries.english: You have made the whole world subject; you, by fortune, have made your estate as gold.

entries.farsi: از مشارق و ز مغارب بى‏لجاج / ‏سوى تو آرند سلطانان خراج
entries.english: From all parts of the East and West sultans, without opposition, bring tribute to you.

entries.farsi: پادشاهان لب همى‏مالند شاد / بر ستانه‏ى خاك تو اى كيقباد
entries.english: Kings are rubbing their lips joyfully on the dust of your threshold, O mighty emperor.

entries.farsi: اسب ياغى چون ببيند اسب ما / رو بگرداند گريزد بى‏عصا
entries.english: When the enemy’s horse sees our horse, it turns its face and flees without flogging’.

entries.farsi: تا كنون معبود و مسجود جهان / ‏بوده‏اى گردى كمينه‏ى بندگان
entries.english: Until now you have been worshipped and adored by the world: you will become the meanest of slaves.

entries.farsi: در هزار آتش شدن زين خوشتر است / ‏كه خداوندى شود بنده پرست
entries.english: To go into a thousand fires is better than this; that a lord should become the servant of a slave.

entries.farsi: نه بكش اول مرا اى شاه چين / نه بكش اول مرا اى شاه چين
entries.english: Nay, kill me first, O king of China, that my eye may not behold this in the king.

entries.farsi: خسروا اول مرا گردن بزن / ‏تا نبيند اين مذلت چشم من
entries.english: O emperor, behead me first, that my eye may not behold this ignominy.

entries.farsi: خود نبودست و مبادا اين چنين / ‏كه زمين گردون شود گردون زمين
entries.english: Truly never has there been—and never may there be!—such a thing as this, that the earth should become the sky, and the sky become the earth;

entries.farsi: بندگان‏مان خواجه‏تاش ما شوند / بى‏دالن‏مان دل خراش ما شوند
entries.english: Our slaves should become our fellow-servants; our timorous ones should become those who wound our hearts;

entries.farsi: چشم روشن دشمنان و دوست كور / گشت ما را پس گلستان قعر گور
entries.english: Enemies bright-eyed and friends blind: then the rose-garden has become for us the bottom of the tomb.”

sections.title_fa: ‏تزييف سخن هامان عليه اللعنه
sections.title_en: Showing the falsity of Haman’s speech— the curse be upon him!

entries.farsi: دوست از دشمن همى‏نشناخت او / نرد را كورانه كژ مى‏باخت او
entries.english: He did not know friend from enemy: he was playing backgammon wrong, like a blind man.

entries.farsi: دشمن تو جز تو نبود اى لعين / ‏بى‏گناهان را مگو دشمن به كين
entries.english: Your enemy is none but yourself, O accursed one: do not spitefully call the innocent enemies.

entries.farsi: پيش تو اين حالت بد دولت است / ‏كه دوادو اول و آخر لت است
entries.english: In your sight this evil state is dawlat, whereof the beginning is dawádaw and the end lat (blows).

entries.farsi: گر از اين دولت نتازى خزخزان / ‏اين بهارت را همى‏آيد خزان
entries.english: If by degrees you do not run away from this worldly fortune, autumn will come o’er this spring of yours.

entries.farsi: مشرق و مغرب چو تو بس ديده‏اند / كه سر ايشان ز تن ببريده‏اند
entries.english: East and West have seen many like you, whose heads have been severed from their bodies.

entries.farsi: مشرق و مغرب كه نبود برقرار / چون كنند آخر كسى را پايدار
entries.english: After all, how should East and West, which are not permanent, make any one enduring?

entries.farsi: تو بدان فخر آورى كز ترس و بند / چاپلوست گشت مردم روز چند
entries.english: You take pride in the fact that men, from fear and bondage, have become your flatterers for a few days.

entries.farsi: هر كه را مردم سجودى مى‏كنند / زهر اندر جان او مى‏آگنند
entries.english: When men bow in adoration to any one, they are cramming poison into his soul.

entries.farsi: چون كه بر گردد از او آن ساجدش / ‏داند او كان زهر بود و موبدش
entries.english: When his adorer turns away from him, he knows that that was poisonous and destructive to him.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن را كه ذلت نفسه / واى آنك از سركشى شد چون كه او
entries.english: Oh, blest is he whose carnal soul was abased! Alas who became like a mountain from arrogance

entries.farsi: اين تكبر زهر قاتل دان كه هست / ‏از مى پر زهر شد آن گيج مست
entries.english: Know that this pride is a killing poison: that fool is intoxicated by the poisonous wine.

entries.farsi: چون مى پر زهر نوشد مدبرى / ‏از طرب يك دم بجنباند سرى
entries.english: When an unhappy wretch drinks the poisonous wine, his head in delight for one moment

entries.farsi: بعد يك دم زهر بر جانش فتد / زهر در جانش كند داد و ستد
entries.english: After one moment the poison falls on his spirit the poison exercises sway over his spirit.

entries.farsi: گر ندارى زهرى‏اش را اعتقاد / كاو چو زهر آمد نگر در قوم عاد
entries.english: If you have not firm belief in its being poisonous what poison it is, look at the people of Ad

entries.farsi: ون كه شاهى دست يابد بر شهى / ‏بكشدش يا باز دارد در چهى
entries.english: When one king gains the upper hand over another king, he kills him or confines him in a dungeon;

entries.farsi: ور بيابد خسته‏ى افتاده را / مرهمش سازد شه و بدهد عطا
entries.english: But if he finds a fallen wounded man, the king will make a plaster for him and bestow gifts on him.

entries.farsi: گر نه زهر است آن تكبر پس چرا / كشت شه را بى‏گناه و بى‏خطا
entries.english: If that pride is not poison, then why did he kill the king without crime or offence?

entries.farsi: وين دگر را بى‏ز خدمت چون نواخت / ‏زين دو جنبش زهر را شايد شناخت
entries.english: And how did he treat this other man kindly without service? From these two actions you may recognise pride.

entries.farsi: راه زن هرگز گدايى را نزد / گرگ گرگ مرده را هرگز گزد
entries.english: No highwayman ever attacked a beggar: does a wolf ever bite a dead wolf?

entries.farsi: خضر كشتى را براى آن شكست / ‏تا تواند كشتى از فجار رست
entries.english: Khizr made a breach in the boat in order that the boat might be saved from the wicked.

entries.farsi: چون شكسته مى‏رهد اشكسته شو / امن در فقر است اندر فقر رو
entries.english: Since the broken one will be saved, be you broken. Safety lies in poverty enter into poverty

entries.farsi: آن كهى كاو داشت از كان نقد چند / گشت پاره پاره از زخم كلند
entries.english: The mountain that possessed some cash in its mine was cut to pieces by the strokes of the pick-axe.

entries.farsi: تيغ بهر اوست كاو را گردنى است / ‏سايه كافكنده ست بر وى زخم نيست
entries.english: The sword is for him who has a neck; no blow falls on the shadow that is thrown.

entries.farsi: مهترى نفت است و آتش اى غوى / ‏اى برادر چون بر آذر مى‏روى
entries.english: Eminence is naphtha and fire, O misguided one: O brother, how are you going into the fire?

entries.farsi: هر چه او هموار باشد با زمين / ‏تيرها را كى هدف گردد ببين
entries.english: How should anything that is level with the earth become a target for arrows? Consider!

entries.farsi: سر بر آرد از زمين آن گاه او / چون هدفها زخم يابد بى‏رفو
entries.english: If it raises its head from the earth, then, like targets, it will suffer blows irremediable.

entries.farsi: نردبان خلق اين ما و منى است / ‏عاقبت زين نردبان افتادنى است
entries.english: This egoism is the ladder of the creatures: they must fall from this ladder in the end.

entries.farsi: هر كه بالاتر رود ابله‏تر است / ‏كاستخوان او بتر خواهد شكست
entries.english: The higher any one goes, the more foolish he is, for his bones will be worse broken.

entries.farsi: اين فروع است و اصولش آن بود / كه ترفع شركت يزدان بود
entries.english: This is the derivatives, and its fundamental principles are that to exalt one’s self is co-partnership with God.

entries.farsi: چون بدو زنده شدى آن خود وى است / ‏وحدت محض است آن شركت كى است
entries.english: Unless you have died and become living through Him, you are an enemy seeking to reign in co-partnership with Him

entries.farsi: چون نمردى و نگشتى زنده زو / ياغيى باشى به شركت ملك جو
entries.english: When you have become living through Him that is in truth He: it is absolute Unity; how is it co partnership?

entries.farsi: شرح اين در آينه‏ى اعمال جو / كه نيابى فهم آن از گفت‏وگو
entries.english: Seek the explanation of this in the mirror of works, for you will not gain the understanding of it from speech and discourse.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم آن چه دارم در درون / بس جگرها گردد اندر حال خون
entries.english: If I tell that which I have within, many hearts will immediately be turned into blood,

entries.farsi: بس كنم خود زيركان را اين بس است / ‏بانگ دو كردم اگر در ده كس است
entries.english: I will refrain; indeed, for the intelligent this is enough: I have shouted twice, if anyone is in the village

entries.farsi: حاصل آن هامان بدان گفتار بد / اين چنين راهى بر آن فرعون زد
entries.english: To sum up, Haman by means of those evil words waylaid Pharaoh in such a way as this.

entries.farsi: لقمه‏ى دولت رسيده تا دهان / ‏او گلوى او بريده ناگهان
entries.english: The morsel, felicity, had reached his mouth, he suddenly cut his throat.

entries.farsi: رمن فرعون را داد او به باد / هيچ شه را اين چنين صاحب مباد
entries.english: He gave Pharaoh’s stack to the wind: may no king have such a minister!

sections.title_fa: ‏نوميد شدن موسى عليه السلام از ايمان فرعون به تاثير كردن سخن هامان در دل فرعون
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, despaired of Pharaoh’s accepting the true faith, because the words of Haman made an impression on Pharaoh’s heart.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى لطف بنموديم و جود / خود خداونديت را روزى نبود
entries.english: Moses said, “We have shown kindness and generosity, truly it was not the portion allotted to your dominion

entries.farsi: آن خداوندى كه نبود راستين / مر و را نه دست دان نه آستين
entries.english: The dominion that is not righteous— regard it as having neither hand nor sleeve

entries.farsi: آن خداوندى كه دزديده بود / بى‏دل و بى‏جان و بى‏ديده بود
entries.english: The dominion that is stolen is without heart and without soul and without eye.

entries.farsi: آن خداوندى كه دادندت عوام / ‏باز بستانند از تو همچو وام
entries.english: The dominion which the common have given to you they will take back from you as a debt.

entries.farsi: ده خداوندى عاريت به حق / آن اميران عرب گرد آمدند
entries.english: Give up to God the dominion held on loan, that He may bestow on you the dominion to which all consent.”

sections.title_fa: منازعت اميران عرب با مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام كه ملك را مقاسمت كن با ما تا نزاعى نباشد ‏و جواب فرمودن مصطفى (ص) كه من مأمورم در اين امارت و بحث ايشان از طرفين
sections.title_en: How the Amírs of the Arabs wrangled with Mustafa, on whom be peace, saying, “Share the kingdom with us, in order that there may be no contention”; and how Mustafa,
on whom be peace, answered and said, “I am commanded in respect of this Amírate”; and the arguments on both sides.

entries.farsi: نزد پيغمبر منازع مى‏شدند / كه تو ميرى هر يك از ما هم امير
entries.english: The Amírs of the Arabs assembled and began to wrangle in the Prophet’s presence,

entries.farsi: بخش كن اين ملك و بخش خود بگير / هر يكى در بخش خود انصاف جو
entries.english: Saying, “You are an Amír; every one of us is an Amír likewise: distribute this kingdom and take your share.

entries.farsi: تو ز بخش ما دو دست خود بشو / گفت ميرى مر مرا حق داده است
entries.english: Each is seeking equity in regard to his share: do you wash your hands of our share.”

entries.farsi: ‏سرورى و امر مطلق داده است / كاين قرآن احمد است و دور او
entries.english: He replied, “God has given the Amírate to me: He has given me the chief authority and the absolute command,

entries.farsi: هين بگيريد امر او را اتقوا / قوم گفتندش كه ما هم ز آن قضا
entries.english: Saying, ‘this is the epoch and cycle of Ahmad: listen, accept his command! Have fear of God!’”

entries.farsi: حاكميم و داد اميرى‏مان خدا / گفت ليكن مر مرا حق ملك داد
entries.english: The party said to him, “We too are rulers by that destiny, and God has given the Amírate to us.”

entries.farsi: مر شما را عاريت از بهر زاد / ميرى من تا قيامت باقى است
entries.english: He said, “But to me God gave it as a possession and to you as a loan for the sake of provisions for the road.

entries.farsi: چيست حجت بر فزون جويى تو / در زمان ابرى بر آمد ز امر مر
entries.english: My Amírate is lasting till the Resurrection; the Amírate held on loan will be shattered.”

entries.farsi: ‏ميرى عاريتى خواهد شكست / قوم گفتند اى امير افزون مگو
entries.english: The party said, “O Amír, do not say too much: what is your argument for seeking more?”

entries.farsi: سيل آمد گشت آن اطراف پر / رو به شهر آورد سيل بس مهيب
entries.english: Forthwith, by the bitter command, a cloud arose, the torrent came: the countryside was filled.

entries.farsi: ‏اهل شهر افغان كنان جمله رعيب / گفت پيغمبر كه وقت امتحان
entries.english: The exceedingly frightful torrent set its face towards the town: the townsfolk making loud lamentations all terrified.

entries.farsi: ‏آمد اكنون تا گمان گردد عيان / هر اميرى نيزه‏ى خود در فكند
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Now the time is come for the test, in order that opinion may become ocular vision.”

entries.farsi: تا شود در امتحان آن سيل بند / پس قضيب انداخت در وى مصطفى
entries.english: Each Amír flung his lance, that in the test it might become a barrier against the torrent.

entries.farsi: آن قضيب معجز فرمان روا / نيزه‏ها را همچو خاشاكى ربود
entries.english: Then Mustafa cast his wand upon it— that sovereign wand that reduced to helplessness.

entries.farsi: آب تيز سيل پر جوش عنود / نيزه‏ها گم گشت جمله و آن قضيب
entries.english: The rapid water of the boiling wild torrent swept away the lances like a bit of straw.

entries.farsi: ‏بر سر آب ايستاده چون رقيب / ز اهتمام آن قضيب آن سيل زفت
entries.english: All the lances disappeared, while that wand stood on the surface of the water like a sentry.

entries.farsi: ‏رو بگردانيد و آن سيالب رفت / چون بديدند از وى آن امر عظيم
entries.english: From anxious regard for that wand the mighty torrent turned its face away, and the flood-water departed.

entries.farsi: ‏پس مقر گشتند آن ميران ز بيم / جز سه كس كه حقد ايشان چيره بود
entries.english: When they beheld that great matter wrought by him, those Amírs, by dread, confessed—

entries.farsi: ساحرش گفتند و كاهن از جحود / ملك بر بسته چنان باشد ضعيف
entries.english: Save three persons, whose rancour was prevailing: they, from disbelief, called him a magician and truthsayer.

entries.farsi: ‏ملك بر رسته چنين باشد شريف / نيزه‏ها را گر نديدى با قضيب
entries.english: The kingship that has been tied on is weak like that; the kingship that has grown up is august like this.

entries.farsi: ‏نامشان بين نام او بين اى نجيب / نامشان را سيل تيز مرگ برد
entries.english: If you did not see the lances together with the wand, consider the names of them and consider the name of him, O noble one!

entries.farsi: نام او و دولت تيزش نمرد / پنج نوبت مى‏زنندش بر دوام
entries.english: Their names the rapid torrent of death has borne away; his name and his puissant fortune are not dead.
