entries.farsi: ور خرى آورده‏ام خر را عصا / آن چنان زين آخورت بيرون كنم
entries.english: For him the drum is always beaten five times: on this wise every day till the Day of Resurrection.

entries.farsi: ‏كز عصا گوش و سرت پر خون كنم
entries.english: “If you have intelligence, I have done kindnesses; and if you are an ass, I have brought the rod for the ass.

entries.farsi: ‏همچنين هر روز تا روز قيام / گر ترا عقل است كردم لطفها
entries.english: I will turn you out of this stable in such wise that I will make your ears and head bloody with the rod.

entries.farsi: اندرين آخور خران و مردمان / ‏مى‏نيابند از جفاى تو امان
entries.english: In this stable asses and men are getting no quarter from your oppression.

entries.farsi: نك عصا آورده‏ام بهر ادب / ‏هر خرى را كاو نباشد مستحب
entries.english: Lo, I have brought the rod, for correction’s sake, for every ass that is not approved.

entries.farsi: اژدهايى مى‏شود در قهر تو / كاژدهايى گشته‏اى در فعل و خو
entries.english: It will become a dragon in subduing you, for you have become a dragon in deeds and disposition.

entries.farsi: اژدهاى كوهيى تو بى‏امان / ‏ليك بنگر اژدهاى آسمان
entries.english: You are a mountain-dragon without mercy; but look at the dragon of Heaven!

entries.farsi: اين عصا از دوزخ آمد چاشنى / ‏كه هال بگريز اندر روشنى
entries.english: This rod comes as a taste from Hell, saying, ‘Ho! Take refuge in the Light;

entries.farsi: ور نه درمانى تو در دندان من / ‏مخلصت نبود ز در بندان من
entries.english: Else you will be left helpless in my teeth: there will be no escape for you through my passes.’

entries.farsi: اين عصايى بود اين دم اژدهاست / ‏تا نگويى دوزخ يزدان كجاست
entries.english: This was a rod; it is now a dragon, to the end that you may not say, ‘Where is God’s Hell?’”

sections.title_fa: ‏در بيان آن كه شناساى قدرت حق نپرسد كه بهشت و دوزخ كجاست
sections.title_en: Explaining that one who knows the power of God will not ask, “Where are Paradise and Hell?”

entries.farsi: هر كجا خواهد خدا دوزخ كند / اوج را بر مرغ دام و فخ كند
entries.english: God makes Hell to be wherever He will: He makes the zenith to be a snare and trap for the bird.

entries.farsi: هم ز دندانت بر آيد دردها / تا بگويى دوزخ است و اژدها
entries.english: Likewise from your teeth arise pangs of pain, to the end that you may say, “It is Hell and the dragon.”

entries.farsi: يا كند آب دهانت را عسل / ‏تا بگويى كه بهشت است و حلل
entries.english: Or He makes the water of your mouth to be honey, that you may say, “’It is Paradise and the robes.”

entries.farsi: از بن دندان بروياند شكر / تا بدانى قوت حكم قدر
entries.english: He makes sugar to grow from the roots of the teeth, that you may know the power of the ordinance of the decree.

entries.farsi: پس به دندان بى‏گناهان را مگز / فكر كن از ضربت نامحترز
entries.english: Do not, then, bite the innocent with your teeth: bethink you of the stroke that is not to be guarded against.

entries.farsi: نيل را بر قبطيان حق خون كند / سبطيان را از بال محصون كند
entries.english: God makes the Nile to be blood for the Egyptians; He makes the Israelites safe from calamity,

entries.farsi: تا بدانى پيش حق تمييز هست / ‏در ميان هوشيار راه و مست
entries.english: That you may know that with God there is discrimination between the sober on the Way and the intoxicated.

entries.farsi: نيل تمييز از خدا آموخته‏ست / ‏كه گشاد اين را و آن را سخت بست
entries.english: The Nile has learned from God to discriminate, for it opened for these and shut fast against those.

entries.farsi: در جماد از لطف عقلى شد پديد / و ز نكال از عاقالن دانش رميد
entries.english: His grace makes the Nile intelligent; His wrath makes Cain foolish.

entries.farsi: در جمادات از كرم عقل آفريد / عقل از عاقل به قهر خود بريد
entries.english: He, from kindness, created intelligence in lifeless things; He, because of His wrath cut off intelligence from the intelligent one.

entries.farsi: لطف او عاقل كند مر نيل را / قهر او ابله كند قابيل را
entries.english: By grace intelligence appeared in lifeless matter, and through chastisement knowledge fled from the intelligent.

entries.farsi: عقل چون باران به امر آن جا بريخت / ‏عقل اين سو خشم حق ديد و گريخت
entries.english: There, by command the rain-like intelligence poured down; here, intelligence saw God’s anger and took to flight.

entries.farsi: ابر و خورشيد و مه و نجم بلند / جمله بر ترتيب آيند و روند
entries.english: Clouds and sun and moon and lofty stars, all come and go according to arrangement.

entries.farsi: هر يكى نايد مگر در وقت خويش / ‏كه نه پس ماند ز هنگام و نه پيش
entries.english: None comes but at its appointed hour, so that it neither lags behind the time nor before.

entries.farsi: چون نكردى فهم اين را ز انبيا / دانش آوردند در سنگ و عصا
entries.english: How have not you understood this from the prophets? They brought knowledge into stone and rod,

entries.farsi: تا جمادات دگر را بى‏لباس / ‏چون عصا و سنگ دارى از قياس
entries.english: That you, by analogy, might undoubtingly deem the other lifeless things to be like rod and stone.

entries.farsi: طاعت سنگ و عصا ظاهر شود / و ز جمادات دگر مخبر شود
entries.english: The obedience of stone and rod is made manifest and gives information concerning the other lifeless things,

entries.farsi: كه ز يزدان آگهيم و طايعيم / ‏ما همه بى‏اتفاقى ضايعيم
entries.english: That, “We are cognisant of God and obedient: we all are not by chance and in vain.

entries.farsi: همچو آب نيل دانى وقت غرق / ‏كاو ميان هر دو امت كرد فرق
entries.english: As the water of the Nile: you know that at the time of drowning it made a distinction between the two peoples;

entries.farsi: چون زمين دانيش دانا وقت خسف / ‏در حق قارون كه قهرش كرد و نسف
entries.english: As the earth: you know it to be possessed of knowledge, at the time of sinking, in regard to Qárún whom He subdued and swept away;

entries.farsi: چون قمر كه امر بشنيد و شتافت / ‏پس دو نيمه گشت بر چرخ و شكافت
entries.english: As the moon, which heard the command and hastened and then became two halves in the sky and split;

entries.farsi: چون درخت و سنگ كاندر هر مقام / مصطفى را كرده ظاهر السلام
entries.english: As the trees and stones which everywhere overtly made the salaam to Mustafa.

sections.title_fa: جواب دهرى كه منكر الوهيت است و عالم را قديم مى‏گويد
sections.title_en: Reply to the materialist who disbelieves in the Deity and says that the world is eternal.

entries.farsi: دى يكى مى‏گفت عالم حادث است / ‏فانى است اين چرخ و حقش وارث است
entries.english: Yesterday someone was saying, “The world originated in time: this heaven is passing away, and God is its inheritor.”

entries.farsi: فلسفيى گفت چون دانى حدوث / ‏حادثى ابر چون داند غيوث
entries.english: A philosopher said, “How do you know temporal origin? How should the rain know the temporality of the cloud?

entries.farsi: ذره‏اى خود نيستى از انقالب / ‏تو چه مى‏دانى حدوث آفتاب
entries.english: You are not even a mote of the revolution: how should you know the temporality of the sun?

entries.farsi: اين به تقليد از پدر بشنيده‏اى / ‏از حماقت اندر اين پيچيده‏اى
entries.english: The little worm that is buried in filth— how should it know the end and beginning of the earth?

entries.farsi: كرمكى كاندر حدث باشد دفين / ‏كى بداند آخر و بدو زمين
entries.english: You have heard this by rote from your father: through foolishness you have become involved in this.

entries.farsi: چيست برهان بر حدوث اين بگو / ور نه خامش كن فزون گويى مجو
entries.english: What is the demonstrative argument for its temporality? Tell or else keep silence and do not seek excessive talk.”

entries.farsi: در جدال و در خصام و در ستوه / ‏گشت هنگامه بر آن دو كس گروه
entries.english: He said, “One day I saw two parties searching in this deep sea,

entries.farsi: گفت ديدم اندر اين بحر عميق / ‏بحث مى‏كردند روزى دو فريق
entries.english: In disputation and controversy and desperate battle: a crowd gathered round those two persons.

entries.farsi: من به سوى جمع هنگامه شدم / ‏اطالع از حال ايشان بستدم
entries.english: I went towards the crowded multitude and took notice of their affair.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت گردون فانى است / ‏بى‏گمانى اين بنا را بانى است
entries.english: One was saying the sky will pass away: without any doubt, this edifice has a builder.’

entries.farsi: و آن دگر گفت اين قديم و بى‏كى است / ‏نيستش بانى و يا بانى وى است
entries.english: The other said, ‘it is eternal and timeless: it has no builder, or else it is the builder.’

entries.farsi: گفت منكر گشته‏اى خالق را / روز و شب آرنده و رزاق را
entries.english: He said, ‘You have denied the Creator, the Producer of day and night and the Giver of sustenance.’

entries.farsi: گفت بى‏برهان نخواهم من شنيد / آن چه گولى آن به تقليدى گزيد
entries.english: He said, ‘Without clear evidence, I will not listen to that which an ignoramus has accepted by rote.

entries.farsi: هين بياور حجت و برهان كه من / ‏نشنوم بى‏حجت اين را در زمن
entries.english: Come, bring the proof and evidence, for never in the world will I listen to this without proof.’

entries.farsi: گفت حجت در درون جانم است / ‏در درون جان نهان برهانم است
entries.english: ‘The proof,’ he replied, ‘is within my soul: my evidence is hidden within my soul.

entries.farsi: تو نمى‏بينى هالل از ضعف چشم / ‏من همى‏بينم مكن بر من تو خشم
entries.english: You, from weakness of eye, are not seeing the new moon: I am seeing it, do not you be angry with me.’

entries.farsi: گفت‏وگو بسيار گشت و خلق گيج / ‏در سر و پايان اين چرخ بسيج
entries.english: There was much debate, and the people became perplexed as to the beginning and end of this well-ordered celestial sphere.

entries.farsi: من يقين دارم نشانش آن بود / مر يقين دان را كه در آتش رود
entries.english: He said, ‘Friend, within me is a proof: I have a sign indicating the temporal origin of the sky.

entries.farsi: گفت يارا در درونم حجتى است / ‏بر حدوث آسمانم آيتى است
entries.english: I possess the certainty: for him that has certain knowledge the token thereof is that he will go into the fire.

entries.farsi: در زبان مى‏نايد آن حجت بدان / ‏همچو حال سر عشق عاشقان
entries.english: Like the inmost feelings of love in lovers, that proof, know, does not come on the tongue.

entries.farsi: نيست پيدا سر گفت‏وگوى من / ‏جز كه زردى و نزارى روى من
entries.english: The inmost meaning of my words is not apparent, except the pallor and haggardness of my face.

entries.farsi: اشك و خون بر رخ روانه مى‏دود / حجت حسن و جمالش مى‏شود
entries.english: Tears and blood roll on my cheeks and become the proof of His comeliness and beauty.’

entries.farsi: گفت من اينها ندانم حجتى / ‏كه بود در پيش عامه آيتى
entries.english: He replied, ‘I do not deem these things to be such a proof as would be a manifest sign to the common.’

entries.farsi: گفت چون قلبى و نقدى دم زنند / كه تو قلبى من نكويم ارجمند
entries.english: He said, ‘When a base and a genuine coin boast, saying, “You are base; I am good and valuable,”

entries.farsi: هست آتش امتحان آخرين / ‏كاندر آتش در فتند اين دو قرين
entries.english: Fire is the final test: that these two rivals should be dropped into the fire.

entries.farsi: عام و خاص از حالشان عالم شوند / از گمان و شك سوى ايقان روند
entries.english: The common and the elect will become acquainted with their state and will advance from opinion and doubt to certain knowledge.

entries.farsi: آب و آتش آمد اى جان امتحان / ‏نقد و قلبى را كه آن باشد نهان
entries.english: Water and fire, O soul, are the test for the pure and the base coin that is hidden.

entries.farsi: تا من و تو هر دو در آتش رويم / ‏حجت باقى حيرانان شويم
entries.english: Let me and you, both of us, go into the fire and become a lasting proof for the perplexed.

entries.farsi: تا من و تو هر دو در بحر اوفتيم / ‏كه من و تو اين گره را آيتيم
entries.english: Let me and you, both of us, fall into the sea, for you and I are a sign unto this multitude.’

entries.farsi: همچنان كردند و در آتش شدند / هر دو خود را بر تف آتش زدند
entries.english: Even so they did and entered the fire: both cast themselves upon the heat of the fire.

entries.farsi: آن خدا گوينده مرد مدعى / ‏رست و سوزيد اندر آتش آن دعى
entries.english: The God-proclaiming man who engaged in controversy was saved, while that bastard was burnt in the fire.

entries.farsi: از موذن بشنو اين اعالم را / كورى افزون روان خام را
entries.english: Hear from the muezzin this announcement, to the confusion of the foolish transgressors,

entries.farsi: كه نسوزيده‏ست اين نام از اجل / ‏كش مستى صدر بوده ست و اجل
entries.english: That this name has not been burnt by Death, since its bearer was a prince and most noble.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران زين رهان اندر قران / ‏بر دريده پرده‏هاى منكران
entries.english: In the course of time hundreds of thousands of the veils of the unbelievers have been rent by this laying down of stakes.

entries.farsi: چون گرو بستند غالب شد صواب / در دوام و معجزات و در جواب
entries.english: When they made the wager, the truth prevailed as regards immortality and evidentiary miracles and the answer.

entries.farsi: فهم كردم كان كه دم زد از سبق / ‏و ز حدوث چرخ پيروز است و حق
entries.english: I perceived that he who spoke of the priority and of the temporal origin of the celestial sphere was victorious and in the right.”

entries.farsi: حجت منكر هماره زرد رو / يك نشان بر صدق آن انكار كو
entries.english: The unbeliever’s argument is always shamefaced: where is a single sign that indicates the truth of that unbelief?

entries.farsi: يك مناره در ثناى منكران / ‏كو در اين عالم كه تا باشد نشان
entries.english: Where in this world is a single minaret in praise of the unbelievers, so that it should be a sign?

entries.farsi: روى دينار و درم از نامشان / ‏تا قيامت مى‏دهد زين حق نشان
entries.english: Where is a single pulpit where a preacher commemorates the life of an unbeliever?

entries.farsi: منبرى كو كه بر آن جا مخبرى / ‏ياد آرد روزگار منكرى
entries.english: The face of gold and silver coins, from their names, is giving a token of this truth till the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: سكه‏ى شاهان همى‏گردد دگر / سكه‏ى احمد ببين تا مستقر
entries.english: The dies of the kings are ever being changed: behold the die of Ahmad till the end of the world.

entries.farsi: بر رخ نقره و يا روى زرى / ‏وانما بر سكه نام منكرى
entries.english: Show the name of a single unbeliever on the design on the face of any piece of silver or gold!

entries.farsi: خود مگير اين معجزه چون آفتاب / ‏صد زبان بين نام او أُمُّ الكتاب
entries.english: Even do not admit, behold this Miracle, like the sun, hundred-tongued, whereof the name is Ummu ’l-Kitáb.

entries.farsi: زهره نى كس را كه يك حرفى از آن / ‏يا بدزدد يا فزايد در بيان
entries.english: None dares either steal a single letter thereof or add to the plain Word.

entries.farsi: يار غالب شو كه تا غالب شوى / ‏يار مغلوبان مشو هين اى غوى
entries.english: Become a friend to the conqueror that you may conquer: beware, do not become a friend to the vanquished, O misguided man!

entries.farsi: حجت منكر همين آمد كه من / ‏غير اين ظاهر نمى‏بينم وطن
entries.english: The unbeliever’s argument is just this, that he says, “I see no place of abode except this external.”

entries.farsi: هيچ ننديشد كه هر جا ظاهرى است / ‏آن ز حكمتهاى پنهان مخبرى است
entries.english: He never reflects that, wherever there is anything external, that gives information of hidden wise purposes.

entries.farsi: فايده‏ى هر ظاهرى خود باطن است / ‏همچو نفع اندر دواها كامن است
entries.english: The usefulness of every external object is, indeed, internal: it is latent, like the beneficial quality in medicines.

sections.title_fa: تفسير اين آيت كه ما خَلَقْنَا السَّ مواتِ وَ الأَْرْضَ وَ ما بَيْنَهُما إِلاَّ بِالْحَقِ‏نيافريدمشان بهر همين كه شما مى‏بينيد بلكه بهر معنى و حكمت باقيه كه شما نمى‏بينيد آن را
sections.title_en: Commentary on the Verse, “And We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between
them save with real ground”: “I did not create them for the sake of just this which you see; nay, but for the sake of the essential meaning and everlasting providence which you see not.”

entries.farsi: بلكه بهر ميهمانان و كهان / ‏كه به فرجه وارهند از اندهان
entries.english: Does any painter paint a beautiful picture for the sake of the picture itself, without hope of conferring benefit?

entries.farsi: هيچ نقاشى نگارد زين نقش / ‏بى‏اميد نفع بهر عين نقش
entries.english: Nay, for the sake of guests and young people who by diverting themselves may be relieved from cares.

entries.farsi: شادى بچگان و ياد دوستان / ‏دوستان رفته را از نقش آن
entries.english: From his picture the joy of children and the remembering of departed friends by their friends.

entries.farsi: هيچ كوزه‏گر كند كوزه شتاب / ‏بهر عين كوزه نه بر بوى آب
entries.english: Does any potter make a pot in haste for the sake of the pot itself and not in hope of the water?

entries.farsi: هيچ كاسه‏گر كند كاسه‏ى تمام / ‏بهر عين كاسه نه بهر طعام
entries.english: Does any bowl-maker make a finished bowl for the sake of the bowl itself and not for the sake of the food?

entries.farsi: هيچ خطاطى نويسد خط به فن / ‏بهر عين خط نه بهر خواندن
entries.english: Does any calligrapher write artistically for the sake of the writing itself and not for the sake of the reading?

entries.farsi: نقش ظاهر بهر نقش غايب است / ‏و آن براى غايب ديگر ببست
entries.english: The external form is for the sake of the unseen form; and that took shape for the sake of another unseen.

entries.farsi: تا سوم چارم دهم بر مى‏شمر / اين فوايد را به مقدار نظر
entries.english: Count up these corollaries to the third, fourth, or tenth in proportion to insight.

entries.farsi: همچو بازيهاى شطرنج اى پسر / فايده‏ى هر لعب در تالى نگر
entries.english: As the moves in chess, O son: behold the result of each move in the next one.

entries.farsi: اين نهاده بهر آن لعب نهان / ‏و آن براى آن و آن بهر فالن
entries.english: They made this for the sake of that concealed move, and that for the next, and that for such and such.

entries.farsi: همچنين ديده جهات اندر جهات / ‏در پى هم تا رسى در برد و مات
entries.english: Even so, having perceived reasons within reasons, one after the other, in order that you may arrive at victory and checkmate

entries.farsi: اول از بهر دوم باشد چنان / ‏كه شدن بر پايه‏هاى نردبان
entries.english: The first is for the sake of the second, like mounting on the steps of a ladder;

entries.farsi: و آن دوم بهر سوم مى‏دان تمام / ‏تا رسى تو پايه پايه تا به بام
entries.english: And deem the second to be for the sake of the third, to the end, in order that you may arrive, step by step, at the roof.

entries.farsi: شهوت خوردن ز بهر آن منى / ‏و آن منى از بهر نسل و روشنى
entries.english: The desire to eat is for the sake of the semen: that semen is for the sake of procreation and the light.

entries.farsi: كند بينش مى‏نبيند غير اين / ‏عقل او بى‏سير چون نبت زمين
entries.english: The man of dull sight sees naught but this: his intelligence is without motion, like the plants of the earth.

entries.farsi: نبت را چه خوانده چه ناخوانده / ‏هست پاى او به گل درمانده
entries.english: Whether the plant is summoned or not summoned, its foot remains stuck fast in the mud.

entries.farsi: گر سرش جنبد به سير باد رو / تو به سر جنبانى‏اش غره مشو
entries.english: If its head move with the motion of the wind, go, be not deceived by its moving its head.

entries.farsi: آن سرش گويد سمعنا اى صبا / پاى او گويد عصينا خلنا
entries.english: Its head says, “We obey, O zephyr!” Its foot says, “We refuse to obey: let us alone!”

entries.farsi: چون نداند سير مى‏راند چو عام / ‏بر توكل مى‏نهد چون كور گام
entries.english: Since he does not know how to move, he advances like the common, stepping on trust, like a blind man.

entries.farsi: بر توكل تا چه آيد در نبرد / چون توكل كردن اصحاب نرد
entries.english: Consider what comes of acting on trust in warfare: like the trust of dice-players.

entries.farsi: و آن نظرهايى كه آن افسرده نيست / ‏جز رونده و جز درنده‏ى پرده نيست
entries.english: But those insights that are not frozen are nothing if not piercing and veil-rending.

entries.farsi: آن چه در ده سال خواهد آمدن / ‏اين زمان بيند به چشم خويشتن
entries.english: He sees with his own eye at the present moment that which will come to pass in ten years.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر كس به اندازه‏ى نظر / غيب و مستقبل ببيند خير و شر
entries.english: Similarly, everyone sees the unseen and the future, good and evil, according to the measure of his insight.

entries.farsi: چون كه سد پيش و سد پس نماند / شد گزاره چشم و لوح غيب خواند
entries.english: When the barrier in front and the barrier behind are removed, the eye penetrates and reads the tablet of the Unseen.

entries.farsi: چون نظر پس كرد تا بدو وجود / ماجرا و آغاز هستى رو نمود
entries.english: When he looks back to the origin of existence, the past circumstances and beginning of existence display themselves—

entries.farsi: بحث امالك زمين با كبريا / در خليفه كردن باباى ما
entries.english: The disputation of the terrestrial angels with the Majesty as to making our Father the Vicegerent

entries.farsi: چون نظر در پيش افكند او بديد / آن چه خواهد بود تا محشر پديد
entries.english: When he casts his eye forward he sees plainly that which shall be till the Congregation.

entries.farsi: پس ز پس مى‏بيند او تا اصل اصل / ‏پيش مى‏بيند عيان تا روز فصل
entries.english: Therefore he sees back to the root of the root, and he sees forward clairvoyantly to the Day of Decision.

entries.farsi: هر كسى اندازه‏ى روشن دلى / ‏غيب را بيند به قدر صيقلى
entries.english: Every one, according to the measure of his spiritual enlightenment, sees the things unseen in proportion to the polishing.

entries.farsi: هر كه صيقل بيش كرد او بيش ديد / بيشتر آمد بر او صورت پديد
entries.english: The more he polishes, the more he sees and the more visible does the form become to him.

entries.farsi: گر تو گويى كان صفا فضل خداست / ‏نيز اين توفيق صيقل ز آن عطاست
entries.english: If you say that that purity is the grace of God, this success in polishing is also from that bounty.

entries.farsi: قدر همت باشد آن جهد و دعا / ‏لَيْسَ لِلإِْنْسانِ إِلاَّ ما سعى
entries.english: That work and prayer is in proportion to the aspiration: Man has nothing but what he has strived after.

entries.farsi: واهب همت خداوند است و بس / ‏همت شاهى ندارد هيچ خس
entries.english: God alone is the giver of aspiration: no base churl aspires to be a king.

entries.farsi: نيست تخصيص خدا كس را به كار / مانع طوع و مراد و اختيار
entries.english: God’s assignment of a particular lot to any one does not hinder consent and will and choice;

entries.farsi: ليك چون رنجى دهد بد بخت را / او گريزاند به كفران رخت را
entries.english: But when He brings some trouble on an ill-fated man, he ungratefully packs off in flight;

entries.farsi: نيك بختى را چو حق رنجى دهد / رخت را نزديكتر وا مى‏نهد
entries.english: When God brings some trouble on a good-fortuned man, he always abides nearer to God.

entries.farsi: بد دالن از بيم جان در كارزار / كرده اسباب هزيمت اختيار
entries.english: In battle the pusillanimous from fear for their lives have chosen the means of flight,

entries.farsi: رستمان را ترس و غم وا پيش برد / هم ز ترس آن بد دل اندر خويش مرد
entries.english: The courageous, also from fear for their lives, have charged towards the ranks of the enemy.

entries.farsi: پر دالن در جنگ هم از بيم جان / ‏حمله كرده سوى صف دشمنان
entries.english: Rustams are borne onward by fear and pain; from fear, too, the man of infirm spirit dies within himself.

entries.farsi: چون محك آمد بال و بيم جان / ‏ز آن پديد آيد شجاع از هر جبان
entries.english: Tribulation and fear for one’s life are like a touchstone: thereby the brave man is distinguished from every coward.

sections.title_fa: ‏وحى كردن حق به موسى عليه السلام كه اى موسى من كه خالقم تعالى ترا دوست مى‏دارم
sections.title_en: How God made a revelation to Moses, on whom be peace, saying, “O Moses, I who am the exalted Creator love you.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى را به وحى دل خدا / كاى گزيده دوست مى‏دارم ترا
entries.english: God spoke to Moses by inspiration of the heart, saying, “O chosen one, I love you.”

entries.farsi: گفت چه خصلت بود اى ذو الكرم / ‏موجب آن تا من آن افزون كنم
entries.english: He said, “O Bountiful One, what disposition is the cause of that, in order that I may augment it.”

entries.farsi: گفت چون طفلى به پيش والده / ‏وقت قهرش دست هم در وى زده
entries.english: He said, “You are like a child in the presence of its mother: when she chastises it, it still lays hold of her.

entries.farsi: خود نداند كه جز او ديار هست / ‏هم از او مخمور هم از اوست مست
entries.english: It does not even know that there is any one in the world except her: it is both afflicted with headache by her and intoxicated by her.

entries.farsi: از كسى يارى نخواهد غير او / اوست جمله‏ى شر او و خير او
entries.english: If its mother gives it a slap, still it comes to its mother and clings to her.

entries.farsi: مادرش گر سيليى بر وى زند / هم به مادر آيد و بر وى تند
entries.english: It does not seek help from anyone but her: she is all its evil and it’s good.

entries.farsi: خاطر تو هم ز ما در خير و شر / التفاتش نيست جاهاى دگر
entries.english: Your heart, likewise, in good or evil never turns from Me to other quarters.

entries.farsi: غير من پيشت چو سنگ است و كلوخ / ‏گر صبى و گر جوان و گر شيوخ
entries.english: In your sight all besides Me are as stones and clods, whether boys or youths or old men.”

entries.farsi: همچنانك إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ در حنين / ‏در بال از غير تو ال نستعين
entries.english: Just as You we worship in yearning entreaty, in tribulation we ask help of none but You.

entries.farsi: هست اين إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ حصر را / در لغت و آن از پى نفى ريا
entries.english: This You we worship is idiomatically for the purpose of appropriation, and that is for the purpose of negating hypocrisy.

entries.farsi: هست إِيَّاكَ نَسْ تَعِينُ هم بهر حصر / حصر كرده استعانت را و قصر
entries.english: Of You we ask help also is for the purpose of appropriation: he appropriates and restricts the asking of help,

entries.farsi: كه عبادت مر ترا آريم و بس / طمع يارى هم ز تو داريم و بس
entries.english: Meaning, “We perform worship to You alone; we have hope of help from You alone.”

sections.title_fa: خشم كردن پادشاه بر نديم و شفاعت كردن شفيع آن مغضوب عليه را و از پادشاه درخواستن و ‏پادشاه شفاعت او قبول كردن و رنجيدن نديم از شفيع كه چرا شفاعت كردى
sections.title_en: How a king was enraged with his boon-companion, and an intercessor interceded on behalf of the object of anger and begged the king; and how the king accepted his intercession, the boon-companion resented the action of the intercessor and asked, “Why did you intercede?”

entries.farsi: پادشاهى بر نديمى خشم كرد / خواست تا از وى بر آرد دود و گرد
entries.english: A king was enraged with a boon-companion and was about to reduce him to smoke and dust.

entries.farsi: كرد شه شمشير بيرون از غالف / ‏تا زند بر وى جزاى آن خالف
entries.english: The king drew his sword from the scabbard that he might inflict upon him the punishment for that disobedience.

entries.farsi: جز عماد الملك نامى در خواص / ‏در شفاعت مصطفى وارانه خاص
entries.english: No one had the courage to utter neither a word nor any intercessor to venture on intercession,

entries.farsi: هيچ كس را زهره نه تا دم زند / يا شفيعى بر شفاعت بر تند
entries.english: Except one amongst the courtiers named Imádu ’l-Mulk, privileged in respect of intercession, like Mustafa

entries.farsi: بر جهيد و زود در سجده فتاد / در زمان شه تيغ قهر از كف نهاد
entries.english: He sprang up and at once prostrated himself: the king immediately put away from his hand the sword of vengeance,

entries.farsi: چون كه آمد پاى تو اندر ميان / ‏راضيم گر كرد مجرم صد زيان
entries.english: And said, “If he is the Devil, I forgive him; and if he has done a satanic deed, I cover it up

entries.farsi: گفت اگر ديو است من بخشيدمش / ‏ور بليسى كرد من پوشيدمش
entries.english: Since you have intervened, I am satisfied, if the culprit has committed a hundred acts of harm.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران خشم بتوانم شكست / ‏كه ترا آن فضل و آن مقدار هست
entries.english: I can break a hundred thousand angers, seeing that you have such excellence and such worth;

entries.farsi: البه‏ات را هيچ نتوانم شكست / ‏ز انكه البه‏ى تو يقين البه‏ى من است
entries.english: Nowise can I break your supplication, because your supplication is assuredly my supplication.

entries.farsi: گر زمين و آسمان بر هم زدى / ‏ز انتقام اين مرد بيرون نامدى
entries.english: If he had thrown earth and heaven into confusion, this man would not have escaped from vengeance;

entries.farsi: ور شدى ذره به ذره البه‏گر / او نبردى اين زمان از تيغ سر
entries.english: And if atom by atom had become a suppliant, he would not have saved his head from the sword at this moment.

entries.farsi: بر تو مى‏ننهيم منت اى كريم / ‏ليك شرح عزت تست اى نديم
entries.english: We confer no obligation on you, O noble one; but it is to explain your honour, O boon-companion.

entries.farsi: اين نكردى تو كه من كردم يقين / ‏اى صفاتت در صفات ما دفين
entries.english: You did not make this, for assuredly I made it, O you whose qualities are buried in my qualities.

entries.farsi: تو در اين مستعملى نى عاملى / ‏ز انكه محمول منى نى حاملى
entries.english: In this you are the one employed to do the work, not the doer, inasmuch as you are borne by me and art not the bearer.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ گشته‏اى / ‏خويشتن در موج چون كف هشته‏اى
entries.english: You have become You did not throw when you threw: like the foam, you have abandoned yourself in the wave.

entries.farsi: ال شدى پهلوى الا خانه گير / اين عجب كه هم اسيرى هم امير
entries.english: You have become ‘not’; take up your abode beside ‘except.’ This is wonderful, that you are both a prisoner and a prince.

entries.farsi: آن چه دادى تو ندادى شاه داد / اوست پس اللهَّ اعلم بالرشاد
entries.english: You did not give what you gave: the king gave it. He alone is. God best knows the right course.”

entries.farsi: و آن نديم رسته از زخم و بال / زين شفيع آزرد و برگشت از وال
entries.english: And the boon-companion who had been delivered from the stroke of calamity was offended with this intercessor and drew back from fealty.

entries.farsi: دوستى ببريد ز آن مخلص تمام / ‏رو به حايط كرد تا نارد سالم
entries.english: He cut off all friendship with that sincere man, and turned his face to the wall in order that he might not give the salaam.

entries.farsi: زين شفيع خويشتن بيگانه شد / زين تعجب خلق در افسانه شد
entries.english: He became estranged from his intercessor; in astonishment at this the people began to talk,

entries.farsi: كه نه مجنون است يارى چون بريد / از كسى كه جان او را واخريد
entries.english: Saying, “He is not mad, how did he cut off friendly relations with the person who redeemed his life?

entries.farsi: باژگونه رفت و بيزارى گرفت / ‏با چنين دل دار كين دارى گرفت
entries.english: He redeemed him from beheading at that moment: he ought to have become the dust of his shoe.

entries.farsi: واخريدش آن دم از گردن زدن / ‏خاك نعل پاش بايستى شدن
entries.english: He has gone the reverse way and has taken renouncing: he has taken to cherishing enmity against a beloved like this.”

entries.farsi: پس مالمت كرد او را مصلحى / ‏كاين جفا چون مى‏كنى با ناصحى
entries.english: Then a certain mentor reproached him, saying, “Why are you acting so unjustly towards a loyal friend?

entries.farsi: جان تو بخريد آن دل دار خاص / ‏آن دم از گردن زدن كردت خالص
entries.english: That elect beloved redeemed your life and saved you from beheading at that moment.

entries.farsi: گر بدى كردى نبايستى رميد / خاصه نيكى كرد آن يار حميد
entries.english: If he had done evil, you ought not to have turned away that praiseworthy friend was especially your benefactor.”

entries.farsi: گفت بهر شاه مبذول است جان / ‏او چرا آيد شفيع اندر ميان
entries.english: He replied, “Life is freely given for the king’s sake: why should he come as an intercessor between?

entries.farsi: لى مع اللهَّ وقت بود آن دم مرا / ‏ال يسع فيه نبى مجتبى
entries.english: At that moment mine was ‘I am with God in a state wherein no chosen prophet is my peer.’

entries.farsi: من نخواهم رحمتى جز زخم شاه / ‏من نخواهم غير آن شه را پناه
entries.english: I desire no mercy but the blows of the king; I desire no refuge except that king.

entries.farsi: غير شه را بهر آن ال كرده‏ام / ‏كه به سوى شه توال كرده‏ام
entries.english: I have negated all besides the king for the reason that I have devoted myself to the king.

entries.farsi: گر ببرد او به قهر خود سرم / ‏شاه بخشد شصت جان ديگرم
entries.english: The king, if he beheads me in his wrath, will bestow on me sixty other lives.

entries.farsi: كار من سربازى و بى‏خويشى است / ‏كار شاهنشاه من سر بخشى است
entries.english: It is my business to hazard my head and to be selfless; it is the business of my sovereign king to give a head.”

entries.farsi: فخر آن سر كه كف شاهش برد / ننگ آن سر كاو به غيرى سر برد
entries.english: Honour to the head that is severed by the King’s hand! Shame on the head that betakes itself to another!

entries.farsi: خود طواف آن كه او شه بين بود / فوق قهر و لطف و كفر و دين بود
entries.english: The night which the King in his wrath covered with pitch holds in disdain a thousand days of festival.

entries.farsi: شب كه شاه از قهر در قيرش كشيد / ننگ دارد از هزاران روز عيد
entries.english: Truly, the circumambulation performed by him who beholds the King is above wrath and grace and infidelity and religion.

entries.farsi: ز آن نيامد يك عبارت در جهان / ‏كه نهان است و نهان است و نهان
entries.english: Not one word expressing it has come into the world, for it is hidden, hidden, hidden,

entries.farsi: ز انكه اين اسما و الفاظ حميد / از گالبه‏ى آدمى آمد پديد
entries.english: Inasmuch as these glorious names and words were manifested from the reel of Adam.

entries.farsi: علم الاسما بد آدم را امام / ‏ليك نه اندر لباس عين و الم
entries.english: He taught the names was an Imam for Adam; but not in the garb of ‘ayn and lám.

entries.farsi: چون نهاد از آب و گل بر سر كاله / ‏گشت آن اسماى جانى رو سياه
entries.english: When he put on his head the cap of water and clay, those spiritual names became black-faced,

entries.farsi: كه نقاب حرف و دم در خود كشيد / تا شود بر آب و گل معنى پديد
entries.english: For they assumed the veil of letters and breath, in order that the essential reality might be made manifest to the water and clay.

entries.farsi: گر چه از يك وجه منطق كاشف است / ليك از ده وجه پرده و مكنف است
entries.english: Although from one point of view speech is a revealer, yet from ten points of view it is a curtain and concealer.

sections.title_fa: گفتن خليل مر جبرئيل را عليهما السلام چون پرسيدش كه ا لك حاجه خليل جوابش داد كه اما اليك فال
sections.title_en: How Khalíl (Abraham) answered Gabriel, on both of whom be peace, when he asked him, “Have you any need?”—“As regards need of you, no!”

entries.farsi: من خليل وقتم و او جبرئيل / ‏من نخواهم در بال او را دليل
entries.english: “I am the Khalíl of the present time, and he is the Gabriel: I do not want him as a guide in calamity.

entries.farsi: او ادب ناموخت از جبريل راد / كه بپرسيد از خليل حق مراد
entries.english: He did not learn respectfulness from noble Gabriel, who asked the Friend of God what was his wish,

entries.farsi: بهر اين دنياست مرسل رابطه / ‏مومنان را ز انكه هست او واسطه
entries.english: Saying, ‘Have you a wish?—that I may help; otherwise, I will flee and make a speedy departure.’

entries.farsi: كه مرادت هست تا يارى كنم / ‏ور نه بگريزم سبكبارى كنم
entries.english: Abraham said, ‘No; go out of the way! After direct vision the intermediary is an inconvenience.’

entries.farsi: گفت ابراهيم نى رو از ميان / ‏واسطه زحمت بود بعد العيان
entries.english: On account of this present life the messenger is a link for the true believers, because he is the intermediary.

entries.farsi: هر دل ار سامع بدى وحى نهان / ‏حرف و صوتى كى بدى اندر جهان
entries.english: If every heart were hearing the hidden revelation, how should there be in the world any words and sounds?

entries.farsi: گر چه او محو حق است و بى‏سر است / ‏ليك كار من از آن نازكتر است
entries.english: Though he is lost in God and headless, yet my case is more delicate than that.

entries.farsi: كرده‏ى او كرده‏ى شاه است ليك / ‏پيش ضعفم بد نماينده ست نيك
entries.english: His act is the act of the king, but to my infirmity the good appears to be evil.”

entries.farsi: آن چه عين لطف باشد بر عوام / ‏قهر شد بر نازنينان كرام
entries.english: That which is the very essence of grace to the common becomes wrath to the noble favourites.

entries.farsi: بس بال و رنج مى‏بايد كشيد / عامه را تا فرق بتوانند ديد
entries.english: Much tribulation and pain must the common endure in order that they may be able to perceive the difference;

entries.farsi: كاين حروف واسطه‏اى يار غار / پيش واصل خار باشد خار خار
entries.english: For, O companion in the Cave, these intermediary words are, in the sight of one united, thorns, thorns, thorns.

entries.farsi: بس بال و رنج بايست و وقوف / ‏تا رهد آن روح صافى از حروف
entries.english: Much tribulation and pain and waiting were needed in order that that pure spirit might be delivered from the words;

entries.farsi: ليك بعضى زين صدا كرتر شدند / باز بعضى صافى و برتر شدند
entries.english: But some have become deafer to this echo; some, again, have become purified and have mounted higher.

entries.farsi: همچو آب نيل آمد اين بال / سعد را آب است و خون بر اشقيا
entries.english: This tribulation is like the water of the Nile: it is water to the blessed and blood to the damned.

entries.farsi: هر كه پايان بين‏تر او مسعودتر / جدتر او كارد كه افزون ديد بر
entries.english: The more one descries the end, the more blessed is he: the greater one sees the crop, the more zealously he sows,

entries.farsi: ز انكه داند كاين جهان كاشتن / ‏هست بهر محشر و برداشتن
entries.english: Because he knows that this world of sowing is for the sake of the Congregation and gathering in.

entries.farsi: هيچ عقدى بهر عين خود نبود / بلكه از بهر مقام ربح و سود
entries.english: No contract was for the sake of itself; nay, but for the sake of the position of gain and profit.

entries.farsi: بل براى قهر خصم اندر حسد / يا فزونى جستن و اظهار خود
entries.english: There is no disbeliever, if you look, whose disbelief is for the sake of the disbelief itself;

entries.farsi: هيچ نبود منكرى گر بنگرى / ‏منكرى‏اش بهر عين منكرى
entries.english: Nay, but for the purpose of subduing his adversary in envy, or seeking superiority and self-display

entries.farsi: ز آن همى‏پرسى چرا اين مى‏كنى / ‏كه صور زيت است و معنى روشنى
entries.english: And that superiority too is for the sake of some other desire: the forms give no relish without the essential meanings.

entries.farsi: و آن فزونى هم پى طمع دگر / بى‏معانى چاشنى ندهد صور
entries.english: You ask “Why are you doing this?” because the forms are the oil and the essential meaning is the light.

entries.farsi: ور نه اين گفتن چرا از بهر چيست / ‏چون كه صورت بهر عين صورتى است
entries.english: Otherwise, what is this saying “why”?— since the form is for the sake of the form itself.

entries.farsi: اين چرا گفتن سؤال از فايده‏ست / ‏جز براى اين چرا گفتن بد است
entries.english: This saying “why” is a question concerning the use: it is bad to say “why” for any cause but this.

entries.farsi: از چه رو فاييده‏جويى اى امين / ‏چون بود فاييده‏ى اين خود همين
entries.english: Wherefore, O trusty one, should you desire the use?— since the use of this is only this.

entries.farsi: پس نقوش آسمان و اهل زمين / ‏نيست حكمت كان بود بهر همين
entries.english: Hence it is not wisdom that the forms of heaven and the people of the earth should be for this only.

entries.farsi: گر حكيمى نيست اين ترتيب چيست / ‏ور حكيمى هست پس فعلش تهى است
entries.english: If there is no Wise, what is this orderly arrangement? And if there is a Wise, how is His action devoid?

entries.farsi: كس نسازد نقش گرمابه و خضاب / ‏جز پى قصد صواب و ناصواب
entries.english: No one makes pictures and colouring in a bath-house except for purpose right or wrong.

sections.title_fa: ‏مطالبه كردن موسى عليه السلام حضرت را كه خلقت خلقا و اهلكتهم و جواب آمدن
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, besought the Lord, saying, “You did create creatures and destroy them,” and how the answer came.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى اى خداوند حساب / ‏نقش كردى باز چون كردى خراب
entries.english: Moses said, “O Lord of the Reckoning, You did create the form: how did You destroy it again?”

entries.farsi: نر و ماده نقش كردى جان فزا / و آنگهان ويران كنى اين را چرا
entries.english: You have made the form, male and female, that gives unto the spirit increase; and then You do ruin it: why?”

entries.farsi: گفت حق دانم كه اين پرسش ترا / نيست از انكار و غفلت و ز هوا
entries.english: God said, “I know that this question of yours is not from disbelief and heedlessness and idle fancy;

entries.farsi: ور نه تاديب و عتابت كردمى / ‏بهر اين پرسش ترا آزردمى
entries.english: Else I should have corrected and chastised you: I should have afflicted you on account of this question.

entries.farsi: ليك مى‏خواهى كه در افعال ما / باز جويى حكمت و سر بقا
entries.english: But you wish to discover in My actions the wisdom and hidden meaning of duration,

entries.farsi: تا از آن واقف كنى مر عام را / پخته گردانى بدين هر خام را
entries.english: That you may acquaint the common therewith and by this means make every raw person to become cooked.

entries.farsi: قاصدا سايل شدى در كاشفى / ‏بر عوام ار چه كه تو ز آن واقفى
entries.english: You have become a questioner on purpose to disclose to the common, albeit you are acquainted with it;

entries.farsi: ز انكه نيم علم آمد اين سؤال / ‏هر برونى را نباشد اين مجال
entries.english: For this questioning is the half of knowledge, and this ability does not belong to every outsider.”

entries.farsi: هم سؤال از علم خيزد هم جواب / ‏همچنان كه خار و گل از خاك و آب
entries.english: Both question and answer arise from knowledge, just as the thorn and the rose from earth and water.

entries.farsi: ز آشنايى خيزد اين بغض و وال / ‏وز غذاى خوش بود سقم و قوى
entries.english: Both perdition and salvation arise from knowledge, just as bitter and sweet from moisture.

entries.farsi: هم ضالل از علم خيزد هم هدى / همچنان كه تلخ و شيرين از ندا
entries.english: This hatred and love arise from acquaintance, and from wholesome food sickness and powers.

entries.farsi: مستفيد اعجمى شد آن كليم / ‏تا عجميان را كند زين سر عليم
entries.english: That Kalím became an ignorant enquirer in order that he might make the ignorant acquainted with this mystery.

entries.farsi: ما هم از وى اعجمى سازيم خويش / ‏پاسخش آريم چون بيگانه پيش
entries.english: Let us too feign ourselves to be ignorant thereof and elicit the answer to it as strangers.

entries.farsi: خر فروشان خصم يكديگر شدند / تا كليد قفل آن عقد آمدند
entries.english: The ass-sellers became rivals to one another in order that they might open the way to the contract.

entries.farsi: پس بفرمودش خدا اى ذو لباب / ‏چون بپرسيدى بيا بشنو جواب
entries.english: Then God spoke unto him, saying, “O you who possess the most excellent, since you have asked, come, hear the answer.

entries.farsi: موسيا تخمى بكار اندر زمين / ‏تا تو خود هم وادهى انصاف اين
entries.english: O Moses, sow some seed in the earth that you yourself may render justice to this.”

entries.farsi: داس بگرفت و مر آن را مى‏بريد / پس ندا از غيب در گوشش رسيد
entries.english: When Moses had sown and the seed-corn was complete and its ears had gained beauty and symmetry,

entries.farsi: چون كه موسى كشت و شد كشتش تمام / ‏خوشه‏هايش يافت خوبى و نظام
entries.english: He took the sickle and was cutting that; then a voice from the Unseen reached his ear,

entries.farsi: كه چرا كشتى كنى و پرورى / ‏چون كمالى يافت آن را مى‏برى
entries.english: Crying, “Why do you sow and tend some seed-corn and are cutting it when it has attained to perfection?”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب ز آن كنم ويران و پست / ‏كه در اينجا دانه هست و كاه هست
entries.english: He replied, “O Lord, I destroy and lay it low because straw is here and grain.

entries.farsi: دانه اليق نيست در انبار كاه / ‏كاه در انبار گندم هم تباه
entries.english: The grain is not suitable in the straw-barn; the straw likewise is bad in the wheat-barn.

entries.farsi: نيست حكمت اين دو را آميختن / ‏فرق واجب مى‏كند در بيختن
entries.english: It is not wisdom to mix these two: it makes necessary the separation in winnowing.”

entries.farsi: گفت اين دانش تو از كى يافتى / ‏كه به دانش بيدرى بر ساختى
entries.english: He said, “From whom did you gain this knowledge, so that by means of the knowledge you did construct a threshing-floor?”

entries.farsi: گفت تمييزم تو دادى اى خدا / گفت پس تمييز چون نبود مرا
entries.english: He replied, “You, O God, gave me discernment.” He said, “Then how should I not have discernment?”

entries.farsi: اين صدفها نيست در يك مرتبه / ‏در يكى در است و در ديگر شبه
entries.english: Amongst the created beings are pure spirits; there are spirits dark and muddy.

entries.farsi: در خاليق روحهاى پاك هست / ‏روحهاى تيره‏ى گلناك هست
entries.english: These shells are not in one grade: in one is the pearl and in another the bead.

entries.farsi: واجب است اظهار اين نيك و تباه / ‏همچنانك اظهار گندمها ز كاه
entries.english: It is necessary to make manifest this good and evil, just as to make manifest the wheat from the straw.

entries.farsi: بهر اظهار است اين خلق جهان / تا نماند گنج حكمتها نهان
entries.english: These creatures of the world are for the purpose of manifestation, to the end that the treasure of providences may not remain hidden.

entries.farsi: كنت كنزا گفت مخفيا شنو / جوهر خود گم مكن اظهار شو
entries.english: He said, “I was a hidden treasure”: listen! Do not let your substance be lost: become manifest!

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه روح حيوانى و عقل جزوى و وهم و خيال بر مثال دوغند و روح كه باقى است در اين ‏دوغ همچو روغن پنهان است
sections.title_en: Explaining that the animal spirit and the particular reason and the imagination and the fancy may be compared to buttermilk, while the spirit, which is everlasting,
is hidden in this buttermilk, like the butter.

entries.farsi: جوهر صدقت خفى شد در دروغ / ‏همچو طعم روغن اندر طعم دوغ
entries.english: Your true substance is concealed in falsehood, like the taste of butter in the taste of buttermilk.

entries.farsi: آن دروغت اين تن فانى بود / راستت آن جان ربانى بود
entries.english: Your falsehood is this perishable body; your truth is that lordly spirit.

entries.farsi: سالها اين دوغ تن پيدا و فاش / ‏روغن جان اندر او فانى و الش
entries.english: Years this buttermilk, the body, is visible and manifest, the butter, the spirit, is perishing and negated within it,

entries.farsi: تا فرستد حق رسولى بنده‏اى / ‏دوغ را در خمره جنباننده‏اى
entries.english: Till God send a messenger, a servant, a shaker of the buttermilk in the churn,

entries.farsi: تا بجنباند به هنجار و به فن / ‏تا بدانم من كه پنهان بود من
entries.english: That he may shake with method and skill, to the end that I may know that ego was hidden;

entries.farsi: يا كالم بنده‏اى كان جزو اوست / ‏در رود در گوش او كاو وحى جوست
entries.english: Or the speech of a servant, which is part is part of him, enter into the ear of him who is seeking inspiration.

entries.farsi: اذن مومن وحى ما را واعى است / ‏آن چنان گوشى قرين داعى است
entries.english: The true believer’s ear is retaining our inspiration: such an ear is closely linked to the caller.

entries.farsi: همچنان كه گوش طفل از گفت مام / ‏پر شود ناطق شود او در كالم
entries.english: Just as the infant’s ear is filled with its mother’s words, it begins to speak articulately;

entries.farsi: ور نباشد طفل را گوش رشد / گفت مادر نشنود گنگى شود
entries.english: And if the infant have not a right ear, it does not hear its mother’s words and becomes a mute.

entries.farsi: دايما هر كر اصلى گنگ بود / ناطق آن كس شد كه از مادر شنود
entries.english: Every one born deaf has always been dumb: that one who heard from his mother became a speaker.

entries.farsi: دان كه گوش كر و گنگ از آفتى است / ‏كه پذيراى دم و تعليم نيست
entries.english: Know that the deaf ear and the dumb man are the result of a certain defect; for it is not capable of words and being taught.

entries.farsi: آن كه بى‏تعليم بد ناطق خداست / ‏كه صفات او ز علتها جداست
entries.english: The one that possessed speech without being taught is God, whose attributes are separated from infirmities,

entries.farsi: يا چو آدم كرده تلقينش خدا / بى‏حجاب مادر و دايه و ازا
entries.english: Or one like Adam whom God instructed without the screen of mother and nurse and necessaries,

entries.farsi: يا مسيحى كه به تعليم ودود / در والدت ناطق آمد در وجود
entries.english: Or the Messiah who, through being taught by the Loving, at his birth came speaking into the world,

entries.farsi: از براى دفع تهمت در والد / كه نزاده‏ست از زنا و از فساد
entries.english: For the purpose of repelling the suspicion as to his birth that he was not born of fornication and wickedness

entries.farsi: جنبشى بايست اندر اجتهاد / تا كه دوغ آن روغن از دل باز داد
entries.english: A shaking was required in the effort that the buttermilk might render back that butter from its heart.

entries.farsi: روغن اندر دوغ باشد چون عدم / ‏دوغ در هستى بر آورده علم
entries.english: The butter in the buttermilk is like non-existence; the buttermilk has raised its banner in existence.

entries.farsi: آن كه هستت مى‏نمايد هست پوست / ‏و انكه فانى مى‏نمايد اصل اوست
entries.english: That which seems to you to be existent is skin, while that which seems to have perished—that is the root.

entries.farsi: دوغ روغن ناگرفته است و كهن / ‏تا بنگزينى بنه خرجش مكن
entries.english: The buttermilk has not taken butter and is old: lay it and do not squander it till you pick out.

entries.farsi: هين بگردانش به دانش دست دست / ‏تا نمايد آن چه پنهان كرده است
entries.english: Listen, turn it knowingly from hand to hand, that it may reveal that which it has hidden;

entries.farsi: ز انكه اين فانى دليل باقى است / ‏البه‏ى مستان دليل ساقى است
entries.english: For this perishable is a proof of the everlasting: the maundering of the intoxicated is a proof of the Cupbearer.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثال ديگر هم در اين معنى
sections.title_en: Another parable on the same subject

entries.farsi: هست بازيهاى آن شير علم / ‏مخبرى از بادهاى مكتتم
entries.english: The gambols of the lion on the banner are indicative of winds concealed.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى جنبش آن بادها / شير مرده كى بجستى در هوا
entries.english: If there were not the movement of those winds, how would the dead lion leap into the air?

entries.farsi: ز آن شناسى باد را گر آن صباست / ‏يا دبور است اين بيان آن خفاست
entries.english: By that you know whether the wind is the east-wind or the west wind: this is the explanation of that occult matter.

entries.farsi: اين بدن مانند آن شير علم / ‏فكر مى‏جنباند او را دم‏به‏دم
entries.english: This body is like the lion on the banner: thought is causing it to move continually.

entries.farsi: فكر كان از مشرق آيد آن صباست / ‏وان كه از مغرب دبور با وباست
entries.english: The thought that comes from the east is the east-wind, and that which from the west is the west-wind fraught with pesti­ lence. The east of this wind of thought is different; the west of this wind of thought is from Yonder side.

entries.farsi: مشرق اين باد فكرت ديگر است / ‏مغرب اين باد فكرت ز آن سر است
entries.english: The moon is inanimate, and its east is inanimate: the heart’s east is the soul of the soul of Soul.

entries.farsi: مه جماد است و بود شرقش جماد / جان جان جان بود شرق فؤاد
entries.english: The east of that Sun which illumines the inward part— the sun of day is the husk and reflection thereof;

entries.farsi: شرق خورشيدى كه شد باطن فروز / قشر و عكس آن بود خورشيد روز
entries.english: For when the body is dead without the flame, neither day nor night appears to it;

entries.farsi: ز انكه چون مرده بود تن بى‏لهب / ‏پيش او نه روز بنمايد نه شب
entries.english: But though it is not, when this is in perfection, it maintains itself intact without night and day,

entries.farsi: ور نباشد آن چو اين باشد تمام / ‏بى‏شب و بى‏روز دارد انتظام
entries.english: Just as the eye, without moon and sun, sees moon and sun in dream

entries.farsi: همچنان كه چشم مى‏بيند به خواب / ‏بى‏مه و خورشيد ماه و آفتاب
entries.english: Since our sleep is the brother of death, O such and such, know that brother from this brother.

entries.farsi: نوم ما چون شد اخ الموت اى فالن / ‏زين برادر آن برادر را بدان
entries.english: And if they tell you that that is the branch of this, do not hear it, O follower of authority, without certain knowledge.

entries.farsi: ور بگويندت كه هست آن فرع اين / ‏مشنو آن را اى مقلد بى‏يقين
entries.english: During sleep your spirit is beholding the representation of a state which you will not behold, whilst you are awake, in twenty years,

entries.farsi: مى‏ببيند خواب جانت وصف حال / ‏كه به بيدارى نبينى بيست سال
entries.english: And you are running, for lifetimes, to the sagacious kings in quest of the interpretation thereof,

entries.farsi: در پى تعبير آن تو عمرها / مى‏دوى سوى شهان با دها
entries.english: Saying, “Tell, what is the interpretation of that dream?” To call such a mystery a “branch” is currishness.

entries.farsi: كه بگو آن خواب را تعبير چيست / ‏فرع گفتن اين چنين سر را سگى است
entries.english: This is the sleep of the common; but truly the sleep of the elect is the root of privilege and election.

entries.farsi: خواب عام است اين و خود خواب خواص / ‏باشد اصل اجتبا و اختصاص
entries.english: There needs to be the elephant, in order that, when he sleeps supinely, he may dream of the land of Hindustan.

entries.farsi: خر نبيند هيچ هندستان به خواب / ‏خر ز هندستان نكرده‏ست اغتراب
entries.english: The ass does not dream of Hindustan at all: the ass has never journeyed from Hindustan to a foreign country.

entries.farsi: پيل بايد تا چو خسبد او ستان / ‏خواب بيند خطه‏ى هندوستان
entries.english: There is need of the elephant-like and very robust spirit, that in sleep it may be able to go speedily to Hindustan.

entries.farsi: جان همچون پيل بايد نيك زفت / ‏تا به خواب او هند داند رفت تفت
entries.english: Because of desire the elephant remembers Hindustan; then by night that remembrance of his takes form.

entries.farsi: اذْكُرُوا اللهََّ كار هر اوباش نيست / ‏ارْجِعِي بر پاى هر قالش نيست
entries.english: Remember Allah is not a work of every rascal; Return thou is not on the foot of every reprobate.

entries.farsi: ليك تو آيس مشو هم پيل باش / ‏ور نه پيلى در پى تبديل باش
entries.english: But still do not you despair, be an elephant; and if you are not an elephant, be in quest of transmutation.

entries.farsi: كيميا سازان گردون را ببين / ‏بشنو از ميناگران هر دم طنين
entries.english: Behold the alchemists of Heaven; hear at every moment the sound from the makers of the philosophers’ stone.

entries.farsi: نقش بندانند در جو فلك / ‏كارسازانند بهر لى و لك
entries.english: They are designers in the celestial atmosphere; they are workers for me and you.

entries.farsi: گر نبينى خلق مشكين جيب را / بنگر اى شب كور اين آسيب را
entries.english: If you do not see the musky-bosomed people, behold this touch, O night-blind one.

entries.farsi: هر دم آسيب است بر ادراك تو / نبت نو نو رسته بين از خاك تو
entries.english: At every moment the touch is upon your apprehension: behold the plants ever springing up anew from your earth!

entries.farsi: زين بد ابراهيم ادهم ديده خواب / ‏بسط هندستان دل را بى‏حجاب
entries.english: Of this was Ibrahim son of Adham, who beheld in sleep, without veil, the unfolding of the spiritual Hindustan.

entries.farsi: الجرم زنجيرها را بر دريد / مملكت برهم زد و شد ناپديد
entries.english: Of necessity, he burst the chains asunder and dashed his kingdom to pieces and disappeared.

entries.farsi: آن نشان ديد هندستان بود / كه جهد از خواب و ديوانه شود
entries.english: The sign of beholding Hindustan is that he starts up from sleep and becomes mad.

entries.farsi: مى‏فشاند خاك بر تدبيرها / مى‏دراند حلقه‏ى زنجيرها
entries.english: He will scatter dust upon plans and will burst the links of the chains,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه گفت پيغمبر ز نور / كه نشانش آن بود اندر صدور
entries.english: Even as the Prophet said of the light, that the sign thereof in breasts

entries.farsi: كه تجافى آرد از دار الغرور / هم انابت آرد از دار السرور
entries.english: Is that he withdraws from the abode of delusion and also turns back from the abode of joy.

entries.farsi: بهر شرح اين حديث مصطفى / داستانى بشنو اى يار صفا
entries.english: For the exposition of this hadíth of Mustafa, listen to a tale, O sincere friend.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن پادشاه زاده كه پادشاهى حقيقى به وى روى نمود، يَوْمَ يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ وَ أُمِّهِ وَ أَبِيهِ‏نقد وقت او شد، پادشاهى اين خاك توده‏ى كودك طبعان كه قلعه گيرى نام كنند آن كودك كه چيره آيد بر سر خاك توده بر آيد و الف زند كه قلعه مراست كودكان ديگر بر وى رشك برند كه التراب ربيع الصبيان، آن پادشاه زاده چو از قيد رنگها برست گفت من اين خاكهاى رنگين را همان خاك دون مى‏گويم زر و اطلس و اكسون نمى‏گويم من از اين اكسون رستم به يكسون رفتم، وَ آتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَ بِيًّا
ارشاد حق را مرور سالها حاجت نيست در قدرت كُنْ فَيَكُونُ هيچ كس سخن قابليت نگويد
sections.title_en: Story of the prince to whom the true kingdom displayed itself, “on the Day when a man shall flee from his brother and his mother and his father” became the object of his immediate experience; the kingdom of this earth-heap of the childish called “castle-taking,” the child that gains the victory mounts upon the earth-heap and says boastfully, “The castle belongs to me,” while the other children envy him; for earth is the pastime of boys. When the prince was delivered from the bondage of colours,

he said, “I say that these coloured pieces of earth are just the same vile earth; I do not call them gold and satin and brocade: I have been delivered from this brocade (aksún) and have gone to that which is simple (yaksún).” “And We bestowed wisdom upon him whilst he was yet a boy”; it needs not the passing of years for the guidance of God: none speaks of the capacity to receive in the Power of Be, and it is.

entries.farsi: پادشاهى داشت يك برنا پسر / باطن و ظاهر مزين از هنر
entries.english: A certain king had a young son, adorned with excellence within and without.

entries.farsi: خواب ديد او كان پسر ناگه بمرد / صافى عالم بر آن شه گشت درد
entries.english: He dreamed that suddenly that son died: the pure pleasure of the world was changed, for the king, to dregs.

entries.farsi: خشك شد از تاب آتش مشك او / كه نماند از تف آتش اشك او
entries.english: His water-skin was dried up by the heat of the fire, for because of the glow of the fire his tears remained not.

entries.farsi: آن چنان پر شد ز دود و درد شاه / ‏كه نمى‏يابيد در وى راه آه
entries.english: The king became so full of smoke and grief that sighs were finding no way into him.

entries.farsi: خواست مردن قالبش بى‏كار شد / عمر مانده بود شه بيدار شد
entries.english: He was about to die, his body became inert; his life had been left: the king awoke.

entries.farsi: شاديى آمد ز بيداريش پيش / ‏كه نديده بود اندر عمر خويش
entries.english: From awaking, there came to him a joy which he had not experienced in his life;

entries.farsi: كه ز شادى خواست هم فانى شدن / ‏بس مطوق آمد اين جان و بدن
entries.english: For from joy likewise he was about to perish: this spirit and body is mightily shackled with the collar.

entries.farsi: از دم غم مى‏بميرد اين چراغ / ‏و ز دم شادى بميرد اينت الغ
entries.english: This lamp dies from the breath of sorrow, and it also dies from the breath of joy. Here, look, is a pleasant jest!

entries.farsi: در ميان اين دو مرگ او زنده است / ‏اين مطوق شكل جاى خنده است
entries.english: He is living between these two deaths: this that resembles one shackled with a collar is an occasion for laughter.

entries.farsi: شاه با خود گفت شادى را سبب / ‏آن چنان غم بود از تسبيب رب
entries.english: The king said to himself, “In consequence of the Lord’s causation such a sorrow as that was the cause of joy.”

entries.farsi: اى عجب يك چيز از يك روى مرگ / ‏و آن ز يك روى دگر احيا و برگ
entries.english: Oh, wonderful the same thing from one aspect death and from another aspect a quickening with life and a provision!

entries.farsi: آن يكى نسبت بدان حالت هالك / ‏باز هم آن سوى ديگر امتساك
entries.english: The same thing is destructive in relation to one circumstance, while again it is preservative in regard to another.

entries.farsi: شادى تن سوى دنياوى كمال / ‏سوى روز عاقبت نقص و زوال
entries.english: Bodily joy is perfection in regard to that which is of the present world, defect and failure in regard to the Day of the latter end.

entries.farsi: خنده را در خواب هم تعبير خوان / ‏گريه گويد با دريغ و اندهان
entries.english: The dream interpreter, too, declares laughter in dreams to be weeping with regrets and grief,

entries.farsi: شاه انديشيد كاين غم خود گذشت / ‏ليك جان از جنس اين بد ظن بگشت
entries.english: for weeping in dreams joy and gladness are in the interpretation, O gleeful man.

entries.farsi: گريه را در خواب شادى و فرح / ‏هست در تعبير اى صاحب مرح
entries.english: The king pondered, saying, “This sorrow, indeed, is past, but my soul has become suspicious of one of the same kind;

entries.farsi: ور رسد خارى چنين اندر قدم / ‏كه رود گل يادگارى بايدم
entries.english: And if such a thorn enters my foot that the rose departs, I must have a keepsake.”

entries.farsi: چون فنا را شد سبب بى‏منتهى / پس كدامين راه را بنديم ما
entries.english: Since the causes of mortality are infinite, which road, then, shall we bar?

entries.farsi: صد دريچه و در سوى مرگ لديغ / ‏مى‏كند اندر گشادن ژيغ ژيغ
entries.english: A hundred windows and doors facing towards mordant death are ever creaking as they are opened,

entries.farsi: ژيغ ژيغ تلخ آن درهاى مرگ / ‏نشنود گوش حريص از حرص برگ
entries.english: From greed for provision the ear of the covetous does not hear the harsh creaking of those doors of death.

entries.farsi: از سوى تن دردها بانگ در است / ‏و ز سوى خصمان جفا بانگ در است
entries.english: From the side of the body, pains are the noise of the door; and from the side of enemies, maltreatment is the noise of the door.

entries.farsi: جان من بر خوان دمى فهرست طب / ‏نار علتها نظر كن ملتهب
entries.english: My dear friend read for one moment the table of contents of Medicine; look at the flaming fire of diseases!

entries.farsi: ز آن همه‏ى غرها در اين خانه ره است / ‏هر دو گامى پر ز كژدمها چه است
entries.english: Through all those tumours there is a way into this house: at every two steps there is a pit full of scorpions.

entries.farsi: باد تند است و چراغم ابترى / ‏زو بگيرانم چراغ ديگرى
entries.english: “The wind is fierce and my lamp is a docked one: I will light another lamp from it,

entries.farsi: تا بود كز هر دو يك وافى شود / گر به باد آن يك چراغ از جا رود
entries.english: So that maybe one complete will arise from them both, if that one lamp be put out by the wind,”

entries.farsi: تا كه روزى كاين بميرد ناگهان / ‏پيش چشم خود نهد او شمع جان
entries.english: Like the gnostic who, for the sake of freedom from care, has lit the candle of the heart from this defective lamp of the body,

entries.farsi: همچو عارف كز تن ناقص چراغ / ‏شمع دل افروخت از بهر فراغ
entries.english: In order that, one day when this dies of a sudden, he may place before his eye the candle of the spirit.

entries.farsi: او نكرد اين فهم پس داد از غرر / شمع فانى را به فانيى دگر
entries.english: He did not understand this; therefore in his heedlessness he applied the perishing candle to another perishable.

sections.title_fa: ‏عروس آوردن پادشاه فرزند خود را از خوف انقطاع نسل
sections.title_en: How the king brought his son a bride for fear of his race coming to an end.

entries.farsi: پس عروسى خواست بايد بهر او / تا نمايد زين تزوج نسل رو
entries.english: “It is necessary, then, to seek a bride for him, that from this marriage offspring may appear,

entries.farsi: گر رود سوى فنا اين باز باز / فرخ او گردد ز بعد باز باز
entries.english: If this falcon returns to the state of mortality his young may become a falcon after the falcon,

entries.farsi: صورت اين باز گر ز ينجا رود / معنى او در ولد باقى بود
entries.english: If the form of this falcon go from here, his inward meaning may endure in his son.

entries.farsi: بهر اين فرمود آن شاه نبيه / ‏مصطفى كه الولد سر ابيه
entries.english: On account of this, that renowned king, Mustafa, said, ‘The son is the marrow of his father.’

entries.farsi: بهر اين معنى همه‏ى خلق از شعف / ‏مى‏بياموزند طفالن را حرف
entries.english: For this reason all people, by heartfelt love, teach their children trades,

entries.farsi: تا بماند آن معانى در جهان / ‏چون شود آن قالب ايشان نهان
entries.english: To the end that these inward meanings may remain in the world when that body of theirs becomes hidden.

entries.farsi: حق به حكمت حرصشان داده ست جد / بهر رشد هر صغير مستعد
entries.english: God in His wisdom has given them intense desire for the right guidance of every little one capable.

entries.farsi: من هم از بهر دوام نسل خويش / ‏جفت خواهم پور خود را خوب كيش
entries.english: I too, for the purpose of the continuance of my race, will seek for my son a wife of good principles.

entries.farsi: دخترى خواهم ز نسل صالحى / ‏نى ز نسل پادشاهى كالحى
entries.english: I will seek a girl who is the offspring of a righteous man, not the offspring of a stern-faced king.”

entries.farsi: شاه خود اين صالح است آزاد اوست / نى اسير حرص فرج است و گلوست
entries.english: This righteous man is himself a king, he is free, he is not the prisoner of lust and gluttony.

entries.farsi: شد مفازه باديه‏ى خون‏خوار نام / ‏نيك بخت آن پيس را كردند عام
entries.english: They have given prisoners the title of “king” by inversion, just as Kafur is the name of that Negro.

entries.farsi: مر اسيران را لقب كردند شاه / ‏عكس چون كافور نام آن سياه
entries.english: The blood-drinking wilderness is named mafáza (place of safety); the common call the leper Níkbakht.

entries.farsi: بر اسير شهوت و خشم و امل / ‏بر نوشته مير يا صدر اجل
entries.english: They have described the prisoner of lust and anger and ambition by the name of Mír or Sadr-i ajall (most honourable prince).

entries.farsi: آن اسيران اجل را عام داد / نام اميران اجل اندر بالد
entries.english: To those prisoners of Doom (asírán-i ajal) the common in the lands have given the title of “most honourable Amírs” (amírán-i ajall).

entries.farsi: صدر خوانندش كه در صف نعال / ‏جان او پست است يعنى جاه و مال
entries.english: They call high-placed (Sadr) him whose soul is low in the vestibule, that is to say, power and riches.

entries.farsi: شاه چون با زاهدى خويشى گزيد / اين خبر در گوش خاتونان رسيد
entries.english: When the king chose relationship with an ascetic, this news came to the ears of ladies.

sections.title_fa: اختيار كردن پادشاه دختر درويش زاهدى را از جهت پسر و اعتراض كردن اهل حرم و ننگ داشتن ‏ايشان از پيوندى درويش
sections.title_en: How the king chose the daughter of a poor ascetic for his son and how the ladies of the harem
raised objections and disdained the alliance with the dervish.

entries.farsi: مادر شه زاده گفت از نقص عقل / ‏شرط كفويت بود در عقل و نقل
entries.english: The prince’s mother, from deficiency of understanding, said, “According to reason and tradition equality is requisite.

entries.farsi: تو ز شح و بخل خواهى و ز دها / تا ببندى پور ما را بر گدا
entries.english: You from stinginess and miserliness and shrewdness wish to ally our son with a beggar.”

entries.farsi: گفت صالح را گدا گفتن خطاست / ‏كاو غنى القلب از داد خداست
entries.english: He said, “It is a fault to call the righteous man a beggar, for through the grace of God he is spiritually rich.

entries.farsi: در قناعت مى‏گريزد از تقى / نه از لئيمى و كسل همچون گدا
entries.english: He is taking refuge in contentment because of piety, not because of meanness and laziness, like the beggar.

entries.farsi: قلتى كان از قناعت وز تقاست / ‏آن ز فقر و قلت دونان جداست
entries.english: The penury which arises from contentment and piety is distinct from the poverty and penury of the base.

entries.farsi: حبه‏اى آن گر بيابد سر نهد / وين ز گنج زر به همت مى‏جهد
entries.english: If that one finds a single coin, he bows his head, while this one in his lofty aspiration recoils from a treasure of gold.

entries.farsi: شه كه او از حرص قصد هر حرام / ‏مى‏كند او را گدا گويد همام
entries.english: The king who from greed is betaking himself to everything unlawful— the man of noble mind calls him a beggar.”

entries.farsi: گفت كو شهر و قالع او را جهيز / يا نثار گوهر و دينار ريز
entries.english: She said, “Where are his cities and castles the wedding-outfit, or scattering gems and pieces of gold?”

entries.farsi: در مالحت خود نظير خود نداشت / ‏چهره‏اش تابان‏تر از خورشيد چاشت
entries.english: He said, “Go! Whoever prefers to care for religion, God cuts off from him all remaining cares.”

entries.farsi: غالب آمد شاه و دادش دخترى / ‏از نژاد صالحى خوش جوهرى
entries.english: The king prevailed and gave to him a maiden of goodly nature, belonging to the family of a righteous man.

entries.farsi: گفت رو هر كاو غم دين بر گزيد / باقى غمها خدا از وى بريد
entries.english: Truly, she had none to rival her in loveliness: her face was brighter than the sun at morn.

entries.farsi: حسن دختر اين خصالش آن چنان / ‏كز نكويى مى‏نگنجد در بيان
entries.english: Such was the maiden’s beauty; and her qualities were such that, on account of their excellence, they are not contained in description.

entries.farsi: صيد دين كن تا رسد اندر تبع / ‏حسن و مال و جاه و بخت منتفع
entries.english: Make religion your prey, that in consequence there may come beauty and riches and power and advantageous fortune.

entries.farsi: آخرت قطار اشتر دان به ملك / ‏در تبع دنياش همچون پشم و پشك
entries.english: Know that the next world, in respect of ownership, is files of camels: the present world is its corollary, like the hair and dung.

entries.farsi: پشم بگزينى شتر نبود ترا / ور بود اشتر چه قيمت پشم را
entries.english: You choose the hair, the camel will not be yours, and if the camel be yours, what value has the hair?

entries.farsi: چون بر آمد اين نكاح آن شاه را / با نژاد صالحان بى‏مرا
entries.english: When the marriage with the family of the uncontentious righteous folk was achieved by the king,

entries.farsi: از قضا كمپيركى جادو كه بود / عاشق شه زاده‏ى با حسن و جود
entries.english: By destiny a decrepit old witch, who was in love with the handsome and generous prince—

entries.farsi: دويى كردش عجوزه‏ى كابلى / كه برد ز آن رشك سحر بابلى
entries.english: An old woman of Kabul—bewitched him with a sorcery of which the magic of Babylon would be envious.

entries.farsi: شه بچه شد عاشق كمپير زشت / ‏تا عروس و آن عروسى را بهشت
entries.english: The prince fell in love with the ugly hag, so that he abandoned his bride and the wedding.

entries.farsi: يك سيه ديوى و كابولى زنى / ‏گشت بر شه زاده ناگه ره زنى
entries.english: A black devil and woman of Kabul suddenly waylaid the prince.

entries.farsi: آن نود ساله عجوز گنده كس / ‏نه خرد هشت آن ملك را و نه بس
entries.english: That stinking ninety year old hag left the prince neither wisdom nor understanding.

entries.farsi: تا به سالى بود شه زاده اسير / بوسه جايش نعل كفش گنده پير
entries.english: For a year the prince was captivated: the sole of the hag’s shoe was the place where he bestowed his kisses.

entries.farsi: صحبت كمپير او را مى‏درود / تا ز كاهش نيم جانى مانده بود
entries.english: Association with the hag was mowing him, till through wasting away half a spirit remained.

entries.farsi: ديگران از ضعف وى با درد سر / او ز سكر سحر از خود بى‏خبر
entries.english: Others had the headache on account of his weakness, he, from the intoxicating effect of the sorcery, was unconscious of himself.

entries.farsi: اين جهان بر شاه چون زندان شده / ‏وين پسر بر گريه‏شان خندان شده
entries.english: This world had become a prison to the king, while this son was laughing at their tears.

entries.farsi: شاه بس بى‏چاره شد در برد و مات / ‏روز و شب مى‏كرد قربان و زكات
entries.english: The king became exceedingly desperate in the struggle: day and night he was offering sacrifice and giving alms,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر چاره كه مى‏كرد آن پدر / عشق كمپيرك همى‏شد بيشتر
entries.english: For whatever remedy the father might apply, love for the old hag would always increase.

entries.farsi: پس يقين گشتش كه مطلق آن سرى است / ‏چاره او را بعد از اين البه‏گرى است
entries.english: Then it became clear to him that that was absolutely a mystery, and that thenceforth his remedy was supplication.

entries.farsi: سجده مى‏كرد او كه فرمانت رواست / ‏غير حق بر ملك حق فرمان كه راست
entries.english: He was prostrating himself in prayer, saying, “It beseems You to command: to whom but God belongs the command over God’s kingdom?

entries.farsi: ليك اين مسكين همى‏سوزد چو عود / دست گيرش اى رحيم و اى ودود
entries.english: But this poor wretch is burning like aloes-wood: take his hand, O Merciful and Loving One!”

entries.farsi: تا ز يا رب يا رب و افغان شاه / ‏ساحرى استاد پيش آمد ز راه
entries.english: Until, because of the “O Lord! O Lord!” and lamentation of the king, a master-magician came from the road into his presence.

sections.title_fa: ‏مستجاب شدن دعاى پادشاه در خالص پسرش از جادوى كابلى
sections.title_en: How the king’s prayer for the deliverance of his son from the witch of Kabul was granted.

entries.farsi: او شنيده بود از دور اين خبر / كه اسير پيره زن گشت آن پسر
entries.english: He had heard from afar the news that that boy had been captivated by an old woman,

entries.farsi: كان عجوزه بود اندر جادويى / ‏بى‏نظير و ايمن از مثل و دويى
entries.english: A crone who in witchery was unrivalled and secure from likeness and duality

entries.farsi: دست بر بالاى دست است اى فتى / در فن و در زور تا ذات خدا
entries.english: Hand is above hand, O youth, in skill and in strength up to the Essence of God.

entries.farsi: منتهاى دستها دست خداست / ‏بحر بى‏شك منتهاى سيلهاست
entries.english: The ultimate end of hands is the Hand of God: the ultimate end of torrents is undoubtedly the sea.

entries.farsi: هم از او گيرند مايه ابرها / هم بدو باشد نهايت سيل را
entries.english: From it the clouds take their origin, and in it too the torrent has an end.

entries.farsi: گفت شاهش كاين پسر از دست رفت / گفت اينك آمدم درمان زفت
entries.english: The king said to him, “This boy has passed out of control.” He said, “Look you, I am come as a potent remedy.

entries.farsi: نيست همتا زال را زين ساحران / ‏جز من داهى رسيده ز آن كران
entries.english: None of these sorcerers is equal to the old woman except me, the sagacious one, who have arrived from yonder shore.

entries.farsi: چون كف موسى به امر كردگار / نك بر آرم من ز سحر او دمار
entries.english: Lo, by command of the Creator, I, like the hand of Moses, will utterly destroy her sorcery;

entries.farsi: كه مرا اين علم آمد ز آن طرف / ‏نه ز شاگردى سحر مستخف
entries.english: For to me this knowledge has come from yonder region, not from having been schooled in the sorcery which is held cheap.

entries.farsi: آمدم تا بر گشايم سحر او / تا نماند شاه زاده زرد رو
entries.english: I am come to undo her sorcery, so that the prince may not remain pale-faced.

entries.farsi: سوى گورستان برو وقت سحور / پهلوى ديوار هست اسپيد گور
entries.english: Go to the graveyard at the hour of the meal taken before dawn: beside the wall is a whitened tomb.

entries.farsi: سوى قبله باز كاو آن جاى را / تا ببينى قدرت و صنع خدا
entries.english: Dig up that place in the direction of the qibla, that you may behold the power and the working of God.”

entries.farsi: بس دراز است اين حكايت تو ملول / ‏زبده را گويم رها كردم فضول
entries.english: This story is very long, and you are weary: I will relate the cream, I dismiss what is superfluous.

entries.farsi: آن گرههاى گران را بر گشاد / پس ز محنت پور شه را راه داد
entries.english: He untied those heavy knots: then he gave to the king’s son a way out of the affliction.

entries.farsi: آن پسر با خويش آمد شد دوان / ‏سوى تخت شاه با صد امتحان
entries.english: The boy came to himself and with a hundred tribulations went running towards the throne of the king.

entries.farsi: سجده كرد و بر زمين مى‏زد ذقن / ‏در بغل كرده پسر تيغ و كفن
entries.english: He made prostration and was beating his chin on the earth: the boy held in his arms a sword and winding-sheet.

entries.farsi: شاه آيين بست و اهل شهر شاد / و آن عروس نااميد بى‏مراد
entries.english: The king ordered the city to be decorated, and the citizens and the despairing disappointed bride rejoiced.

entries.farsi: عالم از سر زنده گشت و پر فروز / عالم از سر زنده گشت و پر فروز
entries.english: The world revived once more and was filled with radiance: “Oh, what a wondrous difference between that day and to-day!”

entries.farsi: جادوى كمپير از غصه بمرد / روى و خوى زشت با مالك سپرد
entries.english: The king made such a wedding-feast for him that sugared julep was before the dogs.

entries.farsi: يك عروسى كرد شاه او را چنان / ‏كه جالب قند بد پيش سگان
entries.english: The old witch died of vexation and gave up her hideous face and nature to Malik.

entries.farsi: شاه زاده در تعجب مانده بود / كز من او عقل و نظر چون در ربود
entries.english: The prince was left in amazement: “How did she rob me of understanding and insight?”

entries.farsi: نو عروسى ديد همچون ماه حسن / ‏كه همى‏زد بر مليحان راه حسن
entries.english: He beheld a newly wedded bride like the beauteous moon, who was infesting the road of beauty against the fair ones.

entries.farsi: گشت بى‏هوش و به رو اندر فتاد / تا سه روز از جسم وى گم شد فؤاد
entries.english: He became senseless and fell on his face: for three days the heart vanished from his body.

entries.farsi: سه شبانه روز او ز خود بى‏هوش گشت / ‏تا كه خلق از غشى او پر جوش گشت
entries.english: Three days and nights he became unconscious of himself, so that the people were perturbed by his swoon.

entries.farsi: از گالب و از عالج آمد بخود / اندك اندك فهم گشتش نيك و بد
entries.english: By means of rose-water and remedies he came to himself: little by little, good and evil were apprehended by him.

entries.farsi: بعد سالى گفت شاهش در سخن / ‏كاى پسر ياد آر از آن يار كهن
entries.english: After a year the king said to him jokingly in conversation, “O son, bethink you of that old friend,

entries.farsi: ياد آور ز آن ضجيع و ز آن فراش / ‏تا بدين حد بى‏وفا و مر مباش
entries.english: Bethink you of that bedfellow and that bed: do not be so faithless and harsh!”

entries.farsi: همچنان باشد چو مومن راه يافت / ‏سوى نور حق ز ظلمت روى تافت
entries.english: “Go to!” said he; “I have found the abode of joy; I am delivered from the pit of the abode of delusion.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو من يافتم دار السرور / وارهيدم از چه دار الغرور
entries.english: It is even so: when the true believer has found the way towards the Light of God, he averts his face from the darkness.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه شه زاده آدمى بچه است و خليفه‏ى خداست پدرش آدم صفى خليفه‏ى حق مسجود ماليك و آن كمپير كابلى دنياست كه آدمى بچه را از پدر ببريد به سحر و انبيا و اوليا آن ‏طبيب تدارك كننده
sections.title_en: Explaining that the prince is Man, the vicegerent of God, and that his father is Adam,

the chosen one, the vicegerent of God, he to whom the angels bowed in worship; and that the old hag of Kabul is the World which separated Man from his Father by sorcery, while the prophets and saints are like the physician who applied the remedy.

entries.farsi: اى برادر دان كه شه زاده توى / ‏در جهان كهنه زاده از نوى
entries.english: O brother, know that you are the prince born anew in the old world.

entries.farsi: كابلى جادو اين دنياست كاو / كرد مردان را اسير رنگ و بو
entries.english: The witch of Kabul is this World which made men captive to colour and perfume.

entries.farsi: چون در افكندت در اين آلوده روذ / دم‏به‏دم مى‏خوان و مى‏دم قُلْ أعوذ
entries.english: Since she has cast you into this polluted stream, continually recite and utter, Say, I take refuge.

entries.farsi: تا رهى زين جادويى و زين قلق / ‏استعاذت خواه از رب الفلق
entries.english: In order that you may be delivered from this witchery and this distress, beg of the Lord of the daybreak that you may say “I take refuge.”

entries.farsi: ز آن نبى دنيات را سحاره خواند / كاو به افسون خلق را در چه نشاند
entries.english: The Prophet called this world of yours an enchantress because through her spells she lodged mankind in the pit.

entries.farsi: هين فسون گرم دارد گنده پير / كرده شاهان را دم گرمش اسير
entries.english: Beware! The stinking hag has hot spells: her hot breath has made kings captive.

entries.farsi: در درون سينه نفاثات اوست / ‏عقده‏هاى سحر را اثبات اوست
entries.english: She is the witches who blow within breast: she is the maintaining the knots of sorcery.
