entries.farsi: ساحره‏ى دنيا قوى دانا زنى است / ‏حل سحر او به پاى عامه نيست
entries.english: The sorceress, the World, is a mightily cunning woman: it is not in the power of the common to undo her sorcery;

entries.farsi: ور گشادى عقد او را عقلها / انبيا را كى فرستادى خدا
entries.english: And if understandings could undo her knot, how should God have sent the prophets?

entries.farsi: هين طلب كن خوش دمى عقده گشا / راز دان يَفْعَلُ اللهَُّ ما يشاء
entries.english: Listen; seek one whose breath is pure, a looser of knots, one who knows the mystery of God does what He wills.

entries.farsi: همچو ماهى بسته استت او به شست / ‏شاه زاده ماند سالى و تو شصت
entries.english: She has imprisoned you, like a fish, in her net: the prince remained one year, and you sixty.

entries.farsi: شصت سال از شست او در محنتى / ‏نه خوشى نه بر طريق سنتى
entries.english: From her net you art in tribulation sixty years: neither are you happy nor do you walk in the way of the Sunna.

entries.farsi: فاسقى بد بخت نه دنيات خوب / ‏نه رهيده از وبال و از ذنوب
entries.english: You are a miserable unrighteous man: neither is your worldly life good nor are you delivered from guilt and sins.

entries.farsi: نفخ او اين عقده‏ها را سخت كرد / پس طلب كن نفخه‏ى خالق فرد
entries.english: Her breathing has made these knots tight: seek, then, the breathing of the unique Creator,

entries.farsi: تا نَفَخْ تُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي ترا / وا رهاند زين و گويد برتر آ
entries.english: In order that “I breathed of My spirit into him” may deliver you from this and say, “Come higher!”

entries.farsi: جز به نفخ حق نسوزد نفخ سحر / نفخ قهر است اين و آن دم نفخ مهر
entries.english: The breathing of sorcery is not consumed save by the breathing of God: this is the breathing of wrath, that exhalation is the breathing of love.

entries.farsi: رحمت او سابق است از قهر او / سابقى خواهى برو سابق بجو
entries.english: His mercy is prior to His wrath: you desire priority, go; seek that which is prior,

entries.farsi: تا رسى اندر نفوس زوجت / كاى شه مسحور اينك مخرجت
entries.english: That you may attain unto the souls that are wedded; for lo, this, O enchanted prince, is your way of escape.

entries.farsi: با وجود زال نايد آن حالل / ‏در شبيكه در بر آن پر دالل
entries.english: With the existence of the old woman, there can be no undoing, in the net and in the arms of that full of blandishments.

entries.farsi: نه بگفته‏ست آن سراج امتان / ‏اين جهان و آن جهان را ضرتان
entries.english: Has not the Lamp of the peoples called this world and that world the two fellow-wives?

entries.farsi: پس وصال اين فراق آن بود / صحت اين تن سقام جان بود
entries.english: Therefore union with this is separation from that: the health of this body is the sickness of the spirit.

entries.farsi: سخت مى‏آيد فراق اين ممر / پس فراق آن مقر دان سخت‏تر
entries.english: Hard is the separation from this transitory abode: know, then, that the separation from that permanent abode is harder

entries.farsi: اى كه صبرت نيست از دنياى دون / ‏چونت صبر است از خدا اى دوست چون
entries.english: Since it is hard for you to be separated from the form, how hard must it be to be parted from its Maker!

entries.farsi: چون فراق نقش سخت آيد ترا / تا چه سخت آيد ز نقاشش جدا
entries.english: O you that have not the patience to do without the vile world, how, O friend, how have you the patience to do without God?

entries.farsi: چون كه صبرت نيست زين آب سياه / ‏چون صبورى دارى از چشمه‏ى اله
entries.english: Since you have not the patience to do without this black water, how have you the patience to do without God’s fountain?

entries.farsi: چون كه بى‏اين شرب كم دارى سكون / ‏چون ز ابرارى جدا و ز يشربون
entries.english: Since you are restless without this drink, how are you apart from the righteous and from they shall drink?

entries.farsi: گر ببينى يك نفس حسن ودود / اندر آتش افكنى جان و وجود
entries.english: If for one moment you behold the beauty of the Loving One and cast your soul and existence into the fire,

entries.farsi: جيفه بينى بعد از آن اين شرب را / چون ببينى كر و فر قرب را
entries.english: After that you will regard this drink as a carcass, when you behold the glory and splendour of nearness.

entries.farsi: همچو شه زاده رسى در يار خويش / پس برون آرى ز پا تو خار خويش
entries.english: Like the prince, you will attain unto your Beloved; then you will draw out from your foot the thorn of self.

entries.farsi: جهد كن در بى‏خودى خود را بياب / ‏زودتر و اهلل اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: Strive for selflessness, find yourself as soon as possible— and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: هر زمانى هين مشو با خويش جفت / ‏هر زمان چون خر در آب و گل ميفت
entries.english: Take heed, never be wedded to self: do not, like an ass, be always falling into water and mud.

entries.farsi: از قصور چشم باشد آن عثار / كه نبيند شيب و بالا كوروار
entries.english: That stumbling arises from shortsightedness; for like a blind man, he does not see the ups and downs.

entries.farsi: بوى پيراهان يوسف كن سند / ز انكه بويش چشم روشن مى‏كند
entries.english: Make the scent of Joseph’s shirt your stay, because his scent makes the eye clear.

entries.farsi: صورت پنهان و آن نور جبين / ‏كرده چشم انبيا را دور بين
entries.english: The hidden Form and the Light of that Brow have made the eyes of the prophets far-seeing.

entries.farsi: صورتش نور است و در تحقيق نار / گر ضيا خواهى دو دست از وى بدار
entries.english: The Light of that Countenance will deliver from the fire: listen, be not content with borrowed light.

entries.farsi: نور آن رخسار برهاند ز نار / هين مشو قانع به نور مستعار
entries.english: This light makes the eye to see that which is transient: it makes body and mind and spirit to be scabby.

entries.farsi: چشم را اين نور حالى بين كند / جسم و عقل و روح را گرگين كند
entries.english: It has the appearance of light, but in reality it is fire: keep your hands off it, if you desire the radiance.

entries.farsi: دم به دم در رو فتد هر جا رود / ديده و جانى كه حالى بين بود
entries.english: The eye and spirit that sees the transient falls on its face continually wherever it goes.

entries.farsi: دور بيند دور بين بى‏هنر / همچنان كه دور ديدن خواب در
entries.english: A far-seeing man who lacks knowledge may see far, just as far sight in dreams.

entries.farsi: خفته باشى بر لب جو خشك لب / ‏مى‏دوى سوى سراب اندر طلب
entries.english: You are asleep with parched lips on the bank of the river, and are running in search of water towards the mirage.

entries.farsi: دور مى‏بينى سراب و مى‏دوى / ‏عاشق آن بينش خود مى‏شوى
entries.english: You see the mirage far away and run: you become in love with your own sight.

entries.farsi: مى‏زنى در خواب با ياران تو الف / ‏كه منم بينا دل و پرده شكاف
entries.english: In the dream you boast to your friends, saying, “I am the one whose heart possesses vision, and the one that rends the veil.

entries.farsi: نك بدان سو آب ديدم هين شتاب / ‏تا رويم آن جا و آن باشد سراب
entries.english: Lo, I see water yonder: listen, make haste that we may go there”— and it is the mirage.

entries.farsi: هر قدم زين آب تازى دورتر / دو دوان سوى سراب با غرر
entries.english: At every step you hurry farther away from the water, whilst you keep running on towards the perilous mirage.

entries.farsi: بس كسا عزمى به جايى مى‏كند / از مقامى كان غرض در وى بود
entries.english: Your very setting-out has become the barrier from this that has come close to you.

entries.farsi: عين آن عزمت حجاب اين شده / ‏كه به تو پيوسته است و آمده
entries.english: Oh, many a one sets out to some place from the spot where the object of his quest is.

entries.farsi: ديد و الف خفته مى‏نايد بكار / جز خيالى نيست دست از وى بدار
entries.english: The sight and boasting of the sleeper is of no avail; it is naught but a phantasy: hold aloof from it.

entries.farsi: خوابناكى ليك هم بر راه خسب / ‏اللهَّ اللهَّ بر ره اهلل خسب
entries.english: You are sleepy, but anyhow sleep on the Way: for God’s sake, for God’s sake, sleep on the Way of God,

entries.farsi: تا بود كه سالكى برتو زند / از خيالات نعاست بر كند
entries.english: That perchance a Traveller may attach himself to you and tear you from the phantasies of slumber.

entries.farsi: خفته را گر فكر گردد همچو موى / ‏او از آن دقت نيابد راه كوى
entries.english: If the sleeper’s thought become as a hair, he will not find the way to the Abode by that subtlety.

entries.farsi: فكر خفته گر دو تا و گر سه تاست / ‏هم خطا اندر خطا اندر خطاست
entries.english: Whether the sleeper’s thought is twofold or threefold, still it is error on error on error.

entries.farsi: خفته مى‏بيند عطشهاى شديد / آب اقرب منه مِنْ حَبْلِ الوريد
entries.english: The waves are beating upon him relentlessly; he asleep is running in the long wilderness.

entries.farsi: موج بر وى مى‏زند بى‏احتراز / خفته پويان در بيابان دراز
entries.english: The sleeper dreams of the sore pangs of thirst, the water is nearer unto him than the neck-vein.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن زاهد كه در سال قحط شاد و خندان بود با مفلسى و بسيارى عيال و خلق مى‏مردند از ‏گرسنگى گفتندش چه هنگام شادى است كه هنگام صد تعزيت است گفت مرا بارى نيست
sections.title_en: Story of the ascetic who, notwithstanding his destitution and numerous family, was rejoicing and laughing in a year of drought whilst the people were dying of hunger.
They said to him, “What is the occasion for joy? It is an occasion for a hundred tears.” “For me at any rate it is not,” he replied.

entries.farsi: همچنان كان زاهد اندر سال قحط / بود او خندان و گريان جمله رهط
entries.english: Even as that ascetic was laughing in a year of drought, while all folk were weeping

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش چه جاى خنده است / ‏قحط بيخ مومنان بر كنده است
entries.english: So they said to him, “What is the occasion for laughter, the drought has uprooted the true believers?

entries.farsi: رحمت از ما چشم خود بر دوخته‏ست / ‏ز آفتاب تيز، صحرا سوخته است
entries.english: Mercy has closed its eyes to us: the plain is burnt by the fierce sun.

entries.farsi: كشت و باغ و رز سيه استاده است / ‏در زمين نم نيست نه بالا نه پست
entries.english: Crops and vineyards and vines are standing black: there is no moisture in the earth, neither up nor down.

entries.farsi: خلق مى‏ميرند زين قحط و عذاب / ‏ده ده و صد صد چو ماهى دور از آب
entries.english: The people are dying from this drought and torment by tens and hundreds like fish far from the water.

entries.farsi: بر مسلمانان نمى‏آرى تو رحم / ‏مومنان خويشند و يك تن شحم و لحم
entries.english: You are taking no pity on the Moslems; the true believers are kinsmen and one body fat and flesh.

entries.farsi: رنج يك جزوى ز تن رنج همه ست / ‏گر دم صلح است يا خود ملحمه ست
entries.english: The pain of one part of the body is the pain of all, whether it be the hour of peace or war.”

entries.farsi: گفت در چشم شما قحط است اين / ‏پيش چشمم چون بهشت است اين زمين
entries.english: He replied, “In your eyes this is a drought, to my eye this earth is like Paradise.

entries.farsi: من همى‏بينم به هر دشت و مكان / ‏خوشه‏ها انبه رسيده تا ميان
entries.english: I am beholding in every desert and everywhere ears of wheat in abundance, reaching up to the waist;

entries.farsi: خوشه‏ها در موج از باد صبا / پر بيابان سبزتر از گندنا
entries.english: The wilderness full of ears of wheat in waves by the east-wind, greener than the leek

entries.farsi: ز آزمون من دست بروى مى‏زنم / ‏دست و چشم خويش را چون بر كنم
entries.english: By way of trial I am putting my hand thereon: how should I remove my hand and eye?

entries.farsi: يار فرعون تنيد اى قوم دون / ‏ز آن نمايد مر شما را نيل خون
entries.english: You are friends of Pharaoh, the body, O base people: hence the Nile seems to you to be blood.

entries.farsi: يار موساى خرد گرديد زود / تا نماند خون و بينيد آب رود
entries.english: Quickly become friends of Moses, the intellect, in order that the blood may remain not and you may behold the river-water.

entries.farsi: از پدر با تو جفايى مى‏رود / آن پدر در چشم تو سگ مى‏شود
entries.english: An injustice is proceeding from you towards your father, that father will become a dog in your eyes.

entries.farsi: آن پدر سگ نيست تاثير جفاست / ‏كه چنان رحمت نظر را سگ نماست
entries.english: That father is not a dog: it is the effect of injustice that such mercy appears to your sight a cur.

entries.farsi: گرگ مى‏ديدند يوسف را به چشم / ‏چون كه اخوان را حسودى بود و خشم
entries.english: Since the Joseph’s brothers had envy and anger, they were regarding Joseph as the wolf.

entries.farsi: با پدر چون صلح كردى خشم رفت / ‏آن سگى شد، گشت بابا يار تفت
entries.english: When you have made peace with your father, anger is gone; that currishness departs, and your father at once becomes your friend.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه مجموع عالم صورت عقل كل است چون با عقل كل به كژ روى جفا كردى صورت عالم ترا غم فزايد اغلب احوال چنان كه دل با پدر بد كردى صورت پدر غم فزايد ترا و نتوانى رويش را ‏ديدن اگر چه پيش از آن نور ديده بوده باشد و راحت جان

sections.title_en: Explaining that the whole world is the form of Universal Reason, when by trespassing you act unjustly towards Universal Reason, in most cases the aspect of the world increases your vexation, just as when you show ill-feeling to your father the aspect of your father increases your vexation and you cannot look on his face, though before that he will have been the light of your eye and the comfort of your soul.

entries.farsi: كل عالم صورت عقل كل است / ‏كاوست باباى هر آنك اهل قل است
entries.english: The whole world is the form of Universal Reason, which is the father of whosoever is a follower of the Word.

entries.farsi: چون كسى با عقل كل كفران فزود / صورت كل پيش او هم سگ نمود
entries.english: When any one shows excessive ingratitude to Universal Reason, the form of the universe appears to him a cur accordingly.

entries.farsi: صلح كن با اين پدر عاقى بهل / ‏تا كه فرش زر نمايد آب و گل
entries.english: Make peace with this Father, abandon disobedience, that the water and clay may appear a carpet of gold.

entries.farsi: پس قيامت نقد حال تو بود / پيش تو چرخ و زمين مبدل شود
entries.english: Then the Resurrection will become your present state: heaven and earth will be transfigured before you.

entries.farsi: من كه صلحم دايما با اين پدر / اين جهان چون جنت استم در نظر
entries.english: Since I am ever at peace with this Father, this world is like Paradise in my sight.

entries.farsi: هر زمان نو صورتى و نو جمال / ‏تا ز نو ديدن فرو ميرد مالل
entries.english: At every moment a new form and a new beauty, so that from seeing the new ennui dies away

entries.farsi: بانگ آبش مى‏رسد در گوش من / ‏مست مى‏گردد ضمير و هوش من
entries.english: I see the world to be full of bounty— the waters constantly gushing from the springs.

entries.farsi: من همى‏بينم جهان را پر نعيم / ‏آبها از چشمه‏ها جوشان مقيم
entries.english: The noise of their water is coming into my ear: my inner consciousness and intelligence are being intoxicated.

entries.farsi: شاخه‏ها رقصان شده چون تايبان / ‏برگها كف زن مثال مطربان
entries.english: The boughs dancing like penitents, the leaves clapping their hands like minstrels.

entries.farsi: از هزاران مى‏نگويم من يكى / ‏ز انكه آگنده‏ست هر گوش از شكى
entries.english: The gleam of the mirror is flashing through the felt cloth: think how it will be if the mirror be displayed!

entries.farsi: برق آيينه‏ست المع از نمد / گر نمايد آينه تا چون بود
entries.english: I am not telling one out of thousands, because every ear is filled with a doubt.

sections.title_fa: 3270پيش وهم اين گفت مژده دادن است ‏عقل گويد مژده چه نقد من است

entries.english: To Opinion this saying is a joyful announcement, Reason says, ‘What announcement? It is my cash in hand.’”

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى فرزندان عزير عليه السلام كه از پدر احوال پدر مى‏پرسيدند و عزير مى‏گفت آرى ديدمش مى‏آيد ‏بعضى شناختندش بى‏هوش شدند بعضى نشناختند مى‏گفتند خود مژده داد اين بى‏هوش‏شدن چيست
sections.title_en: Story of the sons of ‘Uzayr, on whom be peace, who were making inquiries about their father

from their father. “Yes,” he replied, “I have seen him: he is coming.” Some recognised him and became unconscious, others did not recognise him and said, “He has only announced: what is this unconsciousness?”

entries.farsi: همچو پوران عزير اندر گذر / آمده پرسان ز احوال پدر
entries.english: Like the sons of Uzayr who came into the thoroughfare, asking news of their father.

entries.farsi: گشته ايشان پير و باباشان جوان / ‏پس پدرشان پيش آمد ناگهان
entries.english: They had grown old, while their father had been made young. Then suddenly their father met them.

entries.farsi: پس بپرسيدند از او كاى رهگذر / از عزير ما عجب دارى خبر
entries.english: So they inquired of him, saying, “O wayfarer, we wonder if you have news of our Uzayr;

entries.farsi: كه كسى‏مان گفت كامروز آن سند / بعد نوميدى ز بيرون مى‏رسد
entries.english: For someone told us that to-day that man of authority would arrive from abroad after we had given up hope.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى بعد من خواهد رسيد / آن يكى خوش شد چو اين مژده شنيد
entries.english: “Yes,” he replied, “he will arrive after me.” That one rejoiced when he heard the good tidings,

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏زد كاى مبشر باش شاد / و آن دگر بشناخت بى‏هوش اوفتاد
entries.english: Crying, “Joy to you, O bringer of the good news!” But the other recognised and fell unconscious,

entries.farsi: كه چه جاى مژده است اى خيره ‏سر / كه در افتاديم در كان شكر
entries.english: Saying, “What occasion is there for good tidings, O scatterbrain, when we have fallen into the mine of sugar?”

entries.farsi: وهم را مژده ست و پيش عقل نقد / ز انكه چشم وهم شد محجوب فقد
entries.english: To Opinion it is good tidings, whereas in the sight of Reason it is ready cash, because the eye of Opinion is veiled by missing.

entries.farsi: كافران را درد و مومن را بشير / ليك نقد حال در چشم بصير
entries.english: It is pain to the infidels and glad news to the faithful, but in the eye of the seer it is immediate experience.

entries.farsi: ز انكه عاشق در دم نقد است مست / ‏الجرم از كفر و ايمان برتر است
entries.english: Inasmuch as the lover is intoxicated at the moment of immediacy, he is necessarily superior to infidelity and faith.

entries.farsi: كفر و ايمان هر دو خود دربان اوست / ‏كاوست مغز و كفر و دين او را دو پوست
entries.english: Indeed, both infidelity and faith are his door-keeper; for he is the kernel, while infidelity and religion are his two rinds.

entries.farsi: قشرهاى خشك را جا آتش است / ‏قشر پيوسته به مغز جان خوش است
entries.english: Infidelity is the dry peel that has averted its face; faith, again, is the peel that has gained a delicious flavour.

entries.farsi: كفر قشر خشك رو بر تافته / ‏باز ايمان قشر لذت يافته
entries.english: The place for the dry peels is the fire; the peel attached to the spiritual kernel is sweet.

entries.farsi: مغز خود از مرتبه‏ى خوش برتر است / ‏برتر است از خوش كه لذت گستر است
entries.english: The kernel itself is above the grade of “sweet”: it is above “sweet” because it is the dispenser of deliciousness.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد باز گرد / تا بر آرد موسى‏ام از بحر گرد
entries.english: This discourse has no end: turn back, that my Moses may cleave the sea asunder.

entries.farsi: در خور عقل عوام اين گفته شد / از سخن باقى آن بنهفته شد
entries.english: This of the discourse has been spoken suitably to the intelligence of the common; the remainder thereof has been concealed.

entries.farsi: زر عقلت ريزه است اى متهم / ‏بر قراضه مهر سكه چون نهم
entries.english: The gold, your intelligence, is in fragments, O suspected one: how should I set the stamp of the die upon clippings?

entries.farsi: عقل تو قسمت شده بر صد مهم / ‏بر هزاران آرزو و طم و رم
entries.english: Your intelligence is distributed over a hundred important affairs, over thousands of desires and great matters and small.

entries.farsi: ‏بر هزاران آرزو و طم و رم / ‏تا شوى خوش چون سمرقند و دمشق
entries.english: You must unite the parts by means of love, to the end that you may become sweet as Samarkand and Damascus.

entries.farsi: جو جوى چون جمع گردى ز اشتباه / ‏پس توان زد بر تو سكه‏ى پادشاه
entries.english: When you become united, grain by grain, from perplexity, then it is possible to stamp upon you the King’s die;

entries.farsi: ور ز مثقالى شوى افزون تو خام / ‏از تو سازد شه يكى زرينه جام
entries.english: And if you, foolish man, become greater than a mithqal (dinar), the King will make of you a cup of gold.

entries.farsi: پس بر او هم نام و هم القاب شاه / ‏باشد و هم صورتش اى وصل خواه
entries.english: Then thereon will be both the name and the titles of the King and also his effigy, O you that crave to attain,

entries.farsi: تا كه معشوقت بود هم نان هم آب / ‏هم چراغ و شاهد و نقل و شراب
entries.english: So that the Beloved will be to you both bread, water, lamp, minion, dessert and wine

entries.farsi: جمع كن خود را جماعت رحمت است / ‏تا توانم با تو گفتن آن چه هست
entries.english: Unite yourself—union is mercy— that I may be able to speak unto you that which is;

entries.farsi: ز انكه گفتن از براى باورى است / ‏جان شرك از باورى حق برى است
entries.english: For speaking is for the purpose of belief: the spirit of polytheism is quit of belief in God.

entries.farsi: جان قسمت گشته بر حشو فلك / ‏در ميان شصت سودا مشترك
entries.english: The spirit that has been distributed over the contents of the sphere is shared amongst sixty passions;

entries.farsi: پس خموشى به دهد او را ثبوت / ‏پس جواب احمقان آمد سكوت
entries.english: Therefore silence is best: it gives peace to that spirit; therefore “Silence is the answer to fools.”

entries.farsi: اين همى‏دانم ولى مستى تن / ‏مى‏گشايد بى‏مراد من دهن
entries.english: This I know, but intoxication of the body is opening my mouth without volition on my part,

entries.farsi: آن چنانك از عطسه و از خامياز / اين دهان گردد به ناخواه تو باز
entries.english: Just as in sneezing and yawning this mouth becomes open without your willing it.

sections.title_fa: تفسير اين حديث كه انى الستغفر اللهَّ فى كل يوم سبعين مرة
sections.title_en: Commentary on the Tradition, “Truly, I ask pardon of God seventy times every day.”

entries.farsi: ليك آن مستى شود توبه شكن / ‏منسى است اين مستى تن جامه كن
entries.english: Like the Prophet, I repent seventy times daily of speaking and giving out;

entries.farsi: همچو پيغمبر ز گفتن و ز نثار / توبه آرم روز من هفتاد بار
entries.english: But that intoxication becomes a breaker of penitence: this intoxication of the body causes oblivion and tears the robe.

entries.farsi: حكمت اظهار تاريخ دراز / مستيى انداخت بر داناى راز
entries.english: The purpose of making manifest the long ago cast intoxication upon the knower of the mystery,

entries.farsi: راز پنهان را چنين طبل و علم / ‏آب جوشان گشته از جف القلم
entries.english: With such drum and banner the hidden mystery has become water gushing from “the Pen is dry.”

entries.farsi: رحمت بى‏حد روانه هر زمان / ‏خفته‏ايد از درك آن اى مردمان
entries.english: The infinite Mercy is flowing continually: you are asleep to the perception thereof, O men!

entries.farsi: جامه‏ى خفته خورد از جوى آب / ‏خفته اندر خواب جوياى سراب
entries.english: The sleeper’s garment drinks water from the river, the sleeper is seeking the mirage in his dream.

entries.farsi: مى‏دود كانجاى بوى آب هست / زين تفكر راه را بر خويش بست
entries.english: He keeps running, saying, “Yonder there is hope of water”: by this thought he has barred the way against himself.

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن جا گفت ز ينجا دور شد / بر خيالى از حقى مهجور شد
entries.english: Because he said “yonder” he became far from her: in a vain imagination he was banished from a reality.

entries.farsi: دور بينانند و بس خفته روان / ‏رحمتى آريدشان اى رهروان
entries.english: They are far-seeing and very fast asleep spiritually: take some mercy upon them, O travellers on the Way!

entries.farsi: من نديدم تشنگى خواب آورد / خواب آرد تشنگى بى‏خرد
entries.english: I never saw thirst induce sleep: the thirst of the unintelligent induces sleep.

entries.farsi: خود خرد آن است كاو از حق چريد / نه خرد كان را عطارد آوريد
entries.english: The intelligence, indeed, is that which was fed by God, not the intelligence that was bestowed by Mercury.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان آن كه عقل جزوى تا به گور بيش نبيند در باقى مقلد اوليا و انبياست
sections.title_en: Explaining that the particular intellect does not see beyond the grave and, as regards all the rest, is subject to the authority of the saints and prophets

entries.farsi: پيش بينى اين خرد تا گور بود / و آن صاحب دل به نفخ صور بود
entries.english: The foresight of this intellect extends to the grave, while that of the spiritual man is till the blast of the trumpet.

entries.farsi: اين خرد از گور و خاكى نگذرد / وين قدم عرصه‏ى عجايب نسپرد
entries.english: This intellect does not pass beyond a grave and sepulchre, and this foot does not tread the arena of marvels.

entries.farsi: زين قدم وين عقل رو بيزار شو / چشم غيبى جوى و برخوردار شو
entries.english: Go, become quit of this foot and this intellect: seek the eye appertaining to the invisible and enjoy.

entries.farsi: همچو موسى نور كى يابد ز جيب / ‏سخره‏ى استاد و شاگرد كتاب
entries.english: How should one subservient to a preceptor and in pupilage to a book find, like Moses, light from bosom?

entries.farsi: زين نظر وين عقل نايد جز دوار / پس نظر بگذار و بگزين انتظار
entries.english: From this study and this intellect comes naught but vertigo; therefore leave this study and adopt expectation.

entries.farsi: از سخن گويى مجوييد ارتفاع / ‏منتظر را به ز گفتن استماع
entries.english: Do not seek eminence from disputation: for him who is expectant listening is better than speaking.

entries.farsi: منصب تعليم نوعى شهوت است / ‏هر خيال شهوتى در ره بت است
entries.english: The office of teaching is a sort of sensual desire: every sensual fancy is an idol in the Way.

entries.farsi: گر به فضلش پى ببردى هر فضول / ‏كى فرستادى خدا چندين رسول
entries.english: If every busybody had found the track to His grace, how should God have sent so many prophets?

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى همچو برق است و درخش / ‏در درخشى كى توان شد سوى وخش
entries.english: The particular intellect is like the lightning and the flash: how is it possible to go to Wakhsh in a flash?

entries.farsi: نيست نور برق بهر ره برى / بلكه امر است ابر را كه مى‏گرى
entries.english: The light of the lightning is not for guidance on the way; nay, it is a command to the cloud to weep.

entries.farsi: برق عقل ما براى گريه است / ‏تا بگريد نيستى در شوق هست
entries.english: The lightning of our intellect is for the sake of weeping; to the end that nonexistence may weep in longing for existence.

entries.farsi: عقل كودك گفت بر كتاب تن / ‏ليك نتواند بخود آموختن
entries.english: The child’s intellect said, “Attend school”; but it cannot learn by itself.

entries.farsi: عقل رنجور آردش سوى طبيب / ‏ليك نبود در دوا عقلش مصيب
entries.english: The sick man’s intellect leads him to the physician; but his intellect is not successful in curing him.

entries.farsi: مى‏ربودند اندكى ز آن رازها / تا شهب مى‏راندشان زود از سما
entries.english: Mark, the devils were going heavenward and listening to the secrets on high

entries.farsi: نك شياطين سوى گردون مى‏شدند / گوش بر اسرار بالا مى‏زدند
entries.english: And carrying away a little of those secrets, till the shooting stars quickly drove them from heaven,

entries.farsi: كه رويد آن جا رسولى آمده‏ست / ‏هر چه مى‏خواهيد از او آيد به دست
entries.english: Saying, “Begone! A prophet is come there from him will be obtained whatever you crave.

entries.farsi: گر همى‏جوييد در بى‏بها / ادخلوا الابيات من ابوابها
entries.english: If you are seeking priceless pearls, enter the houses by their doors.

entries.farsi: مى‏زن آن حلقه‏ى در و بر باب بيست / ‏از سوى بام فلكتان راه نيست
entries.english: Keep knocking that door-ring and stand at the door: there is no way for you in the direction of the vault of heaven.

entries.farsi: نيست حاجت‏تان بدين راه دراز / خاكيى را داده‏ايم اسرار راز
entries.english: You need not take this long road: We have bestowed on an earthly one the secrets of the mystery.

entries.farsi: پيس او آييد اگر خاين نه‏ايد / نيشكر گرديد از او گر چه نييد
entries.english: Come to him, if you are not disloyal; be made sugar-cane by him, though you are reeds.”

entries.farsi: سبزه روياند ز خاكت آن دليل / ‏نيست كم از سم اسب جبرئيل
entries.english: That Guide will cause verdure to grow from your earth: he is not inferior to the hoof of the horse of Gabriel.

entries.farsi: سبزه گردى تازه گردى در نوى / ‏گر تو خاك اسب جبريلى شوى
entries.english: You will be made greenery; you will be made fresh anew, if you become the dust of the horse of a Gabriel—

entries.farsi: سبزه‏ى جان بخش كان را سامرى / ‏كرد در گوساله تا شد گوهرى
entries.english: The life-giving verdure which Sámirí put into the calf, so that it became endowed with the essence

entries.farsi: جان گرفت و بانگ زد ز آن سبزه او / آن چنان بانگى كه شد فتنه‏ى عدو
entries.english: From that verdure it took life and bellowed— such a bellowing as confounded the foe.

entries.farsi: گر امين آييد سوى اهل راز / وارهيد از سر كله مانند باز
entries.english: If you come loyally to the possessors of the mystery, you will be freed from the hood, like a falcon—

entries.farsi: ز آن كله مر چشم بازان را سد است / ‏كه همه‏ى ميلش سوى جنس خود است
entries.english: The hood that binds eye and ear, whereby the falcon is wretched and abject

entries.farsi: سر كاله چشم بند گوش بند / كه از او باز است مسكين و نژند
entries.english: The hood is a blind on the eyes of falcons because its whole desire is for its own kind.

entries.farsi: چون بريد از جنس با شه گشت يار / بر گشايد چشم او را باز دار
entries.english: When it has been severed from its kind, it associates with the king: the falconer unveils its eye.

entries.farsi: راند ديوان را حق از مرصاد خويش / ‏عقل جزوى را ز استبداد خويش
entries.english: God drove the devils from His place of watch, the particular intellect from its autonomy,

entries.farsi: كه سرى كم كن نه اى تو مستبد / بلكه شاگرد دلى و مستعد
entries.english: Saying, “Do not domineer: you are not autonomous; nay, you are the pupil of the heart and predisposed.

entries.farsi: رو بر دل رو كه تو جزو دلى / هين كه بنده‏ى پادشاه عادلى
entries.english: Go to the heart, go, for you are a part of the heart: take heed, for you are a slave of the just King.”

entries.farsi: بندگى او به از سلطانى است / ‏كه أَنَا خَيْرٌ دم شيطانى است
entries.english: To be His slave is better than being a sovereign, for “I am better” is the word of Satan.

entries.farsi: فرق بين و بر گزين تو اى حبيس / ‏بندگى آدم از كبر بليس
entries.english: Do you see the distinction and pick out, O prisoner, the slavery of Adam from the pride of Iblis.

entries.farsi: گفت آنك هست خورشيد ره او / ‏حرف طوبى هر كه ذلت نفسه
entries.english: He who is the Sun of the Way uttered the saying, “Good betide every one whose carnal soul is abased!”

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى طوبى ببين و خوش بخسب / ‏سر بنه در سايه بى‏سركش بخسب
entries.english: Behold the shade of Túbá and sleep well; lay your head in the shade and sleep without lifting your head.

entries.farsi: ظل ذلت نفسه خوش مضجعى است / مستعد آن صفا را مهجعى است
entries.english: The shade of “whose carnal soul is abased” is a pleasant place for reclining: it is a sleeping-place for him that is predisposed to that purity.

entries.farsi: گر از اين سايه روى سوى منى / زود طاغى گردى و ره گم كنى
entries.english: If you go from this shade towards egoism, you will soon become disobedient and lose the way.

sections.title_fa: ‏ِبيان آن كه يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ال تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللهَِّ وَ رَسُ ولِه ‏چون كه سلطان نه اى رعيت باش چون نبى نيستى ز امت باش
sections.title_en: Explaining, “O you that believe, do not put forward in the presence of God and His Apostle.”
Since you are not the Prophet, be one of the religious community; since you are not the sovereign, be a subject.

entries.farsi: ور نه گر چه مستعد و قابلى / ‏مسخ گردى تو ز الف كاملى
entries.english: Go therefore; be silent in submission beneath the shade of the command of the Shaykh and Master;

entries.farsi: پس برو خاموش باش از انقياد / ‏زير ظل امر شيخ و اوستاد
entries.english: Otherwise, though you are predisposed and capable, you will become deformed through boasting of perfection.

entries.farsi: هم ز استعداد وامانى اگر / سركشى ز استاد راز و با خبر
entries.english: You will be deprived even of predisposition, if you rebel against the Master of the mystery who is endowed with knowledge.

entries.farsi: صبر كن در موزه دوزى‏تو هنوز / ور بوى بى‏صبر گردى پاره دوز
entries.english: Do you still have patience in cobbling; for if you be impatient, you will become a rag-tailor.

entries.farsi: كهنه دوزان گر بديشان صبر و حلم / ‏جمله نو دوزان شدندى هم به علم
entries.english: If the tailors of old clothes had patience and forbearance, all of them too would become tailors of new garments through knowledge.

entries.farsi: بس بكوشى و به آخر از كالل / ‏هم تو گويى خويش كالعقل عقال
entries.english: You strive much, and at last even you yourself say in weariness that the intellect is a fetter,

entries.farsi: همچو آن مرد مفلسف روز مرگ / ‏عقل را مى‏ديد بس بى‏بال و برگ
entries.english: Like the philosopher on the day of his death perceived his intellect to be very poor and feeble,

entries.farsi: بى‏غرض مى‏كرد آن دم اعتراف / ‏كز ذكاوت رانديم اسب از گزاف
entries.english: And in that hour disinterestedly confessed, saying, “by acuteness of mind we galloped in vain.

entries.farsi: آشنا هيچ است اندر بحر روح / ‏نيست اينجا چاره جز كشتى نوح
entries.english: In delusion we drew away from the holy men, we swam in the sea of phantasy.”

entries.farsi: از غرورى سر كشيديم از رجال / آشنا كرديم در بحر خيال
entries.english: In the spiritual Sea swimming is naught: here is no resource but the ship of Noah.

entries.farsi: اين چنين فرمود آن شاه رسل / ‏كه منم كشتى در اين درياى كل
entries.english: Thus said that king of the prophets, “I am the ship in this universal Sea,

entries.farsi: يا كسى كاو در بصيرتهاى من / ‏شد خليفه‏ى راستى بر جاى من
entries.english: Or that person who, in respect of my vision, has become a true vicegerent in my stead”

entries.farsi: كشتى نوحيم در دريا كه تا / ‏رو نگردانى ز كشتى اى فتى
entries.english: We are the Noah’s Ark in the Sea, in order that you may not turn your face away from the ship, O youth.

entries.farsi: همچو كنعان سوى هر كوهى مرو / از نبى ال عاصِ مَ الْيَوْمَ شنو
entries.english: Go not, like Canaan, to every mountain: hear from the Qur’an, “There is naught that will protect to-day.”

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد پست اين كشتى ز بند / مى‏نمايد كوه فكرت بس بلند
entries.english: This ship, because of the bandage, seems to you low, the mountain of thought seems very high.

entries.farsi: پست منگر هان و هان اين پست را / بنگر آن فضل حق پيوست را
entries.english: Beware, beware! Do not regard this “low” with contempt: regard the grace of God that is attached.

entries.farsi: در علو كوه فكرت كم نگر / كه يكى موجش كند زير و زبر
entries.english: Do not regard the height of the mountain of thought, for a single wave turns it upside down.

entries.farsi: گر تو كنعانى ندارى باورم / گر دو صد چندين نصيحت پرورم
entries.english: If you are Canaan, you will not believe me though I foster two hundred times as many counsels.

entries.farsi: گوش كنعان كى پذيرد اين كالم / ‏كه بر او مهر خداى است و ختام
entries.english: How should Canaan’s ear accept these words? For God’s seal and signet is upon it.

entries.farsi: كى گذارد موعظه بر مهر حق / ‏كى بگرداند حدث حكم سبق
entries.english: How should admonition pass through God’s seal? How should the new avert the pre-ordainment?

entries.farsi: ليك مى‏گويم حديث خوش پيى / ‏بر اميد آن كه تو كنعان نه‏اى
entries.english: But I am telling the news of good fortune in the hope that you are not Canaan.

entries.farsi: آخر اين اقرار خواهى كرد هين / ‏هم ز اول روز آخر را ببين
entries.english: You will make this confession at last. Listen; from the first day behold the last!

entries.farsi: مى‏توانى ديد آخر را مكن / ‏چشم آخر بينت را كور كهن
entries.english: You can see the end: do not make blind and old yours eye that sees the end.

entries.farsi: هر كه آخر بين بود مسعودوار / نبودش در دم زره رفتن عثار
entries.english: Whosoever is blessedly a seer of the end will never stumble in wayfaring.

entries.farsi: گر نخواهى هر دمى اين خفت و خيز / كن ز خاك پاى مردى چشم تيز
entries.english: Unless you desire this incessant lying down and rising up, sharpen your eye with the dust on the foot of a holy man.

entries.farsi: كحل ديده ساز خاك پاش را / تا بيندازى سر اوباش را
entries.english: Make the dust of his foot eye salve for yours eye, that you may strike off the head of the blackguards;

entries.farsi: كه از اين شاگردى و زين افتقار / سوزنى باشى شوى تو ذو الفقار
entries.english: For through this pupilage and this poverty, you are a needle, you will become Dhu ’l-faqár.

entries.farsi: سرمه كن تو خاك هر بگزيده را / هم بسوزد هم بسازد ديده را
entries.english: Use the dust of every elect one as eye salve: it will both burn the eye and do it good.

entries.farsi: چشم اشتر ز آن بود بس نور بار / كاو خورد از بهر نور چشم خار
entries.english: The eye of the camel is very luminous because he eats thorns for the sake of the light of his eye.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى شكايت استر با شتر كه من بسيار در رو مى‏افتم در راه رفتن تو كم در روى مى‏آيى اين چراست، و جواب گفتن شتر او را
sections.title_en: Story of the mule’s complaining to the camel, “I often fall on my face when going along,
while you seldom do so: why is this?” and the camel’s answer to him.

entries.farsi: اشترى را ديد روزى استرى / ‏چون كه با او جمع شد در آخورى
entries.english: One day a mule saw a camel, since he had been put into a stable with him.

entries.farsi: گفت من بسيار مى‏افتم به رو / در گريوه و راه و در بازار و كو
entries.english: He said, “I often fall on my face in hill and road and in market and street.

entries.farsi: خاصه از بالاى كه تا زير كوه / ‏در سر آيم هر زمانى از شكوه
entries.english: Particularly from the top of the mountain to the bottom I come down on my head every moment from terror.

entries.farsi: كم همى‏افتى تو در رو بهر چيست / ‏يا مگر خود جان پاكت دولتى است
entries.english: You do not fall on your face: why is it? Or maybe in truth your pure spirit is destined to felicity.

entries.farsi: در سر آيم هر دم و زانو زنم / ‏پوز و زانو ز آن خطا پر خون كنم
entries.english: I come down on my head every instant and strike my knees: by that slipping I make muzzle and knees all bloody.

entries.farsi: كژ شود پالان و رختم بر سرم / ‏و ز مكارى هر زمان زخمى خورم
entries.english: My pack-saddle and trappings become awry on my head, and I always get a beating from the muleteer;

entries.farsi: همچو كم عقلى كه از عقل تباه / ‏بشكند توبه به هر دم در گناه
entries.english: Like the unintelligent man who, from corrupt understanding, in sin continually breaks penitence.

entries.farsi: مسخره‏ى ابليس گردد در زمن / ‏از ضعيفى راى آن توبه شكن
entries.english: Through weakness of resolution that breaker of penitence becomes the laughing-stock of Iblis in the world.

entries.farsi: در سر آيد هر زمان چون اسب لنگ / ‏كه بود بارش گران و راه سنگ
entries.english: He constantly comes down on his head, like a lame horse, for his load is heavy and the road is stones.

entries.farsi: مى‏خورد از غيب بر سر زخم او / از شكست توبه آن ادبار خو
entries.english: He is always getting blows on his head from the Unseen, that man of luckless nature, from breaking penitence.

entries.farsi: باز توبه مى‏كند با راى سست / ‏ديو يك تف كرد و توبه‏ش را سكست
entries.english: Then again he repents with infirm resolution: the Devil spits and shatters his penitence.

entries.farsi: ضعف اندر ضعف و كبرش آن چنان / ‏كه به خوارى بنگرد در واصالن
entries.english: Weakness on weakness! His arrogance is such that he regards with contempt those that attain.

entries.farsi: اى شتر كه تو مثال مومنى / ‏كم فتى در رو و كم بينى زنى
entries.english: O camel, you who are a type of the true believer do not fall on your face, nor do you turn up your nose.

entries.farsi: تو چه دارى كه چنين بى‏آفتى / ‏بى‏عثارى و كم اندر رو فتى
entries.english: What have you that you are so untouched by bane and free from stumbling and do not fall on your face?”

entries.farsi: گفت گر چه هر سعادت از خداست / ‏در ميان ما و تو بس فرق‏هاست
entries.english: He said, “Though every felicity is from God, there are many differences between me and you.

entries.farsi: سربلندم من دو چشم من بلند / بينش عالى امان است از گزند
entries.english: I have a high head, my eyes are high: lofty vision is a protection against injury.

entries.farsi: از سر كه من ببينم پاى كوه / ‏هر گو و هموار را من توه توه
entries.english: From the top of the mountain I see the mountain’s base; I see every hollow and level, fold by fold,

entries.farsi: همچنان كه ديد آن صدر اجل / ‏پيش كار خويش تا روز اجل
entries.english: Just as that most noble prince saw his future destiny till the day of death.

entries.farsi: آن چه خواهد بود بعد بيست سال / ‏ديد اندر حال آن نيكو خصال
entries.english: That person of goodly qualities knows at the present time what will happen after twenty years.

entries.farsi: حال خود تنها نديد آن متقى / ‏بلكه حال مغربى و مشرقى
entries.english: That God-fearing man did not see his own destiny only; nay, the destiny of inhabitant of the West and East.

entries.farsi: نور در چشم و دلش سازد سكن / ‏بهر چه سازد پى حب الوطن
entries.english: The Light makes its abode in his eye and heart. Wherefore does it make? For love of home.

entries.farsi: همچو يوسف كاو بديد اول به خواب / ‏كه سجودش كرد ماه و آفتاب
entries.english: Like Joseph, who at first dreamed that the sun and moon bowed in worship before him:

entries.farsi: از پس ده سال بلكه بيشتر / آن چه يوسف ديده بد بر كرد سر
entries.english: After ten years, nay, more, that which Joseph had seen came to pass.

entries.farsi: نيست آن ينظر بنور اللهَّ گزاف / ‏نور ربانى بود گردون شكاف
entries.english: That, ‘he sees by the Light of God,’ is not vain: the Divine Light rives the sky asunder.

entries.farsi: نيست اندر چشم تو آن نور رو / هستى اندر حس حيوانى گرو
entries.english: In yours eye that Light is not. Go! You are in pawn to the animal senses.

entries.farsi: تو ز ضعف چشم بينى پيش پا / تو ضعيف و هم ضعيفت پيشوا
entries.english: From weakness of eye you see in front of your foot: you are weak and your guide, too, is weak.

entries.farsi: پيشوا چشم است دست و پاى را / كاو ببيند جاى را ناجاى را
entries.english: The eye is the guide for hand and foot, for it sees the right and the wrong place.

entries.farsi: ديگر آن كه چشم من روشن‏تر است / ‏ديگر آن كه خلقت من اطهر است
entries.english: Another thing is that my eye is clearer; another, that my nature is purer,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هستم من ز اوالد حالل / ‏نه ز اوالد زنا و اهل ضالل
entries.english: Because I am one of the lawfully begotten, not one of the children of adultery and the people of perdition.

entries.farsi: تو ز اوالد زنايى بى‏گمان / ‏تير كژ پرد چو بد باشد كمان
entries.english: You are one of the children of adultery: without doubt the arrow flies crookedly when the bow is bad.”

sections.title_fa: تصديق كردن استر جوابهاى شتر را و اقرار آوردن به فضل او بر خود و از او استعانت خواستن و ‏بدو پناه گرفتن به صدق و نواختن شتر او را و ره نمودن و يارى دادن پدرانه و شاهانه
sections.title_en: How the mule declared the replies of the camel to be true and acknowledged his (the camel’s) su­
periority to himself and besought his aid and took refuge with him sincerely; and how the camel treated him with kindness and showed him the way and gave help in fatherly and kingly fashion.

entries.farsi: گفت استر راست گفتى اى شتر / اين بگفت و چشم كرد از اشك پر
entries.english: The mule said, “You have spoken the truth, O camel.” This he said and filled his eye with tears.

entries.farsi: ساعتى بگريست و در پايش فتاد / گفت اى بگزيده‏ى رب العباد
entries.english: He wept awhile and fell at his feet and said, “O chosen of the Lord of men,

entries.farsi: چه زيان دارد گر از فرخندگى / ‏در پذيرى تو مرا در بندگى
entries.english: What harm will it do if you, by your blessedness, will receive me into your service?”

entries.farsi: دادى انصاف و رهيدى از بال / تو عدو بودى شدى ز اهل وال
entries.english: He said, “Since you have made confession in my presence, go, for you are saved from the contaminations of Time.

entries.farsi: گفت چون اقرار كردى پيش من / ‏رو كه رستى تو ز آفات زمن
entries.english: You have given justice and are saved from tribulation: you were an enemy; you have become one of the loyal.

entries.farsi: خوى بد در ذات تو اصلى نبود / كز بد اصلى نيايد جز جحود
entries.english: The evil disposition was not original in your person; for from original evil comes nothing but denial.

entries.farsi: آن بد عاريتى باشد كه او / آرد اقرار و شود او توبه جو
entries.english: The borrowed evil is such that he makes confession and desires to repent;

entries.farsi: همچو آدم زلتش عاريه بود / ال جرم اندر زمان توبه نمود
entries.english: Like Adam, whose lapse was temporary: of necessity he showed penitence at once.

entries.farsi: چون كه اصلى بود جرم آن بليس / ‏ره نبودش جانب توبه‏ى نفيس
entries.english: Since the sin of Iblis was original, for him there was no way to precious penitence.

entries.farsi: رو كه رستى از خود و از خوى بد / و از زبانه‏ى نار و از دندان دد
entries.english: Go, for you are delivered from yourself and from the evil disposition and from the tongue of the Fire and from the teeth of the wild beasts.

entries.farsi: رو كه اكنون دست در دولت زدى / ‏در فگندى خود به بخت سرمدى
entries.english: Go, for now you have grasped felicity, you have thrown yourself into everlasting fortune.

entries.farsi: ادخلى تو فى عبادى يافتى / ‏ادخلى فى جنتى دريافتى
entries.english: You have gained Enter in amongst My servants; you have annexed Enter into My Paradise.

entries.farsi: در عبادش راه كردى خويش را / رفتى اندر خلد از راه خفا
entries.english: You have made a way for yourself amongst His servants; you have gone into Eden by the secret way.

entries.farsi: اهدنا گفتى صراط مستقيم / ‏دست تو بگرفت و بردت تا نعيم
entries.english: ‘Guide us,’ you said, ‘in the straight path’: He took your hand and led you to the abode of bliss.

entries.farsi: نار بودى نور گشتى اى عزيز / غوره بودى گشتى انگور و مويز
entries.english: You were fire: you have become light, O noble one; you were an unripe grape: you have become a (ripe) grape and raisin.

entries.farsi: اخترى بودى شدى تو آفتاب / ‏شاد باش اللهَّ اعلم بالصواب
entries.english: You were a star: you have become the Sun. Rejoice! God best knows the right.”

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين بگير / شهد خويش اندر فگن در حوض شير
entries.english: O Ziyá’u ’l-Haqq Husamu’ddin, take your honey and cast it into the basin of milk,

entries.farsi: تا رهد آن شير از تغيير طعم / ‏يابد از بحر مزه تكثير طعم
entries.english: To the end that that milk may escape from having its savour corrupted and may gain much increase of savour from the Sea of Deliciousness,

entries.farsi: ُمتصل گردد بدان بحر أَ لَسْ ت / ‏چون كه شد دريا ز هر تغيير رست
entries.english: May be united with the Sea of Alast: when it becomes the Sea, it is delivered from every corruption;

entries.farsi: منفذى يابد در آن بحر عسل / ‏آفتى را نبود اندر وى عمل
entries.english: It finds a passage into that Sea of honey, no contamination will have an effect upon it.

entries.farsi: غره‏اى كن شيروار اى شير حق / ‏تا رود آن غره بر هفتم طبق
entries.english: Roar like a lion, O Lion of God, in order that that roar may mount to the seventh tier!

entries.farsi: چه خبر جان ملول سير را / كى شناسد موش غره‏ى شير را
entries.english: What knowledge has the weary surfeited soul? How should the mouse know the roar of the lion?

entries.farsi: بر نويس احوال خود با آب زر / بهر هر دريا دلى نيكو گهر
entries.english: Write your experiences with gold-water for the sake of every one of goodly substance whose heart is as the sea.

entries.farsi: آب نيل است اين حديث جان فزا / يا ربش در چشم قبطى خون نما
entries.english: This spirit-augmenting discourse is the water of the Nile: O Lord, let it seem blood to the eye of the Egyptian!

sections.title_fa: البه كردن قبطى سبطى را كه يك سبو به نيت خويش از نيل پر كن و بر لب من نه تا بخورم به حق دوستى و برادرى، كه سبو كه شما سبطيان بهر خود پر مى‏كنيد از نيل آب صاف ‏ است و سبو كه ما قبطيان پر مى‏كنيم خون صاف است

sections.title_en: How the Egyptian entreated the Israelite, saying, “Of yours own intention fill a jug from the Nile and put it to my lips, that I may drink. by the right of friendship and brotherhood; for the jug which you Israelites fill from the Nile for yourselves is pure water, while the jug which we Egyptians fill is pure blood.”

entries.farsi: من شنيدم كه در آمد قبطيى / ‏از عطش اندر وثاق سبطيى
entries.english: I heard that an Egyptian, on account of thirst, came into the house of an Israelite.

entries.farsi: گفت هستم يار و خويشاوند تو / گشته‏ام امروز حاجتمند تو
entries.english: He said, “I am your friend and kinsman: to-day I have become in need of you,

entries.farsi: ز انكه موسى جادويى كرد و فسون / تا كه آب نيل ما را كرد خون
entries.english: Because Moses wrought sorcery and enchantments, so that he made the water of the Nile to be blood for us

entries.farsi: سبطيان زو آب صافى مى‏خورند / پيش قبطى خون شد آب از چشم بند
entries.english: The Israelites drink pure water from it, to the Egyptians the water has become blood from the spell laid on our eyes.

entries.farsi: قبط اينك مى‏مرند از تشنگى / ‏از پى ادبار خود يا بد رگى
entries.english: Look, the Egyptians are dying of thirst in consequence of their ill-fortune or their evil nature.

entries.farsi: بهر خود يك طاس را پر آب كن / ‏تا خورد از آبت اين يار كهن
entries.english: Fill one cup with water for yourself that this old friend may drink of your water.

entries.farsi: چون براى خود كنى آن طاس پر / خون نباشد آب باشد پاك و حر
entries.english: When you fill that cup for yours own sake, it will not be blood, it will be water pure and free (from taint).

entries.farsi: من طفيل تو بنوشم آب هم / ‏كه طفيلى در تبع بجهد ز غم
entries.english: I too will drink the water as your parasite; for a parasite, in following, is relieved from anguish.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى جان و جهان خدمت كنم / ‏پاس دارم اى دو چشم روشنم
entries.english: He said, “O soul and world, I will do service; I will pay regard, O my two bright eyes!

entries.farsi: بر مراد تو روم شادى كنم / ‏بنده‏ى تو باشم آزادى كنم
entries.english: I will do according to your desire, I will rejoice; I will be your slave, I will act as a freeman.”

entries.farsi: طاس را از نيل او پر آب كرد / بر دهان بنهاد و نيمى را بخورد
entries.english: He filled the cup with water from the Nile, put it to his lips, and drank one half.

entries.farsi: طاس را كژ كرد سوى آب خواه / ‏كه بخور تو هم، شد آن خون سياه
entries.english: He tilted the cup towards him who craved the water, saying, “Drink you too!” That became black blood.

entries.farsi: باز از اين سو كرد كژ خون آب شد / قبطى اندر خشم و اندر تاب شد
entries.english: Again he tilted it on this side: the blood became water. The Egyptian was enraged and incensed.

entries.farsi: ساعتى بنشست تا خشمش برفت / ‏بعد از آن گفتش كه اى صمصام زفت
entries.english: He sat down awhile till his anger departed; after that, he said to him, “O mighty sword,

entries.farsi: اى برادر اين گره را چاره چيست / ‏گفت اين را او خورد كاو متقى است
entries.english: O brother, what is the expedient for this knot?” He said, “He that is God-fearing drinks this.”

entries.farsi: متقى آن است كاو بيزار شد / از ره فرعون و موسى‏وار شد
entries.english: The God-fearing man is he that has become quit of the way of Pharaoh and has become like unto Moses.

entries.farsi: قوم موسى شو بخور اين آب را / صلح كن با مه ببين مهتاب را
entries.english: Become the people of Moses and drink this water; make peace with the Moon and behold the moonbeams.

entries.farsi: خشم بنشان چشم بگشا شاد شو / عبرت از ياران بگير استاد شو
entries.english: There are a hundred thousand shades of darkness in yours eye from your wrath against the servants of God.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران ظلمت است از خشم تو / بر عباد اللهَّ اندر چشم تو
entries.english: Extinguish wrath, open the eye, rejoice, take a lesson from friends, become a teacher (of the Truth).

entries.farsi: كى طفيل من شوى در اغتراف / ‏چون ترا كفرى است همچون كوه قاف
entries.english: How will you become my parasite in scooping up when you have a doubt as Mount Qaf?

entries.farsi: كوه در سوراخ سوزن كى رود / جز مگر كان رشته‏ى يكتا شود
entries.english: How should a mountain go into the eye of a needle, unless indeed it becomes a single thread?

entries.farsi: كوه را كه كن به استغفار و خوش / ‏جام مغفوران بگير و خوش بكش
entries.english: By asking forgiveness make the mountain a straw, and take joyously the cup of the forgiven and drain joyously!

entries.farsi: تو بدين تزوير چون نوشى از آن / ‏چون حرامش كرد حق بر كافران
entries.english: Inasmuch as God has made it unlawful to the unbelievers, how will you drink of it with this imposture?

entries.farsi: خالق تزوير تزوير ترا / كى خرد اى مفترى مفترا
entries.english: How should the Creator of imposture buy your imposture, O fabricator of fiction?

entries.farsi: آل موسى شو كه حيلت سود نيست / ‏حيله‏ات باد تهى پيمودنى است
entries.english: Become the kinsfolk of Moses, for deceit is useless: your deceit is measuring the empty wind.

entries.farsi: زهره دارد آب كز امر صمد / گردد او با كافران آبى كند
entries.english: Will the water dare to turn aside from the command of the Lord and bestow refreshment on the unbelievers?

entries.farsi: يا تو پندارى كه تو نان مى‏خورى / ‏زهر مار و كاهش جان مى‏خورى
entries.english: Or do you suppose that you are eating bread? You are eating snake-venom and wasting away of the spirit

entries.farsi: نان كجا اصالح آن جانى كند / كاو دل از فرمان جانان بر كند
entries.english: How should bread restore to health the spirit that averts its heart from the command of the Beloved Spirit?

entries.farsi: يا تو پندارى كه حرف مثنوى / ‏چون بخوانى رايگانش بشنوى
entries.english: Or do you suppose that when you read the words of the Masnavi you hear them freely?

entries.farsi: يا كالم حكمت و سر نهان / ‏اندر آيد زغبه در گوش و دهان
entries.english: Or that the discourse of wisdom and the hidden mystery comes easily into your ear and mouth?

entries.farsi: اندر آيد ليك چون افسانه‏ها / پوست بنمايد نه مغز دانه‏ها
entries.english: It comes in, but, like fables, it shows the husk, not the kernel of the berries,

entries.farsi: در سر و رو در كشيده چادرى / ‏رو نهان كرده ز چشمت دلبرى
entries.english: a sweetheart who has drawn a veil over her head and face and has hid­ den her face from yours eye.

entries.farsi: شاهنامه يا كليله پيش تو / همچنان باشد كه قرآن از عتو
entries.english: By reason of contumacy the Sháhnáma or Kalíla seems to you just like the Qur’an.

entries.farsi: فرق آن گه باشد از حق و مجاز / كه كند كحل عنايت چشم باز
entries.english: The difference between truth and falsehood is at the moment when the eye salve of favour opens the eye;

entries.farsi: ور نه پشك و مشك پيش اخشمى / ‏هر دو يكسان است چون نبود شمى
entries.english: Otherwise, shit and musk smell the same to one whose nose is blocked, since there is no sense of smell.

entries.farsi: خويشتن مشغول كردن از مالل / ‏باشدش قصد از كالم ذو الجلال
entries.english: His aim is to divert himself from ennui, and neglect the Word of the Almighty,

entries.farsi: كاتش وسواس را و غصه را / ز آن سخن بنشاند و سازد دوا
entries.english: That by means of that discourse he may quench the fire of distress and anxiety and provide a cure.

entries.farsi: بهر اين مقدار آتش شاندن / ‏آب پاك و بول يكسان شد به فن
entries.english: For the purpose of quenching this amount of fire, pure water and urine do the same job.

entries.farsi: آتش وسواس را اين بول و آب / ‏هر دو بنشانند همچون وقت خواب
entries.english: Both this urine and water will quench the fire of distress, just as during sleep.

entries.farsi: ليك گر واقف شوى زين آب پاك / ‏كه كالم ايزد است و روحناك
entries.english: But if you become acquainted with this pure water, which is the Word of God and spiritual,

entries.farsi: نيست گردد وسوسه‏ى كلى ز جان / ‏دل بيابد ره به سوى گلستان
entries.english: All distress will vanish from the soul, and the heart will find its way to the Rose-garden,

entries.farsi: ز انكه در باغى و در جويى پرد / هر كه از سر صحف بويى برد
entries.english: Because everyone who catches a scent of the mystery of the scriptures flies into an orchard with a running brook

entries.farsi: يا تو پندارى كه روى اوليا / آن چنان كه هست مى‏بينيم ما
entries.english: Or do you suppose that we see the face of the Saints as it is?

entries.farsi: در تعجب مانده پيغمبر از آن / ‏چون نمى‏بينند رويم مومنان
entries.english: Hence the Prophet remained in astonishment, saying, “How are the true believers not seeing my face?

entries.farsi: چون نمى‏بينند نور روم خلق / ‏كه سبق برده ست بر خورشيد شرق
entries.english: How are the people not seeing the light of my face, which has borne away the prize from the orient sun?

entries.farsi: ور همى‏بينند اين حيرت چراست / ‏تا كه وحى آمد كه آن رو در خفاست
entries.english: And if they are seeing, wherefore is this perplexity?”— until a revelation came, saying, “That face is in concealment.

entries.farsi: سوى تو ماه است و سوى خلق ابر / تا نبيند رايگان روى تو گبر
entries.english: In relation to you it is the moon, and in relation to the people it is the cloud, in order that the infidel may not see your face for nothing.

entries.farsi: سوى تو دانه است و سوى خلق دام / ‏تا ننوشد زين شراب خاص عام
entries.english: In relation to you it is the bait, and in relation to the people it is the trap, in order that the common may not drink of this chosen wine.”

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان كه تَراهُمْ ينظرون / ‏نقش حمامند هُمْ ال يبصرون
entries.english: God said, “You see them looking,” they are the pictures in a bathhouse: they do not see.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد صورت اى صورت پرست / ‏كان دو چشم مرده‏ى او ناظر است
entries.english: The form appears, O worshipper of form, as though its two dead eyes were looking.

entries.farsi: پيش چشم نقش مى‏آرى ادب / ‏كاو چرا پاسم نمى‏دارد عجب
entries.english: You are showing reverence before the eye of the image, saying, “I wonder why it pays no regard to me.

entries.farsi: از چه بس بى‏پاسخ است اين نقش نيك / ‏كه نمى‏گويد سالمم را عليك
entries.english: Why is this goodly image so unresponsive that it does not say ‘alayk in reply to my salaam?

entries.farsi: مى‏نجنباند سر و سبلت ز جود / پاس آن كه كردمش من صد سجود
entries.english: It does not nod its head and moustache generously in regard for my having made a hundred prostrations before it.”

entries.farsi: حق اگر چه سر نجنباند برون / ‏پاس آن ذوقى دهد در اندرون
entries.english: God, though He does not nod the head outwardly, in regard for that bestows an inward delight,

entries.farsi: كه دو صد جنبيدن سر ارزد آن / ‏سر چنين جنباند آخر عقل و جان
entries.english: Which is worth two hundred nods of the head: in this fashion, after all, do Intellect and Spirit nod the head.

entries.farsi: عقل را خدمت كنى در اجتهاد / پاس عقل آن است كافزايد رشاد
entries.english: You serve Intellect in earnest, the regard of Intellect is, that it increases righteousness.

entries.farsi: حق نجنباند به ظاهر سر ترا / ليك سازد بر سران سرور ترا
entries.english: God does not nod the head to you outwardly, but He makes you a prince over the princes.

entries.farsi: مر ترا چيزى دهد يزدان نهان / ‏كه سجود تو كنند اهل جهان
entries.english: To you God gives secretly something that the people of the world bow down before you,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه داد سنگى را هنر / تا عزيز خلق شد يعنى كه زر
entries.english: Just as He gave to a stone such virtue that it was honoured by His creatures: that is to say, gold.

entries.farsi: قطره‏ى آبى بيابد لطف حق / ‏گوهرى گردد برد از زر سبق
entries.english: If a drop of water gains the favour of God, it becomes a pearl and bears away the palm from gold.

entries.farsi: جسم خاك است و چو حق تابيش داد / در جهان گيرى چو مه شد اوستاد
entries.english: The body is earth; and when God gave it a spark it became adept, like the moon, in taking possession of the world.

entries.farsi: هين طلسم است اين و نقش مرده است / ‏احمقان را چشمش از ره برده است
entries.english: Beware! This world is a talisman and a dead image: its eye has led the foolish astray from the path.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد او كه چشمى مى‏زند / ابلهان سازيده‏اند او را سند
entries.english: It appears to wink: the foolish have made it their support.

sections.title_fa: درخواستن قبطى دعاى خير و هدايت از سبطى و دعاكردن سبطى قبطى را به خير و مستجاب شدن ‏از اكرم الاكرمين و ارحم الراحمين
sections.title_en: How the Egyptian sought blessing and guidance from the Israelite, and how the Israelite prayed for the Egyptian and received a favourable answer to his prayer
from the Most Gracious and Merciful.

entries.farsi: كه بود كه قفل اين دل وا شود / زشت را در بزم خوبان جا شود
entries.english: The Egyptian said, “Offer a prayer, since from blackness of heart I have not the mouth,

entries.farsi: گفت قبطى تو دعايى كن كه من / ‏از سياهى دل ندارم آن دهن
entries.english: For it may be that the lock of this heart will be opened and that a place will be to this ugly one at the banquet of the beauteous.

entries.farsi: مسخى از تو صاحب خوبى شود / يا بليسى باز كروبى شود
entries.english: Through you the deformed may become endowed with beauty, or an Iblís may again become one of the Cherubim;

entries.farsi: يا به فر دست مريم بوى مشك / يابد و ترى و ميوه شاخ خشك
entries.english: Or, by the august influence of Mary’s hand, the withered bough may acquire the fragrance of musk and freshness and fruit.”

entries.farsi: سبطى آن دم در سجود افتاد و گفت / ‏كاى خداى عالم جهر و نهفت
entries.english: Thereupon the Israelite fell to worship and said, “O God who know the manifest and the hidden,

entries.farsi: جز تو پيش كى بر آرد بنده دست / ‏هم دعا و هم اجابت از تو است
entries.english: To whom but You should Your servant lift his hand? Both the prayer and the answer are from You.

entries.farsi: هم ز اول تو دهى ميل دعا / تو دهى آخر دعاها را جزا
entries.english: You at first give the desire for prayer, and You at last give likewise the recompense for prayers.

entries.farsi: اول و آخر تويى ما در ميان / ‏هيچ هيچى كه نيايد در بيان
entries.english: You are the First and the Last: we between are nothing, a nothing that does not come into expression.”

entries.farsi: اين چنين مى‏گفت تا افتاد طشت / ‏از سر بام و دلش بى‏هوش گشت
entries.english: He was speaking in this wise, till he fell into ecstasy and his heart became senseless.

entries.farsi: باز آمد او به هوش اندر دعا / ‏لَيْسَ لِلإِْنْسانِ إِلاَّ ما سعى
entries.english: In prayer, he came back to his senses: Man shall have nothing but what he has wrought.

entries.farsi: در دعا بود او كه ناگه نعره‏اى / از دل قبطى بجست و غره‏اى
entries.english: He was praying when suddenly a loud cry and roar burst from the heart of the Egyptian,

entries.farsi: آتشى در جان من انداختند / مر بليسى را به جان بنواختند
entries.english: “Come, make haste and submit the Faith, that I may quickly cut the old girdle.

entries.farsi: كه هال بشتاب و ايمان عرضه كن / ‏تا ببرم زود زنار كهن
entries.english: They have cast a fire into my heart; they have shown affection with their soul for an Iblís.

entries.farsi: دوستى تو و از تو ناشگفت / ‏حمد هلل عاقبت دستم گرفت
entries.english: Praise be to God! Your friendship and not being able to do without you have succoured me at last.

entries.farsi: كيميايى بود صحبتهاى تو / كم مباد از خانه‏ى دل پاى تو
entries.english: My being with you was an elixir: may your foot never disappear from the house of my heart!

entries.farsi: تو يكى شاخى بدى از نخل خلد / چون گرفتم او مرا تا خلد برد
entries.english: You were a bough of the palm-tree of Paradise: when I grasped it, it bore me to Paradise.

entries.farsi: سيل بود آن كه تنم را در ربود / برد سليم تا لب درياى جود
entries.english: That which carried away my body was a torrent: the torrent bore me to the brink of the Sea of Bounty.

entries.farsi: من به بوى آب رفتم سوى سيل / ‏بحر ديدم در گرفتم كيل كيل
entries.english: I went towards the torrent in hope of water: I beheld the Sea and took pearls, bushel on bushel.”

entries.farsi: طاس آوردش كه اكنون آب گير / گفت رو شد آبها پيشم حقير
entries.english: He brought the cup to him, saying, “Now take the water!” “Go,” he replied; “waters have become despicable in my sight.

entries.farsi: شربتى خوردم ز اللهَّ اشترى / تا به محشر تشنگى نايد مرا
entries.english: I have drunk such a draught from God has purchased that no thirst will come to me till the Last Judgement.

entries.farsi: آن كه جو و چشمه‏ها را آب داد / چشمه‏اى در اندرون من گشاد
entries.english: He who gave water to the rivers and fountains has opened a fountain within me.

entries.farsi: اين جگر كه بود گرم و آب خوار / گشت پيش همت او آب خوار
entries.english: This heart, which was hot and water-drinking— to its high aspiration water has become vile.

entries.farsi: كاف كافى آمد او بهر عباد / ‏صدق وعده‏ى كهيعص
entries.english: He, for the sake of His servants, became the káf of Káfí (All-sufficing), the truth of the promise of Káf, Há, Yá, ‘Ayn, Sád.

entries.farsi: كافى‏ام بدهم ترا من جمله خير / بى‏سبب بى‏واسطه‏ى يارى غير
entries.english: ‘I am All-sufficing: I will give you all good, without a secondary cause, without the mediation of another’s aid.

entries.farsi: كافى‏ام بى‏نان ترا سيرى دهم / ‏بى‏سپاه و لشكرت ميرى دهم
entries.english: I am All-sufficing: I will satisfy you without bread, I will give you sovereignty without soldiers and armies.

entries.farsi: بى‏بهارت نرگس و نسرين دهم / ‏بى‏كتاب و اوستا تلقين دهم
entries.english: I will give you narcissi and wild-roses without the spring; I will give you instruction without a book and teacher.

entries.farsi: كافى‏ام بى‏داروات درمان كنم / ‏گور را و چاه را ميدان كنم
entries.english: I am All-sufficing: I will heal you without medicine; I will make the grave and the pit a playing-field.

entries.farsi: موسيى را دل دهم با يك عصا / تا زند بر عالمى شمشيرها
entries.english: To a Moses I give heart with a single rod, that he may brandish swords against a multitude.

entries.farsi: دست موسى را دهم يك نور و تاب / ‏كه طپانچه مى‏زند بر آفتاب
entries.english: A light and splendour do I give to the hand of Moses that it is slapping the sun.

entries.farsi: چوب را مارى كنم من هفت سر / كه نزايد ماده مار او را ز نر
entries.english: I make the wooden staff a seven-headed dragon, which the female dragon does not bring to birth from the male.

entries.farsi: خون نياميزم در آب نيل من / ‏خود كنم خون عين آبش را به فن
entries.english: I do not mingle blood in the water of the Nile: in truth by My cunning I make the very essence of its water to be blood.

entries.farsi: شادى‏ات را غم كنم چون آب نيل / ‏كه نيابى سوى شاديها سبيل
entries.english: I turn your joy into sorrow like the water of the Nile, so that you will not find the way to rejoicings.

entries.farsi: باز چون تجديد ايمان بر تنى / ‏باز از فرعون بيزارى كنى
entries.english: Again, when you are intent on renewing your faith and giving up Pharaoh once more,

entries.farsi: موسى رحمت ببينى آمده / ‏نيل خون بينى از او آبى شده
entries.english: You will see the Moses of Mercy come, you will see the Nile of blood turned by him into water.

entries.farsi: من گمان بردم كه ايمان آورم / ‏تا از اين طوفان خون آبى خورم
entries.english: When you keep safe within the end of the rope, the Nile of your spiritual delight will never be changed into blood.’

entries.farsi: چون سر رشته نگه دارى درون / ‏نيل ذوق تو نگردد هيچ خون
entries.english: I thought I would profess the Faith in order that from this deluge of blood I might drink some water.

entries.farsi: من چه دانستم كه تبديلى كند / در نهاد من مرا نيلى كند
entries.english: How did I know that He would work a transformation in my nature and make me a Nile?

entries.farsi: سوى چشم خود بكى نيلم روان / ‏برقرارم پيش چشم ديگران
entries.english: To my own eye, I am a flowing Nile, to the eyes of others I am at rest.”

entries.farsi: همچنان كه اين جهان پيش نبى / ‏غرق تسبيح است و پيش ما غبى
entries.english: Just as, to the Prophet, this world is plunged in glorification of God, while to us it is heedless.

entries.farsi: پيش چشمش اين جهان پر عشق و داد / پيش چشم ديگران مرده و جماد
entries.english: To his eye, this world is filled with love and bounty; to the eyes of others it is dead and inert.

entries.farsi: پست و بالا پيش چشمش تيز رو / از كلوخ و خشت او نكته نشو
entries.english: To his eye, vale and hill are moving swiftly: he hears subtle discourse from clod and brick.

entries.farsi: با عوام اين جمله بسته و مرده‏اى / ‏زين عجب‏تر من نديدم پرده‏اى
entries.english: To the common, this entire world is bound and dead: I have not seen a veil more wonderful than this.

entries.farsi: عامه گفتندى كه پيغمبر ترش / ‏از چه گشته ست و شده ست او ذوق كش
entries.english: To our eye, the graves are alike; to the eyes of the saints, a garden, and a pit.

entries.farsi: گورها يكسان به پيش چشم ما / روضه و حفره به چشم اوليا
entries.english: The common would say, “Why has the Prophet become sour and why has he become pleasure-killing?”

entries.farsi: خاص گفتندى كه سوى چشمتان / ‏مى‏نمايد او ترش اى امتان
entries.english: The elect would say, “To your eyes, O peoples, he appears to be sour;

entries.farsi: از سر امرودبن بنمايد آن / ‏منعكس صورت، به زير آ اى جوان
entries.english: come for once into our eyes, that you may behold the laughs in the Súra beginning with the words Hal atá (Did not there come?).”

entries.farsi: يك زمان در چشم ما آييد تا / ‏خنده‏ها بينيد اندر هَلْ أتى
entries.english: That appears in the form of inversion from the top of the pear-tree: come down, O youth!

entries.farsi: آن درخت هستى است امرودبن / ‏تا بر آن جايى نمايد نو كهن
entries.english: The pear-tree is the tree of existence: while you are there, the new appears old.

entries.farsi: تا بر آن جايى ببينى خارزار / پر ز كژدمهاى خشم و پر ز مار
entries.english: While you are there, you will see a thorn-brake full of the scorpions of wrath and full of snakes.

entries.farsi: چون فرود آيى ببينى رايگان / ‏يك جهان پر گل رخان و دايگان
entries.english: When you come down, you will behold, free of cost, a world filled with rose-cheeked nurses.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن زن پليد كار كه شوهر را گفت كه آن خيالات از سر امرودبن مى‏نمايد ترا كه چنينها نمايد چشم آدمى را سر آن امرودبن، از سر امرودبن فرود آى تا آن خيالها برود، و اگر كسى گويد كه آن چه آن مرد مى‏ديد خيال نبود جواب اين مثال است نه مثل، در مثال همين قدر بس بود كه اگر بر سر ‏امرودبن نرفتى هرگز آنها نديدى خواه خيال خواه حقيقت

sections.title_en: Story of the lewd woman who said to her husband, “Those illusions appear to you from the top of the pear-tree, for the top of that pear-tree cause the human eye to see such things: come down from the top of the pear-tree that those illusions may vanish.” And if anyone should say that what that man saw was not an illusion, the answer is that this is a parable, not a simile.
In the parable this amount is sufficient, for if he had not gone to the top of the pear tree, he would never have seen those things, whether illusory or real.

entries.farsi: آن زنى مى‏خواست تا با مول خود / بر زند در پيش شوى گول خود
entries.english: That woman desired to embrace her paramour in the presence of her foolish husband.

entries.farsi: پس به شوهر گفت زن كاى نيك بخت / چون بر آمد بر درخت آن زن گريست
entries.english: Therefore the woman said to her husband, “O fortunate one, I will climb the tree to gather fruit.”

entries.farsi: ‏چون ز بالا سوى شوهر بنگريست / گفت شوهر را كه اى مأبون رد
entries.english: As soon as she had climbed the tree, the woman burst into tears when from the top she looked in the direction of her husband.

entries.farsi: كيست آن لوطى كه بر تو مى‏فتد / تو به زير او چو زن بغنوده‏اى
entries.english: O despicable catamite, who is the rascal that has fallen upon you?

entries.farsi: ‏اى فالن تو خود مخنث بوده‏اى / گفت شوهر نه سرت گويى بگشت
entries.english: You swoon under him like a woman. Are you then a mere eunuch?

entries.farsi: ‏ور نه اينجا نيست غير من به دشت / زن مكرر كرد كان با برطله
entries.english: “Nay,” said the husband: “one would think your head is turned; at any rate, there is nobody here on the plain except me.”

entries.farsi: ‏كيست بر پشتت فرو خفته هله / گفت اى زن هين فرود آ از درخت
entries.english: The wife repeated “who is that mounted upon you?”

entries.farsi: ‏زن كشيد آن مول را اندر برش / گفت شوهر كيست آن اى روسپى
entries.english: “Listen, wife,” he replied, “come down from the tree, for your head is turned and you have become very stupid.”

entries.farsi: ‏كه سرت گشت و خرف گشتى تو سخت / چون فرود آمد بر آمد شوهرش
entries.english: When she came down, her husband went up: the woman drew her paramour into her arms.

entries.farsi: ‏كه به بالاى تو آمد چون كپى / گفت زن نه نيست اينجا غير من
entries.english: The husband said, “O harlot who is riding you?”

entries.farsi: ‏هين سرت بر گشته شد هرزه متن / او مكرر كرد بر زن آن سخن
entries.english: “Nay,” said the wife, “there is no one here but me. Listen, your head is turned: don’t talk nonsense.”

entries.farsi: ‏گفت زن اين هست از امرودبن / از سر امرودبن من همچنان
entries.english: He repeated the charge against his wife. “This,” said the wife “is from the pear-tree.

entries.farsi: ‏كژ همى‏ديدم كه تو اى قلتبان / هين فرود آ تا ببينى هيچ نيست
entries.english: From the top of the pear-tree I was seeing just as falsely as you, O cuckold.

entries.farsi: ‏اين همه تخييل از امروبنى است / هزل تعليم است آن را جد شنو
entries.english: Listen, come down, that you may see there is nothing: all this illusion is caused by a pear-tree.”

entries.farsi: تو مشو بر ظاهر هزلش گرو / هر جدى هزل است پيش هازالن
entries.english: Jesting is teaching: listen to it in earnest, do not be in pawn to its appearance of jest.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بدان امرودبن راهى است نيك / نقل كن ز امرودبن كاكنون بر او
entries.english: To jesters every earnest matter is a jest; to the wise jests are earnest.

entries.farsi: ‏هزلها جد است پيش عاقالن / كاهالن امرودبن جويند ليك
entries.english: Lazy folk seek the pear-tree, but it is a good way to that pear-tree.

entries.farsi: كه بر او ديده كژ و احول بود / چون فرود آيى از اين امرودبن
entries.english: Descend from the pear-tree on which at present you have become giddy-eyed and giddy-faced.

entries.farsi: گشته‏اى تو خيره چشم و خيره رو / اين منى و هستى اول بود
entries.english: This is the primal egoism and self-existence wherein the eye is awry and squinting.

entries.farsi: ‏كژ نماند فكرت و چشم و سخن / يك درخت بخت بينى گشته اين
entries.english: When you come down from this pear-tree, your thoughts and eyes and words will no more be awry.

entries.farsi: ‏شاخ او بر آسمان هفتمين / چون فرود آيى از او گردى جدا
entries.english: You will see that this has become a tree of fortune, its boughs reaching to the Seventh Heaven.

entries.farsi: راست بينى بخشد آن چشم ترا / راست بينى گر بدى آسان و زب
entries.english: When you come down and part from it, God in His mercy will cause it to be transformed.

entries.farsi: مبدلش گرداند از رحمت خدا / زين تواضع كه فرود آيى خدا
entries.english: On account of this humility shown by you in coming down, God will bestow on yours eye true vision.

entries.farsi: ‏مصطفى كى خواستى آن را ز رب / گفت بنما جزو جزو از فوق و پست
entries.english: If true vision were easy and facile, how should Mustafa have desired it from the Lord?

entries.farsi: ‏آن چنان كه پيش تو آن جزو هست / بعد از آن بر رو بر آن امرودبن
entries.english: He said, “Show me each part from above and below such as that part is in Your sight.”

entries.farsi: ‏كه مبدل گشت و سبز از امر كن / چون درخت موسوى شد اين درخت
entries.english: Afterwards go up the pear-tree which has been transformed and made verdant by the command, “Be.”

entries.farsi: ‏چون سوى موسى كشانيدى تو رخت / آتش او را سبز و خرم مى‏كند
entries.english: This tree has become like the tree connected with Moses, inasmuch as you have transported your baggage towards Moses.

entries.farsi: شاخ او إِنِّي أَنَا اللهَُّ مى‏زند / زير ظلش جمله حاجاتت روا
entries.english: The fire makes it verdant and flourishing; its boughs cry “Lo, I am God.”

entries.farsi: اين چنين باشد الهى كيميا / آن منى و هستى‏ات باشد حالل
entries.english: Beneath its shade all your needs are fulfilled: such is the Divine alchemy.

entries.farsi: اصله ثابت و فرعه فى السما
entries.english: That personality and existence is lawful to you, since you behold therein the attributes of the Almighty.

entries.farsi: ‏كه در او بينى صفات ذو الجلال / شد درخت كژ مقوم حق نما
entries.english: The crooked tree has become straight, God-revealing: its root fixed and its branches in the sky.

sections.title_en: The remainder of the story of Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: كامدش پيغام از وحى مهم / ‏كه كژى بگذار اكنون فاستقم
entries.english: For there came to him from the peremptory Revelation a message, saying, and “Put crookedness aside now, and be upright.”

entries.farsi: اين درخت تن عصاى موسى است / ‏كامرش آمد كه بيندازش ز دست
entries.english: This tree of the body is Moses’ rod, concerning which the command came to him—“Let it fall from your hand,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى خير او و شر او / بعد از آن بر گير او را ز امر هو
entries.english: That you may behold its good and evil; after that, take it up by His command.”

entries.farsi: پيش از افكندن نبود او غير چوب / ‏چون به امرش بر گرفتى گشت خوب
entries.english: Before his dropping it, it was nothing but wood; whenever he took it up by His command, it became good.

entries.farsi: اول او بد برگ افشان بره را / گشت معجز آن گروه غره را
entries.english: At first it was shaking down leaves for the lambs; it reduced to impotence that deluded people.

entries.farsi: گشت حاكم بر سر فرعونيان / ‏آبشان خون كرد و كف بر سر زنان
entries.english: It became ruler over the party of Pharaoh: it turned their water into blood and caused them to beat their heads with their hands.

entries.farsi: از مزارع‏شان بر آمد قحط و مرگ / ‏از ملخهايى كه مى‏خوردند برگ
entries.english: From their sown fields arose famine and death on account of the locusts which devoured the leaves,

entries.farsi: تا بر آمد بى‏خود از موسى دعا / چون نظر افتادش اندر منتها
entries.english: Till from Moses, when he considered the ultimate issue, there went up involuntarily a prayer —

entries.farsi: كاين همه اعجاز و كوشيدن چراست / ‏چون نخواهند اين جماعت گشت راست
entries.english: “For what reason is all this disablement and striving, since this multitude will never become righteous?”

entries.farsi: امر آمد كه اتباع نوح كن / ‏ترك پايان بينى مشروح كن
entries.english: The command came, saying, “Follow Noah! Refrain from considering the end as it has been disclosed .

entries.farsi: ز آن تغافل كن چو داعى رهى / ‏امر بَلِّغْ هست نبود آن تهى
entries.english: Take no heed of that, since you are one who calls to the Way. The command, ‘Deliver the message,’ is there : it is not void.”

entries.farsi: كمترين حكمت كاز اين الحاح تو / جلوه گردد آن لجاج و آن عتو
entries.english: The least purpose is that through this persistence of yours that obstinacy and rebellious pride may be displayed,

entries.farsi: تا كه ره بنمودن و اضالل حق / ‏فاش گردد بر همه‏ى اهل فرق
entries.english: So that God’s showing the way and letting be lost may become evident to all the followers of religious sects.

entries.farsi: چون كه مقصود از وجود اظهار بود / بايدش از پند و اغوا آزمود
entries.english: Inasmuch as the object of existence was the manifestation, it must be tested by means of exhorting and leading astray.

entries.farsi: ديو الحاح غوايت مى‏كند / شيخ الحاح هدايت مى‏كند
entries.english: The Devil persists in error; the Shaykh persists in guiding aright.

entries.farsi: چون پياپى گشت آن امر شجون / ‏نيل مى‏آمد سراسر جمله خون
entries.english: When that grievous command proceeded step by step, the whole Nile was turned into blood from end to end,

entries.farsi: تا به نفس خويش فرعون آمدش / ‏البه مى‏كردش دو تا گشته قدش
entries.english: Till Pharaoh came in person to him, humbly entreating him, his tall figure bent double,

entries.farsi: كانچه ما كرديم اى سلطان مكن / ‏نيست ما را روى ايراد سخن
entries.english: And said, “ O sovereign, do not that which we did: we have not the face to offer words.

entries.farsi: پاره پاره گردمت فرمان پذير / من به عزت خو گرم سختم مگير
entries.english: I will become obedient to your command with every bit; I am accustomed to be held in honour: do not deal hardly with me.

entries.farsi: هين بجنبان لب به رحمت اى امين / ‏تا ببندد اين دهانه‏ى آتشين
entries.english: Listen; move your lips in mercy, O trusted one, that it may shut this fiery mouth.”
