entries.farsi: گفت يا رب مى‏فريبد او مرا / مى‏فريبد او فريبيده‏ى ترا
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, he is deceiving me; he is deceiving Your deceiver.

entries.farsi: بشنوم يا من دهم هم خدعه‏اش / ‏تا بداند اصل را آن فرع‏كش
entries.english: Shall I hearken or shall I too give him deceit, in order that that puller of the branch may recognise the root:

entries.farsi: كاصل هر مكرى و حيله پيش ماست / ‏هر چه بر خاك است اصلش از سماست
entries.english: For the root of every cunning and contrivance is with use: whatever is on the earth, its root is from Heaven.”

entries.farsi: گفت حق آن سگ نيرزد هم بدان / ‏پيش سگ انداز از دور استخوان
entries.english: God said, “That cur is not worth even that: fling a bone to the cur from a distance.

entries.farsi: هين بجنبان آن عصا تا خاكها / وا دهد هر چه ملخ كردش فنا
entries.english: Listen; shake the rod, so that the sods may give back whatever the locusts have destroyed,

entries.farsi: كه سببها نيست حاجت مر مرا / آن سبب بهر حجاب است و غطا
entries.english: And let the locusts immediately become black, that the people may behold the transformation wrought by God ;

entries.farsi: و آن ملخها در زمان گردد سياه / ‏تا ببيند خلق تبديل اله
entries.english: For I have no need of means: those means are for the purpose of a screen and covering,

entries.farsi: بندگى ناكرده و ناشسته روى / ‏لقمه‏ى دوزخ بگشته لقمه جوى
entries.english: To the end that the natural philosopher may attach himself to the drug; and that the astronomer may turn his face to the star ;

entries.farsi: تا طبيعى خويش بر دارو زند / تا منجم رو به استاره كند
entries.english: And that the hypocrite, from greed, may come to market at daybreak for fear of no demand for his wares,

entries.farsi: تا منافق از حريصى بامداد / سوى بازار آيد از بيم كساد
entries.english: Without having performed his devotions or washed his face: seeking morsels he has become a morsel for Hell.”

entries.farsi: آكل و مأكول آمد جان عام / ‏همچو آن بره‏ى چرنده از حطام
entries.english: The spirit of the vulgar is devouring and being devoured, like the lamb that feeds on hay.

entries.farsi: مى‏چرد آن بره و قصاب شاد / كاو براى ما چرد برگ مراد
entries.english: The lamb is feeding, while the butcher rejoices, saying, “For our sake it feeds on the grass of desire.”

entries.farsi: كار دوزخ مى‏كنى در خوردنى / ‏بهر او خود را تو فربه مى‏كنى
entries.english: In respect of food and drink you are doing the work of Hell: you are fattening yourself for its sake.

entries.farsi: خوردن تن مانع اين خوردن است / ‏جان چو بازرگان و تن چون ره زن است
entries.english: Do your own work, feed on the daily bread of Wisdom, that the glorious heart may become fat.

entries.farsi: كار خود كن روزى حكمت بچر / تا شود فربه دل با كر و فر
entries.english: Bodily eating and drinking is the obstacle to this eating and drinking: the spirit is like a merchant, while the body is like a highwayman.

entries.farsi: شمع تاجر آن گه است افروخته / ‏كه بود ره زن چو هيزم سوخته
entries.english: At the time when the highwayman is consumed like firewood is the candle of the spirit resplendent;

entries.farsi: كه تو آن هوشى و باقى هوش پوش / ‏خويشتن را گم مكن ياوه مكوش
entries.english: For you are that intelligence, and the rest is a mask concealing the intelligence. Do not lose yourself, do not strive in vain!

entries.farsi: دان كه هر شهوت چو خمر است و چو بنگ / ‏پرده‏ى هوش است و عاقل زوست دنگ
entries.english: Know that every sensual desire is like wine and beng: it is a veil over the intelligence, and thereby the rational man is stupe­ fied. The intoxication of the intelligence is not wine alone: whatsoever is sensual shuts the eye and ear.

entries.farsi: خمر تنها نيست سر مستى هوش / ‏هر چه شهوانى است بندد چشم و گوش
entries.english: Iblis was far removed from wine-drinking: he was drunk with pride and unbelief.

entries.farsi: آن بليس از خمر خوردن دور بود / مست بود او از تكبر و ز جحود
entries.english: The drunken man is he who sees that which is not: what is a piece of copper or iron appears to be gold.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد موسيا / لب بجنبان تا برون روژد گيا
entries.english: This discourse hath no end. “O Moses, move your lips that the herbage may spring forth.”

entries.farsi: همچنان كرد و هم اندر دم زمين / ‏سبز گشت از سنبل و حب ثمين
entries.english: He did so, and immediately the earth became green with hyacinths and costly grains.

entries.farsi: اندر افتادند در لوت آن نفر / قحط ديده مرده از جوع البقر
entries.english: That folk fell to the food, since they had suffered famine and were dead from ravenous hunger.

entries.farsi: چند روزى سير خوردند از عطا / آن دمى و آدمى و چار پا
entries.english: For several days they ate their fill of the gift, those who were inspired by that breath and the human beings and the quadrupeds.

entries.farsi: چون شكم پر گشت و بر نعمت زدند / و آن ضرورت رفت پس طاغى شدند
entries.english: When their bellies were filled and they grasped the bounty and the necessity was gone, then they waxed insolent.

entries.farsi: بى‏تف آتش نگردد نفس خوب / ‏تا نشد آهن چو اخگر هين مكوب
entries.english: The carnal soul is a follower of Pharaoh: beware, do not satisfy it, lest it remember its ancient infidelity.

entries.farsi: نفس فرعونى است هان سيرش مكن / ‏تا نيارد ياد از آن كفر كهن
entries.english: Without the glowing heat of the fire the carnal soul will never become good: listen, do not beat the iron till it has become like live coals.

entries.farsi: بى‏مجاعت نيست تن جنبش‏كنان / ‏آهن سردى است مى‏كوبى بدان
entries.english: Without hunger the body makes no movement: it is cold iron you are beating. Know!

entries.farsi: گر بگريد ور بنالد زار زار / او نخواهد شد مسلمان هوش دار
entries.english: Though it weep and wail most piteously, it will never become a true believer. Take heed!

entries.farsi: او چو فرعون است در قحط آن چنان / ‏پيش موسى سر نهد البه‏كنان
entries.english: It is like Pharaoh: in famine it lays its head before Moses, as he did, making supplication;

entries.farsi: چون كه مستغنى شد او طاغى شود / خر چو بار انداخت اسكيزه زند
entries.english: When it has been freed from want, it rebels: when the donkey has cast off his load, he kicks.

entries.farsi: پس فراموشش شود چون رفت پيش / ‏كار او ز آن آه و زاريهاى خويش
entries.english: So, when its business has gone forward, it forgets its sighs and lamentations.

entries.farsi: سالها مردى كه در شهرى بود / يك زمان كه چشم در خوابى رود
entries.english: The man who lives in a city years, as soon as his eye goes asleep,

entries.farsi: شهر ديگر بيند او پر نيك و بد / هيچ در يادش نيايد شهر خود
entries.english: Beholds another city full of good and evil, and his own city comes not into his memory at all,

entries.farsi: كه من آن جا بوده‏ام اين شهر نو / نيست آن من درينجايم گرو
entries.english: So that, “ I have lived there; this new city is not mine: here I am in pawn.”

entries.farsi: بل چنان داند كه خود پيوسته او / هم در اين شهرش بده ست ابداع و خو
entries.english: Nay, he thinks that in truth he has always lived in this very city and has been born and bred in it.

entries.farsi: مى‏نيارد ياد كاين دنيا چو خواب / ‏مى‏فرو پوشد چو اختر را سحاب
entries.english: What wonder if the spirit does not remember its abodes, which have been its dwelling-place and birthplace aforetime,

entries.farsi: چه عجب گر روح موطنهاى خويش / ‏كه بده‏ستش مسكن و ميالد پيش
entries.english: Since this world, like sleep, is covering it over as clouds cover the stars?—

entries.farsi: خاصه چندين شهرها را كوفته / ‏گردها از درك او ناروفته
entries.english: Especially as it has trodden so many cities, and the dust has not been swept from its perceptive faculty,

entries.farsi: اجتهاد گرم ناكرده كه تا / دل شود صاف و ببيند ماجرا
entries.english: Nor has it made ardent efforts that its heart should become pure and behold the past;

entries.farsi: سر برون آرد دلش از بخش راز / اول و آخر ببيند چشم باز
entries.english: That its heart should put forth its head from the aperture of the mystery and should see the beginning and the end with open eye.

sections.title_fa: اطوار و منازل آدمى از ابتدا
sections.title_en: The diverse modes and stages of the nature of Man from the beginning.

entries.farsi: آمده اول به اقليم جماد / و ز جمادى در نباتى اوفتاد
entries.english: First he came into the clime of inorganic things, and from the state of inorganic things he passed into the vegetable state.

entries.farsi: سالها اندر نباتى عمر كرد / وز جمادى ياد ناورد از نبرد
entries.english: Years he lived in the vegetable state and did not remember the inorganic state because of the opposition;

entries.farsi: و ز نباتى چون به حيوانى فتاد / نامدش حال نباتى هيچ ياد
entries.english: And when he passed from the vegetable into the animal state, the vegetable state was not remembered by him at all,

entries.farsi: جز همين ميلى كه دارد سوى آن / ‏خاصه در وقت بهار و ضيمران
entries.english: Save only for the inclination which he has towards that, especially in the season of spring and sweet herbs—

entries.farsi: همچو ميل كودكان با مادران / ‏سر ميل خود نداند در لبان
entries.english: Like the inclination of babes towards their mothers: it does not know the secret of its desire for being suckled;

entries.farsi: جزو عقل اين از آن عقل كل است / ‏جنبش اين سايه ز آن شاخ گل است
entries.english: Like the excessive inclination of every novice towards the noble spiritual Elder, whose fortune is young.

entries.farsi: همچو ميل مفرط هر نو مريد / سوى آن پير جوان بخت مجيد
entries.english: The particular intelligence of this is derived from that Universal Intelligence: the motion of this shadow is derived from that Rose-bough.

entries.farsi: سايه‏اش فانى شود آخر در او / پس بداند سر ميل و جستجو
entries.english: His shadow disappears at last in him; then he knows the secret of his inclination and search and seeking.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى شاخ دگر اى نيك بخت / ‏كى بجنبد گر نجنبد اين درخت
entries.english: How should the shadow of the other’s bough move, O fortunate one, if this Tree moves not?

entries.farsi: باز از حيوان سوى انسانى‏اش / ‏مى‏كشيد آن خالقى كه دانى‏اش
entries.english: Again, the Creator, whom you know, was leading him from the animal towards humanity.

entries.farsi: همچنين اقليم تا اقليم رفت / تا شد اكنون عاقل و دانا و زفت
entries.english: Thus did he advance from place to place, till he has now become intelligent and wise and mighty?

entries.farsi: عقلهاى اولينش ياد نيست / ‏هم از اين عقلش تحول كردنى است
entries.english: He has no remembrance of his former intelligences; from this intelligence also there is a migration to be made by him,

entries.farsi: تا رهد زين عقل پر حرص و طلب / ‏صد هزاران عقل بيند بو العجب
entries.english: That he may escape from this intelligence full of greed and self-seeking and may behold a hundred thousand intelligences most marvellous

entries.farsi: گر چه خفته گشت و شد ناسى ز پيش / ‏كى گذارندش در آن نسيان خويش
entries.english: Though he fell asleep and became oblivious of the past, how should they leave him in that self-forgetfulness?

entries.farsi: باز از آن خوابش به بيدارى كشند / كه كند بر حالت خود ريش‏خند
entries.english: From that sleep they will bring him back again to wakefulness that he may mock at his state

entries.farsi: كه چه غم بود آن كه مى‏خوردم به خواب / چون فراموشم شد احوال صواب
entries.english: Saying, “What was that sorrow I was suffering in my sleep? How did I forget the states of truth?

entries.farsi: چون ندانستم كه آن غم و اعتالل / ‏فعل خواب است و فريب است و خيال
entries.english: How did not I know that that sorrow and disease is the effect of sleep and is illusion and phantasy?”

entries.farsi: همچنان دنيا كه حلم نايم است / ‏خفته پندارد كه اين خود دايم است
entries.english: Even so this world, which is the sleeper’s dream: the sleeper fancies that it is really enduring,

entries.farsi: تا بر آيد ناگهان صبح اجل / ‏وارهد از ظلمت ظن و دغل
entries.english: Till on a sudden there shall rise the dawn of Death and he shall be delivered from the darkness of opinion and falsehood.

entries.farsi: خنده‏اش گيرد از آن غمهاى خويش / ‏چون ببيند مستقر و جاى خويش
entries.english: Laughter at those sorrows of his will take possession of him when he sees his permanent abode and dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: هر چه تو در خواب بينى نيك و بد / روز محشر يك به يك پيدا شود
entries.english: Everything good or evil that you see in your sleep will be made manifest, one by one, on the Day of the Congregation.

entries.farsi: آن چه كردى اندر اين خواب جهان / ‏گرددت هنگام بيدارى عيان
entries.english: That which you did in this sleep in the world will become evident to you at the time of awaking.

entries.farsi: تا نپندارى كه اين بد كردنى است / اندر اين خواب و ترا تعبير نيست
entries.english: Take care not to imagine that this is an evil action committed in this sleep and that there is no interpretation for you.

entries.farsi: بلكه اين خنده بود گريه و زفير / روز تعبير اى ستمگر بر اسير
entries.english: Nay, this laughter will be tears and moans on the Day of interpretation, O oppressor of the captive!

entries.farsi: گريه و درد و غم و زارى خود / شادمانى دان به بيدارى خود
entries.english: Know that in the hour of your awakening your tears and grief and sorrow and lamentation will turn to joy.

entries.farsi: اى دريده پوستين يوسفان / ‏گرگ برخيزى از اين خواب گران
entries.english: O you that have torn the coat of Josephs, you will arise from this heavy slumber a wolf.

entries.farsi: گشته گرگان يك به يك خوهاى تو / مى‏درانند از غضب اعضاى تو
entries.english: Your dispositions, one by one, having become wolves will tear your limbs in wrath.

entries.farsi: خون نخسبد بعد مرگت در قصاص / ‏تو مگو كه مردم و يابم خالص
entries.english: According to retaliation, the blood will not sleep after your death: do not say, “I shall die and obtain release.”

entries.farsi: اين قصاص نقد حيلت سازى است / ‏پيش زخم آن قصاص اين بازى است
entries.english: This immediate retaliation is temporary: in comparison with the blow of that retaliation this is a play.

entries.farsi: اين جزا تسكين جنگ و فتنه است / ‏آن چو اخصاء است و اين چون ختنه است
entries.english: God has called the present world a play because this penalty is a play in comparison with that penalty.

entries.farsi: زين لعب خوانده‏ست دنيا را خدا / كاين جزا لعب است پيش آن جزا
entries.english: This penalty is a means of allaying war and civil strife: that one is like a castration, while this one resembles a circumcision.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه خلق دوزخ گرسنگانند و نالانند به حق كه روزيهاى ما را فربه گردان و زود زاد به ما رسان كه ما را صبر نماند
sections.title_en: Explaining that the people of Hell are hungry and make lamentable entreaty to God, saying, “Cause our portions to be fat and let the provender reach us quickly, for we can endure no more.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد موسيا / هين رها كن آن خران را در گيا
entries.english: This discourse has no end. “Listen, O Moses, let those asses go to the grass,

entries.farsi: تا همه ز آن خوش علف فربه شوند / هين كه گرگانند ما را خشم‏مند
entries.english: That they may all be fattened by that goodly fodder. Listen, for We have wrathful wolves.

entries.farsi: ناله‏ى گرگان خود را موقنيم / ‏اين خران را طعمه‏ى ايشان كنيم
entries.english: We surely know the plaintive cry of Our wolves: We make these asses a means of livelihood for them.

entries.farsi: اين خران را كيمياى خوش دمى / ‏از لب تو خواست كردن آدمى
entries.english: The gracious alchemy breathed from your lips wished to make these asses human.

entries.farsi: تو بسى كردى به دعوت لطف و جود / آن خران را طالع و روزى نبود
entries.english: Much kindness and favour did you show in calling them, it was not the fortune and provision allotted to those asses.

entries.farsi: پس فرو پوشان لحاف نعمتى / ‏تا بردشان زود خواب غفلتى
entries.english: Therefore let bounty’s quilt cover them, that the slumber of forgetfulness may overtake them speedily,

entries.farsi: تا چو بجهند از چنين خواب اين رده / ‏شمع مرده باشد و ساقى شده
entries.english: So that, when this troop shall start up from suchlike slumber, the candle will have been extinguished and the cup-bearer will have gone.

entries.farsi: داشت طغيانشان ترا در حيرتى / ‏پس بنوشند از جزاهم حسرتى
entries.english: Their rebellious disobedience kept you in a perplexity: therefore they shall suffer in retribution a sorrow,

entries.farsi: تا كه عدل ما قدم بيرون نهد / در جزا هر زشت را در خور دهد
entries.english: To the end that Our justice may step forth and bestow in retribution what is appropriate to every evil-doer;

entries.farsi: كان شهى كه مى‏نديدنديش فاش / ‏بود با ايشان نهان اندر معاش
entries.english: For the King, whom they were not seeing openly, was with them secretly in their lives.”

entries.farsi: چون خرد با تست مشرف بر تنت / ‏گر چه زو قاصر بود اين ديدنت
entries.english: Inasmuch as the intellect is with you, overseeing your body, and though this perception of yours is unable to apprehend it,

entries.farsi: نيست قاصر ديدن او اى فالن / از سكون و جنبشت در امتحان
entries.english: its perception, O such and such, is not unable to apprehend your motion and rest when it tries,

entries.farsi: چه عجب گر خالق آن عقل نيز / با تو باشد چون نه‏اى تو مستجيز
entries.english: What wonder if the Creator of that intellect too is with you? How are you not conceding?

entries.farsi: از خرد غافل شود بر بد تند / بعد آن عقلش مالمت مى‏كند
entries.english: He pays no heed to his intellect and embarks on evil; afterwards his intellect rebukes him.

entries.farsi: تو شدى غافل ز عقلت عقل نى / ‏كز حضور استش مالمت كردنى
entries.english: You forgot your intellect, your intellect did not, since that act of rebuke is the result of its presence.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى حاضر و غافل بدى / ‏در مالمت كى ترا سيلى زدى
entries.english: If it had not been present and had been heedless, how should it have slapped you in rebuke?

entries.farsi: ور از او غافل نبودى نفس تو / كى چنان كردى جنون و تفس تو
entries.english: And if your carnal soul had not been inattentive to it, how should your madness and heat have acted thus?

entries.farsi: قرب بى‏چون است عقلت را به تو / نيست چپ و راست و پس يا پيش رو
entries.english: Hence you and your intellect are like the astrolabe: by this means you may know the nearness of the Sun of existence.

entries.farsi: پس تو و عقلت چو اصطرالب بود / زين بدانى قرب خورشيد وجود
entries.english: Your intellect is indescribably near to you: it is neither to the left nor to the right nor behind nor in front.

entries.farsi: قرب بى‏چون چون نباشد شاه را / كه نيابد بحث عقل آن راه را
entries.english: How should not the King be indescribably near? For intellectual search cannot find the way.

entries.farsi: نيست آن جنبش كه در اصبع تراست / ‏پيش اصبع يا پسش يا چپ و راست
entries.english: The motion that you have in your finger is not in front of your finger or behind it or to the left or to the right.

entries.farsi: از چه ره مى‏آيد اندر اصبعت / ‏كه اصبعت بى‏او ندارد منفعت
entries.english: At the time of sleep and death it goes from it; at the time of waking it rejoins it.

entries.farsi: وقت خواب و مرگ از وى مى‏رود / وقت بيدارى قرينش مى‏شود
entries.english: By what way doth it come into your finger, without which your finger has no use?

entries.farsi: نور چشم و مردمك در ديده‏ات / ‏از چه ره آمد بغير شش جهت
entries.english: The light of the eye and pupil, by what other way than the six directions doth it come into your eye?

entries.farsi: عالم خلق است با سوى و جهات / ‏بى‏جهت دان عالم امر و صفات
entries.english: The world of creation is endued with quarters and directions, know that the world of the Command and Attributes is without direction.

entries.farsi: بى‏جهت بد عقل و عالم البيان / ‏عقل‏تر از عقل و جان‏تر هم ز جان
entries.english: Know, O beloved that the world of the Command is without direction: of necessity the Commander is more without direction.

entries.farsi: بى‏جهت دان عالم امر اى صنم / ‏بى‏جهت‏تر باشد آمر الجرم
entries.english: The intellect was without direction, and the Knower of the exposition is more intelligent than intellect and more spiritual even than spirit.

entries.farsi: بى‏تعلق نيست مخلوقى بدو / آن تعلق هست بى‏چون اى عمو
entries.english: No created being is unconnected with Him: that connection, O uncle, is indescribable,

entries.farsi: ز انكه فصل و وصل نبود در روان / ‏غير فصل و وصل ننديشد گمان
entries.english: Because in the spirit there is no separating and uniting, while thought cannot think except of separating and uniting.

entries.farsi: غير فصل و وصل پى بر از دليل / ‏ليك پى بردن بنشاند غليل
entries.english: Pursue that which is without separation and union by a spiritual guide; but the pursuit will not allay your thirst.

entries.farsi: پى پياپى مى‏بر از دورى ز اصل / ‏تا رگ مرديت آرد سوى وصل
entries.english: Pursue incessantly, if you are far from the Source, that the vein of manhood may bring you to the attainment.

entries.farsi: اين تعلق را خرد چون ره برد / بسته‏ى فصل است و وصل است اين خرد
entries.english: How should the intellect find the way to this connection? This intellect is in bondage to separation and union.

entries.farsi: زين وصيت كرد ما را مصطفى / بحث كم جوييد در ذات خدا
entries.english: Hence Mustafa enjoined us, saying, “Do not seek to investigate the Essence of God.”

entries.farsi: آن كه در ذاتش تفكر كردنى است / در حقيقت آن نظر در ذات نيست
entries.english: That One whose Essence is an object of thought, in reality the speculation is not concerning the Essence.

entries.farsi: هر يكى در پرده‏ى موصول خوست / ‏وهم او آن است كان خود عين هوست
entries.english: It is his opinion, because on the way to God there are a hundred thousand veils.

entries.farsi: هست آن پندار او زيرا به راه / ‏صد هزاران پرده آمد تا اله
entries.english: Everyone is naturally attached to some veil and judges that it is in truth the identity (‘ayn) of Him.

entries.farsi: و انكه اندر وهم او ترك ادب / ‏بى‏ادب را سر نگونى داد رب
entries.english: Therefore the Prophet banished this judgement from him, lest he should be conceiving in error a vain imagination.

entries.farsi: پس پيمبر دفع كرد اين وهم از او / تا نباشد در غلط سودا پز او
entries.english: And him in whose judgement there is irreverence, the Lord has doomed the irreverent to fall headlong.

entries.farsi: سر نگونى آن بود كاو سوى زير / مى‏رود پندارد او كاو هست چير
entries.english: To fall headlong is that he goes downward and thinks that he is superior,

entries.farsi: در عجبهايش به فكر اندر رويد / از عظيمى و ز مهابت گم شويد
entries.english: Because such is the case of the drunken man who does not know heaven from earth

entries.farsi: ز انكه حد مست باشد اين چنين / ‏كاو نداند آسمان را از زمين
entries.english: Go and think upon His wonders, become lost in majesty and awe.

entries.farsi: جز كه ال احصى نگويد او ز جان / ‏كز شمار و حد برون است آن بيان
entries.english: When he loses beard and moustache from His work, he will know his station and will be silent concerning the Worker.

entries.farsi: چون ز صنعش ريش و سبلت گم كند / حد خود داند ز صانع تن زند
entries.english: He will only say from his soul, “I cannot,” because the declaration thereof is beyond reckoning and bound.

sections.title_fa: رفنت ذو القرنني به كوه قاف و درخواست كردن كه اى كوه قاف از عظمت صفت حق ما را بگو و گفنت كوه قاف كه صفت عظمت او در گفت نيايد كه پيش آن ادراكها فنا شود و البه كردن ذو القرنني كه ‏از صنايعش كه در خاطر دارى و بر تو گفنت آن آسانتر بود بگوى

sections.title_en: How Dhu ’l-Qarnayn went to Mount Qáf and made petition, saying, “O Mount Qáf, tell me of the majesty of the Attributes of God”; and how Mount Qáf said that the description of His majesty is ineffable, since perceptions vanish before it; and how Dhu ’l-Qarnayn made humble supplication, saying, “Tell of His works that you have in mind and of which it is more easy for you to speak.”

entries.farsi: رفت ذو القرنين سوى كوه قاف / ‏ديد او را كز زمرد بود صاف
entries.english: Dhu ’l-Qarnayn went towards Mount Qáf: he saw that it was of pure emerald,

entries.farsi: گرد عالم حلقه گشته او محيط / ماند حيران اندر آن خلق بسيط
entries.english: And that it had become a ring surrounding the world. He was amazed at that immense creation.

entries.farsi: گفت تو كوهى دگرها چيستند / كه به پيش عظم تو بازيستند
entries.english: He said, “You are the mountain: what are the others? For beside your magnitude they are playthings.”

entries.farsi: گفت رگهاى من‏اند آن كوهها / من به هر شهرى رگى دارم نهان
entries.english: It replied, “Those mountains are my veins: they are not like unto me in beauty and glory.

entries.farsi: ‏بر عروقم بسته اطراف جهان / حق چو خواهد زلزله‏ى شهرى مرا
entries.english: I have a hidden vein in every land: the regions of the world are fastened to my veins.

entries.farsi: گويد او من بر جهانم عرق را / پس بجنبانم من آن رگ را به قهر
entries.english: When God wills an earthquake in any land, He bids me and I cause the vein to throb.

entries.farsi: كه بدان رگ متصل گشته ست شهر / چون بگويد بس، شود ساكن رگم
entries.english: Then I make to move mightily the vein with which the land is connected.

entries.farsi: ‏ساكنم و ز روى فعل اندر تگم / همچو مرهم ساكن و بس كاركن
entries.english: When He says ‘Enough!’ my vein rests. I am at rest, but actually I am in rapid motion”—

entries.farsi: ‏چون خرد ساكن و ز او جنبان سخن / نزد آن كس كه نداند عقلش اين
entries.english: At rest, like the ointment, and very active; at rest, like the intellect, while the speech by it is moving

entries.farsi: ‏زلزله هست از بخارات زمين
entries.english: In the opinion of him whose intelligence does not perceive this, earthquakes are caused by terrestrial vapours.

sections.title_fa: مورى بر كاغذى مى‏رفت نبشتن قلم ديد قلم را ستودن گرفت، مورى ديگر كه چشم تيزتر بود گفت ستايش انگشتان را كن كه اين هنر از ايشان مى‏بينم، مورى ديگر كه از هر دو چشم روشن‏تر بود ‏گفت من بازو را ستايم كه انگشتان فرع بازواند الى آخره

sections.title_en: An ant, walking on a piece of paper, saw the pen writing and began to praise the pen. Another ant, which was more keen-sighted, said, “Praise the fingers, for I deem this accomplishment to proceed from them.” Another ant, more clear-sighted than either, said, “I praise the arm, for the fingers are a branch of the arm,” et cetera.

entries.farsi: موركى بر كاغذى ديد او قلم / ‏گفت با مورى دگر اين راز هم
entries.english: A little ant saw a pen on a paper, and told this mystery to another ant,

entries.farsi: كه عجايب نقشها آن كلك كرد / همچو ريحان و چو سوسن زار و ورد
entries.english: Saying, “That pen made wonderful pictures like sweet basil and beds of lilies and roses.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن مور اصبع است آن پيشه‏ور / وين قلم در فعل فرع است و اثر
entries.english: The other ant said, “That artist is the finger, and this pen is actually the derivative and the sign.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن مور سوم كز بازو است / ‏كه اصبع الغر ز زورش نقش بست
entries.english: A third ant said, “It is the work of the arm, by whose strength the slender finger depicted it.”

entries.farsi: همچنين مى‏رفت بالا تا يكى / ‏مهتر موران فطن بود اندكى
entries.english: In this fashion it was carried upward till a chief of the ants, was a little bit sagacious,

entries.farsi: گفت كز صورت مبينيد اين هنر / كه به خواب و مرگ گردد بى‏خبر
entries.english: Said, “Do not regard this accomplishment as proceeding from the form, which becomes unconscious in sleep and death.

entries.farsi: صورت آمد چون لباس و چون عصا / جز به عقل و جان نجنبد نقشها
entries.english: Form is like a garment or a staff: figures do not move except by means of intellect and spirit.”

entries.farsi: بى‏خبر بود او كه آن عقل و فؤاد / يك زمان از وى عنايت بر كند
entries.english: He was unaware that without the controlling influence of God that intellect and heart would be inert.

entries.farsi: عقل زيرك ابلهى‏ها مى‏كند / چونش گويا يافت ذو القرنين گفت
entries.english: If He withdraws His favour from it for a single moment, the acute intellect will commit follies.

entries.farsi: ‏چون كه كوه قاف در نطق سفت / كاى سخن گوى خبير راز دان
entries.english: When Dhu ’l-Qarnayn found it speaking, he said, after Mount Qáf had bored the pearls of speech,

entries.farsi: ‏از صفات حق بكن با من بيان / گفت رو كان وصف از آن هايل‏تر است
entries.english: “O eloquent one, who are wise and know the mystery, expound to me the Attributes of God.”

entries.farsi: كه بيان بر وى تواند برد دست / يا قلم را زهره باشد كه به سر
entries.english: It answered, “Go, for those qualities are too terrible for exposition to put its hand on them,

entries.farsi: از عجبهاى حق اى حبر نكو / گفت اينك دشت سيصد ساله راه
entries.english: Or for the pen to dare inscribe with its point information concerning them on the pages.”

entries.farsi: بر نويسد بر صحايف ز آن خبر / گفت كمتر داستانى باز گو
entries.english: He said, “Relate a lesser tale concerning the wonders of God, O goodly divine.”

entries.farsi: ‏كوههاى برف پر كرده ست شاه / كوه بر كه بى‏شمار و بى‏عدد
entries.english: It said, “Look, the King has made a plain full of snow-mountains, for the distance of a three hundred years’ journey—

entries.farsi: مى‏رسد در هر زمان برفش مدد / كوه برفى مى‏زند بر ديگرى
entries.english: Mountain on mountain, beyond count and number: the snow comes continually to replenish them.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏رساند برف سردى تا ثرى / كوه برفى مى‏زند بر كوه برف
entries.english: One snow-mountain is being piled on another: the snow brings coldness to the earth.

entries.farsi: ‏دم‏به‏دم ز انبار بى‏حد شگرف / گر نبودى اين چنين وادى شها
entries.english: At every moment snow-mountain is being piled on snow-mountain from the illimitable and vast storehouse.

entries.farsi: تف دوزخ محو كردى مر مرا / غافالن را كوههاى برف دان
entries.english: O king, if there were not a valley like this, the glowing heat of Hell would annihilate me.”

entries.farsi: ‏تا نسوزد پرده‏هاى عاقالن / گر نبودى عكس جهل برف باف
entries.english: Know that the heedless are snow-mountains, to the end that the veils of the intelligent may not be consumed.

entries.farsi: ‏سوختى از نار شوق آن كوه قاف / آتش از قهر خدا خود ذره‏اى است
entries.english: Were it not for the reflection of snow-weaving ignorance, that Mount Qáf would be consumed by the fire of longing.

entries.farsi: ‏بهر تهديد لئيمان دره‏اى است / با چنين قهرى كه زفت و فايق است
entries.english: The Fire in truth is an atom of God’s wrath; it is a whip to threaten the base.

entries.farsi: ‏برد لطفش بين كه بر وى سابق است / سبق بى‏چون و چگونه‏ى معنوى
entries.english: Notwithstanding such a wrath, which is mighty and surpasses all, know that the coolness of His clemency is prior to it

entries.farsi: ‏سابق و مسبوق ديدى بى‏دوى / گر نديدى آن بود از فهم پست
entries.english: A spiritual priority, unqualified and unconditioned: Have you seen the prior and the posterior without duality?

entries.farsi: ‏كه عقول خلق ز آن كان يك جو است / عيب بر خود نه نه بر آيات دين
entries.english: If you have not seen them that is because of feeble understanding; for the minds of God’s creatures are a single grain of that mine.

entries.farsi: ‏كى رسد بر چرخ دين مرغ گلين / مرغ را جوالنگه عالى هواست
entries.english: Lay the blame on yourself, not on the evidences of the Religion: how should the bird of clay reach the sky of the Religion?

entries.farsi: ز انكه نشو او ز شهوت وز هواست / پس تو حيران باش بى‏ال و بلى
entries.english: The bird’s lofty soaring-place is the air, since its origin is from lust and sensuality.

entries.farsi: ‏تا ز رحمت پيشت آيد محملى / چون ز فهم اين عجايب كودنى
entries.english: Therefore be dumbfounded without nay or yea, in order that a litter may come from Mercy to carry you.

entries.farsi: ‏گر بلى گويى تكلف مى‏كنى / ور بگويى نه زند نه گردنت
entries.english: Forasmuch as you are too dull to apprehend these wonders, if you say “yea” you will be straying from the truth;

entries.farsi: قهر بر بندد بدان نه روزنت / پس همين حيران و واله باش و بس
entries.english: And if you say “nay,” the “nay” will be-head you: on account of that “nay” Wrath will shut your window.

entries.farsi: ‏تا در آيد نصر حق از پيش و پس / چون كه حيران گشتى و گيج و فنا
entries.english: Be, then, only dumbfounded and distraught, nothing else that God’s aid may come in from before and behind.

entries.farsi: با زبان حال گفتى اهدنا / زفت زفت است و چو لرزان مى‏شوى
entries.english: When you have become dumbfounded and crazed and non-existent, you have said with mute eloquence, “Lead us.”

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏شود آن زفت نرم و مستوى / ز انكه شكل زفت بهر منكر است
entries.english: It is mighty, mighty; but when you begin to tremble, that mighty becomes assuaged and equable,

entries.farsi: ‏چون كه عاجز آمدى لطف و بر است
entries.english: Because the mighty shape is for the unbeliever; when you have become helpless, it is mercy and kindness.

sections.title_fa: نمودن جبرئيل عليه السلام خود را به مصطفى صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله به صورت خويش و از هفت صد ‏پر او چون يك پر ظاهر شد افق را بگرفت و آفتاب محجوب شد با همه شعاعش

sections.title_en: How Gabriel, on whom be peace, showed himself to Mustafa, God bless and save him, in his own shape; and how, when one of his seven hundred wings became visible, it covered the horizon, and the sun with all its radiance was veiled over.

entries.farsi: مصطفى مى‏گفت پيش جبرئيل / ‏كه چنان كه صورت تست اى خليل
entries.english: Mustafa said in the presence of Gabriel, “Even as your shape is, O friend,

entries.farsi: مر مرا بنما تو محسوس آشكار / تا ببينم مر ترا نظاره وار
entries.english: Show it to me sensibly and visibly, that I may behold you as spectators.”

entries.farsi: گفت نتوانى و طاقت نبودت / ‏حس ضعيف است و تنك سخت آيدت
entries.english: He replied, “You cannot and have not the power to endure it; the sense is weak and frail: it would be too painful for you.

entries.farsi: گفت بنما تا ببيند اين جسد / تا چه حد حس نازك است و بى‏مدد
entries.english: “Show yourself,” said he, “that this body may perceive to what an extent the senses are frail and without resources.”

entries.farsi: آدمى را هست حس تن سقيم / ‏ليك در باطن يكى خلقى عظيم
entries.english: Man’s bodily senses are infirm, but he has a potent nature within.

entries.farsi: بر مثال سنگ و آهن اين تنه / ‏ليك هست او در صفت آتش زنه
entries.english: This body resembles flint and steel, but in quality it is a striker of fire.

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن مولد ايجاد نار / زاد آتش بر دو والد قهربار
entries.english: Flint and steel are the birth-place whence fire is brought into being: (from them) fire is born, domineering over both its parents.

entries.farsi: باز آتش دست كار وصف تن / ‏هست قاهر بر تن او و شعله زن
entries.english: Fire, again, exercises sway over the bodily nature: it is dominant over the body and flaming;

entries.farsi: باز در تن شعله ابراهيم‏وار / كه از او مقهور گردد برج نار
entries.english: Yet again, there is in the body an Abraham-like flame whereby the tower of fire is subdued.

entries.farsi: الجرم گفت آن رسول ذو فنون / ‏رمز نحن الاخرون السابقون
entries.english: In consequence the all-accomplished Prophet said symbolically, “We are the last and the first.”

entries.farsi: ظاهر اين دو به سندانى زبون / ‏در صفت از كان آهنها فزون
entries.english: The material form of these two is vanquished by a anvil, in quality they are superior to the my of iron ores.

entries.farsi: پس به صورت آدمى فرع جهان / ‏وز صفت اصل جهان اين را بدان
entries.english: Therefore Man is in appearance a derivative of the world, and intrinsically the origin of the world. Observe this!

entries.farsi: ظاهرش را پشه‏اى آرد به چرخ / ‏باطنش باشد محيط هفت چرخ
entries.english: A gnat will set his outward frame whirling round; his inward nature encompasses the Seven Heavens.

entries.farsi: چون كه كرد الحاح بنمود اندكى / ‏هيبتى كه كه شود زو مندكى
entries.english: When he persisted, he displayed a little the awful majesty by which a mountain would be reduced to dust.

entries.farsi: شهپرى بگرفته شرق و غرب را / ‏از مهابت گشت بى‏هش مصطفى
entries.english: A single royal wing covered the east and the west: Mustafa became senseless from awe.

entries.farsi: چون ز بيم و ترس بى‏هوشش بديد / جبرئيل آمد در آغوشش كشيد
entries.english: When Gabriel saw him senseless from fear and dread, he came and drew him into his arms.

entries.farsi: آن مهابت قسمت بيگانگان / ‏وين تجمش دوستان را رايگان
entries.english: That awe is the portion of aliens, while this fond affection is freely bestowed on friends.

entries.farsi: هست شاهان را زمان بر نشست / ‏هول سرهنگان و صارم‏ها به دست
entries.english: Kings, when seated on the throne, have formidable guardsmen with swords in their hands,

entries.farsi: بانگ چاووشان و آن چوگانها / كه شود سست از نهيبش جانها
entries.english: Staves and lances and scimitars, so that lions would tremble in awe;

entries.farsi: دور باش و نيزه و شمشيرها / كه بلرزند از مهابت شيرها
entries.english: The shouts of sergeants with their maces, by the terror of which souls are enfeebled

entries.farsi: اين براى خاص و عام ره گذر / كه كندشان از شهنشاهى خبر
entries.english: This is for the high and low in the street, to announce to them an emperor.

entries.farsi: از براى عام باشد اين شكوه / ‏تا كاله كبر ننهند آن گروه
entries.english: This pomp is for the sake of the common, so that those people may not put on the tiara of arrogance;

entries.farsi: تا من و ماهاى ايشان بشكند / نفس خود بين فتنه و شر كم كند
entries.english: That it may break their egoism and that the self-conceited carnal soul may not work mischief and evil.

entries.farsi: شهر از آن ايمن شود كان شهريار / دارد اندر قهر زخم و گير و دار
entries.english: The country is preserved from that by the king’s having force and authority to inflict punishment.

entries.farsi: پس بميرد آن هوسها در نفوس / ‏هيبت شه مانع آيد ز آن نحوس
entries.english: Therefore those vain desires are extinguished in souls: awe of the king prevents that disaster.

entries.farsi: باز چون آيد به سوى بزم خاص / ‏كى بود آن جا مهابت يا قصاص
entries.english: Again, when he comes to the private banquet, how should awe or retaliation be there?

entries.farsi: حلم در حلم است و رحمتها به جوش / ‏نشنوى از غير چنگ و نى خروش
entries.english: There clemency on clemency is and mercies overflowing; you will hear no noise but that of the harp and flute.

entries.farsi: طبل و كوس هول باشد وقت جنگ / ‏وقت عشرت با خواص آواز چنگ
entries.english: In time of war there is the terrible drum and kettle-drum; in the hour of feasting with favourites there is the sound of the harp.

entries.farsi: آن زره و آن خود مر چاليش راست / ‏وين حرير و رود مر تعريش راست
entries.english: The Board of Audit is for the common, while the fair ones resembling peris are for the cup-companion.

entries.farsi: هست ديوان محاسب عام را / و آن پرى رويان حريف جام را
entries.english: The coat of mail and the helmet are for the battle, while this silken raiment and music are for the bower.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد اى جواد / ختم كن و اهلل اعلم بالرشاد
entries.english: This topic has no end, O generous one: conclude it; and God knows best the right course.

entries.farsi: اندر احمد آن حسى كو غارب است / ‏خفته اين دم زير خاك يثرب است
entries.english: The senses in Ahmad, which are mortal, are now laid asleep under the soil of Medina,

entries.farsi: و آن عظيم الخلق او كان صفدر است / ‏بى‏تغير مقعد صدق اندر است
entries.english: But that mighty-natured part of him, which is unconquerable, dwells, without having suffered corruption, within the abode of truth.

entries.farsi: جاى تغييرات اوصاف تن است / ‏روح باقى آفتابى روشن است
entries.english: The bodily attributes are exposed to corruption; the everlasting spirit is a shining sun,

entries.farsi: آفتاب از ذره كى مدهوش شد / شمع از پروانه كى بى‏هوش شد
entries.english: Incorruptible, for it is not of the east; unchangeable, for it is not of the west

entries.farsi: بى‏ز تغييرى كه ال شرقية / بى‏ز تبديلى كه ال غربية
entries.english: How was the sun dumbfounded by a mote? How could the candle be made senseless by a moth?

entries.farsi: جسم احمد را تعلق بد بدان / ‏اين تغير آن تن باشد بدان
entries.english: The body of Ahmad was liable to that: know that this corruption belongs to the body,

entries.farsi: همچو رنجورى و همچون خواب و درد / جان از اين اوصاف باشد پاك و فرد
entries.english: Such as sickness and sleep and pain; the spirit is untouched by these affections and wholly detached.

entries.farsi: خود نتانم ور بگويم وصف جان / ‏زلزله افتد در اين كون و مكان
entries.english: Indeed I cannot describe the spirit, and if I describe it, quaking would fall upon this phenomenal existence and spatiality.

entries.farsi: روبهش گر يك دمى آشفته بود / شير جان مانا كه آن دم خفته بود
entries.english: If its fox was perturbed for a moment, belike the lion-spirit was then asleep.

entries.farsi: خفته بود آن شير كز خواب است پاك / ‏اينت شير نرمسار سهمناك
entries.english: That lion which is immune from sleep was asleep: lo, a complaisant terrible lion!

entries.farsi: خفته سازد شير خود را آن چنان / ‏كه تمامش مرده دانند اين سگان
entries.english: The lion feigns to be asleep so that those curs think he is quite dead;

entries.farsi: ور نه در عالم كه را زهره بدى / ‏كه ربودى از ضعيفى‏تر بدى
entries.english: Else, who in the world would dare to rob a poor man of a grain of laxative mineral?

entries.farsi: كف احمد ز آن نظر مخدوش گشت / ‏بحر او از مهر كف پر جوش گشت
entries.english: The foam of Ahmad was torn by that sight, his sea surged up for love of the foam.

entries.farsi: كف احمد ز آن نظر مخدوش گشت / ‏بحر او از مهر كف پر جوش گشت
entries.english: The moon is entirely a bounteous light-diffusing hand: if the moon have no hand, let it have none!

entries.farsi: احمد ار بگشايد آن پر جليل / ‏تا ابد بى‏هوش ماند جبرئيل
entries.english: If Ahmad should display that glorious pinion, Gabriel would remain dumbfounded forever.

entries.farsi: چون گذشت احمد ز سدره و مرصدش / ‏و ز مقام جبرئيل و از حدش
entries.english: When Ahmad passed beyond the Lote-tree and his place of watch and station and farthest limit,

entries.farsi: گفت او را هين بپر اندر پى‏ام / ‏گفت رو رو من حريف تو نى‏ام
entries.english: He said to him, “Listen; fly after me.” He said, “Go, go; I am no longer your companion.”

entries.farsi: باز گفت او را بيا اى پرده سوز / من به اوج خود نرفتستم هنوز
entries.english: He answered him, saying, “Come, O destroyer of veils: I have not yet advanced to my zenith.”

entries.farsi: گفت بيرون زين حد اى خوش فر من / ‏گر زنم پرى بسوزد پر من
entries.english: He replied, “O my illustrious friend, if I take one flight beyond this limit, my wings will be consumed.”

entries.farsi: بى‏هشيها جمله اينجا بازى است / ‏چند جان دارى كه جان پردازى است
entries.english: This tale of the elect losing their senses in the most elect is amazement on amazement.

entries.farsi: حيرت اندر حيرت آمد اين قصص / ‏بى‏هشى خاصگان اندر اخص
entries.english: Here all unconsciousness is play. How long will you keep possession of your soul? For it is abandoning your soul.

entries.farsi: جبرئيال گر شريفى و عزيز / تو نه‏اى پروانه و نه شمع نيز
entries.english: O “Gabriel,” though you are noble and revered, you are neither the moth nor the candle .

entries.farsi: اين حديث منقلب را گور كن / ‏شير را بر عكس صيد گور كن
entries.english: When the candle calls at the moment of illumination, the soul of the moth does not shrink from burning.

entries.farsi: شمع چون دعوت كند وقت فروز / جان پروانه نپرهيزد ز سوز
entries.english: Bury this topsy-turvy discourse: make the lion the prey of the wild ass.

entries.farsi: آن كه بر نگذشت اجزاش از زمين / ‏پيش او معكوس و قلماشى است اين
entries.english: Stop up your word-sweating water-skin; do not open the bag of your reckless talk.

entries.farsi: بند كن مشك سخن پاشيت را / وامكن انبان قلماشيت را
entries.english: He whose parts have not passed beyond the earth— this is absurd and reckless talk in his view.

entries.farsi: ال تخالفهم حبيبى دارهم / ‏يا غريبا نازال فى دارهم
entries.english: Do not resist them, O my beloved; deal gently with them, O stranger lodging in their home.

entries.farsi: اعط ما شاءوا و راموا و ارضهم / ‏يا ظعينا ساكنا فى ارضهم
entries.english: Give what they wish and desire, and satisfy them, O emigrant dwelling in their land.

entries.farsi: تا رسيدن در شه و در ناز خوش / ‏رازيا با مرغزى مى‏ساز خوش
entries.english: Till coming to the king and to sweet delight, O man of Rayy be on good terms with the man of Merv.

entries.farsi: موسيا در پيش فرعون زمن / نرم بايد گفت قَوْلاً لينا
entries.english: O “Moses,” in presence of the Pharaoh of the time you must speak softly with mild words.

entries.farsi: آب اگر در روغن جوشان كنى / ‏ديگدان و ديگ را ويران كنى
entries.english: If you put water into boiling oil, you will destroy the trivet and the kettle.

entries.farsi: نرم گو ليكن مگو غير صواب / ‏وسوسه مفروش در لين الخطاب
entries.english: Speak softly, but do not speak aught except the truth: do not offer temptation in your mildness of address.

entries.farsi: وقت عصر آمد سخن كوتاه كن / ‏اى كه عصرت عصر را آگاه كن
entries.english: The time of afternoon is come: cut short the discourse, O you whose expression makes (the people of) the age acquainted.

entries.farsi: نطق جان را روضه‏ى جانيستى / ‏گر ز حرف و صوت مستغنيستى
entries.english: Do you tell the clay-eater that sugar is better: do not show injurious softness, do not give him clay.

entries.farsi: گو تو مر گل خواره را كه قند به / ‏نرمى فاسد مكن طينش مده
entries.english: Speech would be a spiritual garden to the soul, if it were independent of letters and sounds.

entries.farsi: اين سر خر در ميان قندزار / اى بسا كس را كه بنهاده ست خار
entries.english: Oh, there are many a one in whom this donkey’s head amidst the sugar plantation has fixed a thorn!

entries.farsi: ظن ببرد از دور كان آن است و بس / ‏چون قچ مغلوب وامى‏رفت پس
entries.english: He, from afar, supposed that it is just that, nothing more; he was retiring, like a ram vanquished in fight.

entries.farsi: صورت حرف آن سر خر دان يقين / ‏در رز معنى و فردوس برين
entries.english: Know for sure that the form is that donkey’s head in the vineyard and highest Paradise of the spiritual reality.

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين در آر / اين سر خر را در آن بطيخ زار
entries.english: O Ziyá’u ’l-Haqq Husámu’ddín, bring this donkey’s head into that melon-field,

entries.farsi: تا سر خر چون بمرد از مسلخه / ‏نشو ديگر بخشدش آن مطبخه
entries.english: In order that, when the donkey’s head has died to the skinning-place, that kitchen may bestow on it another growth.

entries.farsi: هين ز ما صورت‏گرى و جان ز تو / نه غلط هم اين خود و هم آن ز تو
entries.english: Listen, the shaping is from me and the spirit from you; nay, in error: truly both this and that are from you.

entries.farsi: بر فلك محمودى اى خورشيد فاش / ‏بر زمين هم تا ابد محمود باش
entries.english: You are glorified in Heaven, O conspicuous Sun: be also glorified on earth forever,

entries.farsi: تا زمينى با سمايى بلند / يك دل و يك قبله و يك خو شوند
entries.english: That the inhabitant of the earth may become one in heart and one in aim and one in nature with the sublime celestial.

entries.farsi: تفرقه بر خيزد و شرك و دوى / ‏وحدت است اندر وجود معنوى
entries.english: Separation and polytheism and duality will disappear: in real existence there is unity.

entries.farsi: موسى و هارون شوند اندر زمين / ‏مختلط خوش همچو شير و انگبين
entries.english: When my spirit recognises your spirit, they remember their being one in the past,

entries.farsi: ون شناسد جان من جان ترا / ‏ياد آرند اتحاد ما جرى
entries.english: And on the earth become Moses and Aaron, sweetly mingled like milk and honey.

entries.farsi: چون شناسد اندك و منكر شود / منكرى‏اش پرده‏ى ساتر شود
entries.english: When it recognises a little and denies, its denial becomes a veil covering.

entries.farsi: بس شناسايى بگردانيد رو / خشم كرد آن مه ز ناشكرى او
entries.english: Many a one who recognised averted his face: that Moon was angered by his ingratitude.

entries.farsi: زين سبب جان نبى را جان بد / ناشناسا گشت و پشت پاى زد
entries.english: Hence the evil spirit became unable to recognise the spirit of the Prophet and turned on its heel.

entries.farsi: اين همه خواندى فرو خوان لَمْ يكن / ‏تا بدانى لج اين گبر كهن
entries.english: You have read all this: read Lam yakun, that you may know the obstinacy of that old infidel.

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كه نقش احمد فر نمود / نعت او هر گبر را تعويذ بود
entries.english: Before the form of Ahmad displayed its glory, the description of him was a phylactery for every infidel.

entries.farsi: سجده مى‏كردند كاى رب بشر / در عيان آريش هر چه زودتر
entries.english: “There is someone like this”: “till he shall appear”; and their hearts were throbbing at the imagination of his face.

entries.farsi: كاين چنين كس هست تا آيد پديد / از خيال روش دلشان مى‏طپيد
entries.english: They were prostrating themselves, crying, “O Lord of mankind, will You bring him before our eyes as quickly as may be?”

entries.farsi: تا به نام احمد از يستفتحون / ‏ياغيانشان مى‏شدندى سر نگون
entries.english: In order that, by asking to grant them victory in the name of Ahmad, their enemies might be overthrown.

entries.farsi: نقش او مى‏گشت اندر راهشان / ‏در دل و در گوش و در افواهشان
entries.english: Whenever a formidable war arose, Ahmad’s pertinacity in onset was always their succour;

entries.farsi: هر كجا بيمارى مزمن بدى / ‏ياد اوشان داروى شافى شدى
entries.english: Wherever there was a chronic sickness, mention of him was always their healing medicine.

entries.farsi: هر كجا حرب مهولى آمدى / ‏غوثشان كرارى احمد بدى
entries.english: In their way his form was coming into their hearts and into their ears and into their mouths.

entries.farsi: نقش او را كى بيابد هر شغال / ‏بلكه فرع نقش او يعنى خيال
entries.english: How should every jackal perceive his form? Nay, the derivative of his form, that is to say, the imaginal idea.

entries.farsi: آن چنان فرخ بود نقشش بر او / كه رهد در حال ديوار از دو رو
entries.english: If his form should fall on the face of a wall, heart’s blood would trickle from the heart of the wall;

entries.farsi: نقش او بر روى ديوار ار فتد / از دل ديوار خون دل چكد
entries.english: And his form would be so auspicious for it that the wall would at once be saved from having a double face.

entries.farsi: گشته با يك رويى اهل صفا / آن دو رويى عيب مر ديوار را
entries.english: Beside the single-face of the pure, that double-face has become a fault in the wall.

entries.farsi: اين همه تعظيم و تفخيم و وداد / چون بديدندش به صورت برد باد
entries.english: All this veneration and magnification and affection the wind swept away, as soon as they saw him in form.

entries.farsi: قلب آتش ديد و در دم شد سياه / ‏قلب را در قلب كى بوده ست راه
entries.english: The false coin saw the fire and immediately became black: when has there been a way for the false coin to enter the heart?

entries.farsi: قلب مى‏زد الف اشواق محك / ‏تا مريدان را در اندازد به شك
entries.english: The false coin was talking boastfully of its desire for the touchstone, that it might cast the disciples into doubt.

entries.farsi: افتد اندر دام مكرش ناكسى / ‏اين گمان سر بر زند از هر خسى
entries.english: A worthless one falls into the snare of its deceit; from every base fellow the thought pops up,

entries.farsi: كاين اگر نه نقد پاكيزه بدى / كاين اگر نه نقد پاكيزه بدى
entries.english: That if this was not genuine coin, how should it have become eager for the touchstone?

entries.farsi: آن محك كه او نهان دارد صفت / آن محك كه او نهان دارد صفت
entries.english: It desires the touchstone, but one of such a kind that its falseness will not be clearly exposed thereby.

entries.farsi: او محك مى‏خواهد اما آن چنان / ‏كه نگردد قلبى او ز آن عيان
entries.english: The touchstone that keeps hidden the quality is not a touchstone, nor the light of knowledge.

entries.farsi: آينه كاو عيب رو دارد نهان / ‏از براى خاطر هر قلتبان
entries.english: The mirror that keeps hidden the defects of the face to flatter every cuckold

entries.farsi: آينه نبود منافق باشد او / اين چنين آيينه را هرگز مجو
entries.english: Is not a mirror; it is hypocritical. Do not seek such a mirror so long as you can.

sections.title_fa: پایان دفتر چهارم
